-------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeChangeSet | +----------------+----------+----------------------+---------------------------+ | ResourceChange | Modify | DjangoInstance | True (requires replacement)| | ResourceChange | Add | AlarmTopic | False | +----------------+----------+----------------------+---------------------------+ Change details: 1. DjangoInstance will be REPLACED (new instance created, old terminated) - InstanceType: t3.medium → t3.large - Impact: Brief downtime during replacement 2. AlarmTopic will be ADDED - New SNS topic for CloudWatch alarms Review complete - Safe to execute
# Django Models Integration - Describe Change Set from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Q # Review change set before execution stack_name = 'anihpj-django-app' change_set_name = 'upgrade-to-t3-large' # Find stack job stack = JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains=stack_name).first() print(f"Stack: {stack.title if stack else 'Not found'}") # Get changes preview changes = [ {'action': 'Modify', 'resource': 'DjangoInstance', 'replacement': True}, {'action': 'Add', 'resource': 'AlarmTopic', 'replacement': False} ] for change in changes: skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name=f"cf:{change['resource']}:{change['action'].lower()}" )[0] print(f"Change: {change['action']} {change['resource']}") print(f"\n<QuerySet [total changes: {len(changes)}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [total changes: 2]>
Example 2: Check Change Set Status
#!/bin/bash
# Check if change set is ready to execute
STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-change-set \
  --change-set-name add-monitoring \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'Status' \
  --output text)

echo "Change set status: $STATUS"

if [ "$STATUS" == "CREATE_COMPLETE" ]; then
  echo "✓ Ready to execute"
else
  echo "⏳ Not ready - wait for completion"
fi
Change set status: CREATE_COMPLETE ✓ Ready to execute Change set summary: - Created: 2026-01-17T10:30:00Z - Changes: 4 additions, 0 modifications, 0 deletions - Execution time: ~5 minutes - Rollback available: Yes
# Django Models Integration - Check Change Set Status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Check change set readiness change_set_name = 'add-monitoring' status = 'CREATE_COMPLETE' # Track change set status in Skills status_skill = Skills.objects.update_or_create( name=f'changeset:{change_set_name}', defaults={'description': f'status:{status}'} )[0] print(f"Change set: {change_set_name}") print(f"Status: {status}") if status == 'CREATE_COMPLETE': print("✓ Ready to execute") else: print("⏳ Waiting for completion") print(f"<Skills: {status_skill.name} - {status_skill.description}>") # Output: <Skills: changeset:add-monitoring - status:CREATE_COMPLETE>
Example 3: Extract Change Details for Approval
#!/bin/bash
# Generate approval document with change details
aws cloudformation describe-change-set \
  --change-set-name scale-up-production \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --output json > change-set-details.json

# Extract key information
echo "=== Change Set Approval Request ==="
jq -r '.Changes[] | "[\(.ResourceChange.Action)] \(.ResourceChange.LogicalResourceId) - \(.ResourceChange.ResourceType)"' \
  change-set-details.json
=== Change Set Approval Request === [Modify] AutoScalingGroup - AWS::AutoScaling::AutoScalingGroup Property changes: - DesiredCapacity: 3 → 5 - Impact: 2 new EC2 instances launched [Modify] TargetGroup - AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::TargetGroup Property changes: - HealthCheckInterval: 30s → 15s (improved monitoring) Estimated monthly cost increase: +$45 Approval recommended: Yes Risk level: Low
# anihpj/jobpost/services/change_approval.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json class ChangeApproval: def __init__(self, stack_name): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') self.stack_name = stack_name def generate_approval_report(self, change_set_name): approval_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Change Approval', defaults={'description': 'Review and approve changes'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") approver = User.objects.get_or_create( username='change_approver', defaults={'email': 'approvals@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") approver_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=approver, defaults={'bio': 'Change approval specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") approval_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Approval Center', state='Governance', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = self.cf_client.describe_change_set( ChangeSetName=change_set_name, StackName=self.stack_name ) approval_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Approval: {change_set_name}', description=json.dumps(response.get('Changes', [])), author=approver_author, location=approval_location, job_type='APPROVAL', is_active=True ) approval_job.required_skills.add(approval_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return response
Example 4: Get Nested Stack Changes

Describe change set with nested stack changes.

#!/bin/bash
# Get changes including nested stacks
aws cloudformation describe-change-set \
  --change-set-name full-upgrade \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --include-nested-stacks \
  --query 'Changes[?ResourceChange.ChangeSource==`DirectModification`]' \
  --output json
[ { "Type": "Resource", "ResourceChange": { "Action": "Modify", "LogicalResourceId": "NestedVPCStack", "ResourceType": "AWS::CloudFormation::Stack", "ChangeSource": "DirectModification" } } ] Nested stack changes detected: 1
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/describe_nested_changes.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Describe change set with nested stack changes' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') nested_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Nested Stacks', defaults={'description': 'Manage nested stacks'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") stack_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='stack_manager', defaults={'email': 'stacks@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") stack_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=stack_user, defaults={'bio': 'Stack management specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") stack_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Stack Center', state='IaC', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.describe_change_set( ChangeSetName='full-upgrade', StackName='anihpj-django-app', IncludeNestedStacks=True ) changes = [c for c in response.get('Changes', []) if c.get('ResourceChange', {}).get('ChangeSource') == 'DirectModification'] nested_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Nested Stack Changes', description=f'Direct changes: {len(changes)}', author=stack_author, location=stack_location, job_type='NESTED_CHANGE', is_active=True ) nested_job.required_skills.add(nested_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 5: Automated Change Set Validation

Validate change set before execution.

#!/bin/bash
# Validate change set is safe
RESPONSE=$(aws cloudformation describe-change-set \
  --change-set-name upgrade-production \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --output json)

# Check for destructive changes
DELETIONS=$(echo $RESPONSE | jq '[.Changes[] | select(.ResourceChange.Action=="Remove")] | length')

if [ "$DELETIONS" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "⚠️  WARNING: $DELETIONS resources will be deleted"
else
  echo "✓ No deletions - safe to execute"
fi
✓ No deletions - safe to execute Validation Summary: - Additions: 2 - Modifications: 3 - Deletions: 0 Change set is safe to execute
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Change set validation API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ValidateChangeSetView(View): def get(self, request): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') change_set_name = request.GET.get('change_set_name') stack_name = request.GET.get('stack_name') validation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Change Validation', defaults={'description': 'Validate infrastructure changes'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") validator = User.objects.get_or_create( username='change_validator', defaults={'email': 'validate@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") validator_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=validator, defaults={'bio': 'Change validation specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") validation_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Validation Center', state='QA', country='DevOps', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.describe_change_set( ChangeSetName=change_set_name, StackName=stack_name ) deletions = [c for c in response.get('Changes', []) if c.get('ResourceChange', {}).get('Action') == 'Remove'] validation_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Validate: {change_set_name}', description=f'Deletions: {len(deletions)}', author=validator_author, location=validation_location, job_type='VALIDATION', is_active=len(deletions) == 0 ) validation_job.required_skills.add(validation_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({ 'safe': len(deletions) == 0, 'deletions': len(deletions) })

execute-change-set

Executes a change set to apply the proposed changes to the stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation execute-change-set \
  --change-set-name {name} \
  --stack-name {stack-name}
Parameters Description
  • --change-set-name (string, required): Name or ARN of change set to execute
  • --stack-name (string): Stack name (required if change-set-name is not ARN)
  • --disable-rollback: Disable rollback on update failure
Return Value
  • None: Command returns no output on success
5 Detailed CloudFormation execute-change-set Examples
Example 1: Execute Change Set and Monitor
#!/bin/bash
# Execute change set and wait for completion
aws cloudformation execute-change-set \
  --change-set-name upgrade-to-t3-large \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app

echo "Executing change set..."

# Wait for update to complete
aws cloudformation wait stack-update-complete \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app

echo "✓ Stack update completed successfully"
Executing change set... UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS Progress: [=====> ] 33% - Creating new instance (t3.large) [==========> ] 66% - Updating load balancer targets [===============>] 100% - Terminating old instance ✓ Stack update completed successfully Duration: 8 minutes Resources updated: 3 Status: UPDATE_COMPLETE
# Django Models Integration - Execute Change Set from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone # Execute change set and track progress stack_name = 'anihpj-django-app' change_set_name = 'upgrade-to-t3-large' # Track execution exec_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Change Execution', defaults={'description': 'Execute CloudFormation changes'} )[0] stack = JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains=stack_name).first() stack.skills.add(exec_skill) stack.is_active = True stack.save() print(f"Executing: {change_set_name}") print(f"Stack: {stack.title}") print("Status: UPDATE_COMPLETE") print(f"Duration: 8 minutes") print(f"<JobPost: {stack.title} - active={stack.is_active}>") # Output: <JobPost: anihpj-django-app - active=True>
Example 2: Execute with Event Monitoring
#!/bin/bash
# Execute and monitor events in real-time
aws cloudformation execute-change-set \
  --change-set-name add-monitoring \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app

# Monitor stack events
watch -n 5 'aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --max-items 5 \
  --query "StackEvents[*].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,LogicalResourceId]" \
  --output table'
Every 5.0s: aws cloudformation describe-stack-events... ------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeStackEvents | +----------------------+------------------+-----------------------+ | 2026-01-17T10:45:30Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | AlarmTopic | | 2026-01-17T10:45:15Z | CREATE_IN_PROGRESS | HighCPUAlarm | | 2026-01-17T10:45:00Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | SNSSubscription | +----------------------+------------------+-----------------------+ Change set execution in progress... Resources created: 3/4
# Django Models Integration - Execute with Event Monitoring from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import time # Monitor stack events during execution stack_name = 'anihpj-django-app' change_set = 'add-monitoring' events = [ ('10:45:30', 'CREATE_COMPLETE', 'AlarmTopic'), ('10:45:15', 'CREATE_IN_PROGRESS', 'HighCPUAlarm'), ('10:45:00', 'CREATE_COMPLETE', 'SNSSubscription'), ] for timestamp, status, resource in events: skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name=f'resource:{resource}:{status.lower()}' )[0] print(f"[{timestamp}] {status}: {resource}") print(f"\nResources tracked: {Skills.objects.filter(name__startswith='resource:').count()}") print(f"<QuerySet events={len(events)}>") # Output: <QuerySet events=3>
Example 3: Execute Multiple Change Sets in Sequence
#!/bin/bash
# Execute multiple change sets for phased deployment
CHANGE_SETS=("phase1-networking" "phase2-compute" "phase3-monitoring")

for CS in "${CHANGE_SETS[@]}"; do
  echo "Executing: $CS"
  
  aws cloudformation execute-change-set \
    --change-set-name $CS \
    --stack-name anihpj-django-app
  
  # Wait for completion before next phase
  aws cloudformation wait stack-update-complete \
    --stack-name anihpj-django-app
  
  echo "✓ $CS completed"
done

echo "✓ All phases deployed successfully"
Executing: phase1-networking ✓ phase1-networking completed (Duration: 4 minutes) Executing: phase2-compute ✓ phase2-compute completed (Duration: 6 minutes) Executing: phase3-monitoring ✓ phase3-monitoring completed (Duration: 2 minutes) ✓ All phases deployed successfully Total deployment time: 12 minutes Infrastructure ready for Django deployment
# anihpj/jobpost/services/phased_deployment.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class PhasedDeployment: def __init__(self, stack_name): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') self.stack_name = stack_name def execute_phases(self, change_sets): deploy_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Phased Deployment', defaults={'description': 'Multi-phase deployments'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") deploy_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='deploy_manager', defaults={'email': 'deploy@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") deploy_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=deploy_user, defaults={'bio': 'Deployment manager'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") deploy_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Deploy Center', state='Release', country='DevOps', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") for cs in change_sets: self.cf_client.execute_change_set(ChangeSetName=cs, StackName=self.stack_name) waiter = self.cf_client.get_waiter('stack_update_complete') waiter.wait(StackName=self.stack_name) print(f"Completed: {cs}") phase_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Phased Deployment Complete', description=f'Phases: {len(change_sets)}', author=deploy_author, location=deploy_location, job_type='PHASED_DEPLOY', is_active=False ) phase_job.required_skills.add(deploy_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 4: Execute with Rollback Configuration

Execute change set with rollback monitoring.

#!/bin/bash
# Execute with rollback configuration
aws cloudformation execute-change-set \
  --change-set-name production-update \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --disable-rollback

# Monitor and rollback if needed
STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
  --output text)

if [[ "$STATUS" == *"FAILED"* ]]; then
  echo "Update failed - manual rollback required"
else
  echo "Update successful: $STATUS"
fi
Executing change set with rollback disabled... Stack Status: UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS Resources updating: 4 Update successful: UPDATE_COMPLETE Rollback was not needed Duration: 5 minutes
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/execute_with_rollback.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Execute change set with rollback configuration' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--change-set-name', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') rollback_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Rollback Management', defaults={'description': 'Handle deployment rollbacks'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") rollback_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='rollback_admin', defaults={'email': 'rollback@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") rollback_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=rollback_user, defaults={'bio': 'Rollback specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") rollback_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Rollback Center', state='Recovery', country='DevOps', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") cf_client.execute_change_set( ChangeSetName=options['change_set_name'], StackName=options['stack_name'], DisableRollback=True ) waiter = cf_client.get_waiter('stack_update_complete') waiter.wait(StackName=options['stack_name']) rollback_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f"Execute: {options['change_set_name']}", description='Rollback disabled', author=rollback_author, location=rollback_location, job_type='EXECUTE_ROLLBACK', is_active=False ) rollback_job.required_skills.add(rollback_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 5: Blue-Green Deployment via Change Sets

Execute change set for blue-green deployment pattern.

#!/bin/bash
# Blue-green deployment using change sets
# Step 1: Create green environment
aws cloudformation execute-change-set \
  --change-set-name create-green-env \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app

aws cloudformation wait stack-update-complete \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app

# Step 2: Switch traffic
aws cloudformation execute-change-set \
  --change-set-name switch-traffic-to-green \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app

echo "Blue-green deployment complete"
Blue-Green Deployment Progress: Phase 1: Create Green Environment [=========> ] 60% - Green instances launching [===============>] 100% - Green environment ready Phase 2: Switch Traffic [=========> ] 60% - Updating load balancer [===============>] 100% - Traffic routed to green Blue-green deployment complete Zero downtime achieved
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Blue-green deployment API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class BlueGreenDeployView(View): def post(self, request): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = request.POST.get('stack_name') bluegreen_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Blue-Green Deployment', defaults={'description': 'Zero-downtime deployments'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") deploy_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='bluegreen_admin', defaults={'email': 'bluegreen@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") deploy_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=deploy_user, defaults={'bio': 'Blue-green deployment expert'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") deploy_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Deploy HQ', state='Production', country='Cloud', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Execute green environment cf_client.execute_change_set(ChangeSetName='create-green-env', StackName=stack_name) waiter = cf_client.get_waiter('stack_update_complete') waiter.wait(StackName=stack_name) # Switch traffic cf_client.execute_change_set(ChangeSetName='switch-traffic-to-green', StackName=stack_name) waiter.wait(StackName=stack_name) bluegreen_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Blue-Green Deployment', description='Zero-downtime deployment complete', author=deploy_author, location=deploy_location, job_type='BLUE_GREEN', is_active=False ) bluegreen_job.required_skills.add(bluegreen_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({'status': 'complete', 'downtime': 0})

delete-change-set

Deletes a CloudFormation change set without executing it. Use this command to clean up rejected or obsolete change sets from your anihpj Django infrastructure stack, maintaining a clean deployment history and avoiding clutter in your change management workflow.

Syntax
aws cloudformation delete-change-set \
  --change-set-name {name-or-arn} \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --change-set-name (string, required): Name or ARN of the change set to delete. Example: rejected-update-20260117
  • --stack-name (string): Name of the stack associated with the change set. Required if change-set-name is not an ARN
  • --region (string): AWS region where the stack exists. Example: us-east-1
Return Value
  • None: Command returns empty response on successful deletion
  • Error: Returns error if change set not found or already executed
5 Detailed CloudFormation delete-change-set Examples
Example 1: Delete Rejected Change Set After Review

Remove a change set that was rejected during code review for the Django infrastructure stack.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete change set after review rejection
aws cloudformation delete-change-set \
  --change-set-name risky-security-update \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Change set deleted - no infrastructure changes applied"

# Verify deletion
aws cloudformation list-change-sets \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'Summaries[?ChangeSetName==`risky-security-update`]' \
  --output json
✓ Change set deleted - no infrastructure changes applied Verification - Change set not found: [] Deletion Summary: - Change Set: risky-security-update - Stack: anihpj-django-app - Status: Successfully deleted - Infrastructure Impact: None (changes not applied)
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/delete_change_set.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Delete a rejected CloudFormation change set and track in Django' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--change-set-name', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, default='anihpj-django-app') def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') change_set_name = options['change_set_name'] stack_name = options['stack_name'] # Delete change set try: cf_client.delete_change_set( ChangeSetName=change_set_name, StackName=stack_name ) success = True except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}") success = False # Create change management skill change_mgmt_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Change Management', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure change review and control'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get reviewer user reviewer = User.objects.filter(is_staff=True).first() if not reviewer: reviewer = User.objects.create_user( username='change_reviewer', email='review@anihpj.com' ) print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author reviewer_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=reviewer, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure change reviewer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Review location review_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Review Board', state='Cloud Governance', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Track deletion deletion_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Change Set Deleted: {change_set_name}', description=f'Stack: {stack_name}, Success: {success}', author=reviewer_author, location=review_location, job_type='CHANGE_MANAGEMENT', is_active=False ) deletion_post.required_skills.add(change_mgmt_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") print(f"{'✓' if success else '✗'} Deletion tracked as JobPost #{deletion_post.id}")
Example 2: Clean Up Multiple Old Change Sets

Delete all stale change sets from the Django stack to maintain clean deployment history.

#!/bin/bash
# Clean up all old change sets
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
DELETED_COUNT=0

# Get all change sets
CHANGE_SETS=$(aws cloudformation list-change-sets \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status!=`CREATE_IN_PROGRESS`].ChangeSetName' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

for CS in $CHANGE_SETS; do
    aws cloudformation delete-change-set \
      --change-set-name "$CS" \
      --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
      --region us-east-1
    echo "✓ Deleted: $CS"
    ((DELETED_COUNT++))
done

echo ""
echo "Cleanup complete: $DELETED_COUNT change sets deleted"
✓ Deleted: old-update-2026-01-10 ✓ Deleted: test-changes-2026-01-12 ✓ Deleted: deprecated-config-2026-01-15 ✓ Deleted: failed-migration-2026-01-16 ✓ Deleted: rollback-attempt-2026-01-16 Cleanup complete: 5 change sets deleted Stack Status: Clean Remaining change sets: 0 Ready for new deployments
# anihpj/jobpost/services/change_set_cleanup.py from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ChangeSetCleanupService: def __init__(self, stack_name='anihpj-django-app'): self.stack_name = stack_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def cleanup_all(self): # List all change sets response = self.cf_client.list_change_sets(StackName=self.stack_name) change_sets = response.get('Summaries', []) # Create cleanup skill cleanup_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure Cleanup', defaults={'description': 'CloudFormation resource cleanup'} )[0] automation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='DevOps Automation', defaults={'description': 'Automated infrastructure management'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get automation user automation_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='cleanup_automation', defaults={ 'email': 'cleanup@anihpj.com', 'is_active': True } )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author automation_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=automation_user, defaults={'bio': 'Automated cleanup service'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Cleanup location cleanup_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Maintenance Center', state='Operations', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") deleted_count = 0 for cs in change_sets: if cs['Status'] != 'CREATE_IN_PROGRESS': try: self.cf_client.delete_change_set( ChangeSetName=cs['ChangeSetName'], StackName=self.stack_name ) print(f"✓ Deleted: {cs['ChangeSetName']}") deleted_count += 1 except Exception as e: print(f"✗ Failed: {cs['ChangeSetName']} - {e}") # Track cleanup cleanup_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Change Set Cleanup: {self.stack_name}', description=f'Deleted {deleted_count} of {len(change_sets)} change sets', author=automation_author, location=cleanup_location, job_type='MAINTENANCE', is_active=False ) cleanup_post.required_skills.add(cleanup_skill, automation_skill) print(f"\nJobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") print(f"Cleanup logged as JobPost #{cleanup_post.id}") return deleted_count
Example 3: Delete Change Set by ARN

Delete a specific change set using its full ARN identifier for precise targeting.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete change set by ARN
CHANGE_SET_ARN="arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:changeSet/failed-update/abc123"

aws cloudformation delete-change-set \
  --change-set-name "$CHANGE_SET_ARN" \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Deleted change set by ARN"

# Get stack name from ARN for logging
STACK_NAME=$(echo $CHANGE_SET_ARN | cut -d'/' -f2)
echo "Stack: $STACK_NAME"
✓ Deleted change set by ARN Change Set ARN: arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:changeSet/failed-update/abc123 Stack: anihpj-django-app Region: us-east-1 Deletion confirmed - ARN no longer valid
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Delete change set by ARN from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class DeleteChangeSetByARNView(View): def post(self, request): change_set_arn = request.POST.get('change_set_arn') cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') try: cf_client.delete_change_set(ChangeSetName=change_set_arn) success = True message = 'Change set deleted successfully' except Exception as e: success = False message = str(e) # Track deletion skill arn_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='ARN Management', defaults={'description': 'AWS Resource Name handling'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get requesting user user = request.user if request.user.is_authenticated else None all_users = User.objects.all() print(f"Users: {all_users.count()}") # Authors authors = Author.objects.all() print(f"Authors: {authors.count()}") # Location aws_location = Location.objects.filter(remote=True).first() print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Log deletion jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True) print(f"Active JobPosts: {jobs.count()}") return JsonResponse({ 'success': success, 'message': message, 'arn': change_set_arn })
Example 4: Delete Failed Change Sets Only

Clean up only change sets that failed creation, keeping successful ones for audit.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete only FAILED change sets
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

FAILED_CS=$(aws cloudformation list-change-sets \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`FAILED`].ChangeSetName' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

if [ -z "$FAILED_CS" ]; then
    echo "No failed change sets found"
else
    for CS in $FAILED_CS; do
        aws cloudformation delete-change-set \
          --change-set-name "$CS" \
          --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
          --region us-east-1
        echo "✓ Deleted failed: $CS"
    done
fi
✓ Deleted failed: invalid-template-20260115 ✓ Deleted failed: missing-params-20260116 Cleanup Summary: - Failed change sets deleted: 2 - Successful change sets retained: 3 - Stack history preserved for audit
# anihpj/jobpost/tasks.py - Celery task for failed change set cleanup from celery import shared_task from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 @shared_task def cleanup_failed_change_sets(stack_name='anihpj-django-app'): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') # List change sets response = cf_client.list_change_sets(StackName=stack_name) change_sets = response.get('Summaries', []) # Filter failed ones failed_cs = [cs for cs in change_sets if cs['Status'] == 'FAILED'] # Create task skill celery_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Celery Tasks', defaults={'description': 'Asynchronous task processing'} )[0] error_handling_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Error Handling', defaults={'description': 'Failed resource cleanup'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get system user system_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='celery_worker', defaults={'email': 'celery@anihpj.com', 'is_active': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # System author system_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=system_user, defaults={'bio': 'Celery task worker'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Task location task_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Task Queue', state='Background', country='Celery', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") deleted_count = 0 for cs in failed_cs: try: cf_client.delete_change_set( ChangeSetName=cs['ChangeSetName'], StackName=stack_name ) print(f"✓ Deleted: {cs['ChangeSetName']}") deleted_count += 1 except Exception as e: print(f"✗ Error: {e}") # Log task completion task_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Failed CS Cleanup Task: {stack_name}', description=f'Deleted {deleted_count} failed change sets', author=system_author, location=task_location, job_type='TASK', is_active=False ) task_post.required_skills.add(celery_skill, error_handling_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return deleted_count
Example 5: Conditional Delete with Confirmation

Implement safe deletion with status check and user confirmation before proceeding.

#!/bin/bash
# Safe delete with confirmation
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
CHANGE_SET_NAME="production-update-20260117"

# Get change set details first
echo "Checking change set status..."
STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-change-set \
  --change-set-name $CHANGE_SET_NAME \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Status' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

echo "Change Set: $CHANGE_SET_NAME"
echo "Status: $STATUS"

if [ "$STATUS" = "CREATE_COMPLETE" ]; then
    read -p "This change set is ready to execute. Delete anyway? (y/n): " confirm
    if [ "$confirm" = "y" ]; then
        aws cloudformation delete-change-set \
          --change-set-name $CHANGE_SET_NAME \
          --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
          --region us-east-1
        echo "✓ Deleted"
    else
        echo "Deletion cancelled"
    fi
else
    echo "Safe to delete (status: $STATUS)"
    aws cloudformation delete-change-set \
      --change-set-name $CHANGE_SET_NAME \
      --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
      --region us-east-1
    echo "✓ Deleted"
fi
Checking change set status... Change Set: production-update-20260117 Status: CREATE_COMPLETE This change set is ready to execute. Delete anyway? (y/n): y ✓ Deleted Deletion Log: - Change Set: production-update-20260117 - Previous Status: CREATE_COMPLETE (ready to execute) - Action: User confirmed deletion - Result: Successfully deleted
# anihpj/jobpost/services/safe_change_set_delete.py from django.contrib.auth.decorators import permission_required from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class SafeChangeSetDeleter: def __init__(self, stack_name='anihpj-django-app'): self.stack_name = stack_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def safe_delete(self, change_set_name, user, force=False): # Check current status try: response = self.cf_client.describe_change_set( ChangeSetName=change_set_name, StackName=self.stack_name ) status = response['Status'] except Exception as e: return {'success': False, 'error': str(e)} # Safety check if status == 'CREATE_COMPLETE' and not force: return { 'success': False, 'requires_confirmation': True, 'message': 'Change set is ready to execute. Set force=True to delete.' } # Create safety skill safety_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Safe Operations', defaults={'description': 'Confirmed infrastructure changes'} )[0] governance_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Governance', defaults={'description': 'Change control governance'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Validate user permissions if not user.is_staff: return {'success': False, 'error': 'Insufficient permissions'} all_users = User.objects.filter(is_staff=True) print(f"Staff users: {all_users.count()}") # Get user's author profile author = Author.objects.filter(user=user).first() print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Governance location gov_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Governance Board', state='Approval Center', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Proceed with deletion self.cf_client.delete_change_set( ChangeSetName=change_set_name, StackName=self.stack_name ) # Log safe deletion deletion_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Safe Delete: {change_set_name}', description=f'Status was {status}, force={force}, user={user.username}', author=author, location=gov_location, job_type='GOVERNANCE', is_active=False ) deletion_post.required_skills.add(safety_skill, governance_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return { 'success': True, 'change_set': change_set_name, 'previous_status': status, 'job_post_id': deletion_post.id }

list-change-sets

Lists all change sets associated with a specified CloudFormation stack. Use this to view pending changes, executed updates, and failed change sets for your anihpj Django infrastructure stack, enabling proper change management and audit tracking.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-change-sets \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --next-token {pagination-token} \
  --region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Name or unique ID of the stack to list change sets for. Example: anihpj-django-app
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token from previous response for additional results
  • --region (string): AWS region where the stack exists. Example: us-east-1
Return Value
  • Summaries (list): Array of change set summary objects containing:
    • StackId: ID of the stack associated with the change set
    • StackName: Name of the stack
    • ChangeSetId: Unique identifier for the change set
    • ChangeSetName: Name of the change set
    • ExecutionStatus: Execution status (AVAILABLE, EXECUTE_IN_PROGRESS, etc.)
    • Status: Status (CREATE_PENDING, CREATE_IN_PROGRESS, CREATE_COMPLETE, etc.)
    • StatusReason: Reason for current status
    • CreationTime: Time the change set was created
    • Description: Description provided during creation
  • NextToken (string): Token for pagination if more results exist
5 Detailed CloudFormation list-change-sets Examples
Example 1: List All Change Sets with Status

View all change sets for the Django application stack with their current status and creation time.

#!/bin/bash
# List all change sets for Django stack
aws cloudformation list-change-sets \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[ChangeSetName,Status,ExecutionStatus,CreationTime,Description]' \
  --output table \
  --region us-east-1
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListChangeSets - anihpj-django-app | +---------------------------+------------------+-------------+------------------------+------------------+ | ChangeSetName | Status | ExecStatus | CreationTime | Description | +---------------------------+------------------+-------------+------------------------+------------------+ | upgrade-to-t3-large | CREATE_COMPLETE | AVAILABLE | 2026-01-17T09:00:00Z | Scale EC2 up | | add-cloudwatch-alarms | CREATE_COMPLETE | AVAILABLE | 2026-01-17T10:15:00Z | Add monitoring | | security-hardening | CREATE_COMPLETE | AVAILABLE | 2026-01-17T11:30:00Z | Security updates | | deprecated-update | FAILED | UNAVAILABLE | 2026-01-16T08:00:00Z | Failed change | +---------------------------+------------------+-------------+------------------------+------------------+ Summary: - Total change sets: 4 - Available for execution: 3 - Failed: 1
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/list_change_sets.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'List all CloudFormation change sets and track in Django' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, default='anihpj-django-app') def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = options['stack_name'] # List change sets response = cf_client.list_change_sets(StackName=stack_name) change_sets = response.get('Summaries', []) # Create change management skill change_mgmt_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Change Management', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure change tracking and approval'} )[0] audit_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Audit Trail', defaults={'description': 'Change history and compliance tracking'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get audit user audit_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='change_auditor', defaults={ 'email': 'audit@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True } )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author audit_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=audit_user, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure change auditor'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Audit location audit_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Audit Center', state='Compliance', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Process change sets print(f"\nChange Sets for {stack_name}:") print("-" * 60) status_counts = {} for cs in change_sets: status = cs['Status'] status_counts[status] = status_counts.get(status, 0) + 1 print(f"Name: {cs['ChangeSetName']}") print(f"Status: {status}") print(f"Execution: {cs.get('ExecutionStatus', 'N/A')}") print(f"Created: {cs['CreationTime']}") print("-" * 40) # Create audit job post audit_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Change Set Audit: {stack_name}', description=f'{len(change_sets)} change sets reviewed', author=audit_author, location=audit_location, job_type='AUDIT', is_active=False ) audit_post.required_skills.add(change_mgmt_skill, audit_skill) print(f"\nStatus Summary: {status_counts}") print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Filter Pending Change Sets

List only change sets that are ready to execute but haven't been applied yet.

#!/bin/bash
# Find change sets pending execution
aws cloudformation list-change-sets \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`CREATE_COMPLETE` && ExecutionStatus==`AVAILABLE`].[ChangeSetName,Description,CreationTime]' \
  --output table \
  --region us-east-1
-------------------------------------------------------------------- | Pending Change Sets - Ready to Execute | +---------------------------+------------------------+-------------+ | ChangeSetName | Description | Created | +---------------------------+------------------------+-------------+ | upgrade-to-t3-large | Scale EC2 for traffic | 2026-01-17 | | add-cloudwatch-alarms | Production monitoring | 2026-01-17 | | security-hardening | Apply security patches | 2026-01-17 | +---------------------------+------------------------+-------------+ Pending change sets: 3 Action: Review and approve for execution
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Pending change sets view from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class PendingChangeSetsView(View): def get(self, request, stack_name): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') response = cf_client.list_change_sets(StackName=stack_name) change_sets = response.get('Summaries', []) # Filter pending pending = [ cs for cs in change_sets if cs['Status'] == 'CREATE_COMPLETE' and cs.get('ExecutionStatus') == 'AVAILABLE' ] # Track approval skill approval_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Change Approval', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure change review and approval'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get approvers (staff users) approvers = User.objects.filter(is_staff=True) print(f"Approvers: {approvers.count()}") # Get authors who created changes authors = Author.objects.all() print(f"Authors: {authors.count()}") # Approval locations locations = Location.objects.all() print(f"Locations: {locations.count()}") # Related job posts pending_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(job_type='PENDING_APPROVAL') print(f"Pending approval jobs: {pending_jobs.count()}") return JsonResponse({ 'stack_name': stack_name, 'pending_count': len(pending), 'change_sets': [ { 'name': cs['ChangeSetName'], 'description': cs.get('Description', ''), 'created': str(cs['CreationTime']) } for cs in pending ] })
Example 3: Generate Change Sets Report

Export all change sets to JSON for comprehensive audit reporting.

#!/bin/bash
# Generate comprehensive change sets report
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
REPORT_FILE="change-sets-report-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

aws cloudformation list-change-sets \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 > $REPORT_FILE

# Generate summary
echo "Change Sets Report Generated"
echo "File: $REPORT_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Summary:"
cat $REPORT_FILE | jq '
  .Summaries | {
    total: length,
    by_status: group_by(.Status) | map({status: .[0].Status, count: length}),
    oldest: min_by(.CreationTime).CreationTime,
    newest: max_by(.CreationTime).CreationTime
  }'
Change Sets Report Generated File: change-sets-report-20260117.json Summary: { "total": 8, "by_status": [ {"status": "CREATE_COMPLETE", "count": 5}, {"status": "FAILED", "count": 2}, {"status": "DELETE_COMPLETE", "count": 1} ], "oldest": "2026-01-10T08:00:00Z", "newest": "2026-01-17T11:30:00Z" } Report ready for audit review
# anihpj/jobpost/services/change_set_reporter.py import json from datetime import datetime from django.conf import settings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import os class ChangeSetReporter: def __init__(self, stack_name='anihpj-django-app'): self.stack_name = stack_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def generate_report(self): response = self.cf_client.list_change_sets(StackName=self.stack_name) change_sets = response.get('Summaries', []) # Create reporting skills reporting_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure Reporting', defaults={'description': 'CloudFormation change set reporting'} )[0] analytics_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Data Analytics', defaults={'description': 'Change data analysis and insights'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get reporting user reporter = User.objects.get_or_create( username='report_generator', defaults={'email': 'reports@anihpj.com', 'is_active': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author report_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=reporter, defaults={'bio': 'Automated report generation system'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Reports location reports_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Reports Center', state='Analytics', country='Corporate', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Analyze data status_counts = {} for cs in change_sets: status = cs['Status'] status_counts[status] = status_counts.get(status, 0) + 1 # Generate report report = { 'stack_name': self.stack_name, 'generated_at': datetime.now().isoformat(), 'total_change_sets': len(change_sets), 'by_status': status_counts, 'change_sets': [ { 'name': cs['ChangeSetName'], 'status': cs['Status'], 'created': str(cs['CreationTime']), 'description': cs.get('Description', '') } for cs in change_sets ] } # Save report report_dir = os.path.join(settings.BASE_DIR, 'reports') os.makedirs(report_dir, exist_ok=True) filename = f'change-sets-{datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d")}.json' filepath = os.path.join(report_dir, filename) with open(filepath, 'w') as f: json.dump(report, f, indent=2) print(f"\nReport saved: {filename}") # Track report generation report_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Change Sets Report: {self.stack_name}', description=f'{len(change_sets)} change sets documented', author=report_author, location=reports_location, job_type='REPORT', is_active=False ) report_post.required_skills.add(reporting_skill, analytics_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return report
Example 4: Monitor Change Set Age

Identify old change sets that should be cleaned up or executed.

#!/bin/bash
# Find change sets older than 7 days
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
THRESHOLD_DATE=$(date -d '7 days ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

aws cloudformation list-change-sets \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | jq --arg threshold "$THRESHOLD_DATE" '
    .Summaries | 
    map(select(.CreationTime < $threshold)) |
    map({name: .ChangeSetName, age_days: ((now - (.CreationTime | fromdateiso8601)) / 86400 | floor), status: .Status})'
[ { "name": "stale-update-2026-01-05", "age_days": 12, "status": "CREATE_COMPLETE" }, { "name": "abandoned-change-2026-01-08", "age_days": 9, "status": "FAILED" } ] Stale change sets found: 2 Recommendation: Delete or execute these change sets
# anihpj/jobpost/tasks.py - Monitor stale change sets from celery import shared_task from datetime import datetime, timedelta from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 @shared_task def monitor_stale_change_sets(stack_name='anihpj-django-app', max_age_days=7): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') response = cf_client.list_change_sets(StackName=stack_name) change_sets = response.get('Summaries', []) threshold = datetime.now() - timedelta(days=max_age_days) # Find stale change sets stale = [] for cs in change_sets: created = cs['CreationTime'].replace(tzinfo=None) if created < threshold: age_days = (datetime.now() - created).days stale.append({ 'name': cs['ChangeSetName'], 'status': cs['Status'], 'age_days': age_days }) # Create monitoring skill monitoring_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Stale Resource Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Identify and alert on old resources'} )[0] hygiene_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure Hygiene', defaults={'description': 'Cleanup and maintenance practices'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get monitoring user monitor_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='stale_monitor', defaults={'email': 'stale-alerts@anihpj.com'} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author monitor_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=monitor_user, defaults={'bio': 'Stale resource monitoring bot'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Monitoring location monitor_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Monitoring Hub', state='Operations', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Log findings if stale: print(f"\nStale change sets ({len(stale)}):") for s in stale: print(f" - {s['name']}: {s['age_days']} days old ({s['status']})") # Create alert job post alert_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'ALERT: Stale Change Sets in {stack_name}', description=f'{len(stale)} change sets older than {max_age_days} days', author=monitor_author, location=monitor_location, job_type='ALERT', is_active=True ) alert_post.required_skills.add(monitoring_skill, hygiene_skill) print(f"Alert created: JobPost #{alert_post.id}") else: print("No stale change sets found") print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return stale
Example 5: Compare Change Sets Across Environments

List and compare change sets between production and staging stacks.

#!/bin/bash
# Compare change sets across environments
echo "Production Change Sets:"
aws cloudformation list-change-sets \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app-prod \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`CREATE_COMPLETE`].ChangeSetName' \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1

echo ""
echo "Staging Change Sets:"
aws cloudformation list-change-sets \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app-staging \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`CREATE_COMPLETE`].ChangeSetName' \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1

# Find change sets in staging but not in prod
echo ""
echo "Staging-only changes (ready to promote):"
comm -23 \
  <(aws cloudformation list-change-sets --stack-name anihpj-django-app-staging --query 'Summaries[].ChangeSetName' --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sort) \
  <(aws cloudformation list-change-sets --stack-name anihpj-django-app-prod --query 'Summaries[].ChangeSetName' --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sort)
Production Change Sets: ["security-patch-v2", "scale-up-resources"] Staging Change Sets: ["security-patch-v2", "scale-up-resources", "new-feature-deploy", "monitoring-update"] Staging-only changes (ready to promote): monitoring-update new-feature-deploy Promotion candidates: 2 Ready for production deployment review
# anihpj/jobpost/services/environment_change_comparator.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class EnvironmentChangeComparator: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def compare_environments(self, prod_stack, staging_stack): # Get change sets from both environments prod_response = self.cf_client.list_change_sets(StackName=prod_stack) staging_response = self.cf_client.list_change_sets(StackName=staging_stack) prod_cs = {cs['ChangeSetName'] for cs in prod_response.get('Summaries', [])} staging_cs = {cs['ChangeSetName'] for cs in staging_response.get('Summaries', [])} # Find differences only_staging = staging_cs - prod_cs only_prod = prod_cs - staging_cs # Create comparison skill comparison_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Environment Comparison', defaults={'description': 'Multi-environment change tracking'} )[0] promotion_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Change Promotion', defaults={'description': 'Staging to production promotion'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get release manager release_manager = User.objects.get_or_create( username='release_manager', defaults={'email': 'releases@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author rm_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=release_manager, defaults={'bio': 'Release management and promotion'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Production location prod_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Production', state='us-east-1', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") print(f"\nEnvironment Comparison:") print(f"Production change sets: {len(prod_cs)}") print(f"Staging change sets: {len(staging_cs)}") if only_staging: print(f"\nReady for promotion ({len(only_staging)}):") for cs in only_staging: print(f" - {cs}") if only_prod: print(f"\nOnly in production ({len(only_prod)}):") for cs in only_prod: print(f" - {cs}") # Create comparison job post comparison_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Change Set Comparison: {prod_stack} vs {staging_stack}', description=f'Promotion candidates: {len(only_staging)}', author=rm_author, location=prod_location, job_type='RELEASE', is_active=len(only_staging) > 0 ) comparison_post.required_skills.add(comparison_skill, promotion_skill) print(f"\nJobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return { 'promotion_candidates': list(only_staging), 'prod_only': list(only_prod) }

📦 Stack Sets Operations

create-stack-set

Creates a CloudFormation stack set to deploy identical stacks across multiple AWS accounts and regions simultaneously. Use this for deploying your anihpj Django application infrastructure consistently across development, staging, and production environments in different regions for high availability and disaster recovery.

Syntax
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name {name} \
  --template-body file://template.yaml \
  --description {description} \
  --parameters ParameterKey={key},ParameterValue={value} \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --permission-model {SELF_MANAGED|SERVICE_MANAGED} \
  --administration-role-arn {role-arn} \
  --execution-role-name {role-name} \
  --tags Key={key},Value={value}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name (string, required): Unique name for the stack set. Example: anihpj-multi-region-django
  • --template-body (string): Local path to template file using file:// prefix
  • --template-url (string): S3 URL of the template for templates larger than 51,200 bytes
  • --description (string): Description of the stack set purpose
  • --parameters (list): Input parameters as ParameterKey/ParameterValue pairs for all stack instances
  • --capabilities (list): Required capabilities: CAPABILITY_IAM, CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM, CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND
  • --permission-model (string): SELF_MANAGED (default) or SERVICE_MANAGED (AWS Organizations)
  • --administration-role-arn (string): ARN of the IAM role for stack set administration (SELF_MANAGED)
  • --execution-role-name (string): Name of the execution role in target accounts
  • --auto-deployment (structure): Enable/disable auto-deployment for new accounts (SERVICE_MANAGED)
  • --tags (list): Tags to apply to the stack set. Format: Key=Environment,Value=production
Return Value
  • StackSetId (string): Unique identifier for the created stack set in the format: name:guid
5 Detailed CloudFormation create-stack-set Examples
Example 1: Create Multi-Region Django Stack Set

Create a stack set to deploy the anihpj Django application across multiple AWS regions for global availability.

#!/bin/bash
# Create multi-region stack set for Django
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --template-body file://django-regional-infrastructure.yaml \
  --description "Django application for anihpj jobpost - multi-region deployment" \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.medium \
    ParameterKey=DBInstanceClass,ParameterValue=db.t3.micro \
    ParameterKey=Environment,ParameterValue=production \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM \
  --administration-role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/AWSCloudFormationStackSetAdministrationRole \
  --execution-role-name AWSCloudFormationStackSetExecutionRole \
  --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=ManagedBy,Value=StackSet \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Stack set created - ready for regional deployments"
{ "StackSetId": "anihpj-multi-region-django:1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7890-abcd-ef1234567890" } ✓ Stack set created - ready for regional deployments Stack Set Summary: - Name: anihpj-multi-region-django - Permission Model: SELF_MANAGED - Status: ACTIVE - Description: Django application for anihpj jobpost - multi-region deployment Next Steps: 1. Create stack instances in target regions 2. Deploy to: us-east-1, us-west-2, eu-west-1
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/create_stack_set.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Create CloudFormation stack set for multi-region deployment' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--name', type=str, default='anihpj-multi-region-django') parser.add_argument('--template', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_set_name = options['name'] # Read template with open(options['template'], 'r') as f: template_body = f.read() # Create stack set response = cf_client.create_stack_set( StackSetName=stack_set_name, TemplateBody=template_body, Description='Django application for anihpj jobpost - multi-region', Parameters=[ {'ParameterKey': 'InstanceType', 'ParameterValue': 't3.medium'}, {'ParameterKey': 'Environment', 'ParameterValue': 'production'} ], Capabilities=['CAPABILITY_IAM', 'CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM'], Tags=[ {'Key': 'Project', 'Value': 'anihpj'}, {'Key': 'ManagedBy', 'Value': 'StackSet'} ] ) stack_set_id = response['StackSetId'] # Create multi-region skill multi_region_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-Region Deployment', defaults={'description': 'Global infrastructure deployment'} )[0] stack_set_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='CloudFormation StackSets', defaults={'description': 'Cross-account/region deployments'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get deployment engineer deploy_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='multi_region_deployer', defaults={'email': 'deploy@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author deploy_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=deploy_user, defaults={'bio': 'Multi-region deployment engineer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Global location global_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Global', state='Multi-Region', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create deployment job post deployment_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Stack Set Created: {stack_set_name}', description=f'ID: {stack_set_id}', author=deploy_author, location=global_location, job_type='DEPLOYMENT', is_active=True ) deployment_post.required_skills.add(multi_region_skill, stack_set_skill) print(f"\nStack Set ID: {stack_set_id}") print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Create Organization-Wide Security Stack Set

Deploy security baseline configurations across all accounts in your AWS Organization.

#!/bin/bash
# Create organization-wide security stack set
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-org-security-baseline \
  --template-body file://security-baseline.yaml \
  --description "Security baseline for all anihpj accounts" \
  --permission-model SERVICE_MANAGED \
  --auto-deployment Enabled=true,RetainStacksOnAccountRemoval=false \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --call-as DELEGATED_ADMIN \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Organization security stack set created"
{ "StackSetId": "anihpj-org-security-baseline:9i8j7k6l-5m4n-3o2p-1q0r-abcdefghijkl" } ✓ Organization security stack set created Stack Set Configuration: - Name: anihpj-org-security-baseline - Permission Model: SERVICE_MANAGED - Auto-Deployment: ENABLED - Retain on Removal: false Organization Integration: - All member accounts will receive security baseline - New accounts automatically provisioned - Managed by AWS Organizations
# anihpj/jobpost/services/security_stack_set.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class SecurityStackSetService: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def create_org_security_baseline(self, template_path): with open(template_path, 'r') as f: template_body = f.read() response = self.cf_client.create_stack_set( StackSetName='anihpj-org-security-baseline', TemplateBody=template_body, Description='Security baseline for all anihpj accounts', PermissionModel='SERVICE_MANAGED', AutoDeployment={ 'Enabled': True, 'RetainStacksOnAccountRemoval': False }, Capabilities=['CAPABILITY_IAM'], CallAs='DELEGATED_ADMIN' ) stack_set_id = response['StackSetId'] # Create security skills security_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Security Baseline', defaults={'description': 'Organization-wide security configuration'} )[0] org_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='AWS Organizations', defaults={'description': 'Multi-account management'} )[0] compliance_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Security Compliance', defaults={'description': 'Security standards enforcement'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get security admin security_admin = User.objects.get_or_create( username='security_admin', defaults={'email': 'security@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author security_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=security_admin, defaults={'bio': 'Security administration and compliance'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Security operations location security_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Security Operations', state='Organization', country='AWS', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create security job post security_post = JobPost.objects.create( title='Org Security Baseline Deployed', description=f'Stack Set: {stack_set_id}', author=security_author, location=security_location, job_type='SECURITY', is_active=True ) security_post.required_skills.add(security_skill, org_skill, compliance_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return stack_set_id
Example 3: Create Networking Stack Set with Custom Execution Role

Deploy VPC and networking infrastructure across accounts using custom IAM roles.

#!/bin/bash
# Create networking stack set with custom roles
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-network-infrastructure \
  --template-body file://vpc-network.yaml \
  --description "Standardized VPC networking for anihpj Django app" \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=VPCCidr,ParameterValue=10.0.0.0/16 \
    ParameterKey=EnableNATGateway,ParameterValue=true \
  --permission-model SELF_MANAGED \
  --administration-role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/AnihpjStackSetAdminRole \
  --execution-role-name AnihpjStackSetExecutionRole \
  --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=Layer,Value=networking \
  --region us-east-1
{ "StackSetId": "anihpj-network-infrastructure:a1b2c3d4-e5f6-g7h8-i9j0-klmnopqrstuv" } Stack Set Created: - Name: anihpj-network-infrastructure - Admin Role: AnihpjStackSetAdminRole - Execution Role: AnihpjStackSetExecutionRole - VPC CIDR: 10.0.0.0/16 - NAT Gateway: Enabled Network Configuration Ready: - Public subnets for Django web servers - Private subnets for RDS databases - NAT Gateway for outbound internet access
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Network stack set creation API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from django.contrib.auth.decorators import permission_required from django.utils.decorators import method_decorator from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class CreateNetworkStackSetView(View): @method_decorator(permission_required('jobpost.can_deploy')) def post(self, request): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') vpc_cidr = request.POST.get('vpc_cidr', '10.0.0.0/16') enable_nat = request.POST.get('enable_nat', 'true') # Create network skills networking_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='AWS VPC Networking', defaults={'description': 'Virtual Private Cloud architecture'} )[0] iac_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure as Code', defaults={'description': 'Automated infrastructure provisioning'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get network engineer network_user = request.user all_users = User.objects.filter(is_staff=True) print(f"Staff users: {all_users.count()}") # Get author profile author = Author.objects.filter(user=network_user).first() all_authors = Author.objects.all() print(f"Authors: {all_authors.count()}") # Network operations location network_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Network Operations', state='Infrastructure', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create job post for tracking network_post = JobPost.objects.create( title='Network Stack Set: anihpj-network-infrastructure', description=f'VPC CIDR: {vpc_cidr}, NAT: {enable_nat}', author=author, location=network_location, job_type='INFRASTRUCTURE', is_active=True ) network_post.required_skills.add(networking_skill, iac_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({ 'status': 'created', 'stack_set_name': 'anihpj-network-infrastructure', 'job_post_id': network_post.id })
Example 4: Create Database Stack Set with Parameter Overrides

Deploy RDS database infrastructure with different configurations per account.

#!/bin/bash
# Create database stack set
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-database-infrastructure \
  --template-body file://rds-postgresql.yaml \
  --description "PostgreSQL databases for anihpj Django jobpost app" \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=DBInstanceClass,ParameterValue=db.t3.micro \
    ParameterKey=Engine,ParameterValue=postgres \
    ParameterKey=EngineVersion,ParameterValue=15.3 \
    ParameterKey=AllocatedStorage,ParameterValue=20 \
    ParameterKey=MultiAZ,ParameterValue=false \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=Component,Value=database \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Database stack set created"
echo "Note: Use parameter overrides in create-stack-instances for per-account sizing"
{ "StackSetId": "anihpj-database-infrastructure:d4e5f6g7-h8i9-j0k1-l2m3-nopqrstuvwxy" } ✓ Database stack set created Default Parameters: - Instance Class: db.t3.micro - Engine: PostgreSQL 15.3 - Storage: 20 GB - Multi-AZ: false Override Options for Instances: - Production: db.t3.large, 100GB, Multi-AZ: true - Staging: db.t3.small, 50GB, Multi-AZ: false - Development: db.t3.micro, 20GB, Multi-AZ: false
# anihpj/jobpost/services/database_stack_set.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json class DatabaseStackSetService: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def create_database_stack_set(self, template_path, config): with open(template_path, 'r') as f: template_body = f.read() parameters = [ {'ParameterKey': 'DBInstanceClass', 'ParameterValue': config.get('instance_class', 'db.t3.micro')}, {'ParameterKey': 'Engine', 'ParameterValue': 'postgres'}, {'ParameterKey': 'EngineVersion', 'ParameterValue': '15.3'}, {'ParameterKey': 'AllocatedStorage', 'ParameterValue': str(config.get('storage', 20))}, {'ParameterKey': 'MultiAZ', 'ParameterValue': str(config.get('multi_az', False)).lower()} ] response = self.cf_client.create_stack_set( StackSetName='anihpj-database-infrastructure', TemplateBody=template_body, Description='PostgreSQL databases for anihpj Django jobpost app', Parameters=parameters, Capabilities=['CAPABILITY_IAM'], Tags=[ {'Key': 'Project', 'Value': 'anihpj'}, {'Key': 'Component', 'Value': 'database'} ] ) stack_set_id = response['StackSetId'] # Create database skills database_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='AWS RDS', defaults={'description': 'Relational Database Service management'} )[0] postgresql_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='PostgreSQL', defaults={'description': 'PostgreSQL database administration'} )[0] dba_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Database Administration', defaults={'description': 'Database deployment and management'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get DBA user dba_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='database_admin', defaults={'email': 'dba@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author dba_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=dba_user, defaults={'bio': 'Database administrator and architect'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Database location db_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Database Center', state='Data Tier', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create database job post db_post = JobPost.objects.create( title='Database Stack Set: anihpj-database-infrastructure', description=f'PostgreSQL {config.get("instance_class", "db.t3.micro")}', author=dba_author, location=db_location, job_type='DATABASE', is_active=True ) db_post.required_skills.add(database_skill, postgresql_skill, dba_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return stack_set_id
Example 5: Create Stack Set with Managed Execution

Create a stack set with managed execution mode for controlled deployment rollouts.

#!/bin/bash
# Create stack set with managed execution
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-managed-deployment \
  --template-body file://django-full-stack.yaml \
  --description "Managed Django deployment with controlled execution" \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.medium \
    ParameterKey=Environment,ParameterValue=production \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND \
  --permission-model SELF_MANAGED \
  --managed-execution Active=true \
  --administration-role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/StackSetAdminRole \
  --execution-role-name StackSetExecutionRole \
  --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=Deployment,Value=managed \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Managed stack set created"
echo "Operations will be queued and executed serially"
{ "StackSetId": "anihpj-managed-deployment:m1n2o3p4-q5r6-s7t8-u9v0-wxyzabcdefgh" } ✓ Managed stack set created Managed Execution Configuration: - Active: true - Operations: Queued and executed serially - Benefit: Prevents concurrent operations conflicts Stack Set Features: - CAPABILITY_IAM: Can create IAM resources - CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM: Can create named IAM resources - CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND: Can use macros and transforms Ready for controlled deployment rollout
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/create_managed_stack_set.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Create managed execution stack set for controlled deployments' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') # Create managed execution skills managed_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Managed Execution', defaults={'description': 'Controlled stack set operations'} )[0] deployment_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Deployment Management', defaults={'description': 'Orchestrated infrastructure deployment'} )[0] governance_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Cloud Governance', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure governance and controls'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get deployment manager deploy_manager = User.objects.get_or_create( username='deployment_manager', defaults={ 'email': 'deployments@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True, 'is_superuser': False } )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author manager_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=deploy_manager, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure deployment manager'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Deployment management location deploy_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Deployment Center', state='Operations', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Simulate stack set creation print("\nCreating managed stack set...") print("- Name: anihpj-managed-deployment") print("- Managed Execution: Active") print("- Capabilities: IAM, NAMED_IAM, AUTO_EXPAND") # Create governance job post governance_post = JobPost.objects.create( title='Managed Stack Set: anihpj-managed-deployment', description='Controlled execution mode enabled', author=manager_author, location=deploy_location, job_type='GOVERNANCE', is_active=True ) governance_post.required_skills.add(managed_skill, deployment_skill, governance_skill) print(f"\nJobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") print(f"Stack set tracked as JobPost #{governance_post.id}") # List all related job posts stack_set_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains='Stack Set') print(f"Total Stack Set jobs: {stack_set_jobs.count()}")

update-stack-set

Updates an existing CloudFormation stack set with a new template, parameters, or operational configurations. Changes are propagated to all stack instances across accounts and regions based on the specified operation preferences, enabling consistent updates to your anihpj Django infrastructure globally.

Syntax
aws cloudformation update-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name {name} \
  --template-body file://template.yaml \
  --parameters ParameterKey={key},ParameterValue={value} \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --operation-preferences RegionConcurrencyType={type},MaxConcurrentPercentage={pct} \
  --use-previous-template \
  --accounts {account-ids} \
  --regions {region-list}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name (string, required): Name or ID of the stack set to update. Example: anihpj-multi-region-django
  • --template-body (string): New template content using file:// prefix for local files
  • --template-url (string): S3 URL of the new template
  • --use-previous-template (flag): Use existing template, only update parameters
  • --parameters (list): Updated parameters as ParameterKey/ParameterValue pairs
  • --capabilities (list): IAM capabilities: CAPABILITY_IAM, CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM, CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND
  • --operation-preferences (structure): Control update rollout:
    • RegionConcurrencyType: SEQUENTIAL or PARALLEL
    • MaxConcurrentCount: Max accounts updated simultaneously
    • MaxConcurrentPercentage: Max percentage updated simultaneously
    • FailureToleranceCount: Max failures before stopping
    • FailureTolerancePercentage: Max failure percentage before stopping
  • --accounts (list): Specific accounts to update (SELF_MANAGED)
  • --regions (list): Specific regions to update
  • --tags (list): Updated tags for the stack set
Return Value
  • OperationId (string): Unique identifier for the update operation to track progress
5 Detailed CloudFormation update-stack-set Examples
Example 1: Rolling Update Across All Regions

Update the Django stack set with a new template using a rolling deployment strategy.

#!/bin/bash
# Rolling update with controlled concurrency
aws cloudformation update-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --template-body file://django-infrastructure-v2.yaml \
  --description "Django app v2.0 - Performance improvements" \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.medium \
    ParameterKey=Environment,ParameterValue=production \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM \
  --operation-preferences \
    RegionConcurrencyType=SEQUENTIAL,\
    MaxConcurrentPercentage=25,\
    FailureTolerancePercentage=10 \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Rolling update initiated"
{ "OperationId": "abc12345-de67-89fg-hijk-lmnopqrstuvw" } ✓ Rolling update initiated Update Strategy: - Region Concurrency: SEQUENTIAL (one region at a time) - Max Concurrent: 25% of instances per region - Failure Tolerance: 10% Deployment Order: 1. us-east-1 (3 instances) 2. us-west-2 (3 instances) 3. eu-west-1 (3 instances) Estimated completion: 45 minutes
# anihpj/jobpost/services/stack_set_updater.py from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class StackSetUpdater: def __init__(self, stack_set_name='anihpj-multi-region-django'): self.stack_set_name = stack_set_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def rolling_update(self, template_path, parameters): with open(template_path, 'r') as f: template_body = f.read() cf_params = [ {'ParameterKey': k, 'ParameterValue': v} for k, v in parameters.items() ] response = self.cf_client.update_stack_set( StackSetName=self.stack_set_name, TemplateBody=template_body, Parameters=cf_params, Capabilities=['CAPABILITY_IAM', 'CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM'], OperationPreferences={ 'RegionConcurrencyType': 'SEQUENTIAL', 'MaxConcurrentPercentage': 25, 'FailureTolerancePercentage': 10 } ) operation_id = response['OperationId'] #
