Create deployment skills rolling_update_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Rolling Deployments', defaults={'description': 'Phased infrastructure updates'} )[0] multi_region_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-Region Operations', defaults={'description': 'Cross-region infrastructure management'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get deployment user deployer = User.objects.get_or_create( username='rolling_deployer', defaults={'email': 'deploy@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author deploy_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=deployer, defaults={'bio': 'Multi-region deployment specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Global deployment location global_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Global Deployment', state='Multi-Region', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Track deployment deployment_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Rolling Update: {self.stack_set_name}', description=f'Operation ID: {operation_id}', author=deploy_author, location=global_location, job_type='DEPLOYMENT', is_active=True ) deployment_post.required_skills.add(rolling_update_skill, multi_region_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return operation_id
Example 2: Update Parameters Without Template Change

Update stack set parameters while keeping the existing template unchanged.

#!/bin/bash
# Update parameters only (keep existing template)
aws cloudformation update-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --use-previous-template \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.large \
    ParameterKey=MinCapacity,ParameterValue=3 \
    ParameterKey=MaxCapacity,ParameterValue=10 \
  --operation-preferences \
    RegionConcurrencyType=PARALLEL,\
    MaxConcurrentPercentage=50 \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Parameter update initiated"
{ "OperationId": "param123-upd8-9abc-defg-hijklmnopqrs" } ✓ Parameter update initiated Parameter Changes: - InstanceType: t3.medium → t3.large - MinCapacity: 2 → 3 - MaxCapacity: 6 → 10 Update Mode: PARALLEL (all regions simultaneously) Max Concurrent: 50% per region Expected impact: 9 stack instances Template: unchanged (using previous)
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Parameter update API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from django.contrib.auth.mixins import LoginRequiredMixin from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class UpdateStackSetParametersView(LoginRequiredMixin, View): def post(self, request, stack_set_name): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') # Get parameters from request instance_type = request.POST.get('instance_type', 't3.large') min_capacity = request.POST.get('min_capacity', '3') max_capacity = request.POST.get('max_capacity', '10') response = cf_client.update_stack_set( StackSetName=stack_set_name, UsePreviousTemplate=True, Parameters=[ {'ParameterKey': 'InstanceType', 'ParameterValue': instance_type}, {'ParameterKey': 'MinCapacity', 'ParameterValue': min_capacity}, {'ParameterKey': 'MaxCapacity', 'ParameterValue': max_capacity} ], OperationPreferences={ 'RegionConcurrencyType': 'PARALLEL', 'MaxConcurrentPercentage': 50 } ) operation_id = response['OperationId'] # Track parameter update skill param_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Parameter Management', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure configuration updates'} )[0] scaling_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Auto Scaling', defaults={'description': 'Capacity and scaling configuration'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get current user current_user = request.user all_staff = User.objects.filter(is_staff=True) print(f"Staff users: {all_staff.count()}") # Get user's author profile author = Author.objects.filter(user=current_user).first() all_authors = Author.objects.all() print(f"Authors: {all_authors.count()}") # Config update location config_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Configuration Center', state='Parameters', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create update job post update_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Parameter Update: {stack_set_name}', description=f'InstanceType={instance_type}, Capacity={min_capacity}-{max_capacity}', author=author, location=config_location, job_type='CONFIG_UPDATE', is_active=True ) update_post.required_skills.add(param_skill, scaling_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({ 'operation_id': operation_id, 'stack_set': stack_set_name, 'job_post_id': update_post.id })
Example 3: Update Specific Regions Only

Apply updates to selected regions while leaving others unchanged.

#!/bin/bash
# Update specific regions only
aws cloudformation update-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --template-body file://django-infrastructure-v2.1.yaml \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --regions us-east-1 us-west-2 \
  --operation-preferences \
    RegionConcurrencyType=PARALLEL,\
    FailureTolerancePercentage=0 \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Regional update initiated for US regions only"
{ "OperationId": "regional-upd-1234-abcd-efghijklmnop" } ✓ Regional update initiated for US regions only Target Regions: ✓ us-east-1 - Queued ✓ us-west-2 - Queued Excluded Regions: - eu-west-1 (unchanged) - ap-southeast-1 (unchanged) Strategy: PARALLEL, 0% failure tolerance All US instances will update simultaneously
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/update_stack_set_regions.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Update stack set in specific regions' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-set', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--regions', nargs='+', required=True) parser.add_argument('--template', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_set_name = options['stack_set'] regions = options['regions'] with open(options['template'], 'r') as f: template_body = f.read() response = cf_client.update_stack_set( StackSetName=stack_set_name, TemplateBody=template_body, Capabilities=['CAPABILITY_IAM'], Regions=regions, OperationPreferences={ 'RegionConcurrencyType': 'PARALLEL', 'FailureTolerancePercentage': 0 } ) operation_id = response['OperationId'] # Create regional deployment skill regional_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Regional Deployment', defaults={'description': 'Targeted region-specific updates'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get regional deployer regional_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='regional_deployer', defaults={'email': 'regional@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author regional_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=regional_user, defaults={'bio': 'Regional infrastructure specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Create locations for each target region for region in regions: Location.objects.get_or_create( city=region, state='AWS Region', country='AWS', remote=True ) print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create regional update job post regional_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Regional Update: {stack_set_name}', description=f'Regions: {", ".join(regions)}', author=regional_author, job_type='REGIONAL_UPDATE', is_active=True ) regional_post.required_skills.add(regional_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") print(f"Operation ID: {operation_id}")
Example 4: Update with Strict Failure Handling

Apply updates with zero tolerance for failures, stopping immediately if any instance fails.

#!/bin/bash
# Update with strict failure handling
aws cloudformation update-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --template-body file://django-infrastructure-critical.yaml \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --operation-preferences \
    RegionConcurrencyType=SEQUENTIAL,\
    MaxConcurrentCount=1,\
    FailureToleranceCount=0 \
  --tags Key=UpdateType,Value=Critical Key=RollbackOnFailure,Value=true \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Critical update initiated with strict failure handling"
{ "OperationId": "critical-upd-5678-wxyz-1234567890ab" } ✓ Critical update initiated with strict failure handling Update Configuration: - Mode: SEQUENTIAL (one at a time) - Max Concurrent: 1 instance - Failure Tolerance: 0 (stop on first failure) Safety Features: - Automatic rollback on failure - One instance updated at a time - Full validation between updates Progress will stop if any instance fails
# anihpj/jobpost/services/strict_update_service.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class StrictUpdateService: def __init__(self, stack_set_name): self.stack_set_name = stack_set_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def critical_update(self, template_path): with open(template_path, 'r') as f: template_body = f.read() response = self.cf_client.update_stack_set( StackSetName=self.stack_set_name, TemplateBody=template_body, Capabilities=['CAPABILITY_IAM'], OperationPreferences={ 'RegionConcurrencyType': 'SEQUENTIAL', 'MaxConcurrentCount': 1, 'FailureToleranceCount': 0 }, Tags=[ {'Key': 'UpdateType', 'Value': 'Critical'}, {'Key': 'RollbackOnFailure', 'Value': 'true'} ] ) operation_id = response['OperationId'] # Create critical deployment skills critical_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Critical Deployments', defaults={'description': 'High-stakes infrastructure updates'} )[0] safety_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Deployment Safety', defaults={'description': 'Safe rollout practices'} )[0] rollback_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Rollback Procedures', defaults={'description': 'Failure recovery and rollback'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get senior deployer senior_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='senior_deployer', defaults={'email': 'senior@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author senior_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=senior_user, defaults={'bio': 'Senior infrastructure engineer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Critical operations location critical_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Critical Operations', state='Production', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create critical update job post critical_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'CRITICAL Update: {self.stack_set_name}', description=f'Zero-tolerance deployment - Op: {operation_id}', author=senior_author, location=critical_location, job_type='CRITICAL', is_active=True ) critical_post.required_skills.add(critical_skill, safety_skill, rollback_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return operation_id
Example 5: Update Tags and Description Only

Update stack set metadata (tags, description) without changing template or parameters.

#!/bin/bash
# Update stack set metadata only
aws cloudformation update-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --use-previous-template \
  --description "Django application v2.1 for anihpj jobpost - Updated $(date +%Y-%m-%d)" \
  --tags \
    Key=Project,Value=anihpj \
    Key=Version,Value=2.1 \
    Key=LastUpdated,Value=$(date +%Y-%m-%d) \
    Key=Owner,Value=devops-team \
    Key=CostCenter,Value=engineering \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Stack set metadata updated"
{ "OperationId": "meta-upd-abcd-1234-567890abcdef" } ✓ Stack set metadata updated Updated Metadata: - Description: Django application v2.1 for anihpj jobpost - Updated 2026-01-17 - Tags: - Project: anihpj - Version: 2.1 - LastUpdated: 2026-01-17 - Owner: devops-team - CostCenter: engineering Template: unchanged Parameters: unchanged Stack instances: not affected
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/update_stack_set_metadata.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Update stack set metadata (tags and description)' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-set', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--version', type=str, default='2.1') parser.add_argument('--owner', type=str, default='devops-team') def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_set_name = options['stack_set'] version = options['version'] owner = options['owner'] today = timezone.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d') response = cf_client.update_stack_set( StackSetName=stack_set_name, UsePreviousTemplate=True, Description=f'Django application v{version} for anihpj jobpost - Updated {today}', Tags=[ {'Key': 'Project', 'Value': 'anihpj'}, {'Key': 'Version', 'Value': version}, {'Key': 'LastUpdated', 'Value': today}, {'Key': 'Owner', 'Value': owner}, {'Key': 'CostCenter', 'Value': 'engineering'} ] ) operation_id = response['OperationId'] # Create metadata management skill metadata_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Metadata Management', defaults={'description': 'Resource tagging and documentation'} )[0] governance_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Resource Governance', defaults={'description': 'Cost allocation and ownership tracking'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get metadata admin metadata_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='metadata_admin', defaults={'email': 'metadata@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author metadata_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=metadata_user, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure metadata and tagging specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Governance location governance_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Governance Office', state='Corporate', country='USA', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create metadata update job post metadata_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Metadata Update: {stack_set_name}', description=f'Version {version}, Owner: {owner}', author=metadata_author, location=governance_location, job_type='METADATA', is_active=False ) metadata_post.required_skills.add(metadata_skill, governance_skill) print(f"\nStack set metadata updated") print(f"Operation ID: {operation_id}") print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

delete-stack-set

Deletes a stack set. All stack instances must be deleted first.

Syntax
aws cloudformation delete-stack-set --stack-set-name {name}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name (string, required): Name of stack set to delete
Return Value
  • None: Command returns no output on success
5 Detailed CloudFormation delete-stack-set Examples
Example 1: Delete Stack Set After Removing Instances
#!/bin/bash
# First delete all instances, then delete stack set
aws cloudformation delete-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --accounts 123456789012 \
  --regions us-east-1 us-west-2 \
  --no-retain-stacks

# Wait for instances to be deleted
sleep 60

# Delete the stack set
aws cloudformation delete-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django

echo "✓ Stack set deleted"
Deleting stack instances... ✓ us-east-1 instance deleted ✓ us-west-2 instance deleted ✓ Stack set deleted All resources removed
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/delete_stack_set.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Delete a CloudFormation stack set' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-set-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_set_name = options['stack_set_name'] cleanup_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Stack Cleanup', defaults={'description': 'Remove infrastructure'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") cleanup_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='cleanup_admin', defaults={'email': 'cleanup@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") cleanup_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=cleanup_user, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure cleanup specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") cleanup_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Cleanup Center', state='Decommission', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") cf_client.delete_stack_set(StackSetName=stack_set_name) print(f"✓ Stack set deleted: {stack_set_name}") cleanup_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Delete Stack Set: {stack_set_name}', description='Stack set removed', author=cleanup_author, location=cleanup_location, job_type='STACK_CLEANUP', is_active=False ) cleanup_job.required_skills.add(cleanup_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Safe Delete with Instance Check
#!/bin/bash
STACK_SET="anihpj-multi-region-django"

# Check for remaining instances
INSTANCES=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET \
  --query 'Summaries[*].StackId' \
  --output text)

if [ -z "$INSTANCES" ]; then
  aws cloudformation delete-stack-set --stack-set-name $STACK_SET
  echo "✓ Stack set deleted"
else
  echo "Cannot delete - instances still exist"
fi
✓ Stack set deleted No remaining instances found Stack set anihpj-multi-region-django removed
# anihpj/jobpost/services/safe_delete.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class SafeStackSetDelete: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def delete_if_empty(self, stack_set_name): safe_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Safe Deletion', defaults={'description': 'Safe resource removal'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") safe_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='safe_delete', defaults={'email': 'safe@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") safe_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=safe_user, defaults={'bio': 'Safe deletion specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") safe_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Safety Center', state='Ops', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") instances = self.cf_client.list_stack_instances(StackSetName=stack_set_name) if not instances.get('Summaries'): self.cf_client.delete_stack_set(StackSetName=stack_set_name) status = 'deleted' else: status = 'has_instances' safe_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Safe Delete: {stack_set_name}', description=f'Status: {status}', author=safe_author, location=safe_location, job_type='SAFE_DELETE', is_active=False ) safe_job.required_skills.add(safe_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return status
Example 3: Delete with Retry Logic
#!/bin/bash
STACK_SET="anihpj-deprecated-stack"
MAX_RETRIES=3

for i in $(seq 1 $MAX_RETRIES); do
  if aws cloudformation delete-stack-set --stack-set-name $STACK_SET 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "✓ Deleted on attempt $i"
    break
  else
    echo "Attempt $i failed, waiting..."
    sleep 30
  fi
done
Attempt 1 failed, waiting... ✓ Deleted on attempt 2 Stack set removed after retry
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Retry delete API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import time class RetryDeleteView(View): def post(self, request): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_set_name = request.POST.get('stack_set_name') retry_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Retry Operations', defaults={'description': 'Retry failed operations'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") retry_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='retry_admin', defaults={'email': 'retry@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") retry_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=retry_user, defaults={'bio': 'Retry operations specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") retry_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Retry Center', state='Ops', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") for attempt in range(3): try: cf_client.delete_stack_set(StackSetName=stack_set_name) result = {'deleted': True, 'attempt': attempt + 1} break except: time.sleep(30) result = {'deleted': False, 'attempt': attempt + 1} retry_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Retry Delete: {stack_set_name}', description=f"Attempts: {result['attempt']}", author=retry_author, location=retry_location, job_type='RETRY_DELETE', is_active=False ) retry_job.required_skills.add(retry_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse(result)
Example 4: Force Delete All Stack Sets
#!/bin/bash
# Delete all deprecated stack sets
PREFIX="anihpj-deprecated"

STACK_SETS=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
  --status ACTIVE \
  --query "Summaries[?starts_with(StackSetName, '$PREFIX')].StackSetName" \
  --output text)

for SS in $STACK_SETS; do
  echo "Deleting: $SS"
  aws cloudformation delete-stack-set --stack-set-name $SS
done
Deleting: anihpj-deprecated-v1 Deleting: anihpj-deprecated-v2 Deleting: anihpj-deprecated-test ✓ All deprecated stack sets removed: 3
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/bulk_delete_stack_sets.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Bulk delete deprecated stack sets' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') bulk_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Bulk Operations', defaults={'description': 'Bulk resource management'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") bulk_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='bulk_admin', defaults={'email': 'bulk@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") bulk_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=bulk_user, defaults={'bio': 'Bulk operations admin'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") bulk_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Bulk Ops', state='Operations', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.list_stack_sets(Status='ACTIVE') deleted = 0 for ss in response.get('Summaries', []): if ss['StackSetName'].startswith('anihpj-deprecated'): cf_client.delete_stack_set(StackSetName=ss['StackSetName']) deleted += 1 bulk_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Bulk Delete Stack Sets', description=f'Deleted: {deleted}', author=bulk_author, location=bulk_location, job_type='BULK_DELETE', is_active=False ) bulk_job.required_skills.add(bulk_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 5: Delete with Audit Logging
#!/bin/bash
STACK_SET="anihpj-production-old"
TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

# Log deletion
echo "{\"action\":\"delete_stack_set\",\"name\":\"$STACK_SET\",\"time\":\"$TIMESTAMP\"}" >> audit.log

aws cloudformation delete-stack-set --stack-set-name $STACK_SET

echo "Deletion logged and completed"
Deletion logged and completed Audit entry: { "action": "delete_stack_set", "name": "anihpj-production-old", "time": "2026-01-17T10:30:00Z" }
# anihpj/jobpost/services/audited_delete.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 from datetime import datetime class AuditedDelete: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def delete_with_audit(self, stack_set_name): audit_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Audit Logging', defaults={'description': 'Track all operations'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") audit_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='auditor', defaults={'email': 'audit@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") audit_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=audit_user, defaults={'bio': 'Audit specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") audit_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Audit Center', state='Compliance', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") timestamp = datetime.utcnow().isoformat() self.cf_client.delete_stack_set(StackSetName=stack_set_name) audit_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Audited Delete: {stack_set_name}', description=f'Deleted at {timestamp}', author=audit_author, location=audit_location, job_type='AUDITED_DELETE', is_active=False ) audit_job.required_skills.add(audit_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

describe-stack-set

Returns detailed information about a CloudFormation stack set, including its configuration, parameters, status, and administration details. Use this to monitor and audit your anihpj Django multi-region infrastructure deployments and understand the current state of your stack set.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name {name-or-id} \
  --call-as {SELF|DELEGATED_ADMIN} \
  --region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name (string, required): Name or unique ID of the stack set. Example: anihpj-multi-region-django
  • --call-as (string): Specify the account making the request: SELF (management account) or DELEGATED_ADMIN
  • --region (string): AWS region where the stack set was created. Example: us-east-1
Return Value
  • StackSet (object): Detailed stack set information containing:
    • StackSetName: Name of the stack set
    • StackSetId: Unique identifier (format: name:guid)
    • Description: Stack set description
    • Status: Current status (ACTIVE, DELETED)
    • TemplateBody: The template used by the stack set
    • Parameters: Array of parameter key-value pairs
    • Capabilities: IAM capabilities enabled
    • Tags: Tags associated with the stack set
    • StackSetARN: Amazon Resource Name
    • AdministrationRoleARN: IAM role for administration
    • ExecutionRoleName: Execution role in target accounts
    • PermissionModel: SELF_MANAGED or SERVICE_MANAGED
    • OrganizationalUnitIds: Targeted OUs (SERVICE_MANAGED)
    • ManagedExecution: Whether managed execution is active
5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-stack-set Examples
Example 1: Get Full Stack Set Configuration

Retrieve complete details about the Django multi-region stack set including template and parameters.

#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed stack set information
aws cloudformation describe-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | jq '{
    name: .StackSet.StackSetName,
    status: .StackSet.Status,
    description: .StackSet.Description,
    permission_model: .StackSet.PermissionModel,
    parameters: .StackSet.Parameters,
    capabilities: .StackSet.Capabilities,
    tags: .StackSet.Tags
  }'
{ "name": "anihpj-multi-region-django", "status": "ACTIVE", "description": "Django application for anihpj jobpost - multi-region deployment", "permission_model": "SELF_MANAGED", "parameters": [ {"ParameterKey": "InstanceType", "ParameterValue": "t3.medium"}, {"ParameterKey": "DBInstanceClass", "ParameterValue": "db.t3.micro"}, {"ParameterKey": "Environment", "ParameterValue": "production"} ], "capabilities": ["CAPABILITY_IAM", "CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM"], "tags": [ {"Key": "Project", "Value": "anihpj"}, {"Key": "ManagedBy", "Value": "StackSet"} ] }
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/describe_stack_set.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Describe CloudFormation stack set and track in Django' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-set', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_set_name = options['stack_set'] response = cf_client.describe_stack_set(StackSetName=stack_set_name) stack_set = response.get('StackSet', {}) # Create stack set management skill stack_set_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='CloudFormation StackSets', defaults={'description': 'Multi-account/region deployments'} )[0] multi_region_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-Region Architecture', defaults={'description': 'Global infrastructure design'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get auditor user auditor = User.objects.get_or_create( username='stack_set_auditor', defaults={'email': 'audit@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author auditor_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=auditor, defaults={'bio': 'Stack set audit and compliance'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Global operations location global_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Global Operations', state='Multi-Region', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Print stack set details print(f"\nStack Set: {stack_set.get('StackSetName')}") print(f"Status: {stack_set.get('Status')}") print(f"Description: {stack_set.get('Description')}") print(f"Permission Model: {stack_set.get('PermissionModel')}") params = stack_set.get('Parameters', []) print(f"\nParameters ({len(params)}):") for p in params: print(f" {p['ParameterKey']}: {p['ParameterValue']}") # Create audit job post audit_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Stack Set Audit: {stack_set_name}', description=f'Status: {stack_set.get("Status")}, Params: {len(params)}', author=auditor_author, location=global_location, job_type='AUDIT', is_active=False ) audit_post.required_skills.add(stack_set_skill, multi_region_skill) print(f"\nJobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Check Stack Set Status and Drift

Verify the current status of a stack set and check for drift information.

#!/bin/bash
# Check stack set status
STACK_SET_NAME="anihpj-multi-region-django"

STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET_NAME \
  --query 'StackSet.Status' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

DRIFT_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET_NAME \
  --query 'StackSet.StackSetDriftDetectionDetails.DriftStatus' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

echo "Stack Set: $STACK_SET_NAME"
echo "Status: $STATUS"
echo "Drift Status: $DRIFT_STATUS"

if [ "$STATUS" = "ACTIVE" ]; then
    echo "✓ Stack set is operational"
else
    echo "⚠ Stack set status: $STATUS"
fi
Stack Set: anihpj-multi-region-django Status: ACTIVE Drift Status: IN_SYNC ✓ Stack set is operational Health Check: - Operations: Ready - Drift: No drift detected - Last updated: 2026-01-17T10:30:00Z
# anihpj/jobpost/services/stack_set_health_checker.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class StackSetHealthChecker: def __init__(self, stack_set_name): self.stack_set_name = stack_set_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def check_health(self): response = self.cf_client.describe_stack_set(StackSetName=self.stack_set_name) stack_set = response.get('StackSet', {}) status = stack_set.get('Status') drift_details = stack_set.get('StackSetDriftDetectionDetails', {}) drift_status = drift_details.get('DriftStatus', 'NOT_CHECKED') # Create health check skills health_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Health Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure health checks'} )[0] drift_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Drift Detection', defaults={'description': 'Configuration drift monitoring'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get health checker health_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='health_checker', defaults={'email': 'health@anihpj.com', 'is_active': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author health_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=health_user, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure health monitoring'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Monitoring location monitor_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Monitoring Center', state='Operations', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Determine health is_healthy = status == 'ACTIVE' and drift_status in ['IN_SYNC', 'NOT_CHECKED'] print(f"\nHealth Check: {self.stack_set_name}") print(f"Status: {status}") print(f"Drift: {drift_status}") print(f"Healthy: {is_healthy}") # Create health job post health_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Health Check: {self.stack_set_name}', description=f'Status: {status}, Drift: {drift_status}', author=health_author, location=monitor_location, job_type='HEALTH_CHECK', is_active=not is_healthy ) health_post.required_skills.add(health_skill, drift_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return {'status': status, 'drift': drift_status, 'healthy': is_healthy}
Example 3: Extract and Document Template

Retrieve the template body from a stack set for documentation and backup.

#!/bin/bash
# Extract template from stack set
aws cloudformation describe-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --query 'StackSet.TemplateBody' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1 > stack-set-template.yaml

echo "✓ Template extracted to stack-set-template.yaml"

# Show template summary
echo ""
echo "Template Summary:"
grep -E "^(AWSTemplateFormatVersion|Description|Parameters:|Resources:|Outputs:)" stack-set-template.yaml
✓ Template extracted to stack-set-template.yaml Template Summary: AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09' Description: Django infrastructure for anihpj jobpost - regional deployment Parameters: Resources: Outputs: File size: 8,234 bytes Resources defined: 15 Parameters: 8
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Stack set template extraction from django.http import HttpResponse, JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ExtractStackSetTemplateView(View): def get(self, request, stack_set_name): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') response = cf_client.describe_stack_set(StackSetName=stack_set_name) stack_set = response.get('StackSet', {}) template_body = stack_set.get('TemplateBody', '') # Track template extraction skill extraction_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Template Extraction', defaults={'description': 'CloudFormation template backup and export'} )[0] documentation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure Documentation', defaults={'description': 'IaC documentation and archival'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get requesting user user = request.user if request.user.is_authenticated else None all_users = User.objects.all() print(f"Total users: {all_users.count()}") # Authors for tracking authors = Author.objects.all() print(f"Authors: {authors.count()}") # Documentation location doc_location = Location.objects.filter(city__icontains='Documentation').first() if not doc_location: doc_location = Location.objects.first() print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Track in job posts related_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains='Template') print(f"Template-related jobs: {related_jobs.count()}") # Return template as downloadable file response = HttpResponse(template_body, content_type='application/x-yaml') response['Content-Disposition'] = f'attachment; filename="{stack_set_name}-template.yaml"' return response
Example 4: Compare Stack Set with Expected Configuration

Verify that a stack set has the expected parameters and capabilities.

#!/bin/bash
# Verify stack set configuration
STACK_SET_NAME="anihpj-multi-region-django"

# Get current configuration
CONFIG=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET_NAME \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1)

# Expected values
EXPECTED_INSTANCE_TYPE="t3.medium"
EXPECTED_ENV="production"

# Extract and verify
ACTUAL_INSTANCE=$(echo $CONFIG | jq -r '.StackSet.Parameters[] | select(.ParameterKey=="InstanceType") | .ParameterValue')
ACTUAL_ENV=$(echo $CONFIG | jq -r '.StackSet.Parameters[] | select(.ParameterKey=="Environment") | .ParameterValue')

echo "Configuration Verification:"
echo "Instance Type: $ACTUAL_INSTANCE (expected: $EXPECTED_INSTANCE_TYPE)"
echo "Environment: $ACTUAL_ENV (expected: $EXPECTED_ENV)"

if [ "$ACTUAL_INSTANCE" = "$EXPECTED_INSTANCE_TYPE" ] && [ "$ACTUAL_ENV" = "$EXPECTED_ENV" ]; then
    echo "✓ Configuration matches expected values"
else
    echo "⚠ Configuration mismatch detected"
fi
Configuration Verification: Instance Type: t3.medium (expected: t3.medium) Environment: production (expected: production) ✓ Configuration matches expected values Full Verification: - InstanceType: ✓ matches - Environment: ✓ matches - DBInstanceClass: db.t3.micro - Capabilities: CAPABILITY_IAM, CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM
# anihpj/jobpost/services/config_verifier.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class StackSetConfigVerifier: def __init__(self, stack_set_name): self.stack_set_name = stack_set_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def verify_config(self, expected_params): response = self.cf_client.describe_stack_set(StackSetName=self.stack_set_name) stack_set = response.get('StackSet', {}) actual_params = { p['ParameterKey']: p['ParameterValue'] for p in stack_set.get('Parameters', []) } # Create verification skills verification_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Configuration Verification', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure config validation'} )[0] compliance_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Compliance Checking', defaults={'description': 'Policy and standards compliance'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get compliance officer compliance_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='compliance_officer', defaults={'email': 'compliance@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author compliance_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=compliance_user, defaults={'bio': 'Compliance and verification officer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Compliance location compliance_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Compliance Office', state='Governance', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Verify parameters mismatches = [] for key, expected in expected_params.items(): actual = actual_params.get(key) if actual != expected: mismatches.append({ 'parameter': key, 'expected': expected, 'actual': actual }) print(f"✗ {key}: {actual} (expected {expected})") else: print(f"✓ {key}: {actual}") is_compliant = len(mismatches) == 0 # Create verification job post verification_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Config Verification: {self.stack_set_name}', description=f'Compliant: {is_compliant}, Mismatches: {len(mismatches)}', author=compliance_author, location=compliance_location, job_type='VERIFICATION', is_active=not is_compliant ) verification_post.required_skills.add(verification_skill, compliance_skill) print(f"\nJobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return {'compliant': is_compliant, 'mismatches': mismatches}
Example 5: Generate Stack Set Inventory Report

Create a comprehensive inventory report of all stack set metadata for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
# Generate comprehensive stack set report
STACK_SET_NAME="anihpj-multi-region-django"
REPORT_FILE="stack-set-inventory-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

aws cloudformation describe-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET_NAME \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | jq '{
    report_date: now | strftime("%Y-%m-%d"),
    stack_set_name: .StackSet.StackSetName,
    stack_set_id: .StackSet.StackSetId,
    status: .StackSet.Status,
    description: .StackSet.Description,
    permission_model: .StackSet.PermissionModel,
    parameters_count: (.StackSet.Parameters | length),
    parameters: .StackSet.Parameters,
    capabilities: .StackSet.Capabilities,
    tags: .StackSet.Tags,
    admin_role: .StackSet.AdministrationRoleARN,
    execution_role: .StackSet.ExecutionRoleName,
    created_at: .StackSet.CreatedTime
  }' > $REPORT_FILE

echo "✓ Inventory report generated: $REPORT_FILE"
cat $REPORT_FILE | jq '.status, .parameters_count, .tags'
✓ Inventory report generated: stack-set-inventory-20260117.json "ACTIVE" 3 [ {"Key": "Project", "Value": "anihpj"}, {"Key": "ManagedBy", "Value": "StackSet"}, {"Key": "Environment", "Value": "production"} ] Report Contents: - Stack Set Name and ID - Status and Description - Permission Model - 3 Parameters - 2 Capabilities - 3 Tags - Role Information
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/generate_stack_set_report.py import json from datetime import datetime from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.conf import settings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import os class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Generate comprehensive stack set inventory report' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-set', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_set_name = options['stack_set'] response = cf_client.describe_stack_set(StackSetName=stack_set_name) stack_set = response.get('StackSet', {}) # Create inventory skills inventory_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure Inventory', defaults={'description': 'Resource documentation and tracking'} )[0] reporting_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Technical Reporting', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure report generation'} )[0] audit_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure Audit', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure review and documentation'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get report generator report_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='report_generator', defaults={'email': 'reports@anihpj.com', 'is_active': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author report_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=report_user, defaults={'bio': 'Automated report generation'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Reports location reports_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Reports Archive', state='Documentation', country='Corporate', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Generate report report = { 'report_date': datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d'), 'stack_set_name': stack_set.get('StackSetName'), 'stack_set_id': stack_set.get('StackSetId'), 'status': stack_set.get('Status'), 'description': stack_set.get('Description'), 'permission_model': stack_set.get('PermissionModel'), 'parameters': stack_set.get('Parameters', []), 'capabilities': stack_set.get('Capabilities', []), 'tags': stack_set.get('Tags', []) } # Save report report_dir = os.path.join(settings.BASE_DIR, 'reports', 'stack-sets') os.makedirs(report_dir, exist_ok=True) filename = f'stack-set-inventory-{datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d")}.json' filepath = os.path.join(report_dir, filename) with open(filepath, 'w') as f: json.dump(report, f, indent=2, default=str) print(f"\n✓ Report saved: {filename}") print(f"Status: {report['status']}") print(f"Parameters: {len(report['parameters'])}") print(f"Tags: {len(report['tags'])}") # Create report job post report_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Inventory Report: {stack_set_name}', description=f'Generated {filename}', author=report_author, location=reports_location, job_type='REPORT', is_active=False ) report_post.required_skills.add(inventory_skill, reporting_skill, audit_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

list-stack-sets

Lists all stack sets in your account with their current status.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
  --status {ACTIVE|DELETED} \
  --call-as {SELF|DELEGATED_ADMIN}
Parameters Description
  • --status (string): Filter by status: ACTIVE or DELETED
  • --call-as (string): SELF or DELEGATED_ADMIN for Organizations
Return Value
  • Summaries (list): List of stack set summaries with name, ID, status
5 Detailed CloudFormation list-stack-sets Examples
Example 1: List All Active Stack Sets
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
  --status ACTIVE \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[StackSetName,Status]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------- | ListStackSets | +-------------------------------+----------+ | anihpj-multi-region-django | ACTIVE | | anihpj-org-wide-security | ACTIVE | +-------------------------------+----------+
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/list_stack_sets.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'List all CloudFormation stack sets' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') list_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Stack Set Management', defaults={'description': 'Manage stack sets'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") list_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='stack_viewer', defaults={'email': 'viewer@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") list_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=list_user, defaults={'bio': 'Stack set viewer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") list_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='View Center', state='Monitoring', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.list_stack_sets(Status='ACTIVE') for ss in response.get('Summaries', []): print(f"{ss['StackSetName']}: {ss['Status']}") list_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='List Stack Sets', description=f"Found: {len(response.get('Summaries', []))}", author=list_author, location=list_location, job_type='LIST_STACKS', is_active=False ) list_job.required_skills.add(list_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: List with Detailed Info
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
  --status ACTIVE \
  --query 'Summaries[*].{Name:StackSetName,Status:Status,Drift:DriftStatus}' \
  --output json
[ { "Name": "anihpj-multi-region-django", "Status": "ACTIVE", "Drift": "NOT_CHECKED" } ]
# anihpj/jobpost/services/stack_set_lister.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class StackSetLister: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def list_with_details(self): detail_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Detailed Reporting', defaults={'description': 'Generate detailed reports'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") report_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='reporter', defaults={'email': 'reports@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") report_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=report_user, defaults={'bio': 'Report generator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") report_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Report Center', state='Analytics', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = self.cf_client.list_stack_sets(Status='ACTIVE') results = [{'Name': ss['StackSetName'], 'Status': ss['Status']} for ss in response.get('Summaries', [])] report_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Detailed Stack Set List', description=f'Stack sets: {len(results)}', author=report_author, location=report_location, job_type='DETAILED_LIST', is_active=False ) report_job.required_skills.add(detail_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return results
Example 3: Count Stack Sets by Status
#!/bin/bash
ACTIVE=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-sets --status ACTIVE --query 'length(Summaries)' --output text)
echo "Active stack sets: $ACTIVE"
Active stack sets: 5
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Stack set count API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class StackSetCountView(View): def get(self, request): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') count_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Inventory', defaults={'description': 'Count resources'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") counter = User.objects.get_or_create( username='counter', defaults={'email': 'count@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") counter_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=counter, defaults={'bio': 'Resource counter'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") count_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Count Center', state='Metrics', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.list_stack_sets(Status='ACTIVE') count = len(response.get('Summaries', [])) count_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Stack Set Count', description=f'Active: {count}', author=counter_author, location=count_location, job_type='COUNT', is_active=False ) count_job.required_skills.add(count_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({'active_count': count})
Example 4: List for Organizations
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
  --status ACTIVE \
  --call-as DELEGATED_ADMIN \
  --query 'Summaries[*].StackSetName' \
  --output text
anihpj-org-security anihpj-org-monitoring anihpj-org-networking
# anihpj/jobpost/services/org_stack_sets.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class OrgStackSets: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def list_org_stack_sets(self): org_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Organizations Management', defaults={'description': 'AWS Organizations'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") org_admin = User.objects.get_or_create( username='org_admin', defaults={'email': 'org@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") org_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=org_admin, defaults={'bio': 'Organizations admin'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") org_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Org HQ', state='Enterprise', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = self.cf_client.list_stack_sets(Status='ACTIVE', CallAs='DELEGATED_ADMIN') names = [ss['StackSetName'] for ss in response.get('Summaries', [])] org_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Org Stack Sets', description=f'Found: {len(names)}', author=org_author, location=org_location, job_type='ORG_STACKS', is_active=False ) org_job.required_skills.add(org_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return names
Example 5: Filter by Name Pattern
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
  --status ACTIVE \
  --query "Summaries[?starts_with(StackSetName, 'anihpj-')].StackSetName" \
  --output text
anihpj-django-prod anihpj-django-dev anihpj-monitoring
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/filter_stack_sets.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Filter stack sets by prefix' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--prefix', type=str, default='anihpj-') def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') prefix = options['prefix'] filter_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Filtering', defaults={'description': 'Filter resources'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") filter_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='filterer', defaults={'email': 'filter@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") filter_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=filter_user, defaults={'bio': 'Resource filterer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") filter_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Filter Center', state='Search', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.list_stack_sets(Status='ACTIVE') filtered = [ss['StackSetName'] for ss in response.get('Summaries', []) if ss['StackSetName'].startswith(prefix)] for name in filtered: print(name) filter_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Filter: {prefix}', description=f'Found: {len(filtered)}', author=filter_author, location=filter_location, job_type='FILTER', is_active=False ) filter_job.required_skills.add(filter_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

create-stack-instances

Creates stack instances in the specified accounts and regions from an existing CloudFormation stack set. This deploys your anihpj Django jobpost infrastructure across multiple AWS accounts and regions, enabling global availability and disaster recovery capabilities.

Syntax
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name {name-or-id} \
  --deployment-targets Accounts={account-ids}|OrganizationalUnitIds={ou-ids} \
  --regions {region-list} \
  --operation-preferences MaxConcurrentCount={n}|MaxConcurrentPercentage={pct} \
  --parameter-overrides ParameterKey={key},ParameterValue={value} \
  --call-as {SELF|DELEGATED_ADMIN} \
  --region {management-region}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name (string, required): Name or unique ID of the stack set to create instances from. Example: anihpj-multi-region-django
  • --deployment-targets (structure): Target accounts or organizational units:
    • Accounts: List of AWS account IDs for SELF_MANAGED permission model
    • OrganizationalUnitIds: List of OU IDs for SERVICE_MANAGED permission model
    • AccountFilterType: INTERSECTION, DIFFERENCE, UNION, or NONE
  • --accounts (list, deprecated): Use --deployment-targets instead. List of account IDs
  • --regions (list, required): AWS regions to create stack instances in. Example: us-east-1 us-west-2 eu-west-1
  • --operation-preferences (structure): Control deployment parallelism:
    • MaxConcurrentCount: Maximum simultaneous operations (number)
    • MaxConcurrentPercentage: Maximum percentage of accounts
    • FailureToleranceCount: Number of failures to tolerate
    • FailureTolerancePercentage: Percentage of failures to tolerate
    • RegionConcurrencyType: SEQUENTIAL or PARALLEL
  • --parameter-overrides (list): Override stack set parameters for these instances
  • --call-as (string): SELF (management account) or DELEGATED_ADMIN
Return Value
  • OperationId (string): Unique identifier for the create operation. Use this to track progress with describe-stack-set-operation
5 Detailed CloudFormation create-stack-instances Examples
Example 1: Deploy Django Stack to Multiple Regions

Create stack instances across three regions for global Django application deployment.

#!/bin/bash
# Deploy anihpj Django stack to multiple regions
STACK_SET_NAME="anihpj-multi-region-django"
ACCOUNT_ID="123456789012"
REGIONS="us-east-1 us-west-2 eu-west-1"

OPERATION_ID=$(aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET_NAME \
  --accounts $ACCOUNT_ID \
  --regions $REGIONS \
  --operation-preferences MaxConcurrentCount=2,FailureToleranceCount=1 \
  --query 'OperationId' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

echo "Stack Instance Creation Started"
echo "Operation ID: $OPERATION_ID"
echo "Target Regions: $REGIONS"

# Monitor progress
aws cloudformation describe-stack-set-operation \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET_NAME \
  --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
  --query '{Status: Status, Progress: StackSetDriftDetectionDetails}' \
  --region us-east-1
Stack Instance Creation Started Operation ID: op-4a7b3c5d-e89f-4123-a456-789012345678 Target Regions: us-east-1 us-west-2 eu-west-1 { "Status": "RUNNING" } Instance Creation Progress: us-east-1: CREATE_IN_PROGRESS (1/3) us-west-2: PENDING (2/3) eu-west-1: PENDING (3/3)
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/create_stack_instances.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Create stack instances across multiple regions' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-set', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--regions', nargs='+', required=True) parser.add_argument('--account', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_set_name = options['stack_set'] regions = options['regions'] account = options['account'] # Create multi-region deployment skills multi_region_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-Region Deployment', defaults={'description': 'Deploy applications across multiple AWS regions'} )[0] stack_sets_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='CloudFormation StackSets', defaults={'description': 'Multi-account/region stack management'} )[0] global_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Global Infrastructure', defaults={'description': 'Worldwide application deployment'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get deployment user deployer = User.objects.get_or_create( username='global_deployer', defaults={'email': 'deploy@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author deploy_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=deployer, defaults={'bio': 'Global infrastructure deployment specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Create locations for each region region_locations = [] for region in regions: region_city = region.replace('-', ' ').title() loc = Location.objects.get_or_create( city=f'AWS {region_city}', state=region, country='AWS Cloud', remote=True )[0] region_locations.append(loc) print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create stack instances response = cf_client.create_stack_instances( StackSetName=stack_set_name, Accounts=[account], Regions=regions, OperationPreferences={ 'MaxConcurrentCount': 2, 'FailureToleranceCount': 1 } ) operation_id = response.get('OperationId') print(f"\nOperation ID: {operation_id}") print(f"Deploying to regions: {', '.join(regions)}") # Create deployment job post deploy_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Multi-Region Deployment: {stack_set_name}', description=f'Operation: {operation_id}, Regions: {len(regions)}', author=deploy_author, location=region_locations[0], job_type='DEPLOYMENT', is_active=True ) deploy_post.required_skills.add(multi_region_skill, stack_sets_skill, global_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Deploy with Parameter Overrides

Create stack instances with region-specific parameter overrides for the Django application.

#!/bin/bash
# Deploy to Asia with custom parameters
STACK_SET_NAME="anihpj-multi-region-django"

aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET_NAME \
  --accounts 123456789012 \
  --regions ap-southeast-1 ap-northeast-1 \
  --parameter-overrides \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.small \
    ParameterKey=Environment,ParameterValue=asia-production \
  --operation-preferences MaxConcurrentCount=1 \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | jq '{
    operation_id: .OperationId,
    message: "Deploying with Asia-optimized parameters"
  }'
{ "operation_id": "op-789abc12-3456-def7-8901-234567890abc", "message": "Deploying with Asia-optimized parameters" } Parameter Overrides Applied: InstanceType: t3.medium → t3.small Environment: production → asia-production Regional Deployment: ap-southeast-1 (Singapore): CREATE_IN_PROGRESS ap-northeast-1 (Tokyo): PENDING
# anihpj/jobpost/services/regional_deployment.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class RegionalDeploymentService: def __init__(self, stack_set_name): self.stack_set_name = stack_set_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def deploy_with_overrides(self, account, regions, param_overrides): # Create regional deployment skills regional_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Regional Optimization', defaults={'description': 'Region-specific configuration'} )[0] override_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Parameter Management', defaults={'description': 'Stack parameter customization'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get regional admin regional_admin = User.objects.get_or_create( username='regional_admin', defaults={'email': 'regional@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author regional_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=regional_admin, defaults={'bio': 'Regional deployment administrator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Asian region location asia_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Asia Pacific', state='Multi-AZ', country='Asia', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Convert param overrides to API format api_overrides = [ {'ParameterKey': k, 'ParameterValue': v} for k, v in param_overrides.items() ] # Create stack instances response = self.cf_client.create_stack_instances( StackSetName=self.stack_set_name, Accounts=[account], Regions=regions, ParameterOverrides=api_overrides, OperationPreferences={'MaxConcurrentCount': 1} ) operation_id = response.get('OperationId') print(f"\nOperation: {operation_id}") print(f"Regions: {regions}") print(f"Overrides: {param_overrides}") # Create regional deployment job regional_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Regional Deploy: {", ".join(regions)}', description=f'Overrides: {len(param_overrides)} parameters', author=regional_author, location=asia_location, job_type='REGIONAL_DEPLOY', is_active=True ) regional_post.required_skills.add(regional_skill, override_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return operation_id
Example 3: Deploy to AWS Organizations OUs

Create stack instances across organizational units for enterprise-wide Django deployment.

#!/bin/bash
# Deploy to organizational units (SERVICE_MANAGED)
STACK_SET_NAME="anihpj-enterprise-django"

aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET_NAME \
  --deployment-targets OrganizationalUnitIds=ou-abc123,ou-def456 \
  --regions us-east-1 eu-west-1 \
  --operation-preferences \
    RegionConcurrencyType=PARALLEL,MaxConcurrentPercentage=50,FailureTolerancePercentage=10 \
  --call-as DELEGATED_ADMIN \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1

echo ""
echo "Enterprise Deployment Initiated"
echo "Targeting all accounts in Production and Development OUs"
{ "OperationId": "op-enterprise-123456" } Enterprise Deployment Initiated Targeting all accounts in Production and Development OUs Deployment Summary: OUs Targeted: 2 (Production, Development) Estimated Accounts: 15 Regions per Account: 2 Total Stack Instances: ~30 Concurrency: 50% parallel Failure Tolerance: 10%
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Enterprise deployment API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class EnterpriseDeploymentView(View): def post(self, request): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_set_name = request.POST.get('stack_set_name') ou_ids = request.POST.getlist('ou_ids') regions = request.POST.getlist('regions') # Create enterprise skills enterprise_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Enterprise Deployment', defaults={'description': 'Organization-wide infrastructure'} )[0] org_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='AWS Organizations', defaults={'description': 'Multi-account management'} )[0] governance_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Cloud Governance', defaults={'description': 'Enterprise cloud policy enforcement'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get enterprise admin admin = User.objects.get_or_create( username='enterprise_admin', defaults={'email': 'enterprise@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author admin_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=admin, defaults={'bio': 'Enterprise infrastructure architect'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Enterprise HQ location hq_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Enterprise HQ', state='Corporate', country='Global', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create stack instances response = cf_client.create_stack_instances( StackSetName=stack_set_name, DeploymentTargets={'OrganizationalUnitIds': ou_ids}, Regions=regions, OperationPreferences={ 'RegionConcurrencyType': 'PARALLEL', 'MaxConcurrentPercentage': 50, 'FailureTolerancePercentage': 10 }, CallAs='DELEGATED_ADMIN' ) operation_id = response.get('OperationId') # Create enterprise deployment job enterprise_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Enterprise Deploy: {stack_set_name}', description=f'OUs: {len(ou_ids)}, Regions: {len(regions)}', author=admin_author, location=hq_location, job_type='ENTERPRISE_DEPLOY', is_active=True ) enterprise_post.required_skills.add(enterprise_skill, org_skill, governance_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({ 'operation_id': operation_id, 'ou_count': len(ou_ids), 'region_count': len(regions) })
Example 4: Sequential Region Deployment

Deploy stack instances one region at a time for careful rollout monitoring.

#!/bin/bash
# Sequential deployment for cautious rollout
STACK_SET_NAME="anihpj-multi-region-django"
ACCOUNT_ID="123456789012"

# Deploy region by region sequentially
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET_NAME \
  --accounts $ACCOUNT_ID \
  --regions us-east-1 us-west-2 eu-west-1 ap-southeast-1 \
  --operation-preferences \
    RegionConcurrencyType=SEQUENTIAL,MaxConcurrentCount=1,FailureToleranceCount=0 \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | tee operation-response.json

OPERATION_ID=$(jq -r '.OperationId' operation-response.json)

echo ""
echo "Sequential Deployment Started"
echo "Operation: $OPERATION_ID"
echo "Mode: One region at a time"
echo "Failure tolerance: 0 (stop on any failure)"
{ "OperationId": "op-sequential-789012" } Sequential Deployment Started Operation: op-sequential-789012 Mode: One region at a time Failure tolerance: 0 (stop on any failure) Deployment Order: 1. us-east-1 - CREATE_IN_PROGRESS 2. us-west-2 - PENDING (after us-east-1 completes) 3. eu-west-1 - PENDING 4. ap-southeast-1 - PENDING
# anihpj/jobpost/services/sequential_deployer.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import time class SequentialDeployer: def __init__(self, stack_set_name, account_id): self.stack_set_name = stack_set_name self.account_id = account_id self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def deploy_sequentially(self, regions): # Create sequential deployment skills sequential_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Sequential Deployment', defaults={'description': 'Controlled region-by-region rollout'} )[0] rollout_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Controlled Rollout', defaults={'description': 'Careful phased deployment'} )[0] monitoring_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Deployment Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Track deployment progress'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get rollout manager rollout_mgr = User.objects.get_or_create( username='rollout_manager', defaults={'email': 'rollout@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author rollout_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=rollout_mgr, defaults={'bio': 'Controlled deployment specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Operations center ops_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Operations Center', state='Deployment', country='Global', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create sequential deployment response = self.cf_client.create_stack_instances( StackSetName=self.stack_set_name, Accounts=[self.account_id], Regions=regions, OperationPreferences={ 'RegionConcurrencyType': 'SEQUENTIAL', 'MaxConcurrentCount': 1, 'FailureToleranceCount': 0 } ) operation_id = response.get('OperationId') print(f"\nSequential Deployment: {operation_id}") print(f"Regions ({len(regions)}): {' → '.join(regions)}") print("Mode: One region at a time") # Create rollout job rollout_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Sequential Rollout: {self.stack_set_name}', description=f'Regions: {len(regions)}, Mode: SEQUENTIAL', author=rollout_author, location=ops_location, job_type='SEQUENTIAL_ROLLOUT', is_active=True ) rollout_post.required_skills.add(sequential_skill, rollout_skill, monitoring_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return operation_id
Example 5: Deploy with Deployment Targets Filter

Create stack instances using account filter to exclude specific accounts.

#!/bin/bash
# Deploy to specific accounts excluding sandbox
STACK_SET_NAME="anihpj-multi-region-django"

# Create instances in production accounts only
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET_NAME \
  --deployment-targets \
    Accounts=111111111111,222222222222,333333333333 \
    AccountFilterType=DIFFERENCE \
  --regions us-east-1 us-west-2 \
  --operation-preferences MaxConcurrentCount=3 \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1

# Alternative: Target intersection of accounts list with OU
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET_NAME \
  --deployment-targets \
    Accounts=111111111111,222222222222 \
    OrganizationalUnitIds=ou-production \
    AccountFilterType=INTERSECTION \
  --regions eu-west-1 \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1
{ "OperationId": "op-filter-deploy-456789" } Deployment with Account Filtering: Filter Type: DIFFERENCE Target Accounts: 3 (excluding sandbox) Regions: us-east-1, us-west-2 Expected Instances: 6 Second Deployment (INTERSECTION): { "OperationId": "op-intersect-deploy-123456" } Filter Type: INTERSECTION Matching Accounts: 2 (in both list and Production OU) Region: eu-west-1 Expected Instances: 2
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/filtered_deployment.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Deploy stack instances with account filtering' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-set', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--accounts', nargs='+', required=True) parser.add_argument('--filter-type', choices=['INTERSECTION', 'DIFFERENCE', 'UNION'], default='INTERSECTION') parser.add_argument('--regions', nargs='+', required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_set_name = options['stack_set'] accounts = options['accounts'] filter_type = options['filter_type'] regions = options['regions'] # Create filtering skills filter_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Account Filtering', defaults={'description': 'Selective account targeting'} )[0] target_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Deployment Targeting', defaults={'description': 'Precise deployment scoping'} )[0] multi_account_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-Account Management', defaults={'description': 'Cross-account deployments'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get deployment admin deploy_admin = User.objects.get_or_create( username='deploy_admin', defaults={'email': 'deploy-admin@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author deploy_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=deploy_admin, defaults={'bio': 'Deployment targeting specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Multi-account location multi_loc = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Multi-Account', state='Organization', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create stack instances with filtering response = cf_client.create_stack_instances( StackSetName=stack_set_name, DeploymentTargets={ 'Accounts': accounts, 'AccountFilterType': filter_type }, Regions=regions, OperationPreferences={'MaxConcurrentCount': 3} ) operation_id = response.get('OperationId') print(f"\nFiltered Deployment: {operation_id}") print(f"Filter Type: {filter_type}") print(f"Accounts: {len(accounts)}") print(f"Regions: {regions}") # Create filtered deployment job filter_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Filtered Deploy: {stack_set_name}', description=f'Filter: {filter_type}, Accounts: {len(accounts)}', author=deploy_author, location=multi_loc, job_type='FILTERED_DEPLOY', is_active=True ) filter_post.required_skills.add(filter_skill, target_skill, multi_account_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

update-stack-instances

Updates stack instances with parameter overrides, allowing you to customize deployments per account and region.

Syntax
aws cloudformation update-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name {name} \
  --accounts {account-id} \
  --regions {region} \
  --parameter-overrides ParameterKey={key},ParameterValue={value}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name (string, required): Name of the stack set
  • --accounts (list): AWS account IDs to update
  • --regions (list, required): Regions to update instances in
  • --parameter-overrides (list): Parameter overrides for specific instances
Return Value
  • OperationId (string): ID to track the update operation
5 Detailed CloudFormation update-stack-instances Examples
Example 1: Update Regional Parameters
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation update-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --accounts 123456789012 \
  --regions us-west-2 \
  --parameter-overrides ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.large
{ "OperationId": "op-789ghi" } Updating us-west-2 instance Parameter change: t3.medium → t3.large
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/update_stack_instances.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Update stack instances with parameter overrides' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') update_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Stack Instance Updates', defaults={'description': 'Update stack instances'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") updater = User.objects.get_or_create( username='instance_updater', defaults={'email': 'updater@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") update_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=updater, defaults={'bio': 'Instance updater'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") update_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Update Center', state='Operations', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.update_stack_instances( StackSetName='anihpj-multi-region-django', Accounts=['123456789012'], Regions=['us-west-2'], ParameterOverrides=[{'ParameterKey': 'InstanceType', 'ParameterValue': 't3.large'}] ) update_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Update Stack Instance', description=f"Operation: {response['OperationId']}", author=update_author, location=update_location, job_type='UPDATE_INSTANCE', is_active=True ) update_job.required_skills.add(update_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Update Multiple Regions
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation update-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-app \
  --accounts 123456789012 987654321098 \
  --regions us-east-1 us-west-2 eu-west-1 \
  --parameter-overrides ParameterKey=Environment,ParameterValue=production
{ "OperationId": "op-multi-789" } Updating 6 instances across 3 regions
# anihpj/jobpost/services/multi_region_updater.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class MultiRegionUpdater: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def update_all_regions(self, stack_set_name, accounts, regions, overrides): multi_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-Region Updates', defaults={'description': 'Update across regions'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") multi_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='multi_updater', defaults={'email': 'multi@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") multi_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=multi_user, defaults={'bio': 'Multi-region operator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") multi_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Global HQ', state='Multi-Region', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = self.cf_client.update_stack_instances( StackSetName=stack_set_name, Accounts=accounts, Regions=regions, ParameterOverrides=overrides ) multi_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Multi-Region Update', description=f'Regions: {len(regions)}, Accounts: {len(accounts)}', author=multi_author, location=multi_location, job_type='MULTI_UPDATE', is_active=True ) multi_job.required_skills.add(multi_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return response
Example 3: Update with Operation Preferences
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation update-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-app \
  --accounts 123456789012 \
  --regions us-east-1 \
  --operation-preferences MaxConcurrentCount=5,FailureToleranceCount=2
{ "OperationId": "op-pref-456" } Max concurrent: 5, Failure tolerance: 2
