# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Controlled update API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ControlledUpdateView(View): def post(self, request): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') control_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Controlled Deployments', defaults={'description': 'Controlled rollouts'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") control_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='controller', defaults={'email': 'control@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") control_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=control_user, defaults={'bio': 'Deployment controller'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") control_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Control Center', state='Ops', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.update_stack_instances( StackSetName='anihpj-django-app', Accounts=['123456789012'], Regions=['us-east-1'], OperationPreferences={'MaxConcurrentCount': 5, 'FailureToleranceCount': 2} ) control_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Controlled Update', description='Max concurrent: 5', author=control_author, location=control_location, job_type='CONTROLLED', is_active=True ) control_job.required_skills.add(control_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({'operation_id': response['OperationId']})
Example 4: Update for Organizations
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation update-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-org-security \
  --deployment-targets OrganizationalUnitIds=ou-abc123 \
  --regions us-east-1 \
  --call-as DELEGATED_ADMIN
{ "OperationId": "op-org-789" } Updating all accounts in OU ou-abc123
# anihpj/jobpost/services/org_updater.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class OrgStackUpdater: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def update_org_instances(self, stack_set_name, ou_ids, regions): org_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Organization Updates', defaults={'description': 'Update across organization'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") org_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='org_operator', defaults={'email': 'orgops@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") org_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=org_user, defaults={'bio': 'Organization operator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") org_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Org Center', state='Enterprise', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = self.cf_client.update_stack_instances( StackSetName=stack_set_name, DeploymentTargets={'OrganizationalUnitIds': ou_ids}, Regions=regions, CallAs='DELEGATED_ADMIN' ) org_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Org Update', description=f'OUs: {len(ou_ids)}', author=org_author, location=org_location, job_type='ORG_UPDATE', is_active=True ) org_job.required_skills.add(org_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return response
Example 5: Selective Parameter Update
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation update-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-app \
  --accounts 123456789012 \
  --regions us-west-2 \
  --parameter-overrides \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.xlarge \
    ParameterKey=MinCapacity,ParameterValue=3 \
    ParameterKey=MaxCapacity,ParameterValue=10
{ "OperationId": "op-params-321" } Updating 3 parameters in us-west-2
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/selective_update.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Update stack instances with multiple parameters' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') param_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Parameter Management', defaults={'description': 'Manage parameters'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") param_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='param_admin', defaults={'email': 'params@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") param_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=param_user, defaults={'bio': 'Parameter admin'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") param_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Param Center', state='Config', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") overrides = [ {'ParameterKey': 'InstanceType', 'ParameterValue': 't3.xlarge'}, {'ParameterKey': 'MinCapacity', 'ParameterValue': '3'}, {'ParameterKey': 'MaxCapacity', 'ParameterValue': '10'} ] response = cf_client.update_stack_instances( StackSetName='anihpj-django-app', Accounts=['123456789012'], Regions=['us-west-2'], ParameterOverrides=overrides ) param_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Selective Update', description=f'Parameters: {len(overrides)}', author=param_author, location=param_location, job_type='PARAM_UPDATE', is_active=True ) param_job.required_skills.add(param_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

delete-stack-instances

Deletes stack instances from specified accounts and regions, removing deployed resources.

Syntax
aws cloudformation delete-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name {name} \
  --accounts {account-id} \
  --regions {region} \
  --no-retain-stacks
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name (string, required): Name of the stack set
  • --accounts (list): AWS account IDs to delete from
  • --regions (list, required): Regions to delete instances from
  • --no-retain-stacks: Also delete the underlying stack resources
  • --retain-stacks: Keep underlying resources (orphan them)
Return Value
  • OperationId (string): ID to track the delete operation
5 Detailed CloudFormation delete-stack-instances Examples
Example 1: Remove Regional Instances
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation delete-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --accounts 123456789012 \
  --regions eu-west-1 \
  --no-retain-stacks
{ "OperationId": "op-012jkl" } Deleting instance in eu-west-1 All resources will be removed
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/delete_stack_instances.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Delete stack instances from regions' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') delete_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Instance Deletion', defaults={'description': 'Delete stack instances'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") deleter = User.objects.get_or_create( username='instance_deleter', defaults={'email': 'deleter@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") delete_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=deleter, defaults={'bio': 'Instance deleter'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") delete_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Delete Center', state='Cleanup', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.delete_stack_instances( StackSetName='anihpj-multi-region-django', Accounts=['123456789012'], Regions=['eu-west-1'], RetainStacks=False ) delete_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Delete Instance', description=f"Operation: {response['OperationId']}", author=delete_author, location=delete_location, job_type='DELETE_INSTANCE', is_active=False ) delete_job.required_skills.add(delete_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Delete Multiple Regions
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation delete-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-app \
  --accounts 123456789012 \
  --regions us-west-2 eu-west-1 ap-southeast-1 \
  --no-retain-stacks
{ "OperationId": "op-multi-del-456" } Deleting instances from 3 regions
# anihpj/jobpost/services/multi_region_deleter.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class MultiRegionDeleter: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def delete_from_regions(self, stack_set_name, accounts, regions): multi_del_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-Region Deletion', defaults={'description': 'Delete across regions'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") multi_del_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='multi_deleter', defaults={'email': 'multidel@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") multi_del_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=multi_del_user, defaults={'bio': 'Multi-region deleter'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") multi_del_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Global Cleanup', state='Multi-Region', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = self.cf_client.delete_stack_instances( StackSetName=stack_set_name, Accounts=accounts, Regions=regions, RetainStacks=False ) multi_del_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Multi-Region Delete', description=f'Regions: {len(regions)}', author=multi_del_author, location=multi_del_location, job_type='MULTI_DELETE', is_active=False ) multi_del_job.required_skills.add(multi_del_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return response
Example 3: Retain Stacks on Delete
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation delete-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-app \
  --accounts 123456789012 \
  --regions us-east-1 \
  --retain-stacks
{ "OperationId": "op-retain-789" } Instance removed, resources retained (orphaned)
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Orphan stack API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class OrphanStackView(View): def post(self, request): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') orphan_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Stack Orphaning', defaults={'description': 'Orphan stack resources'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") orphan_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='orphaner', defaults={'email': 'orphan@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") orphan_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=orphan_user, defaults={'bio': 'Stack orphaner'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") orphan_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Orphan Center', state='Migration', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.delete_stack_instances( StackSetName='anihpj-django-app', Accounts=['123456789012'], Regions=['us-east-1'], RetainStacks=True ) orphan_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Orphan Stack', description='Resources retained', author=orphan_author, location=orphan_location, job_type='ORPHAN', is_active=False ) orphan_job.required_skills.add(orphan_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({'operation_id': response['OperationId']})
Example 4: Delete from Organization
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation delete-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-org-security \
  --deployment-targets OrganizationalUnitIds=ou-abc123 \
  --regions us-east-1 us-west-2 \
  --no-retain-stacks \
  --call-as DELEGATED_ADMIN
{ "OperationId": "op-org-del-321" } Deleting from all accounts in OU
# anihpj/jobpost/services/org_deleter.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class OrgStackDeleter: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def delete_org_instances(self, stack_set_name, ou_ids, regions): org_del_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Organization Cleanup', defaults={'description': 'Delete across organization'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") org_del_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='org_deleter', defaults={'email': 'orgdel@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") org_del_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=org_del_user, defaults={'bio': 'Organization deleter'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") org_del_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Org Cleanup', state='Enterprise', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = self.cf_client.delete_stack_instances( StackSetName=stack_set_name, DeploymentTargets={'OrganizationalUnitIds': ou_ids}, Regions=regions, RetainStacks=False, CallAs='DELEGATED_ADMIN' ) org_del_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Org Cleanup', description=f'OUs: {len(ou_ids)}', author=org_del_author, location=org_del_location, job_type='ORG_DELETE', is_active=False ) org_del_job.required_skills.add(org_del_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return response
Example 5: Delete with Operation Preferences
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation delete-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-app \
  --accounts 123456789012 987654321098 \
  --regions us-east-1 \
  --no-retain-stacks \
  --operation-preferences RegionConcurrencyType=PARALLEL,FailureToleranceCount=1
{ "OperationId": "op-pref-del-654" } Parallel deletion with failure tolerance: 1
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/controlled_delete.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Delete stack instances with operation preferences' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') control_del_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Controlled Deletion', defaults={'description': 'Safe deletion practices'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") control_del_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='safe_deleter', defaults={'email': 'safedel@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") control_del_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=control_del_user, defaults={'bio': 'Safe deletion operator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") control_del_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Safe Delete', state='Operations', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.delete_stack_instances( StackSetName='anihpj-django-app', Accounts=['123456789012', '987654321098'], Regions=['us-east-1'], RetainStacks=False, OperationPreferences={'RegionConcurrencyType': 'PARALLEL', 'FailureToleranceCount': 1} ) control_del_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Controlled Delete', description='Parallel with tolerance', author=control_del_author, location=control_del_location, job_type='CONTROLLED_DELETE', is_active=False ) control_del_job.required_skills.add(control_del_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

list-stack-instances

Lists all stack instances belonging to a stack set with their deployment status.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name {name} \
  --filters Name={filter-name},Values={value}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name (string, required): Name of the stack set
  • --filters (list): Filter by DETAILED_STATUS, DRIFT_STATUS
  • --stack-instance-account (string): Filter by account ID
  • --stack-instance-region (string): Filter by region
Return Value
  • Summaries (list): List of stack instance summaries with status info
5 Detailed CloudFormation list-stack-instances Examples
Example 1: List All Instances
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-multi-region-django \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[Region,Account,Status]' \
  --output table
---------------------------------------------------- | ListStackInstances | +---------------+---------------+------------------+ | us-east-1 | 123456789012 | CURRENT | | us-west-2 | 123456789012 | CURRENT | | eu-west-1 | 123456789012 | CURRENT | +---------------+---------------+------------------+
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/list_stack_instances.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'List all stack instances for a stack set' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') list_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Instance Listing', defaults={'description': 'List stack instances'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") lister = User.objects.get_or_create( username='instance_lister', defaults={'email': 'lister@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") list_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=lister, defaults={'bio': 'Instance lister'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") list_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='List Center', state='Inventory', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.list_stack_instances(StackSetName='anihpj-multi-region-django') for instance in response.get('Summaries', []): print(f"{instance['Region']}: {instance['Status']}") list_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='List Instances', description=f"Found: {len(response.get('Summaries', []))}", author=list_author, location=list_location, job_type='LIST_INSTANCES', is_active=False ) list_job.required_skills.add(list_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Filter by Status
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-app \
  --filters Name=DETAILED_STATUS,Values=SUCCEEDED \
  --query 'Summaries[*].{Region:Region,Account:Account}' \
  --output json
[ {"Region": "us-east-1", "Account": "123456789012"}, {"Region": "us-west-2", "Account": "123456789012"} ]
# anihpj/jobpost/services/instance_filter.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class InstanceFilter: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def filter_by_status(self, stack_set_name, status): filter_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Status Filtering', defaults={'description': 'Filter by status'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") filter_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='status_filter', defaults={'email': 'status@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") filter_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=filter_user, defaults={'bio': 'Status filterer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") filter_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Filter Center', state='Analysis', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = self.cf_client.list_stack_instances( StackSetName=stack_set_name, Filters=[{'Name': 'DETAILED_STATUS', 'Values': status}] ) filter_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Filter: {status}', description=f'Found: {len(response.get("Summaries", []))}', author=filter_author, location=filter_location, job_type='FILTER_STATUS', is_active=False ) filter_job.required_skills.add(filter_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return response.get('Summaries', [])
Example 3: Count by Region
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'Summaries | group_by(@, &Region) | @' \
  --output text
us-east-1: 3 instances us-west-2: 2 instances eu-west-1: 2 instances
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Region count API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from collections import Counter from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class RegionCountView(View): def get(self, request, stack_set_name): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') count_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Region Analysis', defaults={'description': 'Analyze by region'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") counter = User.objects.get_or_create( username='region_counter', defaults={'email': 'regions@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") count_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=counter, defaults={'bio': 'Region counter'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") count_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Count Center', state='Metrics', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.list_stack_instances(StackSetName=stack_set_name) regions = Counter([s['Region'] for s in response.get('Summaries', [])]) count_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Region Count', description=f'Regions: {len(regions)}', author=count_author, location=count_location, job_type='REGION_COUNT', is_active=False ) count_job.required_skills.add(count_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse(dict(regions))
Example 4: Filter by Drift Status
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-app \
  --filters Name=DRIFT_STATUS,Values=DRIFTED \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[Region,Account,DriftStatus]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------- | Region | Account | DriftStatus | +------------+--------------+-------------+ | us-west-2 | 123456789012 | DRIFTED | +------------+--------------+-------------+
# anihpj/jobpost/services/drift_filter.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class DriftFilter: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def find_drifted(self, stack_set_name): drift_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Drift Detection', defaults={'description': 'Find drifted instances'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") drift_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='drift_finder', defaults={'email': 'drift@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") drift_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=drift_user, defaults={'bio': 'Drift finder'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") drift_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Drift Center', state='Compliance', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = self.cf_client.list_stack_instances( StackSetName=stack_set_name, Filters=[{'Name': 'DRIFT_STATUS', 'Values': 'DRIFTED'}] ) drifted = response.get('Summaries', []) drift_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Drifted Instances', description=f'Found: {len(drifted)}', author=drift_author, location=drift_location, job_type='DRIFT_CHECK', is_active=len(drifted) > 0 ) drift_job.required_skills.add(drift_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return drifted
Example 5: List for Specific Account
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-app \
  --stack-instance-account 123456789012 \
  --query 'Summaries[*].{Region:Region,Status:Status,Reason:StatusReason}' \
  --output json
[ {"Region": "us-east-1", "Status": "CURRENT", "Reason": null}, {"Region": "us-west-2", "Status": "OUTDATED", "Reason": "Pending update"} ]
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/account_instances.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'List instances for specific account' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--account', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--stack-set', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') account_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Account Analysis', defaults={'description': 'Analyze by account'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") account_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='account_analyst', defaults={'email': 'account@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") account_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=account_user, defaults={'bio': 'Account analyst'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") account_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Account Center', state='Analysis', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.list_stack_instances( StackSetName=options['stack_set'], StackInstanceAccount=options['account'] ) for instance in response.get('Summaries', []): self.stdout.write(f"{instance['Region']}: {instance['Status']}") account_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f"Account: {options['account'][:8]}...", description=f"Instances: {len(response.get('Summaries', []))}", author=account_author, location=account_location, job_type='ACCOUNT_LIST', is_active=False ) account_job.required_skills.add(account_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

🔍 Drift Detection

detect-stack-drift

Initiates drift detection to identify differences between the expected template configuration and the actual deployed resources. This is critical for maintaining compliance in your anihpj Django application infrastructure, identifying manual changes made outside CloudFormation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation detect-stack-drift \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --logical-resource-ids {resource-id-1} {resource-id-2} \
  --region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Name or unique ID of the stack to detect drift for. Example: anihpj-django-app
  • --logical-resource-ids (list): Specific logical resource IDs to check for drift. If omitted, all supported resources are checked
  • --region (string): AWS region where the stack exists. Example: us-east-1
Return Value
  • StackDriftDetectionId (string): Unique identifier for the drift detection operation. Use this with describe-stack-drift-detection-status to check progress and results
5 Detailed CloudFormation detect-stack-drift Examples
Example 1: Initiate Full Stack Drift Detection

Start drift detection for all resources in the Django application stack.

#!/bin/bash
# Detect drift for the Django application stack
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

DRIFT_ID=$(aws cloudformation detect-stack-drift \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'StackDriftDetectionId' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

echo "Drift Detection Started"
echo "Stack: $STACK_NAME"
echo "Detection ID: $DRIFT_ID"

# Wait for detection to complete (up to 60 seconds)
echo ""
echo "Waiting for detection to complete..."
for i in {1..12}; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status \
    --stack-drift-detection-id $DRIFT_ID \
    --query 'DetectionStatus' \
    --output text \
    --region us-east-1)
  
  if [ "$STATUS" = "DETECTION_COMPLETE" ] || [ "$STATUS" = "DETECTION_FAILED" ]; then
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done

# Get results
aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status \
  --stack-drift-detection-id $DRIFT_ID \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | jq '{
    stack: .StackId,
    status: .DetectionStatus,
    drift_status: .StackDriftStatus,
    drifted_count: .DriftedStackResourceCount
  }'
Drift Detection Started Stack: anihpj-django-app Detection ID: drift-abc123-456def-789ghi Waiting for detection to complete... { "stack": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-app/guid", "status": "DETECTION_COMPLETE", "drift_status": "DRIFTED", "drifted_count": 2 } ⚠ Stack has drifted from template 2 resources have been modified outside CloudFormation
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/detect_drift.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import time class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Detect CloudFormation stack drift' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = options['stack_name'] # Create drift detection skills drift_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Drift Detection', defaults={'description': 'Detect infrastructure configuration drift'} )[0] compliance_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Compliance Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure compliance checking'} )[0] iac_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure as Code', defaults={'description': 'CloudFormation and IaC management'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get drift auditor auditor = User.objects.get_or_create( username='drift_auditor', defaults={'email': 'drift@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author auditor_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=auditor, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure drift and compliance auditor'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Compliance location compliance_loc = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Compliance Center', state='Governance', country='Corporate', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Initiate drift detection response = cf_client.detect_stack_drift(StackName=stack_name) drift_id = response.get('StackDriftDetectionId') print(f"\nDrift Detection ID: {drift_id}") print("Waiting for detection...") # Wait for completion for _ in range(12): status_response = cf_client.describe_stack_drift_detection_status( StackDriftDetectionId=drift_id ) status = status_response.get('DetectionStatus') if status in ['DETECTION_COMPLETE', 'DETECTION_FAILED']: break time.sleep(5) drift_status = status_response.get('StackDriftStatus', 'UNKNOWN') drifted_count = status_response.get('DriftedStackResourceCount', 0) print(f"Status: {drift_status}") print(f"Drifted Resources: {drifted_count}") # Create drift detection job drift_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Drift Detection: {stack_name}', description=f'Status: {drift_status}, Drifted: {drifted_count}', author=auditor_author, location=compliance_loc, job_type='DRIFT_CHECK', is_active=drift_status == 'DRIFTED' ) drift_post.required_skills.add(drift_skill, compliance_skill, iac_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Detect Drift for Specific Resources

Check drift only for critical EC2 and security group resources.

#!/bin/bash
# Detect drift for specific critical resources
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

aws cloudformation detect-stack-drift \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --logical-resource-ids DjangoEC2Instance DjangoSecurityGroup LoadBalancer \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1

echo ""
echo "Drift detection started for critical resources only:"
echo "  - DjangoEC2Instance"
echo "  - DjangoSecurityGroup"
echo "  - LoadBalancer"
{ "StackDriftDetectionId": "drift-critical-123456" } Drift detection started for critical resources only: - DjangoEC2Instance - DjangoSecurityGroup - LoadBalancer Targeted Detection Benefits: - Faster completion - Focus on security-critical resources - Lower API usage
# anihpj/jobpost/services/targeted_drift_checker.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class TargetedDriftChecker: def __init__(self, stack_name): self.stack_name = stack_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def check_critical_resources(self, resource_ids): # Create targeted checking skills targeted_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Targeted Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Focused resource checking'} )[0] security_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Security Compliance', defaults={'description': 'Security resource validation'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get security auditor security_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='security_auditor', defaults={'email': 'security@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author security_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=security_user, defaults={'bio': 'Security compliance auditor'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Security operations center soc_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Security Operations', state='Compliance', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Detect drift for specific resources response = self.cf_client.detect_stack_drift( StackName=self.stack_name, LogicalResourceIds=resource_ids ) drift_id = response.get('StackDriftDetectionId') print(f"\nTargeted Drift Check: {drift_id}") print(f"Resources: {resource_ids}") # Create targeted check job targeted_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Targeted Drift: {self.stack_name}', description=f'Resources: {len(resource_ids)} critical items', author=security_author, location=soc_location, job_type='TARGETED_DRIFT', is_active=True ) targeted_post.required_skills.add(targeted_skill, security_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return drift_id
Example 3: Automated Multi-Stack Drift Monitoring

Schedule drift detection across all anihpj infrastructure stacks.

#!/bin/bash
# Comprehensive drift check for all stacks
STACKS=(
  "anihpj-vpc"
  "anihpj-django-app"
  "anihpj-database"
  "anihpj-storage"
  "anihpj-monitoring"
)

echo "=== Multi-Stack Drift Detection ==="
echo "Started: $(date)"
echo ""

DRIFT_IDS=()
for STACK in "${STACKS[@]}"; do
  DRIFT_ID=$(aws cloudformation detect-stack-drift \
    --stack-name $STACK \
    --query 'StackDriftDetectionId' \
    --output text \
    --region us-east-1 2>/dev/null)
  
  if [ -n "$DRIFT_ID" ]; then
    echo "✓ $STACK: $DRIFT_ID"
    DRIFT_IDS+=("$STACK:$DRIFT_ID")
  else
    echo "✗ $STACK: Failed to initiate"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "Drift detection initiated for ${#DRIFT_IDS[@]} stacks"
=== Multi-Stack Drift Detection === Started: 2026-01-17 10:30:00 UTC ✓ anihpj-vpc: drift-vpc-123456 ✓ anihpj-django-app: drift-app-234567 ✓ anihpj-database: drift-db-345678 ✓ anihpj-storage: drift-storage-456789 ✓ anihpj-monitoring: drift-mon-567890 Drift detection initiated for 5 stacks Estimated completion: ~2-5 minutes per stack
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/multi_stack_drift.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Run drift detection across all infrastructure stacks' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stacks = [ 'anihpj-vpc', 'anihpj-django-app', 'anihpj-database', 'anihpj-storage', 'anihpj-monitoring' ] # Create multi-stack monitoring skills multi_stack_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-Stack Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Cross-stack drift detection'} )[0] automation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Automation', defaults={'description': 'Automated infrastructure checks'} )[0] infrastructure_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure Management', defaults={'description': 'Full stack infrastructure operations'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get infrastructure manager infra_mgr = User.objects.get_or_create( username='infra_manager', defaults={'email': 'infra@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author infra_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=infra_mgr, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure management lead'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # NOC location noc_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Network Operations', state='Infrastructure', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Run drift detection for all stacks drift_results = [] for stack in stacks: try: response = cf_client.detect_stack_drift(StackName=stack) drift_id = response.get('StackDriftDetectionId') drift_results.append({'stack': stack, 'drift_id': drift_id, 'status': 'initiated'}) print(f"✓ {stack}: {drift_id}") except Exception as e: drift_results.append({'stack': stack, 'drift_id': None, 'status': 'failed'}) print(f"✗ {stack}: {str(e)}") successful = [r for r in drift_results if r['status'] == 'initiated'] # Create multi-stack drift job multi_post = JobPost.objects.create( title='Multi-Stack Drift Detection', description=f'Stacks: {len(successful)}/{len(stacks)} initiated', author=infra_author, location=noc_location, job_type='MULTI_DRIFT', is_active=True ) multi_post.required_skills.add(multi_stack_skill, automation_skill, infrastructure_skill) print(f"\nJobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 4: Drift Detection with Immediate Alert

Detect drift and immediately alert if configuration changes are found.

#!/bin/bash
# Drift detection with SNS alerting
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
SNS_TOPIC="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:drift-alerts"

# Start drift detection
DRIFT_ID=$(aws cloudformation detect-stack-drift \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'StackDriftDetectionId' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

echo "Drift detection: $DRIFT_ID"

# Wait for completion
sleep 30

# Get results
RESULT=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status \
  --stack-drift-detection-id $DRIFT_ID \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1)

DRIFT_STATUS=$(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.StackDriftStatus')
DRIFTED_COUNT=$(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.DriftedStackResourceCount')

# Alert if drifted
if [ "$DRIFT_STATUS" = "DRIFTED" ]; then
  aws sns publish \
    --topic-arn $SNS_TOPIC \
    --subject "⚠️ Stack Drift Detected: $STACK_NAME" \
    --message "Stack $STACK_NAME has $DRIFTED_COUNT drifted resources. Investigation required." \
    --region us-east-1
  echo "⚠️ ALERT: Drift detected and notification sent"
else
  echo "✓ No drift detected"
fi
Drift detection: drift-alert-789012 Checking status... ⚠️ ALERT: Drift detected and notification sent Alert Details: Stack: anihpj-django-app Status: DRIFTED Drifted Resources: 2 SNS Notification: Sent MessageId: msg-abc123-def456
# anihpj/jobpost/services/drift_alerter.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import time class DriftAlerter: def __init__(self, stack_name, sns_topic): self.stack_name = stack_name self.sns_topic = sns_topic self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') self.sns_client = boto3.client('sns', region_name='us-east-1') def detect_and_alert(self): # Create alerting skills alerting_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Alert Management', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure alerting and notifications'} )[0] incident_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Incident Response', defaults={'description': 'Respond to infrastructure incidents'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get on-call engineer oncall = User.objects.get_or_create( username='oncall_engineer', defaults={'email': 'oncall@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author oncall_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=oncall, defaults={'bio': 'On-call infrastructure engineer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Alert center alert_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Alert Center', state='Operations', country='24x7', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Start drift detection response = self.cf_client.detect_stack_drift(StackName=self.stack_name) drift_id = response.get('StackDriftDetectionId') # Wait for completion time.sleep(30) status_response = self.cf_client.describe_stack_drift_detection_status( StackDriftDetectionId=drift_id ) drift_status = status_response.get('StackDriftStatus') drifted_count = status_response.get('DriftedStackResourceCount', 0) alert_sent = False if drift_status == 'DRIFTED': self.sns_client.publish( TopicArn=self.sns_topic, Subject=f'⚠️ Stack Drift: {self.stack_name}', Message=f'{drifted_count} resources drifted' ) alert_sent = True print(f"⚠️ Alert sent: {drifted_count} drifted resources") else: print(f"✓ No drift detected") # Create alert job alert_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Drift Alert: {self.stack_name}', description=f'Status: {drift_status}, Alert sent: {alert_sent}', author=oncall_author, location=alert_location, job_type='DRIFT_ALERT', is_active=alert_sent ) alert_post.required_skills.add(alerting_skill, incident_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return {'drift_status': drift_status, 'alert_sent': alert_sent}
Example 5: Generate Drift Compliance Report

Run drift detection and generate a compliance report for auditing.

#!/bin/bash
# Generate comprehensive drift compliance report
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
REPORT_FILE="drift-compliance-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

# Start detection
DRIFT_ID=$(aws cloudformation detect-stack-drift \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'StackDriftDetectionId' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

echo "Detection ID: $DRIFT_ID"
echo "Waiting for completion..."
sleep 45

# Get detection status
STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status \
  --stack-drift-detection-id $DRIFT_ID \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1)

# Get detailed resource drifts
DRIFTS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource-drifts \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --stack-resource-drift-status-filters MODIFIED DELETED \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1)

# Create compliance report
jq -n \
  --arg date "$(date -Is)" \
  --arg stack "$STACK_NAME" \
  --argjson status "$STATUS" \
  --argjson drifts "$DRIFTS" \
  '{
    report_date: $date,
    stack_name: $stack,
    detection_status: $status.DetectionStatus,
    drift_status: $status.StackDriftStatus,
    drifted_count: $status.DriftedStackResourceCount,
    resources: $drifts.StackResourceDrifts
  }' > $REPORT_FILE

echo ""
echo "✓ Compliance report generated: $REPORT_FILE"
Detection ID: drift-report-123456 Waiting for completion... ✓ Compliance report generated: drift-compliance-20260117.json Report Summary: Date: 2026-01-17T10:30:00+00:00 Stack: anihpj-django-app Detection Status: DETECTION_COMPLETE Drift Status: DRIFTED Drifted Resources: 2 Drifted Resources: 1. DjangoSecurityGroup (MODIFIED) 2. DjangoEC2Instance (MODIFIED)
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/drift_compliance_report.py import json from datetime import datetime from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.conf import settings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import time import os class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Generate drift compliance report' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = options['stack_name'] # Create compliance reporting skills compliance_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Compliance Reporting', defaults={'description': 'Generate compliance reports'} )[0] audit_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure Audit', defaults={'description': 'Audit infrastructure configuration'} )[0] documentation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Technical Documentation', defaults={'description': 'Create technical reports'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get compliance officer compliance_officer = User.objects.get_or_create( username='compliance_officer', defaults={'email': 'compliance@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author compliance_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=compliance_officer, defaults={'bio': 'Compliance and audit officer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Compliance office compliance_loc = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Compliance Office', state='Governance', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Run drift detection response = cf_client.detect_stack_drift(StackName=stack_name) drift_id = response.get('StackDriftDetectionId') print(f"Detection ID: {drift_id}") print("Waiting for completion...") time.sleep(45) # Get status status_response = cf_client.describe_stack_drift_detection_status( StackDriftDetectionId=drift_id ) # Get resource drifts drift_response = cf_client.describe_stack_resource_drifts( StackName=stack_name, StackResourceDriftStatusFilters=['MODIFIED', 'DELETED'] ) # Build report report = { 'report_date': datetime.now().isoformat(), 'stack_name': stack_name, 'detection_status': status_response.get('DetectionStatus'), 'drift_status': status_response.get('StackDriftStatus'), 'drifted_count': status_response.get('DriftedStackResourceCount', 0), 'resources': drift_response.get('StackResourceDrifts', []) } # Save report report_dir = os.path.join(settings.BASE_DIR, 'reports', 'compliance') os.makedirs(report_dir, exist_ok=True) filename = f'drift-compliance-{datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d")}.json' filepath = os.path.join(report_dir, filename) with open(filepath, 'w') as f: json.dump(report, f, indent=2, default=str) print(f"\n✓ Report saved: {filename}") print(f"Drift Status: {report['drift_status']}") print(f"Drifted Resources: {report['drifted_count']}") # Create compliance job compliance_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Drift Compliance: {stack_name}', description=f'Report: {filename}, Drifted: {report["drifted_count"]}', author=compliance_author, location=compliance_loc, job_type='COMPLIANCE_REPORT', is_active=False ) compliance_post.required_skills.add(compliance_skill, audit_skill, documentation_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

describe-stack-drift-detection-status

Returns status information about a drift detection operation, including completion status and drift results.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status \
  --stack-drift-detection-id {detection-id}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-drift-detection-id (string, required): ID from detect-stack-drift
Return Value
  • DetectionStatus (string): DETECTION_IN_PROGRESS, DETECTION_FAILED, DETECTION_COMPLETE
  • StackDriftStatus (string): DRIFTED, IN_SYNC, NOT_CHECKED
  • DriftedStackResourceCount (integer): Number of drifted resources
5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-stack-drift-detection-status Examples
Example 1: Check Detection Status
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status \
  --stack-drift-detection-id abc123-def456 \
  --query '[DetectionStatus,StackDriftStatus,DriftedStackResourceCount]' \
  --output text
DETECTION_COMPLETE DRIFTED 2 Drift Summary: - Status: Detection complete - Result: Stack has drifted - Drifted resources: 2
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/check_drift_status.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Check drift detection status' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--detection-id', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') drift_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Drift Status', defaults={'description': 'Check drift detection status'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") checker = User.objects.get_or_create( username='drift_checker', defaults={'email': 'driftcheck@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") drift_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=checker, defaults={'bio': 'Drift status checker'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") drift_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Drift Center', state='Compliance', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.describe_stack_drift_detection_status( StackDriftDetectionId=options['detection_id'] ) self.stdout.write(f"Status: {response['DetectionStatus']}") self.stdout.write(f"Drift: {response.get('StackDriftStatus', 'N/A')}") drift_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Drift Status Check', description=f"Status: {response.get('StackDriftStatus', 'Unknown')}", author=drift_author, location=drift_location, job_type='DRIFT_STATUS', is_active=False ) drift_job.required_skills.add(drift_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Full Status Report
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status \
  --stack-drift-detection-id abc123-def456 \
  --output json
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django/abc123", "StackDriftDetectionId": "abc123-def456", "StackDriftStatus": "DRIFTED", "DetectionStatus": "DETECTION_COMPLETE", "DriftedStackResourceCount": 2, "Timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z" }
# anihpj/jobpost/services/drift_reporter.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class DriftReporter: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def get_full_report(self, detection_id): report_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Drift Reporting', defaults={'description': 'Generate drift reports'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") reporter = User.objects.get_or_create( username='drift_reporter', defaults={'email': 'driftreport@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") report_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=reporter, defaults={'bio': 'Drift reporter'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") report_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Report Center', state='Analytics', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = self.cf_client.describe_stack_drift_detection_status( StackDriftDetectionId=detection_id ) report_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Drift Report', description=f"Drifted: {response.get('DriftedStackResourceCount', 0)}", author=report_author, location=report_location, job_type='DRIFT_REPORT', is_active=False ) report_job.required_skills.add(report_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return response
Example 3: Wait for Detection Complete
#!/bin/bash
DETECTION_ID="abc123-def456"

while true; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status \
    --stack-drift-detection-id $DETECTION_ID \
    --query 'DetectionStatus' --output text)
  
  if [ "$STATUS" = "DETECTION_COMPLETE" ] || [ "$STATUS" = "DETECTION_FAILED" ]; then
    break
  fi
  echo "Waiting... ($STATUS)"
  sleep 10
done

echo "Final status: $STATUS"
Waiting... (DETECTION_IN_PROGRESS) Waiting... (DETECTION_IN_PROGRESS) Final status: DETECTION_COMPLETE
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Drift wait API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import time class DriftWaitView(View): def get(self, request, detection_id): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') wait_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Async Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Wait for async operations'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") waiter = User.objects.get_or_create( username='drift_waiter', defaults={'email': 'wait@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") wait_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=waiter, defaults={'bio': 'Drift waiter'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") wait_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Wait Center', state='Async', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") for _ in range(30): response = cf_client.describe_stack_drift_detection_status( StackDriftDetectionId=detection_id ) if response['DetectionStatus'] in ['DETECTION_COMPLETE', 'DETECTION_FAILED']: break time.sleep(2) wait_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Wait Complete', description=f"Final: {response['DetectionStatus']}", author=wait_author, location=wait_location, job_type='DRIFT_WAIT', is_active=False ) wait_job.required_skills.add(wait_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse(response)
Example 4: Check Multiple Detections
#!/bin/bash
for ID in abc123 def456 ghi789; do
  echo "Detection: $ID"
  aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status \
    --stack-drift-detection-id $ID \
    --query '{Status:DetectionStatus,Drift:StackDriftStatus}' \
    --output table
done
Detection: abc123 ---------------------------------- | Status | Drift | | DETECTION_COMPLETE | DRIFTED | ---------------------------------- Detection: def456 ---------------------------------- | Status | Drift | | DETECTION_COMPLETE | IN_SYNC | ----------------------------------
# anihpj/jobpost/services/multi_drift_checker.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class MultiDriftChecker: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def check_multiple(self, detection_ids): multi_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Batch Drift Check', defaults={'description': 'Check multiple detections'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") multi_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='batch_checker', defaults={'email': 'batch@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") multi_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=multi_user, defaults={'bio': 'Batch checker'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") multi_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Batch Center', state='Bulk Ops', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") results = [] for detection_id in detection_ids: response = self.cf_client.describe_stack_drift_detection_status( StackDriftDetectionId=detection_id ) results.append({'id': detection_id, 'status': response.get('StackDriftStatus')}) multi_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Batch Drift Check', description=f'Checked: {len(detection_ids)}', author=multi_author, location=multi_location, job_type='BATCH_DRIFT', is_active=False ) multi_job.required_skills.add(multi_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return results
Example 5: Get Timing Information
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation describe-stack-drift-detection-status \
  --stack-drift-detection-id abc123-def456 \
  --query '{Started:Timestamp,Stack:StackId,Drifted:DriftedStackResourceCount}' \
  --output yaml
Started: '2024-01-15T10:25:00Z' Stack: arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django/xyz Drifted: 2
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/drift_timing.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Get drift detection timing information' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--detection-id', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') timing_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Timing Analysis', defaults={'description': 'Analyze operation timing'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") timer = User.objects.get_or_create( username='timing_analyst', defaults={'email': 'timing@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") timing_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=timer, defaults={'bio': 'Timing analyst'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") timing_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Timing Center', state='Metrics', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.describe_stack_drift_detection_status( StackDriftDetectionId=options['detection_id'] ) self.stdout.write(f"Started: {response.get('Timestamp')}") self.stdout.write(f"Drifted: {response.get('DriftedStackResourceCount', 0)}") timing_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Drift Timing', description=f"Started: {response.get('Timestamp')}", author=timing_author, location=timing_location, job_type='TIMING', is_active=False ) timing_job.required_skills.add(timing_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

describe-stack-resource-drifts

Returns detailed drift information for each resource in a CloudFormation stack, including property-level differences between expected and actual configurations. Use this to investigate specific changes made to your anihpj Django infrastructure outside of CloudFormation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource-drifts \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --stack-resource-drift-status-filters {filter-list} \
  --max-results {n} \
  --next-token {token} \
  --region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Name or unique ID of the stack. Example: anihpj-django-app
  • --stack-resource-drift-status-filters (list): Filter by drift status: IN_SYNC, MODIFIED, DELETED, NOT_CHECKED. Example: MODIFIED DELETED
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum number of results to return (1-100). Default: 100
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token from previous response
  • --region (string): AWS region where the stack exists. Example: us-east-1
Return Value
  • StackResourceDrifts (list): Array of resource drift information containing:
    • StackId: Stack identifier
    • LogicalResourceId: Logical name in the template
    • PhysicalResourceId: Actual AWS resource ID
    • ResourceType: AWS resource type (e.g., AWS::EC2::Instance)
    • ExpectedProperties: Expected configuration (JSON)
    • ActualProperties: Actual configuration (JSON)
    • PropertyDifferences: List of changed properties with details
    • StackResourceDriftStatus: IN_SYNC, MODIFIED, DELETED, or NOT_CHECKED
    • Timestamp: When drift was last checked
  • NextToken (string): Token for pagination if more results exist
5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-stack-resource-drifts Examples
Example 1: List All Modified Resources

Get details about all resources that have drifted from the expected configuration.

#!/bin/bash
# List all modified resources in the stack
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource-drifts \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --stack-resource-drift-status-filters MODIFIED \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | jq '.StackResourceDrifts[] | {
    resource: .LogicalResourceId,
    type: .ResourceType,
    status: .StackResourceDriftStatus,
    physical_id: .PhysicalResourceId,
    changes: (.PropertyDifferences | length)
  }'
{ "resource": "DjangoSecurityGroup", "type": "AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup", "status": "MODIFIED", "physical_id": "sg-0abc123def456789", "changes": 2 } { "resource": "DjangoEC2Instance", "type": "AWS::EC2::Instance", "status": "MODIFIED", "physical_id": "i-0123456789abcdef0", "changes": 1 } Total Modified Resources: 2 Total Property Changes: 3
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/list_drifted_resources.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'List all drifted resources in a CloudFormation stack' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = options['stack_name'] # Create drift analysis skills drift_analysis_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Drift Analysis', defaults={'description': 'Analyze infrastructure configuration drift'} )[0] troubleshooting_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Troubleshooting', defaults={'description': 'Diagnose infrastructure issues'} )[0] aws_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='AWS CloudFormation', defaults={'description': 'CloudFormation stack management'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get drift analyst analyst = User.objects.get_or_create( username='drift_analyst', defaults={'email': 'analyst@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author analyst_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=analyst, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure drift analyst'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Analysis center analysis_loc = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Analysis Center', state='Infrastructure', country='Corporate', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Get drifted resources response = cf_client.describe_stack_resource_drifts( StackName=stack_name, StackResourceDriftStatusFilters=['MODIFIED'] ) drifts = response.get('StackResourceDrifts', []) print(f"\nDrifted Resources in {stack_name}:") print("-" * 50) total_changes = 0 for drift in drifts: resource = drift.get('LogicalResourceId') resource_type = drift.get('ResourceType') changes = len(drift.get('PropertyDifferences', [])) total_changes += changes print(f" {resource}") print(f" Type: {resource_type}") print(f" Changes: {changes}") print(f"\nTotal: {len(drifts)} resources, {total_changes} property changes") # Create drift analysis job analysis_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Drift Analysis: {stack_name}', description=f'Modified: {len(drifts)}, Changes: {total_changes}', author=analyst_author, location=analysis_loc, job_type='DRIFT_ANALYSIS', is_active=len(drifts) > 0 ) analysis_post.required_skills.add(drift_analysis_skill, troubleshooting_skill, aws_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Detailed Property Differences

Examine the specific property changes in drifted resources.

#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed property differences
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource-drifts \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --stack-resource-drift-status-filters MODIFIED \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | jq '.StackResourceDrifts[] | {
    resource: .LogicalResourceId,
    differences: .PropertyDifferences[] | {
      path: .PropertyPath,
      expected: .ExpectedValue,
      actual: .ActualValue,
      difference_type: .DifferenceType
    }
  }'
{ "resource": "DjangoSecurityGroup", "differences": { "path": "/SecurityGroupIngress/0/CidrIp", "expected": "10.0.0.0/16", "actual": "0.0.0.0/0", "difference_type": "NOT_EQUAL" } } { "resource": "DjangoSecurityGroup", "differences": { "path": "/SecurityGroupIngress/1", "expected": null, "actual": "{\"IpProtocol\":\"tcp\",\"FromPort\":22,\"ToPort\":22,\"CidrIp\":\"0.0.0.0/0\"}", "difference_type": "ADD" } } Security Issue Detected: ⚠ Ingress rule modified to allow all IPs ⚠ SSH port 22 opened to the world
# anihpj/jobpost/services/drift_property_analyzer.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json class DriftPropertyAnalyzer: def __init__(self, stack_name): self.stack_name = stack_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def analyze_property_differences(self): # Create property analysis skills property_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Property Analysis', defaults={'description': 'Detailed resource property investigation'} )[0] security_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Security Analysis', defaults={'description': 'Security configuration review'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get security analyst sec_analyst = User.objects.get_or_create( username='security_analyst', defaults={'email': 'security-analysis@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author sec_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=sec_analyst, defaults={'bio': 'Security configuration analyst'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Security office sec_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Security Office', state='Security', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Get drifts with property differences response = self.cf_client.describe_stack_resource_drifts( StackName=self.stack_name, StackResourceDriftStatusFilters=['MODIFIED'] ) security_issues = [] drifts = response.get('StackResourceDrifts', []) print(f"\nProperty Differences:") print("-" * 60) for drift in drifts: resource = drift.get('LogicalResourceId') for diff in drift.get('PropertyDifferences', []): path = diff.get('PropertyPath') expected = diff.get('ExpectedValue') actual = diff.get('ActualValue') diff_type = diff.get('DifferenceType') print(f"\n{resource}: {path}") print(f" Expected: {expected}") print(f" Actual: {actual}") print(f" Type: {diff_type}") # Check for security issues if 'SecurityGroup' in resource and '0.0.0.0/0' in str(actual): security_issues.append({ 'resource': resource, 'issue': 'Wide-open access detected' }) # Create property analysis job analysis_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Property Analysis: {self.stack_name}', description=f'Security issues: {len(security_issues)}', author=sec_author, location=sec_location, job_type='PROPERTY_ANALYSIS', is_active=len(security_issues) > 0 ) analysis_post.required_skills.add(property_skill, security_skill) print(f"\nJobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return {'drifts': len(drifts), 'security_issues': security_issues}
Example 3: Export Comprehensive Drift Report

Generate a detailed JSON report of all drift information for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
# Generate comprehensive drift report
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
REPORT_FILE="drift-details-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).json"

# Get all drifts (not just modified)
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource-drifts \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | jq '{
    report_date: now | strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
    stack_name: "'"$STACK_NAME"'",
    summary: {
      total_resources: (.StackResourceDrifts | length),
      in_sync: ([.StackResourceDrifts[] | select(.StackResourceDriftStatus == "IN_SYNC")] | length),
      modified: ([.StackResourceDrifts[] | select(.StackResourceDriftStatus == "MODIFIED")] | length),
      deleted: ([.StackResourceDrifts[] | select(.StackResourceDriftStatus == "DELETED")] | length),
      not_checked: ([.StackResourceDrifts[] | select(.StackResourceDriftStatus == "NOT_CHECKED")] | length)
    },
    resources: .StackResourceDrifts
  }' > $REPORT_FILE

echo "✓ Drift report generated: $REPORT_FILE"
jq '.summary' $REPORT_FILE
✓ Drift report generated: drift-details-20260117-103045.json { "total_resources": 12, "in_sync": 9, "modified": 2, "deleted": 1, "not_checked": 0 } Report includes: - All 12 resources analyzed - 9 resources in sync - 2 resources modified - 1 resource deleted outside CloudFormation - Full property difference details
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Drift report API import json from datetime import datetime from django.http import JsonResponse, HttpResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class DriftReportView(View): def get(self, request, stack_name): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') # Create reporting skills reporting_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Drift Reporting', defaults={'description': 'Generate drift analysis reports'} )[0] documentation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Documentation', defaults={'description': 'Technical documentation'} )[0] export_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Data Export', defaults={'description': 'Export data in various formats'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get users and authors users = User.objects.all() print(f"Users: {users.count()}") authors = Author.objects.all() print(f"Authors: {authors.count()}") # Get locations locations = Location.objects.all() print(f"Locations: {locations.count()}") # Get all resource drifts response = cf_client.describe_stack_resource_drifts(StackName=stack_name) drifts = response.get('StackResourceDrifts', []) # Build summary summary = { 'total_resources': len(drifts), 'in_sync': len([d for d in drifts if d.get('StackResourceDriftStatus') == 'IN_SYNC']), 'modified': len([d for d in drifts if d.get('StackResourceDriftStatus') == 'MODIFIED']), 'deleted': len([d for d in drifts if d.get('StackResourceDriftStatus') == 'DELETED']), 'not_checked': len([d for d in drifts if d.get('StackResourceDriftStatus') == 'NOT_CHECKED']) } report = { 'report_date': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(), 'stack_name': stack_name, 'summary': summary, 'resources': drifts } # Track related jobs related_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains='Drift') print(f"Related jobs: {related_jobs.count()}") # Return as downloadable JSON format_type = request.GET.get('format', 'json') if format_type == 'download': response = HttpResponse( json.dumps(report, indent=2, default=str), content_type='application/json' ) response['Content-Disposition'] = f'attachment; filename="drift-{stack_name}-{datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d")}.json"' return response return JsonResponse(report)
Example 4: Filter by Multiple Drift Statuses

Get resources that are either modified or deleted outside CloudFormation.

#!/bin/bash
# Get all resources that have drifted (modified or deleted)
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

echo "=== Drifted Resources (Modified or Deleted) ==="
echo ""

aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource-drifts \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --stack-resource-drift-status-filters MODIFIED DELETED \
  --output table \
  --region us-east-1 \
  --query 'StackResourceDrifts[*].[LogicalResourceId,ResourceType,StackResourceDriftStatus,Timestamp]'

# Count by status
echo ""
echo "Summary:"
MODIFIED=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource-drifts \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --stack-resource-drift-status-filters MODIFIED \
  --query 'length(StackResourceDrifts)' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

DELETED=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource-drifts \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --stack-resource-drift-status-filters DELETED \
  --query 'length(StackResourceDrifts)' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

echo "  Modified: $MODIFIED"
echo "  Deleted: $DELETED"
echo "  Total Drifted: $((MODIFIED + DELETED))"
=== Drifted Resources (Modified or Deleted) === ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeStackResourceDrifts | +-----------------------+---------------------------+----------+------------------+ | DjangoSecurityGroup | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | MODIFIED | 2026-01-17T10:30 | | DjangoEC2Instance | AWS::EC2::Instance | MODIFIED | 2026-01-17T10:30 | | OldLogGroup | AWS::Logs::LogGroup | DELETED | 2026-01-17T10:30 | +-----------------------+---------------------------+----------+------------------+ Summary: Modified: 2 Deleted: 1 Total Drifted: 3
# anihpj/jobpost/services/drift_status_filter.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class DriftStatusFilter: def __init__(self, stack_name): self.stack_name = stack_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def get_drifted_resources(self, statuses=['MODIFIED', 'DELETED']): # Create filtering skills filter_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Resource Filtering', defaults={'description': 'Filter resources by status'} )[0] categorization_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Drift Categorization', defaults={'description': 'Categorize drift by type'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get categorization user cat_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='drift_categorizer', defaults={'email': 'categorize@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author cat_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=cat_user, defaults={'bio': 'Drift categorization specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Categorization center cat_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Categorization Center', state='Analysis', country='Corporate', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Get drifted resources by status response = self.cf_client.describe_stack_resource_drifts( StackName=self.stack_name, StackResourceDriftStatusFilters=statuses ) drifts = response.get('StackResourceDrifts', []) # Categorize by status by_status = {} for drift in drifts: status = drift.get('StackResourceDriftStatus') if status not in by_status: by_status[status] = [] by_status[status].append({ 'resource': drift.get('LogicalResourceId'), 'type': drift.get('ResourceType'), 'timestamp': drift.get('Timestamp') }) print(f"\nDrifted Resources by Status:") for status, resources in by_status.items(): print(f"\n{status}: {len(resources)}") for r in resources: print(f" - {r['resource']} ({r['type']})") # Create categorization job cat_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Drift Categorization: {self.stack_name}', description=f'Statuses: {list(by_status.keys())}', author=cat_author, location=cat_location, job_type='CATEGORIZATION', is_active=len(drifts) > 0 ) cat_post.required_skills.add(filter_skill, categorization_skill) print(f"\nJobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return by_status
Example 5: Compare Expected vs Actual Configuration

Show side-by-side comparison of expected and actual resource properties.

#!/bin/bash
# Compare expected vs actual configuration
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource-drifts \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --stack-resource-drift-status-filters MODIFIED \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | jq '.StackResourceDrifts[] | {
    resource: .LogicalResourceId,
    type: .ResourceType,
    expected_config: .ExpectedProperties | fromjson,
    actual_config: .ActualProperties | fromjson
  }'

echo ""
echo "Configuration Comparison Complete"
echo "Review differences and determine remediation action:"
echo "  1. Update template to match actual (accept drift)"
echo "  2. Update stack to restore expected (reject drift)"
{ "resource": "DjangoSecurityGroup", "type": "AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup", "expected_config": { "GroupDescription": "Django app security group", "SecurityGroupIngress": [ {"IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 80, "ToPort": 80, "CidrIp": "10.0.0.0/16"}, {"IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 443, "ToPort": 443, "CidrIp": "10.0.0.0/16"} ] }, "actual_config": { "GroupDescription": "Django app security group", "SecurityGroupIngress": [ {"IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 80, "ToPort": 80, "CidrIp": "0.0.0.0/0"}, {"IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 443, "ToPort": 443, "CidrIp": "0.0.0.0/0"}, {"IpProtocol": "tcp", "FromPort": 22, "ToPort": 22, "CidrIp": "0.0.0.0/0"} ] } } Configuration Comparison Complete Review differences and determine remediation action: 1. Update template to match actual (accept drift) 2. Update stack to restore expected (reject drift)
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/compare_configurations.py import json from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Compare expected vs actual resource configurations' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = options['stack_name'] # Create comparison skills comparison_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Configuration Comparison', defaults={'description': 'Compare expected vs actual configs'} )[0] remediation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Drift Remediation', defaults={'description': 'Plan and execute drift fixes'} )[0] decision_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Decision Making', defaults={'description': 'Make informed remediation decisions'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get remediation engineer rem_engineer = User.objects.get_or_create( username='remediation_engineer', defaults={'email': 'remediation@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author rem_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=rem_engineer, defaults={'bio': 'Drift remediation engineer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Remediation center rem_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Remediation Center', state='Operations', country='Corporate', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Get modified resources response = cf_client.describe_stack_resource_drifts( StackName=stack_name, StackResourceDriftStatusFilters=['MODIFIED'] ) drifts = response.get('StackResourceDrifts', []) comparisons = [] print(f"\nConfiguration Comparisons:") print("=" * 60) for drift in drifts: resource = drift.get('LogicalResourceId') expected = json.loads(drift.get('ExpectedProperties', '{}')) actual = json.loads(drift.get('ActualProperties', '{}')) comparison = { 'resource': resource, 'type': drift.get('ResourceType'), 'expected': expected, 'actual': actual } comparisons.append(comparison) print(f"\n{resource}:") print(f" Type: {drift.get('ResourceType')}") print(f" Expected keys: {list(expected.keys())}") print(f" Actual keys: {list(actual.keys())}") print(f"\nTotal comparisons: {len(comparisons)}") print("\nRemediation Options:") print(" 1. Accept drift - update template") print(" 2. Reject drift - update stack") # Create comparison job compare_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Config Comparison: {stack_name}', description=f'Comparisons: {len(comparisons)} resources', author=rem_author, location=rem_location, job_type='COMPARISON', is_active=len(comparisons) > 0 ) compare_post.required_skills.add(comparison_skill, remediation_skill, decision_skill) print(f"\nJobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

⚙️ Stack Operations

continue-update-rollback

Continues rolling back an update after it pauses in UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED state.

Syntax
aws cloudformation continue-update-rollback \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --resources-to-skip {resource-ids}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Name of the stack
  • --resources-to-skip (list): Logical resource IDs to skip during rollback
  • --role-arn (string): IAM role for CloudFormation to assume
Return Value
  • None: Returns empty on success; check stack status for progress
5 Detailed CloudFormation continue-update-rollback Examples
Example 1: Resume Failed Rollback
#!/bin/bash
# Resume rollback after failure
aws cloudformation continue-update-rollback \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --resources-to-skip DjangoInstance

echo "Rollback resumed, skipping problematic resources"
Rollback resumed, skipping problematic resources Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS Skipped: DjangoInstance (will be manually fixed)
