# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/continue_rollback.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Continue a failed rollback' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--skip', nargs='*', default=[]) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') rollback_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Rollback Management', defaults={'description': 'Manage stack rollbacks'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") rollback_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='rollback_mgr', defaults={'email': 'rollback@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") rollback_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=rollback_user, defaults={'bio': 'Rollback manager'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") rollback_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Rollback Center', state='Recovery', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") params = {'StackName': options['stack_name']} if options['skip']: params['ResourcesToSkip'] = options['skip'] cf_client.continue_update_rollback(**params) self.stdout.write("Rollback resumed") rollback_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f"Rollback: {options['stack_name']}", description=f"Skipped: {len(options['skip'])}", author=rollback_author, location=rollback_location, job_type='ROLLBACK', is_active=True ) rollback_job.required_skills.add(rollback_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Skip Multiple Resources
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation continue-update-rollback \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --resources-to-skip DjangoInstance DjangoSecurityGroup DjangoASG
Rollback continuing... Skipping: DjangoInstance, DjangoSecurityGroup, DjangoASG
# anihpj/jobpost/services/multi_skip_rollback.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class MultiSkipRollback: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def continue_with_skips(self, stack_name, resources_to_skip): skip_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Resource Skipping', defaults={'description': 'Skip resources during rollback'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") skip_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='skip_operator', defaults={'email': 'skip@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") skip_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=skip_user, defaults={'bio': 'Skip operator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") skip_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Skip Center', state='Recovery', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") self.cf_client.continue_update_rollback( StackName=stack_name, ResourcesToSkip=resources_to_skip ) skip_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Multi-Skip Rollback', description=f'Skipped: {len(resources_to_skip)}', author=skip_author, location=skip_location, job_type='SKIP_ROLLBACK', is_active=True ) skip_job.required_skills.add(skip_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 3: Rollback with Role
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation continue-update-rollback \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CloudFormationRole
Rollback continuing with role: CloudFormationRole
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Rollback API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class RollbackWithRoleView(View): def post(self, request, stack_name): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') role_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Role-Based Operations', defaults={'description': 'Operations with IAM roles'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") role_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='role_operator', defaults={'email': 'role@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") role_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=role_user, defaults={'bio': 'Role operator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") role_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Role Center', state='IAM', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") role_arn = request.POST.get('role_arn') cf_client.continue_update_rollback( StackName=stack_name, RoleARN=role_arn ) role_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Role Rollback', description=f'Using role', author=role_author, location=role_location, job_type='ROLE_ROLLBACK', is_active=True ) role_job.required_skills.add(role_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({'status': 'rolling_back'})
Example 4: Check Status After Continue
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation continue-update-rollback --stack-name anihpj-django-app

# Wait and check status
sleep 10
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
  --output text
UPDATE_ROLLBACK_COMPLETE
# anihpj/jobpost/services/rollback_monitor.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import time class RollbackMonitor: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def continue_and_monitor(self, stack_name): monitor_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Rollback Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Monitor rollback progress'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") monitor_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='rollback_monitor', defaults={'email': 'monitor@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") monitor_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=monitor_user, defaults={'bio': 'Rollback monitor'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") monitor_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Monitor Center', state='Ops', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") self.cf_client.continue_update_rollback(StackName=stack_name) for _ in range(60): time.sleep(10) response = self.cf_client.describe_stacks(StackName=stack_name) status = response['Stacks'][0]['StackStatus'] if 'COMPLETE' in status or 'FAILED' in status: break monitor_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Rollback Monitor', description=f'Final: {status}', author=monitor_author, location=monitor_location, job_type='MONITOR', is_active=False ) monitor_job.required_skills.add(monitor_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return status
Example 5: Automated Rollback Recovery
#!/bin/bash
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

# Get failed resources
FAILED=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query "StackResources[?ResourceStatus=='UPDATE_FAILED'].LogicalResourceId" \
  --output text)

if [ -n "$FAILED" ]; then
  aws cloudformation continue-update-rollback \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --resources-to-skip $FAILED
fi
Found failed resources: DjangoInstance DjangoASG Continuing rollback, skipping failed resources...
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/auto_rollback.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Automatically continue rollback skipping failed resources' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = options['stack_name'] auto_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Automated Recovery', defaults={'description': 'Auto-recover from failures'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") auto_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='auto_recovery', defaults={'email': 'auto@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") auto_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=auto_user, defaults={'bio': 'Auto recovery'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") auto_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Auto Center', state='Automation', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Find failed resources resources = cf_client.describe_stack_resources(StackName=stack_name) failed = [r['LogicalResourceId'] for r in resources['StackResources'] if r['ResourceStatus'] == 'UPDATE_FAILED'] if failed: cf_client.continue_update_rollback( StackName=stack_name, ResourcesToSkip=failed ) self.stdout.write(f"Skipping: {failed}") auto_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Auto Rollback', description=f'Skipped: {len(failed)}', author=auto_author, location=auto_location, job_type='AUTO_ROLLBACK', is_active=True ) auto_job.required_skills.add(auto_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

cancel-update-stack

Cancels an update on a stack in UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS state, triggering a rollback.

Syntax
aws cloudformation cancel-update-stack --stack-name {stack-name}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Name or ID of the stack to cancel
Return Value
  • None: Returns empty on success; stack enters UPDATE_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS
5 Detailed CloudFormation cancel-update-stack Examples
Example 1: Cancel In-Progress Update
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation cancel-update-stack --stack-name anihpj-django-app
echo "Update canceled, rolling back changes"
Update canceled, rolling back changes Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS Rolling back to previous stable state
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/cancel_update.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Cancel an in-progress stack update' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') cancel_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Update Cancellation', defaults={'description': 'Cancel stack updates'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") canceler = User.objects.get_or_create( username='update_canceler', defaults={'email': 'cancel@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") cancel_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=canceler, defaults={'bio': 'Update canceler'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") cancel_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Cancel Center', state='Recovery', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") cf_client.cancel_update_stack(StackName=options['stack_name']) self.stdout.write("Update canceled") cancel_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f"Cancel: {options['stack_name']}", description='Update canceled, rolling back', author=cancel_author, location=cancel_location, job_type='CANCEL_UPDATE', is_active=True ) cancel_job.required_skills.add(cancel_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Cancel and Monitor
#!/bin/bash
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

aws cloudformation cancel-update-stack --stack-name $STACK_NAME

# Monitor rollback
while true; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' --output text)
  echo "Status: $STATUS"
  [[ "$STATUS" == *"COMPLETE"* ]] && break
  sleep 10
done
Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_COMPLETE
# anihpj/jobpost/services/cancel_monitor.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import time class CancelMonitor: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def cancel_and_wait(self, stack_name): monitor_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Cancel Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Monitor cancellation'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") monitor_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='cancel_monitor', defaults={'email': 'cancelmon@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") monitor_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=monitor_user, defaults={'bio': 'Cancel monitor'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") monitor_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Monitor Center', state='Ops', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") self.cf_client.cancel_update_stack(StackName=stack_name) for _ in range(60): time.sleep(10) response = self.cf_client.describe_stacks(StackName=stack_name) status = response['Stacks'][0]['StackStatus'] print(f"Status: {status}") if 'COMPLETE' in status: break monitor_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Cancel Complete', description=f'Final: {status}', author=monitor_author, location=monitor_location, job_type='CANCEL_MONITOR', is_active=False ) monitor_job.required_skills.add(monitor_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return status
Example 3: Emergency Cancel Script
#!/bin/bash
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

# Check if update in progress
STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' --output text)

if [ "$STATUS" = "UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS" ]; then
  echo "Canceling update..."
  aws cloudformation cancel-update-stack --stack-name $STACK_NAME
else
  echo "No update in progress ($STATUS)"
fi
Canceling update... Update canceled successfully
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Emergency cancel API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class EmergencyCancelView(View): def post(self, request, stack_name): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') emergency_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Emergency Operations', defaults={'description': 'Emergency interventions'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") emergency_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='emergency_ops', defaults={'email': 'emergency@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") emergency_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=emergency_user, defaults={'bio': 'Emergency operator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") emergency_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Emergency Center', state='Crisis', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.describe_stacks(StackName=stack_name) status = response['Stacks'][0]['StackStatus'] if status == 'UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS': cf_client.cancel_update_stack(StackName=stack_name) message = 'Update canceled' else: message = f'No update in progress ({status})' emergency_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Emergency Cancel', description=message, author=emergency_author, location=emergency_location, job_type='EMERGENCY', is_active=True ) emergency_job.required_skills.add(emergency_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({'status': message})
Example 4: Cancel Multiple Stacks
#!/bin/bash
for STACK in anihpj-app anihpj-api anihpj-worker; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --stack-name $STACK \
    --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' --output text 2>/dev/null)
  
  if [ "$STATUS" = "UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS" ]; then
    aws cloudformation cancel-update-stack --stack-name $STACK
    echo "Canceled: $STACK"
  fi
done
Canceled: anihpj-app Canceled: anihpj-api
# anihpj/jobpost/services/batch_cancel.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class BatchCancel: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def cancel_all_in_progress(self, stack_names): batch_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Batch Cancellation', defaults={'description': 'Cancel multiple stacks'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") batch_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='batch_cancel', defaults={'email': 'batch@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") batch_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=batch_user, defaults={'bio': 'Batch operator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") batch_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Batch Center', state='Bulk Ops', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") canceled = [] for stack_name in stack_names: try: response = self.cf_client.describe_stacks(StackName=stack_name) if response['Stacks'][0]['StackStatus'] == 'UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS': self.cf_client.cancel_update_stack(StackName=stack_name) canceled.append(stack_name) except Exception: pass batch_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Batch Cancel', description=f'Canceled: {len(canceled)}', author=batch_author, location=batch_location, job_type='BATCH_CANCEL', is_active=False ) batch_job.required_skills.add(batch_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return canceled
Example 5: Cancel with Notification
#!/bin/bash
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
SNS_TOPIC="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:ops-alerts"

aws cloudformation cancel-update-stack --stack-name $STACK_NAME

aws sns publish \
  --topic-arn $SNS_TOPIC \
  --subject "Stack Update Canceled" \
  --message "Update for $STACK_NAME was canceled and is rolling back"
Update canceled Notification sent to ops-alerts
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/cancel_notify.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Cancel update and notify' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--sns-topic', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') sns_client = boto3.client('sns', region_name='us-east-1') notify_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Cancel Notification', defaults={'description': 'Notify on cancellation'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") notifier = User.objects.get_or_create( username='cancel_notifier', defaults={'email': 'notify@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") notify_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=notifier, defaults={'bio': 'Cancel notifier'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") notify_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Notify Center', state='Alerts', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") cf_client.cancel_update_stack(StackName=options['stack_name']) sns_client.publish( TopicArn=options['sns_topic'], Subject='Stack Update Canceled', Message=f"Update for {options['stack_name']} canceled" ) notify_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Cancel + Notify', description='Canceled and notified', author=notify_author, location=notify_location, job_type='CANCEL_NOTIFY', is_active=False ) notify_job.required_skills.add(notify_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

set-stack-policy

Sets a stack policy that protects resources from being updated or deleted.

Syntax
aws cloudformation set-stack-policy \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --stack-policy-body file://policy.json
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Name of the stack to protect
  • --stack-policy-body (string): Policy document as JSON string or file
  • --stack-policy-url (string): S3 URL of the policy document
Return Value
  • None: Returns empty on success
5 Detailed CloudFormation set-stack-policy Examples
Example 1: Protect Database from Deletion
#!/bin/bash
cat > stack-policy.json <<'EOF'
{
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Deny",
      "Action": "Update:Delete",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "LogicalResourceId/JobPostDatabase"
    },
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": "Update:*",
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "*"
    }
  ]
}
EOF

aws cloudformation set-stack-policy \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --stack-policy-body file://stack-policy.json

echo "✓ Database protected from deletion"
✓ Database protected from deletion Policy applied to: anihpj-django-app Protected resources: JobPostDatabase Action: Update:Delete now denied
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/set_stack_policy.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Set stack policy to protect resources' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--protect', nargs='+', required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') policy_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Stack Policy', defaults={'description': 'Manage stack policies'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") policy_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='policy_admin', defaults={'email': 'policy@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") policy_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=policy_user, defaults={'bio': 'Policy administrator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") policy_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Policy Center', state='Governance', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") statements = [{'Effect': 'Deny', 'Action': 'Update:Delete', 'Principal': '*', 'Resource': f'LogicalResourceId/{r}'} for r in options['protect']] statements.append({'Effect': 'Allow', 'Action': 'Update:*', 'Principal': '*', 'Resource': '*'}) policy = json.dumps({'Statement': statements}) cf_client.set_stack_policy(StackName=options['stack_name'], StackPolicyBody=policy) policy_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f"Policy: {options['stack_name']}", description=f"Protected: {len(options['protect'])}", author=policy_author, location=policy_location, job_type='SET_POLICY', is_active=False ) policy_job.required_skills.add(policy_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Protect Multiple Resources
#!/bin/bash
cat > multi-policy.json <<'EOF'
{
  "Statement": [
    {"Effect": "Deny", "Action": "Update:*", "Principal": "*", "Resource": "LogicalResourceId/JobPostDatabase"},
    {"Effect": "Deny", "Action": "Update:*", "Principal": "*", "Resource": "LogicalResourceId/UserData"},
    {"Effect": "Allow", "Action": "Update:*", "Principal": "*", "Resource": "*"}
  ]
}
EOF

aws cloudformation set-stack-policy \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --stack-policy-body file://multi-policy.json
Policy set with multiple protections: - JobPostDatabase: Fully protected - UserData: Fully protected
# anihpj/jobpost/services/multi_protect.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json class MultiProtect: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def protect_resources(self, stack_name, resources): multi_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-Resource Protection', defaults={'description': 'Protect multiple resources'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") multi_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='multi_protector', defaults={'email': 'protect@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") multi_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=multi_user, defaults={'bio': 'Multi-resource protector'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") multi_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Protect Center', state='Security', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") statements = [{'Effect': 'Deny', 'Action': 'Update:*', 'Principal': '*', 'Resource': f'LogicalResourceId/{r}'} for r in resources] statements.append({'Effect': 'Allow', 'Action': 'Update:*', 'Principal': '*', 'Resource': '*'}) self.cf_client.set_stack_policy( StackName=stack_name, StackPolicyBody=json.dumps({'Statement': statements}) ) multi_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Multi-Protect', description=f'Resources: {len(resources)}', author=multi_author, location=multi_location, job_type='MULTI_PROTECT', is_active=False ) multi_job.required_skills.add(multi_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 3: Conditional Protection
#!/bin/bash
cat > conditional-policy.json <<'EOF'
{
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Deny",
      "Action": ["Update:Replace", "Update:Delete"],
      "Principal": "*",
      "Resource": "*",
      "Condition": {
        "StringEquals": {"ResourceType": ["AWS::RDS::DBInstance"]}
      }
    },
    {"Effect": "Allow", "Action": "Update:*", "Principal": "*", "Resource": "*"}
  ]
}
EOF

aws cloudformation set-stack-policy \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --stack-policy-body file://conditional-policy.json
Conditional policy applied: All RDS instances protected from Replace/Delete
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Conditional policy API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json class ConditionalPolicyView(View): def post(self, request, stack_name): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') cond_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Conditional Policies', defaults={'description': 'Apply conditional policies'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") cond_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='cond_policy', defaults={'email': 'conditional@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") cond_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=cond_user, defaults={'bio': 'Conditional policy admin'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") cond_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Conditional Center', state='Advanced', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") resource_types = request.POST.getlist('resource_types') policy = { 'Statement': [ {'Effect': 'Deny', 'Action': ['Update:Replace', 'Update:Delete'], 'Principal': '*', 'Resource': '*', 'Condition': {'StringEquals': {'ResourceType': resource_types}}}, {'Effect': 'Allow', 'Action': 'Update:*', 'Principal': '*', 'Resource': '*'} ] } cf_client.set_stack_policy(StackName=stack_name, StackPolicyBody=json.dumps(policy)) cond_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Conditional Policy', description=f'Types: {len(resource_types)}', author=cond_author, location=cond_location, job_type='CONDITIONAL', is_active=False ) cond_job.required_skills.add(cond_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({'status': 'applied'})
Example 4: Policy from S3
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation set-stack-policy \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --stack-policy-url https://s3.amazonaws.com/anihpj-policies/stack-policy.json
Policy loaded from S3 and applied
# anihpj/jobpost/services/s3_policy.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class S3PolicyLoader: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def apply_from_s3(self, stack_name, s3_url): s3_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='S3 Policy Management', defaults={'description': 'Load policies from S3'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") s3_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='s3_policy', defaults={'email': 's3policy@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") s3_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=s3_user, defaults={'bio': 'S3 policy loader'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") s3_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='S3 Center', state='Storage', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") self.cf_client.set_stack_policy(StackName=stack_name, StackPolicyURL=s3_url) s3_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='S3 Policy', description=f'Loaded from S3', author=s3_author, location=s3_location, job_type='S3_POLICY', is_active=False ) s3_job.required_skills.add(s3_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 5: Clear Stack Policy
#!/bin/bash
# Allow all updates (remove protection)
cat > allow-all.json <<'EOF'
{
  "Statement": [
    {"Effect": "Allow", "Action": "Update:*", "Principal": "*", "Resource": "*"}
  ]
}
EOF

aws cloudformation set-stack-policy \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --stack-policy-body file://allow-all.json

echo "Policy cleared - all updates allowed"
Policy cleared - all updates allowed All resources now unprotected
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/clear_policy.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Clear stack policy to allow all updates' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') clear_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Policy Clearing', defaults={'description': 'Clear stack policies'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") clearer = User.objects.get_or_create( username='policy_clearer', defaults={'email': 'clear@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") clear_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=clearer, defaults={'bio': 'Policy clearer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") clear_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Clear Center', state='Admin', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") allow_all = {'Statement': [{'Effect': 'Allow', 'Action': 'Update:*', 'Principal': '*', 'Resource': '*'}]} cf_client.set_stack_policy(StackName=options['stack_name'], StackPolicyBody=json.dumps(allow_all)) clear_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f"Clear: {options['stack_name']}", description='Policy cleared', author=clear_author, location=clear_location, job_type='CLEAR_POLICY', is_active=False ) clear_job.required_skills.add(clear_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

get-stack-policy

Returns the stack policy for a specified stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation get-stack-policy --stack-name {stack-name}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Name of the stack
Return Value
  • StackPolicyBody (string): JSON policy document
5 Detailed CloudFormation get-stack-policy Examples
Example 1: View Stack Policy
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation get-stack-policy \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackPolicyBody' \
  --output text | jq
{ "Statement": [ { "Effect": "Deny", "Action": "Update:Delete", "Principal": "*", "Resource": "LogicalResourceId/JobPostDatabase" } ] } Policy Summary: - JobPostDatabase: Protected from deletion - All other resources: Unrestricted
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/get_stack_policy.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Get and display stack policy' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') get_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Policy Retrieval', defaults={'description': 'Retrieve stack policies'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") getter = User.objects.get_or_create( username='policy_getter', defaults={'email': 'getpolicy@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") get_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=getter, defaults={'bio': 'Policy retriever'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") get_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Policy Center', state='Review', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.get_stack_policy(StackName=options['stack_name']) policy = response.get('StackPolicyBody') if policy: parsed = json.loads(policy) self.stdout.write(json.dumps(parsed, indent=2)) else: self.stdout.write("No policy set") get_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f"Get Policy: {options['stack_name']}", description='Policy retrieved', author=get_author, location=get_location, job_type='GET_POLICY', is_active=False ) get_job.required_skills.add(get_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Check Protected Resources
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation get-stack-policy --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackPolicyBody' --output text | \
  jq '.Statement[] | select(.Effect == "Deny") | .Resource'
"LogicalResourceId/JobPostDatabase" "LogicalResourceId/UserData"
# anihpj/jobpost/services/policy_analyzer.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json class PolicyAnalyzer: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def get_protected_resources(self, stack_name): analyze_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Policy Analysis', defaults={'description': 'Analyze stack policies'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") analyzer = User.objects.get_or_create( username='policy_analyzer', defaults={'email': 'analyze@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") analyze_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=analyzer, defaults={'bio': 'Policy analyzer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") analyze_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Analyze Center', state='Review', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = self.cf_client.get_stack_policy(StackName=stack_name) policy = json.loads(response.get('StackPolicyBody', '{}')) protected = [s['Resource'] for s in policy.get('Statement', []) if s.get('Effect') == 'Deny'] analyze_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Policy Analysis', description=f'Protected: {len(protected)}', author=analyze_author, location=analyze_location, job_type='ANALYZE_POLICY', is_active=False ) analyze_job.required_skills.add(analyze_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return protected
Example 3: Save Policy to File
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation get-stack-policy \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackPolicyBody' \
  --output text > policy-backup.json

echo "Policy saved to policy-backup.json"
Policy saved to policy-backup.json
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Policy backup API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from django.conf import settings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json import os class PolicyBackupView(View): def get(self, request, stack_name): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') backup_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Policy Backup', defaults={'description': 'Backup stack policies'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") backup_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='policy_backup', defaults={'email': 'backup@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") backup_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=backup_user, defaults={'bio': 'Policy backup'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") backup_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Backup Center', state='DR', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.get_stack_policy(StackName=stack_name) policy = response.get('StackPolicyBody') if policy: filepath = os.path.join(settings.BASE_DIR, 'backups', f'{stack_name}-policy.json') os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(filepath), exist_ok=True) with open(filepath, 'w') as f: f.write(policy) backup_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Policy Backup', description='Policy backed up', author=backup_author, location=backup_location, job_type='BACKUP_POLICY', is_active=False ) backup_job.required_skills.add(backup_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({'backed_up': bool(policy)})
Example 4: Compare Policies
#!/bin/bash
for STACK in anihpj-app anihpj-api; do
  echo "=== $STACK ==="
  aws cloudformation get-stack-policy --stack-name $STACK \
    --query 'StackPolicyBody' --output text 2>/dev/null || echo "No policy"
done
=== anihpj-app === {"Statement":[{"Effect":"Deny"...}]} === anihpj-api === No policy
# anihpj/jobpost/services/policy_comparer.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class PolicyComparer: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def compare_policies(self, stack_names): compare_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Policy Comparison', defaults={'description': 'Compare stack policies'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") comparer = User.objects.get_or_create( username='policy_comparer', defaults={'email': 'compare@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") compare_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=comparer, defaults={'bio': 'Policy comparer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") compare_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Compare Center', state='Analysis', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") results = {} for stack in stack_names: try: response = self.cf_client.get_stack_policy(StackName=stack) results[stack] = response.get('StackPolicyBody') except Exception: results[stack] = None compare_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Policy Compare', description=f'Compared: {len(stack_names)}', author=compare_author, location=compare_location, job_type='COMPARE_POLICY', is_active=False ) compare_job.required_skills.add(compare_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return results
Example 5: Policy Audit Report
#!/bin/bash
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

echo "Stack Policy Audit: $STACK_NAME"
echo "================================"

POLICY=$(aws cloudformation get-stack-policy \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'StackPolicyBody' --output text)

if [ -n "$POLICY" ]; then
  echo "Protected resources:"
  echo "$POLICY" | jq -r '.Statement[] | select(.Effect=="Deny") | "  - \(.Resource)"'
else
  echo "⚠️ No policy - stack unprotected"
fi
Stack Policy Audit: anihpj-django-app ================================ Protected resources: - LogicalResourceId/JobPostDatabase - LogicalResourceId/UserData
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/policy_audit.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Audit stack policy' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') audit_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Policy Audit', defaults={'description': 'Audit stack policies'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") auditor = User.objects.get_or_create( username='policy_auditor', defaults={'email': 'audit@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") audit_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=auditor, defaults={'bio': 'Policy auditor'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") audit_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Audit Center', state='Compliance', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.get_stack_policy(StackName=options['stack_name']) policy = response.get('StackPolicyBody') self.stdout.write(f"Stack Policy Audit: {options['stack_name']}") if policy: parsed = json.loads(policy) for stmt in parsed.get('Statement', []): if stmt.get('Effect') == 'Deny': self.stdout.write(f" Protected: {stmt.get('Resource')}") else: self.stdout.write("⚠️ No policy - stack unprotected") audit_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Policy Audit', description=f"Audited: {options['stack_name']}", author=audit_author, location=audit_location, job_type='AUDIT_POLICY', is_active=False ) audit_job.required_skills.add(audit_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

estimate-template-cost

Returns URL to AWS Simple Monthly Calculator with pre-populated template resources.

Syntax
aws cloudformation estimate-template-cost \
  --template-body file://template.yaml \
  --parameters ParameterKey={key},ParameterValue={value}
Parameters Description
  • --template-body (string): Template content or file path
  • --template-url (string): S3 URL of the template
  • --parameters (list): Parameter values for the template
Return Value
  • Url (string): URL to AWS Pricing Calculator with pre-filled data
5 Detailed CloudFormation estimate-template-cost Examples
Example 1: Estimate Infrastructure Cost
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation estimate-template-cost \
  --template-body file://django-infrastructure.yaml \
  --parameters ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.medium \
  --query 'Url' \
  --output text
https://calculator.aws/#/estimate?id=abc123def456 Cost Estimation URL generated Opens AWS Pricing Calculator with: - EC2 t3.medium instance - RDS db.t3.micro - VPC and networking - Estimated monthly cost: ~$85-$120
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/estimate_cost.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Estimate template deployment cost' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--template', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') cost_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Cost Estimation', defaults={'description': 'Estimate infrastructure costs'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") estimator = User.objects.get_or_create( username='cost_estimator', defaults={'email': 'cost@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") cost_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=estimator, defaults={'bio': 'Cost estimator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") cost_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Cost Center', state='Finance', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") with open(options['template']) as f: template = f.read() response = cf_client.estimate_template_cost(TemplateBody=template) self.stdout.write(f"Calculator URL: {response['Url']}") cost_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Cost Estimate', description='Calculator URL generated', author=cost_author, location=cost_location, job_type='COST_ESTIMATE', is_active=False ) cost_job.required_skills.add(cost_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Estimate with Parameters
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation estimate-template-cost \
  --template-body file://django-app.yaml \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.xlarge \
    ParameterKey=DBInstanceClass,ParameterValue=db.r5.large \
    ParameterKey=Environment,ParameterValue=production
{ "Url": "https://calculator.aws/#/estimate?id=xyz789" } Production-grade estimate with larger instances
# anihpj/jobpost/services/param_cost_estimator.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ParamCostEstimator: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def estimate_with_params(self, template_path, parameters): param_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Parameter Cost Analysis', defaults={'description': 'Cost analysis with parameters'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") param_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='param_cost', defaults={'email': 'paramcost@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") param_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=param_user, defaults={'bio': 'Parameter cost analyst'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") param_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Param Cost', state='Finance', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") with open(template_path) as f: template = f.read() params = [{'ParameterKey': k, 'ParameterValue': v} for k, v in parameters.items()] response = self.cf_client.estimate_template_cost(TemplateBody=template, Parameters=params) param_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Param Cost Estimate', description=f'Parameters: {len(params)}', author=param_author, location=param_location, job_type='PARAM_COST', is_active=False ) param_job.required_skills.add(param_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return response['Url']
Example 3: Estimate from S3
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation estimate-template-cost \
  --template-url https://s3.amazonaws.com/anihpj-templates/production.yaml \
  --query 'Url' \
  --output text
https://calculator.aws/#/estimate?id=s3template123
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - S3 cost estimate API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class S3CostEstimateView(View): def get(self, request): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') s3_cost_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='S3 Template Cost', defaults={'description': 'Estimate costs from S3 templates'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") s3_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='s3_cost', defaults={'email': 's3cost@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") s3_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=s3_user, defaults={'bio': 'S3 cost estimator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") s3_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='S3 Cost', state='Finance', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") template_url = request.GET.get('template_url') response = cf_client.estimate_template_cost(TemplateURL=template_url) s3_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='S3 Cost Estimate', description='From S3 template', author=s3_author, location=s3_location, job_type='S3_COST', is_active=False ) s3_job.required_skills.add(s3_cost_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({'calculator_url': response['Url']})
Example 4: Compare Environment Costs
#!/bin/bash
for ENV in dev staging prod; do
  echo "=== $ENV environment ==="
  aws cloudformation estimate-template-cost \
    --template-body file://django-app.yaml \
    --parameters ParameterKey=Environment,ParameterValue=$ENV \
    --query 'Url' --output text
done
=== dev environment === https://calculator.aws/#/estimate?id=dev123 === staging environment === https://calculator.aws/#/estimate?id=staging456 === prod environment === https://calculator.aws/#/estimate?id=prod789
# anihpj/jobpost/services/env_cost_compare.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class EnvCostCompare: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def compare_environments(self, template_path, environments): compare_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Environment Cost Compare', defaults={'description': 'Compare costs by env'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") compare_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='env_compare', defaults={'email': 'envcompare@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") compare_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=compare_user, defaults={'bio': 'Environment cost comparer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") compare_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Compare Cost', state='Finance', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") with open(template_path) as f: template = f.read() results = {} for env in environments: response = self.cf_client.estimate_template_cost( TemplateBody=template, Parameters=[{'ParameterKey': 'Environment', 'ParameterValue': env}] ) results[env] = response['Url'] compare_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Env Cost Compare', description=f'Envs: {len(environments)}', author=compare_author, location=compare_location, job_type='ENV_COMPARE', is_active=False ) compare_job.required_skills.add(compare_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return results
Example 5: Cost Report Generation
#!/bin/bash
TEMPLATE="django-infrastructure.yaml"
URL=$(aws cloudformation estimate-template-cost \
  --template-body file://$TEMPLATE \
  --query 'Url' --output text)

echo "Cost Estimation Report"
echo "======================"
echo "Template: $TEMPLATE"
echo "Calculator: $URL"
echo "Generated: $(date)"
Cost Estimation Report ====================== Template: django-infrastructure.yaml Calculator: https://calculator.aws/#/estimate?id=report123 Generated: Mon Jan 15 10:30:00 UTC 2024
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/cost_report.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 from datetime import datetime class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Generate cost estimation report' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--template', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') report_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Cost Reporting', defaults={'description': 'Generate cost reports'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") reporter = User.objects.get_or_create( username='cost_reporter', defaults={'email': 'costreport@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") report_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=reporter, defaults={'bio': 'Cost reporter'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") report_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Report Center', state='Finance', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") with open(options['template']) as f: template = f.read() response = cf_client.estimate_template_cost(TemplateBody=template) self.stdout.write("Cost Estimation Report") self.stdout.write("=" * 22) self.stdout.write(f"Template: {options['template']}") self.stdout.write(f"Calculator: {response['Url']}") self.stdout.write(f"Generated: {datetime.now()}") report_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Cost Report', description=f'Template: {options["template"]}', author=report_author, location=report_location, job_type='COST_REPORT', is_active=False ) report_job.required_skills.add(report_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

describe-stack-events

Returns all stack-related events for a specified stack in reverse chronological order. This is essential for monitoring Django deployment progress, troubleshooting failed resources, and understanding the sequence of infrastructure changes in your anihpj jobpost application stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --next-token {pagination-token} \
  --region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Name or unique stack ID of the stack to retrieve events for. Example: anihpj-django-app
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token from a previous response for retrieving additional events
  • --region (string): AWS region where the stack is located. Example: us-east-1
Return Value
  • StackEvents (list): Array of stack event objects containing:
    • StackId: Unique identifier of the stack
    • EventId: Unique identifier for this event
    • StackName: Name of the stack
    • LogicalResourceId: Logical name of the resource
    • PhysicalResourceId: Physical name/ID of the resource
    • ResourceType: Type of AWS resource (AWS::EC2::Instance, etc.)
    • Timestamp: Time when the event occurred
    • ResourceStatus: Current status (CREATE_IN_PROGRESS, CREATE_COMPLETE, etc.)
    • ResourceStatusReason: Success message or error details
  • NextToken (string): Token for pagination if more events exist
5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-stack-events Examples
Example 1: Monitor Django Stack Creation Progress

Track real-time deployment progress of the anihpj Django application stack and display recent events.

#!/bin/bash
# Monitor Django stack creation events
aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackEvents[0:15].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,ResourceType,LogicalResourceId,ResourceStatusReason]' \
  --output table \
  --region us-east-1
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeStackEvents - anihpj-django-app | +------------------------+-------------------+---------------------------+------------------+-----------+ | 2026-01-17T10:45:30Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | AWS::EC2::Instance | DjangoInstance | - | | 2026-01-17T10:43:15Z | CREATE_IN_PROGRESS| AWS::EC2::Instance | DjangoInstance | Resource creation Initiated | | 2026-01-17T10:43:00Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | DjangoSG | - | | 2026-01-17T10:42:45Z | CREATE_IN_PROGRESS| AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | DjangoSG | Resource creation Initiated | | 2026-01-17T10:42:30Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | JobPostDatabase | - | | 2026-01-17T10:35:00Z | CREATE_IN_PROGRESS| AWS::RDS::DBInstance | JobPostDatabase | Resource creation Initiated | | 2026-01-17T10:34:45Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | AWS::EC2::Subnet | PublicSubnet | - | | 2026-01-17T10:34:30Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | AWS::EC2::VPC | VPC | - | | 2026-01-17T10:34:00Z | CREATE_IN_PROGRESS| AWS::CloudFormation::Stack| anihpj-django-app| User Initiated | +------------------------+-------------------+---------------------------+------------------+-----------+ Stack Progress: 9 of 12 resources complete (75%)
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/monitor_stack_events.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Monitor CloudFormation stack events and log deployment progress' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, default='anihpj-django-app') def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = options['stack_name'] # Get stack events response = cf_client.describe_stack_events(StackName=stack_name) # Log to Django models for tracking admin_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='deployment_monitor', defaults={'email': 'deploy@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=admin_user, defaults={'bio': 'Automated deployment monitoring system'} )[0] # Track deployment skills aws_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='AWS CloudFormation', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure as Code deployment'} )[0] # Create deployment tracking job post deployment_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='AWS Cloud', state='us-east-1', country='USA', remote=True )[0] events = response.get('StackEvents', []) for event in events[:10]: print(f"Timestamp: {event['Timestamp']}") print(f"Resource: {event['LogicalResourceId']}") print(f"Type: {event['ResourceType']}") print(f"Status: {event['ResourceStatus']}") if 'ResourceStatusReason' in event: print(f"Reason: {event['ResourceStatusReason']}") print("-" * 50) # Create job post for deployment tracking job_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Deployment Monitor: {stack_name}', description=f'Tracking {len(events)} stack events', author=author, location=deployment_location, job_type='CONTRACT', salary_min=0, salary_max=0, is_active=True ) job_post.required_skills.add(aws_skill) print(f"\n✓ Logged {len(events)} events to JobPost #{job_post.id}")
Example 2: Filter Events by Resource Type

View only EC2-related events to troubleshoot Django server deployment issues.

#!/bin/bash
# Filter EC2-specific events
aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackEvents[?contains(ResourceType, `EC2`)].[Timestamp,LogicalResourceId,ResourceStatus,ResourceStatusReason]' \
  --output table \
  --region us-east-1
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | EC2 Resource Events - anihpj-django-app | +------------------------+------------------+-------------------+------------------+ | 2026-01-17T10:45:30Z | DjangoInstance | CREATE_COMPLETE | - | | 2026-01-17T10:43:15Z | DjangoInstance | CREATE_IN_PROGRESS| Resource creation| | 2026-01-17T10:43:00Z | DjangoSG | CREATE_COMPLETE | - | | 2026-01-17T10:42:45Z | DjangoSG | CREATE_IN_PROGRESS| Resource creation| | 2026-01-17T10:42:30Z | PublicSubnet | CREATE_COMPLETE | - | | 2026-01-17T10:42:15Z | VPC | CREATE_COMPLETE | - | +------------------------+------------------+-------------------+------------------+ EC2 Resources: 6 events found All EC2 resources deployed successfully
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - EC2 event filtering view from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class EC2EventFilterView(View): def get(self, request, stack_name): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') response = cf_client.describe_stack_events(StackName=stack_name) events = response.get('StackEvents', []) # Filter EC2 events ec2_events = [e for e in events if 'EC2' in e.get('ResourceType', '')] # Log skill usage ec2_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Amazon EC2', defaults={'description': 'Elastic Compute Cloud instances'} )[0] print(f"Skills tracked: {Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains='EC2').count()}") print(f"EC2 events found: {len(ec2_events)}") # Track users monitoring EC2 monitoring_users = User.objects.filter(is_staff=True) print(f"Staff users with access: {monitoring_users.count()}") # Get deployment authors authors = Author.objects.select_related('user').all() print(f"Authors tracking deployments: {authors.count()}") # EC2 deployment locations aws_locations = Location.objects.filter(city='AWS Cloud') print(f"AWS deployment locations: {aws_locations.count()}") # Related job posts ec2_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(required_skills=ec2_skill) print(f"EC2-related job posts: {ec2_jobs.count()}") return JsonResponse({ 'ec2_events': len(ec2_events), 'total_events': len(events), 'resource_types': list(set(e['ResourceType'] for e in ec2_events)) })
Example 3: Identify Failed Resources

Find and diagnose failed resource creation events to troubleshoot deployment issues.

#!/bin/bash
# Find failed events
aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackEvents[?contains(ResourceStatus, `FAILED`)].[Timestamp,LogicalResourceId,ResourceType,ResourceStatusReason]' \
  --output table \
  --region us-east-1
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | Failed Events - anihpj-django-app | +------------------------+------------------+------------------------+---------------------+ | 2026-01-17T10:48:30Z | RDSInstance | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | Cannot create DB instance. | | | | | Insufficient capacity | | 2026-01-17T10:47:15Z | LambdaFunction | AWS::Lambda::Function | Resource handler returned| | | | | message: "Role does not | | | | | have permission" | +------------------------+------------------+------------------------+---------------------+ Failed Resources: 2 Action Required: Review IAM permissions and RDS instance availability
# anihpj/jobpost/services/deployment_alerts.py from django.core.mail import send_mail from django.conf import settings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class DeploymentAlertService: def __init__(self, stack_name): self.stack_name = stack_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def check_failed_events(self): response = self.cf_client.describe_stack_events(StackName=self.stack_name) events = response.get('StackEvents', []) failed_events = [e for e in events if 'FAILED' in e.get('ResourceStatus', '')] if failed_events: # Get DevOps users to alert devops_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='DevOps', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure and deployment management'} )[0] # Find staff users with DevOps skills devops_users = User.objects.filter( is_staff=True, email__isnull=False ) print(f"DevOps users to alert: {devops_users.count()}") # Get related authors authors = Author.objects.filter(user__in=devops_users) print(f"Authors with DevOps access: {authors.count()}") # Create incident location incident_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='AWS Incident', state='us-east-1', country='USA', remote=True )[0] print(f"Incident location: {incident_location.city}") # Create incident job post for tracking for event in failed_events: print(f"FAILED: {event['LogicalResourceId']}") print(f"Reason: {event.get('ResourceStatusReason', 'Unknown')}") # Log all skills for debugging all_skills = Skills.objects.all() print(f"Total skills in database: {all_skills.count()}") # Track incident in JobPost incident_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'INCIDENT: {self.stack_name} deployment failure', description=f'Failed resources: {len(failed_events)}', author=authors.first() if authors.exists() else None, location=incident_location, job_type='URGENT', is_active=True ) incident_post.required_skills.add(devops_skill) print(f"Incident tracked: JobPost #{incident_post.id}") return failed_events
Example 4: Export Events to JSON for Analysis

Export all stack events to a JSON file for detailed analysis and reporting.

#!/bin/bash
# Export events to JSON file
aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 > stack-events-$(date +%Y%m%d).json

echo "✓ Events exported to stack-events-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

# Show summary
cat stack-events-$(date +%Y%m%d).json | jq '.StackEvents | length'
cat stack-events-$(date +%Y%m%d).json | jq '.StackEvents | group_by(.ResourceStatus) | map({status: .[0].ResourceStatus, count: length})'
✓ Events exported to stack-events-20260117.json Event count: 47 [ {"status": "CREATE_COMPLETE", "count": 15}, {"status": "CREATE_IN_PROGRESS", "count": 15}, {"status": "UPDATE_COMPLETE", "count": 8}, {"status": "UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS", "count": 8}, {"status": "DELETE_COMPLETE", "count": 1} ] File size: 156 KB Ready for analysis
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/export_events.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json from datetime import datetime class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Export CloudFormation events and create analysis report' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = 'anihpj-django-app' response = cf_client.describe_stack_events(StackName=stack_name) events = response.get('StackEvents', []) # Export to JSON filename = f'stack-events-{datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d")}.json' # Group events by status status_counts = {} for event in events: status = event['ResourceStatus'] status_counts[status] = status_counts.get(status, 0) + 1 # Create report skill reporting_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='AWS Reporting', defaults={'description': 'CloudFormation event analysis and reporting'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()} total") # Get report author admin = User.objects.filter(is_superuser=True).first() print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()} total") if admin: author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=admin, defaults={'bio': 'System administrator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()} total") # Report location report_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Analytics Center', state='Cloud', country='USA', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()} total") # Print status summary print("\nEvent Status Summary:") for status, count in status_counts.items(): print(f" {status}: {count}") # Create report job post report_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Stack Event Report: {filename}', description=f'Total events: {len(events)}', author=author if admin else None, location=report_location, job_type='REPORT', is_active=False ) report_post.required_skills.add(reporting_skill) print(f"\nJobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()} total") print(f"Report saved as JobPost #{report_post.id}")
Example 5: Real-time Event Monitoring Script

Create a continuous monitoring script that watches for new stack events and alerts on status changes.

#!/bin/bash
# Real-time stack event monitor
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
LAST_EVENT_ID=""

while true; do
    # Get latest events
    EVENTS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
        --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
        --query 'StackEvents[0:5]' \
        --output json \
        --region us-east-1)
    
    CURRENT_EVENT_ID=$(echo $EVENTS | jq -r '.[0].EventId')
    
    if [ "$CURRENT_EVENT_ID" != "$LAST_EVENT_ID" ]; then
        echo "$(date '+%H:%M:%S') - New event detected:"
        echo $EVENTS | jq -r '.[] | "\(.Timestamp) | \(.ResourceStatus) | \(.LogicalResourceId)"'
        LAST_EVENT_ID=$CURRENT_EVENT_ID
    fi
    
    sleep 10
done
Starting real-time monitor for: anihpj-django-app Press Ctrl+C to stop 10:45:30 - New event detected: 2026-01-17T10:45:30Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | DjangoInstance 2026-01-17T10:45:15Z | CREATE_IN_PROGRESS | DjangoInstance 2026-01-17T10:45:00Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | DjangoSG 10:45:40 - Monitoring... (no new events) 10:45:50 - Monitoring... (no new events) 10:46:00 - New event detected: 2026-01-17T10:46:00Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | JobPostDatabase 2026-01-17T10:45:30Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | DjangoInstance Stack Status: CREATE_COMPLETE All 12 resources deployed successfully!
# anihpj/jobpost/services/realtime_monitor.py from django.db.models import Count from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import time from datetime import datetime class RealtimeStackMonitor: def __init__(self, stack_name='anihpj-django-app'): self.stack_name = stack_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') self.last_event_id = None def monitor(self, interval=10, duration=300): """Monitor stack events for specified duration""" # Setup monitoring skill monitoring_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Real-time Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Live infrastructure event tracking'} )[0] # Get or create monitoring user monitor_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='stack_monitor', defaults={ 'email': 'monitor@anihpj.com', 'is_active': True } )[0] # Create author for event logs monitor_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=monitor_user, defaults={'bio': 'Automated stack monitoring service'} )[0] # Monitoring location monitor_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Monitoring Hub', state='Cloud Region', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] start_time = time.time() event_count = 0 print(f"Starting real-time monitor for: {self.stack_name}") print(f"Duration: {duration} seconds, Interval: {interval} seconds") print("-" * 60) while time.time() - start_time < duration: response = self.cf_client.describe_stack_events(StackName=self.stack_name) events = response.get('StackEvents', []) if events: current_event_id = events[0]['EventId'] if current_event_id != self.last_event_id: event_count += 1 event = events[0] print(f"\n{datetime.now().strftime('%H:%M:%S')} - New event:") print(f" Resource: {event['LogicalResourceId']}") print(f" Status: {event['ResourceStatus']}") print(f" Type: {event['ResourceType']}") self.last_event_id = current_event_id time.sleep(interval) # Create summary job post summary_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Monitor Session: {self.stack_name}', description=f'Events captured: {event_count} over {duration}s', author=monitor_author, location=monitor_location, job_type='MONITORING', is_active=False ) summary_post.required_skills.add(monitoring_skill) print(f"\n{'='*60}") print(f"Monitoring complete. Events: {event_count}") print(f"Skills tracked: {Skills.objects.count()}") print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") print(f"Session saved as JobPost #{summary_post.id}")

describe-stack-resources

Returns AWS resource descriptions for running and completed resources in a CloudFormation stack. Use this to inventory all infrastructure components deployed for your anihpj Django application including EC2 instances, RDS databases, security groups, and networking resources.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --logical-resource-id {resource-id} \
  --physical-resource-id {physical-id} \
  --region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string): Name or unique stack ID. Required if logical-resource-id not specified. Example: anihpj-django-app
  • --logical-resource-id (string): Logical name of the resource as defined in the template. Example: DjangoInstance
  • --physical-resource-id (string): Physical name/ID assigned by AWS. Example: i-0abc123def456789
  • --region (string): AWS region where the stack exists. Example: us-east-1
Return Value
  • StackResources (list): Array of stack resource objects containing:
    • StackName: Name of the stack
    • StackId: Unique identifier of the stack
    • LogicalResourceId: Logical name from template
    • PhysicalResourceId: AWS-assigned physical ID
    • ResourceType: AWS resource type (AWS::EC2::Instance, etc.)
    • Timestamp: Time resource was created/updated
    • ResourceStatus: Current status of the resource
    • ResourceStatusReason: Explanation for current status
    • Description: Description from the template if provided
    • DriftInformation: Drift detection status
5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-stack-resources Examples
Example 1: List All Django Stack Resources

Retrieve complete inventory of all AWS resources deployed for the anihpj Django application stack.

#!/bin/bash
# List all resources in Django stack
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackResources[*].[LogicalResourceId,ResourceType,PhysicalResourceId,ResourceStatus]' \
  --output table \
  --region us-east-1
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | DescribeStackResources - anihpj-django-app | +----------------------+---------------------------+----------------------------+-------------------+ | LogicalResourceId | ResourceType | PhysicalResourceId | ResourceStatus | +----------------------+---------------------------+----------------------------+-------------------+ | VPC | AWS::EC2::VPC | vpc-0abc123def456789 | CREATE_COMPLETE | | PublicSubnet | AWS::EC2::Subnet | subnet-0abc123def456789 | CREATE_COMPLETE | | PrivateSubnet | AWS::EC2::Subnet | subnet-0def456789abc123 | CREATE_COMPLETE | | InternetGateway | AWS::EC2::InternetGateway | igw-0abc123def456789 | CREATE_COMPLETE | | DjangoSG | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | sg-0abc123def456789 | CREATE_COMPLETE | | RDSSG | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | sg-0def456789abc123 | CREATE_COMPLETE | | DjangoInstance | AWS::EC2::Instance | i-0abc123def456789 | CREATE_COMPLETE | | JobPostDatabase | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | anihpj-jobpost-db | CREATE_COMPLETE | | S3Bucket | AWS::S3::Bucket | anihpj-static-assets | CREATE_COMPLETE | | DjangoRole | AWS::IAM::Role | anihpj-django-role | CREATE_COMPLETE | +----------------------+---------------------------+----------------------------+-------------------+ Total Resources: 10 All resources: CREATE_COMPLETE
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/inventory_stack_resources.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Inventory all CloudFormation stack resources and map to Django models' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = 'anihpj-django-app' response = cf_client.describe_stack_resources(StackName=stack_name) resources = response.get('StackResources', []) # Create infrastructure skill infra_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='AWS Infrastructure', defaults={'description': 'CloudFormation-managed AWS resources'} )[0] # Get admin user for resource tracking admin_user = User.objects.filter(is_superuser=True).first() if not admin_user: admin_user = User.objects.create_user( username='infra_admin', email='infra@anihpj.com', password='secure_password' ) # Create author for documentation author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=admin_user, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure documentation specialist'} )[0] # Cloud location cloud_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='AWS Cloud', state='us-east-1', country='USA', remote=True )[0] print(f"Stack: {stack_name}") print(f"Total Resources: {len(resources)}") print("-" * 60) resource_types = {} for resource in resources: resource_type = resource['ResourceType'] resource_types[resource_type] = resource_types.get(resource_type, 0) + 1 print(f"Logical ID: {resource['LogicalResourceId']}") print(f"Physical ID: {resource['PhysicalResourceId']}") print(f"Type: {resource_type}") print(f"Status: {resource['ResourceStatus']}") print("-" * 40) # Create inventory job post inventory_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Infrastructure Inventory: {stack_name}', description=f'{len(resources)} resources across {len(resource_types)} types', author=author, location=cloud_location, job_type='DOCUMENTATION', is_active=False ) inventory_post.required_skills.add(infra_skill) print(f"\nSkills: {Skills.objects.count()}") print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") print(f"Inventory saved as JobPost #{inventory_post.id}")
Example 2: Get Specific Resource Details

Retrieve detailed information about a specific resource by its logical ID.

#!/bin/bash
# Get Django EC2 instance details
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --logical-resource-id DjangoInstance \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | jq '.StackResources[0]'
{ "StackName": "anihpj-django-app", "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-app/abc123", "LogicalResourceId": "DjangoInstance", "PhysicalResourceId": "i-0abc123def456789", "ResourceType": "AWS::EC2::Instance", "Timestamp": "2026-01-17T10:45:30.000Z", "ResourceStatus": "CREATE_COMPLETE", "DriftInformation": { "StackResourceDriftStatus": "NOT_CHECKED" } } Instance Details: - ID: i-0abc123def456789 - Type: t3.medium - Status: running - Public IP: 54.123.456.789
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Resource detail view from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ResourceDetailView(View): def get(self, request, stack_name, logical_id): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') response = cf_client.describe_stack_resources( StackName=stack_name, LogicalResourceId=logical_id ) resources = response.get('StackResources', []) if not resources: return JsonResponse({'error': 'Resource not found'}, status=404) resource = resources[0] # Track resource access skill resource_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name=resource['ResourceType'].split('::')[-1], defaults={'description': f"AWS {resource['ResourceType']}"} )[0] print(f"Skill tracked: {resource_skill.name}") # Log access user access_user = request.user if request.user.is_authenticated else None print(f"Accessing user: {access_user}") # Get all system users all_users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True) print(f"Active users: {all_users.count()}") # Get authors authors = Author.objects.all() print(f"Authors in system: {authors.count()}") # Resource location aws_location = Location.objects.filter(city='AWS Cloud').first() print(f"AWS location: {aws_location}") # Related jobs for this resource type related_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(required_skills=resource_skill) print(f"Related job posts: {related_jobs.count()}") return JsonResponse({ 'logical_id': resource['LogicalResourceId'], 'physical_id': resource['PhysicalResourceId'], 'type': resource['ResourceType'], 'status': resource['ResourceStatus'] })
Example 3: Group Resources by Type

Organize stack resources by AWS service type for better understanding of infrastructure composition.

#!/bin/bash
# Group resources by type
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | jq '
    .StackResources | group_by(.ResourceType) | 
    map({
      type: .[0].ResourceType,
      count: length,
      resources: [.[].LogicalResourceId]
    })'
[ { "type": "AWS::EC2::Instance", "count": 1, "resources": ["DjangoInstance"] }, { "type": "AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup", "count": 2, "resources": ["DjangoSG", "RDSSG"] }, { "type": "AWS::EC2::Subnet", "count": 2, "resources": ["PublicSubnet", "PrivateSubnet"] }, { "type": "AWS::EC2::VPC", "count": 1, "resources": ["VPC"] }, { "type": "AWS::RDS::DBInstance", "count": 1, "resources": ["JobPostDatabase"] }, { "type": "AWS::S3::Bucket", "count": 1, "resources": ["S3Bucket"] } ] Infrastructure Summary: - Compute: 1 EC2 instance - Database: 1 RDS instance - Storage: 1 S3 bucket - Networking: 1 VPC, 2 subnets, 2 security groups
# anihpj/jobpost/services/resource_analyzer.py from collections import defaultdict from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ResourceAnalyzer: def __init__(self, stack_name='anihpj-django-app'): self.stack_name = stack_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def analyze_by_type(self): response = self.cf_client.describe_stack_resources(StackName=self.stack_name) resources = response.get('StackResources', []) # Group by type grouped = defaultdict(list) for r in resources: grouped[r['ResourceType']].append(r['LogicalResourceId']) # Create skills for each resource type for resource_type in grouped.keys(): service_name = resource_type.split('::')[1] # EC2, RDS, S3 skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name=f'AWS {service_name}', defaults={'description': f'AWS {service_name} service expertise'} )[0] print(f"Skill: {skill.name}") # List all skills all_skills = Skills.objects.filter(name__startswith='AWS') print(f"\nAWS Skills: {all_skills.count()}") for skill in all_skills: print(f" -
