{skill.name}") # Get users with infrastructure access infra_users = User.objects.filter(is_staff=True) print(f"\nInfra users: {infra_users.count()}") # Get authors authors = Author.objects.select_related('user').all() print(f"Authors: {authors.count()}") # AWS locations aws_locations = Location.objects.filter(city__icontains='AWS') print(f"AWS locations: {aws_locations.count()}") # Print resource analysis print(f"\nResource Analysis for {self.stack_name}:") for resource_type, logical_ids in grouped.items(): print(f" {resource_type}: {len(logical_ids)}") for lid in logical_ids: print(f" - {lid}") # Create analysis job post analysis_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Resource Analysis: {self.stack_name}', description=f'{len(resources)} resources in {len(grouped)} categories', job_type='ANALYSIS', is_active=False ) # Add all AWS skills for skill in all_skills: analysis_post.required_skills.add(skill) print(f"\nJobPosts total: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return dict(grouped)
Example 4: Export Resource Inventory to CSV

Generate a CSV inventory of all stack resources for documentation and compliance.

#!/bin/bash
# Export resources to CSV
echo "LogicalId,ResourceType,PhysicalId,Status,Timestamp" > resources.csv

aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | jq -r '
    .StackResources[] | 
    [.LogicalResourceId, .ResourceType, .PhysicalResourceId, .ResourceStatus, .Timestamp] | 
    @csv' >> resources.csv

echo "✓ Exported to resources.csv"
wc -l resources.csv
✓ Exported to resources.csv 11 resources.csv Contents preview: LogicalId,ResourceType,PhysicalId,Status,Timestamp "VPC","AWS::EC2::VPC","vpc-0abc123","CREATE_COMPLETE","2026-01-17T10:30:00Z" "PublicSubnet","AWS::EC2::Subnet","subnet-0abc123","CREATE_COMPLETE","2026-01-17T10:31:00Z" "DjangoInstance","AWS::EC2::Instance","i-0abc123","CREATE_COMPLETE","2026-01-17T10:45:00Z" "JobPostDatabase","AWS::RDS::DBInstance","anihpj-db","CREATE_COMPLETE","2026-01-17T10:42:00Z" ... File size: 1.2 KB Ready for compliance review
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/export_resources_csv.py import csv from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Export CloudFormation resources to CSV and track in Django' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = 'anihpj-django-app' response = cf_client.describe_stack_resources(StackName=stack_name) resources = response.get('StackResources', []) # Create export skill export_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Data Export', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure data export and reporting'} )[0] compliance_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Compliance', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure compliance and auditing'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get compliance officer user compliance_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='compliance_officer', defaults={ 'email': 'compliance@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True } )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Author for compliance docs compliance_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=compliance_user, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure compliance documentation'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Compliance location compliance_loc = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Compliance Office', state='Corporate', country='USA', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Write CSV filename = f'resources-{timezone.now().strftime("%Y%m%d")}.csv' with open(filename, 'w', newline='') as csvfile: writer = csv.writer(csvfile) writer.writerow(['LogicalId', 'ResourceType', 'PhysicalId', 'Status', 'Timestamp']) for resource in resources: writer.writerow([ resource['LogicalResourceId'], resource['ResourceType'], resource['PhysicalResourceId'], resource['ResourceStatus'], resource['Timestamp'] ]) print(f"\n✓ Exported {len(resources)} resources to {filename}") # Create compliance job post compliance_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Compliance Export: {stack_name}', description=f'Exported {len(resources)} resources on {timezone.now().date()}', author=compliance_author, location=compliance_loc, job_type='COMPLIANCE', is_active=False ) compliance_post.required_skills.add(export_skill, compliance_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") print(f"Export tracked as JobPost #{compliance_post.id}")
Example 5: Compare Resources Across Environments

Compare stack resources between production and staging environments to ensure consistency.

#!/bin/bash
# Compare resources between environments
echo "Comparing stack resources..."

# Get production resources
PROD_RESOURCES=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app-prod \
  --query 'StackResources[*].ResourceType' \
  --output json --region us-east-1 | jq -S 'sort')

# Get staging resources
STAGING_RESOURCES=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app-staging \
  --query 'StackResources[*].ResourceType' \
  --output json --region us-east-1 | jq -S 'sort')

# Compare
echo "Production resources: $(echo $PROD_RESOURCES | jq length)"
echo "Staging resources: $(echo $STAGING_RESOURCES | jq length)"

if [ "$PROD_RESOURCES" == "$STAGING_RESOURCES" ]; then
    echo "✓ Environments match!"
else
    echo "⚠ Environment drift detected"
    diff <(echo "$PROD_RESOURCES") <(echo "$STAGING_RESOURCES")
fi
Comparing stack resources... Production resources: 12 Staging resources: 10 ⚠ Environment drift detected Differences: < "AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm" < "AWS::CloudWatch::Dashboard" Missing in staging: - CloudWatch Alarm (production monitoring) - CloudWatch Dashboard (metrics visualization) Recommendation: Deploy monitoring resources to staging
# anihpj/jobpost/services/environment_comparator.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class EnvironmentComparator: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def compare_stacks(self, prod_stack, staging_stack): # Get resources from both stacks prod_response = self.cf_client.describe_stack_resources(StackName=prod_stack) staging_response = self.cf_client.describe_stack_resources(StackName=staging_stack) prod_resources = {r['ResourceType'] for r in prod_response['StackResources']} staging_resources = {r['ResourceType'] for r in staging_response['StackResources']} # Create comparison skill comparison_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Environment Comparison', defaults={'description': 'Multi-environment infrastructure analysis'} )[0] devops_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='DevOps Engineering', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure automation and deployment'} )[0] print(f"Skills tracked: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get DevOps team users devops_users = User.objects.filter( groups__name='DevOps' ).distinct() if User.objects.exists() else User.objects.none() all_users = User.objects.all() print(f"Total users: {all_users.count()}") # Get authors for documentation authors = Author.objects.all() print(f"Authors: {authors.count()}") # Production and staging locations prod_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Production', state='us-east-1', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] staging_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Staging', state='us-east-1', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Find differences only_in_prod = prod_resources - staging_resources only_in_staging = staging_resources - prod_resources print(f"\nComparison Results:") print(f"Production: {len(prod_resources)} resource types") print(f"Staging: {len(staging_resources)} resource types") if only_in_prod: print(f"\nOnly in Production ({len(only_in_prod)}):") for rt in only_in_prod: print(f" - {rt}") if only_in_staging: print(f"\nOnly in Staging ({len(only_in_staging)}):") for rt in only_in_staging: print(f" - {rt}") drift_detected = bool(only_in_prod or only_in_staging) # Create comparison job post comparison_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Environment Comparison: {prod_stack} vs {staging_stack}', description=f'Drift detected: {drift_detected}. Prod: {len(prod_resources)}, Staging: {len(staging_resources)}', location=prod_location, job_type='AUDIT', is_active=drift_detected ) comparison_post.required_skills.add(comparison_skill, devops_skill) print(f"\nJobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") print(f"Comparison saved as JobPost #{comparison_post.id}") return { 'drift_detected': drift_detected, 'only_in_prod': list(only_in_prod), 'only_in_staging': list(only_in_staging) }

list-stack-resources

Returns descriptions of all resources in a specified CloudFormation stack. Use this command to get a paginated list of resources with their logical IDs, physical IDs, resource types, and statuses for your anihpj Django application infrastructure.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-stack-resources \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --next-token {token} \
  --region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Name or unique stack ID. Example: anihpj-django-app
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token from previous response for large resource lists
  • --region (string): AWS region where the stack exists. Example: us-east-1
Return Value
  • StackResourceSummaries (list): Array of resource summaries containing:
    • LogicalResourceId: Logical name from the template
    • PhysicalResourceId: Actual AWS resource ID
    • ResourceType: AWS resource type
    • ResourceStatus: Current status
    • LastUpdatedTimestamp: Last modification time
    • DriftInformation: Drift detection information
  • NextToken (string): Pagination token for more results
5 Detailed CloudFormation list-stack-resources Examples
Example 1: List All Resources in Django Stack

Get a summary of all resources deployed in the anihpj Django application stack.

#!/bin/bash
# List all resources in anihpj stack
aws cloudformation list-stack-resources \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackResourceSummaries[*].[LogicalResourceId,ResourceType,ResourceStatus]' \
  --output table \
  --region us-east-1
---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListStackResources | +------------------------+---------------------------+---------------+ | AnihpjVPC | AWS::EC2::VPC | CREATE_COMPLETE | | AnihpjSubnet1 | AWS::EC2::Subnet | CREATE_COMPLETE | | AnihpjSubnet2 | AWS::EC2::Subnet | CREATE_COMPLETE | | AnihpjSecurityGroup | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | CREATE_COMPLETE | | AnihpjEC2Instance | AWS::EC2::Instance | CREATE_COMPLETE | | AnihpjDatabase | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | CREATE_COMPLETE | | AnihpjLoadBalancer | AWS::ELB::LoadBalancer | CREATE_COMPLETE | +------------------------+---------------------------+---------------+ Total resources: 7
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/list_stack_resources.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'List all resources in a CloudFormation stack' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = options['stack_name'] # Create inventory skill inventory_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Resource Inventory', defaults={'description': 'Track and document cloud resources'} )[0] cloudformation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='CloudFormation', defaults={'description': 'AWS Infrastructure as Code'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get inventory user inventory_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='resource_inventory', defaults={'email': 'inventory@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author inventory_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=inventory_user, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure resource tracking'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Operations location ops_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Operations Center', state='Infrastructure', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # List all resources with pagination resources = [] paginator = cf_client.get_paginator('list_stack_resources') for page in paginator.paginate(StackName=stack_name): resources.extend(page.get('StackResourceSummaries', [])) print(f"\nResources in {stack_name}:") print("-" * 60) for resource in resources: print(f" {resource['LogicalResourceId']}: {resource['ResourceType']}") print(f" Status: {resource['ResourceStatus']}") print(f"\nTotal resources: {len(resources)}") # Create inventory job post inventory_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Resource Inventory: {stack_name}', description=f'Total resources: {len(resources)}', author=inventory_author, location=ops_location, job_type='INVENTORY', is_active=False ) inventory_post.required_skills.add(inventory_skill, cloudformation_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Filter Resources by Type

List only EC2-related resources from the anihpj stack.

#!/bin/bash
# Filter EC2 resources
aws cloudformation list-stack-resources \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackResourceSummaries[?starts_with(ResourceType, `AWS::EC2`)].{
    Logical: LogicalResourceId,
    Physical: PhysicalResourceId,
    Type: ResourceType,
    Status: ResourceStatus
  }' \
  --output table \
  --region us-east-1
--------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListStackResources | +-------------------+----------------------+---------------------+---------+ | Logical | Physical | Type | Status | +-------------------+----------------------+---------------------+---------+ | AnihpjVPC | vpc-0abc123def456 | AWS::EC2::VPC | CREATE_COMPLETE | | AnihpjSubnet1 | subnet-0123456789 | AWS::EC2::Subnet | CREATE_COMPLETE | | AnihpjSecurityGroup| sg-0fedcba987654 | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | CREATE_COMPLETE | | AnihpjEC2Instance | i-0abcdef123456789 | AWS::EC2::Instance | CREATE_COMPLETE | +-------------------+----------------------+---------------------+---------+ EC2 resources: 4 of 12 total
# anihpj/jobpost/services/resource_filter.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ResourceFilter: def __init__(self, stack_name): self.stack_name = stack_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def filter_by_type(self, type_prefix): # Create filtering skills filter_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Resource Filtering', defaults={'description': 'Filter resources by type'} )[0] ec2_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='EC2 Management', defaults={'description': 'EC2 instance management'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get filter user filter_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='resource_filter', defaults={'email': 'filter@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author filter_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=filter_user, defaults={'bio': 'Resource filtering specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Filter location filter_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Filter Operations', state='Analysis', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Get all resources response = self.cf_client.list_stack_resources(StackName=self.stack_name) all_resources = response.get('StackResourceSummaries', []) # Filter by type filtered = [r for r in all_resources if r['ResourceType'].startswith(type_prefix)] print(f"\n{type_prefix} Resources:") for r in filtered: print(f" {r['LogicalResourceId']}: {r['PhysicalResourceId']}") print(f"\nFiltered: {len(filtered)} of {len(all_resources)} total") # Create filter job filter_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Filter: {type_prefix} in {self.stack_name}', description=f'Found {len(filtered)} resources', author=filter_author, location=filter_location, job_type='FILTER', is_active=False ) filter_post.required_skills.add(filter_skill, ec2_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return filtered
Example 3: Check Resource Drift Status

List resources and show their drift detection status.

#!/bin/bash
# List resources with drift status
aws cloudformation list-stack-resources \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackResourceSummaries[*].{
    Resource: LogicalResourceId,
    Type: ResourceType,
    Status: ResourceStatus,
    Drift: DriftInformation.StackResourceDriftStatus
  }' \
  --output table \
  --region us-east-1
------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListStackResources | +-----------------------+---------------------------+----------+---------+ | Resource | Type | Status | Drift | +-----------------------+---------------------------+----------+---------+ | AnihpjVPC | AWS::EC2::VPC | CREATE_COMPLETE | IN_SYNC | | AnihpjSecurityGroup | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | CREATE_COMPLETE | MODIFIED | | AnihpjEC2Instance | AWS::EC2::Instance | CREATE_COMPLETE | IN_SYNC | | AnihpjDatabase | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | CREATE_COMPLETE | NOT_CHECKED | +-----------------------+---------------------------+----------+---------+ ⚠ 1 resource has drifted from template
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Resource drift status API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ResourceDriftStatusView(View): def get(self, request, stack_name): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') # Create drift skills drift_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Drift Detection', defaults={'description': 'Monitor configuration drift'} )[0] compliance_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Compliance', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure compliance monitoring'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get users users = User.objects.all() print(f"Users: {users.count()}") # Get authors authors = Author.objects.all() print(f"Authors: {authors.count()}") # Get locations locations = Location.objects.all() print(f"Locations: {locations.count()}") # Get resources with drift info response = cf_client.list_stack_resources(StackName=stack_name) resources = response.get('StackResourceSummaries', []) drift_summary = { 'in_sync': 0, 'modified': 0, 'deleted': 0, 'not_checked': 0 } for r in resources: drift_status = r.get('DriftInformation', {}).get('StackResourceDriftStatus', 'NOT_CHECKED') if drift_status == 'IN_SYNC': drift_summary['in_sync'] += 1 elif drift_status == 'MODIFIED': drift_summary['modified'] += 1 elif drift_status == 'DELETED': drift_summary['deleted'] += 1 else: drift_summary['not_checked'] += 1 # Track related jobs drift_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains='Drift') print(f"Drift-related jobs: {drift_jobs.count()}") return JsonResponse({ 'stack': stack_name, 'total_resources': len(resources), 'drift_summary': drift_summary })
Example 4: Export Resource Inventory to JSON

Create a complete inventory export for documentation and auditing.

#!/bin/bash
# Export full resource inventory
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
OUTPUT_FILE="resource-inventory-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

aws cloudformation list-stack-resources \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | jq '{
    stack_name: "'"$STACK_NAME"'",
    export_date: now | strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
    resource_count: (.StackResourceSummaries | length),
    resources: [.StackResourceSummaries[] | {
      logical_id: .LogicalResourceId,
      physical_id: .PhysicalResourceId,
      type: .ResourceType,
      status: .ResourceStatus,
      last_updated: .LastUpdatedTimestamp
    }]
  }' > $OUTPUT_FILE

echo "✓ Inventory exported: $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo "Resources: $(jq '.resource_count' $OUTPUT_FILE)"
✓ Inventory exported: resource-inventory-20260117.json Resources: 12 Export contents: - Stack name - Export timestamp - 12 resources with full details - Physical and logical IDs - Resource types and statuses
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/export_resource_inventory.py import json from datetime import datetime from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.conf import settings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import os class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Export stack resource inventory to JSON' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = options['stack_name'] # Create export skills export_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Data Export', defaults={'description': 'Export infrastructure data'} )[0] documentation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Documentation', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure documentation'} )[0] audit_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Audit', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure auditing'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get export user export_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='inventory_export', defaults={'email': 'export@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author export_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=export_user, defaults={'bio': 'Inventory export specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Export location export_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Export Center', state='Documentation', country='Corporate', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Get all resources response = cf_client.list_stack_resources(StackName=stack_name) resources = response.get('StackResourceSummaries', []) # Build export export_data = { 'stack_name': stack_name, 'export_date': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(), 'resource_count': len(resources), 'resources': [ { 'logical_id': r['LogicalResourceId'], 'physical_id': r.get('PhysicalResourceId'), 'type': r['ResourceType'], 'status': r['ResourceStatus'] } for r in resources ] } # Save export export_dir = os.path.join(settings.BASE_DIR, 'exports', 'inventory') os.makedirs(export_dir, exist_ok=True) filename = f'resource-inventory-{datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d")}.json' filepath = os.path.join(export_dir, filename) with open(filepath, 'w') as f: json.dump(export_data, f, indent=2) print(f"\n✓ Exported: {filename}") print(f"Resources: {len(resources)}") # Create export job export_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Inventory Export: {stack_name}', description=f'Exported {len(resources)} resources', author=export_author, location=export_location, job_type='EXPORT', is_active=False ) export_post.required_skills.add(export_skill, documentation_skill, audit_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 5: Compare Resources Across Stacks

Compare resource counts and types between production and staging stacks.

#!/bin/bash
# Compare resources between environments
PROD_STACK="anihpj-django-prod"
STAGING_STACK="anihpj-django-staging"

echo "=== Resource Comparison ==="
echo ""

for STACK in $PROD_STACK $STAGING_STACK; do
  COUNT=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-resources \
    --stack-name $STACK \
    --query 'length(StackResourceSummaries)' \
    --output text \
    --region us-east-1)
  
  TYPES=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-resources \
    --stack-name $STACK \
    --query 'StackResourceSummaries[*].ResourceType' \
    --output text \
    --region us-east-1 | tr '\t' '\n' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -5)
  
  echo "$STACK: $COUNT resources"
  echo "Top resource types:"
  echo "$TYPES"
  echo ""
done
=== Resource Comparison === anihpj-django-prod: 15 resources Top resource types: 4 AWS::EC2::Subnet 3 AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup 2 AWS::Lambda::Function 2 AWS::RDS::DBInstance 1 AWS::EC2::VPC anihpj-django-staging: 12 resources Top resource types: 4 AWS::EC2::Subnet 2 AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup 1 AWS::Lambda::Function 1 AWS::RDS::DBInstance 1 AWS::EC2::VPC Difference: Production has 3 more resources
# anihpj/jobpost/services/stack_comparison.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 from collections import Counter class StackResourceComparison: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def compare_stacks(self, prod_stack, staging_stack): # Create comparison skills comparison_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Stack Comparison', defaults={'description': 'Compare resources across stacks'} )[0] analysis_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Resource Analysis', defaults={'description': 'Analyze infrastructure resources'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get comparison user compare_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='stack_comparator', defaults={'email': 'compare@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author compare_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=compare_user, defaults={'bio': 'Stack comparison analyst'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Comparison location compare_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Analysis Center', state='Comparison', country='Corporate', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Get resources from both stacks prod_response = self.cf_client.list_stack_resources(StackName=prod_stack) staging_response = self.cf_client.list_stack_resources(StackName=staging_stack) prod_resources = prod_response.get('StackResourceSummaries', []) staging_resources = staging_response.get('StackResourceSummaries', []) prod_types = Counter([r['ResourceType'] for r in prod_resources]) staging_types = Counter([r['ResourceType'] for r in staging_resources]) print(f"\n{prod_stack}: {len(prod_resources)} resources") print(f"{staging_stack}: {len(staging_resources)} resources") print(f"Difference: {len(prod_resources) - len(staging_resources)}") # Create comparison job compare_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Compare: {prod_stack} vs {staging_stack}', description=f'Prod: {len(prod_resources)}, Staging: {len(staging_resources)}', author=compare_author, location=compare_location, job_type='COMPARISON', is_active=False ) compare_post.required_skills.add(comparison_skill, analysis_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return { 'prod_count': len(prod_resources), 'staging_count': len(staging_resources), 'prod_types': dict(prod_types), 'staging_types': dict(staging_types) }

get-template

Returns the template body for a specified CloudFormation stack. Use this command to retrieve the exact YAML or JSON template that was used to create or update your anihpj Django infrastructure stack, enabling version control, backup, and compliance documentation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation get-template \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --template-stage {Original|Processed} \
  --change-set-name {change-set-name} \
  --region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name (string, required): Name or unique stack ID of the stack whose template to retrieve. Example: anihpj-django-app
  • --template-stage (string): Template stage to retrieve. Values: Original (as submitted), Processed (after transforms). Default: Original
  • --change-set-name (string): Name or ID of a change set to get the template from
  • --region (string): AWS region where the stack exists. Example: us-east-1
Return Value
  • TemplateBody (string): Structure containing the template body (YAML or JSON format)
  • StagesAvailable (list): List of template stages available (Original and/or Processed)
5 Detailed CloudFormation get-template Examples
Example 1: Download Django Stack Template for Backup

Retrieve and save the current deployed template for backup, version control, and disaster recovery.

#!/bin/bash
# Download current stack template for backup
aws cloudformation get-template \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'TemplateBody' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1 > templates/backup/anihpj-django-$(date +%Y%m%d).yaml

echo "✓ Template downloaded"
ls -la templates/backup/*.yaml
✓ Template downloaded -rw-r--r-- 1 user user 8,234 Jan 17 2026 templates/backup/anihpj-django-20260115.yaml -rw-r--r-- 1 user user 8,567 Jan 17 2026 templates/backup/anihpj-django-20260116.yaml -rw-r--r-- 1 user user 9,123 Jan 17 2026 templates/backup/anihpj-django-20260117.yaml Template Summary: - AWSTemplateFormatVersion: 2010-09-09 - Description: Django infrastructure for anihpj jobpost application - Resources: 15 AWS resources defined - Parameters: 8 input parameters - Outputs: 5 exported values
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/backup_template.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from django.conf import settings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import os class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Backup CloudFormation template and track in Django models' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = 'anihpj-django-app' # Get template response = cf_client.get_template(StackName=stack_name) template_body = response.get('TemplateBody', '') # Create backup skill backup_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure Backup', defaults={'description': 'CloudFormation template backup and version control'} )[0] iac_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure as Code', defaults={'description': 'YAML/JSON template management'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get backup operator user backup_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='backup_operator', defaults={ 'email': 'backup@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True } )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author backup_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=backup_user, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure backup automation'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Backup location backup_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Backup Center', state='DR Region', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Save template to file backup_dir = os.path.join(settings.BASE_DIR, 'templates', 'backup') os.makedirs(backup_dir, exist_ok=True) filename = f'anihpj-django-{timezone.now().strftime("%Y%m%d")}.yaml' filepath = os.path.join(backup_dir, filename) with open(filepath, 'w') as f: f.write(template_body if isinstance(template_body, str) else str(template_body)) template_size = len(template_body) print(f"\n✓ Template backed up: {filename}") print(f"Size: {template_size} bytes") # Create backup job post backup_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Template Backup: {stack_name}', description=f'Backed up {template_size} bytes to {filename}', author=backup_author, location=backup_location, job_type='BACKUP', is_active=False ) backup_post.required_skills.add(backup_skill, iac_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") print(f"Backup tracked as JobPost #{backup_post.id}")
Example 2: Compare Deployed vs Local Template

Retrieve deployed template and compare with local version to detect configuration drift.

#!/bin/bash
# Compare deployed template with local version
aws cloudformation get-template \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'TemplateBody' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1 > /tmp/deployed-template.yaml

echo "Comparing templates..."
diff --color=always templates/django-infrastructure.yaml /tmp/deployed-template.yaml

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "✓ Templates are identical"
else
    echo "⚠ Template drift detected!"
fi
Comparing templates... 5c5 < Description: Django infrastructure for anihpj - LOCAL VERSION --- > Description: Django infrastructure for anihpj jobpost application 23c23 < InstanceType: t3.large --- > InstanceType: t3.medium 45,46c45 < AllocatedStorage: 50 < MaxAllocatedStorage: 100 --- > AllocatedStorage: 20 ⚠ Template drift detected! Changes found: - Instance type: local=t3.large, deployed=t3.medium - Storage: local=50GB (max 100GB), deployed=20GB - Description text differs
# anihpj/jobpost/services/template_comparator.py import difflib from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class TemplateComparator: def __init__(self, stack_name='anihpj-django-app'): self.stack_name = stack_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def compare_with_local(self, local_template_path): # Get deployed template response = self.cf_client.get_template(StackName=self.stack_name) deployed_template = response.get('TemplateBody', '') if isinstance(deployed_template, dict): import yaml deployed_template = yaml.dump(deployed_template, default_flow_style=False) # Read local template with open(local_template_path, 'r') as f: local_template = f.read() # Create comparison skill comparison_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Template Comparison', defaults={'description': 'IaC template drift detection'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get DevOps user devops_user = User.objects.filter(is_staff=True).first() print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") if devops_user: devops_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=devops_user, defaults={'bio': 'DevOps engineer'} )[0] else: devops_author = None print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Get locations dev_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Development', state='Local', country='USA', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Compute diff deployed_lines = deployed_template.splitlines() local_lines = local_template.splitlines() diff = list(difflib.unified_diff( local_lines, deployed_lines, fromfile='local', tofile='deployed', lineterm='' )) changes_detected = len(diff) > 0 print(f"\nTemplate Comparison: {self.stack_name}") print(f"Local lines: {len(local_lines)}") print(f"Deployed lines: {len(deployed_lines)}") print(f"Drift detected: {changes_detected}") if changes_detected: print("\nChanges:") for line in diff[:20]: # Show first 20 diff lines print(f" {line}") # Create comparison job post comparison_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Template Comparison: {self.stack_name}', description=f'Drift: {changes_detected}, {len(diff)} lines differ', author=devops_author, location=dev_location, job_type='AUDIT', is_active=changes_detected ) comparison_post.required_skills.add(comparison_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return { 'drift_detected': changes_detected, 'diff_lines': len(diff), 'diff': diff }
Example 3: Retrieve Processed Template with Transforms

Get the processed template after AWS transforms have been applied (SAM, includes, etc.).

#!/bin/bash
# Get processed template (after transforms)
aws cloudformation get-template \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --template-stage Processed \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 > processed-template.json

# Compare original vs processed
echo "Template stages available:"
aws cloudformation get-template \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StagesAvailable' \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1

# Show resource count in processed template
cat processed-template.json | jq '.TemplateBody | fromjson | .Resources | keys | length'
Template stages available: ["Original", "Processed"] Processed template saved to: processed-template.json Resource count in processed template: 18 Transform expansion: - Original resources: 12 - Processed resources: 18 - Transforms applied: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31 Expanded resources: - AWS::Lambda::Function (from SAM) - AWS::ApiGateway::RestApi (from SAM) - AWS::IAM::Role (auto-generated)
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Template retrieval API view from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json class TemplateRetrievalView(View): def get(self, request, stack_name): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') template_stage = request.GET.get('stage', 'Original') try: response = cf_client.get_template( StackName=stack_name, TemplateStage=template_stage ) except cf_client.exceptions.ClientError as e: return JsonResponse({'error': str(e)}, status=404) template_body = response.get('TemplateBody', {}) stages_available = response.get('StagesAvailable', []) # Track template access skill template_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='CloudFormation Templates', defaults={'description': 'AWS IaC template management'} )[0] transform_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='AWS Transforms', defaults={'description': 'SAM and macro transforms'} )[0] print(f"Skills tracked: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Log requesting user user = request.user if request.user.is_authenticated else None all_users = User.objects.all() print(f"Total users: {all_users.count()}") # Get related authors authors = Author.objects.all() print(f"Authors: {authors.count()}") # AWS locations locations = Location.objects.filter(remote=True) print(f"Remote locations: {locations.count()}") # Count resources in template resource_count = 0 if isinstance(template_body, dict): resource_count = len(template_body.get('Resources', {})) # Track job posts with template skills template_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(required_skills=template_skill) print(f"Template-related jobs: {template_jobs.count()}") return JsonResponse({ 'stack_name': stack_name, 'template_stage': template_stage, 'stages_available': stages_available, 'resource_count': resource_count, 'template_size': len(json.dumps(template_body)) })
Example 4: Extract and Analyze Template Parameters

Retrieve template and extract parameter definitions for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
# Extract parameters from template
aws cloudformation get-template \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'TemplateBody' \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1 | jq '
    if type == "string" then fromjson else . end |
    .Parameters | to_entries | map({
      name: .key,
      type: .value.Type,
      default: .value.Default,
      description: .value.Description
    })'
[ { "name": "InstanceType", "type": "String", "default": "t3.medium", "description": "EC2 instance type for Django application" }, { "name": "DBInstanceClass", "type": "String", "default": "db.t3.micro", "description": "RDS instance class for PostgreSQL database" }, { "name": "KeyPairName", "type": "AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName", "default": null, "description": "EC2 key pair for SSH access" }, { "name": "Environment", "type": "String", "default": "production", "description": "Deployment environment (production, staging, dev)" }, { "name": "DomainName", "type": "String", "default": "anihpj.com", "description": "Domain name for the Django application" } ] Parameters found: 5 Required: 1 (KeyPairName) With defaults: 4
# anihpj/jobpost/services/template_analyzer.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json import yaml class TemplateAnalyzer: def __init__(self, stack_name='anihpj-django-app'): self.stack_name = stack_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def analyze_parameters(self): response = self.cf_client.get_template(StackName=self.stack_name) template_body = response.get('TemplateBody', {}) # Parse template if string if isinstance(template_body, str): try: template_body = yaml.safe_load(template_body) except: template_body = json.loads(template_body) parameters = template_body.get('Parameters', {}) # Create analysis skills analysis_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Template Analysis', defaults={'description': 'CloudFormation template parameter analysis'} )[0] documentation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Technical Documentation', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure documentation'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get documentation user doc_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='doc_generator', defaults={ 'email': 'docs@anihpj.com', 'is_active': True } )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create doc author doc_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=doc_user, defaults={'bio': 'Automated documentation generator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Documentation location doc_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Documentation Hub', state='Cloud', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Analyze parameters param_analysis = [] required_count = 0 print(f"\nParameters for {self.stack_name}:") for name, config in parameters.items(): has_default = 'Default' in config if not has_default: required_count += 1 param_info = { 'name': name, 'type': config.get('Type', 'String'), 'default': config.get('Default'), 'description': config.get('Description', 'No description'), 'required': not has_default } param_analysis.append(param_info) print(f" {name}:") print(f" Type: {param_info['type']}") print(f" Default: {param_info['default']}") print(f" Required: {param_info['required']}") # Create documentation job post doc_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Parameter Documentation: {self.stack_name}', description=f'{len(parameters)} parameters, {required_count} required', author=doc_author, location=doc_location, job_type='DOCUMENTATION', is_active=False ) doc_post.required_skills.add(analysis_skill, documentation_skill) print(f"\nJobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return param_analysis
Example 5: Version Control Integration

Retrieve template and commit to Git repository for version control and audit trail.

#!/bin/bash
# Fetch and commit template to Git
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
TEMPLATE_DIR="infrastructure/cloudformation"
TEMPLATE_FILE="$TEMPLATE_DIR/$STACK_NAME.yaml"

# Ensure directory exists
mkdir -p $TEMPLATE_DIR

# Download current template
aws cloudformation get-template \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'TemplateBody' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1 > $TEMPLATE_FILE

# Add metadata header
TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
STACK_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
  --output text)

sed -i "1i# Downloaded: $TIMESTAMP\n# Stack Status: $STACK_STATUS\n" $TEMPLATE_FILE

# Commit to Git
git add $TEMPLATE_FILE
git commit -m "feat(infra): Update $STACK_NAME template - $(date +%Y%m%d)"
echo "✓ Template committed to version control"
Downloading template... Adding metadata header... # Downloaded: 2026-01-17T15:30:00Z # Stack Status: CREATE_COMPLETE [main abc1234] feat(infra): Update anihpj-django-app template - 20260117 1 file changed, 245 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-) ✓ Template committed to version control Git history for infrastructure/cloudformation/anihpj-django-app.yaml: - abc1234 (HEAD) feat(infra): Update anihpj-django-app template - 20260117 - def5678 feat(infra): Update anihpj-django-app template - 20260116 - ghi9012 feat(infra): Initial Django stack template
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/version_control_template.py import subprocess from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from django.conf import settings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import os class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Download CloudFormation template and commit to version control' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') stack_name = 'anihpj-django-app' # Get template response = cf_client.get_template(StackName=stack_name) template_body = response.get('TemplateBody', '') # Create version control skills git_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Git Version Control', defaults={'description': 'Git-based infrastructure version control'} )[0] iac_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure as Code', defaults={'description': 'CloudFormation template management'} )[0] cicd_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='CI/CD', defaults={'description': 'Continuous integration and deployment'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get DevOps user devops_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='devops_automation', defaults={ 'email': 'devops@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True } )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author devops_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=devops_user, defaults={'bio': 'DevOps automation pipeline'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Version control location vcs_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Version Control', state='Git Repository', country='Cloud', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Save template template_dir = os.path.join(settings.BASE_DIR, 'infrastructure', 'cloudformation') os.makedirs(template_dir, exist_ok=True) template_file = os.path.join(template_dir, f'{stack_name}.yaml') # Add metadata header timestamp = timezone.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ') header = f"# Downloaded: {timestamp}\n# Stack: {stack_name}\n\n" with open(template_file, 'w') as f: f.write(header) f.write(template_body if isinstance(template_body, str) else str(template_body)) print(f"\n✓ Template saved: {template_file}") # Git operations (simulated) commit_msg = f'feat(infra): Update {stack_name} template - {timezone.now().strftime("%Y%m%d")}' print(f"Commit message: {commit_msg}") # Create version control job post vcs_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Template VCS: {stack_name}', description=f'Committed template at {timestamp}', author=devops_author, location=vcs_location, job_type='VERSION_CONTROL', is_active=False ) vcs_post.required_skills.add(git_skill, iac_skill, cicd_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") print(f"Version control tracked as JobPost #{vcs_post.id}")

get-template-summary

Returns summary information about a template without creating a stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation get-template-summary \
  --template-body file://template.yaml \
  --stack-name {stack-name}
Parameters Description
  • --template-body (string): Template content or file path
  • --template-url (string): S3 URL of the template
  • --stack-name (string): Name of existing stack to get summary
Return Value
  • Parameters (list): Template parameters
  • ResourceTypes (list): AWS resource types in template
  • Capabilities (list): Required capabilities
5 Detailed CloudFormation get-template-summary Examples
Example 1: Preview Template Contents
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation get-template-summary \
  --template-body file://django-infrastructure.yaml \
  --query '[Parameters[*].ParameterKey, ResourceTypes]' \
  --output json
[ ["InstanceType", "DBInstanceClass", "KeyPairName"], ["AWS::EC2::Instance", "AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup", "AWS::RDS::DBInstance", "AWS::EC2::VPC"] ] Template Summary: - Parameters: 3 - Resources: 15 - Resource types: 8 unique types
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/get_template_summary.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Get summary of CloudFormation template' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--template', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') summary_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Template Summary', defaults={'description': 'Analyze template contents'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") summarizer = User.objects.get_or_create( username='template_summarizer', defaults={'email': 'summary@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") summary_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=summarizer, defaults={'bio': 'Template summarizer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") summary_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Summary Center', state='Analysis', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") with open(options['template']) as f: template = f.read() response = cf_client.get_template_summary(TemplateBody=template) self.stdout.write(f"Parameters: {len(response.get('Parameters', []))}") self.stdout.write(f"Resource Types: {len(response.get('ResourceTypes', []))}") summary_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Template Summary', description=f"Resources: {len(response.get('ResourceTypes', []))}", author=summary_author, location=summary_location, job_type='SUMMARY', is_active=False ) summary_job.required_skills.add(summary_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Check Required Capabilities
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation get-template-summary \
  --template-body file://django-app.yaml \
  --query 'Capabilities' \
  --output text
CAPABILITY_IAM CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND
# anihpj/jobpost/services/capability_checker.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class CapabilityChecker: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def check_capabilities(self, template_path): cap_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Capability Analysis', defaults={'description': 'Check required capabilities'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") cap_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='cap_checker', defaults={'email': 'caps@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") cap_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=cap_user, defaults={'bio': 'Capability checker'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") cap_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Cap Center', state='Security', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") with open(template_path) as f: template = f.read() response = self.cf_client.get_template_summary(TemplateBody=template) caps = response.get('Capabilities', []) cap_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Capability Check', description=f'Required: {len(caps)}', author=cap_author, location=cap_location, job_type='CAP_CHECK', is_active=False ) cap_job.required_skills.add(cap_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return caps
Example 3: List All Parameters
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation get-template-summary \
  --template-body file://django-app.yaml \
  --query 'Parameters[*].{Key:ParameterKey,Default:DefaultValue,Type:ParameterType}' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------------- | Key | Default | Type | +----------------+--------------+----------------+ | InstanceType | t3.medium | String | | Environment | dev | String | | DBPassword | (none) | NoEcho | --------------------------------------------------
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Parameter list API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ParameterListView(View): def post(self, request): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') param_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Parameter Listing', defaults={'description': 'List template parameters'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") param_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='param_lister', defaults={'email': 'paramlist@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") param_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=param_user, defaults={'bio': 'Parameter lister'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") param_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Param List', state='Config', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") template = request.POST.get('template') response = cf_client.get_template_summary(TemplateBody=template) param_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Parameter List', description=f"Params: {len(response.get('Parameters', []))}", author=param_author, location=param_location, job_type='PARAM_LIST', is_active=False ) param_job.required_skills.add(param_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({'parameters': response.get('Parameters', [])})
Example 4: Summary from Existing Stack
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation get-template-summary \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query '{Resources:ResourceTypes,Params:Parameters[*].ParameterKey}' \
  --output json
{ "Resources": ["AWS::EC2::Instance", "AWS::RDS::DBInstance"], "Params": ["InstanceType", "DBPassword"] }
# anihpj/jobpost/services/stack_summary.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class StackSummary: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def get_stack_summary(self, stack_name): stack_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Stack Summary', defaults={'description': 'Get summary from stack'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") stack_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='stack_summarizer', defaults={'email': 'stacksum@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") stack_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=stack_user, defaults={'bio': 'Stack summarizer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") stack_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Stack Summary', state='Analysis', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = self.cf_client.get_template_summary(StackName=stack_name) stack_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Stack: {stack_name}', description=f"Types: {len(response.get('ResourceTypes', []))}", author=stack_author, location=stack_location, job_type='STACK_SUMMARY', is_active=False ) stack_job.required_skills.add(stack_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return response
Example 5: Validate and Summarize
#!/bin/bash
TEMPLATE="django-infrastructure.yaml"

echo "Validating and summarizing: $TEMPLATE"

# Validate first
aws cloudformation validate-template --template-body file://$TEMPLATE > /dev/null && \
echo "✓ Valid" && \
aws cloudformation get-template-summary \
  --template-body file://$TEMPLATE \
  --query '{Params:length(Parameters),Resources:length(ResourceTypes)}' \
  --output text
Validating and summarizing: django-infrastructure.yaml ✓ Valid 5 12
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/validate_summarize.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Validate and summarize template' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--template', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') validate_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Template Validation', defaults={'description': 'Validate templates'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") validator = User.objects.get_or_create( username='validator', defaults={'email': 'validate@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") val_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=validator, defaults={'bio': 'Template validator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") val_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Validate Center', state='QA', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") with open(options['template']) as f: template = f.read() # Validate cf_client.validate_template(TemplateBody=template) self.stdout.write("✓ Valid") # Summarize response = cf_client.get_template_summary(TemplateBody=template) self.stdout.write(f"Params: {len(response.get('Parameters', []))}") self.stdout.write(f"Resources: {len(response.get('ResourceTypes', []))}") val_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Validate & Summarize', description='Template validated and summarized', author=val_author, location=val_location, job_type='VALIDATE', is_active=False ) val_job.required_skills.add(validate_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

list-exports

Lists all exported output values from stacks for cross-stack references.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-exports --next-token {token}
Parameters Description
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token from previous response
Return Value
  • Exports (list): List of exports with Name, Value, ExportingStackId
5 Detailed CloudFormation list-exports Examples
Example 1: List All Cross-Stack Exports
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-exports \
  --query 'Exports[*].[Name,Value,ExportingStackId]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListExports | +-------------------------+-------------------+---------------------------+ | anihpj-vpc-id | vpc-0abc123 | arn:aws:cf:.../anihpj-vpc| | anihpj-db-endpoint | anihpj-db.rds... | arn:aws:cf:.../anihpj-db | | anihpj-s3-bucket | anihpj-media-prod | arn:aws:cf:.../anihpj-s3 | +-------------------------+-------------------+---------------------------+ Exports available for cross-stack references
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/list_exports.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'List all CloudFormation exports' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') export_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Export Management', defaults={'description': 'Manage stack exports'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") exporter = User.objects.get_or_create( username='export_lister', defaults={'email': 'exports@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") export_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=exporter, defaults={'bio': 'Export lister'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") export_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Export Center', state='Cross-Stack', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.list_exports() for export in response.get('Exports', []): self.stdout.write(f"{export['Name']}: {export['Value']}") export_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='List Exports', description=f"Found: {len(response.get('Exports', []))}", author=export_author, location=export_location, job_type='LIST_EXPORTS', is_active=False ) export_job.required_skills.add(export_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Find Specific Export
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-exports \
  --query "Exports[?starts_with(Name, 'anihpj-')].{Name:Name,Value:Value}" \
  --output json
[ {"Name": "anihpj-vpc-id", "Value": "vpc-0abc123"}, {"Name": "anihpj-subnet-public", "Value": "subnet-xyz789"} ]
# anihpj/jobpost/services/export_finder.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ExportFinder: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def find_by_prefix(self, prefix): find_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Export Finding', defaults={'description': 'Find exports by pattern'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") finder = User.objects.get_or_create( username='export_finder', defaults={'email': 'find@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") find_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=finder, defaults={'bio': 'Export finder'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") find_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Find Center', state='Search', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = self.cf_client.list_exports() matches = [e for e in response.get('Exports', []) if e['Name'].startswith(prefix)] find_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Find: {prefix}', description=f'Found: {len(matches)}', author=find_author, location=find_location, job_type='FIND_EXPORT', is_active=False ) find_job.required_skills.add(find_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return matches
Example 3: Export Inventory
#!/bin/bash
echo "Export Inventory"
echo "================"
aws cloudformation list-exports \
  --query 'length(Exports)' --output text
echo "total exports"

aws cloudformation list-exports \
  --query 'Exports[*].ExportingStackId' --output text | \
  tr '\t' '\n' | sort -u | wc -l
echo "unique stacks"
Export Inventory ================ 15 total exports 5 unique stacks
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Export inventory API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ExportInventoryView(View): def get(self, request): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') inv_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Export Inventory', defaults={'description': 'Inventory stack exports'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") inv_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='export_inventory', defaults={'email': 'inventory@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") inv_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=inv_user, defaults={'bio': 'Export inventory'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") inv_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Inventory Center', state='Tracking', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.list_exports() exports = response.get('Exports', []) stacks = set(e['ExportingStackId'] for e in exports) inv_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Export Inventory', description=f'Exports: {len(exports)}, Stacks: {len(stacks)}', author=inv_author, location=inv_location, job_type='INVENTORY', is_active=False ) inv_job.required_skills.add(inv_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({'total_exports': len(exports), 'unique_stacks': len(stacks)})
Example 4: Export Dependency Map
#!/bin/bash
echo "Export Dependency Map"
for EXPORT in $(aws cloudformation list-exports \
  --query 'Exports[*].Name' --output text); do
  IMPORTERS=$(aws cloudformation list-imports \
    --export-name "$EXPORT" \
    --query 'length(Imports)' --output text 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  echo "$EXPORT: $IMPORTERS importers"
done
Export Dependency Map anihpj-vpc-id: 3 importers anihpj-db-endpoint: 2 importers anihpj-s3-bucket: 1 importers
# anihpj/jobpost/services/dependency_mapper.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class DependencyMapper: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def map_dependencies(self): dep_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Dependency Mapping', defaults={'description': 'Map export dependencies'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") mapper = User.objects.get_or_create( username='dep_mapper', defaults={'email': 'deps@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") dep_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=mapper, defaults={'bio': 'Dependency mapper'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") dep_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Dep Center', state='Dependencies', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") exports = self.cf_client.list_exports().get('Exports', []) dep_map = {} for export in exports: try: imports = self.cf_client.list_imports(ExportName=export['Name']) dep_map[export['Name']] = len(imports.get('Imports', [])) except Exception: dep_map[export['Name']] = 0 dep_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Dependency Map', description=f'Exports: {len(dep_map)}', author=dep_author, location=dep_location, job_type='DEP_MAP', is_active=False ) dep_job.required_skills.add(dep_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return dep_map
Example 5: Export Report
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-exports \
  --output json > exports-report.json

echo "Export report saved to exports-report.json"
jq '.Exports | length' exports-report.json
echo "exports documented"
Export report saved to exports-report.json 15 exports documented
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/export_report.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.conf import settings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json import os class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Generate export report' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') report_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Export Reporting', defaults={'description': 'Generate export reports'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") reporter = User.objects.get_or_create( username='export_reporter', defaults={'email': 'report@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") report_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=reporter, defaults={'bio': 'Export reporter'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") report_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Report Center', state='Documentation', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.list_exports() report_dir = os.path.join(settings.BASE_DIR, 'reports') os.makedirs(report_dir, exist_ok=True) filepath = os.path.join(report_dir, 'exports-report.json') with open(filepath, 'w') as f: json.dump(response, f, indent=2, default=str) self.stdout.write(f"Report saved: {filepath}") report_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Export Report', description=f'Exports: {len(response.get("Exports", []))}', author=report_author, location=report_location, job_type='EXPORT_REPORT', is_active=False ) report_job.required_skills.add(report_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

list-imports

Lists stacks that are importing an exported output value.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-imports --export-name {export-name}
Parameters Description
  • --export-name (string, required): Name of the export to find importers for
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token
Return Value
  • Imports (list): List of stack names using this export
5 Detailed CloudFormation list-imports Examples
Example 1: Find Stacks Using Export
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-imports \
  --export-name anihpj-vpc-id \
  --query 'Imports' \
  --output table
--------------------------------------- | ListImports | +-------------------------------------+ | anihpj-django-app | | anihpj-storage-cdn | | anihpj-monitoring | +-------------------------------------+ 3 stacks depend on: anihpj-vpc-id ⚠ Cannot delete VPC stack while these stacks exist
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/list_imports.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'List stacks importing an export' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--export-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') import_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Import Tracking', defaults={'description': 'Track stack imports'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") tracker = User.objects.get_or_create( username='import_tracker', defaults={'email': 'imports@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") import_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=tracker, defaults={'bio': 'Import tracker'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") import_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Import Center', state='Dependencies', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = cf_client.list_imports(ExportName=options['export_name']) for stack in response.get('Imports', []): self.stdout.write(stack) import_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f"Imports: {options['export_name']}", description=f"Importers: {len(response.get('Imports', []))}", author=import_author, location=import_location, job_type='LIST_IMPORTS', is_active=False ) import_job.required_skills.add(import_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Check Before Delete
#!/bin/bash
EXPORT_NAME="anihpj-vpc-id"

IMPORTERS=$(aws cloudformation list-imports \
  --export-name $EXPORT_NAME \
  --query 'length(Imports)' --output text)

if [ "$IMPORTERS" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "⚠️ Cannot delete: $IMPORTERS stacks depend on this export"
  aws cloudformation list-imports --export-name $EXPORT_NAME --query 'Imports'
else
  echo "✓ Safe to delete: no dependencies"
fi
⚠️ Cannot delete: 3 stacks depend on this export ["anihpj-django-app", "anihpj-storage-cdn", "anihpj-monitoring"]
# anihpj/jobpost/services/delete_checker.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class DeleteChecker: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def can_delete(self, export_name): check_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Delete Safety Check', defaults={'description': 'Check before deletion'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") checker = User.objects.get_or_create( username='delete_checker', defaults={'email': 'delcheck@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") check_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=checker, defaults={'bio': 'Delete checker'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") check_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Check Center', state='Safety', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") try: response = self.cf_client.list_imports(ExportName=export_name) importers = response.get('Imports', []) except Exception: importers = [] safe = len(importers) == 0 check_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Delete Check: {export_name}', description=f"Safe: {safe}, Deps: {len(importers)}", author=check_author, location=check_location, job_type='DELETE_CHECK', is_active=False ) check_job.required_skills.add(check_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return {'safe': safe, 'importers': importers}
Example 3: Full Dependency Analysis
#!/bin/bash
STACK="anihpj-vpc"

echo "Dependency analysis for stack: $STACK"

# Get exports from this stack
EXPORTS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks --stack-name $STACK \
  --query 'Stacks[0].Outputs[?ExportName!=null].ExportName' --output text)

for EXPORT in $EXPORTS; do
  echo "Export: $EXPORT"
  aws cloudformation list-imports --export-name $EXPORT \
    --query 'Imports' --output text 2>/dev/null || echo "  (no importers)"
done
Dependency analysis for stack: anihpj-vpc Export: anihpj-vpc-id anihpj-django-app anihpj-api Export: anihpj-subnet-public anihpj-django-app
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Dependency analysis API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class DependencyAnalysisView(View): def get(self, request, stack_name): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') analysis_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Dependency Analysis', defaults={'description': 'Analyze stack dependencies'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") analyst = User.objects.get_or_create( username='dep_analyst', defaults={'email': 'analyst@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") analysis_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=analyst, defaults={'bio': 'Dependency analyst'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") analysis_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Analysis Center', state='Dependencies', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") stacks = cf_client.describe_stacks(StackName=stack_name) outputs = stacks['Stacks'][0].get('Outputs', []) analysis = {} for output in outputs: if 'ExportName' in output: try: imports = cf_client.list_imports(ExportName=output['ExportName']) analysis[output['ExportName']] = imports.get('Imports', []) except Exception: analysis[output['ExportName']] = [] analysis_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Analysis: {stack_name}', description=f'Exports: {len(analysis)}', author=analysis_author, location=analysis_location, job_type='ANALYSIS', is_active=False ) analysis_job.required_skills.add(analysis_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse(analysis)
Example 4: Count All Importers
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation list-imports \
  --export-name anihpj-vpc-id \
  --query 'length(Imports)' \
  --output text
3
# anihpj/jobpost/services/importer_counter.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ImporterCounter: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def count_importers(self, export_name): count_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Importer Counting', defaults={'description': 'Count importers'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") counter = User.objects.get_or_create( username='importer_counter', defaults={'email': 'count@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") count_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=counter, defaults={'bio': 'Importer counter'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") count_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Count Center', state='Metrics', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") response = self.cf_client.list_imports(ExportName=export_name) count = len(response.get('Imports', [])) count_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Count: {export_name}', description=f'Importers: {count}', author=count_author, location=count_location, job_type='COUNT', is_active=False ) count_job.required_skills.add(count_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return count
Example 5: Impact Report
#!/bin/bash
EXPORT="anihpj-vpc-id"

echo "Impact Report: $EXPORT"
echo "========================"

IMPORTERS=$(aws cloudformation list-imports \
  --export-name $EXPORT \
  --query 'Imports' --output json)

echo "Dependent stacks:"
echo "$IMPORTERS" | jq -r '.[]'
echo ""
echo "Total impact: $(echo $IMPORTERS | jq 'length') stacks"
Impact Report: anihpj-vpc-id ======================== Dependent stacks: anihpj-django-app anihpj-storage-cdn anihpj-monitoring Total impact: 3 stacks
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/impact_report.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Generate impact report for export' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--export-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') impact_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Impact Analysis', defaults={'description': 'Analyze change impact'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") impact_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='impact_analyst', defaults={'email': 'impact@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") impact_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=impact_user, defaults={'bio': 'Impact analyst'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") impact_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Impact Center', state='Risk', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") export_name = options['export_name'] response = cf_client.list_imports(ExportName=export_name) importers = response.get('Imports', []) self.stdout.write(f"Impact Report: {export_name}") self.stdout.write("=" * 30) self.stdout.write("Dependent stacks:") for stack in importers: self.stdout.write(f" - {stack}") self.stdout.write(f"\nTotal impact: {len(importers)} stacks") impact_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Impact: {export_name}', description=f'Impact: {len(importers)} stacks', author=impact_author, location=impact_location, job_type='IMPACT', is_active=False ) impact_job.required_skills.add(impact_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

deploy

High-level command that packages and deploys CloudFormation templates, automatically creating a new stack or updating an existing one. This is the recommended method for deploying your anihpj Django jobpost application infrastructure as it handles change set creation and execution automatically.

Syntax
aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file {path-to-template} \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --s3-bucket {bucket-name} \
  --s3-prefix {prefix} \
  --parameter-overrides {key}={value} \
  --capabilities {capabilities} \
  --no-execute-changeset \
  --role-arn {role-arn} \
  --notification-arns {sns-topic-arns} \
  --tags {key}={value} \
  --disable-rollback \
  --region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --template-file (string, required): Path to local CloudFormation template file (YAML or JSON). Example: django-infrastructure.yaml
  • --stack-name (string, required): Name for the stack (creates new or updates existing). Example: anihpj-django-app
  • --s3-bucket (string): S3 bucket for uploading templates larger than 51,200 bytes
  • --s3-prefix (string): Prefix for S3 object key. Example: cloudformation/templates
  • --parameter-overrides (list): Stack parameters as key=value pairs. Example: InstanceType=t3.medium
  • --capabilities (list): Required IAM capabilities:
    • CAPABILITY_IAM: For stacks that create IAM resources
    • CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM: For IAM resources with custom names
    • CAPABILITY_AUTO_EXPAND: For stacks with macros or transforms
  • --no-execute-changeset: Create change set but don't execute (for review)
  • --role-arn (string): IAM role ARN for CloudFormation to assume during deployment
  • --notification-arns (list): SNS topic ARNs for stack event notifications
  • --tags (list): Tags to apply to the stack as key=value pairs
  • --disable-rollback: Disable automatic rollback on failure (for debugging)
  • --no-fail-on-empty-changeset: Return success if no changes are needed
Return Value
  • Exit Code 0: Stack deployment succeeded (CREATE_COMPLETE or UPDATE_COMPLETE)
  • Exit Code 1: Stack deployment failed
  • Exit Code 255: No changes needed (unless --no-fail-on-empty-changeset)
  • Console Output: Progress messages showing change set creation and stack operation status
5 Detailed CloudFormation deploy Examples
Example 1: Full Production Deployment

Deploy the complete Django application stack with all production settings.

#!/bin/bash
# Deploy or update the Django application stack
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
TEMPLATE_FILE="django-infrastructure.yaml"

echo "=== Deploying Django Infrastructure ==="
echo "Stack: $STACK_NAME"
echo "Template: $TEMPLATE_FILE"
echo ""

aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file $TEMPLATE_FILE \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --parameter-overrides \
    InstanceType=t3.medium \
    DBInstanceClass=db.t3.micro \
    Environment=production \
    DBUsername=anihpj_admin \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM \
  --tags \
    Project=anihpj \
    Application=jobpost \
    Environment=production \
    ManagedBy=CloudFormation \
  --no-fail-on-empty-changeset \
  --region us-east-1

RESULT=$?
if [ $RESULT -eq 0 ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "✓ Deployment successful!"
  
  # Get stack outputs
  aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
