    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --query 'Stacks[0].Outputs' \
    --output table \
    --region us-east-1
else
  echo "✗ Deployment failed with exit code: $RESULT"
fi
=== Deploying Django Infrastructure === Stack: anihpj-django-app Template: django-infrastructure.yaml Waiting for changeset to be created... Waiting for stack create/update to complete... Successfully created/updated stack - anihpj-django-app ✓ Deployment successful! -------------------------------------------------------------------- | Stack Outputs | +------------------+----------------------------------------------+ | WebsiteURL | http://anihpj-lb-123456.us-east-1.elb.amazonaws.com | | DatabaseEndpoint | anihpj-db.cluster-abc123.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com | | EC2InstanceId | i-0123456789abcdef0 | +------------------+----------------------------------------------+ Deployment Summary: Duration: 12 minutes Status: UPDATE_COMPLETE Resources: 15 updated
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/deploy_infrastructure.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from django.utils import timezone from django.conf import settings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import subprocess import json import os class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Deploy CloudFormation infrastructure for Django application' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--template', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--environment', type=str, default='production') def handle(self, *args, **options): template = options['template'] stack_name = options['stack_name'] environment = options['environment'] # Create deployment skills deploy_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='CloudFormation Deploy', defaults={'description': 'Deploy infrastructure using CloudFormation'} )[0] iac_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure as Code', defaults={'description': 'Manage infrastructure through code'} )[0] aws_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='AWS Architecture', defaults={'description': 'Design and deploy AWS infrastructure'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get deployment manager deploy_mgr = User.objects.get_or_create( username='deploy_manager', defaults={'email': 'deploy@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author deploy_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=deploy_mgr, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure deployment manager'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Deployment center deploy_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Deployment Center', state='Operations', country='AWS Cloud', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Build deploy command cmd = [ 'aws', 'cloudformation', 'deploy', '--template-file', template, '--stack-name', stack_name, '--parameter-overrides', f'Environment={environment}', 'InstanceType=t3.medium', '--capabilities', 'CAPABILITY_IAM', 'CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM', '--tags', 'Project=anihpj', f'Environment={environment}', '--no-fail-on-empty-changeset', '--region', 'us-east-1' ] print(f"\nDeploying: {stack_name}") print(f"Template: {template}") print(f"Environment: {environment}") # Execute deployment result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True) success = result.returncode == 0 if success: print("✓ Deployment successful!") else: print(f"✗ Deployment failed: {result.stderr}") # Create deployment job deploy_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Deploy: {stack_name}', description=f'Environment: {environment}, Success: {success}', author=deploy_author, location=deploy_location, job_type='DEPLOYMENT', is_active=not success ) deploy_post.required_skills.add(deploy_skill, iac_skill, aws_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Deploy with Change Set Review

Create a change set without executing for manual review before applying.

#!/bin/bash
# Deploy but hold for review
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

echo "Creating change set for review (not executing)..."

aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file django-infrastructure.yaml \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --parameter-overrides \
    InstanceType=t3.large \
    DBInstanceClass=db.t3.small \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --no-execute-changeset \
  --region us-east-1

# Get the change set name
CHANGE_SET=$(aws cloudformation list-change-sets \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`CREATE_COMPLETE`] | [0].ChangeSetName' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

echo ""
echo "Change set created: $CHANGE_SET"
echo ""
echo "Review changes:"
aws cloudformation describe-change-set \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --change-set-name $CHANGE_SET \
  --query 'Changes[*].ResourceChange.{Action:Action,Resource:LogicalResourceId,Type:ResourceType}' \
  --output table \
  --region us-east-1
Creating change set for review (not executing)... Waiting for changeset to be created... Changeset created successfully. Run the following command to review changes: aws cloudformation describe-change-set --change-set-name awscli-cloudformation-package-deploy-1234567890 --stack-name anihpj-django-app Change set created: awscli-cloudformation-package-deploy-1234567890 Review changes: ---------------------------------------------------------- | Resource Changes | +---------+----------------------+------------------------+ | Action | Resource | Type | +---------+----------------------+------------------------+ | Modify | DjangoEC2Instance | AWS::EC2::Instance | | Modify | DjangoDatabase | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | +---------+----------------------+------------------------+ To execute: aws cloudformation execute-change-set --change-set-name ...
# anihpj/jobpost/services/changeset_reviewer.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import subprocess import boto3 class ChangeSetReviewer: def __init__(self, stack_name): self.stack_name = stack_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def create_for_review(self, template_file, parameters): # Create review skills review_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Change Set Review', defaults={'description': 'Review infrastructure changes before deployment'} )[0] approval_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Change Approval', defaults={'description': 'Approve or reject infrastructure changes'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get change approver approver = User.objects.get_or_create( username='change_approver', defaults={'email': 'approver@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author approver_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=approver, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure change approver'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Approval office approval_loc = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Change Approval', state='Governance', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Build parameter overrides param_overrides = [f'{k}={v}' for k, v in parameters.items()] cmd = [ 'aws', 'cloudformation', 'deploy', '--template-file', template_file, '--stack-name', self.stack_name, '--parameter-overrides'] + param_overrides + [ '--capabilities', 'CAPABILITY_IAM', '--no-execute-changeset', '--region', 'us-east-1' ] print(f"Creating change set for review...") result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True) # Get pending change sets cs_response = self.cf_client.list_change_sets(StackName=self.stack_name) pending = [cs for cs in cs_response.get('Summaries', []) if cs.get('Status') == 'CREATE_COMPLETE'] if pending: change_set_name = pending[0].get('ChangeSetName') # Get change details details = self.cf_client.describe_change_set( StackName=self.stack_name, ChangeSetName=change_set_name ) changes = details.get('Changes', []) print(f"\nChange Set: {change_set_name}") print(f"Changes: {len(changes)}") for change in changes: rc = change.get('ResourceChange', {}) print(f" {rc.get('Action')}: {rc.get('LogicalResourceId')}") # Create review job review_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Review Change Set: {self.stack_name}', description=f'Pending changes: {len(pending)}', author=approver_author, location=approval_loc, job_type='CHANGE_REVIEW', is_active=True ) review_post.required_skills.add(review_skill, approval_skill) print(f"\nJobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 3: Deploy with SNS Notifications

Deploy stack with SNS notifications for deployment status updates.

#!/bin/bash
# Deploy with notification to team channels
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
SNS_TOPIC="arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:deployment-notifications"

aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file django-infrastructure.yaml \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --parameter-overrides \
    InstanceType=t3.medium \
    Environment=production \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --notification-arns $SNS_TOPIC \
  --tags \
    Project=anihpj \
    Environment=production \
    DeployedBy=$(whoami) \
    DeployTime=$(date -Is) \
  --region us-east-1

echo ""
echo "Deployment complete. Notifications sent to SNS topic."
Waiting for changeset to be created... Waiting for stack create/update to complete... Successfully created/updated stack - anihpj-django-app Deployment complete. Notifications sent to SNS topic. SNS Notifications Sent: 1. CHANGE_SET_CREATE_COMPLETE 2. UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS 3. Various resource updates... 4. UPDATE_COMPLETE Team notified via Slack/Email integration
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Deployment API with notifications from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import subprocess import boto3 class DeployWithNotificationView(View): def post(self, request): stack_name = request.POST.get('stack_name') template_file = request.POST.get('template_file') sns_topic = request.POST.get('sns_topic') # Create notification skills notification_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Deployment Notifications', defaults={'description': 'Send deployment status updates'} )[0] integration_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Team Integration', defaults={'description': 'Integrate with team communication'} )[0] devops_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='DevOps', defaults={'description': 'Development and operations practices'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get DevOps engineer devops_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='devops_engineer', defaults={'email': 'devops@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author devops_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=devops_user, defaults={'bio': 'DevOps automation engineer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # DevOps center devops_loc = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='DevOps Center', state='Automation', country='Cloud', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Build command with notifications cmd = [ 'aws', 'cloudformation', 'deploy', '--template-file', template_file, '--stack-name', stack_name, '--capabilities', 'CAPABILITY_IAM', '--notification-arns', sns_topic, '--tags', 'Project=anihpj', '--region', 'us-east-1' ] # Execute deployment result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True) success = result.returncode == 0 # Create notification job notify_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Notified Deploy: {stack_name}', description=f'SNS: {sns_topic}, Success: {success}', author=devops_author, location=devops_loc, job_type='NOTIFIED_DEPLOY', is_active=not success ) notify_post.required_skills.add(notification_skill, integration_skill, devops_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({ 'success': success, 'stack_name': stack_name, 'notifications': sns_topic })
Example 4: Deploy with Role Assumption

Deploy using a specific IAM role for cross-account or restricted deployments.

#!/bin/bash
# Deploy using a specific deployment role
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
DEPLOY_ROLE="arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CloudFormationDeployRole"

aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file django-infrastructure.yaml \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --parameter-overrides \
    InstanceType=t3.medium \
    Environment=production \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM \
  --role-arn $DEPLOY_ROLE \
  --tags \
    Project=anihpj \
    Environment=production \
    DeployRole=CloudFormationDeployRole \
  --region us-east-1

echo ""
echo "Deployment executed using role: $DEPLOY_ROLE"
echo "This ensures least-privilege deployment permissions"
Waiting for changeset to be created... Waiting for stack create/update to complete... Successfully created/updated stack - anihpj-django-app Deployment executed using role: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CloudFormationDeployRole This ensures least-privilege deployment permissions Role Permissions Used: - cloudformation:* - ec2:CreateInstance - rds:CreateDBInstance - iam:PassRole - s3:GetObject (for templates)
# anihpj/jobpost/services/secure_deployer.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import subprocess class SecureDeployer: def __init__(self, stack_name, role_arn): self.stack_name = stack_name self.role_arn = role_arn def deploy_with_role(self, template_file, parameters): # Create security deployment skills secure_deploy_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Secure Deployment', defaults={'description': 'Deploy with least-privilege roles'} )[0] iam_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='IAM Management', defaults={'description': 'Identity and access management'} )[0] least_priv_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Least Privilege', defaults={'description': 'Implement least privilege access'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get security deployer sec_deployer = User.objects.get_or_create( username='secure_deployer', defaults={'email': 'secure-deploy@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author sec_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=sec_deployer, defaults={'bio': 'Secure infrastructure deployer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Security office security_loc = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Security Office', state='Compliance', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Build parameters param_overrides = [f'{k}={v}' for k, v in parameters.items()] cmd = [ 'aws', 'cloudformation', 'deploy', '--template-file', template_file, '--stack-name', self.stack_name, '--parameter-overrides'] + param_overrides + [ '--capabilities', 'CAPABILITY_IAM', 'CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM', '--role-arn', self.role_arn, '--tags', 'Project=anihpj', 'SecureDeploy=true', '--region', 'us-east-1' ] print(f"Deploying with role: {self.role_arn}") result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True) success = result.returncode == 0 print(f"Deployment success: {success}") # Create secure deployment job secure_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Secure Deploy: {self.stack_name}', description=f'Role: {self.role_arn.split("/")[-1]}', author=sec_author, location=security_loc, job_type='SECURE_DEPLOY', is_active=not success ) secure_post.required_skills.add(secure_deploy_skill, iam_skill, least_priv_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return success
Example 5: CI/CD Pipeline Deployment

Deploy as part of a CI/CD pipeline with comprehensive error handling.

#!/bin/bash
# CI/CD Pipeline Deployment Script
set -e

STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
TEMPLATE_FILE="django-infrastructure.yaml"
ENVIRONMENT="${CI_ENVIRONMENT:-staging}"
S3_BUCKET="anihpj-deployment-${ENVIRONMENT}"

echo "=== CI/CD Deployment Pipeline ==="
echo "Commit: ${CI_COMMIT_SHA:-local}"
echo "Branch: ${CI_BRANCH:-local}"
echo "Environment: $ENVIRONMENT"
echo ""

# Validate template first
echo "Step 1: Validating template..."
aws cloudformation validate-template \
  --template-body file://$TEMPLATE_FILE \
  --region us-east-1 > /dev/null

echo "✓ Template valid"
echo ""

# Deploy with all CI/CD best practices
echo "Step 2: Deploying stack..."
aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file $TEMPLATE_FILE \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --s3-bucket $S3_BUCKET \
  --s3-prefix "templates/${CI_COMMIT_SHA:-latest}" \
  --parameter-overrides \
    InstanceType=t3.medium \
    Environment=$ENVIRONMENT \
    Version=${CI_COMMIT_SHA:-dev} \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM \
  --tags \
    Project=anihpj \
    Environment=$ENVIRONMENT \
    Commit=${CI_COMMIT_SHA:-local} \
    Branch=${CI_BRANCH:-local} \
    Pipeline=${CI_PIPELINE_ID:-manual} \
  --no-fail-on-empty-changeset \
  --region us-east-1

echo ""
echo "✓ Deployment complete"

# Output stack information
echo ""
echo "Step 3: Stack outputs..."
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Stacks[0].Outputs' \
  --output table \
  --region us-east-1
=== CI/CD Deployment Pipeline === Commit: abc123def456 Branch: main Environment: production Step 1: Validating template... ✓ Template valid Step 2: Deploying stack... Waiting for changeset to be created... Waiting for stack create/update to complete... Successfully created/updated stack - anihpj-django-app ✓ Deployment complete Step 3: Stack outputs... -------------------------------------------------------------------- | Stack Outputs | +------------------+----------------------------------------------+ | WebsiteURL | https://anihpj.example.com | | DatabaseEndpoint | anihpj-db.cluster-abc123.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com | | StackVersion | abc123def456 | +------------------+----------------------------------------------+ Pipeline Status: SUCCESS Duration: 14m 32s
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/cicd_deploy.py import os import subprocess from datetime import datetime from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'CI/CD pipeline deployment for Django infrastructure' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--template', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--stack-name', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--environment', type=str, default='staging') def handle(self, *args, **options): template = options['template'] stack_name = options['stack_name'] environment = options['environment'] # Get CI environment variables commit_sha = os.environ.get('CI_COMMIT_SHA', 'local') branch = os.environ.get('CI_BRANCH', 'local') pipeline_id = os.environ.get('CI_PIPELINE_ID', 'manual') # Create CI/CD skills cicd_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='CI/CD Pipelines', defaults={'description': 'Continuous integration and deployment'} )[0] automation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Deployment Automation', defaults={'description': 'Automated deployment processes'} )[0] validation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Template Validation', defaults={'description': 'Validate infrastructure templates'} )[0] gitops_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='GitOps', defaults={'description': 'Git-based operations'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get pipeline user pipeline_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='cicd_pipeline', defaults={'email': 'pipeline@anihpj.com', 'is_active': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author pipeline_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=pipeline_user, defaults={'bio': 'CI/CD automation pipeline'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # CI/CD location cicd_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='CI/CD Pipeline', state='Automation', country='Cloud', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Step 1: Validate template print("\n=== CI/CD Deployment ===") print(f"Commit: {commit_sha}") print(f"Branch: {branch}") print(f"Environment: {environment}") validate_cmd = [ 'aws', 'cloudformation', 'validate-template', '--template-body', f'file://{template}', '--region', 'us-east-1' ] validate_result = subprocess.run(validate_cmd, capture_output=True, text=True) if validate_result.returncode != 0: print(f"✗ Template validation failed") return print("✓ Template valid") # Step 2: Deploy deploy_cmd = [ 'aws', 'cloudformation', 'deploy', '--template-file', template, '--stack-name', stack_name, '--parameter-overrides', f'Environment={environment}', f'Version={commit_sha}', '--capabilities', 'CAPABILITY_IAM', 'CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM', '--tags', 'Project=anihpj', f'Environment={environment}', f'Commit={commit_sha}', f'Branch={branch}', f'Pipeline={pipeline_id}', '--no-fail-on-empty-changeset', '--region', 'us-east-1' ] deploy_result = subprocess.run(deploy_cmd, capture_output=True, text=True) success = deploy_result.returncode == 0 if success: print("✓ Deployment complete") else: print(f"✗ Deployment failed: {deploy_result.stderr}") # Create pipeline job pipeline_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'CI/CD Deploy: {stack_name}', description=f'Commit: {commit_sha[:8]}, Pipeline: {pipeline_id}', author=pipeline_author, location=cicd_location, job_type='CICD_DEPLOY', is_active=not success ) pipeline_post.required_skills.add(cicd_skill, automation_skill, validation_skill, gitops_skill) print(f"\nJobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

package

Packages local artifacts (Lambda code, nested templates) and uploads to S3.

Syntax
aws cloudformation package \
  --template-file template.yaml \
  --s3-bucket {bucket-name} \
  --s3-prefix {prefix} \
  --output-template-file packaged-template.yaml
Parameters Description
  • --template-file (string, required): Path to local template
  • --s3-bucket (string, required): S3 bucket for artifacts
  • --s3-prefix (string): Prefix for S3 object keys
  • --output-template-file (string): Output path for packaged template
  • --use-json: Output in JSON format instead of YAML
Return Value
  • Packaged Template: Template with S3 URIs replacing local paths
5 Detailed CloudFormation package Examples
Example 1: Package Lambda Function
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation package \
  --template-file django-with-lambda.yaml \
  --s3-bucket anihpj-deployment-artifacts \
  --output-template-file packaged-template.yaml

aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file packaged-template.yaml \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM
Uploading to anihpj-deployment-artifacts/abc123... ✓ Lambda function code uploaded ✓ Nested templates uploaded ✓ Template transformed packaged-template.yaml created Ready for deployment
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/package_template.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import subprocess class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Package CloudFormation template' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--template', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--bucket', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--output', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): package_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Template Packaging', defaults={'description': 'Package CF templates'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") packager = User.objects.get_or_create( username='template_packager', defaults={'email': 'package@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") package_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=packager, defaults={'bio': 'Template packager'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") package_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Package Center', state='Build', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") result = subprocess.run([ 'aws', 'cloudformation', 'package', '--template-file', options['template'], '--s3-bucket', options['bucket'], '--output-template-file', options['output'] ], capture_output=True, text=True) self.stdout.write(result.stdout) package_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Package Template', description=f"Output: {options['output']}", author=package_author, location=package_location, job_type='PACKAGE', is_active=False ) package_job.required_skills.add(package_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Package with Prefix
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation package \
  --template-file django-app.yaml \
  --s3-bucket anihpj-artifacts \
  --s3-prefix cloudformation/$(date +%Y-%m-%d) \
  --output-template-file packaged.yaml
Uploading to anihpj-artifacts/cloudformation/2024-01-15/abc123... Successfully packaged artifacts
# anihpj/jobpost/services/dated_packager.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime import subprocess class DatedPackager: def package_with_date(self, template, bucket, output): dated_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Dated Packaging', defaults={'description': 'Package with date prefix'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") dated_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='dated_packager', defaults={'email': 'dated@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") dated_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=dated_user, defaults={'bio': 'Dated packager'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") dated_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Dated Center', state='Build', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") date_prefix = f"cloudformation/{datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}" subprocess.run([ 'aws', 'cloudformation', 'package', '--template-file', template, '--s3-bucket', bucket, '--s3-prefix', date_prefix, '--output-template-file', output ]) dated_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Dated Package', description=f'Prefix: {date_prefix}', author=dated_author, location=dated_location, job_type='DATED_PACKAGE', is_active=False ) dated_job.required_skills.add(dated_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 3: Package to JSON
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation package \
  --template-file django-app.yaml \
  --s3-bucket anihpj-artifacts \
  --output-template-file packaged.json \
  --use-json
Successfully packaged artifacts Output in JSON format: packaged.json
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - JSON package API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import subprocess class JSONPackageView(View): def post(self, request): json_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='JSON Packaging', defaults={'description': 'Package to JSON format'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") json_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='json_packager', defaults={'email': 'json@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") json_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=json_user, defaults={'bio': 'JSON packager'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") json_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='JSON Center', state='Format', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") template = request.POST.get('template') bucket = request.POST.get('bucket') output = request.POST.get('output') subprocess.run([ 'aws', 'cloudformation', 'package', '--template-file', template, '--s3-bucket', bucket, '--output-template-file', output, '--use-json' ]) json_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='JSON Package', description='JSON format output', author=json_author, location=json_location, job_type='JSON_PACKAGE', is_active=False ) json_job.required_skills.add(json_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({'output': output})
Example 4: Package and Deploy Pipeline
#!/bin/bash
TEMPLATE="django-infrastructure.yaml"
BUCKET="anihpj-deploy"
STACK="anihpj-django-app"

# Package
aws cloudformation package \
  --template-file $TEMPLATE \
  --s3-bucket $BUCKET \
  --output-template-file packaged.yaml

# Deploy
aws cloudformation deploy \
  --template-file packaged.yaml \
  --stack-name $STACK \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM \
  --no-fail-on-empty-changeset
Uploading artifacts to anihpj-deploy... Package complete Waiting for changeset to be created... Waiting for stack update to complete... Successfully deployed
# anihpj/jobpost/services/package_deploy_pipeline.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import subprocess class PackageDeployPipeline: def run(self, template, bucket, stack): pipeline_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Package Deploy Pipeline', defaults={'description': 'Full deployment pipeline'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") pipeline_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='pipeline_runner', defaults={'email': 'pipeline@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") pipeline_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=pipeline_user, defaults={'bio': 'Pipeline runner'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") pipeline_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Pipeline Center', state='CICD', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Package subprocess.run([ 'aws', 'cloudformation', 'package', '--template-file', template, '--s3-bucket', bucket, '--output-template-file', 'packaged.yaml' ]) # Deploy subprocess.run([ 'aws', 'cloudformation', 'deploy', '--template-file', 'packaged.yaml', '--stack-name', stack, '--capabilities', 'CAPABILITY_IAM', 'CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM', '--no-fail-on-empty-changeset' ]) pipeline_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Package & Deploy', description=f'Stack: {stack}', author=pipeline_author, location=pipeline_location, job_type='PIPELINE', is_active=False ) pipeline_job.required_skills.add(pipeline_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 5: Package with KMS Encryption
#!/bin/bash
aws cloudformation package \
  --template-file django-secure.yaml \
  --s3-bucket anihpj-secure-artifacts \
  --kms-key-id alias/anihpj-deploy-key \
  --output-template-file packaged-secure.yaml
Uploading with KMS encryption... Key: alias/anihpj-deploy-key Successfully packaged with encryption
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/secure_package.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import subprocess class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Package template with KMS encryption' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--template', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--bucket', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--kms-key', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): secure_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Secure Packaging', defaults={'description': 'KMS encrypted packaging'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") secure_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='secure_packager', defaults={'email': 'secure@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") secure_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=secure_user, defaults={'bio': 'Secure packager'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") secure_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Secure Center', state='Security', country='AWS', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") subprocess.run([ 'aws', 'cloudformation', 'package', '--template-file', options['template'], '--s3-bucket', options['bucket'], '--kms-key-id', options['kms_key'], '--output-template-file', 'packaged-secure.yaml' ]) secure_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Secure Package', description='KMS encrypted', author=secure_author, location=secure_location, job_type='SECURE_PACKAGE', is_active=False ) secure_job.required_skills.add(secure_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

activate-organizations-access

Activates trusted access with AWS Organizations for CloudFormation StackSets.

Syntax
aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access
Parameters Description
  • No parameters required - This command activates trusted access with AWS Organizations
Return Value

Returns no output on success. Use describe-organizations-access to verify the activation status.

5 Detailed CloudFormation activate-organizations-access Examples
Example 1: Enable Organizations Integration for anihpj Multi-Account Deployment
#!/bin/bash
# Activate Organizations access for StackSets
aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access

# Verify activation
aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access

echo "Organizations access activated for anihpj multi-account infrastructure"
{ "Status": "ENABLED", "StatusMessage": "Organizations access activated" } Organizations access activated for anihpj multi-account infrastructure
# Django queryset - Tracking Organizations access across accounts from apps.models import CloudFormationOrganization, Account # Check organization status org_access = CloudFormationOrganization.objects.filter( status='ENABLED' ).first() print(f"Status: {org_access.status}") # Get all managed accounts managed_accounts = Account.objects.filter( organization=org_access, is_active=True ).values_list('account_id', 'name') print(f"Managed accounts: {list(managed_accounts)}")
Example 2: Setup Multi-Account Django Deployment for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Enable Organizations access
aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access

# Create StackSet for Django infrastructure across accounts
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --template-body file://django-infrastructure.yaml \
  --permission-model SERVICE_MANAGED \
  --auto-deployment Enabled=true,RetainStacksOnAccountRemoval=false \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM

# Deploy to organizational units
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --deployment-targets OrganizationalUnitIds=ou-prod-123456 \
  --regions us-east-1 us-west-2
Organizations access: ENABLED StackSet created: anihpj-django-infrastructure Deploying to OU: ou-prod-123456 Instances creating in us-east-1, us-west-2 Multi-account Django infrastructure deployment initiated
# Django queryset - Managing multi-account Django deployments from apps.models import StackSet, StackInstance, OrganizationalUnit # Get StackSet with instances stackset = StackSet.objects.get(name='anihpj-django-infrastructure') instances = StackInstance.objects.filter( stack_set=stackset, status='RUNNING' ).select_related('region', 'account') for instance in instances: print(f"Account: {instance.account.name}, Region: {instance.region.code}")
Example 3: Enable for anihpj CI/CD Pipeline Cross-Account Deployment
#!/bin/bash
# Activate Organizations access
aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access

# Create StackSet for CI/CD pipeline resources
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-cicd-pipeline \
  --template-body file://cicd-resources.yaml \
  --permission-model SERVICE_MANAGED \
  --auto-deployment Enabled=true \
  --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=Environment,Value=multi-account

echo "CI/CD pipeline ready for cross-account deployment"
Organizations access activated StackSet anihpj-cicd-pipeline created Auto-deployment enabled across organization CI/CD pipeline ready for cross-account deployment
# Django queryset - CI/CD pipeline resources tracking from apps.models import StackSet, PipelineDeployment # Track pipeline StackSet cicd_stackset = StackSet.objects.filter( name__contains='cicd-pipeline', permission_model='SERVICE_MANAGED' ).annotate( deployment_count=Count('instances') ) for ss in cicd_stackset: print(f"Pipeline: {ss.name}, Deployments: {ss.deployment_count}")
Example 4: Centralized Security Configuration for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Enable Organizations access
aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access

# Deploy security baseline to all accounts
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-security-baseline \
  --template-body file://security-baseline.yaml \
  --permission-model SERVICE_MANAGED \
  --auto-deployment Enabled=true \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM

# Deploy to entire organization
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-security-baseline \
  --deployment-targets OrganizationalUnitIds=r-root \
  --regions us-east-1

echo "Security baseline deployed to all anihpj accounts"
Organizations access: ACTIVE Security baseline StackSet created Deploying to root organization All accounts will receive security configuration Security baseline deployed to all anihpj accounts
# Django queryset - Security baseline compliance from apps.models import StackSet, SecurityBaseline, Account # Check security baseline deployment status security_stackset = StackSet.objects.get(name='anihpj-security-baseline') compliant_accounts = Account.objects.filter( stackinstances__stack_set=security_stackset, stackinstances__status='CURRENT' ).count() total_accounts = Account.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() print(f"Security compliance: {compliant_accounts}/{total_accounts} accounts")
Example 5: anihpj Multi-Region Django Application Deployment
#!/bin/bash
# Activate Organizations for multi-account multi-region deployment
aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access

# Create StackSet for Django application
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-app-global \
  --template-body file://django-app.yaml \
  --permission-model SERVICE_MANAGED \
  --auto-deployment Enabled=true,RetainStacksOnAccountRemoval=true \
  --parameters ParameterKey=ProjectName,ParameterValue=anihpj \
               ParameterKey=Environment,ParameterValue=production

# Deploy to production accounts in multiple regions
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-app-global \
  --deployment-targets OrganizationalUnitIds=ou-prod-123456 \
  --regions us-east-1 eu-west-1 ap-southeast-1
Organizations access activated StackSet: anihpj-django-app-global Deploying to 3 regions across production accounts - us-east-1: Creating - eu-west-1: Creating - ap-southeast-1: Creating Global Django deployment initiated
# Django queryset - Multi-region global deployment tracking from apps.models import StackSet, StackInstance, Region # Track global deployment global_stackset = StackSet.objects.get(name='anihpj-django-app-global') regional_status = StackInstance.objects.filter( stack_set=global_stackset ).values('region__code').annotate( count=Count('id'), active=Count('id', filter=Q(status='CURRENT')) ) for region in regional_status: print(f"Region: {region['region__code']}, Active: {region['active']}/{region['count']}")

activate-type

Activates a public third-party extension for use in CloudFormation operations.

Syntax
aws cloudformation activate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name} \
  --publisher-id {publisher-id}
Parameters Description
  • --type - The extension type (RESOURCE, MODULE, or HOOK)
  • --type-name - The name of the extension to activate
  • --publisher-id - The publisher ID of the extension
  • --execution-role-arn - (Optional) The IAM role for the extension to assume
  • --version-bump - (Optional) Version update behavior (MAJOR, MINOR)
Return Value

Returns the ARN of the activated extension type.

5 Detailed CloudFormation activate-type Examples
Example 1: Activate MongoDB Atlas Extension for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Activate MongoDB Atlas CloudFormation extension
aws cloudformation activate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster \
  --publisher-id bb989456c78c398a858fef18f2ca1bfc1fbba082

# Verify activation
aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster

echo "MongoDB Atlas extension activated for anihpj"
{ "Arn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster" } Extension activated successfully MongoDB Atlas extension activated for anihpj
# Django queryset - Track activated extensions from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension # Register MongoDB extension extension, created = CloudFormationExtension.objects.update_or_create( type_name='MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster', defaults={ 'type': 'RESOURCE', 'status': 'ACTIVATED', 'publisher_id': 'bb989456c78c398a858fef18f2ca1bfc1fbba082' } ) print(f"Extension: {extension.type_name}, Status: {extension.status}")
Example 2: Activate Datadog Extension for anihpj Monitoring
#!/bin/bash
# Activate Datadog CloudFormation extension
aws cloudformation activate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name Datadog::Integrations::AWS \
  --publisher-id 408988dff9e863704bcc72e7e13f8d645cee8311

# Set as default version
aws cloudformation set-type-default-version \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name Datadog::Integrations::AWS

echo "Datadog monitoring extension activated for anihpj"
Extension activation initiated Type: Datadog::Integrations::AWS Status: ACTIVATED Default version set Datadog monitoring extension activated for anihpj
# Django queryset - Datadog monitoring integration from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension, MonitoringIntegration # Track Datadog extension datadog_ext = CloudFormationExtension.objects.get( type_name='Datadog::Integrations::AWS' ) MonitoringIntegration.objects.create( extension=datadog_ext, project_name='anihpj', is_default=True ) print(f"Datadog integration enabled for anihpj")
Example 3: Activate New Relic Extension for anihpj APM
#!/bin/bash
# Activate New Relic extension
aws cloudformation activate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads \
  --publisher-id 759f81f13de8436c8833e4439e2b1b1a4ea71583

# List activated types
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --filters TypeNamePrefix=NewRelic \
  --provisioning-type FULLY_MUTABLE

echo "New Relic APM extension activated for anihpj Django monitoring"
Activating New Relic extension... Status: ACTIVATED Visibility: PUBLIC NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads ready New Relic APM extension activated for anihpj Django monitoring
# Django queryset - New Relic APM tracking from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension, APMConfiguration # Get all APM extensions apm_extensions = CloudFormationExtension.objects.filter( type_name__icontains='observability', status='ACTIVATED' ) for ext in apm_extensions: print(f"APM: {ext.type_name}, Status: {ext.status}")
Example 4: Activate PagerDuty Extension for anihpj Incident Management
#!/bin/bash
# Activate PagerDuty CloudFormation extension
aws cloudformation activate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name PagerDuty::EscalationPolicies::EscalationPolicy \
  --publisher-id 9f0b8a1d8c5e4a3f2b1c0d9e8f7a6b5c

# Configure automatic versioning
aws cloudformation set-type-configuration \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name PagerDuty::EscalationPolicies::EscalationPolicy \
  --configuration-alias default \
  --configuration '{"PagerDutyAccess":{"ApiKey":"{{resolve:secretsmanager:anihpj/pagerduty:SecretString:api_key}}"}}'

echo "PagerDuty incident management activated for anihpj"
Extension activated Configuration set with Secrets Manager integration Incident management ready PagerDuty incident management activated for anihpj
# Django queryset - PagerDuty incident management setup from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension, IncidentManagement # Configure PagerDuty pagerduty_ext = CloudFormationExtension.objects.get( type_name__icontains='PagerDuty' ) incident_config = IncidentManagement.objects.create( extension=pagerduty_ext, project='anihpj', secret_arn='arn:aws:secretsmanager:us-east-1:123456789012:secret:anihpj/pagerduty' ) print(f"Incident management configured: {incident_config}")
Example 5: Activate GitHub Extension for anihpj Repository Management
#!/bin/bash
# Activate GitHub CloudFormation extension
aws cloudformation activate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name GitHub::Repositories::Repository \
  --publisher-id 408988dff9e863704bcc72e7e13f8d645cee8312

# Create anihpj repository using CloudFormation
cat > github-repo.yaml << 'EOF'
Resources:
  AnihpjRepo:
    Type: GitHub::Repositories::Repository
    Properties:
      Name: anihpj-django-app
      Description: anihpj Django application repository
      Private: true
      AutoInit: true
EOF

aws cloudformation create-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-github-repo \
  --template-body file://github-repo.yaml
GitHub extension activated Repository resource type available Stack creation initiated Repository: anihpj-django-app will be created Private: true Auto-initialized with README
# Django queryset - GitHub repository tracking from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension, GitHubRepository # Track GitHub extension and repository github_ext = CloudFormationExtension.objects.get( type_name='GitHub::Repositories::Repository' ) repo = GitHubRepository.objects.create( extension=github_ext, name='anihpj-django-app', private=True, stack_name='anihpj-github-repo' ) print(f"Repository created: {repo.name}, Private: {repo.private}")

batch-describe-type-configurations

Returns configuration data for multiple registered extensions in batch.

Syntax
aws cloudformation batch-describe-type-configurations \
  --type-configuration-identifiers TypeArn={arn1} TypeArn={arn2}
Parameters Description
  • --type-configuration-identifiers - List of type configuration identifiers with TypeArn values
Return Value

Returns TypeConfigurations array with configuration data for each extension, and Errors array for any failed lookups.

5 Detailed CloudFormation batch-describe-type-configurations Examples
Example 1: Check anihpj Extension Configurations
#!/bin/bash
# Get configurations for multiple extensions used in anihpj
aws cloudformation batch-describe-type-configurations \
  --type-configuration-identifiers \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/Datadog-Integrations-AWS \
  --output json | jq '.TypeConfigurations[] | {TypeArn, Configuration}'
{ "TypeArn": "...MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster", "Configuration": "{\"MongoDBAtlasAccess\":{\"ApiKey\":\"{{resolve:secretsmanager:...}}\"}" } { "TypeArn": "...Datadog-Integrations-AWS", "Configuration": "{\"DatadogAccess\":{\"ApiKey\":\"{{resolve:secretsmanager:...}}\"}" } All anihpj extension configurations retrieved
# Django queryset - Extension configuration tracking from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension, ExtensionConfiguration # Get configurations for project extensions configs = ExtensionConfiguration.objects.filter( extension__project='anihpj', extension__status='ACTIVATED' ).select_related('extension') for config in configs: print(f"Extension: {config.extension.type_name}") print(f"Config: {config.configuration[:50]}...")
Example 2: Audit anihpj Third-Party Extension Settings
#!/bin/bash
# List all activated types for anihpj
TYPE_ARNS=$(aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PUBLIC \
  --provisioning-type FULLY_MUTABLE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].TypeArn' \
  --output text)

# Batch describe configurations
aws cloudformation batch-describe-type-configurations \
  --type-configuration-identifiers $(echo $TYPE_ARNS | xargs -n1 echo | head -5 | xargs -I {} echo "TypeArn={}") \
  --query 'TypeConfigurations[*].[TypeName,Alias,IsDefaultConfiguration]' \
  --output table
--------------------------------------------------------------- | BatchDescribeTypeConfigurations | +--------------------------------+----------+------------------+ | MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster | default | True | | Datadog::Integrations::AWS | default | True | | NewRelic::Observability::... | prod | True | | PagerDuty::EscalationPo... | default | True | | GitHub::Repositories::Repo... | default | False | +--------------------------------+----------+------------------+ anihpj extension audit complete
# Django queryset - Third-party extension audit from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension # Audit all third-party extensions third_party = CloudFormationExtension.objects.filter( visibility='PUBLIC', provisioning_type='FULLY_MUTABLE' ).values('type_name', 'alias', 'is_default') for ext in third_party: print(f"{ext['type_name']}: {ext['alias']} (Default: {ext['is_default']})")
Example 3: Verify anihpj Secrets Manager Integration
#!/bin/bash
# Check which extensions use Secrets Manager for anihpj
aws cloudformation batch-describe-type-configurations \
  --type-configuration-identifiers \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/Datadog-Integrations-AWS \
  --query 'TypeConfigurations[*].Configuration' \
  --output text | grep -o 'secretsmanager:[^}]*'

echo "Secrets Manager integrations for anihpj verified"
secretsmanager:anihpj/mongodb:SecretString:api_key secretsmanager:anihpj/datadog:SecretString:api_key All extensions using Secrets Manager for secure credential storage Secrets Manager integrations for anihpj verified
# Django queryset - Secrets Manager integration verification from apps.models import ExtensionConfiguration # Find extensions using Secrets Manager secrets_configs = ExtensionConfiguration.objects.filter( configuration__icontains='secretsmanager' ) for config in secrets_configs: print(f"Extension: {config.extension.type_name}") print(f"Secret ARN found in config")
Example 4: Compare anihpj Dev and Prod Extension Configurations
#!/bin/bash
# Get configurations for dev environment
DEV_CONFIG=$(aws cloudformation batch-describe-type-configurations \
  --type-configuration-identifiers \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster \
  --query 'TypeConfigurations[0].Configuration' \
  --output text)

# Get configurations for prod environment  
PROD_CONFIG=$(aws cloudformation batch-describe-type-configurations \
  --type-configuration-identifiers \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:987654321098:type/resource/MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster \
  --query 'TypeConfigurations[0].Configuration' \
  --output text)

echo "Dev Config: $DEV_CONFIG"
echo "Prod Config: $PROD_CONFIG"
echo "anihpj environment configuration comparison complete"
Dev Config: {"MongoDBAtlasAccess":{"ApiKey":"{{resolve:secretsmanager:anihpj-dev/mongodb}}"}} Prod Config: {"MongoDBAtlasAccess":{"ApiKey":"{{resolve:secretsmanager:anihpj-prod/mongodb}}"}} Different secret paths for dev/prod isolation anihpj environment configuration comparison complete
# Django queryset - Environment config comparison from apps.models import ExtensionConfiguration, Environment # Compare dev vs prod configs dev_config = ExtensionConfiguration.objects.get( extension__type_name='MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster', environment__name='development' ) prod_config = ExtensionConfiguration.objects.get( extension__type_name='MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster', environment__name='production' ) print(f"Dev secret: {dev_config.secret_path}") print(f"Prod secret: {prod_config.secret_path}")
Example 5: Batch Check anihpj Extension Versions
#!/bin/bash
# Get all extension configurations with version info
aws cloudformation batch-describe-type-configurations \
  --type-configuration-identifiers \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/Datadog-Integrations-AWS \
    TypeArn=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/NewRelic-Observability-Aiworkloads \
  --output json | jq -r '.TypeConfigurations[] | "\(.TypeName): \(.Alias // "default")"'

echo "All anihpj extension versions checked"
MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster: default Datadog::Integrations::AWS: default NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads: prod 3 extensions configured for anihpj All anihpj extension versions checked
# Django queryset - Extension version summary from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension # Get all extensions with version info extensions = CloudFormationExtension.objects.filter( project='anihpj', status='ACTIVATED' ).values('type_name', 'alias') print(f"Extensions configured: {extensions.count()}") for ext in extensions: alias = ext['alias'] or 'default' print(f"{ext['type_name']}: {alias}")

describe-account-limits

Retrieves CloudFormation quotas and limits for your AWS account.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-account-limits
Parameters Description
  • No parameters required - Returns all account limits for CloudFormation
Return Value

Returns AccountLimits array with Name and Value for each quota (StackLimit, StackSetLimit, ResourcesPerStack, etc.).

5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-account-limits Examples
Example 1: Check anihpj Account CloudFormation Limits
#!/bin/bash
# Check CloudFormation limits for anihpj account
aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --query 'AccountLimits[*].[Name,Value]' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj CloudFormation account limits retrieved"
--------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeAccountLimits | +----------------------------------+--------------------+ | StackLimit | 200 | | StackSetLimit | 100 | | StackInstancesPerStackSet | 2000 | | ResourcesPerStack | 500 | | StackOutputsLimit | 200 | | ParametersPerStack | 200 | +----------------------------------+--------------------+ anihpj CloudFormation account limits retrieved
# Django queryset - Account limits tracking from apps.models import AccountLimit, AWSAccount # Store account limits account = AWSAccount.objects.get(project='anihpj') limits = AccountLimit.objects.filter(account=account) for limit in limits: print(f"{limit.name}: {limit.value}")
Example 2: Monitor anihpj Stack Usage Against Limits
#!/bin/bash
# Get current stack count
STACK_COUNT=$(aws cloudformation list-stacks \
  --stack-status-filter CREATE_COMPLETE UPDATE_COMPLETE \
  --query 'length(StackSummaries)' \
  --output text)

# Get stack limit
STACK_LIMIT=$(aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --query 'AccountLimits[?Name==`StackLimit`].Value' \
  --output text)

echo "anihpj Stacks: $STACK_COUNT / $STACK_LIMIT"
echo "Usage: $(( STACK_COUNT * 100 / STACK_LIMIT ))%"

if [ $STACK_COUNT -gt $(( STACK_LIMIT * 80 / 100 )) ]; then
  echo "WARNING: anihpj approaching stack limit!"
fi
anihpj Stacks: 45 / 200 Usage: 22% Healthy stack count for anihpj infrastructure
# Django queryset - Stack usage monitoring from apps.models import Stack, AccountLimit # Calculate stack usage stack_count = Stack.objects.filter( project='anihpj', status__in=['CREATE_COMPLETE', 'UPDATE_COMPLETE'] ).count() stack_limit = AccountLimit.objects.get(name='StackLimit') usage_percent = (stack_count / stack_limit.value) * 100 print(f"Stack usage: {stack_count}/{stack_limit.value} ({usage_percent:.0f}%)")
Example 3: Validate anihpj Django Template Against Resource Limits
#!/bin/bash
# Count resources in Django template
RESOURCE_COUNT=$(cat anihpj-django-stack.yaml | \
  yq '.Resources | length')

# Get resource limit
RESOURCE_LIMIT=$(aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --query 'AccountLimits[?Name==`ResourcesPerStack`].Value' \
  --output text)

echo "Template Resources: $RESOURCE_COUNT / $RESOURCE_LIMIT"

if [ $RESOURCE_COUNT -gt $RESOURCE_LIMIT ]; then
  echo "ERROR: anihpj template exceeds resource limit!"
  echo "Consider splitting into nested stacks"
else
  echo "anihpj template within limits ✓"
fi
Template Resources: 87 / 500 Template within limits ✓ anihpj Django infrastructure template validated
# Django queryset - Template validation against limits from apps.models import CloudFormationTemplate, AccountLimit # Validate template resources template = CloudFormationTemplate.objects.get(name='anihpj-django-stack') resource_limit = AccountLimit.objects.get(name='ResourcesPerStack') is_valid = template.resource_count <= resource_limit.value print(f"Resources: {template.resource_count}/{resource_limit.value}") print(f"Valid: {is_valid}")
Example 4: Check anihpj StackSet Capacity
#!/bin/bash
# Count existing StackSets for anihpj
STACKSET_COUNT=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
  --status ACTIVE \
  --query 'length(Summaries)' \
  --output text)

# Get StackSet limit
STACKSET_LIMIT=$(aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --query 'AccountLimits[?Name==`StackSetLimit`].Value' \
  --output text)

# Get instance limit per StackSet
INSTANCE_LIMIT=$(aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --query 'AccountLimits[?Name==`StackInstancesPerStackSet`].Value' \
  --output text)

echo "anihpj StackSets: $STACKSET_COUNT / $STACKSET_LIMIT"
echo "Max Instances per StackSet: $INSTANCE_LIMIT"
anihpj StackSets: 12 / 100 Max Instances per StackSet: 2000 Capacity available for additional StackSets anihpj multi-account infrastructure has room to grow
# Django queryset - StackSet capacity monitoring from apps.models import StackSet, AccountLimit # Check StackSet capacity stackset_count = StackSet.objects.filter( project='anihpj', status='ACTIVE' ).count() stackset_limit = AccountLimit.objects.get(name='StackSetLimit') instance_limit = AccountLimit.objects.get(name='StackInstancesPerStackSet') print(f"StackSets: {stackset_count}/{stackset_limit.value}") print(f"Max instances per StackSet: {instance_limit.value}")
Example 5: Export anihpj Account Limits Report
#!/bin/bash
# Generate comprehensive limits report for anihpj
aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --output json > anihpj-cfn-limits.json

# Create formatted report
cat > anihpj-limits-report.txt << EOF
anihpj CloudFormation Account Limits Report
Generated: $(date)
============================================

$(aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --query 'AccountLimits[*].[Name,Value]' \
  --output text | column -t)

Current Usage:
- Stacks: $(aws cloudformation list-stacks --stack-status-filter CREATE_COMPLETE UPDATE_COMPLETE --query 'length(StackSummaries)' --output text)
- StackSets: $(aws cloudformation list-stack-sets --status ACTIVE --query 'length(Summaries)' --output text)
EOF

cat anihpj-limits-report.txt
anihpj CloudFormation Account Limits Report Generated: Fri Jan 17 2026 10:30:00 ============================================ StackLimit 200 StackSetLimit 100 StackInstancesPerStackSet 2000 ResourcesPerStack 500 StackOutputsLimit 200 ParametersPerStack 200 Current Usage: - Stacks: 45 - StackSets: 12 Report saved to anihpj-limits-report.txt
# Django queryset - Generate limits report from apps.models import AccountLimit, Stack, StackSet, Report from django.utils import timezone # Generate comprehensive report limits = AccountLimit.objects.all() stack_count = Stack.objects.filter(project='anihpj').count() stackset_count = StackSet.objects.filter(project='anihpj').count() report = Report.objects.create( name='anihpj-cfn-limits-report', generated_at=timezone.now(), data={'limits': list(limits.values()), 'stacks': stack_count, 'stacksets': stackset_count} ) print(f"Report generated: {report.name}")

describe-change-set-hooks

Returns information about hooks associated with a change set.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --change-set-name {change-set-name}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name - Name or ARN of the stack
  • --change-set-name - Name or ARN of the change set
  • --next-token - (Optional) Token for pagination
  • --logical-resource-id - (Optional) Filter by specific resource
Return Value

Returns Hooks array with HookType, HookStatus, InvocationPoint, FailureMode, and TargetDetails for each hook.

5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-change-set-hooks Examples
Example 1: Check Security Hooks on anihpj Django Stack Change Set
#!/bin/bash
# Describe hooks for anihpj stack change set
aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --change-set-name anihpj-update-2026-01 \
  --query 'Hooks[*].[HookType,TargetType,InvocationPoint,Status]' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj change set hooks reviewed"
----------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeChangeSetHooks | +--------------------------------+--------+---------------+-----------+ | AWS::SecurityHub::Compliance | STACK | PRE_PROVISION | SUCCESS | | AWS::GuardDuty::Validator | STACK | PRE_PROVISION | SUCCESS | | Custom::CostValidator | STACK | PRE_PROVISION | WARNING | +--------------------------------+--------+---------------+-----------+ anihpj change set hooks reviewed
# Django queryset - Change set hooks tracking from apps.models import ChangeSet, Hook # Get hooks for change set change_set = ChangeSet.objects.get( stack__name='anihpj-django-app', name='anihpj-update-2026-01' ) hooks = Hook.objects.filter(change_set=change_set) for hook in hooks: print(f"{hook.hook_type}: {hook.status}")
Example 2: Monitor anihpj Compliance Hook Results
#!/bin/bash
# Check compliance hooks for anihpj infrastructure update
aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --change-set-name add-rds-encryption \
  --output json | jq -r '.Hooks[] | select(.HookType | contains("Compliance")) | 
{
  Type: .HookType,
  Status: .Status,
  StatusMessage: .StatusMessage,
  TargetResources: (.TargetDetails.ResourceTargetDetails // [] | map(.LogicalResourceId))
}'
{ "Type": "AWS::SecurityHub::Compliance", "Status": "SUCCESS", "StatusMessage": "All security requirements met", "TargetResources": [ "AnihpjDatabase", "AnihpjDBSubnetGroup" ] } Compliance hooks passed for anihpj RDS encryption update
# Django queryset - Compliance hook monitoring from apps.models import Hook, ComplianceResult # Get compliance hook results compliance_hooks = Hook.objects.filter( change_set__stack__name='anihpj-django-infrastructure', hook_type__icontains='Compliance' ).select_related('change_set') for hook in compliance_hooks: print(f"Status: {hook.status}, Message: {hook.status_message}")
Example 3: Debug Failed Hook for anihpj Stack
#!/bin/bash
# Investigate failed hook in anihpj change set
aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --change-set-name security-update \
  --query 'Hooks[?Status==`FAILED`]' \
  --output json | jq -r '.[] | 
"Hook: \(.HookType)
Status: \(.Status)
Failure Mode: \(.FailureMode)
Message: \(.StatusMessage)
Target: \(.TargetDetails.ResourceTargetDetails[].LogicalResourceId)
"'

echo "anihpj hook failure analyzed"
Hook: Custom::SecurityValidator Status: FAILED Failure Mode: FAIL Message: Security group allows 0.0.0.0/0 ingress on port 22 Target: AnihpjSecurityGroup Change set will not proceed due to FAIL mode anihpj hook failure analyzed - Fix required before deployment
# Django queryset - Failed hook debugging from apps.models import Hook, HookFailure # Get failed hooks failed_hooks = Hook.objects.filter( change_set__stack__name='anihpj-django-app', status='FAILED' ) for hook in failed_hooks: failure = HookFailure.objects.get(hook=hook) print(f"Hook: {hook.hook_type}, Reason: {failure.message}")
Example 4: Review anihpj Cost Validation Hooks
#!/bin/bash
# Check cost validation hooks for anihpj
aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-production \
  --change-set-name scale-up-instances \
  --query 'Hooks[?contains(HookType, `Cost`)].[HookType,Status,StatusMessage]' \
  --output table

# Get hook invocation details
aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-production \
  --change-set-name scale-up-instances \
  --query 'Hooks[?contains(HookType, `Cost`)].TargetDetails' \
  --output json | jq '.'
-------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeChangeSetHooks | +---------------------------+---------+----------------------------+ | Custom::CostValidator | WARNING | Estimated cost: $450/month | +---------------------------+---------+----------------------------+ Target Resources: - AnihpjEC2Instance (t3.micro → t3.large) - AnihpjRDSInstance (db.t3.micro → db.t3.medium) Cost increase acceptable for anihpj production scaling
# Django queryset - Cost validation hooks from apps.models import Hook, CostEstimate # Get cost validation hooks cost_hooks = Hook.objects.filter( change_set__stack__name='anihpj-django-production', hook_type__icontains='Cost' ) for hook in cost_hooks: estimate = CostEstimate.objects.get(hook=hook) print(f"Status: {hook.status}, Estimate: ${estimate.monthly_cost}/month")
Example 5: List All Hook Types for anihpj Change Set
#!/bin/bash
# Comprehensive hook report for anihpj
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
CHANGE_SET_NAME="major-update-2026"

echo "anihpj Change Set Hook Report"
echo "=============================="

aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --change-set-name $CHANGE_SET_NAME \
  --output json | jq -r '
.Hooks | group_by(.InvocationPoint) | .[] | 
"
\(.[0].InvocationPoint) Hooks:
\(map("  • \(.HookType): \(.Status)") | join("\n"))
"'

# Count hooks by status
echo "Hook Status Summary:"
aws cloudformation describe-change-set-hooks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --change-set-name $CHANGE_SET_NAME \
  --query 'Hooks[*].Status' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sort | uniq -c
anihpj Change Set Hook Report ============================== PRE_PROVISION Hooks: • AWS::SecurityHub::Compliance: SUCCESS • AWS::GuardDuty::Validator: SUCCESS • Custom::CostValidator: WARNING • Custom::TagValidator: SUCCESS POST_PROVISION Hooks: • Custom::NotificationHook: SUCCESS Hook Status Summary: 4 SUCCESS 1 WARNING anihpj change set hooks validated
# Django queryset - Hook report generation from apps.models import Hook from django.db.models import Count # Generate hook report hooks = Hook.objects.filter( change_set__stack__name='anihpj-django-app', change_set__name='major-update-2026' ) # Group by status status_counts = hooks.values('status').annotate(count=Count('id')) for status in status_counts: print(f"{status['status']}: {status['count']}")

deactivate-organizations-access

Deactivates trusted access with AWS Organizations for CloudFormation StackSets.

Syntax
aws cloudformation deactivate-organizations-access
Parameters Description
  • No parameters required - This command deactivates trusted access with AWS Organizations
Return Value

Returns no output on success. Use describe-organizations-access to verify the deactivation status.

5 Detailed CloudFormation deactivate-organizations-access Examples
Example 1: Disable Organizations Access for anihpj Maintenance
#!/bin/bash
# Check current status
aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access

# Deactivate Organizations access temporarily
aws cloudformation deactivate-organizations-access

# Verify deactivation
aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access

echo "anihpj Organizations access deactivated for maintenance"
Current Status: ENABLED Deactivating Organizations access... Status: DISABLED anihpj Organizations access deactivated for maintenance
# Django queryset - Organizations access management from apps.models import CloudFormationOrganization # Update organization status org = CloudFormationOrganization.objects.get(project='anihpj') org.status = 'DISABLED' org.save() print(f"Organization status: {org.status}")
Example 2: Remove anihpj Multi-Account Deployment Capability
#!/bin/bash
# List existing StackSets with SERVICE_MANAGED permissions
echo "Existing anihpj StackSets:"
aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
  --status ACTIVE \
  --query 'Summaries[?PermissionModel==`SERVICE_MANAGED`].StackSetName' \
  --output table

# Deactivate Organizations access
aws cloudformation deactivate-organizations-access

echo "anihpj multi-account deployment disabled"
echo "Note: Existing StackSets remain but cannot be updated"
Existing anihpj StackSets: ------------------------------ | anihpj-django-infrastructure | | anihpj-security-baseline | | anihpj-cicd-pipeline | ------------------------------ Organizations access deactivated anihpj multi-account deployment disabled Note: Existing StackSets remain but cannot be updated
# Django queryset - StackSets affected by deactivation from apps.models import StackSet # Find service-managed StackSets service_managed = StackSet.objects.filter( project='anihpj', permission_model='SERVICE_MANAGED', status='ACTIVE' ) print(f"Affected StackSets: {service_managed.count()}") for ss in service_managed: print(f" - {ss.name}")
Example 3: Deactivate for anihpj Security Audit
#!/bin/bash
# Deactivate during security audit
aws cloudformation deactivate-organizations-access

# Document current StackSet deployments
aws cloudformation list-stack-sets --status ACTIVE > anihpj-stacksets-backup.json

# List all instances
for STACKSET in $(aws cloudformation list-stack-sets --status ACTIVE --query 'Summaries[].StackSetName' --output text); do
  echo "StackSet: $STACKSET"
  aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
    --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
    --query 'Summaries[*].[Account,Region,Status]' \
    --output table
done

echo "anihpj Organizations access disabled for security audit"
Organizations access: DISABLED Backing up StackSet configurations... StackSet: anihpj-django-infrastructure ------------------------------------------- | 123456789012 | us-east-1 | CURRENT | | 123456789012 | us-west-2 | CURRENT | ------------------------------------------- anihpj Organizations access disabled for security audit
# Django queryset - Security audit backup from apps.models import StackSet, StackInstance, AuditLog # Log deactivation for audit AuditLog.objects.create( project='anihpj', action='ORGANIZATIONS_DEACTIVATED', reason='Security audit' ) # Backup StackSet state stacksets = StackSet.objects.filter(project='anihpj') for ss in stacksets: instances = StackInstance.objects.filter(stack_set=ss) print(f"{ss.name}: {instances.count()} instances")
Example 4: Temporary Deactivation for anihpj Account Migration
#!/bin/bash
# Deactivate before account migration
echo "Deactivating Organizations access for anihpj account migration..."
aws cloudformation deactivate-organizations-access

# Wait for any in-progress operations to complete
echo "Checking for in-progress StackSet operations..."
aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operations \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`RUNNING`]' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj ready for account migration"
echo "Re-activate with: aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access"
Deactivating Organizations access for anihpj account migration... Status: DISABLED Checking for in-progress StackSet operations... No running operations anihpj ready for account migration Re-activate with: aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access
# Django queryset - Account migration preparation from apps.models import CloudFormationOrganization, StackSetOperation # Verify no running operations running_ops = StackSetOperation.objects.filter( stack_set__project='anihpj', status='RUNNING' ) print(f"Running operations: {running_ops.count()}") if running_ops.count() == 0: print("Safe to proceed with migration")
Example 5: Deactivate and Switch to Self-Managed StackSets for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Deactivate Organizations access
aws cloudformation deactivate-organizations-access

# Create new self-managed StackSet for anihpj
aws cloudformation create-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-selfmanaged \
  --template-body file://django-app.yaml \
  --permission-model SELF_MANAGED \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM

# Deploy using explicit account IDs
aws cloudformation create-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-selfmanaged \
  --accounts 123456789012 987654321098 \
  --regions us-east-1 us-west-2

echo "anihpj switched to self-managed StackSets"
Organizations access deactivated Creating self-managed StackSet: anihpj-django-selfmanaged Permission model: SELF_MANAGED Requires AWSCloudFormationStackSetExecutionRole in target accounts Stack instances creating... anihpj switched to self-managed StackSets
# Django queryset - Switch to self-managed StackSets from apps.models import StackSet # Create self-managed StackSet record new_stackset = StackSet.objects.create( name='anihpj-django-selfmanaged', project='anihpj', permission_model='SELF_MANAGED', status='ACTIVE' ) print(f"Created: {new_stackset.name}") print(f"Permission model: {new_stackset.permission_model}")

deactivate-type

Deactivates a public or private extension in your account and region.

Syntax
aws cloudformation deactivate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name}
Parameters Description
  • --type - The extension type (RESOURCE, MODULE, or HOOK)
  • --type-name - The name of the extension to deactivate
  • --arn - (Alternative) The ARN of the extension to deactivate
Return Value

Returns no output on success. The extension is marked as deactivated and cannot be used in new stacks.

5 Detailed CloudFormation deactivate-type Examples
Example 1: Deactivate Unused MongoDB Extension for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Check if MongoDB extension is in use
echo "Checking anihpj stacks using MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster..."
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --filters TypeNamePrefix=MongoDB

# Deactivate MongoDB extension
aws cloudformation deactivate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster

echo "MongoDB extension deactivated for anihpj account"
Checking stacks using MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster... No active stacks found Deactivating extension... Status: DEACTIVATED MongoDB extension deactivated for anihpj account
# Django queryset - Deactivate extension from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension # Deactivate MongoDB extension extension = CloudFormationExtension.objects.get( type_name='MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster' ) extension.status = 'DEACTIVATED' extension.save() print(f"Extension deactivated: {extension.type_name}")
Example 2: Remove Third-Party Extension from anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# List all activated third-party extensions
echo "anihpj Activated Extensions:"
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PUBLIC \
  --provisioning-type FULLY_MUTABLE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].[TypeName,IsActivated]' \
  --output table

# Deactivate Datadog extension
aws cloudformation deactivate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name Datadog::Integrations::AWS

echo "Datadog extension removed from anihpj"
anihpj Activated Extensions: --------------------------------------------------- | MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster | True | | Datadog::Integrations::AWS | True | | NewRelic::Observability::... | True | --------------------------------------------------- Deactivating Datadog::Integrations::AWS... Extension deactivated Datadog extension removed from anihpj
# Django queryset - Third-party extension removal from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension # List active extensions active_extensions = CloudFormationExtension.objects.filter( visibility='PUBLIC', is_activated=True ) print(f"Active extensions: {active_extensions.count()}") # Deactivate Datadog datadog = CloudFormationExtension.objects.get(type_name='Datadog::Integrations::AWS') datadog.is_activated = False datadog.save()
Example 3: Deactivate All Versions of Extension for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# List all versions of GitHub extension
aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name GitHub::Repositories::Repository \
  --query 'TypeVersionSummaries[*].[VersionId,IsDefaultVersion]' \
  --output table

# Deactivate the extension (all versions)
aws cloudformation deactivate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name GitHub::Repositories::Repository

echo "All versions of GitHub extension deactivated for anihpj"
GitHub Extension Versions: --------------------------------- | 00000001 | True | | 00000002 | False | --------------------------------- Deactivating GitHub::Repositories::Repository... All versions deactivated All versions of GitHub extension deactivated for anihpj
# Django queryset - Deactivate all extension versions from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension, ExtensionVersion # Deactivate all versions ExtensionVersion.objects.filter( extension__type_name='GitHub::Repositories::Repository' ).update(is_activated=False) # Update main extension extension = CloudFormationExtension.objects.get( type_name='GitHub::Repositories::Repository' ) extension.status = 'DEACTIVATED' extension.save()
Example 4: Clean Up anihpj Test Extensions
#!/bin/bash
# List private extensions for anihpj
echo "anihpj Private Extensions:"
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].TypeName' \
  --output text

# Deactivate test extension
aws cloudformation deactivate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Custom::TestResource

# Deregister to completely remove
aws cloudformation deregister-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Custom::TestResource

echo "Test extension removed from anihpj"
anihpj Private Extensions: anihpj::Custom::TestResource anihpj::Custom::DjangoResource Deactivating test extension... Status: DEACTIVATED Deregistering extension... Extension removed Test extension removed from anihpj
# Django queryset - Clean up test extensions from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension # List private extensions private_extensions = CloudFormationExtension.objects.filter( project='anihpj', visibility='PRIVATE' ) # Remove test extension test_ext = CloudFormationExtension.objects.get( type_name='anihpj::Custom::TestResource' ) test_ext.delete() print("Test extension removed")
Example 5: Deactivate Extension Before Cost Optimization for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Check extension usage in anihpj
EXTENSION="NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads"

echo "Checking $EXTENSION usage in anihpj stacks..."
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --filters TypeNamePrefix=NewRelic

# No usage found, deactivate to reduce costs
aws cloudformation deactivate-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $EXTENSION

# Verify deactivation
aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $EXTENSION 2>&1 || echo "Extension fully deactivated"

echo "anihpj cost optimization: $EXTENSION removed"
Checking NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads usage in anihpj stacks... Extension found but not used in any active stacks Deactivating extension... Status: DEACTIVATED Extension fully deactivated anihpj cost optimization: NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads removed
# Django queryset - Cost optimization deactivation from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension # Find unused extensions unused = CloudFormationExtension.objects.filter( project='anihpj', stacks__isnull=True, # Not used in any stack is_activated=True ) for ext in unused: ext.is_activated = False ext.save() print(f"Deactivated unused: {ext.type_name}")

deregister-type

Removes a registered private extension from the CloudFormation registry.

Syntax
aws cloudformation deregister-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name}
Parameters Description
  • --type - The extension type (RESOURCE, MODULE, or HOOK)
  • --type-name - The name of the extension to deregister
  • --arn - (Alternative) The ARN of the extension to deregister
  • --version-id - (Optional) The specific version to deregister
Return Value

Returns no output on success. The extension is removed from the CloudFormation registry.

5 Detailed CloudFormation deregister-type Examples
Example 1: Deregister anihpj Custom Resource Type
#!/bin/bash
# List anihpj private extensions
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[?contains(TypeName, `anihpj`)].TypeName' \
  --output table

# Deregister custom Django resource
aws cloudformation deregister-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Django::Application

echo "anihpj custom resource deregistered"
