anihpj Private Extensions: --------------------------------- | anihpj::Django::Application | | anihpj::Custom::Database | --------------------------------- Deregistering anihpj::Django::Application... Type removed from registry anihpj custom resource deregistered
# Django queryset - Deregister custom resource from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension # Remove extension from registry extension = CloudFormationExtension.objects.get( type_name='anihpj::Django::Application' ) extension.delete() print(f"Extension deregistered: anihpj::Django::Application")
Example 2: Remove Old Version of anihpj Custom Extension
#!/bin/bash
# List all versions of anihpj custom type
aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Custom::DjangoDeployment \
  --query 'TypeVersionSummaries[*].[VersionId,TimeCreated,IsDefaultVersion]' \
  --output table

# Deregister old version
aws cloudformation deregister-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Custom::DjangoDeployment \
  --version-id 00000001

echo "Old version of anihpj custom extension removed"
Extension Versions: ------------------------------------------------ | 00000001 | 2025-11-15 | False | | 00000002 | 2026-01-10 | True | ------------------------------------------------ Deregistering version 00000001... Version removed Old version of anihpj custom extension removed Latest version (00000002) remains active
# Django queryset - Remove old version from apps.models import ExtensionVersion # Delete old version old_version = ExtensionVersion.objects.get( extension__type_name='anihpj::Custom::DjangoDeployment', version_id='00000001' ) old_version.delete() print(f"Version 00000001 removed")
Example 3: Clean Up anihpj Test Extensions
#!/bin/bash
# Find all anihpj test extensions
TEST_TYPES=$(aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[?contains(TypeName, `Test`)].TypeName' \
  --output text)

# Deregister each test type
for TYPE in $TEST_TYPES; do
  echo "Deregistering $TYPE..."
  aws cloudformation deregister-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $TYPE
done

echo "All anihpj test extensions cleaned up"
Deregistering anihpj::Test::DjangoResource... Type removed Deregistering anihpj::Test::DatabaseMigration... Type removed Deregistering anihpj::Test::CacheConfig... Type removed All anihpj test extensions cleaned up
# Django queryset - Bulk cleanup test extensions from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension # Delete all test extensions test_extensions = CloudFormationExtension.objects.filter( type_name__icontains='Test', visibility='PRIVATE' ) count = test_extensions.count() test_extensions.delete() print(f"Deleted {count} test extensions")
Example 4: Deregister anihpj Hook Handler
#!/bin/bash
# List anihpj hooks
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type HOOK \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].[TypeName,Description]' \
  --output table

# Deregister security validation hook
aws cloudformation deregister-type \
  --type HOOK \
  --type-name anihpj::Security::ValidationHook

echo "anihpj security hook deregistered"
anihpj Hooks: --------------------------------------------------------- | anihpj::Security::ValidationHook | Validates security | | anihpj::Cost::EstimationHook | Estimates costs | --------------------------------------------------------- Deregistering anihpj::Security::ValidationHook... Hook removed from registry anihpj security hook deregistered
# Django queryset - Deregister hook from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension # Remove security hook hook = CloudFormationExtension.objects.get( type_name='anihpj::Security::ValidationHook', type='HOOK' ) hook.delete() print("Security hook deregistered")
Example 5: Complete Cleanup of anihpj Private Extension
#!/bin/bash
EXTENSION="anihpj::Django::CustomResource"

# Check if extension is in use
echo "Checking if $EXTENSION is used in any stacks..."
STACKS=$(aws cloudformation list-stacks \
  --stack-status-filter CREATE_COMPLETE UPDATE_COMPLETE \
  --query 'StackSummaries[].StackName' \
  --output text)

IN_USE=false
for STACK in $STACKS; do
  if aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
    --stack-name $STACK \
    --query "StackResources[?ResourceType=='$EXTENSION']" \
    --output text | grep -q .; then
    echo "WARNING: $EXTENSION in use by $STACK"
    IN_USE=true
  fi
done

if [ "$IN_USE" = false ]; then
  # Deactivate first
  aws cloudformation deactivate-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $EXTENSION
  
  # Then deregister
  aws cloudformation deregister-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $EXTENSION
  
  echo "$EXTENSION completely removed from anihpj"
else
  echo "Cannot deregister - extension in use"
fi
Checking if anihpj::Django::CustomResource is used in any stacks... No active usage found Deactivating extension... Status: DEACTIVATED Deregistering extension... Type removed from registry anihpj::Django::CustomResource completely removed from anihpj
# Django queryset - Complete extension cleanup from apps.models import CloudFormationExtension, Stack # Check if extension in use extension = CloudFormationExtension.objects.get( type_name='anihpj::Django::CustomResource' ) in_use = Stack.objects.filter( resources__resource_type=extension.type_name ).exists() if not in_use: extension.delete() print("Extension completely removed")

describe-events

Returns all stack and resource events for a stack. Alias for describe-stack-events.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name {stack-name}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name - Name or ID of the stack
  • --next-token - (Optional) Token for pagination
Return Value

Returns StackEvents array with Timestamp, ResourceStatus, ResourceType, LogicalResourceId, and ResourceStatusReason for each event.

5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-events Examples
Example 1: View Recent Events for anihpj Django Stack
#!/bin/bash
# Get recent events for anihpj stack
aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --max-items 10 \
  --query 'StackEvents[*].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,ResourceType,LogicalResourceId]' \
  --output table
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeEvents | +-------------------------+------------------+----------------------+----------------+ | 2026-01-17T10:30:00Z | UPDATE_COMPLETE | AWS::CloudFormation | anihpj-... | | 2026-01-17T10:29:45Z | UPDATE_COMPLETE | AWS::RDS::DBInstance| AnihpjDB | | 2026-01-17T10:28:30Z | UPDATE_IN_PROG..| AWS::RDS::DBInstance| AnihpjDB | | 2026-01-17T10:27:00Z | UPDATE_IN_PROG..| AWS::CloudFormation | anihpj-... | +-------------------------+------------------+----------------------+----------------+ anihpj stack update events
# Django queryset - Stack events tracking from apps.models import StackEvent, Stack # Get recent events events = StackEvent.objects.filter( stack__name='anihpj-django-app' ).order_by('-timestamp')[:10] for event in events: print(f"{event.timestamp}: {event.resource_status} - {event.logical_resource_id}")
Example 2: Monitor anihpj Stack Creation Events
#!/bin/bash
# Watch stack creation events in real-time
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"

while true; do
  clear
  echo "anihpj Stack Events - $(date)"
  echo "================================"
  
  aws cloudformation describe-events \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --max-items 15 \
    --query 'StackEvents[*].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,LogicalResourceId,ResourceStatusReason]' \
    --output table
  
  # Check if stack creation complete
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
    --output text)
  
  if [[ $STATUS == "CREATE_COMPLETE" ]] || [[ $STATUS == *"FAILED"* ]]; then
    echo "Final Status: $STATUS"
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 10
done
anihpj Stack Events - Fri Jan 17 10:30:00 2026 ================================ | 2026-01-17T10:30:00Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | anihpj-django-production | | | 2026-01-17T10:29:45Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | AnihpjDatabase | | | 2026-01-17T10:28:30Z | CREATE_IN_PROGRESS | AnihpjDatabase | Resource creation initiated | Final Status: CREATE_COMPLETE
# Django queryset - Monitor stack creation from apps.models import Stack, StackEvent # Check stack status stack = Stack.objects.get(name='anihpj-django-production') is_complete = stack.status in ['CREATE_COMPLETE', 'CREATE_FAILED'] # Get latest events events = StackEvent.objects.filter(stack=stack).order_by('-timestamp')[:15] print(f"Stack status: {stack.status}, Complete: {is_complete}")
Example 3: Find Failed Resources in anihpj Stack
#!/bin/bash
# Find all failed events for anihpj stack
aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackEvents[?contains(ResourceStatus, `FAILED`)].[Timestamp,ResourceType,LogicalResourceId,ResourceStatusReason]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "anihpj Stack Failures Summary:"
aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --query 'StackEvents[?contains(ResourceStatus, `FAILED`)] | length(@)' \
  --output text | xargs -I {} echo "Total failures: {}"
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeEvents | +----------------------+--------------------+------------------+-------------------------+ | 2026-01-17T09:15:00Z | AWS::EC2::Security | AnihpjSG | Invalid CIDR block | | 2026-01-17T08:45:00Z | AWS::RDS::DBInst.. | AnihpjDB | Insufficient storage | +----------------------+--------------------+------------------+-------------------------+ anihpj Stack Failures Summary: Total failures: 2
# Django queryset - Find failed resources from apps.models import StackEvent # Get failed events failed_events = StackEvent.objects.filter( stack__name='anihpj-django-app', resource_status__icontains='FAILED' ) print(f"Total failures: {failed_events.count()}") for event in failed_events: print(f"{event.logical_resource_id}: {event.status_reason}")
Example 4: Filter anihpj Events by Resource Type
#!/bin/bash
# Show only RDS events for anihpj database
aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --query 'StackEvents[?ResourceType==`AWS::RDS::DBInstance`].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,LogicalResourceId,ResourceStatusReason]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "EC2 Instance Events:"
aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --query 'StackEvents[?ResourceType==`AWS::EC2::Instance`].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,LogicalResourceId]' \
  --output table
RDS Events: ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | 2026-01-17T10:30:00Z | UPDATE_COMPLETE | AnihpjDatabase | | | 2026-01-17T10:28:30Z | UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS | AnihpjDatabase | Requested update | | 2026-01-17T09:00:00Z | CREATE_COMPLETE | AnihpjDatabase | | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ EC2 Instance Events: ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | 2026-01-17T10:15:00Z | UPDATE_COMPLETE | AnihpjWebServer | | | 2026-01-17T10:14:00Z | UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS | AnihpjWebServer | | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Django queryset - Filter events by resource type from apps.models import StackEvent # Get RDS events rds_events = StackEvent.objects.filter( stack__name='anihpj-django-infrastructure', resource_type='AWS::RDS::DBInstance' ).order_by('-timestamp') # Get EC2 events ec2_events = StackEvent.objects.filter( stack__name='anihpj-django-infrastructure', resource_type='AWS::EC2::Instance' ).order_by('-timestamp')
Example 5: Export anihpj Stack Events to File
#!/bin/bash
# Export all events for anihpj stack to JSON
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
OUTPUT_FILE="anihpj-stack-events-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --output json > $OUTPUT_FILE

# Create readable report
echo "anihpj Stack Events Report" > anihpj-events-report.txt
echo "Generated: $(date)" >> anihpj-events-report.txt
echo "Stack: $STACK_NAME" >> anihpj-events-report.txt
echo "================================" >> anihpj-events-report.txt

aws cloudformation describe-events \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'StackEvents[*].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,LogicalResourceId]' \
  --output text >> anihpj-events-report.txt

echo "Events exported to $OUTPUT_FILE and anihpj-events-report.txt"
Events exported to anihpj-stack-events-20260117.json and anihpj-events-report.txt anihpj Stack Events Report Generated: Fri Jan 17 10:30:00 2026 Stack: anihpj-django-app ================================ 2026-01-17T10:30:00Z UPDATE_COMPLETE anihpj-django-app 2026-01-17T10:29:45Z UPDATE_COMPLETE AnihpjDatabase ... Total events logged: 47
# Django queryset - Export stack events from apps.models import StackEvent, Report from django.utils import timezone import json # Get all events for stack events = StackEvent.objects.filter( stack__name='anihpj-django-app' ).values() # Create report report = Report.objects.create( name='anihpj-stack-events', stack_name='anihpj-django-app', generated_at=timezone.now(), data=json.dumps(list(events), default=str) ) print(f"Report created: {report.name}, Events: {len(events)}")

create-generated-template

Creates a generated template from existing AWS resources to enable importing them into CloudFormation. This reverse-engineers your existing anihpj Django infrastructure into a CloudFormation template for better management and version control.

Syntax
aws cloudformation create-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name {template-name} \
  --resources ResourceIdentifier={id},ResourceType={type} \
  --template-configuration {config} \
  --region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --generated-template-name (string, required): Name for the generated template. Example: anihpj-django-import
  • --resources (list): Resources to include. Each entry needs ResourceIdentifier and ResourceType
  • --template-configuration (structure): Configuration including DeletionPolicy and UpdateReplacePolicy
  • --region (string): AWS region where resources exist. Example: us-east-1
Return Value
  • GeneratedTemplateId (string): Unique identifier for the generated template
5 Detailed CloudFormation create-generated-template Examples
Example 1: Create Template from Existing VPC

Generate a CloudFormation template from existing anihpj VPC resources.

#!/bin/bash
# Create generated template from existing VPC
aws cloudformation create-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-vpc-import \
  --resources \
    "ResourceIdentifier={Vpc=vpc-0abc123def456789},ResourceType=AWS::EC2::VPC" \
    "ResourceIdentifier={SubnetId=subnet-0123456789abcdef},ResourceType=AWS::EC2::Subnet" \
  --template-configuration '{
    "DeletionPolicy": "RETAIN",
    "UpdateReplacePolicy": "RETAIN"
  }' \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1
{ "GeneratedTemplateId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:generatedtemplate/anihpj-vpc-import" } Template generation initiated for anihpj VPC resources Status: CREATE_IN_PROGRESS Resources: 2
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/create_generated_template.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Create a generated template from existing resources' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--template-name', type=str, required=True) parser.add_argument('--vpc-id', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') template_name = options['template_name'] vpc_id = options['vpc_id'] # Create IaC skill iac_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure as Code', defaults={'description': 'Convert resources to code'} )[0] import_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Resource Import', defaults={'description': 'Import existing resources'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get import user import_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='resource_importer', defaults={'email': 'import@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author import_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=import_user, defaults={'bio': 'Resource import specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Import location import_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Import Center', state='Migration', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create generated template response = cf_client.create_generated_template( GeneratedTemplateName=template_name, Resources=[ { 'ResourceIdentifier': {'Vpc': vpc_id}, 'ResourceType': 'AWS::EC2::VPC' } ], TemplateConfiguration={ 'DeletionPolicy': 'RETAIN', 'UpdateReplacePolicy': 'RETAIN' } ) template_id = response.get('GeneratedTemplateId') print(f"\nTemplate Created: {template_name}") print(f"ID: {template_id}") # Create import job import_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Generate Template: {template_name}', description=f'Template ID: {template_id}', author=import_author, location=import_location, job_type='IMPORT', is_active=True ) import_post.required_skills.add(iac_skill, import_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Import Full Django Infrastructure

Create a template from all anihpj Django application resources.

#!/bin/bash
# Import complete Django infrastructure
aws cloudformation create-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-django-full-import \
  --resources \
    "ResourceIdentifier={Vpc=vpc-0abc123},ResourceType=AWS::EC2::VPC" \
    "ResourceIdentifier={InstanceId=i-0123456789abcdef},ResourceType=AWS::EC2::Instance" \
    "ResourceIdentifier={DBInstanceIdentifier=anihpj-db},ResourceType=AWS::RDS::DBInstance" \
    "ResourceIdentifier={LoadBalancerArn=arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...},ResourceType=AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::LoadBalancer" \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Full infrastructure template generation initiated"
{ "GeneratedTemplateId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:generatedtemplate/anihpj-django-full-import" } ✓ Full infrastructure template generation initiated Resources included: - VPC and networking - EC2 Django instances - RDS database - Load balancer
# anihpj/jobpost/services/infrastructure_importer.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class InfrastructureImporter: def __init__(self, template_name): self.template_name = template_name self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def import_full_stack(self, resources_config): # Create full import skills full_import_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Full Stack Import', defaults={'description': 'Import complete infrastructure'} )[0] migration_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Cloud Migration', defaults={'description': 'Migrate to managed infrastructure'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get migration user migration_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='migration_lead', defaults={'email': 'migration@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author migration_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=migration_user, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure migration lead'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Migration HQ migration_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Migration HQ', state='Operations', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create generated template response = self.cf_client.create_generated_template( GeneratedTemplateName=self.template_name, Resources=resources_config, TemplateConfiguration={ 'DeletionPolicy': 'RETAIN', 'UpdateReplacePolicy': 'RETAIN' } ) template_id = response.get('GeneratedTemplateId') print(f"\n✓ Full import initiated: {self.template_name}") print(f"Resources: {len(resources_config)}") # Create migration job migration_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Full Import: {self.template_name}', description=f'Resources: {len(resources_config)}', author=migration_author, location=migration_location, job_type='MIGRATION', is_active=True ) migration_post.required_skills.add(full_import_skill, migration_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return template_id
Example 3: Create Template from Resource Scan

Use resource scan results to create a generated template.

#!/bin/bash
# First, start a resource scan
SCAN_ID=$(aws cloudformation start-resource-scan \
  --query 'ResourceScanId' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

echo "Scan started: $SCAN_ID"
echo "Waiting for scan to complete..."
sleep 60

# Create template from scan results
aws cloudformation create-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-scanned-import \
  --resources "ResourceScanId=$SCAN_ID" \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1
Scan started: scan-abc123def456 Waiting for scan to complete... { "GeneratedTemplateId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:generatedtemplate/anihpj-scanned-import" } Template created from scan results Discovered resources: 25 Template status: CREATE_IN_PROGRESS
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Scan-based template creation from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import time class ScanBasedTemplateView(View): def post(self, request): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') template_name = request.POST.get('template_name') # Create scan skills scan_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Resource Scanning', defaults={'description': 'Scan and discover resources'} )[0] discovery_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Resource Discovery', defaults={'description': 'Automatic resource discovery'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get scanner user scanner = User.objects.get_or_create( username='resource_scanner', defaults={'email': 'scanner@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author scanner_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=scanner, defaults={'bio': 'Resource scanning specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Scan center scan_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Scan Center', state='Discovery', country='AWS', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Start scan scan_response = cf_client.start_resource_scan() scan_id = scan_response.get('ResourceScanId') # Wait for scan (simplified) time.sleep(30) # Create template from scan template_response = cf_client.create_generated_template( GeneratedTemplateName=template_name, Resources=[{'ResourceScanId': scan_id}] ) template_id = template_response.get('GeneratedTemplateId') # Create scan job scan_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Scan Import: {template_name}', description=f'Scan ID: {scan_id}', author=scanner_author, location=scan_location, job_type='SCAN_IMPORT', is_active=True ) scan_post.required_skills.add(scan_skill, discovery_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({ 'scan_id': scan_id, 'template_id': template_id })
Example 4: Create Template with Retain Policies

Generate template with resource protection policies.

#!/bin/bash
# Create template with deletion protection
aws cloudformation create-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-protected-import \
  --resources \
    "ResourceIdentifier={DBInstanceIdentifier=anihpj-production-db},ResourceType=AWS::RDS::DBInstance" \
  --template-configuration '{
    "DeletionPolicy": "SNAPSHOT",
    "UpdateReplacePolicy": "SNAPSHOT"
  }' \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Template created with snapshot protection"
{ "GeneratedTemplateId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:generatedtemplate/anihpj-protected-import" } ✓ Template created with snapshot protection Configuration: - DeletionPolicy: SNAPSHOT (create snapshot before delete) - UpdateReplacePolicy: SNAPSHOT (create snapshot before replace)
# anihpj/jobpost/services/protected_template_creator.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ProtectedTemplateCreator: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def create_protected(self, template_name, resources, deletion_policy='RETAIN'): # Create protection skills protection_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Data Protection', defaults={'description': 'Protect critical resources'} )[0] backup_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Backup Management', defaults={'description': 'Manage resource backups'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get protection user protect_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='data_protector', defaults={'email': 'protect@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author protect_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=protect_user, defaults={'bio': 'Data protection specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Protection center protect_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Protection Center', state='Security', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create protected template response = self.cf_client.create_generated_template( GeneratedTemplateName=template_name, Resources=resources, TemplateConfiguration={ 'DeletionPolicy': deletion_policy, 'UpdateReplacePolicy': deletion_policy } ) template_id = response.get('GeneratedTemplateId') print(f"\n✓ Protected template: {template_name}") print(f"Policy: {deletion_policy}") # Create protection job protect_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Protected Import: {template_name}', description=f'Policy: {deletion_policy}', author=protect_author, location=protect_location, job_type='PROTECTED_IMPORT', is_active=False ) protect_post.required_skills.add(protection_skill, backup_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return template_id
Example 5: Batch Create Templates for Multi-Region

Create generated templates for resources across multiple regions.

#!/bin/bash
# Create templates for each region
REGIONS=("us-east-1" "us-west-2" "eu-west-1")

for REGION in "${REGIONS[@]}"; do
  TEMPLATE_NAME="anihpj-import-$REGION"
  
  # Get VPC ID for region
  VPC_ID=$(aws ec2 describe-vpcs \
    --filters "Name=tag:Name,Values=anihpj-vpc" \
    --query 'Vpcs[0].VpcId' \
    --output text \
    --region $REGION)
  
  if [ "$VPC_ID" != "None" ]; then
    aws cloudformation create-generated-template \
      --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
      --resources "ResourceIdentifier={Vpc=$VPC_ID},ResourceType=AWS::EC2::VPC" \
      --region $REGION
    
    echo "✓ Created template: $TEMPLATE_NAME"
  else
    echo "✗ No VPC found in $REGION"
  fi
done
✓ Created template: anihpj-import-us-east-1 ✓ Created template: anihpj-import-us-west-2 ✓ Created template: anihpj-import-eu-west-1 Multi-region import templates created: 3 Ready for cross-region infrastructure management
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/multi_region_import.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Create generated templates across multiple regions' def handle(self, *args, **options): regions = ['us-east-1', 'us-west-2', 'eu-west-1'] # Create multi-region skill multi_region_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-Region Management', defaults={'description': 'Manage resources across regions'} )[0] global_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Global Infrastructure', defaults={'description': 'Worldwide infrastructure operations'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get global admin global_admin = User.objects.get_or_create( username='global_admin', defaults={'email': 'global@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author global_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=global_admin, defaults={'bio': 'Global infrastructure administrator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Global ops location global_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Global Operations', state='Multi-Region', country='Worldwide', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") created_templates = [] for region in regions: cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name=region) ec2_client = boto3.client('ec2', region_name=region) # Find VPC vpcs = ec2_client.describe_vpcs( Filters=[{'Name': 'tag:Name', 'Values': ['anihpj-vpc']}] ) if vpcs['Vpcs']: vpc_id = vpcs['Vpcs'][0]['VpcId'] template_name = f'anihpj-import-{region}' response = cf_client.create_generated_template( GeneratedTemplateName=template_name, Resources=[{ 'ResourceIdentifier': {'Vpc': vpc_id}, 'ResourceType': 'AWS::EC2::VPC' }] ) created_templates.append(template_name) print(f"✓ {template_name}") print(f"\nTemplates created: {len(created_templates)}") # Create global import job global_post = JobPost.objects.create( title='Multi-Region Import', description=f'Regions: {len(created_templates)}', author=global_author, location=global_location, job_type='MULTI_REGION_IMPORT', is_active=False ) global_post.required_skills.add(multi_region_skill, global_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

delete-generated-template

Deletes a generated template that was created for importing resources into CloudFormation. Use this to clean up unused templates after successful imports or to remove failed import attempts for your anihpj infrastructure.

Syntax
aws cloudformation delete-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name {template-name} \
  --region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --generated-template-name (string, required): Name of the generated template to delete. Example: anihpj-django-import
  • --region (string): AWS region where the template was created. Example: us-east-1
Return Value
  • No return value on success. HTTP 200 status indicates successful deletion.
5 Detailed CloudFormation delete-generated-template Examples
Example 1: Delete Unused Import Template

Remove a generated template after successful resource import.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete generated template after successful import
TEMPLATE_NAME="anihpj-django-import-completed"

aws cloudformation delete-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Deleted template: $TEMPLATE_NAME"
✓ Deleted template: anihpj-django-import-completed Template cleanup complete Resources were already imported to stack
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/delete_generated_template.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Delete a generated template' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--template-name', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') template_name = options['template_name'] # Create cleanup skill cleanup_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Resource Cleanup', defaults={'description': 'Clean up unused resources'} )[0] maintenance_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Maintenance', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure maintenance'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get cleanup user cleanup_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='cleanup_admin', defaults={'email': 'cleanup@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author cleanup_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=cleanup_user, defaults={'bio': 'Resource cleanup administrator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Maintenance location maint_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Maintenance Center', state='Operations', country='Corporate', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Delete the template cf_client.delete_generated_template( GeneratedTemplateName=template_name ) print(f"\n✓ Deleted: {template_name}") # Create cleanup job cleanup_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Template Cleanup: {template_name}', description='Generated template deleted', author=cleanup_author, location=maint_location, job_type='CLEANUP', is_active=False ) cleanup_post.required_skills.add(cleanup_skill, maintenance_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Delete Failed Import Template

Clean up a template that failed during generation.

#!/bin/bash
# Check status first
STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-failed-import \
  --query 'Status' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

echo "Template status: $STATUS"

if [ "$STATUS" = "FAILED" ]; then
  aws cloudformation delete-generated-template \
    --generated-template-name anihpj-failed-import \
    --region us-east-1
  echo "✓ Failed template deleted"
fi
Template status: FAILED ✓ Failed template deleted Cleanup reason: Template generation failed Resources that caused failure: 2
# anihpj/jobpost/services/template_cleanup.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class TemplateCleanup: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def cleanup_failed(self, template_name): # Create failure handling skills failure_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Failure Handling', defaults={'description': 'Handle failed operations'} )[0] recovery_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Recovery Operations', defaults={'description': 'Recover from failures'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get recovery user recovery_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='recovery_admin', defaults={'email': 'recovery@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author recovery_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=recovery_user, defaults={'bio': 'Failure recovery specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Recovery center recovery_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Recovery Center', state='Operations', country='Corporate', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Check status try: response = self.cf_client.describe_generated_template( GeneratedTemplateName=template_name ) status = response.get('Status') if status == 'FAILED': self.cf_client.delete_generated_template( GeneratedTemplateName=template_name ) print(f"✓ Failed template deleted: {template_name}") # Create recovery job recovery_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Failed Cleanup: {template_name}', description='Failed template removed', author=recovery_author, location=recovery_location, job_type='RECOVERY', is_active=False ) recovery_post.required_skills.add(failure_skill, recovery_skill) else: print(f"Template status is {status}, not deleting") except Exception as e: print(f"Error: {e}") print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 3: Batch Delete Old Templates

Clean up all old generated templates from previous import attempts.

#!/bin/bash
# List and delete old templates
echo "Cleaning up old generated templates..."

OLD_TEMPLATES=$(aws cloudformation list-generated-templates \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`COMPLETE`].GeneratedTemplateName' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

for TEMPLATE in $OLD_TEMPLATES; do
  echo "Deleting: $TEMPLATE"
  aws cloudformation delete-generated-template \
    --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE \
    --region us-east-1
done

echo ""
echo "✓ Cleanup complete"
Cleaning up old generated templates... Deleting: anihpj-import-20260101 Deleting: anihpj-import-20260110 Deleting: anihpj-test-import ✓ Cleanup complete Templates deleted: 3 Storage reclaimed
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/batch_cleanup_templates.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Batch delete old generated templates' def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') # Create batch cleanup skills batch_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Batch Operations', defaults={'description': 'Bulk resource management'} )[0] housekeeping_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Housekeeping', defaults={'description': 'Regular maintenance tasks'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get housekeeping user housekeeper = User.objects.get_or_create( username='housekeeper', defaults={'email': 'housekeeping@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author housekeeper_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=housekeeper, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure housekeeping'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Housekeeping location house_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Housekeeping', state='Maintenance', country='Corporate', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # List all templates response = cf_client.list_generated_templates() templates = response.get('Summaries', []) # Delete completed ones deleted = 0 for template in templates: if template.get('Status') == 'COMPLETE': name = template.get('GeneratedTemplateName') cf_client.delete_generated_template(GeneratedTemplateName=name) print(f"Deleted: {name}") deleted += 1 print(f"\n✓ Deleted {deleted} templates") # Create batch cleanup job batch_post = JobPost.objects.create( title='Batch Template Cleanup', description=f'Deleted {deleted} templates', author=housekeeper_author, location=house_location, job_type='BATCH_CLEANUP', is_active=False ) batch_post.required_skills.add(batch_skill, housekeeping_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 4: Delete with Confirmation

Safely delete a template with user confirmation.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete with confirmation
TEMPLATE_NAME="anihpj-production-import"

# Show template details first
echo "Template to delete:"
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --query '{Name:GeneratedTemplateName,Status:Status,Resources:TotalResourcesCount}' \
  --output table \
  --region us-east-1

read -p "Delete this template? (y/N): " CONFIRM

if [ "$CONFIRM" = "y" ] || [ "$CONFIRM" = "Y" ]; then
  aws cloudformation delete-generated-template \
    --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
    --region us-east-1
  echo "✓ Template deleted"
else
  echo "Deletion cancelled"
fi
Template to delete: +---------------------------+--------+-----------+ | Name | Status | Resources | +---------------------------+--------+-----------+ | anihpj-production-import | COMPLETE | 15 | +---------------------------+--------+-----------+ Delete this template? (y/N): y ✓ Template deleted
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Confirmed deletion API from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class ConfirmedDeleteView(View): def post(self, request): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') template_name = request.POST.get('template_name') confirmed = request.POST.get('confirmed') == 'true' # Create confirmation skills confirm_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Confirmed Operations', defaults={'description': 'Operations requiring confirmation'} )[0] safety_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Safe Deletion', defaults={'description': 'Safe resource deletion'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get users users = User.objects.all() print(f"Users: {users.count()}") # Get authors authors = Author.objects.all() print(f"Authors: {authors.count()}") # Get locations locations = Location.objects.all() print(f"Locations: {locations.count()}") if not confirmed: # Return template info for confirmation response = cf_client.describe_generated_template( GeneratedTemplateName=template_name ) return JsonResponse({ 'require_confirmation': True, 'template_name': template_name, 'status': response.get('Status'), 'resources': response.get('TotalResourcesCount') }) # Delete confirmed cf_client.delete_generated_template(GeneratedTemplateName=template_name) # Track deletion in jobs deletion_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains='Cleanup') print(f"Related jobs: {deletion_jobs.count()}") return JsonResponse({ 'deleted': True, 'template_name': template_name })
Example 5: Delete Templates Across Regions

Clean up generated templates from multiple regions.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete templates across all regions
TEMPLATE_PREFIX="anihpj-import"
REGIONS=("us-east-1" "us-west-2" "eu-west-1")

for REGION in "${REGIONS[@]}"; do
  echo "Checking $REGION..."
  
  TEMPLATES=$(aws cloudformation list-generated-templates \
    --query "Summaries[?starts_with(GeneratedTemplateName, '$TEMPLATE_PREFIX')].GeneratedTemplateName" \
    --output text \
    --region $REGION)
  
  for TEMPLATE in $TEMPLATES; do
    aws cloudformation delete-generated-template \
      --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE \
      --region $REGION
    echo "  ✓ Deleted: $TEMPLATE"
  done
done

echo ""
echo "Multi-region cleanup complete"
Checking us-east-1... ✓ Deleted: anihpj-import-vpc ✓ Deleted: anihpj-import-db Checking us-west-2... ✓ Deleted: anihpj-import-vpc Checking eu-west-1... ✓ Deleted: anihpj-import-vpc Multi-region cleanup complete Total deleted: 4 templates
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/multi_region_cleanup.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Delete generated templates across multiple regions' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--prefix', type=str, default='anihpj-import') def handle(self, *args, **options): regions = ['us-east-1', 'us-west-2', 'eu-west-1'] prefix = options['prefix'] # Create multi-region cleanup skills multi_cleanup_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-Region Cleanup', defaults={'description': 'Clean resources across regions'} )[0] global_ops_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Global Operations', defaults={'description': 'Worldwide infrastructure management'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get global admin global_admin = User.objects.get_or_create( username='global_cleanup', defaults={'email': 'global-cleanup@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author global_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=global_admin, defaults={'bio': 'Global cleanup administrator'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Global location global_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Global Cleanup', state='Multi-Region', country='Worldwide', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") total_deleted = 0 for region in regions: cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name=region) response = cf_client.list_generated_templates() templates = response.get('Summaries', []) for template in templates: name = template.get('GeneratedTemplateName') if name.startswith(prefix): cf_client.delete_generated_template(GeneratedTemplateName=name) print(f"✓ {region}: {name}") total_deleted += 1 print(f"\nTotal deleted: {total_deleted}") # Create global cleanup job global_post = JobPost.objects.create( title='Multi-Region Cleanup', description=f'Deleted {total_deleted} templates across {len(regions)} regions', author=global_author, location=global_location, job_type='GLOBAL_CLEANUP', is_active=False ) global_post.required_skills.add(multi_cleanup_skill, global_ops_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

describe-generated-template

Describes a generated template including its status and resources.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name {template-name}
Parameters Description
  • --generated-template-name - Name or ARN of the generated template
Return Value

Returns GeneratedTemplateId, GeneratedTemplateName, Status, StatusReason, Resources, Progress, and TotalWarnings.

5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-generated-template Examples
Example 1: Check Status of anihpj Generated Template
#!/bin/bash
# Describe generated template for anihpj infrastructure
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-django-import \
  --query '{Name:GeneratedTemplateName,Status:Status,ResourceCount:TotalResourcesCount,CreatedTime:CreationTime}' \
  --output table
---------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeGeneratedTemplate | +----------------+--------------------+------------------------+ | CreatedTime | 2026-01-17T09:00:00Z | | Name | anihpj-django-import | | ResourceCount | 15 | | Status | COMPLETE | +----------------+--------------------+------------------------+ anihpj generated template ready for use
# Django Models Integration - Describe Generated Template from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track template status template_name = 'anihpj-django-import' status = 'COMPLETE' resource_count = 15 # Create template skill template_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Generated Templates', defaults={'description': 'IaC template generation'} )[0] print(f"Template: {template_name}") print(f"Status: {status}") print(f"Resources: {resource_count}") print(f"<Skills: {template_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: Generated Templates>
Example 2: View Resources in anihpj Generated Template
#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed resource information
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-infrastructure-import \
  --query 'Resources[*].[LogicalResourceId,ResourceType,ResourceIdentifier]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Template Statistics:"
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-infrastructure-import \
  --query '{Total:TotalResourcesCount,Pending:PendingResourcesCount,Complete:CompleteResourcesCount}' \
  --output json | jq '.'
------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeGeneratedTemplate | +-------------------+----------------------+-----------------------------+ | AnihpjVPC | AWS::EC2::VPC | vpc-abc123 | | AnihpjSubnet1 | AWS::EC2::Subnet | subnet-def456 | | AnihpjDatabase | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | anihpj-db | | AnihpjInstance | AWS::EC2::Instance | i-0123456789abcdef0 | +-------------------+----------------------+-----------------------------+ Template Statistics: { "Total": 15, "Pending": 0, "Complete": 15 } anihpj infrastructure template generation complete
# Django Models Integration - Template Resources from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track template resources template_name = 'anihpj-infrastructure-import' resources = [ {'logical': 'AnihpjVPC', 'type': 'AWS::EC2::VPC'}, {'logical': 'AnihpjSubnet1', 'type': 'AWS::EC2::Subnet'}, {'logical': 'AnihpjDatabase', 'type': 'AWS::RDS::DBInstance'}, {'logical': 'AnihpjInstance', 'type': 'AWS::EC2::Instance'} ] for res in resources: skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name=f"cf:{res['logical']}", defaults={'description': res['type']} )[0] print(f"{res['logical']}: {res['type']}") print(f"\nTotal: {len(resources)} resources") print(f"<QuerySet [resources: {len(resources)}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [resources: 4]>
Example 3: Monitor anihpj Template Generation Progress
#!/bin/bash
TEMPLATE_NAME="anihpj-django-migration"

echo "Monitoring generation of $TEMPLATE_NAME..."

while true; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
    --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
    --query '{Status:Status,Total:TotalResourcesCount,Complete:CompleteResourcesCount,Pending:PendingResourcesCount}' \
    --output json)
  
  echo "$(date): $STATUS"
  
  STATUS_TEXT=$(echo $STATUS | jq -r '.Status')
  if [[ $STATUS_TEXT == "COMPLETE" ]] || [[ $STATUS_TEXT == "FAILED" ]]; then
    echo "Template generation finished: $STATUS_TEXT"
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 5
done

echo "anihpj template ready"
Monitoring generation of anihpj-django-migration... Fri Jan 17 10:25:00 2026: {"Status":"IN_PROGRESS","Total":15,"Complete":8,"Pending":7} Fri Jan 17 10:25:05 2026: {"Status":"IN_PROGRESS","Total":15,"Complete":12,"Pending":3} Fri Jan 17 10:25:10 2026: {"Status":"COMPLETE","Total":15,"Complete":15,"Pending":0} Template generation finished: COMPLETE anihpj template ready
# Django Models Integration - Monitor Template Progress from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import time # Monitor generation progress template_name = 'anihpj-django-migration' states = [ {'status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'complete': 8, 'pending': 7}, {'status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'complete': 12, 'pending': 3}, {'status': 'COMPLETE', 'complete': 15, 'pending': 0} ] monitoring_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Template Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Track generation progress'} )[0] for state in states: print(f"Status: {state['status']} - {state['complete']}/15") print(f"\nGeneration complete") print(f"<Skills: {monitoring_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: Template Monitoring>
Example 4: View anihpj Template Generation Warnings
#!/bin/bash
# Check for warnings or issues in generated template
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-django-import \
  --output json | jq '{
    Name: .GeneratedTemplateName,
    Status: .Status,
    Warnings: .StatusReason,
    Resources: .Resources[] | select(.Warnings != null) | {
      LogicalId: .LogicalResourceId,
      Type: .ResourceType,
      Warnings: .Warnings
    }
  }'

echo "anihpj template warnings reviewed"
{ "Name": "anihpj-django-import", "Status": "COMPLETE", "Warnings": null, "Resources": { "LogicalId": "AnihpjSecurityGroup", "Type": "AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup", "Warnings": "Some properties could not be imported automatically" } } anihpj template warnings reviewed Manual review recommended for AnihpjSecurityGroup
# Django Models Integration - Template Warnings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track template warnings template_name = 'anihpj-django-import' warnings = [ {'resource': 'AnihpjSecurityGroup', 'warning': 'Manual review needed'} ] warning_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Template Warnings', defaults={'description': 'Track generation issues'} )[0] for w in warnings: print(f"Warning: {w['resource']} - {w['warning']}") skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name=f"warning:{w['resource']}" )[0] print(f"\nTotal warnings: {len(warnings)}") print(f"<QuerySet [warnings: {len(warnings)}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [warnings: 1]>
Example 5: Export anihpj Generated Template
#!/bin/bash
# Get generated template details
TEMPLATE_NAME="anihpj-production-import"

# Describe the template
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --output json > anihpj-template-description.json

# Get the actual template body
aws cloudformation get-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --format YAML > anihpj-generated-template.yaml

echo "anihpj Generated Template Exported"
echo "=================================="
echo "Description: anihpj-template-description.json"
echo "Template: anihpj-generated-template.yaml"
echo ""
echo "Resources:"
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --query 'Resources[*].LogicalResourceId' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | nl
anihpj Generated Template Exported ================================== Description: anihpj-template-description.json Template: anihpj-generated-template.yaml Resources: 1 AnihpjVPC 2 AnihpjSubnet1 3 AnihpjSubnet2 4 AnihpjRouteTable 5 AnihpjSecurityGroup 6 AnihpjDatabase 7 AnihpjInstance 8 AnihpjLoadBalancer 9 AnihpjTargetGroup 10 AnihpjListener 11 AnihpjLaunchTemplate 12 AnihpjAutoScalingGroup 13 AnihpjCacheCluster 14 AnihpjS3Bucket 15 AnihpjIAMRole Template ready for deployment

describe-organizations-access

Retrieves the current status of Organizations access for CloudFormation StackSets.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access
Parameters Description
  • No parameters required - Returns the current Organizations access status
Return Value

Returns Status (ENABLED, DISABLED, or DISABLED_PERMANENTLY).

5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-organizations-access Examples
Example 1: Check anihpj Organizations Integration Status
#!/bin/bash
# Check if Organizations access is enabled for anihpj
aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access \
  --query '{Status:Status,StatusMessage:StatusMessage}' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj Organizations access status checked"
---------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeOrganizationsAccess | +---------------+----------------------------------------+ | Status | ENABLED | | StatusMessage | Organizations access is active | +---------------+----------------------------------------+ anihpj Organizations access status checked
# Django Models Integration - Organizations Access from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track Organizations access status status = 'ENABLED' # Create organization skill org_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='AWS Organizations', defaults={'description': 'Multi-account management'} )[0] print(f"Organizations Status: {status}") print(f"Ready for multi-account deployments") print(f"<Skills: {org_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: AWS Organizations>
Example 2: Verify anihpj Multi-Account Deployment Capability
#!/bin/bash
# Check Organizations access before creating StackSet
ORG_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access \
  --query 'Status' \
  --output text)

if [ "$ORG_STATUS" == "ENABLED" ]; then
  echo "✓ Organizations access enabled for anihpj"
  echo "✓ Ready for multi-account StackSet deployments"
  
  # List existing StackSets
  echo ""
  echo "Existing anihpj StackSets:"
  aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
    --status ACTIVE \
    --query 'Summaries[*].[StackSetName,PermissionModel]' \
    --output table
else
  echo "✗ Organizations access not enabled"
  echo "Run: aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access"
fi
✓ Organizations access enabled for anihpj ✓ Ready for multi-account StackSet deployments Existing anihpj StackSets: --------------------------------------------------------- | anihpj-django-infrastructure | SERVICE_MANAGED | | anihpj-security-baseline | SERVICE_MANAGED | | anihpj-cicd-pipeline | SERVICE_MANAGED | --------------------------------------------------------- anihpj multi-account capability verified
# Django Models Integration - Multi-Account Capability from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Verify multi-account capability org_status = 'ENABLED' stack_sets = [ 'anihpj-django-infrastructure', 'anihpj-security-baseline', 'anihpj-cicd-pipeline' ] multi_account_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-Account Deployment', defaults={'description': 'Cross-account infrastructure'} )[0] print(f"Status: {org_status}") print(f"StackSets: {len(stack_sets)}") for ss in stack_sets: print(f" - {ss}") print(f"\n<QuerySet [stack_sets: {len(stack_sets)}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [stack_sets: 3]>
Example 3: Monitor anihpj Organizations Access During Maintenance
#!/bin/bash
# Continuous monitoring of Organizations access
echo "Monitoring anihpj Organizations access..."

while true; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access \
    --query 'Status' \
    --output text)
  
  TIMESTAMP=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
  echo "[$TIMESTAMP] Organizations Status: $STATUS"
  
  if [ "$STATUS" != "ENABLED" ]; then
    echo "WARNING: anihpj Organizations access is $STATUS"
    # Send notification
  fi
  
  sleep 60
done
Monitoring anihpj Organizations access... [2026-01-17 10:30:00] Organizations Status: ENABLED [2026-01-17 10:31:00] Organizations Status: ENABLED [2026-01-17 10:32:00] Organizations Status: ENABLED anihpj Organizations access stable
# Django Models Integration - Monitor Organizations Access from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import time # Monitor access status statuses = ['ENABLED', 'ENABLED', 'ENABLED'] monitoring_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Access Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Monitor organization access'} )[0] for i, status in enumerate(statuses): print(f"Check {i+1}: {status}") print(f"\nAll checks passed: {all(s == 'ENABLED' for s in statuses)}") print(f"<Skills: {monitoring_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: Access Monitoring>
Example 4: anihpj Pre-Deployment Organizations Check
#!/bin/bash
# Comprehensive pre-deployment check for anihpj
echo "anihpj Multi-Account Deployment Pre-Check"
echo "=========================================="

# Check Organizations access
ORG_ACCESS=$(aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access --output json)
echo "$ORG_ACCESS" | jq '{Status, StatusMessage}'

# Check if we're in Organizations management account
ORG_INFO=$(aws organizations describe-organization 2>/dev/null)
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "Organization Details:"
  echo "$ORG_INFO" | jq '{Id:.Organization.Id,MasterAccountId:.Organization.MasterAccountId}'
else
  echo "Not in Organizations management account"
fi

# Check StackSet limits
echo ""
echo "StackSet Capacity:"
aws cloudformation describe-account-limits \
  --query 'AccountLimits[?Name==`StackSetLimit` || Name==`StackInstancesPerStackSet`].[Name,Value]' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj ready for multi-account deployment"
anihpj Multi-Account Deployment Pre-Check ========================================== { "Status": "ENABLED", "StatusMessage": "Organizations access is active" } Organization Details: { "Id": "o-abc123def456", "MasterAccountId": "123456789012" } StackSet Capacity: --------------------------------------------------------- | StackSetLimit | 100 | | StackInstancesPerStackSet | 2000 | --------------------------------------------------------- anihpj ready for multi-account deployment
# Django Models Integration - Pre-Deployment Check from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Pre-deployment validation checks = { 'organizations_access': 'ENABLED', 'org_id': 'o-abc123def456', 'stack_set_limit': 100, 'instances_per_stack_set': 2000 } precheck_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Pre-Deployment Checks', defaults={'description': 'Validate deployment readiness'} )[0] all_passed = True for check, value in checks.items(): passed = value is not None all_passed = all_passed and passed print(f"{check}: {value} {'\u2713' if passed else '\u2717'}") print(f"\nReady: {all_passed}") print(f"<Skills: {precheck_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: Pre-Deployment Checks>
Example 5: anihpj Organizations Access Health Check
#!/bin/bash
# Complete health check for anihpj multi-account setup
echo "anihpj Organizations Health Check"
echo "================================="

# Organizations access status
ORG_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-organizations-access --query 'Status' --output text)
echo "Organizations Access: $ORG_STATUS"

# Check trust relationship
if [ "$ORG_STATUS" == "ENABLED" ]; then
  echo "✓ Organizations trust established"
  
  # Count StackSets using Organizations
  STACKSET_COUNT=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
    --status ACTIVE \
    --query 'length(Summaries[?PermissionModel==`SERVICE_MANAGED`])' \
    --output text)
  echo "✓ SERVICE_MANAGED StackSets: $STACKSET_COUNT"
  
  # Count total stack instances across organization
  TOTAL_INSTANCES=0
  for SS in $(aws cloudformation list-stack-sets --status ACTIVE --query 'Summaries[?PermissionModel==`SERVICE_MANAGED`].StackSetName' --output text); do
    COUNT=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-instances --stack-set-name $SS --query 'length(Summaries)' --output text)
    TOTAL_INSTANCES=$((TOTAL_INSTANCES + COUNT))
  done
  echo "✓ Total Stack Instances: $TOTAL_INSTANCES"
  
  echo ""
  echo "anihpj multi-account infrastructure healthy"
else
  echo "✗ Organizations access not enabled"
  echo "Run: aws cloudformation activate-organizations-access"
fi
anihpj Organizations Health Check ================================= Organizations Access: ENABLED ✓ Organizations trust established ✓ SERVICE_MANAGED StackSets: 3 ✓ Total Stack Instances: 12 anihpj multi-account infrastructure healthy
# Django Models Integration - Organizations Health Check from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Health check results health = { 'organizations_access': 'ENABLED', 'stacksets_count': 3, 'stack_instances': 12 } health_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Health Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Infrastructure health checks'} )[0] print("Health Check Results:") for key, value in health.items(): print(f" {key}: {value}") healthy = health['organizations_access'] == 'ENABLED' print(f"\nHealthy: {healthy}") print(f"<Skills: {health_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: Health Monitoring>

describe-publisher

Returns information about a CloudFormation extension publisher.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
  --publisher-id {publisher-id}
Parameters Description
  • --publisher-id - (Optional) The ID of the extension publisher
Return Value

Returns PublisherId, PublisherStatus, IdentityProvider, and PublisherProfile.

5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-publisher Examples
Example 1: Check MongoDB Atlas Publisher for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Get MongoDB Atlas publisher information
aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
  --publisher-id bb989456c78c398a858fef18f2ca1bfc1fbba082 \
  --query '{PublisherId:PublisherId,PublisherName:PublisherName,IdentityProvider:IdentityProvider,PublisherStatus:PublisherStatus}' \
  --output table

echo "Publisher verified for anihpj MongoDB integration"
------------------------------------------------------------------------ | DescribePublisher | +--------------------+------------------------------------------------+ | IdentityProvider | AWS_Marketplace | | PublisherId | bb989456c78c398a858fef18f2ca1bfc1fbba082 | | PublisherName | MongoDB | | PublisherStatus | VERIFIED | +--------------------+------------------------------------------------+ Publisher verified for anihpj MongoDB integration
# Django Models Integration - Describe Publisher from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track publisher info publisher = { 'id': 'bb989456c78c398a858fef18f2ca1bfc1fbba082', 'name': 'MongoDB', 'status': 'VERIFIED', 'provider': 'AWS_Marketplace' } publisher_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name=f"publisher:{publisher['name']}", defaults={'description': f"{publisher['status']} publisher"} )[0] print(f"Publisher: {publisher['name']}") print(f"Status: {publisher['status']}") print(f"ID: {publisher['id'][:8]}...") print(f"<Skills: {publisher_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: publisher:MongoDB>
Example 2: Verify Datadog Publisher for anihpj Monitoring
#!/bin/bash
# Check Datadog publisher details
aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
  --publisher-id 408988dff9e863704bcc72e7e13f8d645cee8311 \
  --output json | jq '{
    Name: .PublisherName,
    Status: .PublisherStatus,
    Verified: (.PublisherStatus == "VERIFIED"),
    Provider: .IdentityProvider
  }'

echo "anihpj monitoring extension publisher verified"
{ "Name": "Datadog", "Status": "VERIFIED", "Verified": true, "Provider": "AWS_Marketplace" } anihpj monitoring extension publisher verified Safe to use Datadog extensions
# Django Models Integration - Verify Publisher from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track Datadog publisher publisher = { 'name': 'Datadog', 'status': 'VERIFIED', 'verified': True, 'provider': 'AWS_Marketplace' } monitoring_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Datadog Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Application monitoring'} )[0] print(f"Publisher: {publisher['name']}") print(f"Verified: {publisher['verified']}") print(f"Safe to use: Yes") print(f"<Skills: {monitoring_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: Datadog Monitoring>
Example 3: List All Publishers Used by anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Get all activated extensions and their publishers
echo "anihpj Extension Publishers"
echo "============================"

aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PUBLIC \
  --provisioning-type FULLY_MUTABLE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].[TypeName,PublisherId]' \
  --output text | while read TYPE_NAME PUB_ID; do
  
  if [ -n "$PUB_ID" ]; then
    PUB_NAME=$(aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
      --publisher-id $PUB_ID \
      --query 'PublisherName' \
      --output text 2>/dev/null || echo "Unknown")
    
    echo "$TYPE_NAME -> $PUB_NAME ($PUB_ID)"
  fi
done
anihpj Extension Publishers ============================ MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster -> MongoDB (bb989456...) Datadog::Integrations::AWS -> Datadog (40898865...) NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads -> New Relic (759f81f1...) PagerDuty::EscalationPolicies -> PagerDuty (9f0b8a1d...) All publishers verified for anihpj
# Django Models Integration - List Publishers from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track all publishers publishers = [ {'type': 'MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster', 'name': 'MongoDB'}, {'type': 'Datadog::Integrations::AWS', 'name': 'Datadog'}, {'type': 'NewRelic::Observability', 'name': 'New Relic'}, {'type': 'PagerDuty::EscalationPolicies', 'name': 'PagerDuty'} ] for pub in publishers: skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name=f"ext:{pub['name']}", defaults={'description': pub['type']} )[0] print(f"{pub['type']} -> {pub['name']}") print(f"\n<QuerySet [publishers: {len(publishers)}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [publishers: 4]>
Example 4: Validate Publisher Before Activating Extension for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Validate publisher before using their extension
PUBLISHER_ID="bb989456c78c398a858fef18f2ca1bfc1fbba082"
EXTENSION_TYPE="MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster"

echo "Validating publisher for anihpj..."

PUB_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
  --publisher-id $PUBLISHER_ID \
  --output json)

PUB_STATUS=$(echo "$PUB_INFO" | jq -r '.PublisherStatus')
PUB_NAME=$(echo "$PUB_INFO" | jq -r '.PublisherName')

echo "Publisher: $PUB_NAME"
echo "Status: $PUB_STATUS"

if [ "$PUB_STATUS" == "VERIFIED" ]; then
  echo "✓ Publisher verified by AWS"
  echo "Activating $EXTENSION_TYPE for anihpj..."
  
  aws cloudformation activate-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $EXTENSION_TYPE \
    --publisher-id $PUBLISHER_ID
  
  echo "Extension activated for anihpj"
else
  echo "✗ Publisher not verified"
  echo "Consider security implications before proceeding"
fi
Validating publisher for anihpj... Publisher: MongoDB Status: VERIFIED ✓ Publisher verified by AWS Activating MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster for anihpj... Extension activation initiated Extension activated for anihpj
# Django Models Integration - Validate Publisher from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Validate before activation publisher = { 'id': 'bb989456c78c398a858fef18f2ca1bfc1fbba082', 'name': 'MongoDB', 'status': 'VERIFIED' } extension = 'MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster' validation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Extension Validation', defaults={'description': 'Validate publishers'} )[0] is_safe = publisher['status'] == 'VERIFIED' print(f"Publisher: {publisher['name']}") print(f"Safe: {is_safe}") print(f"Activating: {extension}") print(f"<Skills: {validation_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: Extension Validation>
Example 5: Generate anihpj Publisher Trust Report
#!/bin/bash
# Create comprehensive publisher report for anihpj
REPORT_FILE="anihpj-publisher-report.txt"

echo "anihpj CloudFormation Extension Publisher Report" > $REPORT_FILE
echo "Generated: $(date)" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "=================================================" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "" >> $REPORT_FILE

# Get all unique publisher IDs from activated extensions
PUBLISHERS=$(aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PUBLIC \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].PublisherId' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sort -u)

for PUB_ID in $PUBLISHERS; do
  if [ -n "$PUB_ID" ]; then
    echo "Publisher ID: $PUB_ID" >> $REPORT_FILE
    aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
      --publisher-id $PUB_ID \
      --query '{Name:PublisherName,Status:PublisherStatus,Provider:IdentityProvider}' \
      --output text >> $REPORT_FILE
    echo "" >> $REPORT_FILE
  fi
done

cat $REPORT_FILE
echo ""
echo "Report saved to $REPORT_FILE"
anihpj CloudFormation Extension Publisher Report Generated: Fri Jan 17 10:30:00 2026 ================================================= Publisher ID: bb989456c78c398a858fef18f2ca1bfc1fbba082 MongoDB VERIFIED AWS_Marketplace Publisher ID: 408988dff9e863704bcc72e7e13f8d645cee8311 Datadog VERIFIED AWS_Marketplace Publisher ID: 759f81f13de8436c8833e4439e2b1b1a4ea71583 New Relic VERIFIED AWS_Marketplace Report saved to anihpj-publisher-report.txt
# Django Models Integration - Publisher Report from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Generate trust report publishers = [ {'id': 'bb989456...', 'name': 'MongoDB', 'status': 'VERIFIED'}, {'id': '408988df...', 'name': 'Datadog', 'status': 'VERIFIED'}, {'id': '759f81f1...', 'name': 'New Relic', 'status': 'VERIFIED'} ] report_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Trust Reports', defaults={'description': 'Publisher verification reports'} )[0] print("Publisher Trust Report") for pub in publishers: print(f" {pub['name']}: {pub['status']}") verified_count = sum(1 for p in publishers if p['status'] == 'VERIFIED') print(f"\nVerified: {verified_count}/{len(publishers)}") print(f"<QuerySet [publishers: {len(publishers)}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [publishers: 3]>

describe-resource-scan

Describes a resource scan that detects resources in your account not managed by CloudFormation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
  --resource-scan-id {scan-id}
Parameters Description
  • --resource-scan-id - The ID of the resource scan
Return Value

Returns ResourceScanId, Status, StatusReason, StartTime, EndTime, PercentageCompleted, and ResourceTypes.

5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-resource-scan Examples
Example 1: Check anihpj Resource Scan Status
#!/bin/bash
# Start resource scan for anihpj account
SCAN_ID=$(aws cloudformation start-resource-scan \
  --query 'ResourceScanId' \
  --output text)

echo "anihpj Resource Scan ID: $SCAN_ID"

# Monitor scan progress
aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --query '{ScanId:ResourceScanId,Status:Status,Progress:PercentageCompleted,StartTime:StartTime}' \
  --output table
anihpj Resource Scan ID: arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:resourceScan/abc123 ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeResourceScan | +----------------------+----------------------------------------------+ | PercentageCompleted | 35 | | ScanId | arn:aws:cloudformation:...:resourceScan/... | | StartTime | 2026-01-17T10:25:00Z | | Status | IN_PROGRESS | +----------------------+----------------------------------------------+ anihpj resource discovery in progress
# Django Models Integration - Resource Scan Status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track resource scan scan = { 'id': 'abc123', 'status': 'IN_PROGRESS', 'progress': 35 } scan_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Resource Scanning', defaults={'description': 'Discover unmanaged resources'} )[0] print(f"Scan ID: {scan['id']}") print(f"Status: {scan['status']}") print(f"Progress: {scan['progress']}%") print(f"<Skills: {scan_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: Resource Scanning>
Example 2: Monitor anihpj Resource Scan Completion
#!/bin/bash
SCAN_ID="$1"

echo "Monitoring anihpj resource scan..."

while true; do
  SCAN_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
    --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
    --output json)
  
  STATUS=$(echo "$SCAN_STATUS" | jq -r '.Status')
  PROGRESS=$(echo "$SCAN_STATUS" | jq -r '.PercentageCompleted // 0')
  RESOURCES=$(echo "$SCAN_STATUS" | jq -r '.ResourcesScanned // 0')
  
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) - Status: $STATUS | Progress: $PROGRESS% | Resources: $RESOURCES"
  
  if [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETE" ] || [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
    echo "$SCAN_STATUS" | jq '{Status,ResourcesScanned,ResourceTypes,EndTime}'
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 10
done

echo "anihpj resource scan finished"
Monitoring anihpj resource scan... 10:25:00 - Status: IN_PROGRESS | Progress: 25% | Resources: 45 10:25:10 - Status: IN_PROGRESS | Progress: 50% | Resources: 92 10:25:20 - Status: IN_PROGRESS | Progress: 75% | Resources: 138 10:25:30 - Status: COMPLETE | Progress: 100% | Resources: 187 { "Status": "COMPLETE", "ResourcesScanned": 187, "ResourceTypes": 23, "EndTime": "2026-01-17T10:25:35Z" } anihpj resource scan finished
# Django Models Integration - Monitor Scan Completion from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Monitor scan progress states = [ {'progress': 25, 'resources': 45}, {'progress': 50, 'resources': 92}, {'progress': 75, 'resources': 138}, {'progress': 100, 'resources': 187} ] monitoring_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Scan Monitoring', defaults={'description': 'Track scan progress'} )[0] for state in states: print(f"Progress: {state['progress']}% - Resources: {state['resources']}") print(f"\nScan complete: {states[-1]['resources']} resources") print(f"<Skills: {monitoring_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: Scan Monitoring>
Example 3: Find Unmanaged anihpj Django Resources
#!/bin/bash
# Scan for unmanaged resources
SCAN_ID=$(aws cloudformation start-resource-scan --query 'ResourceScanId' --output text)

# Wait for completion
echo "Scanning for unmanaged anihpj resources..."
aws cloudformation wait resource-scan-complete --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID 2>/dev/null

# Get scan results
SCAN_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --output json)

echo "anihpj Resource Scan Results:"
echo "$SCAN_INFO" | jq '{
  TotalResources: .ResourcesScanned,
  ResourceTypes: .ResourceTypes,
  Status: .Status,
  Duration: (.EndTime - .StartTime)
}'

# List discovered resources
echo ""
echo "Sample unmanaged resources:"
aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --max-results 10 \
  --query 'Resources[*].[ResourceType,ResourceIdentifier]' \
  --output table
Scanning for unmanaged anihpj resources... anihpj Resource Scan Results: { "TotalResources": 187, "ResourceTypes": 23, "Status": "COMPLETE", "Duration": "PT5M30S" } Sample unmanaged resources: --------------------------------------------------------------- | AWS::EC2::Instance | i-0abc123def456789 | | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | anihpj-legacy-db | | AWS::S3::Bucket | anihpj-uploads-bucket | | AWS::EC2::VPC | vpc-manual-created | | AWS::DynamoDB::Table | anihpj-sessions | --------------------------------------------------------------- Consider importing these into CloudFormation stacks
# Django Models Integration - Find Unmanaged Resources from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track unmanaged resources unmanaged = [ {'type': 'AWS::EC2::Instance', 'id': 'i-0abc123def456789'}, {'type': 'AWS::RDS::DBInstance', 'id': 'anihpj-legacy-db'}, {'type': 'AWS::S3::Bucket', 'id': 'anihpj-uploads-bucket'} ] discovery_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Resource Discovery', defaults={'description': 'Find unmanaged resources'} )[0] print("Unmanaged Resources:") for res in unmanaged: print(f" {res['type']}: {res['id']}") print(f"\nTotal: {len(unmanaged)}") print(f"<QuerySet [unmanaged: {len(unmanaged)}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [unmanaged: 3]>
Example 4: Check anihpj Scan for Specific Resource Types
#!/bin/bash
SCAN_ID="$1"

# Get scan details
SCAN_DETAILS=$(aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --output json)

echo "anihpj Resource Scan Analysis"
echo "=============================="
echo "$SCAN_DETAILS" | jq '{
  ScanId: .ResourceScanId,
  Status: .Status,
  TotalResources: .ResourcesScanned,
  ResourceTypeCount: .ResourceTypes,
  StartTime: .StartTime,
  EndTime: .EndTime
}'

# Count resources by type
echo ""
echo "Resources by Type:"
aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --query 'Resources[*].ResourceType' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10

echo ""
echo "Use list-resource-scan-resources to see all anihpj resources"
anihpj Resource Scan Analysis ============================== { "ScanId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:resourceScan/abc123", "Status": "COMPLETE", "TotalResources": 187, "ResourceTypeCount": 23, "StartTime": "2026-01-17T10:25:00Z", "EndTime": "2026-01-17T10:30:35Z" } Resources by Type: 45 AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup 23 AWS::EC2::Instance 18 AWS::S3::Bucket 15 AWS::RDS::DBInstance 12 AWS::Lambda::Function 10 AWS::DynamoDB::Table 8 AWS::EC2::VPC 7 AWS::IAM::Role 6 AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster 5 AWS::ECS::Cluster Use list-resource-scan-resources to see all anihpj resources
# Django Models Integration - Analyze Resource Types from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from collections import Counter # Analyze resources by type resource_counts = { 'AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup': 45, 'AWS::EC2::Instance': 23, 'AWS::S3::Bucket': 18, 'AWS::RDS::DBInstance': 15, 'AWS::Lambda::Function': 12 } analysis_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Resource Analysis', defaults={'description': 'Analyze resource types'} )[0] print("Resource Type Analysis:") for rtype, count in resource_counts.items(): print(f" {rtype}: {count}") total = sum(resource_counts.values()) print(f"\nTotal: {total}") print(f"<QuerySet [resource_types: {len(resource_counts)}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [resource_types: 5]>
Example 5: Export anihpj Resource Scan Report
#!/bin/bash
SCAN_ID="$1"
REPORT_FILE="anihpj-resource-scan-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).json"

# Get complete scan details
aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --output json > $REPORT_FILE

# Create summary report
cat > anihpj-scan-summary.txt << EOF
anihpj CloudFormation Resource Scan Summary
Generated: $(date)
==========================================

Scan ID: $SCAN_ID
Status: $(aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID --query 'Status' --output text)
Total Resources: $(aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID --query 'ResourcesScanned' --output text)
Resource Types: $(aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID --query 'ResourceTypes' --output text)

Top Resource Types:
$(aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID --query 'Resources[*].ResourceType' --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -5)

Next Steps:
1. Review unmanaged resources
2. Create generated templates for import
3. Import resources into CloudFormation stacks
EOF

cat anihpj-scan-summary.txt
echo ""
echo "Full scan data saved to $REPORT_FILE"
anihpj CloudFormation Resource Scan Summary Generated: Fri Jan 17 10:35:00 2026 ========================================== Scan ID: arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:resourceScan/abc123 Status: COMPLETE Total Resources: 187 Resource Types: 23 Top Resource Types: 45 AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup 23 AWS::EC2::Instance 18 AWS::S3::Bucket 15 AWS::RDS::DBInstance 12 AWS::Lambda::Function Next Steps: 1.
