Review unmanaged resources 2. Create generated templates for import 3. Import resources into CloudFormation stacks Full scan data saved to anihpj-resource-scan-20260117-103500.json
# Django Models Integration - Export Scan Report from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json # Generate scan report report = { 'scan_id': 'abc123', 'status': 'COMPLETE', 'total_resources': 187, 'resource_types': 23, 'top_types': [ ('AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup', 45), ('AWS::EC2::Instance', 23), ('AWS::S3::Bucket', 18) ] } report_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Scan Reports', defaults={'description': 'Resource scan reporting'} )[0] print("Scan Report Generated") print(f"Total: {report['total_resources']} resources") print(f"Types: {report['resource_types']}") print(f"<Skills: {report_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: Scan Reports>

describe-stack-instance

Returns detailed information about a stack instance in a StackSet.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-instance \
  --stack-set-name {stackset-name} \
  --stack-instance-account {account-id} \
  --stack-instance-region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name - Name or ID of the StackSet
  • --stack-instance-account - AWS account ID of the instance
  • --stack-instance-region - AWS region of the instance
  • --call-as - (Optional) Specify SELF or DELEGATED_ADMIN
Return Value

Returns StackInstance object with Account, Region, Status, StackId, and DriftStatus.

5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-stack-instance Examples
Example 1: Check anihpj Stack Instance Status
#!/bin/bash
# Describe specific anihpj stack instance
aws cloudformation describe-stack-instance \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --stack-instance-account 123456789012 \
  --stack-instance-region us-east-1 \
  --query 'StackInstance.{Account:Account,Region:Region,Status:Status,StatusReason:StatusReason}' \
  --output table
---------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeStackInstance | +--------------+-----------------+------------+-----------------+ | Account | 123456789012 | us-east-1 | CURRENT | | Region | us-east-1 | | | | Status | CURRENT | | | | StatusReason | null | | | +--------------+-----------------+------------+-----------------+ anihpj stack instance healthy
# Django Models Integration - Stack Instance Status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track stack instance instance = { 'account': '123456789012', 'region': 'us-east-1', 'status': 'CURRENT' } instance_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Stack Instances', defaults={'description': 'StackSet instance management'} )[0] print(f"Account: {instance['account']}") print(f"Region: {instance['region']}") print(f"Status: {instance['status']}") healthy = instance['status'] == 'CURRENT' print(f"Healthy: {healthy}") print(f"<Skills: {instance_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: Stack Instances>
Example 2: Get anihpj Stack Instance Drift Status
#!/bin/bash
# Check drift detection status for anihpj instance
aws cloudformation describe-stack-instance \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --stack-instance-account 123456789012 \
  --stack-instance-region us-west-2 \
  --output json | jq '{
    StackId: .StackInstance.StackId,
    Status: .StackInstance.Status,
    DriftStatus: .StackInstance.DriftStatus,
    LastDriftCheck: .StackInstance.LastDriftCheckTimestamp,
    ParameterOverrides: .StackInstance.ParameterOverrides
  }'
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-west-2:123456789012:stack/...", "Status": "CURRENT", "DriftStatus": "IN_SYNC", "LastDriftCheck": "2026-01-17T09:00:00Z", "ParameterOverrides": [ { "ParameterKey": "Environment", "ParameterValue": "production" } ] } anihpj stack instance synchronized with StackSet
# Django Models Integration - Stack Instance Details from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track instance details instance = { 'status': 'CURRENT', 'drift_status': 'IN_SYNC', 'param_overrides': [{'key': 'Environment', 'value': 'production'}] } sync_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Stack Synchronization', defaults={'description': 'StackSet sync status'} )[0] print(f"Status: {instance['status']}") print(f"Drift: {instance['drift_status']}") print(f"Overrides: {len(instance['param_overrides'])}") print(f"<Skills: {sync_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: Stack Synchronization>
Example 3: Review anihpj Parameter Overrides for Instance
#!/bin/bash
# Get parameter overrides for anihpj production instance
STACK_SET="anihpj-django-app"
ACCOUNT="123456789012"
REGION="us-east-1"

aws cloudformation describe-stack-instance \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET \
  --stack-instance-account $ACCOUNT \
  --stack-instance-region $REGION \
  --query 'StackInstance.ParameterOverrides[*].[ParameterKey,ParameterValue]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Base StackSet parameters:"
aws cloudformation describe-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET \
  --query 'StackSet.Parameters[*].[ParameterKey,ParameterValue]' \
  --output table
Instance Parameter Overrides: --------------------------------------------------------- | Environment | production | | InstanceType | t3.large | | DBInstanceClass | db.t3.medium | --------------------------------------------------------- Base StackSet parameters: --------------------------------------------------------- | Environment | dev | | InstanceType | t3.micro | | DBInstanceClass | db.t3.micro | --------------------------------------------------------- anihpj production instance uses custom parameters
# Django Models Integration - Parameter Overrides from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track parameter differences base_params = [ ('Environment', 'dev'), ('InstanceType', 't3.micro'), ('DBInstanceClass', 'db.t3.micro') ] overrides = [ ('Environment', 'production'), ('InstanceType', 't3.large'), ('DBInstanceClass', 'db.t3.medium') ] param_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Parameter Overrides', defaults={'description': 'Instance-specific params'} )[0] print("Overrides vs Base:") for (key, base), (_, override) in zip(base_params, overrides): print(f" {key}: {base} -> {override}") print(f"<Skills: {param_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: Parameter Overrides>
Example 4: Check All anihpj Stack Instances in Region
#!/bin/bash
# List all anihpj instances in specific region
STACK_SET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
REGION="us-east-1"

echo "anihpj Stack Instances in $REGION:"
echo "==================================="

# Get all accounts with instances in region
ACCOUNTS=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET \
  --filters "Region=$REGION" \
  --query 'Summaries[*].Account' \
  --output text)

for ACCOUNT in $ACCOUNTS; do
  echo ""
  echo "Account: $ACCOUNT"
  aws cloudformation describe-stack-instance \
    --stack-set-name $STACK_SET \
    --stack-instance-account $ACCOUNT \
    --stack-instance-region $REGION \
    --query 'StackInstance.{Status:Status,DriftStatus:DriftStatus,LastOperation:LastOperationId}' \
    --output table
done
anihpj Stack Instances in us-east-1: =================================== Account: 123456789012 ------------------------------------------------------- | DriftStatus | IN_SYNC | | LastOperation | abc123-operation-id | | Status | CURRENT | ------------------------------------------------------- Account: 987654321098 ------------------------------------------------------- | DriftStatus | DRIFTED | | LastOperation | def456-operation-id | | Status | OUTDATED | ------------------------------------------------------- Account 987654321098 requires attention
# Django Models Integration - Check Region Instances from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track instances in region instances = [ {'account': '123456789012', 'status': 'CURRENT', 'drift': 'IN_SYNC'}, {'account': '987654321098', 'status': 'OUTDATED', 'drift': 'DRIFTED'} ] region_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Regional Instances', defaults={'description': 'Track instances by region'} )[0] print("Instances in us-east-1:") for inst in instances: status = '\u2713' if inst['status'] == 'CURRENT' else '\u2717' print(f" {inst['account']}: {inst['status']} {status}") print(f"\n<QuerySet [instances: {len(instances)}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [instances: 2]>
Example 5: Troubleshoot anihpj Failed Stack Instance
#!/bin/bash
# Detailed troubleshooting for failed instance
STACK_SET="anihpj-django-app"
ACCOUNT="$1"
REGION="$2"

echo "Troubleshooting anihpj Stack Instance"
echo "====================================="

# Get instance details
INSTANCE_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-instance \
  --stack-set-name $STACK_SET \
  --stack-instance-account $ACCOUNT \
  --stack-instance-region $REGION \
  --output json)

echo "$INSTANCE_INFO" | jq '{
  Status: .StackInstance.Status,
  StatusReason: .StackInstance.StatusReason,
  LastOperation: .StackInstance.LastOperationId,
  StackId: .StackInstance.StackId
}'

# Get operation details
OPERATION_ID=$(echo "$INSTANCE_INFO" | jq -r '.StackInstance.LastOperationId')
if [ "$OPERATION_ID" != "null" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "Last Operation Details:"
  aws cloudformation describe-stack-set-operation \
    --stack-set-name $STACK_SET \
    --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
    --query 'StackSetOperation.{Status:Status,Action:Action,EndTime:EndTimestamp}' \
    --output table
fi

# Get stack events if stack exists
STACK_ID=$(echo "$INSTANCE_INFO" | jq -r '.StackInstance.StackId')
if [ "$STACK_ID" != "null" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "Recent Stack Events:"
  aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
    --stack-name $STACK_ID \
    --max-items 5 \
    --query 'StackEvents[*].[Timestamp,ResourceStatus,LogicalResourceId,ResourceStatusReason]' \
    --output table
fi
Troubleshooting anihpj Stack Instance ===================================== { "Status": "OUTDATED", "StatusReason": "Resource update failed", "LastOperation": "operation-abc123", "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/..." } Last Operation Details: ---------------------------------------------------------- | Action | UPDATE | | EndTime | 2026-01-17T09:30:00Z | | Status | FAILED | ---------------------------------------------------------- Recent Stack Events: --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | 2026-01-17T09:30:00Z | UPDATE_FAILED | AnihpjDatabase | Timeout | | 2026-01-17T09:25:00Z | UPDATE_IN_... | AnihpjDatabase | In progress | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- Root cause: Database update timeout in anihpj stack instance
# Django Models Integration - Troubleshoot Instance from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track troubleshooting issue = { 'status': 'OUTDATED', 'reason': 'Resource update failed', 'operation': 'operation-abc123', 'root_cause': 'Database update timeout' } troubleshoot_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Instance Troubleshooting', defaults={'description': 'Debug failed instances'} )[0] print(f"Status: {issue['status']}") print(f"Reason: {issue['reason']}") print(f"Root cause: {issue['root_cause']}") print(f"<Skills: {troubleshoot_skill.name}>") # Output: <Skills: Instance Troubleshooting>

create-stack-refactor

Creates a stack refactor operation to reorganize resources between CloudFormation stacks. Use this to move, split, or consolidate anihpj Django resources across different stacks for better organization and management.

Syntax
aws cloudformation create-stack-refactor \
  --stack-definitions StackName={name},TemplateBody={body} \
  --resource-mappings Source={arn},Destination={arn} \
  --enable-stack-creation | --no-enable-stack-creation \
  --region {region}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-definitions (list, required): Target stack configurations including StackName and TemplateBody or TemplateURL
  • --resource-mappings (list): Mappings from source resources to destination stacks
  • --enable-stack-creation: Allow creation of new stacks during refactor
  • --no-enable-stack-creation: Only update existing stacks
  • --region (string): AWS region. Example: us-east-1
Return Value
  • StackRefactorId (string): Unique identifier for the refactor operation
5 Detailed CloudFormation create-stack-refactor Examples
Example 1: Split Monolithic Stack into Microservices

Refactor a single anihpj stack into separate database and application stacks.

#!/bin/bash
# Split anihpj monolith into separate stacks
aws cloudformation create-stack-refactor \
  --stack-definitions \
    "StackName=anihpj-database,TemplateBody=$(cat database-template.yaml)" \
    "StackName=anihpj-application,TemplateBody=$(cat app-template.yaml)" \
  --resource-mappings \
    "Source=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-monolith/resource/Database,Destination=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-database" \
    "Source=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-monolith/resource/WebServer,Destination=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-application" \
  --enable-stack-creation \
  --region us-east-1

echo "✓ Stack refactor initiated"
{ "StackRefactorId": "refactor-abc123def456" } ✓ Stack refactor initiated Refactor Details: - Source: anihpj-monolith - Targets: anihpj-database, anihpj-application - Resources to move: 2 - New stacks: 2
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/create_stack_refactor.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Create a stack refactor operation' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--source-stack', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') source_stack = options['source_stack'] # Create refactor skills refactor_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Stack Refactoring', defaults={'description': 'Reorganize CloudFormation stacks'} )[0] microservices_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Microservices Architecture', defaults={'description': 'Design microservices patterns'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get architect user architect = User.objects.get_or_create( username='stack_architect', defaults={'email': 'architect@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author architect_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=architect, defaults={'bio': 'Infrastructure architect'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Architecture location arch_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Architecture Center', state='Design', country='Corporate', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create stack refactor db_template = ''' AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09' Resources: AnihpjDatabase: Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance Properties: DBInstanceClass: db.t3.medium ''' app_template = ''' AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09' Resources: AnihpjWebServer: Type: AWS::EC2::Instance Properties: InstanceType: t3.medium ''' response = cf_client.create_stack_refactor( StackDefinitions=[ {'StackName': 'anihpj-database', 'TemplateBody': db_template}, {'StackName': 'anihpj-application', 'TemplateBody': app_template} ], EnableStackCreation=True ) refactor_id = response.get('StackRefactorId') print(f"\n✓ Refactor: {refactor_id}") # Create refactor job refactor_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Stack Refactor: {source_stack}', description=f'Refactor ID: {refactor_id}', author=architect_author, location=arch_location, job_type='REFACTOR', is_active=True ) refactor_post.required_skills.add(refactor_skill, microservices_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")
Example 2: Consolidate Stacks into Single Stack

Merge multiple anihpj stacks into one unified stack.

#!/bin/bash
# Consolidate multiple stacks
cat > unified-template.yaml << 'EOF'
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: Unified anihpj Django stack
Resources:
  AnihpjDatabase:
    Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance
    Properties:
      DBInstanceClass: db.t3.medium
      Engine: postgres
  AnihpjWebServer:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceType: t3.medium
  AnihpjLoadBalancer:
    Type: AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::LoadBalancer
EOF

aws cloudformation create-stack-refactor \
  --stack-definitions \
    "StackName=anihpj-unified,TemplateBody=file://unified-template.yaml" \
  --resource-mappings \
    "Source=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-db/resource/Database,Destination=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-unified" \
    "Source=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-web/resource/Server,Destination=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-unified" \
  --enable-stack-creation \
  --region us-east-1
{ "StackRefactorId": "refactor-consolidate-789" } Consolidation refactor initiated Source stacks: anihpj-db, anihpj-web Target: anihpj-unified Resources being consolidated: 3
# anihpj/jobpost/services/stack_consolidator.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class StackConsolidator: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def consolidate(self, target_name, source_stacks, unified_template): # Create consolidation skills consolidate_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Stack Consolidation', defaults={'description': 'Merge multiple stacks'} )[0] simplification_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure Simplification', defaults={'description': 'Simplify infrastructure'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get consolidation user consolidator = User.objects.get_or_create( username='stack_consolidator', defaults={'email': 'consolidate@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author consolidator_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=consolidator, defaults={'bio': 'Stack consolidation specialist'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Consolidation center consolidate_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Consolidation Center', state='Operations', country='Corporate', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Build resource mappings resource_mappings = [] for stack in source_stacks: resources = self.cf_client.list_stack_resources(StackName=stack) for resource in resources.get('StackResourceSummaries', []): resource_mappings.append({ 'Source': resource['PhysicalResourceId'], 'Destination': f'arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/{target_name}' }) response = self.cf_client.create_stack_refactor( StackDefinitions=[{ 'StackName': target_name, 'TemplateBody': unified_template }], ResourceMappings=resource_mappings, EnableStackCreation=True ) refactor_id = response.get('StackRefactorId') print(f"\n✓ Consolidation: {refactor_id}") print(f"Sources: {len(source_stacks)}") # Create consolidation job consolidate_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Consolidate to: {target_name}', description=f'From {len(source_stacks)} stacks', author=consolidator_author, location=consolidate_location, job_type='CONSOLIDATION', is_active=True ) consolidate_post.required_skills.add(consolidate_skill, simplification_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return refactor_id
Example 3: Move Resources Between Environments

Refactor to move resources from development to production stack.

#!/bin/bash
# Move resources from dev to prod stack
aws cloudformation create-stack-refactor \
  --stack-definitions \
    "StackName=anihpj-prod,TemplateURL=https://s3.amazonaws.com/templates/anihpj-prod.yaml" \
  --resource-mappings \
    "Source=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-dev/resource/TestedFeature,Destination=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-prod" \
  --no-enable-stack-creation \
  --output json \
  --region us-east-1

echo "Resource moved from dev to prod"
{ "StackRefactorId": "refactor-promote-xyz" } Resource moved from dev to prod Migration Details: - Source: anihpj-dev - Target: anihpj-prod - Promoted resources: 1 - Stack creation: disabled (existing stack only)
# anihpj/jobpost/views.py - Environment promotion from django.http import JsonResponse from django.views import View from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class EnvironmentPromotionView(View): def post(self, request): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') source_env = request.POST.get('source_env', 'dev') target_env = request.POST.get('target_env', 'prod') resource_id = request.POST.get('resource_id') # Create promotion skills promotion_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Environment Promotion', defaults={'description': 'Promote resources between environments'} )[0] release_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Release Management', defaults={'description': 'Manage production releases'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get release manager release_mgr = User.objects.get_or_create( username='release_manager', defaults={'email': 'releases@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author release_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=release_mgr, defaults={'bio': 'Release manager'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Production location prod_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Production Center', state='Operations', country='Corporate', remote=False )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Create promotion refactor response = cf_client.create_stack_refactor( StackDefinitions=[{ 'StackName': f'anihpj-{target_env}', 'TemplateURL': f'https://s3.amazonaws.com/templates/anihpj-{target_env}.yaml' }], ResourceMappings=[{ 'Source': f'arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-{source_env}/resource/{resource_id}', 'Destination': f'arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-{target_env}' }], EnableStackCreation=False ) refactor_id = response.get('StackRefactorId') # Create promotion job promotion_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Promote: {source_env} → {target_env}', description=f'Resource: {resource_id}', author=release_author, location=prod_location, job_type='PROMOTION', is_active=False ) promotion_post.required_skills.add(promotion_skill, release_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return JsonResponse({ 'refactor_id': refactor_id, 'source': source_env, 'target': target_env })
Example 4: Refactor for Multi-AZ Architecture

Reorganize resources across multiple availability zones.

#!/bin/bash
# Refactor single-AZ to multi-AZ architecture
cat > multi-az-template.yaml << 'EOF'
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Resources:
  AnihpjWebAZ1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: us-east-1a
      InstanceType: t3.medium
  AnihpjWebAZ2:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Instance
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: us-east-1b
      InstanceType: t3.medium
  AnihpjDatabaseMultiAZ:
    Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance
    Properties:
      MultiAZ: true
      DBInstanceClass: db.t3.medium
EOF

aws cloudformation create-stack-refactor \
  --stack-definitions \
    "StackName=anihpj-multi-az,TemplateBody=file://multi-az-template.yaml" \
  --resource-mappings \
    "Source=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-single-az/resource/WebServer,Destination=arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-multi-az" \
  --enable-stack-creation \
  --region us-east-1
{ "StackRefactorId": "refactor-multi-az-123" } Multi-AZ refactor initiated Source: anihpj-single-az (1 AZ) Target: anihpj-multi-az (2 AZs) High availability: enabled
# anihpj/jobpost/services/multi_az_refactor.py from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 class MultiAZRefactor: def __init__(self): self.cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') def create_multi_az(self, source_stack, target_stack, multi_az_template): # Create HA skills ha_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='High Availability', defaults={'description': 'Design HA architectures'} )[0] multi_az_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-AZ Deployment', defaults={'description': 'Deploy across availability zones'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get HA architect ha_architect = User.objects.get_or_create( username='ha_architect', defaults={'email': 'ha@anihpj.com', 'is_staff': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author ha_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=ha_architect, defaults={'bio': 'High availability architect'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # HA location ha_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='HA Center', state='Resilience', country='Multi-AZ', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Get source resources resources = self.cf_client.list_stack_resources(StackName=source_stack) mappings = [] for resource in resources.get('StackResourceSummaries', []): mappings.append({ 'Source': resource['PhysicalResourceId'], 'Destination': f'arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/{target_stack}' }) response = self.cf_client.create_stack_refactor( StackDefinitions=[{ 'StackName': target_stack, 'TemplateBody': multi_az_template }], ResourceMappings=mappings, EnableStackCreation=True ) refactor_id = response.get('StackRefactorId') print(f"\n✓ Multi-AZ refactor: {refactor_id}") # Create HA job ha_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Multi-AZ: {target_stack}', description='High availability architecture', author=ha_author, location=ha_location, job_type='MULTI_AZ', is_active=True ) ha_post.required_skills.add(ha_skill, multi_az_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") return refactor_id
Example 5: Refactor with Automation Pipeline

Automate stack refactoring through CI/CD pipeline.

#!/bin/bash
# CI/CD pipeline refactor automation
REFACTOR_CONFIG="refactor-config.json"

cat > $REFACTOR_CONFIG << 'EOF'
{
  "source_stack": "anihpj-legacy",
  "target_stacks": ["anihpj-api", "anihpj-worker", "anihpj-web"],
  "enable_creation": true
}
EOF

# Parse config and execute refactor
SOURCE=$(jq -r '.source_stack' $REFACTOR_CONFIG)
TARGETS=$(jq -r '.target_stacks[]' $REFACTOR_CONFIG)
ENABLE=$(jq -r '.enable_creation' $REFACTOR_CONFIG)

# Build stack definitions
DEFINITIONS=""
for TARGET in $TARGETS; do
  DEFINITIONS+="StackName=$TARGET,TemplateURL=https://s3.amazonaws.com/templates/$TARGET.yaml "
done

REFACTOR_ID=$(aws cloudformation create-stack-refactor \
  --stack-definitions $DEFINITIONS \
  --enable-stack-creation \
  --query 'StackRefactorId' \
  --output text \
  --region us-east-1)

echo "Refactor ID: $REFACTOR_ID"
echo "Waiting for completion..."

# Monitor in background
while true; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-refactor \
    --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
    --query 'Status' \
    --output text)
  
  if [ "$STATUS" = "CREATE_COMPLETE" ]; then
    echo "✓ Refactor complete"
    break
  elif [ "$STATUS" = "FAILED" ]; then
    echo "✗ Refactor failed"
    exit 1
  fi
  
  sleep 30
done
Refactor ID: refactor-pipeline-abc Waiting for completion... Status: CREATE_IN_PROGRESS Status: CREATE_IN_PROGRESS ✓ Refactor complete Pipeline Results: - Source: anihpj-legacy - Targets created: 3 - Duration: 4m 30s
# anihpj/jobpost/management/commands/pipeline_refactor.py from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import boto3 import json import time class Command(BaseCommand): help = 'Automate stack refactoring via pipeline' def add_arguments(self, parser): parser.add_argument('--config', type=str, required=True) def handle(self, *args, **options): cf_client = boto3.client('cloudformation', region_name='us-east-1') # Load config with open(options['config']) as f: config = json.load(f) source_stack = config['source_stack'] target_stacks = config['target_stacks'] # Create pipeline skills pipeline_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='CI/CD Pipeline', defaults={'description': 'Automated deployment pipelines'} )[0] automation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure Automation', defaults={'description': 'Automate infrastructure changes'} )[0] print(f"Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") # Get automation user automation_user = User.objects.get_or_create( username='pipeline_automation', defaults={'email': 'pipeline@anihpj.com', 'is_superuser': True} )[0] print(f"Users: {User.objects.count()}") # Create author automation_author = Author.objects.get_or_create( user=automation_user, defaults={'bio': 'Pipeline automation engineer'} )[0] print(f"Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") # Pipeline location pipeline_location = Location.objects.get_or_create( city='Pipeline Center', state='Automation', country='DevOps', remote=True )[0] print(f"Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") # Build stack definitions stack_definitions = [] for target in target_stacks: stack_definitions.append({ 'StackName': target, 'TemplateURL': f'https://s3.amazonaws.com/templates/{target}.yaml' }) # Create refactor response = cf_client.create_stack_refactor( StackDefinitions=stack_definitions, EnableStackCreation=config.get('enable_creation', True) ) refactor_id = response.get('StackRefactorId') print(f"Refactor: {refactor_id}") # Wait for completion while True: status_response = cf_client.describe_stack_refactor( StackRefactorId=refactor_id ) status = status_response.get('Status') if status == 'CREATE_COMPLETE': print("✓ Refactor complete") break elif status == 'FAILED': print("✗ Refactor failed") break time.sleep(30) # Create pipeline job pipeline_post = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'Pipeline Refactor: {source_stack}', description=f'Targets: {len(target_stacks)}', author=automation_author, location=pipeline_location, job_type='PIPELINE_REFACTOR', is_active=False ) pipeline_post.required_skills.add(pipeline_skill, automation_skill) print(f"JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}")

describe-stack-refactor

Describes a stack refactor operation that reorganizes resources across stacks.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-refactor \
  --stack-refactor-id {refactor-id}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-refactor-id - The ID of the stack refactor operation
Return Value

Returns StackRefactorId, Status, StatusReason, ExecutionStatus, and RefactorResources.

5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-stack-refactor Examples
Example 1: Check anihpj Stack Refactor Status
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor refactor operation for anihpj stack reorganization
REFACTOR_ID="$1"

aws cloudformation describe-stack-refactor \
  --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
  --query '{RefactorId:StackRefactorId,Status:Status,SourceStack:SourceStackId,Actions:Actions[*].{Type:ActionType,Resources:ResourceIdentifier}}' \
  --output json | jq '.'
{ "RefactorId": "refactor-abc123", "Status": "IN_PROGRESS", "SourceStack": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:...:stack/anihpj-monolith", "Actions": [ { "Type": "MOVE", "Resources": "AnihpjDatabase" }, { "Type": "MOVE", "Resources": "AnihpjCacheCluster" } ] } anihpj stack refactoring in progress
# Django: Track stack refactor operations from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Check refactor status refactor_skill = Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains='refactor').first() print(f"Skill: {refactor_skill}") # Find refactor-related jobs refactor_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='refactor', is_active=True ).order_by('-created_at')[:5] print(f"Refactor jobs: {refactor_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(refactor_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Stack Refactoring') # Refactor jobs: 3 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['Monolith Refactor', 'DB Split Refactor', 'Cache Refactor']...>
Example 2: Monitor anihpj Monolith to Microservices Refactor
#!/bin/bash
# Watch refactor progress for splitting anihpj monolith
REFACTOR_ID="$1"

echo "Monitoring anihpj stack refactor..."

while true; do
  STATUS_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-refactor \
    --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
    --output json)
  
  STATUS=$(echo "$STATUS_INFO" | jq -r '.Status')
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) - Status: $STATUS"
  
  if [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETE" ] || [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Refactor Results:"
    echo "$STATUS_INFO" | jq '{
      Status: .Status,
      SourceStack: .SourceStackId,
      Actions: .Actions | length,
      CompletedActions: [.Actions[] | select(.Status == "COMPLETE")] | length,
      FailedActions: [.Actions[] | select(.Status == "FAILED")] | length
    }'
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 15
done
Monitoring anihpj stack refactor... 10:30:00 - Status: IN_PROGRESS 10:30:15 - Status: IN_PROGRESS 10:30:30 - Status: COMPLETE Refactor Results: { "Status": "COMPLETE", "SourceStack": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:...:stack/anihpj-monolith", "Actions": 8, "CompletedActions": 8, "FailedActions": 0 } anihpj stack successfully refactored into microservices
# Django: Monitor microservices refactoring progress from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get microservices skill microservices_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Microservices Architecture' )[0] print(f"Skill: {microservices_skill}") # Track refactor completion jobs completed_refactors = JobPost.objects.filter( job_type='REFACTOR', is_active=False ).select_related('author', 'location') print(f"Completed: {completed_refactors.count()}") print(f"Refactors: <QuerySet {list(completed_refactors.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Microservices Architecture') # Completed: 8 # Refactors: <QuerySet ['DB Refactor', 'Cache Refactor', 'API Refactor']...>
Example 3: Review anihpj Database Stack Split Details
#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed information about database stack split
REFACTOR_ID="refactor-database-split-123"

echo "anihpj Database Stack Refactor Details"
echo "======================================="

aws cloudformation describe-stack-refactor \
  --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
  --output json | jq '{
    RefactorId: .StackRefactorId,
    Status: .Status,
    SourceStack: .SourceStackId,
    TargetStacks: [.Actions[] | {
      Action: .ActionType,
      Resource: .ResourceIdentifier,
      TargetStack: .TargetStackId,
      Status: .Status
    }]
  }'

echo ""
echo "New stack structure for anihpj"
anihpj Database Stack Refactor Details ======================================= { "RefactorId": "refactor-database-split-123", "Status": "COMPLETE", "SourceStack": "arn:aws:cloudformation:...:stack/anihpj-monolith", "TargetStacks": [ { "Action": "MOVE", "Resource": "AnihpjDatabase", "TargetStack": "anihpj-database-stack", "Status": "COMPLETE" }, { "Action": "MOVE", "Resource": "AnihpjDBSubnetGroup", "TargetStack": "anihpj-database-stack", "Status": "COMPLETE" }, { "Action": "MOVE", "Resource": "AnihpjDBSecurityGroup", "TargetStack": "anihpj-database-stack", "Status": "COMPLETE" } ] } New stack structure for anihpj - anihpj-monolith: Application layer only - anihpj-database-stack: Database resources
# Django: Track database stack split details from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Database migration skill db_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Database Migration' )[0] print(f"Skill: {db_skill}") # Find DB split jobs db_split_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='database', description__icontains='split' ).values('title', 'job_type', 'is_active') print(f"DB Split jobs: {db_split_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(db_split_jobs[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Database Migration') # DB Split jobs: 2 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'DB Stack Split', 'job_type': 'REFACTOR', 'is_active': False}]...>
Example 4: Check anihpj Failed Refactor Actions
#!/bin/bash
# Investigate failed actions in anihpj refactor
REFACTOR_ID="$1"

echo "Checking for failed actions in anihpj refactor..."

FAILED_ACTIONS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-refactor \
  --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
  --query 'Actions[?Status==`FAILED`]' \
  --output json)

if [ "$(echo "$FAILED_ACTIONS" | jq 'length')" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "Failed Actions Found:"
  echo "$FAILED_ACTIONS" | jq -r '.[] | "
  Resource: \(.ResourceIdentifier)
  Action: \(.ActionType)
  Status: \(.Status)
  Reason: \(.StatusReason)
  Target: \(.TargetStackId // "N/A")
  "'
else
  echo "No failed actions - anihpj refactor successful"
fi
Checking for failed actions in anihpj refactor... Failed Actions Found: Resource: AnihpjCacheCluster Action: MOVE Status: FAILED Reason: Resource has dependencies that cannot be moved Target: anihpj-cache-stack Resolution required: Move dependent resources or update dependencies
# Django: Track failed refactor actions from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Error handling skill error_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Error Resolution' )[0] print(f"Skill: {error_skill}") # Find failed refactor jobs failed_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='failed', job_type='REFACTOR' ).select_related('author') print(f"Failed refactors: {failed_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(failed_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Error Resolution') # Failed refactors: 1 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['Cache Refactor Failed']...>
Example 5: List All anihpj Stack Refactor Operations
#!/bin/bash
# List all refactor operations for anihpj stacks
echo "anihpj Stack Refactor Operations"
echo "================================="

aws cloudformation list-stack-refactors \
  --query 'StackRefactorSummaries[*].[StackRefactorId,Status,SourceStackId,CreationTime]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Recent refactor details:"

# Get details for most recent refactor
LATEST_REFACTOR=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-refactors \
  --query 'StackRefactorSummaries[0].StackRefactorId' \
  --output text)

if [ "$LATEST_REFACTOR" != "None" ]; then
  aws cloudformation describe-stack-refactor \
    --stack-refactor-id $LATEST_REFACTOR \
    --query '{RefactorId:StackRefactorId,Status:Status,ActionsCount:Actions|length(@)}' \
    --output table
fi
anihpj Stack Refactor Operations ================================= --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | refactor-abc123 | COMPLETE | anihpj-monolith | 2026-01-15T10:00:00Z | | refactor-def456 | IN_PROGRESS | anihpj-app | 2026-01-17T09:30:00Z | | refactor-ghi789 | FAILED | anihpj-legacy | 2026-01-16T14:20:00Z | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- Recent refactor details: --------------------------------------------------------------- | ActionsCount | 8 | | RefactorId | refactor-abc123 | | Status | COMPLETE | --------------------------------------------------------------- anihpj stack reorganization history
# Django: List all refactor operations from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get all refactor-related skills refactor_skills = Skills.objects.filter( name__icontains='refactor' ) print(f"Skills: {refactor_skills.count()}") print(f"Skills: <QuerySet {list(refactor_skills.values_list('name', flat=True))}...>") # List all refactor jobs all_refactors = JobPost.objects.filter( job_type='REFACTOR' ).order_by('-created_at').values('title', 'is_active', 'created_at') print(f"Total refactors: {all_refactors.count()}") print(f"Refactors: <QuerySet {list(all_refactors[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skills: 3 # Skills: <QuerySet ['Stack Refactoring', 'Database Migration', 'Microservices']...> # Total refactors: 5 # Refactors: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Monolith Split', 'is_active': True, 'created_at': datetime...}]...>

describe-stack-resource

Returns detailed information about a specific resource in a stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --logical-resource-id {resource-id}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name - Name or ID of the stack
  • --logical-resource-id - Logical ID of the resource
Return Value

Returns StackResourceDetail with ResourceType, PhysicalResourceId, ResourceStatus, and Metadata.

5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-stack-resource Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Database Resource Details
#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed information about anihpj RDS instance
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --logical-resource-id AnihpjDatabase \
  --query 'StackResourceDetail.{LogicalId:LogicalResourceId,PhysicalId:PhysicalResourceId,Type:ResourceType,Status:ResourceStatus,Timestamp:LastUpdatedTimestamp}' \
  --output table
--------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeStackResource | +----------------+-------------------------------------------------------+ | LogicalId | AnihpjDatabase | | PhysicalId | anihpj-db.abc123.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com | | Status | UPDATE_COMPLETE | | Timestamp | 2026-01-17T10:30:00Z | | Type | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | +----------------+-------------------------------------------------------+ anihpj database resource details retrieved
# Django: Track stack resource details from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Database skill db_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Database Administration' )[0] print(f"Skill: {db_skill}") # Find database-related jobs db_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='database' ).select_related('author', 'location') print(f"DB jobs: {db_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(db_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Database Administration') # DB jobs: 4 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['Database Engineer', 'RDS Admin', 'DB Migration']...>
Example 2: Check anihpj Load Balancer Configuration
#!/bin/bash
# Get load balancer details including drift status
aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --logical-resource-id AnihpjLoadBalancer \
  --output json | jq '{
    LogicalResourceId: .StackResourceDetail.LogicalResourceId,
    PhysicalResourceId: .StackResourceDetail.PhysicalResourceId,
    ResourceType: .StackResourceDetail.ResourceType,
    ResourceStatus: .StackResourceDetail.ResourceStatus,
    DriftStatus: .StackResourceDetail.DriftInformation.StackResourceDriftStatus,
    LastDriftCheck: .StackResourceDetail.DriftInformation.LastCheckTimestamp,
    Metadata: .StackResourceDetail.Metadata
  }'
{ "LogicalResourceId": "AnihpjLoadBalancer", "PhysicalResourceId": "arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:us-east-1:123456789012:loadbalancer/app/anihpj-alb/abc123def456", "ResourceType": "AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::LoadBalancer", "ResourceStatus": "UPDATE_COMPLETE", "DriftStatus": "IN_SYNC", "LastDriftCheck": "2026-01-17T09:00:00Z", "Metadata": null } anihpj load balancer is in sync with template
# Django: Track load balancer resources from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Load balancer skill lb_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Load Balancing' )[0] print(f"Skill: {lb_skill}") # Find load balancer related jobs lb_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='load balancer' ).values('title', 'job_type', 'is_active') print(f"LB jobs: {lb_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(lb_jobs[:2])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Load Balancing') # LB jobs: 2 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'ALB Config', 'job_type': 'INFRASTRUCTURE', 'is_active': True}]...>
Example 3: Get anihpj EC2 Instance Connection Info
#!/bin/bash
# Get EC2 instance details for SSH connection
RESOURCE_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --logical-resource-id AnihpjWebServer \
  --output json)

INSTANCE_ID=$(echo "$RESOURCE_INFO" | jq -r '.StackResourceDetail.PhysicalResourceId')
STATUS=$(echo "$RESOURCE_INFO" | jq -r '.StackResourceDetail.ResourceStatus')

echo "anihpj Web Server:"
echo "  Logical ID: AnihpjWebServer"
echo "  Instance ID: $INSTANCE_ID"
echo "  Status: $STATUS"

# Get instance public IP
PUBLIC_IP=$(aws ec2 describe-instances \
  --instance-ids $INSTANCE_ID \
  --query 'Reservations[0].Instances[0].PublicIpAddress' \
  --output text)

echo "  Public IP: $PUBLIC_IP"
echo ""
echo "SSH command:"
echo "  ssh -i anihpj-key.pem ubuntu@$PUBLIC_IP"
anihpj Web Server: Logical ID: AnihpjWebServer Instance ID: i-0abc123def456789 Status: CREATE_COMPLETE Public IP: 54.123.45.67 SSH command: ssh -i anihpj-key.pem ubuntu@54.123.45.67
# Django: Track EC2 instance connection info from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # EC2 skill ec2_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='EC2 Management' )[0] print(f"Skill: {ec2_skill}") # Find EC2-related jobs ec2_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='ec2' ).order_by('-created_at') print(f"EC2 jobs: {ec2_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(ec2_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='EC2 Management') # EC2 jobs: 5 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['EC2 Server Setup', 'Instance Config', 'SSH Access']...>
Example 4: Check anihpj Resource Drift Details
#!/bin/bash
# Check if specific anihpj resource has drifted
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
RESOURCE_ID="AnihpjSecurityGroup"

RESOURCE_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --logical-resource-id $RESOURCE_ID \
  --output json)

DRIFT_STATUS=$(echo "$RESOURCE_INFO" | jq -r '.StackResourceDetail.DriftInformation.StackResourceDriftStatus')

echo "anihpj Resource: $RESOURCE_ID"
echo "Drift Status: $DRIFT_STATUS"

if [ "$DRIFT_STATUS" == "MODIFIED" ]; then
  echo "⚠️  Resource has drifted from template!"
  echo ""
  echo "Running drift detection for details..."
  
  # Detect drift for specific resource
  aws cloudformation detect-stack-resource-drift \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --logical-resource-id $RESOURCE_ID \
    --query 'StackResourceDrift.{Status:StackResourceDriftStatus,Properties:PropertyDifferences}' \
    --output json | jq '.'
elif [ "$DRIFT_STATUS" == "IN_SYNC" ]; then
  echo "✓ Resource is in sync with template"
else
  echo "Drift status: $DRIFT_STATUS"
fi
anihpj Resource: AnihpjSecurityGroup Drift Status: MODIFIED ⚠️ Resource has drifted from template! Running drift detection for details... { "Status": "MODIFIED", "Properties": [ { "PropertyPath": "/IpPermissions/0/IpRanges", "ExpectedValue": "10.0.0.0/8", "ActualValue": "0.0.0.0/0" } ] } Security group ingress rules modified outside CloudFormation
# Django: Track resource drift status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Drift detection skill drift_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Drift Detection' )[0] print(f"Skill: {drift_skill}") # Find drift-related jobs drift_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='drift' ).select_related('location') print(f"Drift jobs: {drift_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(drift_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Drift Detection') # Drift jobs: 2 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['Drift Remediation', 'Security Drift Fix']...>
Example 5: Export anihpj Stack Resource Documentation
#!/bin/bash
# Create documentation for key anihpj resources
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"
RESOURCES=("AnihpjDatabase" "AnihpjLoadBalancer" "AnihpjWebServer" "AnihpjCacheCluster")

echo "anihpj Production Stack Resource Documentation" > anihpj-resources.md
echo "===============================================" >> anihpj-resources.md
echo "Generated: $(date)" >> anihpj-resources.md
echo "" >> anihpj-resources.md

for RESOURCE in "${RESOURCES[@]}"; do
  echo "## $RESOURCE" >> anihpj-resources.md
  echo "" >> anihpj-resources.md
  
  aws cloudformation describe-stack-resource \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --logical-resource-id $RESOURCE \
    --output json | jq -r '.StackResourceDetail | "
**Type:** \(.ResourceType)
**Physical ID:** \(.PhysicalResourceId)
**Status:** \(.ResourceStatus)
**Last Updated:** \(.LastUpdatedTimestamp)
**Description:** \(.Description // "N/A")
  "' >> anihpj-resources.md
  
  echo "" >> anihpj-resources.md
done

cat anihpj-resources.md
anihpj Production Stack Resource Documentation =============================================== Generated: Fri Jan 17 10:35:00 2026 ## AnihpjDatabase **Type:** AWS::RDS::DBInstance **Physical ID:** anihpj-db.abc123.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com **Status:** UPDATE_COMPLETE **Last Updated:** 2026-01-17T10:30:00Z **Description:** PostgreSQL database for Django application ## AnihpjLoadBalancer **Type:** AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::LoadBalancer **Physical ID:** arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:...:loadbalancer/app/anihpj-alb/abc123 **Status:** CREATE_COMPLETE **Last Updated:** 2026-01-15T14:20:00Z **Description:** Application load balancer for anihpj Resource documentation saved to anihpj-resources.md
# Django: Export stack resource documentation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Documentation skill doc_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Documentation' )[0] print(f"Skill: {doc_skill}") # Get all production jobs production_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='production' ).values('title', 'description', 'created_at') print(f"Production jobs: {production_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(production_jobs[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Documentation') # Production jobs: 4 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Production Setup', 'description': 'Config prod...', 'created_at': datetime...}]...>

describe-stack-set-operation

Returns detailed information about a specific StackSet operation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-stack-set-operation \
  --stack-set-name {stackset-name} \
  --operation-id {operation-id}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name - Name or ID of the StackSet
  • --operation-id - Unique ID of the operation
  • --call-as - (Optional) Specify SELF or DELEGATED_ADMIN
Return Value

Returns StackSetOperation with OperationId, StackSetId, Action, Status, and OperationPreferences.

5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-stack-set-operation Examples
Example 1: Check anihpj StackSet Update Operation
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor StackSet update operation for anihpj
OPERATION_ID="$1"

aws cloudformation describe-stack-set-operation \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
  --query 'StackSetOperation.{OperationId:OperationId,Action:Action,Status:Status,SuccessCount:OperationPreferences.FailureCount,FailureCount:OperationPreferences.FailureCount}' \
  --output table
------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeStackSetOperation | +-------------+--------+------------+--------------+--------------------+ | Action | UPDATE | operation- | 0 | 12 | | FailureCount| 0 | abc123 | | | | OperationId | opera..| | | | | Status | SUCCEEDED | | | | SuccessCount| 12 | | | | +-------------+--------+------------+--------------+--------------------+ anihpj StackSet updated successfully across all instances
# Django: Track StackSet operation status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # StackSet skill stackset_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='StackSet Operations' )[0] print(f"Skill: {stackset_skill}") # Find stackset-related jobs stackset_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='stackset' ).select_related('author') print(f"StackSet jobs: {stackset_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(stackset_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='StackSet Operations') # StackSet jobs: 3 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['StackSet Update', 'Multi-Account Deploy', 'StackSet Migration']...>
Example 2: Monitor anihpj Multi-Region Deployment Progress
#!/bin/bash
# Watch StackSet deployment progress
STACKSET_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
OPERATION_ID="$1"

echo "Monitoring anihpj multi-region deployment..."

while true; do
  OP_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-set-operation \
    --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
    --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
    --output json)
  
  STATUS=$(echo "$OP_INFO" | jq -r '.StackSetOperation.Status')
  ACTION=$(echo "$OP_INFO" | jq -r '.StackSetOperation.Action')
  
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) - Action: $ACTION | Status: $STATUS"
  
  if [ "$STATUS" == "SUCCEEDED" ] || [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ] || [ "$STATUS" == "STOPPED" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Final Results:"
    echo "$OP_INFO" | jq '.StackSetOperation | {
      Status: .Status,
      Action: .Action,
      InstancesDeployed: .StatusReason,
      CreationTime: .CreationTimestamp,
      EndTime: .EndTimestamp
    }'
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 15
done
Monitoring anihpj multi-region deployment... 10:30:00 - Action: CREATE | Status: RUNNING 10:30:15 - Action: CREATE | Status: RUNNING 10:30:30 - Action: CREATE | Status: SUCCEEDED Final Results: { "Status": "SUCCEEDED", "Action": "CREATE", "InstancesDeployed": "12 instances created successfully", "CreationTime": "2026-01-17T10:25:00Z", "EndTime": "2026-01-17T10:30:35Z" } anihpj deployed to all regions
# Django: Track multi-region deployment from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Multi-region skill multiregion_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-Region Deployment' )[0] print(f"Skill: {multiregion_skill}") # Find multi-region jobs multiregion_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='multi-region' ).values('title', 'is_active', 'created_at') print(f"Multi-region jobs: {multiregion_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(multiregion_jobs[:2])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Multi-Region Deployment') # Multi-region jobs: 4 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Global Deployment', 'is_active': True, 'created_at': datetime...}]...>
Example 3: Check anihpj StackSet Operation Failures
#!/bin/bash
# Investigate failed StackSet operation
STACKSET_NAME="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
OPERATION_ID="$1"

echo "Checking anihpj StackSet operation failures..."

# Get operation details
aws cloudformation describe-stack-set-operation \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
  --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
  --output json | jq '{
    Status: .StackSetOperation.Status,
    StatusReason: .StackSetOperation.StatusReason,
    FailureCount: .StackSetOperation.StatusDetails.FailedCount
  }'

# List failed instances
echo ""
echo "Failed Instances:"
aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operation-results \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
  --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`FAILED`].[Account,Region,StatusReason]' \
  --output table
Checking anihpj StackSet operation failures... { "Status": "FAILED", "StatusReason": "Operation failed in 2 instances", "FailureCount": 2 } Failed Instances: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | 987654321098 | us-west-2 | Insufficient IAM permissions | | 111222333444 | eu-west-1 | VPC limit exceeded | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ Address failures before retrying anihpj deployment
# Django: Track StackSet operation failures from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Troubleshooting skill troubleshoot_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Troubleshooting' )[0] print(f"Skill: {troubleshoot_skill}") # Find failed deployment jobs failed_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='failed' ).select_related('location') print(f"Failed jobs: {failed_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(failed_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Troubleshooting') # Failed jobs: 2 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['IAM Failure', 'VPC Limit Exceeded']...>
Example 4: Review anihpj StackSet Operation Preferences
#!/bin/bash
# Check operation preferences used for anihpj deployment
STACKSET_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
OPERATION_ID="$1"

aws cloudformation describe-stack-set-operation \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
  --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
  --query 'StackSetOperation.OperationPreferences' \
  --output json | jq '{
    RegionConcurrencyType: .RegionConcurrencyType,
    RegionOrder: .RegionOrder,
    FailureToleranceCount: .FailureToleranceCount,
    FailureTolerancePercentage: .FailureTolerancePercentage,
    MaxConcurrentCount: .MaxConcurrentCount,
    MaxConcurrentPercentage: .MaxConcurrentPercentage,
    ConcurrencyMode: .ConcurrencyMode
  }'

echo "anihpj deployment preferences reviewed"
{ "RegionConcurrencyType": "PARALLEL", "RegionOrder": ["us-east-1", "us-west-2", "eu-west-1"], "FailureToleranceCount": 2, "FailureTolerancePercentage": null, "MaxConcurrentCount": null, "MaxConcurrentPercentage": 25, "ConcurrencyMode": "SOFT_FAILURE_TOLERANCE" } anihpj deployment preferences reviewed Deploying to 25% of instances at a time, tolerating 2 failures
# Django: Track deployment preferences from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Deployment preferences skill prefs_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Deployment Strategy' )[0] print(f"Skill: {prefs_skill}") # Find deployment strategy jobs strategy_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='deployment' ).values('title', 'job_type') print(f"Strategy jobs: {strategy_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(strategy_jobs[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Deployment Strategy') # Strategy jobs: 5 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Parallel Deploy', 'job_type': 'DEPLOYMENT'}]...>
Example 5: Export anihpj StackSet Operation Report
#!/bin/bash
# Generate comprehensive operation report
STACKSET_NAME="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
OPERATION_ID="$1"
REPORT_FILE="anihpj-operation-$(echo $OPERATION_ID | cut -d'-' -f2).txt"

echo "anihpj StackSet Operation Report" > $REPORT_FILE
echo "=================================" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "Generated: $(date)" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "" >> $REPORT_FILE

# Operation summary
aws cloudformation describe-stack-set-operation \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
  --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
  --output json | jq -r '.StackSetOperation | "
StackSet: \(.StackSetId)
Operation ID: \(.OperationId)
Action: \(.Action)
Status: \(.Status)
Creation Time: \(.CreationTimestamp)
End Time: \(.EndTimestamp // "In Progress")
Status Reason: \(.StatusReason // "N/A")
"' >> $REPORT_FILE

echo "" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "Operation Results:" >> $REPORT_FILE

# Results by region
aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operation-results \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
  --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[Account,Region,Status]' \
  --output text >> $REPORT_FILE

cat $REPORT_FILE
anihpj StackSet Operation Report ================================= Generated: Fri Jan 17 10:40:00 2026 StackSet: arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:...:stackset/anihpj-django-infrastructure Operation ID: operation-abc123def456 Action: UPDATE Status: SUCCEEDED Creation Time: 2026-01-17T10:25:00Z End Time: 2026-01-17T10:35:00Z Status Reason: N/A Operation Results: 123456789012 us-east-1 SUCCEEDED 123456789012 us-west-2 SUCCEEDED 987654321098 us-east-1 SUCCEEDED 987654321098 us-west-2 SUCCEEDED Report saved to anihpj-operation-abc123.txt
# Django: Export StackSet operation report from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Reporting skill report_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Report Generation' )[0] print(f"Skill: {report_skill}") # Get operation report jobs report_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='report' ).order_by('-created_at').values('title', 'author__user__username', 'created_at') print(f"Report jobs: {report_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(report_jobs[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Report Generation') # Report jobs: 3 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Operation Report', 'author__user__username': 'admin', 'created_at': datetime...}]...>

describe-type

Returns detailed information about a CloudFormation extension (resource type, module, or hook).

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name}
Parameters Description
  • --type - The extension type (RESOURCE, MODULE, or HOOK)
  • --type-name - The name of the extension
  • --arn - (Alternative) The ARN of the extension
  • --version-id - (Optional) The specific version ID
Return Value

Returns Arn, Type, TypeName, Description, Schema, ProvisioningType, and DeprecatedStatus.

5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-type Examples
Example 1: Check MongoDB Extension for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Get MongoDB Atlas extension details
aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster \
  --query '{TypeName:TypeName,IsActivated:IsActivated,ProvisioningType:ProvisioningType,DeprecatedStatus:DeprecatedStatus,LatestPublicVersion:LatestPublicVersion}' \
  --output table

echo "MongoDB extension verified for anihpj"
-------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeType | +----------------------+-------------------------------------------+ | DeprecatedStatus | LIVE | | IsActivated | True | | LatestPublicVersion | 1.3.0 | | ProvisioningType | FULLY_MUTABLE | | TypeName | MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster | +----------------------+-------------------------------------------+ MongoDB extension verified for anihpj
# Django: Track extension verification from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # MongoDB skill mongodb_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='MongoDB Administration' )[0] print(f"Skill: {mongodb_skill}") # Find MongoDB jobs mongodb_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='mongodb' ).select_related('author') print(f"MongoDB jobs: {mongodb_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(mongodb_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='MongoDB Administration') # MongoDB jobs: 2 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['MongoDB Setup', 'Atlas Integration']...>
Example 2: Get anihpj Custom Resource Type Schema
#!/bin/bash
# Get schema for custom anihpj resource type
aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Django::Application \
  --query 'Schema' \
  --output text | jq '.properties' | head -30

echo "anihpj custom resource schema retrieved"
{ "ApplicationName": { "type": "string", "description": "Name of the Django application" }, "Environment": { "type": "string", "enum": ["development", "staging", "production"] }, "DatabaseEndpoint": { "type": "string", "description": "RDS database endpoint" }, "CacheEndpoint": { "type": "string", "description": "Redis cache endpoint" }, "SecretArn": { "type": "string", "description": "Secrets Manager ARN for credentials" } } anihpj custom resource schema retrieved
# Django: Track custom resource schemas from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Schema design skill schema_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Schema Design' )[0] print(f"Skill: {schema_skill}") # Find schema-related jobs schema_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='schema' ).values('title', 'job_type', 'is_active') print(f"Schema jobs: {schema_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(schema_jobs[:2])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Schema Design') # Schema jobs: 2 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Resource Schema', 'job_type': 'DESIGN', 'is_active': True}]...>
Example 3: Check Extension Version for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Compare current and latest versions of Datadog extension
CURRENT_VERSION=$(aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name Datadog::Integrations::AWS \
  --query 'DefaultVersionId' \
  --output text)

LATEST_VERSION=$(aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name Datadog::Integrations::AWS \
  --query 'LatestPublicVersion' \
  --output text)

echo "anihpj Datadog Extension:"
echo "  Current Version: $CURRENT_VERSION"
echo "  Latest Version: $LATEST_VERSION"

if [ "$CURRENT_VERSION" != "$LATEST_VERSION" ]; then
  echo "  ⚠️  Update available!"
  echo ""
  echo "To update:"
  echo "  aws cloudformation set-type-default-version \\"
  echo "    --type RESOURCE \\"
  echo "    --type-name Datadog::Integrations::AWS \\"
  echo "    --version-id $LATEST_VERSION"
else
  echo "  ✓ Using latest version"
fi
anihpj Datadog Extension: Current Version: 00000003 Latest Version: 00000005 ⚠️ Update available! To update: aws cloudformation set-type-default-version \ --type RESOURCE \ --type-name Datadog::Integrations::AWS \ --version-id 00000005
# Django: Track extension version updates from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Version management skill version_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Version Management' )[0] print(f"Skill: {version_skill}") # Find version update jobs version_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='update' ).order_by('-created_at') print(f"Update jobs: {version_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(version_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Version Management') # Update jobs: 4 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['Extension Update', 'Version Upgrade', 'Patch Install']...>
Example 4: List Required Permissions for anihpj Extension
#!/bin/bash
# Get required permissions for MongoDB extension
aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster \
  --output json | jq '.RequiredActivatedTypes, .ExecutionRoleArn' | jq -s '{
  RequiredTypes: .[0],
  ExecutionRole: .[1]
}'

# Get type configuration
echo ""
echo "Configuration Requirements:"
aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster \
  --query 'TypeTestsStatus' \
  --output text

echo "Extension ready for anihpj infrastructure"
{ "RequiredTypes": [], "ExecutionRole": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CloudFormationManagedUplo..." } Configuration Requirements: PASSED Extension ready for anihpj infrastructure MongoDB Atlas extension validated and ready to use
# Django: Track extension permissions from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # IAM skill iam_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='IAM Management' )[0] print(f"Skill: {iam_skill}") # Find IAM-related jobs iam_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='permission' ).select_related('location') print(f"IAM jobs: {iam_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(iam_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='IAM Management') # IAM jobs: 3 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['IAM Role Setup', 'Permission Config', 'Access Review']...>
Example 5: Generate anihpj Extension Inventory Report
#!/bin/bash
# Create comprehensive extension report for anihpj
REPORT_FILE="anihpj-extensions-report.txt"

echo "anihpj CloudFormation Extensions Report" > $REPORT_FILE
echo "========================================" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "Generated: $(date)" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "" >> $REPORT_FILE

# List all activated extensions
EXTENSIONS=$(aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PUBLIC \
  --provisioning-type FULLY_MUTABLE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].TypeName' \
  --output text)

for EXT in $EXTENSIONS; do
  echo "Extension: $EXT" >> $REPORT_FILE
  echo "----------------------------------------" >> $REPORT_FILE
  
  aws cloudformation describe-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $EXT \
    --query '{IsActivated:IsActivated,ProvisioningType:ProvisioningType,DefaultVersion:DefaultVersionId,LatestVersion:LatestPublicVersion,Deprecated:DeprecatedStatus}' \
    --output text >> $REPORT_FILE
  
  echo "" >> $REPORT_FILE
done

cat $REPORT_FILE | head -50
anihpj CloudFormation Extensions Report ======================================== Generated: Fri Jan 17 10:45:00 2026 Extension: MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster ---------------------------------------- True FULLY_MUTABLE 00000003 1.3.0 LIVE Extension: Datadog::Integrations::AWS ---------------------------------------- True FULLY_MUTABLE 00000003 1.2.0 LIVE Extension: NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads ---------------------------------------- True FULLY_MUTABLE 00000002 2.0.0 LIVE Report saved to anihpj-extensions-report.txt
# Django: Generate extension inventory from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Inventory skill inventory_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Infrastructure Inventory' )[0] print(f"Skill: {inventory_skill}") # Get all extension jobs extension_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='extension' ).values('title', 'is_active', 'created_at') print(f"Extension jobs: {extension_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(extension_jobs[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Infrastructure Inventory') # Extension jobs: 5 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'MongoDB Extension', 'is_active': True, 'created_at': datetime...}]...>

describe-type-registration

Returns information about an extension registration request.

Syntax
aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
  --registration-token {token}
Parameters Description
  • --registration-token - The registration token from register-type
Return Value

Returns ProgressStatus (IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETE, FAILED), Description, TypeArn, and TypeVersionArn.

5 Detailed CloudFormation describe-type-registration Examples
Example 1: Monitor anihpj Custom Extension Registration
#!/bin/bash
# Check registration status for custom anihpj extension
REGISTRATION_TOKEN="$1"

aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
  --registration-token $REGISTRATION_TOKEN \
  --query '{ProgressStatus:ProgressStatus,TypeArn:TypeArn,TypeVersionArn:TypeVersionArn,Description:Description}' \
  --output table
------------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeTypeRegistration | +-------------------+---------------------------------------------------+ | Description | anihpj Django application custom resource | | ProgressStatus | COMPLETE | | TypeArn | arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:...:type/... | | TypeVersionArn | arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:...:type/.../v1 | +-------------------+---------------------------------------------------+ anihpj custom extension registered successfully
# Django: Track custom extension registration from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Extension development skill ext_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Extension Development' )[0] print(f"Skill: {ext_skill}") # Find extension registration jobs reg_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='registration' ).select_related('author') print(f"Registration jobs: {reg_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(reg_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Extension Development') # Registration jobs: 2 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['Extension Registration', 'Type Registration']...>
Example 2: Wait for anihpj Extension Registration Completion
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor registration progress
REGISTRATION_TOKEN="$1"

echo "Monitoring anihpj extension registration..."

while true; do
  REG_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
    --registration-token $REGISTRATION_TOKEN \
    --output json)
  
  STATUS=$(echo "$REG_INFO" | jq -r '.ProgressStatus')
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) - Status: $STATUS"
  
  if [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETE" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Registration Complete!"
    echo "$REG_INFO" | jq '{
      TypeArn: .TypeArn,
      TypeVersionArn: .TypeVersionArn,
      Description: .Description
    }'
    break
  elif [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Registration Failed!"
    echo "$REG_INFO" | jq '.StatusMessage'
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 5
done
Monitoring anihpj extension registration... 10:40:00 - Status: IN_PROGRESS 10:40:05 - Status: IN_PROGRESS 10:40:10 - Status: COMPLETE Registration Complete! { "TypeArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/anihpj-Django-Application", "TypeVersionArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/anihpj-Django-Application/00000001", "Description": "Custom resource for managing anihpj Django applications" } anihpj extension ready to use
# Django: Wait for extension completion from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Completion tracking skill completion_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Deployment Monitoring' )[0] print(f"Skill: {completion_skill}") # Find completion jobs completion_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='complete' ).values('title', 'is_active', 'created_at') print(f"Completion jobs: {completion_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(completion_jobs[:2])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Deployment Monitoring') # Completion jobs: 3 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Registration Complete', 'is_active': False, 'created_at': datetime...}]...>
Example 3: Debug Failed anihpj Extension Registration
#!/bin/bash
# Investigate failed registration
REGISTRATION_TOKEN="$1"

echo "Investigating failed registration for anihpj extension..."

REG_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
  --registration-token $REGISTRATION_TOKEN \
  --output json)

STATUS=$(echo "$REG_INFO" | jq -r '.ProgressStatus')

if [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
  echo "Registration Status: FAILED"
  echo ""
  echo "Error Details:"
  echo "$REG_INFO" | jq -r '.StatusMessage'
  echo ""
  echo "Type Information:"
  echo "$REG_INFO" | jq '{
    TypeName: .TypeName,
    TypeVersion: .TypeVersionArn
  }'
else
  echo "Registration Status: $STATUS"
  echo "$REG_INFO" | jq '.'
fi
Investigating failed registration for anihpj extension... Registration Status: FAILED Error Details: Schema validation failed: Invalid property 'DatabaseEndpoin' - did you mean 'DatabaseEndpoint'? Type Information: { "TypeName": "anihpj::Django::Application", "TypeVersion": null } Fix schema errors in anihpj extension definition
# Django: Track failed registrations from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Debugging skill debug_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Debugging' )[0] print(f"Skill: {debug_skill}") # Find failed registration jobs failed_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='failed' ).select_related('location') print(f"Failed jobs: {failed_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(failed_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Debugging') # Failed jobs: 1 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['Schema Validation Fix']...>
Example 4: Track Multiple anihpj Extension Registrations
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor multiple extension registrations
declare -a TOKENS=("token1" "token2" "token3")

echo "anihpj Extension Registration Status"
echo "====================================="

for TOKEN in "${TOKENS[@]}"; do
  REG_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
    --registration-token $TOKEN \
    --output json 2>/dev/null)
  
  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    TYPE_NAME=$(echo "$REG_INFO" | jq -r '.TypeName')
    STATUS=$(echo "$REG_INFO" | jq -r '.ProgressStatus')
    
    echo "$TYPE_NAME: $STATUS"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "anihpj extension registration tracking complete"
anihpj Extension Registration Status ===================================== anihpj::Django::Application: COMPLETE anihpj::Django::Database: IN_PROGRESS anihpj::Django::Cache: COMPLETE anihpj extension registration tracking complete
# Django: Track multiple registrations from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Multi-extension skill multi_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Multi-Component Management' )[0] print(f"Skill: {multi_skill}") # Find multi-registration jobs multi_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( job_type='REGISTRATION' ).order_by('-created_at') print(f"Multi jobs: {multi_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(multi_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Multi-Component Management') # Multi jobs: 3 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['Application Ext', 'Database Ext', 'Cache Ext']...>
Example 5: Get anihpj Extension Registration History
#!/bin/bash
# List all type registrations for anihpj
echo "anihpj Extension Registration History"
echo "======================================"

aws cloudformation list-type-registrations \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --query 'RegistrationTokenList[*]' \
  --output text | while read TOKEN; do
  
  REG_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
    --registration-token $TOKEN \
    --output json 2>/dev/null)
  
  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    TYPE_NAME=$(echo "$REG_INFO" | jq -r '.TypeName // "Unknown"')
    
    if [[ $TYPE_NAME == *"anihpj"* ]]; then
      echo ""
      echo "Type: $TYPE_NAME"
      echo "$REG_INFO" | jq -r '{
        Status: .ProgressStatus,
        Version: .TypeVersionArn,
        SubmittedAt: .SubmittedAt
      }'
    fi
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "anihpj registration history complete"
anihpj Extension Registration History ====================================== Type: anihpj::Django::Application { "Status": "COMPLETE", "Version": "arn:aws:cloudformation:...:type/resource/.../00000001", "SubmittedAt": "2026-01-15T10:00:00Z" } Type: anihpj::Django::Database { "Status": "COMPLETE", "Version": "arn:aws:cloudformation:...:type/resource/.../00000001", "SubmittedAt": "2026-01-16T14:30:00Z" } anihpj registration history complete
# Django: Get registration history from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # History tracking skill history_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='History Tracking' )[0] print(f"Skill: {history_skill}") # Get registration history history_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='registration' ).order_by('-created_at').values('title', 'created_at', 'is_active') print(f"History count: {history_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(history_jobs[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='History Tracking') # History count: 4 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'DB Registration', 'created_at': datetime..., 'is_active': False}]...>

detect-stack-resource-drift

Returns drift information for a specific resource in a stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation detect-stack-resource-drift \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --logical-resource-id {resource-id}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name - Name or ID of the stack
  • --logical-resource-id - Logical ID of the resource to check
Return Value

Returns StackResourceDrift with StackResourceDriftStatus (IN_SYNC, MODIFIED, DELETED, NOT_CHECKED) and PropertyDifferences.

5 Detailed CloudFormation detect-stack-resource-drift Examples
Example 1: Check anihpj Security Group Drift
#!/bin/bash
# Detect drift for anihpj security group
aws cloudformation detect-stack-resource-drift \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --logical-resource-id AnihpjSecurityGroup \
  --query 'StackResourceDrift.{Resource:LogicalResourceId,Status:StackResourceDriftStatus,Timestamp:Timestamp}' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj security group drift check complete"
------------------------------------------------------------------------ | DetectStackResourceDrift | +-------------------+----------------------+---------------------------+ | Resource | AnihpjSecurityGroup | | | Status | MODIFIED | | | Timestamp | 2026-01-17T10:45:00Z | | +-------------------+----------------------+---------------------------+ ⚠️ anihpj security group has drifted from template
