# Django: Track resource drift from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Drift detection skill drift_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Drift Detection' )[0] print(f"Skill: {drift_skill}") # Find security-related jobs security_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='security' ).select_related('author') print(f"Security jobs: {security_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(security_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Drift Detection') # Security jobs: 3 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['Security Audit', 'SG Configuration', 'Security Review']...>
Example 2: View anihpj Resource Drift Details
#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed drift information for anihpj database
DRIFT_INFO=$(aws cloudformation detect-stack-resource-drift \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --logical-resource-id AnihpjDatabase \
  --output json)

echo "anihpj Database Drift Analysis"
echo "==============================="

echo "$DRIFT_INFO" | jq '.StackResourceDrift | {
  LogicalResourceId,
  ResourceType,
  DriftStatus: .StackResourceDriftStatus,
  PropertyDifferences: .PropertyDifferences | map({
    Path: .PropertyPath,
    Expected: .ExpectedValue,
    Actual: .ActualValue,
    DifferenceType: .DifferenceType
  })
}'
anihpj Database Drift Analysis =============================== { "LogicalResourceId": "AnihpjDatabase", "ResourceType": "AWS::RDS::DBInstance", "DriftStatus": "MODIFIED", "PropertyDifferences": [ { "Path": "/BackupRetentionPeriod", "Expected": "7", "Actual": "14", "DifferenceType": "NOT_EQUAL" }, { "Path": "/MultiAZ", "Expected": "false", "Actual": "true", "DifferenceType": "NOT_EQUAL" } ] } Backup retention and MultiAZ settings modified outside CloudFormation
# Django: Track database drift details from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Database administration skill db_admin_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Database Administration' )[0] print(f"Skill: {db_admin_skill}") # Find database drift jobs db_drift_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='drift' ).filter(title__icontains='database') print(f"DB drift jobs: {db_drift_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(db_drift_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:2])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Database Administration') # DB drift jobs: 2 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['DB Drift Fix', 'MultiAZ Config']...>
Example 3: Check Multiple anihpj Resources for Drift
#!/bin/bash
# Check drift for multiple anihpj resources
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
RESOURCES=("AnihpjDatabase" "AnihpjSecurityGroup" "AnihpjLoadBalancer" "AnihpjWebServer")

echo "anihpj Resource Drift Summary"
echo "=============================="

for RESOURCE in "${RESOURCES[@]}"; do
  DRIFT=$(aws cloudformation detect-stack-resource-drift \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --logical-resource-id $RESOURCE \
    --query 'StackResourceDrift.StackResourceDriftStatus' \
    --output text 2>/dev/null)
  
  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    if [ "$DRIFT" == "IN_SYNC" ]; then
      echo "✓ $RESOURCE: IN_SYNC"
    elif [ "$DRIFT" == "MODIFIED" ]; then
      echo "⚠️  $RESOURCE: MODIFIED"
    elif [ "$DRIFT" == "DELETED" ]; then
      echo "✗ $RESOURCE: DELETED"
    else
      echo "? $RESOURCE: $DRIFT"
    fi
  else
    echo "- $RESOURCE: Unable to check"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "anihpj drift check complete"
anihpj Resource Drift Summary ============================== ✓ AnihpjDatabase: IN_SYNC ⚠️ AnihpjSecurityGroup: MODIFIED ✓ AnihpjLoadBalancer: IN_SYNC ✓ AnihpjWebServer: IN_SYNC anihpj drift check complete Security group requires attention
# Django: Track multiple resource drift from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Multi-resource monitoring skill multi_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Resource Monitoring' )[0] print(f"Skill: {multi_skill}") # Find monitoring jobs monitor_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='monitor' ).values('title', 'is_active', 'created_at') print(f"Monitor jobs: {monitor_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(monitor_jobs[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Resource Monitoring') # Monitor jobs: 4 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Multi-Resource Monitor', 'is_active': True, 'created_at': datetime...}]...>
Example 4: Generate anihpj Drift Remediation Plan
#!/bin/bash
# Create drift remediation plan for anihpj
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
RESOURCE_ID="AnihpjSecurityGroup"

echo "anihpj Drift Remediation Plan"
echo "=============================="

DRIFT_INFO=$(aws cloudformation detect-stack-resource-drift \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --logical-resource-id $RESOURCE_ID \
  --output json)

STATUS=$(echo "$DRIFT_INFO" | jq -r '.StackResourceDrift.StackResourceDriftStatus')

if [ "$STATUS" == "MODIFIED" ]; then
  echo "Resource: $RESOURCE_ID"
  echo "Status: DRIFTED"
  echo ""
  echo "Property Differences:"
  
  echo "$DRIFT_INFO" | jq -r '.StackResourceDrift.PropertyDifferences[] | "
  Path: \(.PropertyPath)
  Expected: \(.ExpectedValue)
  Actual: \(.ActualValue)
  Type: \(.DifferenceType)
  
  Remediation: 
  - Option 1: Update CloudFormation template to match actual value
  - Option 2: Update resource to match template
    aws cloudformation update-stack --stack-name $STACK_NAME
  "'
elif [ "$STATUS" == "IN_SYNC" ]; then
  echo "✓ Resource is in sync - no remediation needed"
fi
anihpj Drift Remediation Plan ============================== Resource: AnihpjSecurityGroup Status: DRIFTED Property Differences: Path: /IpPermissions/0/IpRanges Expected: ["10.0.0.0/8"] Actual: ["0.0.0.0/0", "10.0.0.0/8"] Type: NOT_EQUAL Remediation: - Option 1: Update CloudFormation template to match actual value - Option 2: Update resource to match template aws cloudformation update-stack --stack-name anihpj-django-app Security rule added manually - decide if it should be in template
# Django: Generate drift remediation plans from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Remediation planning skill remediation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Remediation Planning' )[0] print(f"Skill: {remediation_skill}") # Find remediation jobs remediation_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='remediation' ).select_related('location') print(f"Remediation jobs: {remediation_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(remediation_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:2])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Remediation Planning') # Remediation jobs: 2 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['Drift Remediation', 'Security Fix Plan']...>
Example 5: Export anihpj Drift Report
#!/bin/bash
# Generate comprehensive drift report for anihpj stack
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"
REPORT_FILE="anihpj-drift-report-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

echo "Generating anihpj drift report..."

# Get all resources
RESOURCES=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'StackResources[*].LogicalResourceId' \
  --output text)

# Create JSON array
echo "[" > $REPORT_FILE

FIRST=true
for RESOURCE in $RESOURCES; do
  DRIFT=$(aws cloudformation detect-stack-resource-drift \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --logical-resource-id $RESOURCE \
    --output json 2>/dev/null)
  
  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    if [ "$FIRST" = true ]; then
      FIRST=false
    else
      echo "," >> $REPORT_FILE
    fi
    echo "$DRIFT" | jq '.StackResourceDrift' >> $REPORT_FILE
  fi
done

echo "]" >> $REPORT_FILE

# Generate summary
DRIFTED_COUNT=$(jq '[.[] | select(.StackResourceDriftStatus == "MODIFIED")] | length' $REPORT_FILE)
TOTAL_COUNT=$(jq 'length' $REPORT_FILE)

echo ""
echo "anihpj Drift Report Summary:"
echo "  Total Resources: $TOTAL_COUNT"
echo "  Drifted Resources: $DRIFTED_COUNT"
echo "  Report saved to: $REPORT_FILE"
Generating anihpj drift report... anihpj Drift Report Summary: Total Resources: 24 Drifted Resources: 3 Report saved to: anihpj-drift-report-20260117.json Review drifted resources and update templates or resources accordingly
# Django: Export comprehensive drift reports from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Report generation skill report_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Report Generation' )[0] print(f"Skill: {report_skill}") # Find drift report jobs report_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='drift' ).order_by('-created_at').values('title', 'author__user__username') print(f"Drift report jobs: {report_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(report_jobs[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Report Generation') # Drift report jobs: 3 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Drift Report Export', 'author__user__username': 'admin'}]...>

detect-stack-set-drift

Detect drift on a stack set and all stack instances within it.

Syntax
aws cloudformation detect-stack-set-drift \
  --stack-set-name {stackset-name}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name - Name or ID of the StackSet
  • --operation-preferences - (Optional) Drift detection preferences
  • --call-as - (Optional) Specify SELF or DELEGATED_ADMIN
Return Value

Returns OperationId for tracking the drift detection operation.

5 Detailed CloudFormation detect-stack-set-drift Examples
Example 1: Detect Drift on anihpj StackSet
#!/bin/bash
# Start drift detection for anihpj multi-account StackSet
OPERATION_ID=$(aws cloudformation detect-stack-set-drift \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --query 'OperationId' \
  --output text)

echo "Drift detection started for anihpj StackSet"
echo "Operation ID: $OPERATION_ID"

# Monitor progress
aws cloudformation describe-stack-set-operation \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
  --query 'StackSetOperation.{Status:Status,DriftStatus:StackSetDriftDetectionDetails.DriftStatus}' \
  --output table
Drift detection started for anihpj StackSet Operation ID: operation-drift-abc123 ------------------------------------------------- | DescribeStackSetOperation | +-------------+---------------------------------+ | DriftStatus | IN_PROGRESS | | Status | RUNNING | +-------------+---------------------------------+ anihpj StackSet drift detection initiated
# Django: Track StackSet drift detection from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # StackSet drift skill stackset_drift_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='StackSet Drift Detection' )[0] print(f"Skill: {stackset_drift_skill}") # Find StackSet drift jobs stackset_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='stackset' ).filter(description__icontains='drift') print(f"StackSet drift jobs: {stackset_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(stackset_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:2])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='StackSet Drift Detection') # StackSet drift jobs: 2 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['StackSet Drift Check', 'Multi-Account Drift']...>
Example 2: Monitor anihpj StackSet Drift Detection
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor drift detection progress
STACKSET_NAME="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
OPERATION_ID="$1"

echo "Monitoring anihpj StackSet drift detection..."

while true; do
  OP_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-set-operation \
    --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
    --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
    --output json)
  
  STATUS=$(echo "$OP_INFO" | jq -r '.StackSetOperation.Status')
  DRIFT_STATUS=$(echo "$OP_INFO" | jq -r '.StackSetOperation.StackSetDriftDetectionDetails.DriftStatus // "Checking..."')
  DRIFTED=$(echo "$OP_INFO" | jq -r '.StackSetOperation.StackSetDriftDetectionDetails.DriftedStackInstancesCount // 0')
  
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) - Status: $STATUS | Drift: $DRIFT_STATUS | Drifted Instances: $DRIFTED"
  
  if [ "$STATUS" == "SUCCEEDED" ] || [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Final Results:"
    echo "$OP_INFO" | jq '.StackSetOperation.StackSetDriftDetectionDetails'
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 15
done
Monitoring anihpj StackSet drift detection... 10:50:00 - Status: RUNNING | Drift: IN_PROGRESS | Drifted Instances: 0 10:50:15 - Status: RUNNING | Drift: IN_PROGRESS | Drifted Instances: 2 10:50:30 - Status: SUCCEEDED | Drift: DRIFTED | Drifted Instances: 3 Final Results: { "DriftStatus": "DRIFTED", "DriftedStackInstancesCount": 3, "TotalStackInstancesCount": 12, "InSyncStackInstancesCount": 9 } 3 anihpj stack instances have drifted
# Django: Monitor StackSet drift progress from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Monitoring skill monitor_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='StackSet Monitoring' )[0] print(f"Skill: {monitor_skill}") # Track monitoring jobs monitoring_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='monitor' ).values('title', 'is_active', 'created_at') print(f"Monitoring jobs: {monitoring_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(monitoring_jobs[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='StackSet Monitoring') # Monitoring jobs: 4 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Drift Monitor', 'is_active': True, 'created_at': datetime...}]...>
Example 3: Check anihpj Drifted Stack Instances
#!/bin/bash
# After drift detection, list drifted instances
STACKSET_NAME="anihpj-django-infrastructure"

echo "anihpj Drifted Stack Instances"
echo "==============================="

aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
  --filters Name=DRIFT_STATUS,Values=DRIFTED \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[Account,Region,DriftStatus,LastDriftCheckTimestamp]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Review and remediate drifted instances"
anihpj Drifted Stack Instances =============================== ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | 123456789012 | us-west-2 | DRIFTED | 2026-01-17T10:50:30Z | | 987654321098 | us-east-1 | DRIFTED | 2026-01-17T10:50:28Z | | 111222333444 | eu-west-1 | DRIFTED | 2026-01-17T10:50:25Z | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ Review and remediate drifted instances
# Django: Check drifted stack instances from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Instance checking skill instance_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Instance Management' )[0] print(f"Skill: {instance_skill}") # Find drifted instance jobs drifted_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='drifted' ).select_related('location') print(f"Drifted jobs: {drifted_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(drifted_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:2])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Instance Management') # Drifted jobs: 3 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['Drifted Instance Fix', 'Stack Remediation']...>
Example 4: Scheduled anihpj Drift Detection
#!/bin/bash
# Weekly drift detection for anihpj StackSets
STACKSETS=("anihpj-django-infrastructure" "anihpj-security-baseline" "anihpj-cicd-pipeline")

echo "anihpj Weekly Drift Detection Report"
echo "====================================="
echo "Date: $(date)"
echo ""

for STACKSET in "${STACKSETS[@]}"; do
  echo "Checking: $STACKSET"
  
  OPERATION_ID=$(aws cloudformation detect-stack-set-drift \
    --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
    --query 'OperationId' \
    --output text 2>/dev/null)
  
  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "  Operation: $OPERATION_ID"
    echo "  Status: Detection started"
  else
    echo "  Status: Failed to start detection"
  fi
  echo ""
done

echo "All anihpj StackSet drift detections initiated"
anihpj Weekly Drift Detection Report ===================================== Date: Fri Jan 17 10:55:00 2026 Checking: anihpj-django-infrastructure Operation: operation-drift-abc123 Status: Detection started Checking: anihpj-security-baseline Operation: operation-drift-def456 Status: Detection started Checking: anihpj-cicd-pipeline Operation: operation-drift-ghi789 Status: Detection started All anihpj StackSet drift detections initiated
# Django: Schedule drift detection from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Scheduling skill schedule_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Scheduled Operations' )[0] print(f"Skill: {schedule_skill}") # Find scheduled jobs scheduled_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='scheduled' ).order_by('-created_at') print(f"Scheduled jobs: {scheduled_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(scheduled_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Scheduled Operations') # Scheduled jobs: 3 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['Weekly Drift Check', 'Scheduled Monitoring', 'Auto Detection']...>
Example 5: Export anihpj StackSet Drift Report
#!/bin/bash
# Generate comprehensive drift report
STACKSET_NAME="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
REPORT_FILE="anihpj-stackset-drift-$(date +%Y%m%d).txt"

echo "anihpj StackSet Drift Report" > $REPORT_FILE
echo "Generated: $(date)" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "StackSet: $STACKSET_NAME" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "===============================" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "" >> $REPORT_FILE

# Get StackSet drift status
aws cloudformation describe-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
  --query 'StackSet.{Status:Status,DriftStatus:StackSetDriftDetectionDetails.DriftStatus,LastDriftCheck:StackSetDriftDetectionDetails.LastDriftCheckTimestamp}' \
  --output text >> $REPORT_FILE

echo "" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "Drifted Instances:" >> $REPORT_FILE

aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
  --filters Name=DRIFT_STATUS,Values=DRIFTED \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[Account,Region,Status,DriftStatus]' \
  --output text >> $REPORT_FILE

cat $REPORT_FILE
anihpj StackSet Drift Report Generated: Fri Jan 17 10:55:00 2026 StackSet: anihpj-django-infrastructure =============================== ACTIVE DRIFTED 2026-01-17T10:50:30Z Drifted Instances: 123456789012 us-west-2 CURRENT DRIFTED 987654321098 us-east-1 CURRENT DRIFTED 111222333444 eu-west-1 CURRENT DRIFTED Report saved - remediation required for 3 instances
# Django: Export StackSet drift reports from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Report export skill export_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Report Export' )[0] print(f"Skill: {export_skill}") # Find export report jobs export_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='export' ).values('title', 'author__user__username', 'created_at') print(f"Export jobs: {export_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(export_jobs[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Report Export') # Export jobs: 2 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'StackSet Export', 'author__user__username': 'admin', 'created_at': datetime...}]...>

execute-stack-refactor

Executes a stack refactor operation to move resources between stacks.

Syntax
aws cloudformation execute-stack-refactor \
  --stack-refactor-id {refactor-id}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-refactor-id - The ID of the stack refactor operation to execute
Return Value

Returns no output on success. Use describe-stack-refactor to track execution status.

5 Detailed CloudFormation execute-stack-refactor Examples
Example 1: Execute anihpj Stack Refactor
#!/bin/bash
# Execute the prepared refactor operation
REFACTOR_ID="$1"

echo "Executing anihpj stack refactor..."

aws cloudformation execute-stack-refactor \
  --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID

echo "Refactor execution started"
echo "Refactor ID: $REFACTOR_ID"

# Monitor progress
aws cloudformation describe-stack-refactor \
  --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
  --query '{Status:Status,Actions:Actions|length(@)}' \
  --output table
Executing anihpj stack refactor... Refactor execution started Refactor ID: refactor-abc123 ----------------------------------------- | DescribeStackRefactor | +----------+----------------------------+ | Actions | 5 | | Status | IN_PROGRESS | +----------+----------------------------+ anihpj stack reorganization in progress
# Django: Execute stack refactor from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Stack refactor execution skill execute_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Refactor Execution' )[0] print(f"Skill: {execute_skill}") # Find execution jobs execution_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='execution' ).select_related('author') print(f"Execution jobs: {execution_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(execution_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Refactor Execution') # Execution jobs: 2 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['Stack Execution', 'Refactor Execute', 'Stack Reorganization']...>
Example 2: Execute anihpj Database Stack Split
#!/bin/bash
# Execute database split for anihpj
REFACTOR_ID="refactor-db-split-123"

echo "Executing anihpj database stack split..."

# Execute the refactor
aws cloudformation execute-stack-refactor \
  --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID

# Monitor until complete
while true; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-refactor \
    --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
    --query 'Status' \
    --output text)
  
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) - Status: $STATUS"
  
  if [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETE" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "anihpj database stack split complete!"
    echo "New stacks created:"
    echo "  - anihpj-django-app (application layer)"
    echo "  - anihpj-database (database resources)"
    break
  elif [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
    echo "Refactor failed - check actions for details"
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 10
done
Executing anihpj database stack split... 11:00:00 - Status: IN_PROGRESS 11:00:10 - Status: IN_PROGRESS 11:00:20 - Status: COMPLETE anihpj database stack split complete! New stacks created: - anihpj-django-app (application layer) - anihpj-database (database resources)
# Django: Execute database stack split from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Database split skill split_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Database Stack Split' )[0] print(f"Skill: {split_skill}") # Find database split jobs split_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='split' ).filter(description__icontains='database') print(f"Split jobs: {split_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(split_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:2])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Database Stack Split') # Split jobs: 2 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['DB Split Execute', 'Stack Split Complete']...>
Example 3: Execute anihpj Microservices Refactor with Validation
#!/bin/bash
# Validate and execute anihpj microservices refactor
REFACTOR_ID="$1"

echo "Validating anihpj refactor before execution..."

# Check refactor status
REFACTOR_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-refactor \
  --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
  --output json)

STATUS=$(echo "$REFACTOR_INFO" | jq -r '.Status')
ACTIONS=$(echo "$REFACTOR_INFO" | jq -r '.Actions | length')

echo "Current Status: $STATUS"
echo "Actions to Execute: $ACTIONS"

if [ "$STATUS" == "READY" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "Executing refactor..."
  aws cloudformation execute-stack-refactor \
    --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID
  
  echo "anihpj microservices refactor execution started"
else
  echo "Cannot execute - status is $STATUS"
  echo "Refactor must be in READY status"
fi
Validating anihpj refactor before execution... Current Status: READY Actions to Execute: 8 Executing refactor... anihpj microservices refactor execution started Splitting monolith into: - anihpj-api-service - anihpj-worker-service - anihpj-database-service
# Django: Execute microservices refactor with validation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Validation skill validation_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Refactor Validation' )[0] print(f"Skill: {validation_skill}") # Find validation jobs validation_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( description__icontains='validation' ).order_by('-created_at') print(f"Validation jobs: {validation_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(validation_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:2])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Refactor Validation') # Validation jobs: 2 # Jobs: <QuerySet ['Microservices Validation', 'Pre-Execute Check']...>
Example 4: Execute with Progress Tracking for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Execute refactor with detailed progress tracking
REFACTOR_ID="$1"

echo "anihpj Stack Refactor Execution"
echo "================================"

aws cloudformation execute-stack-refactor \
  --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID

# Track each action
while true; do
  REFACTOR_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-refactor \
    --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
    --output json)
  
  STATUS=$(echo "$REFACTOR_INFO" | jq -r '.Status')
  
  echo ""
  echo "Status: $STATUS"
  echo "Action Progress:"
  echo "$REFACTOR_INFO" | jq -r '.Actions[] | "  \(.ResourceIdentifier): \(.Status)"'
  
  if [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETE" ] || [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 5
done

echo ""
echo "anihpj refactor execution finished"
anihpj Stack Refactor Execution ================================ Status: IN_PROGRESS Action Progress: AnihpjDatabase: COMPLETE AnihpjDBSubnetGroup: IN_PROGRESS AnihpjCacheCluster: PENDING Status: IN_PROGRESS Action Progress: AnihpjDatabase: COMPLETE AnihpjDBSubnetGroup: COMPLETE AnihpjCacheCluster: IN_PROGRESS Status: COMPLETE Action Progress: AnihpjDatabase: COMPLETE AnihpjDBSubnetGroup: COMPLETE AnihpjCacheCluster: COMPLETE anihpj refactor execution finished
# Django: Execute with progress tracking from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Progress tracking skill progress_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create( name='Progress Tracking' )[0] print(f"Skill: {progress_skill}") # Find progress tracking jobs progress_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( title__icontains='progress' ).values('title', 'is_active', 'job_type') print(f"Progress jobs: {progress_jobs.count()}") print(f"Jobs: <QuerySet {list(progress_jobs[:3])}...>") # Output: # Skill: Skills(name='Progress Tracking') # Progress jobs: 3 # Jobs: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Progress Monitor', 'is_active': True, 'job_type': 'MONITORING'}]...>
Example 5: Execute anihpj Refactor with Rollback Plan
#!/bin/bash
# Execute with rollback preparation
REFACTOR_ID="$1"

echo "Preparing anihpj refactor execution..."

# Backup current state
echo "Backing up current stack configurations..."
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-monolith \
  --output json > anihpj-backup-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).json

# Execute refactor
echo ""
echo "Executing refactor..."
aws cloudformation execute-stack-refactor \
  --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID

# Wait for completion
STATUS="IN_PROGRESS"
while [ "$STATUS" == "IN_PROGRESS" ]; do
  sleep 10
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-refactor \
    --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
    --query 'Status' \
    --output text)
  echo "Status: $STATUS"
done

if [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETE" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "anihpj refactor completed successfully"
  echo "New stack structure in place"
else
  echo ""
  echo "Refactor failed - rollback may be needed"
  echo "Backup file available for restoration"
fi
Preparing anihpj refactor execution... Backing up current stack configurations... Backup saved to anihpj-backup-20260117-110500.json Executing refactor... Status: IN_PROGRESS Status: IN_PROGRESS Status: COMPLETE anihpj refactor completed successfully New stack structure in place

get-generated-template

Retrieves a generated CloudFormation template for existing resources.

Syntax
aws cloudformation get-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name {template-name} \
  --format YAML
Parameters Description
  • --generated-template-name - Name or ARN of the generated template
  • --format - (Optional) Output format: JSON or YAML (default: JSON)
Return Value

Returns Status, TemplateBody, and GeneratedTemplateId.

5 Detailed CloudFormation get-generated-template Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Generated Template
#!/bin/bash
# Retrieve generated template for anihpj infrastructure
aws cloudformation get-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-django-import \
  --format YAML > anihpj-generated.yaml

echo "anihpj generated template saved to anihpj-generated.yaml"
echo ""
echo "Template Preview:"
head -50 anihpj-generated.yaml
anihpj generated template saved to anihpj-generated.yaml Template Preview: AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09' Description: Generated template for anihpj Django infrastructure Resources: AnihpjVPC: Type: AWS::EC2::VPC Properties: CidrBlock: 10.0.0.0/16 EnableDnsHostnames: true EnableDnsSupport: true Tags: - Key: Name Value: anihpj-vpc - Key: Project Value: anihpj AnihpjDatabase: Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance Properties: DBInstanceIdentifier: anihpj-db DBInstanceClass: db.t3.medium Engine: postgres ...
Example 2: Get anihpj Template in JSON Format
#!/bin/bash
# Get template in JSON for programmatic processing
aws cloudformation get-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-production-import \
  --format JSON \
  --output text > anihpj-generated.json

# Parse and list resources
echo "anihpj Generated Template Resources:"
cat anihpj-generated.json | jq -r '.Resources | keys[]' | nl

echo ""
echo "Total Resources: $(cat anihpj-generated.json | jq '.Resources | length')"
anihpj Generated Template Resources: 1 AnihpjAutoScalingGroup 2 AnihpjCacheCluster 3 AnihpjDatabase 4 AnihpjIAMRole 5 AnihpjLaunchTemplate 6 AnihpjListener 7 AnihpjLoadBalancer 8 AnihpjRouteTable 9 AnihpjS3Bucket 10 AnihpjSecurityGroup 11 AnihpjSubnet1 12 AnihpjSubnet2 13 AnihpjTargetGroup 14 AnihpjVPC 15 AnihpjWebServer Total Resources: 15
Example 3: Customize anihpj Generated Template
#!/bin/bash
# Get and customize generated template
aws cloudformation get-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name anihpj-legacy-import \
  --format YAML > anihpj-base.yaml

# Add parameters for customization
cat > anihpj-customized.yaml << 'EOF'
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: Customized anihpj Django infrastructure

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Default: production
    AllowedValues:
      - development
      - staging
      - production

  ProjectName:
    Type: String
    Default: anihpj

EOF

# Append generated resources
tail -n +4 anihpj-base.yaml >> anihpj-customized.yaml

echo "Customized template created: anihpj-customized.yaml"
echo "Added parameters for Environment and ProjectName"
Customized template created: anihpj-customized.yaml Added parameters for Environment and ProjectName Template now includes: - Environment parameter (dev/staging/prod) - ProjectName parameter - All 15 imported resources Ready for deployment with customization options
Example 4: Compare Generated vs Existing anihpj Template
#!/bin/bash
# Compare generated template with existing
GENERATED_TEMPLATE="anihpj-import-2026"
EXISTING_TEMPLATE="anihpj-current.yaml"

# Get generated template
aws cloudformation get-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $GENERATED_TEMPLATE \
  --format YAML > anihpj-generated-compare.yaml

# Compare resources
echo "anihpj Template Comparison"
echo "=========================="

GENERATED_RESOURCES=$(cat anihpj-generated-compare.yaml | yq '.Resources | keys | sort | .[]')
EXISTING_RESOURCES=$(cat $EXISTING_TEMPLATE | yq '.Resources | keys | sort | .[]')

echo "Resources in Generated but not in Existing:"
comm -23 <(echo "$GENERATED_RESOURCES") <(echo "$EXISTING_RESOURCES") | sed 's/^/  + /'

echo ""
echo "Resources in Existing but not in Generated:"
comm -13 <(echo "$GENERATED_RESOURCES") <(echo "$EXISTING_RESOURCES") | sed 's/^/  - /'
anihpj Template Comparison ========================== Resources in Generated but not in Existing: + AnihpjLegacyBucket + AnihpjManualSecurityGroup + AnihpjTestInstance Resources in Existing but not in Generated: - AnihpjCloudFrontDist - AnihpjWAFWebACL 3 resources found outside CloudFormation management 2 resources in template not found in account
Example 5: Export anihpj Template for Import
#!/bin/bash
# Prepare generated template for stack import
TEMPLATE_NAME="anihpj-production-import"
OUTPUT_DIR="anihpj-import-package"

mkdir -p $OUTPUT_DIR

# Get the template
aws cloudformation get-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --format YAML > $OUTPUT_DIR/template.yaml

# Get template description
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --output json > $OUTPUT_DIR/template-info.json

# Create import configuration
cat > $OUTPUT_DIR/import-config.json << EOF
{
  "StackName": "anihpj-django-imported",
  "TemplateBody": "file://template.yaml",
  "Parameters": [],
  "Capabilities": ["CAPABILITY_IAM", "CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM"],
  "Tags": [
    {"Key": "Project", "Value": "anihpj"},
    {"Key": "ImportedFrom", "Value": "$TEMPLATE_NAME"}
  ]
}
EOF

echo "anihpj Import Package Created:"
ls -la $OUTPUT_DIR/

echo ""
echo "To import, run:"
echo "  cd $OUTPUT_DIR"
echo "  aws cloudformation create-stack --cli-input-json file://import-config.json"
anihpj Import Package Created: total 24 -rw-r--r-- 1 user user 8234 Jan 17 11:10 template.yaml -rw-r--r-- 1 user user 1456 Jan 17 11:10 template-info.json -rw-r--r-- 1 user user 312 Jan 17 11:10 import-config.json To import, run: cd anihpj-import-package aws cloudformation create-stack --cli-input-json file://import-config.json

get-hook-result

Returns the result of a hook invocation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation get-hook-result \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --hook-type {hook-type} \
  --invocation-point {invocation-point}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name - Name or ID of the stack
  • --hook-type - The type of the hook
  • --invocation-point - PRE_PROVISION or POST_PROVISION
Return Value

Returns HookStatus, HookStatusMessage, and TargetResources.

5 Detailed CloudFormation get-hook-result Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Security Hook Result
#!/bin/bash
# Get security hook result for anihpj stack
aws cloudformation get-hook-result \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --hook-type AWS::SecurityHub::Compliance \
  --invocation-point PRE_PROVISION \
  --query '{Status:Status,Message:Message,Timestamp:Timestamp}' \
  --output table
---------------------------------------------------------------- | GetHookResult | +------------+-------------------------------------------------+ | Message | All security requirements met | | Status | SUCCESS | | Timestamp | 2026-01-17T11:15:00Z | +------------+-------------------------------------------------+ anihpj security hook passed
Example 2: Debug anihpj Failed Hook
#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed hook failure information
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
HOOK_TYPE="Custom::SecurityValidator"

HOOK_RESULT=$(aws cloudformation get-hook-result \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --hook-type $HOOK_TYPE \
  --invocation-point PRE_PROVISION \
  --output json)

echo "anihpj Hook Result Analysis"
echo "============================"

echo "$HOOK_RESULT" | jq '{
  HookType: .HookType,
  Status: .Status,
  Message: .Message,
  FailureMode: .FailureMode,
  TargetResources: .TargetDetails.ResourceTargetDetails
}'

if [ "$(echo "$HOOK_RESULT" | jq -r '.Status')" == "FAILED" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "Hook Failed - Required Actions:"
  echo "$HOOK_RESULT" | jq -r '.Message'
fi
anihpj Hook Result Analysis ============================ { "HookType": "Custom::SecurityValidator", "Status": "FAILED", "Message": "Security group AnihpjSG allows unrestricted SSH access", "FailureMode": "FAIL", "TargetResources": [ { "LogicalResourceId": "AnihpjSecurityGroup" } ] } Hook Failed - Required Actions: Security group AnihpjSG allows unrestricted SSH access Restrict port 22 to specific IP ranges
Example 3: Review anihpj Cost Validation Hook
#!/bin/bash
# Check cost validation hook result
aws cloudformation get-hook-result \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-production \
  --hook-type Custom::CostValidator \
  --invocation-point PRE_PROVISION \
  --output json | jq '{
    Status: .Status,
    EstimatedMonthlyCost: .Message | capture("Estimated: (?\\$[0-9,]+)") | .cost,
    ApprovalRequired: (.Status == "WARNING")
  }'

echo "anihpj cost validation complete"
{ "Status": "WARNING", "EstimatedMonthlyCost": "$1,245", "ApprovalRequired": true } anihpj cost validation complete Deployment will proceed but cost exceeds threshold
Example 4: Get All anihpj Hook Results
#!/bin/bash
# Get all hook results for anihpj stack
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

echo "anihpj Hook Results Summary"
echo "============================"

# List all hook results
aws cloudformation list-hook-results \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'HookResults[*].[HookType,InvocationPoint,Status]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Details for failed hooks:"
aws cloudformation list-hook-results \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'HookResults[?Status==`FAILED`]' \
  --output json | jq -r '.[] | "
Hook: \(.HookType)
Invocation: \(.InvocationPoint)
Message: \(.Message)
"'
anihpj Hook Results Summary ============================ ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | AWS::SecurityHub::Compliance | PRE_PROVISION | SUCCESS | | Custom::CostValidator | PRE_PROVISION | WARNING | | Custom::TagValidator | PRE_PROVISION | SUCCESS | | Custom::SecurityValidator | PRE_PROVISION | FAILED | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ Details for failed hooks: Hook: Custom::SecurityValidator Invocation: PRE_PROVISION Message: Security group allows unrestricted access on port 22 1 hook failed - must resolve before deployment
Example 5: Export anihpj Hook Results for Audit
#!/bin/bash
# Export hook results for compliance audit
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"
AUDIT_FILE="anihpj-hook-audit-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

echo "Generating anihpj Hook Audit Report..."

# Get all hook results
aws cloudformation list-hook-results \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --output json > $AUDIT_FILE

# Create summary
echo ""
echo "anihpj Hook Audit Summary"
echo "=========================="

jq -r '
{
  TotalHooks: (.HookResults | length),
  Successful: ([.HookResults[] | select(.Status == "SUCCESS")] | length),
  Warnings: ([.HookResults[] | select(.Status == "WARNING")] | length),
  Failed: ([.HookResults[] | select(.Status == "FAILED")] | length),
  HookTypes: ([.HookResults[].HookType] | unique)
}
' $AUDIT_FILE

echo ""
echo "Full audit saved to $AUDIT_FILE"
Generating anihpj Hook Audit Report... anihpj Hook Audit Summary ========================== { "TotalHooks": 5, "Successful": 3, "Warnings": 1, "Failed": 1, "HookTypes": [ "AWS::SecurityHub::Compliance", "Custom::CostValidator", "Custom::SecurityValidator", "Custom::TagValidator", "Custom::NotificationHook" ] } Full audit saved to anihpj-hook-audit-20260117.json

import-stacks-to-stack-set

Import existing stacks into a StackSet for centralized management.

Syntax
aws cloudformation import-stacks-to-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name {stackset-name} \
  --stack-ids {stack-arns}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name - Name of the target StackSet
  • --stack-ids - List of stack ARNs to import
  • --operation-preferences - (Optional) Import preferences
Return Value

Returns OperationId to track the import operation.

5 Detailed CloudFormation import-stacks-to-stack-set Examples
Example 1: Import anihpj Stacks to StackSet
#!/bin/bash
# Import existing anihpj stacks into a StackSet
STACKSET_NAME="anihpj-django-managed"

# Get stack ARNs
STACK_IDS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --query 'Stacks[?contains(StackName, `anihpj-django`)].StackId' \
  --output text | tr '\t' ' ')

echo "Importing anihpj stacks to StackSet..."
echo "Stacks: $STACK_IDS"

OPERATION_ID=$(aws cloudformation import-stacks-to-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
  --stack-ids $STACK_IDS \
  --query 'OperationId' \
  --output text)

echo "Import operation: $OPERATION_ID"
Importing anihpj stacks to StackSet... Stacks: arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-prod/... arn:aws:cloudformation:us-west-2:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-staging/... Import operation: operation-import-abc123 anihpj stacks being imported to centralized management
Example 2: Import anihpj Multi-Region Stacks
#!/bin/bash
# Import stacks from multiple regions to StackSet
STACKSET_NAME="anihpj-infrastructure-global"
REGIONS=("us-east-1" "us-west-2" "eu-west-1")

ALL_STACK_IDS=""

for REGION in "${REGIONS[@]}"; do
  echo "Finding anihpj stacks in $REGION..."
  
  STACK_IDS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --region $REGION \
    --query 'Stacks[?contains(StackName, `anihpj`)].StackId' \
    --output text | tr '\t' ' ')
  
  ALL_STACK_IDS="$ALL_STACK_IDS $STACK_IDS"
done

echo ""
echo "Importing all anihpj stacks to StackSet..."

aws cloudformation import-stacks-to-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
  --stack-ids $ALL_STACK_IDS

echo "Import initiated for all regions"
Finding anihpj stacks in us-east-1... Finding anihpj stacks in us-west-2... Finding anihpj stacks in eu-west-1... Importing all anihpj stacks to StackSet... { "OperationId": "operation-import-multi-123" } Import initiated for all regions 6 anihpj stacks across 3 regions being imported
Example 3: Import with Operation Preferences for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Import with controlled rollout
STACKSET_NAME="anihpj-django-centralized"
STACK_IDS="arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-prod/abc123"

aws cloudformation import-stacks-to-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
  --stack-ids $STACK_IDS \
  --operation-preferences \
    RegionConcurrencyType=SEQUENTIAL,\
    FailureToleranceCount=0,\
    MaxConcurrentCount=1

echo "anihpj stacks importing with careful rollout"
echo "Sequential region processing, zero failure tolerance"
{ "OperationId": "operation-import-careful-456" } anihpj stacks importing with careful rollout Sequential region processing, zero failure tolerance Import will stop immediately if any failure occurs
Example 4: Monitor anihpj Stack Import Progress
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor import operation progress
STACKSET_NAME="anihpj-django-managed"
OPERATION_ID="$1"

echo "Monitoring anihpj stack import..."

while true; do
  OP_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-set-operation \
    --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
    --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
    --output json)
  
  STATUS=$(echo "$OP_INFO" | jq -r '.StackSetOperation.Status')
  ACTION=$(echo "$OP_INFO" | jq -r '.StackSetOperation.Action')
  
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) - Action: $ACTION | Status: $STATUS"
  
  if [ "$STATUS" == "SUCCEEDED" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Import successful!"
    echo "Stacks now managed by StackSet: $STACKSET_NAME"
    
    # List imported instances
    aws cloudformation list-stack-instances \
      --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
      --query 'Summaries[*].[Account,Region,Status]' \
      --output table
    break
  elif [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
    echo "Import failed"
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 10
done
Monitoring anihpj stack import... 11:20:00 - Action: CREATE | Status: RUNNING 11:20:10 - Action: CREATE | Status: RUNNING 11:20:20 - Action: CREATE | Status: SUCCEEDED Import successful! Stacks now managed by StackSet: anihpj-django-managed ----------------------------------------------------------- | 123456789012 | us-east-1 | CURRENT | | 123456789012 | us-west-2 | CURRENT | | 987654321098 | us-east-1 | CURRENT | ----------------------------------------------------------- anihpj stacks now centrally managed
Example 5: Import anihpj Legacy Stacks with Validation
#!/bin/bash
# Validate and import legacy stacks
STACKSET_NAME="anihpj-django-unified"

echo "anihpj Legacy Stack Import Process"
echo "==================================="

# Find all anihpj stacks
echo "Step 1: Finding anihpj stacks..."
STACKS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --query 'Stacks[?contains(StackName, `anihpj`) && StackStatus==`CREATE_COMPLETE`].[StackId,StackName]' \
  --output text)

echo "$STACKS" | while read STACK_ID STACK_NAME; do
  echo "  Found: $STACK_NAME"
done

# Validate template compatibility
echo ""
echo "Step 2: Validating template compatibility..."
# (Would compare stack templates with StackSet template)

# Perform import
echo ""
echo "Step 3: Importing to StackSet..."

STACK_IDS=$(echo "$STACKS" | awk '{print $1}' | tr '\n' ' ')

OPERATION_ID=$(aws cloudformation import-stacks-to-stack-set \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
  --stack-ids $STACK_IDS \
  --query 'OperationId' \
  --output text)

echo "Import operation started: $OPERATION_ID"
echo ""
echo "anihpj legacy stacks being imported to unified StackSet"
anihpj Legacy Stack Import Process =================================== Step 1: Finding anihpj stacks... Found: anihpj-django-prod-legacy Found: anihpj-django-staging-legacy Found: anihpj-django-dev-legacy Step 2: Validating template compatibility... Templates compatible with StackSet Step 3: Importing to StackSet... Import operation started: operation-import-legacy-789 anihpj legacy stacks being imported to unified StackSet 3 stacks will be centrally managed after import

list-generated-templates

Lists all generated templates in your account.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-generated-templates
Parameters Description
  • --next-token - (Optional) Token for pagination
  • --max-results - (Optional) Maximum number of results
Return Value

Returns Summaries array with GeneratedTemplateId, GeneratedTemplateName, Status, and ResourcesTotal.

5 Detailed CloudFormation list-generated-templates Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Generated Templates
#!/bin/bash
# List all generated templates
aws cloudformation list-generated-templates \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[GeneratedTemplateName,Status,NumberOfResources,CreationTime]' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj generated templates listed"
---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListGeneratedTemplates | +----------------------------+-----------+------------------+---------------+ | anihpj-production-import | COMPLETE | 15 | 2026-01-15... | | anihpj-staging-import | COMPLETE | 12 | 2026-01-14... | | anihpj-legacy-migration | COMPLETE | 23 | 2026-01-10... | | anihpj-dev-snapshot | IN_PROGRESS| 8 | 2026-01-17... | +----------------------------+-----------+------------------+---------------+ anihpj generated templates listed
Example 2: Filter anihpj Templates by Status
#!/bin/bash
# List only completed templates for anihpj
echo "Completed anihpj Generated Templates:"
aws cloudformation list-generated-templates \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`COMPLETE`].[GeneratedTemplateName,NumberOfResources]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "In-Progress Templates:"
aws cloudformation list-generated-templates \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`IN_PROGRESS`].[GeneratedTemplateName,Status]' \
  --output table
Completed anihpj Generated Templates: -------------------------------------------------- | anihpj-production-import | 15 | | anihpj-staging-import | 12 | | anihpj-legacy-migration | 23 | -------------------------------------------------- In-Progress Templates: -------------------------------------------------- | anihpj-dev-snapshot | IN_PROGRESS | -------------------------------------------------- 3 templates ready, 1 in progress
Example 3: Get anihpj Template Details
#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed info about each anihpj template
echo "anihpj Generated Template Details"
echo "==================================="

aws cloudformation list-generated-templates \
  --output json | jq -r '.Summaries[] | select(.GeneratedTemplateName | contains("anihpj")) | "
Template: \(.GeneratedTemplateName)
  Status: \(.Status)
  Resources: \(.NumberOfResources)
  Created: \(.CreationTime)
  Resource Scan: \(.ResourceScanId // "N/A")
"'
anihpj Generated Template Details =================================== Template: anihpj-production-import Status: COMPLETE Resources: 15 Created: 2026-01-15T10:00:00Z Resource Scan: arn:aws:cloudformation:...:resourceScan/abc123 Template: anihpj-staging-import Status: COMPLETE Resources: 12 Created: 2026-01-14T14:30:00Z Resource Scan: arn:aws:cloudformation:...:resourceScan/def456 Template: anihpj-legacy-migration Status: COMPLETE Resources: 23 Created: 2026-01-10T09:15:00Z Resource Scan: arn:aws:cloudformation:...:resourceScan/ghi789
Example 4: Find Latest anihpj Template
#!/bin/bash
# Get most recent anihpj generated template
LATEST=$(aws cloudformation list-generated-templates \
  --query 'Summaries | sort_by(@, &CreationTime) | [-1]' \
  --output json)

echo "Latest anihpj Generated Template:"
echo "$LATEST" | jq '{
  Name: .GeneratedTemplateName,
  Status: .Status,
  Resources: .NumberOfResources,
  Created: .CreationTime
}'

# Download if complete
STATUS=$(echo "$LATEST" | jq -r '.Status')
NAME=$(echo "$LATEST" | jq -r '.GeneratedTemplateName')

if [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETE" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "Downloading template..."
  aws cloudformation get-generated-template \
    --generated-template-name $NAME \
    --format YAML > ${NAME}.yaml
  echo "Saved to ${NAME}.yaml"
fi
Latest anihpj Generated Template: { "Name": "anihpj-dev-snapshot", "Status": "IN_PROGRESS", "Resources": 8, "Created": "2026-01-17T11:00:00Z" } Template still in progress - check back later
Example 5: Export anihpj Template Inventory
#!/bin/bash
# Create inventory of all anihpj generated templates
REPORT_FILE="anihpj-templates-inventory.csv"

echo "GeneratedTemplateName,Status,ResourceCount,CreationTime" > $REPORT_FILE

aws cloudformation list-generated-templates \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[GeneratedTemplateName,Status,NumberOfResources,CreationTime]' \
  --output text | while read NAME STATUS COUNT TIME; do
  echo "$NAME,$STATUS,$COUNT,$TIME" >> $REPORT_FILE
done

echo "anihpj Template Inventory"
echo "========================="
cat $REPORT_FILE | column -t -s ','

echo ""
echo "Summary:"
echo "  Total: $(tail -n +2 $REPORT_FILE | wc -l) templates"
echo "  Complete: $(grep -c COMPLETE $REPORT_FILE) templates"
echo "  In Progress: $(grep -c IN_PROGRESS $REPORT_FILE) templates"
anihpj Template Inventory ========================= GeneratedTemplateName Status ResourceCount CreationTime anihpj-production-import COMPLETE 15 2026-01-15T10:00:00Z anihpj-staging-import COMPLETE 12 2026-01-14T14:30:00Z anihpj-legacy-migration COMPLETE 23 2026-01-10T09:15:00Z anihpj-dev-snapshot IN_PROGRESS 8 2026-01-17T11:00:00Z Summary: Total: 4 templates Complete: 3 templates In Progress: 1 templates

list-hook-results

Lists all hook results for a stack operation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-hook-results \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --target-type STACK
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name - Name or ID of the stack
  • --target-type - STACK or RESOURCE
  • --target-id - (Optional) Target resource ID if target-type is RESOURCE
Return Value

Returns HookResults array with HookType, InvocationPoint, Status, and StatusMessage.

5 Detailed CloudFormation list-hook-results Examples
Example 1: List anihpj Stack Hook Results
#!/bin/bash
# List all hook results for anihpj stack
aws cloudformation list-hook-results \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-app \
  --target-type STACK \
  --query 'HookResults[*].[HookType,InvocationPoint,Status,HookStatusReason]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListHookResults | +--------------------------------+---------------+---------+-------------------+ | AWS::SecurityHub::Compliance | PRE_PROVISION | SUCCESS | Requirements met | | Custom::CostValidator | PRE_PROVISION | WARNING | Budget exceeded | | Custom::TagValidator | PRE_PROVISION | SUCCESS | Tags valid | | Custom::SecurityValidator | PRE_PROVISION | FAILED | Open port 22 | +--------------------------------+---------------+---------+-------------------+ anihpj stack hooks evaluated
Example 2: Filter Failed Hooks for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Find failed hooks that blocked anihpj deployment
aws cloudformation list-hook-results \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-production \
  --target-type STACK \
  --query 'HookResults[?Status==`FAILED`]' \
  --output json | jq -r '.[] | "
Hook: \(.HookType)
Invocation Point: \(.InvocationPoint)
Failure Mode: \(.FailureMode)
Reason: \(.HookStatusReason)
Target: \(.TargetDetails.ResourceTargetDetails[].LogicalResourceId // "Stack level")
"'

echo "Resolve these failures before deploying anihpj"
Hook: Custom::SecurityValidator Invocation Point: PRE_PROVISION Failure Mode: FAIL Reason: Security group allows 0.0.0.0/0 on port 22 Target: AnihpjSecurityGroup Hook: Custom::EncryptionValidator Invocation Point: PRE_PROVISION Failure Mode: FAIL Reason: RDS instance not encrypted Target: AnihpjDatabase Resolve these failures before deploying anihpj
Example 3: Get Hook Results by Invocation Point
#!/bin/bash
# Separate pre and post provision hooks for anihpj
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"

echo "anihpj PRE_PROVISION Hooks:"
echo "==========================="
aws cloudformation list-hook-results \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --target-type STACK \
  --query 'HookResults[?InvocationPoint==`PRE_PROVISION`].[HookType,Status]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "anihpj POST_PROVISION Hooks:"
echo "============================"
aws cloudformation list-hook-results \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --target-type STACK \
  --query 'HookResults[?InvocationPoint==`POST_PROVISION`].[HookType,Status]' \
  --output table
anihpj PRE_PROVISION Hooks: =========================== ----------------------------------------------------- | AWS::SecurityHub::Compliance | SUCCESS | | Custom::CostValidator | WARNING | | Custom::TagValidator | SUCCESS | ----------------------------------------------------- anihpj POST_PROVISION Hooks: ============================ ----------------------------------------------------- | Custom::NotificationHook | SUCCESS | | Custom::MetricsHook | SUCCESS | ----------------------------------------------------- All post-provision hooks successful
Example 4: Count anihpj Hook Results by Status
#!/bin/bash
# Generate hook status summary for anihpj
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"

echo "anihpj Hook Results Summary"
echo "============================"

RESULTS=$(aws cloudformation list-hook-results \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --target-type STACK \
  --output json)

echo "Total Hooks: $(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '.HookResults | length')"
echo "Successful: $(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.HookResults[] | select(.Status == "SUCCESS")] | length')"
echo "Warnings: $(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.HookResults[] | select(.Status == "WARNING")] | length')"
echo "Failed: $(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.HookResults[] | select(.Status == "FAILED")] | length')"

echo ""
echo "Hook Types Used:"
echo "$RESULTS" | jq -r '.HookResults[].HookType' | sort -u | sed 's/^/  - /'
anihpj Hook Results Summary ============================ Total Hooks: 6 Successful: 4 Warnings: 1 Failed: 1 Hook Types Used: - AWS::SecurityHub::Compliance - Custom::CostValidator - Custom::MetricsHook - Custom::NotificationHook - Custom::SecurityValidator - Custom::TagValidator
Example 5: Export anihpj Hook Results Report
#!/bin/bash
# Create detailed hook report for anihpj audit
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-app"
REPORT_FILE="anihpj-hook-report-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

echo "Generating anihpj Hook Audit Report..."

aws cloudformation list-hook-results \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --target-type STACK \
  --output json > $REPORT_FILE

# Generate readable summary
cat > anihpj-hook-summary.txt << EOF
anihpj CloudFormation Hook Audit Report
Stack: $STACK_NAME
Generated: $(date)
========================================

$(jq -r '.HookResults[] | "
Hook: \(.HookType)
  Invocation: \(.InvocationPoint)
  Status: \(.Status)
  Reason: \(.HookStatusReason // "N/A")
  Failure Mode: \(.FailureMode // "N/A")
"' $REPORT_FILE)

Summary:
  Total: $(jq '.HookResults | length' $REPORT_FILE) hooks
  Passed: $(jq '[.HookResults[] | select(.Status != "FAILED")] | length' $REPORT_FILE)
  Failed: $(jq '[.HookResults[] | select(.Status == "FAILED")] | length' $REPORT_FILE)
EOF

cat anihpj-hook-summary.txt
Generating anihpj Hook Audit Report... anihpj CloudFormation Hook Audit Report Stack: anihpj-django-app Generated: Fri Jan 17 11:30:00 2026 ======================================== Hook: AWS::SecurityHub::Compliance Invocation: PRE_PROVISION Status: SUCCESS Reason: All security requirements met Failure Mode: FAIL Hook: Custom::CostValidator Invocation: PRE_PROVISION Status: WARNING Reason: Estimated cost $1,245/month exceeds $1,000 threshold Failure Mode: WARN Summary: Total: 6 hooks Passed: 5 Failed: 1

list-resource-scan-related-resources

Lists resources related to a specific resource discovered in a scan.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-related-resources \
  --resource-scan-id {scan-id} \
  --resources ResourceIdentifier={identifier},ResourceType={type}
Parameters Description
  • --resource-scan-id - The ID of the resource scan
  • --resources - Resources to find related resources for
  • --next-token - (Optional) Token for pagination
Return Value

Returns RelatedResources array with ResourceIdentifier, ResourceType, and ManagedByStack flag.

5 Detailed CloudFormation list-resource-scan-related-resources Examples
Example 1: Find Resources Related to anihpj VPC
#!/bin/bash
# Find all resources related to anihpj VPC
SCAN_ID="$1"

aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-related-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --resources ResourceIdentifier=vpc-abc123,ResourceType=AWS::EC2::VPC \
  --query 'RelatedResources[*].[ResourceType,ResourceIdentifier]' \
  --output table

echo "Resources related to anihpj VPC found"
------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ListResourceScanRelatedResources | +----------------------------+-----------------------------------------+ | AWS::EC2::Subnet | subnet-def456 | | AWS::EC2::Subnet | subnet-ghi789 | | AWS::EC2::RouteTable | rtb-jkl012 | | AWS::EC2::InternetGateway | igw-mno345 | | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | sg-pqr678 | | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | sg-stu901 | +----------------------------+-----------------------------------------+ Resources related to anihpj VPC found 6 dependent resources identified
Example 2: Map anihpj Database Dependencies
#!/bin/bash
# Find all resources related to anihpj RDS instance
SCAN_ID="$1"
DB_IDENTIFIER="anihpj-db"

echo "anihpj Database Related Resources"
echo "=================================="

aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-related-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --resources ResourceIdentifier=$DB_IDENTIFIER,ResourceType=AWS::RDS::DBInstance \
  --output json | jq -r '.RelatedResources[] | "\(.ResourceType): \(.ResourceIdentifier)"'

echo ""
echo "These resources should be included in the same template"
anihpj Database Related Resources ================================== AWS::RDS::DBSubnetGroup: anihpj-db-subnet-group AWS::RDS::DBParameterGroup: anihpj-db-params AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup: sg-db-access-123 AWS::SecretsManager::Secret: anihpj/db/credentials AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm: anihpj-db-cpu-alarm AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm: anihpj-db-storage-alarm These resources should be included in the same template 6 related resources found
Example 3: Build anihpj Resource Dependency Graph
#!/bin/bash
# Create dependency graph for anihpj resources
SCAN_ID="$1"

echo "Building anihpj Resource Dependency Graph"
echo "=========================================="

# Start with VPC and recursively find related resources
RESOURCES=("vpc-abc123:AWS::EC2::VPC")

for RESOURCE in "${RESOURCES[@]}"; do
  ID=$(echo $RESOURCE | cut -d: -f1)
  TYPE=$(echo $RESOURCE | cut -d: -f2-)
  
  echo ""
  echo "$TYPE: $ID"
  
  aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-related-resources \
    --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
    --resources ResourceIdentifier=$ID,ResourceType=$TYPE \
    --query 'RelatedResources[*].[ResourceType,ResourceIdentifier]' \
    --output text | sed 's/^/  └── /'
done
Building anihpj Resource Dependency Graph ========================================== AWS::EC2::VPC: vpc-abc123 └── AWS::EC2::Subnet subnet-pub-1 └── AWS::EC2::Subnet subnet-pub-2 └── AWS::EC2::Subnet subnet-priv-1 └── AWS::EC2::Subnet subnet-priv-2 └── AWS::EC2::InternetGateway igw-anihpj └── AWS::EC2::RouteTable rtb-main └── AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup sg-web └── AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup sg-db anihpj VPC has 8 related resources
Example 4: Find anihpj EC2 Instance Dependencies
#!/bin/bash
# Map all resources related to anihpj web server
SCAN_ID="$1"
INSTANCE_ID="i-0abc123def456789"

echo "anihpj Web Server Dependencies"
echo "==============================="

RELATED=$(aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-related-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --resources ResourceIdentifier=$INSTANCE_ID,ResourceType=AWS::EC2::Instance \
  --output json)

echo "$RELATED" | jq -r '.RelatedResources | group_by(.ResourceType) | .[] | "
\(.[0].ResourceType):
\(map("  - \(.ResourceIdentifier)") | join("\n"))
"'

echo ""
echo "Total related resources: $(echo "$RELATED" | jq '.RelatedResources | length')"
anihpj Web Server Dependencies =============================== AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup: - sg-web-access - sg-ssh-admin AWS::EC2::Subnet: - subnet-pub-1 AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile: - anihpj-web-profile AWS::EC2::KeyPair: - anihpj-key AWS::EBS::Volume: - vol-data-123 Total related resources: 6
Example 5: Export anihpj Resource Relationships
#!/bin/bash
# Export all resource relationships for anihpj
SCAN_ID="$1"
OUTPUT_FILE="anihpj-resource-relationships.json"

echo "Exporting anihpj Resource Relationships..."

# Get all scanned resources
RESOURCES=$(aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --query 'Resources[*].[ResourceIdentifier,ResourceType]' \
  --output text)

echo "[" > $OUTPUT_FILE

FIRST=true
echo "$RESOURCES" | while read ID TYPE; do
  RELATED=$(aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-related-resources \
    --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
    --resources ResourceIdentifier=$ID,ResourceType=$TYPE \
    --output json 2>/dev/null)
  
  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    if [ "$FIRST" = false ]; then
      echo "," >> $OUTPUT_FILE
    fi
    FIRST=false
    
    echo "{\"Resource\": {\"Id\": \"$ID\", \"Type\": \"$TYPE\"}, \"Related\": $(echo "$RELATED" | jq '.RelatedResources')}" >> $OUTPUT_FILE
  fi
done

echo "]" >> $OUTPUT_FILE

echo "Relationship data saved to $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo "Total resources mapped: $(jq 'length' $OUTPUT_FILE)"
Exporting anihpj Resource Relationships... Relationship data saved to anihpj-resource-relationships.json Total resources mapped: 45 anihpj infrastructure dependency map complete Use this data to plan CloudFormation template structure

list-resource-scan-resources

Lists all resources discovered in a resource scan.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
  --resource-scan-id {scan-id}
Parameters Description
  • --resource-scan-id - The ID of the resource scan
  • --resource-identifier - (Optional) Filter by resource ID
  • --resource-type-prefix - (Optional) Filter by resource type prefix
Return Value

Returns Resources array with ResourceIdentifier, ResourceType, and ManagedByStack flag.

5 Detailed CloudFormation list-resource-scan-resources Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Scanned Resources
#!/bin/bash
# List all resources from anihpj account scan
SCAN_ID="$1"

aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --query 'Resources[*].[ResourceType,ResourceIdentifier,ManagedByStack]' \
  --output table | head -30

echo "..."
echo "Use --max-results to limit output"
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListResourceScanResources | +---------------------------+------------------------+-------------------------+ | AWS::EC2::VPC | vpc-abc123 | False | | AWS::EC2::Subnet | subnet-def456 | False | | AWS::EC2::Instance | i-0abc123def | True | | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | anihpj-db | True | | AWS::S3::Bucket | anihpj-uploads | False | | AWS::Lambda::Function | anihpj-processor | True | | AWS::DynamoDB::Table | anihpj-sessions | False | +---------------------------+------------------------+-------------------------+ ... Use --max-results to limit output
Example 2: Find Unmanaged anihpj Resources
#!/bin/bash
# Find resources not managed by CloudFormation
SCAN_ID="$1"

echo "anihpj Unmanaged Resources"
echo "=========================="
echo "These resources exist but are not in any CloudFormation stack:"
echo ""

aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --query 'Resources[?ManagedByStack==`false`].[ResourceType,ResourceIdentifier]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Consider importing these into CloudFormation for better management"
anihpj Unmanaged Resources ========================== These resources exist but are not in any CloudFormation stack: -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | AWS::EC2::VPC | vpc-legacy-manual | | AWS::S3::Bucket | anihpj-uploads-prod | | AWS::S3::Bucket | anihpj-backups | | AWS::DynamoDB::Table | anihpj-sessions | | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | sg-manual-created | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Consider importing these into CloudFormation for better management 5 unmanaged resources found
Example 3: Filter anihpj Resources by Type
#!/bin/bash
# Get all RDS resources for anihpj
SCAN_ID="$1"

echo "anihpj RDS Resources:"
aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --resource-type-prefix AWS::RDS \
  --query 'Resources[*].[ResourceType,ResourceIdentifier,ManagedByStack]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "anihpj ElastiCache Resources:"
aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --resource-type-prefix AWS::ElastiCache \
  --query 'Resources[*].[ResourceType,ResourceIdentifier,ManagedByStack]' \
  --output table
anihpj RDS Resources: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | anihpj-db | True | | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | anihpj-db-replica | True | | AWS::RDS::DBSubnetGroup | anihpj-db-subnets | True | | AWS::RDS::DBParameterGroup | anihpj-pg14-params | False | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ anihpj ElastiCache Resources: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster | anihpj-redis | True | | AWS::ElastiCache::SubnetGroup | anihpj-cache-sub | True | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ Database infrastructure mostly managed by CloudFormation
Example 4: Count anihpj Resources by Type
#!/bin/bash
# Generate resource type summary for anihpj
SCAN_ID="$1"

echo "anihpj Resource Type Summary"
echo "============================="

aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --query 'Resources[*].ResourceType' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -15

echo ""
echo "Top 15 resource types in anihpj account"
anihpj Resource Type Summary ============================= 45 AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup 23 AWS::EC2::Instance 18 AWS::S3::Bucket 15 AWS::Lambda::Function 12 AWS::IAM::Role 10 AWS::DynamoDB::Table 8 AWS::EC2::Subnet 7 AWS::RDS::DBInstance 6 AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster 5 AWS::EC2::VPC 4 AWS::ECS::Service 4 AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm 3 AWS::SNS::Topic 3 AWS::SQS::Queue 2 AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::LoadBalancer Top 15 resource types in anihpj account
Example 5: Export anihpj Resource Inventory
#!/bin/bash
# Export complete resource inventory for anihpj
SCAN_ID="$1"
OUTPUT_FILE="anihpj-resource-inventory-$(date +%Y%m%d).csv"

echo "ResourceType,ResourceIdentifier,ManagedByStack" > $OUTPUT_FILE

aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --query 'Resources[*].[ResourceType,ResourceIdentifier,ManagedByStack]' \
  --output text | while read TYPE ID MANAGED; do
  echo "$TYPE,$ID,$MANAGED" >> $OUTPUT_FILE
done

echo "anihpj Resource Inventory Exported"
echo "==================================="
echo "File: $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo "Total Resources: $(tail -n +2 $OUTPUT_FILE | wc -l)"
echo "Managed by CFN: $(grep -c True $OUTPUT_FILE)"
echo "Unmanaged: $(grep -c False $OUTPUT_FILE)"
anihpj Resource Inventory Exported =================================== File: anihpj-resource-inventory-20260117.csv Total Resources: 187 Managed by CFN: 142 Unmanaged: 45 CSV file ready for analysis and import planning

list-resource-scans

Lists all resource scans in your account.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-resource-scans
Parameters Description
  • --next-token - (Optional) Token for pagination
Return Value

Returns ResourceScanSummaries array with ResourceScanId, Status, PercentageCompleted, StartTime, and EndTime.

5 Detailed CloudFormation list-resource-scans Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Resource Scans
#!/bin/bash
# List all resource scans in account
aws cloudformation list-resource-scans \
  --query 'ResourceScanSummaries[*].[ResourceScanId,Status,PercentageCompleted,StartTime]' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj resource scans listed"
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListResourceScans | +------------------------------------------------+----------+-----+------------+ | arn:aws:cloudformation:...:resourceScan/abc123 | COMPLETE | 100 | 2026-01-17 | | arn:aws:cloudformation:...:resourceScan/def456 | COMPLETE | 100 | 2026-01-15 | | arn:aws:cloudformation:...:resourceScan/ghi789 | COMPLETE | 100 | 2026-01-10 | | arn:aws:cloudformation:...:resourceScan/jkl012 | IN_PROG | 45 | 2026-01-17 | +------------------------------------------------+----------+-----+------------+ anihpj resource scans listed
Example 2: Get Latest anihpj Scan
#!/bin/bash
# Get the most recent completed scan
LATEST_SCAN=$(aws cloudformation list-resource-scans \
  --query 'ResourceScanSummaries[?Status==`COMPLETE`] | sort_by(@, &EndTime) | [-1]' \
  --output json)

echo "Latest anihpj Resource Scan:"
echo "$LATEST_SCAN" | jq '{
  ScanId: .ResourceScanId,
  Status: .Status,
  StartTime: .StartTime,
  EndTime: .EndTime,
  ResourcesScanned: .ResourcesScanned
}'

# Extract scan ID for further use
SCAN_ID=$(echo "$LATEST_SCAN" | jq -r '.ResourceScanId')
echo ""
echo "Use this scan ID: $SCAN_ID"
Latest anihpj Resource Scan: { "ScanId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:resourceScan/abc123", "Status": "COMPLETE", "StartTime": "2026-01-17T10:00:00Z", "EndTime": "2026-01-17T10:15:00Z", "ResourcesScanned": 187 } Use this scan ID: arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:resourceScan/abc123
Example 3: Check for In-Progress anihpj Scans
#!/bin/bash
# Check if there are any scans currently running
IN_PROGRESS=$(aws cloudformation list-resource-scans \
  --query 'ResourceScanSummaries[?Status==`IN_PROGRESS`]' \
  --output json)

COUNT=$(echo "$IN_PROGRESS" | jq 'length')

if [ "$COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "anihpj Scans In Progress: $COUNT"
  echo ""
  echo "$IN_PROGRESS" | jq -r '.[] | "Scan: \(.ResourceScanId | split("/") | .[-1])
  Progress: \(.PercentageCompleted)%
  Started: \(.StartTime)
"'
else
  echo "No scans currently in progress"
  echo ""
  echo "Start a new scan with:"
  echo "  aws cloudformation start-resource-scan"
fi
anihpj Scans In Progress: 1 Scan: jkl012 Progress: 45% Started: 2026-01-17T11:30:00Z Wait for completion before using scan results
Example 4: Compare anihpj Scan Results Over Time
#!/bin/bash
# Compare resource counts across scans
echo "anihpj Resource Scan History"
echo "============================="

aws cloudformation list-resource-scans \
  --query 'ResourceScanSummaries[?Status==`COMPLETE`] | sort_by(@, &StartTime)' \
  --output json | jq -r '.[] | "\(.StartTime | split("T")[0]): \(.ResourcesScanned) resources"'

echo ""
echo "Resource Growth Analysis:"
aws cloudformation list-resource-scans \
  --query 'ResourceScanSummaries[?Status==`COMPLETE`] | sort_by(@, &StartTime) | [0, -1].[ResourcesScanned]' \
  --output text | xargs -n2 bash -c 'echo "First scan: $0 resources"; echo "Latest scan: $1 resources"; echo "Growth: $(($1 - $0)) resources"'
anihpj Resource Scan History ============================= 2026-01-10: 165 resources 2026-01-15: 178 resources 2026-01-17: 187 resources Resource Growth Analysis: First scan: 165 resources Latest scan: 187 resources Growth: 22 resources anihpj infrastructure growing steadily
Example 5: Export anihpj Scan History Report
#!/bin/bash
# Generate comprehensive scan history report
REPORT_FILE="anihpj-scan-history-report.txt"

echo "anihpj Resource Scan History Report" > $REPORT_FILE
echo "Generated: $(date)" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "=======================================" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "" >> $REPORT_FILE

aws cloudformation list-resource-scans \
  --output json | jq -r '.ResourceScanSummaries[] | "
Scan ID: \(.ResourceScanId | split("/") | .[-1])
Status: \(.Status)
Progress: \(.PercentageCompleted)%
Resources: \(.ResourcesScanned // "N/A")
Started: \(.StartTime)
Completed: \(.EndTime // "In Progress")
"' >> $REPORT_FILE

echo "" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "Summary:" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "  Total Scans: $(aws cloudformation list-resource-scans --query 'ResourceScanSummaries | length' --output text)" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "  Completed: $(aws cloudformation list-resource-scans --query 'ResourceScanSummaries[?Status==`COMPLETE`] | length' --output text)" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "  In Progress: $(aws cloudformation list-resource-scans --query 'ResourceScanSummaries[?Status==`IN_PROGRESS`] | length' --output text)" >> $REPORT_FILE

cat $REPORT_FILE
anihpj Resource Scan History Report Generated: Fri Jan 17 11:45:00 2026 ======================================= Scan ID: abc123 Status: COMPLETE Progress: 100% Resources: 187 Started: 2026-01-17T10:00:00Z Completed: 2026-01-17T10:15:00Z Scan ID: def456 Status: COMPLETE Progress: 100% Resources: 178 Started: 2026-01-15T14:00:00Z Completed: 2026-01-15T14:12:00Z Summary: Total Scans: 4 Completed: 3 In Progress: 1

list-stack-instance-resource-drifts

Returns drift information for resources in a stack instance.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-stack-instance-resource-drifts \
  --stack-set-name {stackset-name} \
  --stack-instance-account {account-id} \
  --stack-instance-region {region} \
  --operation-id {operation-id}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name - Name of the stack set
  • --stack-instance-account - Account ID for the instance
  • --stack-instance-region - Region of the instance
  • --operation-id - The drift detection operation ID
Return Value

Returns Summaries array with LogicalResourceId, ResourceType, and StackResourceDriftStatus.

5 Detailed CloudFormation list-stack-instance-resource-drifts Examples
Example 1: List anihpj Instance Resource Drifts
#!/bin/bash
# Get drift details for specific anihpj stack instance
aws cloudformation list-stack-instance-resource-drifts \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --stack-instance-account 123456789012 \
  --stack-instance-region us-east-1 \
  --operation-id operation-drift-abc123 \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[LogicalResourceId,ResourceType,StackResourceDriftStatus]' \
  --output table
------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ListStackInstanceResourceDrifts | +----------------------+---------------------------+--------------------+ | AnihpjDatabase | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | MODIFIED | | AnihpjSecurityGroup | AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup | MODIFIED | | AnihpjLoadBalancer | AWS::ELB::LoadBalancer | IN_SYNC | | AnihpjCacheCluster | AWS::ElastiCache::Cache.. | IN_SYNC | +----------------------+---------------------------+--------------------+ anihpj instance drift analysis complete
Example 2: Find Modified anihpj Resources
#!/bin/bash
# Filter only drifted resources in anihpj instance
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
ACCOUNT="123456789012"
REGION="us-east-1"
OPERATION_ID="operation-drift-abc123"

echo "anihpj Drifted Resources in $ACCOUNT/$REGION"
echo "=============================================="

aws cloudformation list-stack-instance-resource-drifts \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
  --stack-instance-account $ACCOUNT \
  --stack-instance-region $REGION \
  --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
  --stack-instance-resource-drift-statuses MODIFIED DELETED \
  --output json | jq -r '.Summaries[] | "
Resource: \(.LogicalResourceId)
Type: \(.ResourceType)
Status: \(.StackResourceDriftStatus)
Physical ID: \(.PhysicalResourceId)
"'
anihpj Drifted Resources in 123456789012/us-east-1 ============================================== Resource: AnihpjDatabase Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance Status: MODIFIED Physical ID: anihpj-db Resource: AnihpjSecurityGroup Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup Status: MODIFIED Physical ID: sg-abc123 2 resources have drifted from template
Example 3: Get Property-Level Drift Details for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed property differences
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
ACCOUNT="123456789012"
REGION="us-west-2"
OPERATION_ID="$1"

aws cloudformation list-stack-instance-resource-drifts \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
  --stack-instance-account $ACCOUNT \
  --stack-instance-region $REGION \
  --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
  --output json | jq -r '.Summaries[] | select(.StackResourceDriftStatus == "MODIFIED") | "
Resource: \(.LogicalResourceId)
Property Changes:
\(.PropertyDifferences | map("  \(.PropertyPath): \(.ExpectedValue) -> \(.ActualValue)") | join("\n"))
"'
Resource: AnihpjDatabase Property Changes: /BackupRetentionPeriod: 7 -> 14 /MultiAZ: false -> true Resource: AnihpjSecurityGroup Property Changes: /IpPermissions/0/IpRanges: ["10.0.0.0/8"] -> ["0.0.0.0/0", "10.0.0.0/8"] Manual changes detected - update template or revert changes
Example 4: Compare Drift Across anihpj Accounts
#!/bin/bash
# Compare drift status across multiple accounts
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
OPERATION_ID="$1"
ACCOUNTS=("123456789012" "987654321098" "111222333444")

echo "anihpj Multi-Account Drift Comparison"
echo "======================================"

for ACCOUNT in "${ACCOUNTS[@]}"; do
  for REGION in "us-east-1" "us-west-2"; do
    DRIFTED=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-instance-resource-drifts \
      --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
      --stack-instance-account $ACCOUNT \
      --stack-instance-region $REGION \
      --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
      --stack-instance-resource-drift-statuses MODIFIED \
      --query 'length(Summaries)' \
      --output text 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
      echo "$ACCOUNT/$REGION: $DRIFTED drifted resources"
    fi
  done
done
anihpj Multi-Account Drift Comparison ====================================== 123456789012/us-east-1: 2 drifted resources 123456789012/us-west-2: 0 drifted resources 987654321098/us-east-1: 3 drifted resources 987654321098/us-west-2: 1 drifted resources 111222333444/us-east-1: 0 drifted resources 111222333444/us-west-2: 0 drifted resources Account 987654321098 has most drift - investigate first
Example 5: Export anihpj Instance Drift Report
#!/bin/bash
# Generate detailed drift report for anihpj instance
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
ACCOUNT="$1"
REGION="$2"
OPERATION_ID="$3"
REPORT_FILE="anihpj-drift-${ACCOUNT}-${REGION}.json"

echo "Generating anihpj Instance Drift Report..."

aws cloudformation list-stack-instance-resource-drifts \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
  --stack-instance-account $ACCOUNT \
  --stack-instance-region $REGION \
  --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
  --output json > $REPORT_FILE

# Summary
echo ""
echo "anihpj Drift Report: $ACCOUNT/$REGION"
echo "======================================="
echo "Total Resources: $(jq '.Summaries | length' $REPORT_FILE)"
echo "In Sync: $(jq '[.Summaries[] | select(.StackResourceDriftStatus == "IN_SYNC")] | length' $REPORT_FILE)"
echo "Modified: $(jq '[.Summaries[] | select(.StackResourceDriftStatus == "MODIFIED")] | length' $REPORT_FILE)"
echo "Deleted: $(jq '[.Summaries[] | select(.StackResourceDriftStatus == "DELETED")] | length' $REPORT_FILE)"
echo ""
echo "Full report saved to $REPORT_FILE"
