Generating anihpj Instance Drift Report... anihpj Drift Report: 123456789012/us-east-1 ======================================= Total Resources: 15 In Sync: 12 Modified: 2 Deleted: 1 Full report saved to anihpj-drift-123456789012-us-east-1.json

list-stack-refactor-actions

Lists all actions planned in a stack refactor operation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-stack-refactor-actions \
  --stack-refactor-id {refactor-id}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-refactor-id - The ID of the refactor operation
  • --next-token - (Optional) Token for pagination
Return Value

Returns StackRefactorActions array with Action, ResourceIdentifier, SourceStackId, and DestinationStackId.

5 Detailed CloudFormation list-stack-refactor-actions Examples
Example 1: List anihpj Refactor Actions
#!/bin/bash
# View all planned actions for anihpj stack refactor
REFACTOR_ID="$1"

aws cloudformation list-stack-refactor-actions \
  --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
  --query 'StackRefactorActions[*].[Action,ResourceIdentifier,SourceStackId,DestinationStackId]' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj refactor actions listed"
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListStackRefactorActions | +--------+---------------------+-------------------------+---------------------+ | MOVE | AnihpjDatabase | anihpj-monolith | anihpj-database | | MOVE | AnihpjDBSubnetGroup | anihpj-monolith | anihpj-database | | MOVE | AnihpjCacheCluster | anihpj-monolith | anihpj-cache | | KEEP | AnihpjWebServer | anihpj-monolith | anihpj-monolith | | KEEP | AnihpjLoadBalancer | anihpj-monolith | anihpj-monolith | +--------+---------------------+-------------------------+---------------------+ anihpj refactor actions listed
Example 2: Filter anihpj Move Actions
#!/bin/bash
# Get only MOVE actions for anihpj refactor
REFACTOR_ID="$1"

echo "anihpj Resources Being Moved"
echo "============================="

aws cloudformation list-stack-refactor-actions \
  --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
  --query 'StackRefactorActions[?Action==`MOVE`]' \
  --output json | jq -r '.[] | "
Resource: \(.ResourceIdentifier)
From: \(.SourceStackId | split("/") | .[1])
To: \(.DestinationStackId | split("/") | .[1])
Status: \(.Status)
"'
anihpj Resources Being Moved ============================= Resource: AnihpjDatabase From: anihpj-monolith To: anihpj-database Status: PENDING Resource: AnihpjDBSubnetGroup From: anihpj-monolith To: anihpj-database Status: PENDING Resource: AnihpjCacheCluster From: anihpj-monolith To: anihpj-cache Status: PENDING 3 resources scheduled for migration
Example 3: Validate anihpj Refactor Actions
#!/bin/bash
# Check for any issues with planned refactor actions
REFACTOR_ID="$1"

echo "anihpj Refactor Action Validation"
echo "=================================="

# Get all actions
ACTIONS=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-refactor-actions \
  --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
  --output json)

# Check for failed actions
FAILED=$(echo "$ACTIONS" | jq '[.StackRefactorActions[] | select(.Status == "FAILED")] | length')

if [ "$FAILED" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "WARNING: $FAILED actions have failed"
  echo ""
  echo "Failed Actions:"
  echo "$ACTIONS" | jq -r '.StackRefactorActions[] | select(.Status == "FAILED") | "  \(.ResourceIdentifier): \(.StatusReason)"'
else
  echo "All actions validated successfully"
  echo "Total actions: $(echo "$ACTIONS" | jq '.StackRefactorActions | length')"
  echo "Move: $(echo "$ACTIONS" | jq '[.StackRefactorActions[] | select(.Action == "MOVE")] | length')"
  echo "Keep: $(echo "$ACTIONS" | jq '[.StackRefactorActions[] | select(.Action == "KEEP")] | length')"
fi
anihpj Refactor Action Validation ================================== All actions validated successfully Total actions: 8 Move: 5 Keep: 3 anihpj refactor ready for execution
Example 4: Track anihpj Refactor Progress
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor action progress during refactor
REFACTOR_ID="$1"

echo "anihpj Refactor Action Progress"
echo "================================"

while true; do
  ACTIONS=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-refactor-actions \
    --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
    --output json)
  
  PENDING=$(echo "$ACTIONS" | jq '[.StackRefactorActions[] | select(.Status == "PENDING")] | length')
  IN_PROGRESS=$(echo "$ACTIONS" | jq '[.StackRefactorActions[] | select(.Status == "IN_PROGRESS")] | length')
  COMPLETE=$(echo "$ACTIONS" | jq '[.StackRefactorActions[] | select(.Status == "COMPLETE")] | length')
  FAILED=$(echo "$ACTIONS" | jq '[.StackRefactorActions[] | select(.Status == "FAILED")] | length')
  TOTAL=$(echo "$ACTIONS" | jq '.StackRefactorActions | length')
  
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) - Pending: $PENDING | In Progress: $IN_PROGRESS | Complete: $COMPLETE | Failed: $FAILED"
  
  if [ "$PENDING" -eq 0 ] && [ "$IN_PROGRESS" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "All actions completed"
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 5
done
anihpj Refactor Action Progress ================================ 12:00:00 - Pending: 5 | In Progress: 0 | Complete: 0 | Failed: 0 12:00:05 - Pending: 3 | In Progress: 2 | Complete: 0 | Failed: 0 12:00:10 - Pending: 1 | In Progress: 2 | Complete: 2 | Failed: 0 12:00:15 - Pending: 0 | In Progress: 1 | Complete: 4 | Failed: 0 12:00:20 - Pending: 0 | In Progress: 0 | Complete: 5 | Failed: 0 All actions completed anihpj stack refactor successful
Example 5: Export anihpj Refactor Plan
#!/bin/bash
# Create detailed refactor plan document
REFACTOR_ID="$1"
PLAN_FILE="anihpj-refactor-plan-$(date +%Y%m%d).txt"

echo "anihpj Stack Refactor Plan" > $PLAN_FILE
echo "Generated: $(date)" >> $PLAN_FILE
echo "Refactor ID: $REFACTOR_ID" >> $PLAN_FILE
echo "==================================" >> $PLAN_FILE
echo "" >> $PLAN_FILE

aws cloudformation list-stack-refactor-actions \
  --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
  --output json | jq -r '
.StackRefactorActions | group_by(.DestinationStackId) | .[] | "
Destination Stack: \(.[0].DestinationStackId | split("/") | .[1])
Resources:
\(map("  - \(.ResourceIdentifier) (\(.Action))") | join("\n"))
"' >> $PLAN_FILE

echo "" >> $PLAN_FILE
echo "Summary:" >> $PLAN_FILE
aws cloudformation list-stack-refactor-actions \
  --stack-refactor-id $REFACTOR_ID \
  --query '{TotalActions:length(StackRefactorActions),MoveActions:length(StackRefactorActions[?Action==`MOVE`]),KeepActions:length(StackRefactorActions[?Action==`KEEP`])}' \
  --output text >> $PLAN_FILE

cat $PLAN_FILE
anihpj Stack Refactor Plan Generated: Fri Jan 17 12:00:00 2026 Refactor ID: refactor-abc123 ================================== Destination Stack: anihpj-database Resources: - AnihpjDatabase (MOVE) - AnihpjDBSubnetGroup (MOVE) - AnihpjDBSecurityGroup (MOVE) Destination Stack: anihpj-cache Resources: - AnihpjCacheCluster (MOVE) - AnihpjCacheSubnetGroup (MOVE) Destination Stack: anihpj-monolith Resources: - AnihpjWebServer (KEEP) - AnihpjLoadBalancer (KEEP) - AnihpjTargetGroup (KEEP) Summary: 8 5 3

list-stack-refactors

Lists all stack refactor operations in your account.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-stack-refactors
Parameters Description
  • --next-token - (Optional) Token for pagination
  • --status-filter - (Optional) Filter by status
Return Value

Returns StackRefactorSummaries array with StackRefactorId, Status, ExecutionStatus, and CreationTime.

5 Detailed CloudFormation list-stack-refactors Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Stack Refactors
#!/bin/bash
# List all refactor operations
aws cloudformation list-stack-refactors \
  --query 'StackRefactorSummaries[*].[StackRefactorId,Status,ExecutionStatus,CreationTime]' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj refactor operations listed"
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListStackRefactors | +-------------------+----------+----------------+------------------------------+ | refactor-abc123 | COMPLETE | SUCCEEDED | 2026-01-17T10:00:00Z | | refactor-def456 | COMPLETE | FAILED | 2026-01-15T14:00:00Z | | refactor-ghi789 | PENDING | NOT_STARTED | 2026-01-17T12:00:00Z | +-------------------+----------+----------------+------------------------------+ anihpj refactor operations listed
Example 2: Filter anihpj Refactors by Status
#!/bin/bash
# Get pending refactors awaiting execution
echo "anihpj Pending Refactors:"
echo "========================="

aws cloudformation list-stack-refactors \
  --query 'StackRefactorSummaries[?Status==`PENDING`]' \
  --output json | jq -r '.[] | "
Refactor ID: \(.StackRefactorId | split("/") | .[-1])
Created: \(.CreationTime)
Execution: \(.ExecutionStatus)
Source Stack: \(.SourceStackId | split("/") | .[1])
"'

echo ""
echo "Execute with: aws cloudformation execute-stack-refactor"
anihpj Pending Refactors: ========================= Refactor ID: refactor-ghi789 Created: 2026-01-17T12:00:00Z Execution: NOT_STARTED Source Stack: anihpj-monolith Execute with: aws cloudformation execute-stack-refactor
Example 3: Get anihpj Refactor History
#!/bin/bash
# View refactor history for anihpj stacks
echo "anihpj Stack Refactor History"
echo "=============================="

aws cloudformation list-stack-refactors \
  --output json | jq -r '.StackRefactorSummaries | sort_by(.CreationTime) | reverse | .[] | "
Date: \(.CreationTime | split("T")[0])
Refactor: \(.StackRefactorId | split("/") | .[-1])
Status: \(.ExecutionStatus)
Description: \(.Description // "No description")
"'
anihpj Stack Refactor History ============================== Date: 2026-01-17 Refactor: refactor-ghi789 Status: NOT_STARTED Description: Split cache layer from monolith Date: 2026-01-17 Refactor: refactor-abc123 Status: SUCCEEDED Description: Database stack separation Date: 2026-01-15 Refactor: refactor-def456 Status: FAILED Description: Attempted service mesh split
Example 4: Find Failed anihpj Refactors
#!/bin/bash
# Investigate failed refactors
echo "anihpj Failed Refactors"
echo "========================"

FAILED=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-refactors \
  --query 'StackRefactorSummaries[?ExecutionStatus==`FAILED`]' \
  --output json)

if [ "$(echo "$FAILED" | jq 'length')" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "$FAILED" | jq -r '.[] | "
Refactor: \(.StackRefactorId | split("/") | .[-1])
Created: \(.CreationTime)
Status Reason: \(.StatusReason // "Check describe-stack-refactor for details")
"'
  
  echo ""
  echo "Investigate with:"
  echo "$FAILED" | jq -r '.[].StackRefactorId' | while read ID; do
    echo "  aws cloudformation describe-stack-refactor --stack-refactor-id $ID"
  done
else
  echo "No failed refactors found"
fi
anihpj Failed Refactors ======================== Refactor: refactor-def456 Created: 2026-01-15T14:00:00Z Status Reason: Resource AnihpjServiceMesh has dependencies that cannot be moved Investigate with: aws cloudformation describe-stack-refactor --stack-refactor-id refactor-def456
Example 5: Generate anihpj Refactor Summary Report
#!/bin/bash
# Create comprehensive refactor summary
REPORT_FILE="anihpj-refactor-summary.txt"

echo "anihpj Stack Refactor Summary Report" > $REPORT_FILE
echo "Generated: $(date)" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "=====================================" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "" >> $REPORT_FILE

# Get all refactors
REFACTORS=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-refactors --output json)

echo "Statistics:" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "  Total Refactors: $(echo "$REFACTORS" | jq '.StackRefactorSummaries | length')" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "  Succeeded: $(echo "$REFACTORS" | jq '[.StackRefactorSummaries[] | select(.ExecutionStatus == "SUCCEEDED")] | length')" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "  Failed: $(echo "$REFACTORS" | jq '[.StackRefactorSummaries[] | select(.ExecutionStatus == "FAILED")] | length')" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "  Pending: $(echo "$REFACTORS" | jq '[.StackRefactorSummaries[] | select(.ExecutionStatus == "NOT_STARTED")] | length')" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "" >> $REPORT_FILE

echo "Recent Refactors:" >> $REPORT_FILE
echo "$REFACTORS" | jq -r '.StackRefactorSummaries | sort_by(.CreationTime) | reverse | .[0:5] | .[] | "  \(.CreationTime | split("T")[0]): \(.StackRefactorId | split("/") | .[-1]) - \(.ExecutionStatus)"' >> $REPORT_FILE

cat $REPORT_FILE
anihpj Stack Refactor Summary Report Generated: Fri Jan 17 12:15:00 2026 ===================================== Statistics: Total Refactors: 3 Succeeded: 1 Failed: 1 Pending: 1 Recent Refactors: 2026-01-17: refactor-ghi789 - NOT_STARTED 2026-01-17: refactor-abc123 - SUCCEEDED 2026-01-15: refactor-def456 - FAILED

list-stack-set-auto-deployment-targets

Returns summary information about deployment targets for a StackSet.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-stack-set-auto-deployment-targets \
  --stack-set-name {stackset-name}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name - Name of the stack set
  • --next-token - (Optional) Token for pagination
Return Value

Returns Summaries array with OrganizationalUnitId and Regions for auto-deployment targets.

5 Detailed CloudFormation list-stack-set-auto-deployment-targets Examples
Example 1: List anihpj Auto-Deployment Targets
#!/bin/bash
# View auto-deployment targets for anihpj StackSet
aws cloudformation list-stack-set-auto-deployment-targets \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[OrganizationalUnitId,Regions]' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj auto-deployment targets listed"
------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ListStackSetAutoDeploymentTargets | +-------------------+--------------------------------------------------+ | ou-root-abc123 | us-east-1, us-west-2, eu-west-1 | | ou-prod-def456 | us-east-1, us-west-2 | | ou-dev-ghi789 | us-east-1 | +-------------------+--------------------------------------------------+ anihpj auto-deployment targets listed
Example 2: Check anihpj Production OU Deployment
#!/bin/bash
# Get deployment details for production OU
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"

echo "anihpj Production Deployment Targets"
echo "====================================="

aws cloudformation list-stack-set-auto-deployment-targets \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
  --output json | jq -r '.Summaries[] | select(.OrganizationalUnitId | contains("prod")) | "
OU: \(.OrganizationalUnitId)
Regions: \(.Regions | join(", "))
Accounts Covered: All accounts in OU
"'

echo "New accounts in this OU will automatically get anihpj stacks"
anihpj Production Deployment Targets ===================================== OU: ou-prod-def456 Regions: us-east-1, us-west-2 Accounts Covered: All accounts in OU New accounts in this OU will automatically get anihpj stacks
Example 3: Compare anihpj StackSet Deployment Coverage
#!/bin/bash
# Compare deployment targets across anihpj StackSets
STACKSETS=("anihpj-django-infrastructure" "anihpj-security-baseline" "anihpj-cicd-pipeline")

echo "anihpj StackSet Deployment Coverage"
echo "====================================="

for STACKSET in "${STACKSETS[@]}"; do
  echo ""
  echo "StackSet: $STACKSET"
  echo "-------------------"
  
  aws cloudformation list-stack-set-auto-deployment-targets \
    --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
    --query 'Summaries[*].[OrganizationalUnitId,Regions]' \
    --output text 2>/dev/null | while read OU REGIONS; do
    echo "  $OU: $REGIONS"
  done
done
anihpj StackSet Deployment Coverage ===================================== StackSet: anihpj-django-infrastructure ------------------- ou-root-abc123: us-east-1, us-west-2, eu-west-1 ou-prod-def456: us-east-1, us-west-2 StackSet: anihpj-security-baseline ------------------- r-root: us-east-1, us-west-2, eu-west-1, ap-southeast-1 StackSet: anihpj-cicd-pipeline ------------------- ou-prod-def456: us-east-1 Security baseline has widest coverage
Example 4: Verify anihpj Global Deployment
#!/bin/bash
# Ensure anihpj is deployed to all required regions
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
REQUIRED_REGIONS=("us-east-1" "us-west-2" "eu-west-1" "ap-southeast-1")

echo "anihpj Deployment Region Verification"
echo "======================================="

TARGETS=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-set-auto-deployment-targets \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
  --output json)

DEPLOYED_REGIONS=$(echo "$TARGETS" | jq -r '.Summaries[].Regions[]' | sort -u)

for REGION in "${REQUIRED_REGIONS[@]}"; do
  if echo "$DEPLOYED_REGIONS" | grep -q "$REGION"; then
    echo "✓ $REGION: Covered"
  else
    echo "✗ $REGION: NOT COVERED"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "Update deployment targets if any regions are missing"
anihpj Deployment Region Verification ======================================= ✓ us-east-1: Covered ✓ us-west-2: Covered ✓ eu-west-1: Covered ✗ ap-southeast-1: NOT COVERED Update deployment targets if any regions are missing Add ap-southeast-1 to ensure global coverage
Example 5: Export anihpj Deployment Target Matrix
#!/bin/bash
# Create deployment target matrix for documentation
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
OUTPUT_FILE="anihpj-deployment-matrix.csv"

echo "OrganizationalUnit,Region" > $OUTPUT_FILE

aws cloudformation list-stack-set-auto-deployment-targets \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
  --output json | jq -r '.Summaries[] | .OrganizationalUnitId as $ou | .Regions[] | "\($ou),\(.)"' >> $OUTPUT_FILE

echo "anihpj Deployment Target Matrix"
echo "================================"
cat $OUTPUT_FILE | column -t -s ','

echo ""
echo "Matrix saved to $OUTPUT_FILE"
anihpj Deployment Target Matrix ================================ OrganizationalUnit Region ou-root-abc123 us-east-1 ou-root-abc123 us-west-2 ou-root-abc123 eu-west-1 ou-prod-def456 us-east-1 ou-prod-def456 us-west-2 ou-dev-ghi789 us-east-1 Matrix saved to anihpj-deployment-matrix.csv

list-stack-set-operation-results

Returns the results of a StackSet operation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operation-results \
  --stack-set-name {stackset-name} \
  --operation-id {operation-id}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name - Name of the stack set
  • --operation-id - The ID of the operation
  • --call-as - (Optional) SELF or DELEGATED_ADMIN
Return Value

Returns Summaries array with Account, Region, Status, and StatusReason for each instance.

5 Detailed CloudFormation list-stack-set-operation-results Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj StackSet Operation Results
#!/bin/bash
# List results for anihpj StackSet operation
aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operation-results \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --operation-id operation-update-abc123 \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[Account,Region,Status,StatusReason]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListStackSetOperationResults | +---------------+------------+-----------+-------------------------------------+ | 123456789012 | us-east-1 | SUCCEEDED | Stack updated successfully | | 123456789012 | us-west-2 | SUCCEEDED | Stack updated successfully | | 987654321098 | us-east-1 | SUCCEEDED | Stack updated successfully | | 987654321098 | us-west-2 | FAILED | Insufficient permissions | +---------------+------------+-----------+-------------------------------------+ anihpj operation results retrieved
Example 2: Find anihpj Failed Operations
#!/bin/bash
# Get failed operation results for anihpj
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
OPERATION_ID="$1"

echo "anihpj Failed Operation Results"
echo "================================"

aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operation-results \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
  --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`FAILED`]' \
  --output json | jq -r '.[] | "
Account: \(.Account)
Region: \(.Region)
Status: \(.Status)
Reason: \(.StatusReason)
"'

echo ""
echo "Resolve failures and retry operation"
anihpj Failed Operation Results ================================ Account: 987654321098 Region: us-west-2 Status: FAILED Reason: Role AWSCloudFormationStackSetExecutionRole not found Account: 111222333444 Region: eu-west-1 Status: FAILED Reason: VPC limit exceeded in region Resolve failures and retry operation
Example 3: Generate anihpj Operation Summary
#!/bin/bash
# Create summary of operation results
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
OPERATION_ID="$1"

echo "anihpj StackSet Operation Summary"
echo "=================================="

RESULTS=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operation-results \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
  --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
  --output json)

echo "Total Instances: $(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '.Summaries | length')"
echo "Succeeded: $(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.Summaries[] | select(.Status == "SUCCEEDED")] | length')"
echo "Failed: $(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.Summaries[] | select(.Status == "FAILED")] | length')"
echo "Pending: $(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.Summaries[] | select(.Status == "PENDING")] | length')"
echo "Running: $(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.Summaries[] | select(.Status == "RUNNING")] | length')"

echo ""
echo "Results by Region:"
echo "$RESULTS" | jq -r '.Summaries | group_by(.Region) | .[] | "\(.[0].Region): \(map(select(.Status == "SUCCEEDED")) | length)/\(length) succeeded"'
anihpj StackSet Operation Summary ================================== Total Instances: 12 Succeeded: 10 Failed: 2 Pending: 0 Running: 0 Results by Region: us-east-1: 4/4 succeeded us-west-2: 3/4 succeeded eu-west-1: 3/4 succeeded 2 failures need attention
Example 4: Track anihpj Deployment Progress
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor operation progress in real-time
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
OPERATION_ID="$1"

echo "Tracking anihpj deployment progress..."

while true; do
  RESULTS=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operation-results \
    --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
    --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
    --output json)
  
  SUCCEEDED=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.Summaries[] | select(.Status == "SUCCEEDED")] | length')
  FAILED=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.Summaries[] | select(.Status == "FAILED")] | length')
  PENDING=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.Summaries[] | select(.Status == "PENDING")] | length')
  RUNNING=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '[.Summaries[] | select(.Status == "RUNNING")] | length')
  TOTAL=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq '.Summaries | length')
  
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) - Succeeded: $SUCCEEDED | Failed: $FAILED | Running: $RUNNING | Pending: $PENDING / Total: $TOTAL"
  
  if [ "$PENDING" -eq 0 ] && [ "$RUNNING" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Operation complete!"
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 10
done
Tracking anihpj deployment progress... 12:20:00 - Succeeded: 0 | Failed: 0 | Running: 4 | Pending: 8 / Total: 12 12:20:10 - Succeeded: 4 | Failed: 0 | Running: 4 | Pending: 4 / Total: 12 12:20:20 - Succeeded: 8 | Failed: 1 | Running: 3 | Pending: 0 / Total: 12 12:20:30 - Succeeded: 10 | Failed: 2 | Running: 0 | Pending: 0 / Total: 12 Operation complete! 10 successful, 2 failed
Example 5: Export anihpj Operation Results Report
#!/bin/bash
# Create detailed operation results report
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
OPERATION_ID="$1"
REPORT_FILE="anihpj-operation-results-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).json"

echo "Generating anihpj Operation Results Report..."

aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operation-results \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
  --operation-id $OPERATION_ID \
  --output json > $REPORT_FILE

# Create readable summary
cat > anihpj-operation-summary.txt << EOF
anihpj StackSet Operation Results
StackSet: $STACKSET
Operation ID: $OPERATION_ID
Generated: $(date)
=========================================

$(jq -r '.Summaries | group_by(.Status) | .[] | "
\(.[0].Status):
\(map("  - \(.Account)/\(.Region): \(.StatusReason // "OK")") | join("\n"))
"' $REPORT_FILE)

Total: $(jq '.Summaries | length' $REPORT_FILE) instances
EOF

cat anihpj-operation-summary.txt
echo ""
echo "Full results saved to $REPORT_FILE"
Generating anihpj Operation Results Report... anihpj StackSet Operation Results StackSet: anihpj-django-infrastructure Operation ID: operation-update-abc123 Generated: Fri Jan 17 12:25:00 2026 ========================================= SUCCEEDED: - 123456789012/us-east-1: OK - 123456789012/us-west-2: OK - 987654321098/us-east-1: OK - 111222333444/us-east-1: OK FAILED: - 987654321098/us-west-2: Insufficient permissions - 111222333444/eu-west-1: VPC limit exceeded Total: 6 instances Full results saved to anihpj-operation-results-20260117-122500.json

list-stack-set-operations

Returns summary information about operations performed on a StackSet.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operations \
  --stack-set-name {stackset-name}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name - Name of the stack set
  • --next-token - (Optional) Token for pagination
  • --call-as - (Optional) SELF or DELEGATED_ADMIN
Return Value

Returns Summaries array with OperationId, Action, Status, and CreationTimestamp.

5 Detailed CloudFormation list-stack-set-operations Examples
Example 1: List anihpj StackSet Operations
#!/bin/bash
# List all operations for anihpj StackSet
aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operations \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --query 'Summaries[*].[OperationId,Action,Status,CreationTimestamp]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListStackSetOperations | +---------------------+--------+------------+----------------------------------+ | operation-abc123 | UPDATE | SUCCEEDED | 2026-01-17T10:00:00Z | | operation-def456 | CREATE | SUCCEEDED | 2026-01-15T14:00:00Z | | operation-ghi789 | UPDATE | FAILED | 2026-01-14T09:00:00Z | | operation-jkl012 | CREATE | SUCCEEDED | 2026-01-10T11:00:00Z | +---------------------+--------+------------+----------------------------------+ anihpj StackSet operation history
Example 2: Get Recent anihpj Operations
#!/bin/bash
# Get last 5 operations for anihpj
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"

echo "anihpj Recent StackSet Operations"
echo "==================================="

aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operations \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
  --max-results 5 \
  --output json | jq -r '.Summaries[] | "
Operation: \(.OperationId | split("-") | .[-1])
Action: \(.Action)
Status: \(.Status)
Started: \(.CreationTimestamp)
Ended: \(.EndTimestamp // "In Progress")
"'
anihpj Recent StackSet Operations =================================== Operation: abc123 Action: UPDATE Status: SUCCEEDED Started: 2026-01-17T10:00:00Z Ended: 2026-01-17T10:15:00Z Operation: def456 Action: CREATE Status: SUCCEEDED Started: 2026-01-15T14:00:00Z Ended: 2026-01-15T14:30:00Z Operation: ghi789 Action: UPDATE Status: FAILED Started: 2026-01-14T09:00:00Z Ended: 2026-01-14T09:05:00Z
Example 3: Find Running anihpj Operations
#!/bin/bash
# Check for any running operations
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"

RUNNING=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operations \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`RUNNING`]' \
  --output json)

COUNT=$(echo "$RUNNING" | jq 'length')

if [ "$COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "anihpj Operations Currently Running: $COUNT"
  echo ""
  echo "$RUNNING" | jq -r '.[] | "Operation: \(.OperationId)
  Action: \(.Action)
  Started: \(.CreationTimestamp)"'
else
  echo "No operations currently running on anihpj StackSet"
  echo "Safe to start new operation"
fi
anihpj Operations Currently Running: 1 Operation: operation-mno345 Action: UPDATE Started: 2026-01-17T12:25:00Z Wait for completion before starting new operation
Example 4: Analyze anihpj Operation History
#!/bin/bash
# Analyze operation patterns for anihpj
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"

echo "anihpj StackSet Operation Analysis"
echo "===================================="

OPERATIONS=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operations \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
  --output json)

echo "Total Operations: $(echo "$OPERATIONS" | jq '.Summaries | length')"
echo ""
echo "By Action:"
echo "$OPERATIONS" | jq -r '.Summaries | group_by(.Action) | .[] | "  \(.[0].Action): \(length)"'
echo ""
echo "By Status:"
echo "$OPERATIONS" | jq -r '.Summaries | group_by(.Status) | .[] | "  \(.[0].Status): \(length)"'
echo ""
echo "Success Rate: $(echo "$OPERATIONS" | jq '([.Summaries[] | select(.Status == "SUCCEEDED")] | length) / ([.Summaries[] | select(.Status != "RUNNING")] | length) * 100')%"
anihpj StackSet Operation Analysis ==================================== Total Operations: 15 By Action: CREATE: 3 UPDATE: 10 DELETE: 2 By Status: SUCCEEDED: 12 FAILED: 2 RUNNING: 1 Success Rate: 85.71% anihpj StackSet has good operational health
Example 5: Export anihpj Operation Audit Log
#!/bin/bash
# Create audit log of all StackSet operations
STACKSET="anihpj-django-infrastructure"
AUDIT_FILE="anihpj-stackset-audit-$(date +%Y%m%d).csv"

echo "OperationId,Action,Status,StartTime,EndTime,Duration" > $AUDIT_FILE

aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operations \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
  --output json | jq -r '.Summaries[] | [
    .OperationId,
    .Action,
    .Status,
    .CreationTimestamp,
    (.EndTimestamp // "In Progress"),
    "N/A"
  ] | @csv' >> $AUDIT_FILE

echo "anihpj StackSet Operation Audit Log"
echo "====================================="
cat $AUDIT_FILE | head -10 | column -t -s ','

echo ""
echo "Full audit log saved to $AUDIT_FILE"
anihpj StackSet Operation Audit Log ===================================== OperationId Action Status StartTime EndTime operation-abc123 UPDATE SUCCEEDED 2026-01-17T10:00:00Z 2026-01-17T10:15:00Z operation-def456 CREATE SUCCEEDED 2026-01-15T14:00:00Z 2026-01-15T14:30:00Z operation-ghi789 UPDATE FAILED 2026-01-14T09:00:00Z 2026-01-14T09:05:00Z operation-jkl012 CREATE SUCCEEDED 2026-01-10T11:00:00Z 2026-01-10T11:45:00Z Full audit log saved to anihpj-stackset-audit-20260117.csv

list-type-registrations

Returns a list of registration tokens for the specified extension.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-type-registrations \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name}
Parameters Description
  • --type - RESOURCE, MODULE, or HOOK
  • --type-name - Name of the extension
  • --registration-status-filter - (Optional) COMPLETE, IN_PROGRESS, or FAILED
Return Value

Returns RegistrationTokenList array with registration tokens for the extension.

5 Detailed CloudFormation list-type-registrations Examples
Example 1: List anihpj Extension Registrations
#!/bin/bash
# List all registration attempts for anihpj custom resource
aws cloudformation list-type-registrations \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Django::Application \
  --query 'RegistrationTokenList[*]' \
  --output table

echo "anihpj extension registration history"
------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListTypeRegistrations | +-----------------------------------------------------------------------+ | reg-token-abc123-def456-ghi789 | | reg-token-jkl012-mno345-pqr678 | | reg-token-stu901-vwx234-yza567 | +-----------------------------------------------------------------------+ anihpj extension registration history
Example 2: Get anihpj Registration Details
#!/bin/bash
# Get details for each registration token
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"

echo "anihpj Extension Registration Details"
echo "======================================="

TOKENS=$(aws cloudformation list-type-registrations \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --query 'RegistrationTokenList[]' \
  --output text)

for TOKEN in $TOKENS; do
  REG_INFO=$(aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
    --registration-token $TOKEN \
    --output json 2>/dev/null)
  
  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "$REG_INFO" | jq -r '"
Token: \(.RegistrationToken | split("-") | .[0:2] | join("-"))...
Status: \(.ProgressStatus)
Version: \(.TypeVersionArn | split("/") | .[-1] // "N/A")
"'
  fi
done
anihpj Extension Registration Details ======================================= Token: reg-token-abc123... Status: COMPLETE Version: 00000003 Token: reg-token-jkl012... Status: COMPLETE Version: 00000002 Token: reg-token-stu901... Status: FAILED Version: N/A
Example 3: Filter anihpj Registrations by Status
#!/bin/bash
# Find failed registrations for anihpj
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"

echo "anihpj Failed Registrations"
echo "============================"

aws cloudformation list-type-registrations \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --registration-status-filter FAILED \
  --query 'RegistrationTokenList[]' \
  --output text | while read TOKEN; do
  
  aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
    --registration-token $TOKEN \
    --output json | jq -r '"
Token: \(.RegistrationToken | .[0:20])...
Status: \(.ProgressStatus)
Error: \(.StatusMessage // "Unknown error")
"'
done
anihpj Failed Registrations ============================ Token: reg-token-stu901-vwx... Status: FAILED Error: Schema validation failed: Required property 'DatabaseEndpoint' missing in handlers Fix schema and re-register extension
Example 4: Track anihpj Extension Versions
#!/bin/bash
# Track version history through registrations
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"

echo "anihpj Extension Version History"
echo "=================================="

aws cloudformation list-type-registrations \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --registration-status-filter COMPLETE \
  --output json | jq -r '.RegistrationTokenList[]' | while read TOKEN; do
  
  aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
    --registration-token $TOKEN \
    --output json | jq -r '"
Version: \(.TypeVersionArn | split("/") | .[-1])
Registered: \(.ProgressStatus)
Description: \(.Description // "No description")
"'
done | sort -t: -k2
anihpj Extension Version History ================================== Version: 00000001 Registered: COMPLETE Description: Initial version with basic Django support Version: 00000002 Registered: COMPLETE Description: Added cache cluster support Version: 00000003 Registered: COMPLETE Description: Added multi-region deployment support 3 versions registered for anihpj extension
Example 5: Audit anihpj Extension Registrations
#!/bin/bash
# Create registration audit for all anihpj extensions
EXTENSIONS=("anihpj::Django::Application" "anihpj::Django::Database" "anihpj::Django::Cache")
AUDIT_FILE="anihpj-extension-registrations-audit.txt"

echo "anihpj Extension Registration Audit" > $AUDIT_FILE
echo "Generated: $(date)" >> $AUDIT_FILE
echo "====================================" >> $AUDIT_FILE

for EXT in "${EXTENSIONS[@]}"; do
  echo "" >> $AUDIT_FILE
  echo "Extension: $EXT" >> $AUDIT_FILE
  echo "------------------------" >> $AUDIT_FILE
  
  aws cloudformation list-type-registrations \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $EXT \
    --output json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.RegistrationTokenList | length' | xargs -I {} echo "  Total Registrations: {}" >> $AUDIT_FILE
  
  aws cloudformation list-type-registrations \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $EXT \
    --registration-status-filter COMPLETE \
    --output json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.RegistrationTokenList | length' | xargs -I {} echo "  Successful: {}" >> $AUDIT_FILE
  
  aws cloudformation list-type-registrations \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $EXT \
    --registration-status-filter FAILED \
    --output json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.RegistrationTokenList | length' | xargs -I {} echo "  Failed: {}" >> $AUDIT_FILE
done

cat $AUDIT_FILE
anihpj Extension Registration Audit Generated: Fri Jan 17 12:35:00 2026 ==================================== Extension: anihpj::Django::Application ------------------------ Total Registrations: 5 Successful: 3 Failed: 2 Extension: anihpj::Django::Database ------------------------ Total Registrations: 3 Successful: 3 Failed: 0 Extension: anihpj::Django::Cache ------------------------ Total Registrations: 2 Successful: 2 Failed: 0

list-type-versions

Returns summary information about versions of an extension.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name}
Parameters Description
  • --type - RESOURCE, MODULE, or HOOK
  • --type-name - Name of the extension
  • --deprecated-status - (Optional) LIVE or DEPRECATED
Return Value

Returns TypeVersionSummaries array with VersionId, IsDefaultVersion, and TimeCreated.

5 Detailed CloudFormation list-type-versions Examples
Example 1: List anihpj Extension Versions
#!/bin/bash
# List all versions of anihpj custom resource
aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Django::Application \
  --query 'TypeVersionSummaries[*].[VersionId,IsDefaultVersion,TimeCreated]' \
  --output table
------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ListTypeVersions | +------------+------------------+---------------------------------------+ | 00000001 | False | 2026-01-10T10:00:00Z | | 00000002 | False | 2026-01-12T14:30:00Z | | 00000003 | True | 2026-01-15T09:00:00Z | +------------+------------------+---------------------------------------+ anihpj extension has 3 versions
Example 2: Check MongoDB Extension Versions for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Check available versions of third-party extension
aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster \
  --output json | jq -r '.TypeVersionSummaries[] | "
Version: \(.VersionId)
Default: \(.IsDefaultVersion)
Created: \(.TimeCreated)
Status: \(.DeprecatedStatus // "LIVE")
"'

echo "Consider upgrading anihpj to latest version"
Version: 00000001 Default: False Created: 2025-06-15T10:00:00Z Status: DEPRECATED Version: 00000002 Default: False Created: 2025-09-20T14:30:00Z Status: LIVE Version: 00000003 Default: True Created: 2026-01-05T09:00:00Z Status: LIVE Consider upgrading anihpj to latest version
Example 3: Find Default anihpj Extension Version
#!/bin/bash
# Get current default version for anihpj extension
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"

DEFAULT=$(aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --query 'TypeVersionSummaries[?IsDefaultVersion==`true`]' \
  --output json)

echo "anihpj Extension Default Version:"
echo "$DEFAULT" | jq -r '.[0] | {
  VersionId,
  Created: .TimeCreated,
  Description: .Description
}'

LATEST=$(aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --query 'TypeVersionSummaries | sort_by(@, &TimeCreated) | [-1].VersionId' \
  --output text)

CURRENT=$(echo "$DEFAULT" | jq -r '.[0].VersionId')

if [ "$CURRENT" != "$LATEST" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "Newer version available: $LATEST"
fi
anihpj Extension Default Version: { "VersionId": "00000003", "Created": "2026-01-15T09:00:00Z", "Description": "Added multi-region deployment support" } Using latest version
Example 4: Compare anihpj Extension Versions
#!/bin/bash
# Compare version features for anihpj extension
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"

echo "anihpj Extension Version Comparison"
echo "====================================="

aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --output json | jq -r '.TypeVersionSummaries | sort_by(.TimeCreated) | .[] | "
Version \(.VersionId):
  Created: \(.TimeCreated | split("T")[0])
  Default: \(.IsDefaultVersion)
  Deprecated: \(.DeprecatedStatus // "LIVE")
  Description: \(.Description // "No description")
"'
anihpj Extension Version Comparison ===================================== Version 00000001: Created: 2026-01-10 Default: false Deprecated: LIVE Description: Initial Django application resource Version 00000002: Created: 2026-01-12 Default: false Deprecated: LIVE Description: Added ElastiCache integration Version 00000003: Created: 2026-01-15 Default: true Deprecated: LIVE Description: Multi-region support and improved handlers
Example 5: Deprecate Old anihpj Extension Version
#!/bin/bash
# List and deprecate old versions
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"

echo "anihpj Extension Version Management"
echo "====================================="

# List all versions
aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --query 'TypeVersionSummaries[*].[VersionId,IsDefaultVersion,DeprecatedStatus]' \
  --output table

echo ""
echo "Non-default versions that could be deprecated:"

aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --deprecated-status LIVE \
  --query 'TypeVersionSummaries[?IsDefaultVersion==`false`].VersionId' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | while read VERSION; do
  echo "  $VERSION - To deprecate:"
  echo "    aws cloudformation set-type-default-version --type RESOURCE --type-name $TYPE_NAME --version-id LATEST"
  echo "    aws cloudformation deregister-type --type RESOURCE --type-name $TYPE_NAME --version-id $VERSION"
done
anihpj Extension Version Management ===================================== -------------+-----------------+------------------ | 00000001 | False | LIVE | | 00000002 | False | LIVE | | 00000003 | True | LIVE | -------------+-----------------+------------------ Non-default versions that could be deprecated: 00000001 - To deprecate: aws cloudformation set-type-default-version --type RESOURCE --type-name anihpj::Django::Application --version-id LATEST aws cloudformation deregister-type --type RESOURCE --type-name anihpj::Django::Application --version-id 00000001 00000002 - To deprecate: ...

list-types

Returns summary information about all registered extensions.

Syntax
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE
Parameters Description
  • --type - (Optional) RESOURCE, MODULE, or HOOK
  • --visibility - (Optional) PUBLIC or PRIVATE
  • --provisioning-type - (Optional) FULLY_MUTABLE or IMMUTABLE
Return Value

Returns TypeSummaries array with TypeName, DefaultVersionId, and Description.

5 Detailed CloudFormation list-types Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Private Extensions
#!/bin/bash
# List all private extensions in anihpj account
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].[TypeName,DefaultVersionId,Description]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListTypes | +-------------------------------+-----------+----------------------------------+ | anihpj::Django::Application | 00000003 | Django application resource | | anihpj::Django::Database | 00000002 | Django database configuration | | anihpj::Django::Cache | 00000001 | Redis cache for Django | +-------------------------------+-----------+----------------------------------+ anihpj private extensions listed
Example 2: List Activated Public Extensions for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# List third-party extensions activated for anihpj
echo "anihpj Activated Third-Party Extensions"
echo "========================================"

aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PUBLIC \
  --provisioning-type FULLY_MUTABLE \
  --filters TypeNamePrefix=MongoDB,TypeNamePrefix=Datadog,TypeNamePrefix=NewRelic \
  --output json | jq -r '.TypeSummaries[] | "
Extension: \(.TypeName)
Publisher: \(.PublisherId | .[0:12])...
Version: \(.DefaultVersionId)
Status: \(.TypeTestsStatus // "N/A")
"'
anihpj Activated Third-Party Extensions ======================================== Extension: MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster Publisher: bb989456c78c... Version: 00000003 Status: PASSED Extension: Datadog::Integrations::AWS Publisher: 408988dff9e8... Version: 00000002 Status: PASSED Extension: NewRelic::Observability::Aiworkloads Publisher: 759f81f13de8... Version: 00000001 Status: PASSED
Example 3: Find All anihpj Resources
#!/bin/bash
# Find all extensions with anihpj prefix
echo "All anihpj CloudFormation Extensions"
echo "======================================"

aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --filters TypeNamePrefix=anihpj \
  --output json | jq -r '.TypeSummaries[] | "\(.TypeName): \(.Description // "No description")"'

echo ""
echo "Hooks:"
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type HOOK \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --filters TypeNamePrefix=anihpj \
  --output json | jq -r '.TypeSummaries[] | "\(.TypeName): \(.Description // "No description")"' 2>/dev/null || echo "  No hooks registered"
All anihpj CloudFormation Extensions ====================================== anihpj::Django::Application: Django application custom resource anihpj::Django::Database: Database configuration resource anihpj::Django::Cache: Redis cache configuration Hooks: anihpj::Security::ValidationHook: Security validation hook anihpj::Cost::EstimationHook: Cost estimation hook
Example 4: List Extensions by Provisioning Type
#!/bin/bash
# Categorize extensions by provisioning capability
echo "anihpj Extension Capabilities"
echo "=============================="

echo ""
echo "Fully Mutable (Create/Update/Delete):"
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --provisioning-type FULLY_MUTABLE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].TypeName' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sed 's/^/  - /'

echo ""
echo "Immutable (Create/Delete only):"
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --provisioning-type IMMUTABLE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].TypeName' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sed 's/^/  - /'

echo ""
echo "Non-Provisionable (Read only):"
aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --provisioning-type NON_PROVISIONABLE \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].TypeName' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sed 's/^/  - /'
anihpj Extension Capabilities ============================== Fully Mutable (Create/Update/Delete): - anihpj::Django::Application - anihpj::Django::Database Immutable (Create/Delete only): - anihpj::Django::Cache Non-Provisionable (Read only): - anihpj::Django::Config
Example 5: Export anihpj Extension Inventory
#!/bin/bash
# Create complete extension inventory for anihpj
INVENTORY_FILE="anihpj-extension-inventory.json"

echo "Creating anihpj Extension Inventory..."

# Get all extension types
cat > $INVENTORY_FILE << EOF
{
  "Generated": "$(date -Iseconds)",
  "Account": "$(aws sts get-caller-identity --query 'Account' --output text)",
  "Extensions": {
    "Private": $(aws cloudformation list-types --type RESOURCE --visibility PRIVATE --output json | jq '.TypeSummaries'),
    "PublicActivated": $(aws cloudformation list-types --type RESOURCE --visibility PUBLIC --provisioning-type FULLY_MUTABLE --output json | jq '.TypeSummaries'),
    "Hooks": $(aws cloudformation list-types --type HOOK --visibility PRIVATE --output json | jq '.TypeSummaries // []')
  }
}
EOF

echo "anihpj Extension Inventory Summary:"
echo "====================================="
echo "Private Resources: $(jq '.Extensions.Private | length' $INVENTORY_FILE)"
echo "Public Activated: $(jq '.Extensions.PublicActivated | length' $INVENTORY_FILE)"
echo "Hooks: $(jq '.Extensions.Hooks | length' $INVENTORY_FILE)"
echo ""
echo "Full inventory saved to $INVENTORY_FILE"
Creating anihpj Extension Inventory... anihpj Extension Inventory Summary: ===================================== Private Resources: 4 Public Activated: 5 Hooks: 2 Full inventory saved to anihpj-extension-inventory.json

publish-type

Publishes an extension to the CloudFormation public registry.

Syntax
aws cloudformation publish-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name}
Parameters Description
  • --type - RESOURCE, MODULE, or HOOK
  • --type-name - Name of the extension to publish
  • --public-version-number - (Optional) Version number for public registry
Return Value

Returns PublicTypeArn with the ARN of the published extension.

5 Detailed CloudFormation publish-type Examples
Example 1: Publish anihpj Extension to Public Registry
#!/bin/bash
# Publish anihpj Django extension to public registry
aws cloudformation publish-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Django::Application \
  --public-version-number "1.0.0"

echo "anihpj extension published to public registry"
echo "Public version: 1.0.0"
{ "PublicTypeArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1::type/resource/anihpj-Django-Application/1.0.0" } anihpj extension published to public registry Public version: 1.0.0
Example 2: Publish New Version of anihpj Extension
#!/bin/bash
# Publish updated version of anihpj extension
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"
VERSION="1.1.0"

echo "Publishing anihpj extension v$VERSION..."

# First verify tests pass
TEST_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --query 'TypeTestsStatus' \
  --output text)

if [ "$TEST_STATUS" == "PASSED" ]; then
  aws cloudformation publish-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
    --public-version-number $VERSION
  
  echo "Published successfully!"
else
  echo "Cannot publish - tests status: $TEST_STATUS"
  echo "Run contract tests first"
fi
Publishing anihpj extension v1.1.0... { "PublicTypeArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1::type/resource/anihpj-Django-Application/1.1.0" } Published successfully! anihpj extension v1.1.0 now available in public registry
Example 3: Publish anihpj Hook to Registry
#!/bin/bash
# Publish anihpj security hook
aws cloudformation publish-type \
  --type HOOK \
  --type-name anihpj::Security::ValidationHook \
  --public-version-number "1.0.0"

echo "anihpj security hook published"

# Verify publication
aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type HOOK \
  --type-name anihpj::Security::ValidationHook \
  --query '{TypeName:TypeName,PublicVersionNumber:PublicVersionNumber,Visibility:Visibility}' \
  --output table
{ "PublicTypeArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1::type/hook/anihpj-Security-ValidationHook/1.0.0" } anihpj security hook published ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | DescribeType | +------------------+-------------------+--------------------------------+ | PublicVersionNum | 1.0.0 | | | TypeName | anihpj::Security::ValidationHook | | Visibility | PUBLIC | | +------------------+-------------------+--------------------------------+
Example 4: Pre-Publish Validation for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Validate extension before publishing
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"
VERSION="1.2.0"

echo "anihpj Pre-Publish Validation"
echo "=============================="

# Check publisher registration
PUBLISHER_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
  --query 'PublisherStatus' \
  --output text 2>/dev/null)

if [ "$PUBLISHER_STATUS" != "VERIFIED" ]; then
  echo "✗ Publisher not verified"
  echo "Run: aws cloudformation register-publisher"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Publisher verified"

# Check type tests
TEST_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --query 'TypeTestsStatus' \
  --output text)

if [ "$TEST_STATUS" != "PASSED" ]; then
  echo "✗ Contract tests not passed: $TEST_STATUS"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Contract tests passed"

# Check schema
SCHEMA_VALID=$(aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --query 'Schema' \
  --output text | jq -e '.' > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "true" || echo "false")

echo "✓ Schema valid"

echo ""
echo "Ready to publish anihpj v$VERSION"
echo "Run: aws cloudformation publish-type --type RESOURCE --type-name $TYPE_NAME --public-version-number $VERSION"
anihpj Pre-Publish Validation ============================== ✓ Publisher verified ✓ Contract tests passed ✓ Schema valid Ready to publish anihpj v1.2.0 Run: aws cloudformation publish-type --type RESOURCE --type-name anihpj::Django::Application --public-version-number 1.2.0
Example 5: Publish All anihpj Extensions
#!/bin/bash
# Batch publish all anihpj extensions
VERSION="1.0.0"

echo "Publishing anihpj Extension Suite v$VERSION"
echo "============================================="

# Get all anihpj private extensions
EXTENSIONS=$(aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --filters TypeNamePrefix=anihpj \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].TypeName' \
  --output text)

for EXT in $EXTENSIONS; do
  echo ""
  echo "Publishing: $EXT"
  
  RESULT=$(aws cloudformation publish-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $EXT \
    --public-version-number $VERSION \
    --output json 2>&1)
  
  if echo "$RESULT" | grep -q "PublicTypeArn"; then
    echo "  ✓ Published successfully"
    echo "  ARN: $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.PublicTypeArn')"
  else
    echo "  ✗ Failed: $RESULT"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "anihpj extension suite publication complete"
Publishing anihpj Extension Suite v1.0.0 ============================================= Publishing: anihpj::Django::Application ✓ Published successfully ARN: arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1::type/resource/anihpj-Django-Application/1.0.0 Publishing: anihpj::Django::Database ✓ Published successfully ARN: arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1::type/resource/anihpj-Django-Database/1.0.0 Publishing: anihpj::Django::Cache ✓ Published successfully ARN: arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1::type/resource/anihpj-Django-Cache/1.0.0 anihpj extension suite publication complete

record-handler-progress

Reports progress of a resource handler to CloudFormation during custom resource operations.

Syntax
aws cloudformation record-handler-progress \
  --bearer-token {token} \
  --operation-status IN_PROGRESS \
  --current-operation-status {status}
Parameters Description
  • --bearer-token - The token provided by CloudFormation
  • --operation-status - PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, SUCCESS, or FAILED
  • --current-operation-status - (Optional) Detailed progress message
Return Value

Returns no content on success, signals handler progress to CloudFormation.

5 Detailed CloudFormation record-handler-progress Examples
Example 1: Report anihpj Handler Progress
#!/bin/bash
# Report progress from anihpj custom resource handler
# This is typically called from within Lambda handler code

BEARER_TOKEN="${BEARER_TOKEN:-sample-bearer-token-abc123}"

aws cloudformation record-handler-progress \
  --bearer-token "$BEARER_TOKEN" \
  --operation-status IN_PROGRESS \
  --current-operation-status "Creating Django application resources" \
  --status-message "anihpj application provisioning: 25% complete"

echo "Progress reported to CloudFormation"
Progress reported to CloudFormation anihpj handler progress recorded successfully
Example 2: anihpj Handler Success Report
#!/bin/bash
# Report successful completion
BEARER_TOKEN="${BEARER_TOKEN:-sample-bearer-token-abc123}"
RESOURCE_MODEL='{"ApplicationName":"anihpj","Status":"ACTIVE","Endpoint":"https://anihpj.example.com"}'

aws cloudformation record-handler-progress \
  --bearer-token "$BEARER_TOKEN" \
  --operation-status SUCCESS \
  --resource-model "$RESOURCE_MODEL"

echo "anihpj handler reported successful completion"
anihpj handler reported successful completion CloudFormation will now complete the stack operation
Example 3: Report anihpj Handler Failure
#!/bin/bash
# Report failure from handler
BEARER_TOKEN="${BEARER_TOKEN:-sample-bearer-token-abc123}"

aws cloudformation record-handler-progress \
  --bearer-token "$BEARER_TOKEN" \
  --operation-status FAILED \
  --error-code HandlerInternalFailure \
  --status-message "anihpj database connection failed: timeout after 300s"

echo "Failure reported to CloudFormation"
echo "Stack operation will be marked as failed"
Failure reported to CloudFormation Stack operation will be marked as failed anihpj handler failure recorded
Example 4: anihpj Handler Progress Simulation
#!/bin/bash
# Simulate handler progress updates for testing
# Note: This requires a valid bearer token from CloudFormation

BEARER_TOKEN="${1:-demo-token}"
OPERATION="CREATE"

echo "Simulating anihpj handler progress..."
echo "====================================="

# Progress stages
STAGES=(
  "25|Validating input parameters"
  "50|Creating database resources"
  "75|Deploying Django application"
  "90|Configuring load balancer"
)

for STAGE in "${STAGES[@]}"; do
  PERCENT=$(echo "$STAGE" | cut -d'|' -f1)
  MESSAGE=$(echo "$STAGE" | cut -d'|' -f2)
  
  echo "[$PERCENT%] $MESSAGE"
  
  # In real handler, this would call the API
  # aws cloudformation record-handler-progress \
  #   --bearer-token "$BEARER_TOKEN" \
  #   --operation-status IN_PROGRESS \
  #   --status-message "anihpj $OPERATION: $MESSAGE ($PERCENT%)"
  
  sleep 1
done

echo "[100%] anihpj provisioning complete"
Simulating anihpj handler progress... ===================================== [25%] Validating input parameters [50%] Creating database resources [75%] Deploying Django application [90%] Configuring load balancer [100%] anihpj provisioning complete Handler progress simulation complete
Example 5: anihpj Handler Error Codes Reference
#!/bin/bash
# Document error codes for anihpj handler
cat << 'EOF'
anihpj Custom Resource Handler Error Codes
===========================================

Valid error codes for record-handler-progress:

NotUpdatable
  - Resource property cannot be updated after creation
  - Example: anihpj application name cannot change

InvalidRequest
  - Invalid parameters in resource properties
  - Example: Invalid Django version specified

AccessDenied
  - Handler lacks permissions for operation
  - Example: Cannot access RDS instance

InvalidCredentials
  - Authentication failed
  - Example: Database credentials invalid

AlreadyExists
  - Resource already exists
  - Example: anihpj application name already taken

NotFound
  - Resource not found for update/delete
  - Example: Application ID does not exist

ResourceConflict
  - Operation conflicts with current state
  - Example: Cannot delete while deployment in progress

Throttling
  - Rate limit exceeded
  - Example: Too many API calls

ServiceLimitExceeded
  - Quota limit reached
  - Example: Max applications per account

HandlerInternalFailure
  - Unexpected handler error
  - Example: Lambda timeout

NetworkFailure
  - Network connectivity issue
  - Example: Cannot reach database

GeneralServiceException
  - Unspecified service error
  - Example: AWS service outage

EOF

echo "Use these codes when reporting handler failures"
anihpj Custom Resource Handler Error Codes =========================================== Valid error codes for record-handler-progress: NotUpdatable - Resource property cannot be updated after creation - Example: anihpj application name cannot change InvalidRequest - Invalid parameters in resource properties - Example: Invalid Django version specified AccessDenied - Handler lacks permissions for operation - Example: Cannot access RDS instance ... Use these codes when reporting handler failures

register-publisher

Registers your account as a publisher of CloudFormation extensions.

Syntax
aws cloudformation register-publisher \
  --accept-terms-and-conditions \
  --connection-arn {arn}
Parameters Description
  • --accept-terms-and-conditions - Accept publisher terms
  • --connection-arn - (Optional) CodeConnections ARN for GitHub
Return Value

Returns PublisherId for the registered publisher account.

5 Detailed CloudFormation register-publisher Examples
Example 1: Register anihpj Account as Publisher
#!/bin/bash
# Register account to publish anihpj extensions
aws cloudformation register-publisher \
  --accept-terms-and-conditions

echo "anihpj account registered as extension publisher"
{ "PublisherId": "408988dff9e826b5b153e0c1" } anihpj account registered as extension publisher
Example 2: Register with GitHub Connection for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Register with GitHub for source verification
CONNECTION_ARN="arn:aws:codestar-connections:us-east-1:123456789012:connection/abc123"

aws cloudformation register-publisher \
  --accept-terms-and-conditions \
  --connection-arn $CONNECTION_ARN

echo "anihpj publisher registered with GitHub verification"
echo "Verification email sent to repository owner"
{ "PublisherId": "408988dff9e826b5b153e0c1" } anihpj publisher registered with GitHub verification Verification email sent to repository owner
Example 3: Check anihpj Publisher Registration Status
#!/bin/bash
# Verify publisher registration status
echo "anihpj Publisher Status"
echo "========================"

# First register if not already
PUBLISHER_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
  --query 'PublisherStatus' \
  --output text 2>/dev/null)

if [ -z "$PUBLISHER_STATUS" ]; then
  echo "Not registered. Registering now..."
  aws cloudformation register-publisher \
    --accept-terms-and-conditions
else
  echo "Status: $PUBLISHER_STATUS"
  
  aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
    --output json | jq -r '{
      PublisherId,
      PublisherStatus,
      IdentityProvider,
      PublisherProfile
    }'
fi
anihpj Publisher Status ======================== Status: VERIFIED { "PublisherId": "408988dff9e826b5b153e0c1", "PublisherStatus": "VERIFIED", "IdentityProvider": "AWS_Marketplace", "PublisherProfile": "https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/seller-profile?id=408988dff9e826b5b153e0c1" }
Example 4: Complete anihpj Publisher Setup
#!/bin/bash
# Complete publisher setup workflow
echo "anihpj CloudFormation Publisher Setup"
echo "======================================="

# Step 1: Create CodeStar connection (if using GitHub)
echo "Step 1: Setting up GitHub connection..."
echo "  Manual step: Create connection in AWS Console"
echo "  Navigate to: Developer Tools > Settings > Connections"
echo ""

# Step 2: Register publisher
echo "Step 2: Registering as publisher..."
RESULT=$(aws cloudformation register-publisher \
  --accept-terms-and-conditions \
  --output json 2>&1)

if echo "$RESULT" | grep -q "PublisherId"; then
  PUBLISHER_ID=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.PublisherId')
  echo "  ✓ Registered with ID: $PUBLISHER_ID"
elif echo "$RESULT" | grep -q "already registered"; then
  echo "  ✓ Already registered"
  PUBLISHER_ID=$(aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
    --query 'PublisherId' --output text)
else
  echo "  ✗ Registration failed: $RESULT"
  exit 1
fi

# Step 3: Verify status
echo ""
echo "Step 3: Checking verification status..."
STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
  --query 'PublisherStatus' --output text)
echo "  Publisher Status: $STATUS"

if [ "$STATUS" != "VERIFIED" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "  Next steps to verify:"
  echo "  1. Check email for verification link"
  echo "  2. Complete identity verification"
  echo "  3. Wait for approval (1-2 business days)"
fi

echo ""
echo "anihpj publisher setup complete"
anihpj CloudFormation Publisher Setup ======================================= Step 1: Setting up GitHub connection... Manual step: Create connection in AWS Console Navigate to: Developer Tools > Settings > Connections Step 2: Registering as publisher... ✓ Registered with ID: 408988dff9e826b5b153e0c1 Step 3: Checking verification status... Publisher Status: VERIFIED anihpj publisher setup complete
Example 5: anihpj Publisher Prerequisites Check
#!/bin/bash
# Check prerequisites before registering
echo "anihpj Publisher Prerequisites Check"
echo "======================================"

# Check AWS account
echo "AWS Account: $(aws sts get-caller-identity --query 'Account' --output text)"

# Check IAM permissions
echo ""
echo "Checking IAM permissions..."
REQUIRED_ACTIONS=(
  "cloudformation:RegisterPublisher"
  "cloudformation:DescribePublisher"
  "cloudformation:RegisterType"
  "cloudformation:PublishType"
)

for ACTION in "${REQUIRED_ACTIONS[@]}"; do
  # Simplified check - in practice use IAM policy simulator
  echo "  - $ACTION"
done

# Check existing registration
echo ""
echo "Existing Registration:"
aws cloudformation describe-publisher \
  --output json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '{
    Status: .PublisherStatus,
    Id: .PublisherId
  }' || echo "  Not registered"

echo ""
echo "To register: aws cloudformation register-publisher --accept-terms-and-conditions"
anihpj Publisher Prerequisites Check ====================================== AWS Account: 123456789012 Checking IAM permissions... - cloudformation:RegisterPublisher - cloudformation:DescribePublisher - cloudformation:RegisterType - cloudformation:PublishType Existing Registration: { "Status": "VERIFIED", "Id": "408988dff9e826b5b153e0c1" } Ready to publish anihpj extensions

register-type

Registers an extension as a resource type, hook, or module in CloudFormation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation register-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name} \
  --schema-handler-package {s3-url}
Parameters Description
  • --type - RESOURCE, MODULE, or HOOK
  • --type-name - Name for the extension (format: Organization::Service::Resource)
  • --schema-handler-package - S3 URL to the extension package
Return Value

Returns RegistrationToken to monitor the registration progress.

5 Detailed CloudFormation register-type Examples
Example 1: Register anihpj Custom Resource Type
#!/bin/bash
# Register anihpj Django application resource type
aws cloudformation register-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name "anihpj::Django::Application" \
  --schema-handler-package "s3://anihpj-cfn-extensions/anihpj-django-application.zip"

echo "anihpj custom resource type registration initiated"
{ "RegistrationToken": "reg-token-abc123-def456-ghi789" } anihpj custom resource type registration initiated Use describe-type-registration to check status
Example 2: Register anihpj with Execution Role
#!/bin/bash
# Register with specific execution role
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"
S3_PACKAGE="s3://anihpj-cfn-extensions/anihpj-django-application-v3.zip"
EXEC_ROLE="arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-cfn-extension-role"

echo "Registering anihpj extension..."

RESULT=$(aws cloudformation register-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name "$TYPE_NAME" \
  --schema-handler-package "$S3_PACKAGE" \
  --execution-role-arn "$EXEC_ROLE" \
  --output json)

TOKEN=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.RegistrationToken')
echo "Registration Token: $TOKEN"

# Wait for registration
echo "Waiting for registration to complete..."
for i in {1..30}; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
    --registration-token "$TOKEN" \
    --query 'ProgressStatus' \
    --output text)
  
  echo "  Status: $STATUS"
  
  if [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETE" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "anihpj extension registered successfully!"
    break
  elif [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
    echo ""
    aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
      --registration-token "$TOKEN" \
      --query 'StatusMessage' --output text
    exit 1
  fi
  
  sleep 10
done
Registering anihpj extension... Registration Token: reg-token-abc123-def456-ghi789 Waiting for registration to complete... Status: IN_PROGRESS Status: IN_PROGRESS Status: COMPLETE anihpj extension registered successfully!
Example 3: Register anihpj Security Hook
#!/bin/bash
# Register anihpj security validation hook
aws cloudformation register-type \
  --type HOOK \
  --type-name "anihpj::Security::ValidationHook" \
  --schema-handler-package "s3://anihpj-cfn-extensions/anihpj-security-hook.zip" \
  --logging-config LogRoleArn=arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-hook-logging,LogGroupName=/aws/cloudformation/hooks/anihpj

echo "anihpj security hook registration started"
{ "RegistrationToken": "reg-token-hook-xyz789" } anihpj security hook registration started
Example 4: Complete anihpj Extension Registration Workflow
#!/bin/bash
# Full registration workflow for anihpj extension
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"
VERSION="3.0"

echo "anihpj Extension Registration Workflow"
echo "========================================"

# Step 1: Build and package
echo "Step 1: Building extension package..."
cd /path/to/anihpj-extension
cfn generate
cfn submit --dry-run
echo "  ✓ Package built: anihpj-django-application.zip"

# Step 2: Upload to S3
echo ""
echo "Step 2: Uploading to S3..."
S3_BUCKET="anihpj-cfn-extensions"
S3_KEY="anihpj-django-application-v${VERSION}.zip"
aws s3 cp anihpj-django-application.zip "s3://${S3_BUCKET}/${S3_KEY}"
echo "  ✓ Uploaded to s3://${S3_BUCKET}/${S3_KEY}"

# Step 3: Register type
echo ""
echo "Step 3: Registering type..."
RESULT=$(aws cloudformation register-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name "$TYPE_NAME" \
  --schema-handler-package "s3://${S3_BUCKET}/${S3_KEY}" \
  --output json)

TOKEN=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.RegistrationToken')
echo "  Registration Token: $TOKEN"

# Step 4: Wait and verify
echo ""
echo "Step 4: Verifying registration..."
sleep 30
aws cloudformation describe-type-registration \
  --registration-token "$TOKEN" \
  --output json | jq '{
    Status: .ProgressStatus,
    TypeArn: .TypeVersionArn,
    Description: .Description
  }'

echo ""
echo "anihpj extension registration workflow complete"
anihpj Extension Registration Workflow ======================================== Step 1: Building extension package... ✓ Package built: anihpj-django-application.zip Step 2: Uploading to S3... ✓ Uploaded to s3://anihpj-cfn-extensions/anihpj-django-application-v3.0.zip Step 3: Registering type... Registration Token: reg-token-abc123-def456 Step 4: Verifying registration... { "Status": "COMPLETE", "TypeArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/anihpj-Django-Application/00000003", "Description": "Registered anihpj Django application resource" } anihpj extension registration workflow complete
Example 5: Register All anihpj Extensions
#!/bin/bash
# Batch register all anihpj extensions
S3_BUCKET="anihpj-cfn-extensions"

declare -A EXTENSIONS=(
  ["anihpj::Django::Application"]="anihpj-django-application.zip"
  ["anihpj::Django::Database"]="anihpj-django-database.zip"
  ["anihpj::Django::Cache"]="anihpj-django-cache.zip"
)

echo "Registering anihpj Extension Suite"
echo "===================================="

for TYPE_NAME in "${!EXTENSIONS[@]}"; do
  PACKAGE="${EXTENSIONS[$TYPE_NAME]}"
  
  echo ""
  echo "Registering: $TYPE_NAME"
  
  RESULT=$(aws cloudformation register-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name "$TYPE_NAME" \
    --schema-handler-package "s3://${S3_BUCKET}/${PACKAGE}" \
    --output json 2>&1)
  
  if echo "$RESULT" | grep -q "RegistrationToken"; then
    TOKEN=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.RegistrationToken')
    echo "  Token: $TOKEN"
    echo "  Status: Pending"
  else
    echo "  Error: $RESULT"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "All anihpj extensions submitted for registration"
echo "Use list-type-registrations to monitor progress"
Registering anihpj Extension Suite ==================================== Registering: anihpj::Django::Application Token: reg-token-app-123 Status: Pending Registering: anihpj::Django::Database Token: reg-token-db-456 Status: Pending Registering: anihpj::Django::Cache Token: reg-token-cache-789 Status: Pending All anihpj extensions submitted for registration Use list-type-registrations to monitor progress

rollback-stack

Rolls back a stack in UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED state to the last successful state.

Syntax
aws cloudformation rollback-stack \
  --stack-name {stack-name}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name - Name of the stack to roll back
  • --resources-to-skip - (Optional) Resources to skip during rollback
  • --role-arn - (Optional) IAM role for rollback operations
Return Value

Returns no content on success, initiates rollback to last stable state.

5 Detailed CloudFormation rollback-stack Examples
Example 1: Rollback Failed anihpj Stack Update
#!/bin/bash
# Rollback anihpj stack stuck in UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED
aws cloudformation rollback-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-production

echo "anihpj stack rollback initiated"
anihpj stack rollback initiated Stack will return to last stable state
Example 2: Rollback anihpj Stack with Skip Resources
#!/bin/bash
# Rollback while skipping problematic resources
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"

# First identify resources causing issues
echo "Checking failed resources..."
FAILED_RESOURCES=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'StackResources[?ResourceStatus==`UPDATE_FAILED` || ResourceStatus==`DELETE_FAILED`].LogicalResourceId' \
  --output text)

echo "Failed resources: $FAILED_RESOURCES"
echo ""

# Rollback skipping those resources
echo "Rolling back anihpj stack..."
aws cloudformation rollback-stack \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --resources-to-skip $FAILED_RESOURCES

echo ""
echo "anihpj stack rollback started (skipping: $FAILED_RESOURCES)"
Checking failed resources... Failed resources: CustomLambdaFunction FailedSecurityGroup Rolling back anihpj stack... anihpj stack rollback started (skipping: CustomLambdaFunction FailedSecurityGroup)
Example 3: Monitor anihpj Stack Rollback
#!/bin/bash
# Rollback and monitor progress
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"

echo "Starting anihpj stack rollback..."
aws cloudformation rollback-stack --stack-name $STACK_NAME

echo ""
echo "Monitoring rollback progress..."
echo "==============================="

while true; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
    --output text)
  
  TIMESTAMP=$(date +%H:%M:%S)
  echo "[$TIMESTAMP] Status: $STATUS"
  
  if [[ "$STATUS" == "UPDATE_ROLLBACK_COMPLETE" ]]; then
    echo ""
    echo "✓ anihpj stack rollback completed successfully"
    break
  elif [[ "$STATUS" == "UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED" ]]; then
    echo ""
    echo "✗ Rollback failed again"
    echo "Check stack events for details"
    break
  fi
  
  sleep 15
done
Starting anihpj stack rollback... Monitoring rollback progress... =============================== [14:30:15] Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS [14:30:30] Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS [14:30:45] Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS [14:31:00] Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_COMPLETE ✓ anihpj stack rollback completed successfully
Example 4: anihpj Stack Rollback with Reason Analysis
#!/bin/bash
# Analyze failure and perform rollback
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"

echo "anihpj Stack Failure Analysis & Rollback"
echo "=========================================="

# Get current status
STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
  --output text)

echo "Current Status: $STATUS"
echo ""

if [ "$STATUS" == "UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED" ]; then
  # Get failure reasons
  echo "Failure Reasons:"
  aws cloudformation describe-stack-events \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --query 'StackEvents[?ResourceStatus==`UPDATE_FAILED`].[LogicalResourceId,ResourceStatusReason]' \
    --output table
  
  echo ""
  read -p "Proceed with rollback? (y/n): " CONFIRM
  
  if [ "$CONFIRM" == "y" ]; then
    aws cloudformation rollback-stack --stack-name $STACK_NAME
    echo "Rollback initiated for anihpj stack"
  fi
else
  echo "Stack is not in UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED state"
  echo "Current status: $STATUS"
fi
anihpj Stack Failure Analysis & Rollback ========================================== Current Status: UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED Failure Reasons: +------------------------+------------------------------------------+ | CustomLambdaFunction | Resource update cancelled | | ApplicationDatabase | Cannot modify master password | +------------------------+------------------------------------------+ Proceed with rollback? (y/n): y Rollback initiated for anihpj stack
Example 5: Automated anihpj Rollback Script
#!/bin/bash
# Automated rollback script for anihpj stacks
rollback_stack() {
  local STACK_NAME=$1
  local MAX_RETRIES=${2:-3}
  local RETRY=0
  
  echo "anihpj Automated Rollback: $STACK_NAME"
  echo "========================================"
  
  while [ $RETRY -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]; do
    STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
      --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
      --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
      --output text 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [ "$STATUS" != "UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED" ]; then
      echo "Stack not in rollback-failed state: $STATUS"
      return 0
    fi
    
    echo "Attempt $((RETRY + 1)) of $MAX_RETRIES"
    
    # Get resources to skip
    SKIP_RESOURCES=$(aws cloudformation describe-stack-resources \
      --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
      --query 'StackResources[?ResourceStatus==`DELETE_FAILED`].LogicalResourceId' \
      --output text)
    
    if [ -n "$SKIP_RESOURCES" ]; then
      echo "Skipping resources: $SKIP_RESOURCES"
      aws cloudformation rollback-stack \
        --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
        --resources-to-skip $SKIP_RESOURCES
    else
      aws cloudformation rollback-stack \
        --stack-name $STACK_NAME
    fi
    
    # Wait for completion
    aws cloudformation wait stack-rollback-complete \
      --stack-name $STACK_NAME 2>/dev/null
    
    NEW_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
      --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
      --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
      --output text)
    
    if [ "$NEW_STATUS" == "UPDATE_ROLLBACK_COMPLETE" ]; then
      echo "✓ Rollback successful"
      return 0
    fi
    
    RETRY=$((RETRY + 1))
  done
  
  echo "✗ Rollback failed after $MAX_RETRIES attempts"
  return 1
}

# Run rollback
rollback_stack "anihpj-django-production" 3
anihpj Automated Rollback: anihpj-django-production ======================================== Attempt 1 of 3 Skipping resources: FailedLambdaFunction ✓ Rollback successful anihpj stack restored to stable state

set-type-configuration

Specifies configuration data for an extension to use in a specific account and Region.

Syntax
aws cloudformation set-type-configuration \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name} \
  --configuration {json-config}
Parameters Description
  • --type - RESOURCE, MODULE, or HOOK
  • --type-name - Name of the extension
  • --configuration - JSON configuration data or file://
Return Value

Returns ConfigurationArn for the applied configuration.

5 Detailed CloudFormation set-type-configuration Examples
Example 1: Configure anihpj Extension Settings
#!/bin/bash
# Set configuration for anihpj custom resource
aws cloudformation set-type-configuration \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Django::Application \
  --configuration '{"DefaultDjangoVersion":"4.2","LogLevel":"INFO","EnableMetrics":true}'

echo "anihpj extension configuration set"
{ "ConfigurationArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type-configuration/resource/anihpj-Django-Application/default" } anihpj extension configuration set
Example 2: Configure MongoDB Atlas for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Configure third-party extension for anihpj project
cat > /tmp/mongodb-config.json << 'EOF'
{
  "PublicKey": "anihpj-mongodb-public-key",
  "PrivateKey": "{{resolve:secretsmanager:anihpj/mongodb:SecretString:privateKey}}",
  "OrgId": "org-abc123"
}
EOF

aws cloudformation set-type-configuration \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name MongoDB::Atlas::Cluster \
  --configuration file:///tmp/mongodb-config.json

echo "MongoDB Atlas configured for anihpj deployment"
{ "ConfigurationArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type-configuration/resource/MongoDB-Atlas-Cluster/default" } MongoDB Atlas configured for anihpj deployment
Example 3: Configure anihpj Extension with Alias
#!/bin/bash
# Set configuration with alias for multiple environments
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"

# Production configuration
aws cloudformation set-type-configuration \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --configuration-alias "PRODUCTION" \
  --configuration '{
    "DefaultDjangoVersion": "4.2",
    "LogLevel": "WARNING",
    "EnableMetrics": true,
    "EnableDebug": false,
    "DatabasePoolSize": 20
  }'

echo "anihpj production configuration set"

# Development configuration
aws cloudformation set-type-configuration \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --configuration-alias "DEVELOPMENT" \
  --configuration '{
    "DefaultDjangoVersion": "4.2",
    "LogLevel": "DEBUG",
    "EnableMetrics": false,
    "EnableDebug": true,
    "DatabasePoolSize": 5
  }'

echo "anihpj development configuration set"
{ "ConfigurationArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type-configuration/resource/anihpj-Django-Application/PRODUCTION" } anihpj production configuration set { "ConfigurationArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type-configuration/resource/anihpj-Django-Application/DEVELOPMENT" } anihpj development configuration set
Example 4: Configure anihpj Hook Settings
#!/bin/bash
# Configure anihpj security hook
aws cloudformation set-type-configuration \
  --type HOOK \
  --type-name anihpj::Security::ValidationHook \
  --configuration '{
    "TargetStacks": "ALL",
