    "FailureMode": "FAIL",
    "Properties": {
      "RequireEncryption": true,
      "AllowedInstanceTypes": ["t3.micro", "t3.small", "t3.medium"],
      "BlockPublicAccess": true,
      "RequireTags": ["Environment", "Project", "Owner"]
    }
  }'

echo "anihpj security hook configured"
echo "Will enforce encryption and tagging requirements"
{ "ConfigurationArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type-configuration/hook/anihpj-Security-ValidationHook/default" } anihpj security hook configured Will enforce encryption and tagging requirements
Example 5: Batch Configure All anihpj Extensions
#!/bin/bash
# Configure all anihpj extensions in batch
echo "Configuring anihpj Extension Suite"
echo "===================================="

# Common settings
COMMON_CONFIG='{
  "LogLevel": "INFO",
  "EnableMetrics": true,
  "ProjectName": "anihpj"
}'

# Extension-specific configurations
declare -A CONFIGS
CONFIGS["anihpj::Django::Application"]='{"DefaultDjangoVersion":"4.2","DatabasePoolSize":10}'
CONFIGS["anihpj::Django::Database"]='{"DefaultEngine":"postgres","AutoBackup":true}'
CONFIGS["anihpj::Django::Cache"]='{"DefaultEngine":"redis","NodeType":"cache.t3.micro"}'

for TYPE_NAME in "${!CONFIGS[@]}"; do
  EXTENSION_CONFIG="${CONFIGS[$TYPE_NAME]}"
  
  # Merge common and extension-specific config
  FULL_CONFIG=$(jq -s '.[0] * .[1]' \
    <(echo "$COMMON_CONFIG") \
    <(echo "$EXTENSION_CONFIG"))
  
  echo ""
  echo "Configuring: $TYPE_NAME"
  
  RESULT=$(aws cloudformation set-type-configuration \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name "$TYPE_NAME" \
    --configuration "$FULL_CONFIG" \
    --output json 2>&1)
  
  if echo "$RESULT" | grep -q "ConfigurationArn"; then
    echo "  ✓ Configured successfully"
  else
    echo "  ✗ Error: $RESULT"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "anihpj extension suite configuration complete"
Configuring anihpj Extension Suite ==================================== Configuring: anihpj::Django::Application ✓ Configured successfully Configuring: anihpj::Django::Database ✓ Configured successfully Configuring: anihpj::Django::Cache ✓ Configured successfully anihpj extension suite configuration complete

set-type-default-version

Sets the default version of an extension to be used in CloudFormation operations.

Syntax
aws cloudformation set-type-default-version \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name} \
  --version-id {version}
Parameters Description
  • --type - RESOURCE, MODULE, or HOOK
  • --type-name - Name of the extension
  • --version-id - Version ID to set as default
Return Value

Returns no content on success, sets specified version as the default.

5 Detailed CloudFormation set-type-default-version Examples
Example 1: Set Default anihpj Extension Version
#!/bin/bash
# Set default version for anihpj extension
aws cloudformation set-type-default-version \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Django::Application \
  --version-id 00000003

echo "anihpj extension default set to version 00000003"
anihpj extension default set to version 00000003 New stacks will use this version
Example 2: Upgrade anihpj Extension to Latest Version
#!/bin/bash
# Find and set latest version as default
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"

echo "Finding latest version for anihpj extension..."

LATEST_VERSION=$(aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --query 'TypeVersionSummaries | sort_by(@, &TimeCreated) | [-1].VersionId' \
  --output text)

CURRENT_DEFAULT=$(aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --query 'TypeVersionSummaries[?IsDefaultVersion==`true`].VersionId' \
  --output text)

echo "Current default: $CURRENT_DEFAULT"
echo "Latest version: $LATEST_VERSION"

if [ "$CURRENT_DEFAULT" != "$LATEST_VERSION" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "Upgrading default to latest..."
  aws cloudformation set-type-default-version \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
    --version-id $LATEST_VERSION
  
  echo "anihpj extension upgraded to $LATEST_VERSION"
else
  echo "Already using latest version"
fi
Finding latest version for anihpj extension... Current default: 00000002 Latest version: 00000003 Upgrading default to latest... anihpj extension upgraded to 00000003
Example 3: Rollback anihpj Extension Version
#!/bin/bash
# Rollback to previous version if issues found
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"
ROLLBACK_VERSION="00000002"

echo "Rolling back anihpj extension to version $ROLLBACK_VERSION"

# Get current version for logging
CURRENT=$(aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --query 'DefaultVersionId' \
  --output text)

echo "Current version: $CURRENT"
echo "Rolling back to: $ROLLBACK_VERSION"

aws cloudformation set-type-default-version \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --version-id $ROLLBACK_VERSION

echo ""
echo "anihpj extension rolled back"
echo "New stacks will now use version $ROLLBACK_VERSION"
echo ""
echo "Note: Existing stacks using version $CURRENT are unaffected"
Rolling back anihpj extension to version 00000002 Current version: 00000003 Rolling back to: 00000002 anihpj extension rolled back New stacks will now use version 00000002 Note: Existing stacks using version 00000003 are unaffected
Example 4: Set Default Version Using ARN
#!/bin/bash
# Set default using version ARN
VERSION_ARN="arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/anihpj-Django-Application/00000003"

aws cloudformation set-type-default-version \
  --arn $VERSION_ARN

echo "anihpj extension default set using ARN"

# Verify
aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --arn $VERSION_ARN \
  --query '{TypeName:TypeName,Version:DefaultVersionId,IsDefault:IsDefaultVersion}' \
  --output table
anihpj extension default set using ARN -------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeType | +------------------+-------------+--------------------------+ | IsDefault | True | | | TypeName | anihpj::Django::Application | | Version | 00000003 | | +------------------+-------------+--------------------------+
Example 5: Update All anihpj Extensions to Latest
#!/bin/bash
# Upgrade all anihpj extensions to their latest versions
echo "Upgrading All anihpj Extensions to Latest"
echo "=========================================="

# Get all anihpj extensions
EXTENSIONS=$(aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --filters TypeNamePrefix=anihpj \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].TypeName' \
  --output text)

for TYPE_NAME in $EXTENSIONS; do
  echo ""
  echo "Processing: $TYPE_NAME"
  
  # Get current and latest versions
  CURRENT=$(aws cloudformation describe-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
    --query 'DefaultVersionId' \
    --output text)
  
  LATEST=$(aws cloudformation list-type-versions \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
    --query 'TypeVersionSummaries | sort_by(@, &TimeCreated) | [-1].VersionId' \
    --output text)
  
  if [ "$CURRENT" == "$LATEST" ]; then
    echo "  Already at latest ($CURRENT)"
  else
    echo "  Upgrading: $CURRENT -> $LATEST"
    aws cloudformation set-type-default-version \
      --type RESOURCE \
      --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
      --version-id $LATEST
    echo "  ✓ Upgraded"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "anihpj extension suite upgrade complete"
Upgrading All anihpj Extensions to Latest ========================================== Processing: anihpj::Django::Application Upgrading: 00000002 -> 00000003 ✓ Upgraded Processing: anihpj::Django::Database Already at latest (00000002) Processing: anihpj::Django::Cache Upgrading: 00000001 -> 00000002 ✓ Upgraded anihpj extension suite upgrade complete

signal-resource

Sends a signal to a resource with a wait condition or CreationPolicy.

Syntax
aws cloudformation signal-resource \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --logical-resource-id {resource-id} \
  --unique-id {unique-id} \
  --status SUCCESS
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name - Name of the stack
  • --logical-resource-id - Logical resource ID in the template
  • --unique-id - Unique ID to identify the signal source
  • --status - SUCCESS or FAILURE
Return Value

Returns no content on success, signal is recorded for the wait condition.

5 Detailed CloudFormation signal-resource Examples
Example 1: Signal anihpj Instance Ready
#!/bin/bash
# Signal that anihpj EC2 instance setup is complete
aws cloudformation signal-resource \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-production \
  --logical-resource-id AnihpjWebServer \
  --unique-id $(curl -s http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/instance-id) \
  --status SUCCESS

echo "anihpj instance signaled ready"
anihpj instance signaled ready CloudFormation will proceed with stack creation
Example 2: Signal anihpj ASG Instance Ready
#!/bin/bash
# Signal from user data script after anihpj setup
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"
RESOURCE_ID="AnihpjAutoScalingGroup"
INSTANCE_ID=$(curl -s http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/instance-id)
REGION=$(curl -s http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/placement/region)

# Perform anihpj setup
echo "Setting up anihpj Django application..."
pip install django gunicorn
git clone https://github.com/anihpj/django-app.git /opt/anihpj
cd /opt/anihpj && python manage.py migrate

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "Setup successful, signaling CloudFormation..."
  aws cloudformation signal-resource \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --logical-resource-id $RESOURCE_ID \
    --unique-id $INSTANCE_ID \
    --status SUCCESS \
    --region $REGION
else
  echo "Setup failed, signaling failure..."
  aws cloudformation signal-resource \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --logical-resource-id $RESOURCE_ID \
    --unique-id $INSTANCE_ID \
    --status FAILURE \
    --region $REGION
fi
Setting up anihpj Django application... Installing dependencies... Running migrations... Setup successful, signaling CloudFormation... anihpj instance i-0abc123def signaled SUCCESS
Example 3: Signal anihpj Wait Condition
#!/bin/bash
# Signal wait condition after database migration
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"
WAIT_HANDLE="AnihpjWaitCondition"

echo "Running anihpj database migration..."
cd /opt/anihpj
python manage.py migrate --noinput

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "Migration complete, signaling wait condition..."
  aws cloudformation signal-resource \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --logical-resource-id $WAIT_HANDLE \
    --unique-id "migration-$(date +%s)" \
    --status SUCCESS
  echo "anihpj migration signal sent"
else
  aws cloudformation signal-resource \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --logical-resource-id $WAIT_HANDLE \
    --unique-id "migration-$(date +%s)" \
    --status FAILURE
  echo "Migration failed, failure signaled"
  exit 1
fi
Running anihpj database migration... Applying all migrations... - users: OK - projects: OK - tasks: OK Migration complete, signaling wait condition... anihpj migration signal sent
Example 4: Signal Multiple anihpj Resources
#!/bin/bash
# Signal multiple instances in anihpj cluster
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"
RESOURCE_ID="AnihpjAutoScalingGroup"
INSTANCE_ID=$(curl -s http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/instance-id)

# Track setup progress
signal_progress() {
  local STEP=$1
  local STATUS=$2
  
  echo "Step $STEP: $STATUS"
  
  # Only signal final step
  if [ "$STEP" == "complete" ]; then
    aws cloudformation signal-resource \
      --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
      --logical-resource-id $RESOURCE_ID \
      --unique-id $INSTANCE_ID \
      --status $STATUS
  fi
}

# Setup steps for anihpj
signal_progress "packages" "Installing dependencies..."
pip install -r /opt/anihpj/requirements.txt

signal_progress "migrations" "Running database migrations..."
cd /opt/anihpj && python manage.py migrate

signal_progress "static" "Collecting static files..."
python manage.py collectstatic --noinput

signal_progress "complete" "SUCCESS"

echo "anihpj instance fully configured and signaled"
Step packages: Installing dependencies... Step migrations: Running database migrations... Step static: Collecting static files... Step complete: SUCCESS anihpj instance fully configured and signaled
Example 5: anihpj Health Check Signal Script
#!/bin/bash
# Health check before signaling readiness
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"
RESOURCE_ID="AnihpjWebServer"
INSTANCE_ID=$(curl -s http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/instance-id)
MAX_RETRIES=30
RETRY_INTERVAL=10

echo "anihpj Health Check Script"
echo "=========================="

# Wait for Django to be ready
check_django_health() {
  curl -sf http://localhost:8000/health/ > /dev/null 2>&1
  return $?
}

echo "Waiting for anihpj Django application..."
RETRY=0

while [ $RETRY -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]; do
  if check_django_health; then
    echo "anihpj application healthy!"
    
    # Signal success
    aws cloudformation signal-resource \
      --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
      --logical-resource-id $RESOURCE_ID \
      --unique-id $INSTANCE_ID \
      --status SUCCESS
    
    echo "Success signal sent to CloudFormation"
    exit 0
  fi
  
  RETRY=$((RETRY + 1))
  echo "Health check $RETRY/$MAX_RETRIES failed, retrying in ${RETRY_INTERVAL}s..."
  sleep $RETRY_INTERVAL
done

# Timeout - signal failure
echo "Health check timeout - signaling failure"
aws cloudformation signal-resource \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --logical-resource-id $RESOURCE_ID \
  --unique-id $INSTANCE_ID \
  --status FAILURE

exit 1
anihpj Health Check Script ========================== Waiting for anihpj Django application... Health check 1/30 failed, retrying in 10s... Health check 2/30 failed, retrying in 10s... Health check 3/30 failed, retrying in 10s... anihpj application healthy! Success signal sent to CloudFormation

start-resource-scan

Starts a scan of existing resources in your account for import into CloudFormation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation start-resource-scan
Parameters Description
  • --client-request-token - (Optional) Token for idempotency
Return Value

Returns ResourceScanId to track the scan progress.

5 Detailed CloudFormation start-resource-scan Examples
Example 1: Start Scan for anihpj Resources
#!/bin/bash
# Start resource scan to find anihpj resources for import
aws cloudformation start-resource-scan

echo "Resource scan started for anihpj account"
echo "This will discover resources that can be imported into CloudFormation"
{ "ResourceScanId": "scan-abc123def456" } Resource scan started for anihpj account This will discover resources that can be imported into CloudFormation
Example 2: Start Scan and Monitor Progress
#!/bin/bash
# Start scan and monitor until complete
echo "Starting anihpj resource scan..."

RESULT=$(aws cloudformation start-resource-scan --output json)
SCAN_ID=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.ResourceScanId')

echo "Scan ID: $SCAN_ID"
echo ""
echo "Monitoring scan progress..."

while true; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
    --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
    --query '[Status,PercentageCompleted]' \
    --output text)
  
  SCAN_STATUS=$(echo "$STATUS" | cut -f1)
  PERCENT=$(echo "$STATUS" | cut -f2)
  
  echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] Status: $SCAN_STATUS - ${PERCENT}% complete"
  
  if [ "$SCAN_STATUS" == "COMPLETE" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "anihpj resource scan complete!"
    break
  elif [ "$SCAN_STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
    echo "Scan failed!"
    exit 1
  fi
  
  sleep 30
done

# Show summary
echo ""
echo "anihpj Account Resource Summary:"
aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --query 'ResourceTypes[*]' \
  --output table | head -20
Starting anihpj resource scan... Scan ID: scan-abc123def456 Monitoring scan progress... [14:00:00] Status: IN_PROGRESS - 5% complete [14:00:30] Status: IN_PROGRESS - 25% complete [14:01:00] Status: IN_PROGRESS - 55% complete [14:01:30] Status: IN_PROGRESS - 85% complete [14:02:00] Status: COMPLETE - 100% complete anihpj resource scan complete! anihpj Account Resource Summary: +----------------------------------+ | AWS::EC2::Instance | | AWS::RDS::DBInstance | | AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster | | AWS::Lambda::Function | +----------------------------------+
Example 3: Find anihpj EC2 Resources for Import
#!/bin/bash
# Start scan and find EC2 instances for anihpj import
echo "Scanning for anihpj EC2 resources..."

# Start scan if none in progress
ACTIVE_SCAN=$(aws cloudformation list-resource-scans \
  --scan-status-filter IN_PROGRESS \
  --query 'ResourceScanSummaries[0].ResourceScanId' \
  --output text)

if [ "$ACTIVE_SCAN" == "None" ]; then
  RESULT=$(aws cloudformation start-resource-scan --output json)
  SCAN_ID=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.ResourceScanId')
else
  SCAN_ID=$ACTIVE_SCAN
  echo "Using existing scan: $SCAN_ID"
fi

# Wait for completion
aws cloudformation wait resource-scan-complete \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID 2>/dev/null || sleep 60

# Find EC2 instances
echo ""
echo "anihpj EC2 Instances Found:"
aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --resource-type-prefix "AWS::EC2::Instance" \
  --query 'Resources[*].[ResourceIdentifier.`aws:ec2:instance-id`,ManagedByStack]' \
  --output table
Scanning for anihpj EC2 resources... anihpj EC2 Instances Found: +---------------------+--------------------+ | i-0abc123def456789a | None | | i-0def456ghi789012b | None | | i-0ghi789jkl012345c | anihpj-web-stack | +---------------------+--------------------+ Resources marked 'None' can be imported into CloudFormation
Example 4: Scan for anihpj Database Resources
#!/bin/bash
# Find unmanaged RDS instances for anihpj
echo "Scanning for anihpj database resources..."

# Get latest completed scan
SCAN_ID=$(aws cloudformation list-resource-scans \
  --scan-status-filter COMPLETE \
  --query 'ResourceScanSummaries | sort_by(@, &StartTime) | [-1].ResourceScanId' \
  --output text)

if [ "$SCAN_ID" == "None" ]; then
  echo "No completed scans found. Starting new scan..."
  SCAN_ID=$(aws cloudformation start-resource-scan \
    --query 'ResourceScanId' --output text)
  echo "Waiting for scan to complete..."
  sleep 120
fi

echo "Using scan: $SCAN_ID"
echo ""
echo "anihpj RDS Resources:"
echo "======================"

aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
  --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
  --resource-type-prefix "AWS::RDS" \
  --output json | jq -r '.Resources[] | select(.ManagedByStack == null) | "
Resource: \(.ResourceIdentifier | to_entries | .[0].value)
Type: \(.ResourceType)
Status: Unmanaged (can be imported)
"'
Scanning for anihpj database resources... Using scan: scan-abc123def456 anihpj RDS Resources: ====================== Resource: anihpj-django-db Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance Status: Unmanaged (can be imported) Resource: anihpj-replica-db Type: AWS::RDS::DBInstance Status: Unmanaged (can be imported)
Example 5: Complete anihpj Resource Discovery
#!/bin/bash
# Full resource discovery for anihpj import planning
echo "anihpj Resource Discovery Report"
echo "=================================="
echo "Generated: $(date)"
echo ""

# Start fresh scan
echo "Starting resource scan..."
SCAN_ID=$(aws cloudformation start-resource-scan \
  --query 'ResourceScanId' --output text)

echo "Scan ID: $SCAN_ID"
echo "Waiting for scan to complete (this may take several minutes)..."

# Wait for completion
for i in {1..60}; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-resource-scan \
    --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
    --query 'Status' --output text)
  
  if [ "$STATUS" == "COMPLETE" ]; then
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done

# Generate report
echo ""
echo "RESOURCE SUMMARY"
echo "================"

RESOURCE_TYPES=("AWS::EC2::Instance" "AWS::RDS::DBInstance" "AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster" "AWS::Lambda::Function" "AWS::S3::Bucket")

for TYPE in "${RESOURCE_TYPES[@]}"; do
  COUNT=$(aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
    --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
    --resource-type-prefix "$TYPE" \
    --query 'Resources | length(@)' --output text)
  
  UNMANAGED=$(aws cloudformation list-resource-scan-resources \
    --resource-scan-id $SCAN_ID \
    --resource-type-prefix "$TYPE" \
    --query 'Resources[?ManagedByStack==`null`] | length(@)' --output text)
  
  echo "$TYPE:"
  echo "  Total: $COUNT"
  echo "  Unmanaged: $UNMANAGED"
  echo ""
done

echo "anihpj discovery report complete"
echo "Use list-resource-scan-resources for detailed resource list"
anihpj Resource Discovery Report ================================== Generated: Fri Jan 17 14:15:00 UTC 2026 Starting resource scan... Scan ID: scan-xyz789abc123 Waiting for scan to complete (this may take several minutes)... RESOURCE SUMMARY ================ AWS::EC2::Instance: Total: 8 Unmanaged: 3 AWS::RDS::DBInstance: Total: 4 Unmanaged: 2 AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster: Total: 2 Unmanaged: 1 AWS::Lambda::Function: Total: 15 Unmanaged: 5 AWS::S3::Bucket: Total: 12 Unmanaged: 4 anihpj discovery report complete Use list-resource-scan-resources for detailed resource list

stop-stack-set-operation

Stops an in-progress StackSet operation.

Syntax
aws cloudformation stop-stack-set-operation \
  --stack-set-name {stackset-name} \
  --operation-id {operation-id}
Parameters Description
  • --stack-set-name - Name of the stack set
  • --operation-id - ID of the operation to stop
  • --call-as - (Optional) SELF or DELEGATED_ADMIN
Return Value

Returns no content on success, operation will be stopped after current instance.

5 Detailed CloudFormation stop-stack-set-operation Examples
Example 1: Stop anihpj StackSet Operation
#!/bin/bash
# Stop a running StackSet operation
aws cloudformation stop-stack-set-operation \
  --stack-set-name anihpj-django-infrastructure \
  --operation-id operation-abc123def456

echo "anihpj StackSet operation stop requested"
anihpj StackSet operation stop requested Operation will be stopped after current instance completes
Example 2: Stop Running anihpj Operation
#!/bin/bash
# Find and stop running operation on anihpj StackSet
STACKSET_NAME="anihpj-django-infrastructure"

echo "Finding running operations on anihpj StackSet..."

OPERATION_ID=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operations \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`RUNNING`].OperationId | [0]' \
  --output text)

if [ "$OPERATION_ID" != "None" ] && [ -n "$OPERATION_ID" ]; then
  echo "Found running operation: $OPERATION_ID"
  echo "Stopping operation..."
  
  aws cloudformation stop-stack-set-operation \
    --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
    --operation-id $OPERATION_ID
  
  echo "anihpj operation stop requested"
else
  echo "No running operations found on anihpj StackSet"
fi
Finding running operations on anihpj StackSet... Found running operation: operation-abc123def456 Stopping operation... anihpj operation stop requested
Example 3: Stop All Running anihpj Operations
#!/bin/bash
# Stop all running operations across anihpj StackSets
echo "Stopping All Running anihpj StackSet Operations"
echo "================================================="

STACKSETS=("anihpj-django-infrastructure" "anihpj-django-networking" "anihpj-django-security")

for STACKSET in "${STACKSETS[@]}"; do
  echo ""
  echo "Checking: $STACKSET"
  
  OPERATIONS=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operations \
    --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
    --query 'Summaries[?Status==`RUNNING`].OperationId' \
    --output text 2>/dev/null)
  
  if [ -n "$OPERATIONS" ] && [ "$OPERATIONS" != "None" ]; then
    for OP_ID in $OPERATIONS; do
      echo "  Stopping operation: $OP_ID"
      aws cloudformation stop-stack-set-operation \
        --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
        --operation-id $OP_ID
    done
  else
    echo "  No running operations"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "All anihpj StackSet operations stop requests sent"
Stopping All Running anihpj StackSet Operations ================================================= Checking: anihpj-django-infrastructure Stopping operation: operation-abc123 Checking: anihpj-django-networking No running operations Checking: anihpj-django-security Stopping operation: operation-def456 Stopping operation: operation-ghi789 All anihpj StackSet operations stop requests sent
Example 4: Stop anihpj Operation with Confirmation
#!/bin/bash
# Interactive stop with confirmation
STACKSET_NAME="anihpj-django-infrastructure"

echo "anihpj StackSet Operation Management"
echo "======================================"

# List running operations
echo "Running operations:"
aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operations \
  --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
  --query 'Summaries[?Status==`RUNNING`].[OperationId,Action,CreationTimestamp]' \
  --output table

echo ""
read -p "Enter operation ID to stop (or 'all' for all): " INPUT

if [ "$INPUT" == "all" ]; then
  OPERATIONS=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operations \
    --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
    --query 'Summaries[?Status==`RUNNING`].OperationId' \
    --output text)
  
  for OP_ID in $OPERATIONS; do
    echo "Stopping: $OP_ID"
    aws cloudformation stop-stack-set-operation \
      --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
      --operation-id $OP_ID
  done
else
  aws cloudformation stop-stack-set-operation \
    --stack-set-name $STACKSET_NAME \
    --operation-id $INPUT
  echo "Stopped: $INPUT"
fi

echo ""
echo "anihpj operations stopped"
anihpj StackSet Operation Management ====================================== Running operations: +------------------------+--------+----------------------+ | operation-abc123def456 | UPDATE | 2026-01-17T14:00:00Z | | operation-ghi789jkl012 | CREATE | 2026-01-17T14:05:00Z | +------------------------+--------+----------------------+ Enter operation ID to stop (or 'all' for all): all Stopping: operation-abc123def456 Stopping: operation-ghi789jkl012 anihpj operations stopped
Example 5: anihpj Emergency Operation Shutdown
#!/bin/bash
# Emergency shutdown of all anihpj StackSet operations
echo "!!! anihpj EMERGENCY OPERATION SHUTDOWN !!!"
echo "============================================="
echo ""

# Get all StackSets with anihpj prefix
STACKSETS=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-sets \
  --status ACTIVE \
  --query 'Summaries[?starts_with(StackSetName, `anihpj`)].StackSetName' \
  --output text)

STOPPED_COUNT=0

for STACKSET in $STACKSETS; do
  OPERATIONS=$(aws cloudformation list-stack-set-operations \
    --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
    --query 'Summaries[?Status==`RUNNING`]' \
    --output json)
  
  OP_COUNT=$(echo "$OPERATIONS" | jq 'length')
  
  if [ "$OP_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "StackSet: $STACKSET ($OP_COUNT operations)"
    
    echo "$OPERATIONS" | jq -r '.[].OperationId' | while read OP_ID; do
      aws cloudformation stop-stack-set-operation \
        --stack-set-name $STACKSET \
        --operation-id $OP_ID 2>/dev/null
      echo "  ✓ Stopped: $OP_ID"
      STOPPED_COUNT=$((STOPPED_COUNT + 1))
    done
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "Emergency shutdown complete"
echo "Operations stopped: $STOPPED_COUNT"
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo "1. Investigate the issue"
echo "2. Verify stack instance states"
echo "3. Resume operations when ready"
!!! anihpj EMERGENCY OPERATION SHUTDOWN !!! ============================================= StackSet: anihpj-django-infrastructure (2 operations) ✓ Stopped: operation-abc123 ✓ Stopped: operation-def456 StackSet: anihpj-django-networking (1 operations) ✓ Stopped: operation-ghi789 Emergency shutdown complete Operations stopped: 3 Next steps: 1. Investigate the issue 2. Verify stack instance states 3. Resume operations when ready

test-type

Tests a registered extension to verify it meets CloudFormation resource provider criteria.

Syntax
aws cloudformation test-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name {type-name}
Parameters Description
  • --type - RESOURCE, MODULE, or HOOK
  • --type-name - Name of the extension to test
  • --version-id - (Optional) Specific version to test
  • --log-delivery-bucket - (Optional) S3 bucket for test logs
Return Value

Returns TypeVersionArn for the tested extension version.

5 Detailed CloudFormation test-type Examples
Example 1: Test anihpj Extension
#!/bin/bash
# Run contract tests on anihpj extension
aws cloudformation test-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name anihpj::Django::Application

echo "anihpj extension contract tests initiated"
{ "TypeVersionArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/anihpj-Django-Application/00000003" } anihpj extension contract tests initiated Use describe-type to check test status
Example 2: Test anihpj Extension with Logging
#!/bin/bash
# Test with log delivery for debugging
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"

echo "Testing anihpj extension with logging..."

aws cloudformation test-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --log-delivery-bucket "anihpj-cfn-extension-logs"

echo ""
echo "Test initiated. Monitoring status..."

# Wait and check status
for i in {1..30}; do
  sleep 20
  
  TEST_STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
    --query 'TypeTestsStatus' \
    --output text)
  
  echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] Test status: $TEST_STATUS"
  
  if [ "$TEST_STATUS" == "PASSED" ] || [ "$TEST_STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
    break
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "anihpj extension test complete: $TEST_STATUS"
Testing anihpj extension with logging... Test initiated. Monitoring status... [14:30:00] Test status: IN_PROGRESS [14:30:20] Test status: IN_PROGRESS [14:30:40] Test status: IN_PROGRESS [14:31:00] Test status: PASSED anihpj extension test complete: PASSED
Example 3: Test Specific anihpj Extension Version
#!/bin/bash
# Test specific version before setting as default
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"
VERSION_ARN="arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/anihpj-Django-Application/00000004"

echo "Testing anihpj extension version 00000004..."

aws cloudformation test-type \
  --arn $VERSION_ARN

# Wait for test completion
echo "Waiting for test completion..."
sleep 60

# Check results
aws cloudformation describe-type \
  --arn $VERSION_ARN \
  --query '{
    TypeName: TypeName,
    Version: DefaultVersionId,
    TestStatus: TypeTestsStatus,
    TestStatusDescription: TypeTestsStatusDescription
  }' \
  --output table
Testing anihpj extension version 00000004... { "TypeVersionArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/anihpj-Django-Application/00000004" } Waiting for test completion... ------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeType | +------------------------+----------------------------------------+ | TestStatus | PASSED | | TestStatusDescription | All contract tests passed successfully | | TypeName | anihpj::Django::Application | | Version | 00000004 | +------------------------+----------------------------------------+
Example 4: Test All anihpj Extensions
#!/bin/bash
# Test all anihpj extensions before release
echo "Testing All anihpj Extensions"
echo "==============================="

LOG_BUCKET="anihpj-cfn-extension-logs"

# Get all anihpj private extensions
EXTENSIONS=$(aws cloudformation list-types \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --visibility PRIVATE \
  --filters TypeNamePrefix=anihpj \
  --query 'TypeSummaries[*].TypeName' \
  --output text)

for TYPE_NAME in $EXTENSIONS; do
  echo ""
  echo "Testing: $TYPE_NAME"
  
  aws cloudformation test-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name "$TYPE_NAME" \
    --log-delivery-bucket "$LOG_BUCKET" 2>/dev/null
  
  echo "  Test initiated..."
done

echo ""
echo "All tests initiated. Checking results in 2 minutes..."
sleep 120

echo ""
echo "anihpj Extension Test Results"
echo "==============================="

for TYPE_NAME in $EXTENSIONS; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name "$TYPE_NAME" \
    --query 'TypeTestsStatus' \
    --output text)
  
  if [ "$STATUS" == "PASSED" ]; then
    echo "✓ $TYPE_NAME: PASSED"
  else
    echo "✗ $TYPE_NAME: $STATUS"
  fi
done
Testing All anihpj Extensions =============================== Testing: anihpj::Django::Application Test initiated... Testing: anihpj::Django::Database Test initiated... Testing: anihpj::Django::Cache Test initiated... All tests initiated. Checking results in 2 minutes... anihpj Extension Test Results =============================== ✓ anihpj::Django::Application: PASSED ✓ anihpj::Django::Database: PASSED ✓ anihpj::Django::Cache: PASSED
Example 5: anihpj Extension Test with Failure Analysis
#!/bin/bash
# Test extension and analyze failures
TYPE_NAME="anihpj::Django::Application"
LOG_BUCKET="anihpj-cfn-extension-logs"

echo "anihpj Extension Testing with Analysis"
echo "========================================"

# Start test
echo "Starting contract tests..."
aws cloudformation test-type \
  --type RESOURCE \
  --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
  --log-delivery-bucket $LOG_BUCKET

# Wait for completion
echo "Waiting for tests to complete..."
for i in {1..30}; do
  sleep 30
  
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
    --query 'TypeTestsStatus' \
    --output text)
  
  if [ "$STATUS" != "IN_PROGRESS" ]; then
    break
  fi
done

# Analyze results
echo ""
echo "Test Status: $STATUS"

if [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "Analyzing failure..."
  
  # Get test description
  aws cloudformation describe-type \
    --type RESOURCE \
    --type-name $TYPE_NAME \
    --query 'TypeTestsStatusDescription' \
    --output text
  
  echo ""
  echo "Downloading test logs from S3..."
  
  # List log files
  aws s3 ls "s3://$LOG_BUCKET/$TYPE_NAME/" --recursive | tail -5
  
  echo ""
  echo "Review logs in s3://$LOG_BUCKET/$TYPE_NAME/"
elif [ "$STATUS" == "PASSED" ]; then
  echo "All contract tests passed!"
  echo "Extension is ready for publishing"
fi
anihpj Extension Testing with Analysis ======================================== Starting contract tests... { "TypeVersionArn": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:type/resource/anihpj-Django-Application/00000003" } Waiting for tests to complete... Test Status: PASSED All contract tests passed! Extension is ready for publishing

update-generated-template

Updates a generated template with user-specified resource values.

Syntax
aws cloudformation update-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name {name} \
  --new-generated-template-name {new-name}
Parameters Description
  • --generated-template-name - Name of the generated template to update
  • --new-generated-template-name - (Optional) New name for the template
  • --add-resources - (Optional) Resources to add to the template
  • --remove-resources - (Optional) Resources to remove from the template
Return Value

Returns GeneratedTemplateId for the updated template.

5 Detailed CloudFormation update-generated-template Examples
Example 1: Update anihpj Generated Template Name
#!/bin/bash
# Rename generated template
aws cloudformation update-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name "anihpj-import-template" \
  --new-generated-template-name "anihpj-production-import-v2"

echo "anihpj template renamed successfully"
{ "GeneratedTemplateId": "gen-tpl-abc123" } anihpj template renamed successfully
Example 2: Add Resources to anihpj Template
#!/bin/bash
# Add more resources to generated template
TEMPLATE_NAME="anihpj-production-import"

echo "Adding resources to anihpj template..."

aws cloudformation update-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --add-resources '[
    {
      "ResourceType": "AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster",
      "LogicalResourceId": "AnihpjCacheCluster",
      "ResourceIdentifier": {
        "CacheClusterId": "anihpj-redis-cache"
      }
    },
    {
      "ResourceType": "AWS::Lambda::Function",
      "LogicalResourceId": "AnihpjTaskFunction",
      "ResourceIdentifier": {
        "FunctionName": "anihpj-background-tasks"
      }
    }
  ]'

echo "Resources added to anihpj template"
Adding resources to anihpj template... { "GeneratedTemplateId": "gen-tpl-abc123" } Resources added to anihpj template
Example 3: Remove Resources from anihpj Template
#!/bin/bash
# Remove resources from generated template
TEMPLATE_NAME="anihpj-production-import"

echo "Removing deprecated resources from anihpj template..."

aws cloudformation update-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --remove-resources '["LegacyAnihpjInstance", "OldCacheCluster"]'

echo "Resources removed from anihpj template"

# Verify current resources
echo ""
echo "Remaining resources in template:"
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --query 'Resources[*].LogicalResourceId' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n'
Removing deprecated resources from anihpj template... { "GeneratedTemplateId": "gen-tpl-abc123" } Resources removed from anihpj template Remaining resources in template: AnihpjWebServer AnihpjDatabase AnihpjLoadBalancer AnihpjSecurityGroup
Example 4: Update anihpj Template Configuration
#!/bin/bash
# Update template configuration options
TEMPLATE_NAME="anihpj-production-import"

echo "Updating anihpj template configuration..."

aws cloudformation update-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --template-configuration '{
    "DeletionPolicy": "Retain",
    "UpdateReplacePolicy": "Retain"
  }'

echo "Configuration updated"

# Verify configuration
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --query '{
    Name: GeneratedTemplateName,
    Status: Status,
    DeletionPolicy: TemplateConfiguration.DeletionPolicy,
    UpdateReplacePolicy: TemplateConfiguration.UpdateReplacePolicy
  }' \
  --output table
Updating anihpj template configuration... { "GeneratedTemplateId": "gen-tpl-abc123" } Configuration updated -------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeGeneratedTemplate | +----------------------+-------------------------------------+ | DeletionPolicy | Retain | | Name | anihpj-production-import | | Status | COMPLETE | | UpdateReplacePolicy | Retain | +----------------------+-------------------------------------+
Example 5: Complete anihpj Template Update Workflow
#!/bin/bash
# Complete workflow to refine anihpj generated template
TEMPLATE_NAME="anihpj-production-import"

echo "anihpj Template Refinement Workflow"
echo "====================================="

# Step 1: Review current state
echo "Step 1: Current template status"
aws cloudformation describe-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --query '{Status:Status,ResourceCount:TotalResources,Warnings:TotalWarnings}'

# Step 2: Add missing resources
echo ""
echo "Step 2: Adding missing resources..."
aws cloudformation update-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --add-resources '[
    {
      "ResourceType": "AWS::S3::Bucket",
      "LogicalResourceId": "AnihpjStaticBucket",
      "ResourceIdentifier": {"BucketName": "anihpj-static-assets"}
    }
  ]' 2>/dev/null

# Step 3: Remove test resources
echo "Step 3: Removing test resources..."
aws cloudformation update-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --remove-resources '["TestInstance", "DebugBucket"]' 2>/dev/null

# Step 4: Set protection policies
echo "Step 4: Configuring protection..."
aws cloudformation update-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --template-configuration '{"DeletionPolicy":"Retain","UpdateReplacePolicy":"Retain"}'

# Step 5: Rename for clarity
echo "Step 5: Finalizing template name..."
FINAL_NAME="anihpj-production-import-final-$(date +%Y%m%d)"
aws cloudformation update-generated-template \
  --generated-template-name $TEMPLATE_NAME \
  --new-generated-template-name $FINAL_NAME

echo ""
echo "anihpj template refinement complete"
echo "Final template: $FINAL_NAME"
echo ""
echo "Next: Run get-generated-template to download and review"
anihpj Template Refinement Workflow ===================================== Step 1: Current template status { "Status": "COMPLETE", "ResourceCount": 12, "Warnings": 2 } Step 2: Adding missing resources... Step 3: Removing test resources... Step 4: Configuring protection... Step 5: Finalizing template name... anihpj template refinement complete Final template: anihpj-production-import-final-20260117 Next: Run get-generated-template to download and review

update-termination-protection

Updates termination protection for the specified stack.

Syntax
aws cloudformation update-termination-protection \
  --stack-name {stack-name} \
  --enable-termination-protection
Parameters Description
  • --stack-name - Name of the stack
  • --enable-termination-protection - Enable protection
  • --no-enable-termination-protection - Disable protection
Return Value

Returns StackId confirming the protection status change.

5 Detailed CloudFormation update-termination-protection Examples
Example 1: Enable Protection for anihpj Production
#!/bin/bash
# Enable termination protection for production stack
aws cloudformation update-termination-protection \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-production \
  --enable-termination-protection

echo "Termination protection enabled for anihpj production"
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-production/abc123" } Termination protection enabled for anihpj production
Example 2: Disable Protection for anihpj Dev Stack
#!/bin/bash
# Disable termination protection for dev stack (for cleanup)
aws cloudformation update-termination-protection \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-dev \
  --no-enable-termination-protection

echo "Termination protection disabled for anihpj dev"
echo "Stack can now be deleted"
{ "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-dev/def456" } Termination protection disabled for anihpj dev Stack can now be deleted
Example 3: Enable Protection for All anihpj Production Stacks
#!/bin/bash
# Enable protection for all production stacks
echo "Enabling Termination Protection for anihpj Production Stacks"
echo "=============================================================="

# Find all anihpj production stacks
STACKS=$(aws cloudformation list-stacks \
  --stack-status-filter CREATE_COMPLETE UPDATE_COMPLETE \
  --query 'StackSummaries[?contains(StackName, `anihpj`) && contains(StackName, `production`)].StackName' \
  --output text)

for STACK in $STACKS; do
  echo "Enabling protection for: $STACK"
  aws cloudformation update-termination-protection \
    --stack-name $STACK \
    --enable-termination-protection
  echo "  ✓ Protected"
done

echo ""
echo "All anihpj production stacks protected"
Enabling Termination Protection for anihpj Production Stacks ============================================================== Enabling protection for: anihpj-django-production ✓ Protected Enabling protection for: anihpj-database-production ✓ Protected Enabling protection for: anihpj-networking-production ✓ Protected All anihpj production stacks protected
Example 4: Check and Set anihpj Protection Status
#!/bin/bash
# Check protection status and enable if not set
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"

echo "anihpj Stack Protection Check"
echo "==============================="

PROTECTION=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Stacks[0].EnableTerminationProtection' \
  --output text)

echo "Stack: $STACK_NAME"
echo "Current Protection: $PROTECTION"

if [ "$PROTECTION" == "False" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "WARNING: Production stack is not protected!"
  echo "Enabling termination protection..."
  
  aws cloudformation update-termination-protection \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --enable-termination-protection
  
  echo "✓ Protection enabled"
else
  echo "✓ Stack is already protected"
fi
anihpj Stack Protection Check =============================== Stack: anihpj-django-production Current Protection: False WARNING: Production stack is not protected! Enabling termination protection... ✓ Protection enabled
Example 5: anihpj Environment-Based Protection Policy
#!/bin/bash
# Apply protection policy based on environment
echo "anihpj Stack Protection Policy Enforcement"
echo "============================================="

# Get all anihpj stacks
STACKS=$(aws cloudformation list-stacks \
  --stack-status-filter CREATE_COMPLETE UPDATE_COMPLETE \
  --query 'StackSummaries[?contains(StackName, `anihpj`)].StackName' \
  --output text)

PROTECTED=0
UNPROTECTED=0

for STACK in $STACKS; do
  # Determine environment from stack name
  if echo "$STACK" | grep -qE "(production|prod)"; then
    ENV="production"
    SHOULD_PROTECT=true
  elif echo "$STACK" | grep -qE "(staging|stage)"; then
    ENV="staging"
    SHOULD_PROTECT=true
  else
    ENV="development"
    SHOULD_PROTECT=false
  fi
  
  CURRENT=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --stack-name $STACK \
    --query 'Stacks[0].EnableTerminationProtection' \
    --output text 2>/dev/null)
  
  echo ""
  echo "Stack: $STACK"
  echo "  Environment: $ENV"
  echo "  Current: $CURRENT"
  
  if [ "$SHOULD_PROTECT" == "true" ] && [ "$CURRENT" == "False" ]; then
    echo "  Action: Enabling protection..."
    aws cloudformation update-termination-protection \
      --stack-name $STACK \
      --enable-termination-protection
    echo "  ✓ Protected"
    PROTECTED=$((PROTECTED + 1))
  elif [ "$SHOULD_PROTECT" == "false" ] && [ "$CURRENT" == "True" ]; then
    echo "  Action: Removing protection (dev stack)..."
    aws cloudformation update-termination-protection \
      --stack-name $STACK \
      --no-enable-termination-protection
    echo "  ✓ Protection removed"
    UNPROTECTED=$((UNPROTECTED + 1))
  else
    echo "  Action: No change needed"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "anihpj Protection Policy Summary"
echo "=================================="
echo "Stacks protected: $PROTECTED"
echo "Protection removed: $UNPROTECTED"
anihpj Stack Protection Policy Enforcement ============================================= Stack: anihpj-django-production Environment: production Current: False Action: Enabling protection... ✓ Protected Stack: anihpj-django-staging Environment: staging Current: True Action: No change needed Stack: anihpj-django-dev Environment: development Current: True Action: Removing protection (dev stack)... ✓ Protection removed anihpj Protection Policy Summary ================================== Stacks protected: 1 Protection removed: 1

wait

Wait until a particular condition is satisfied for CloudFormation operations.

Syntax
aws cloudformation wait stack-create-complete \
  --stack-name {stack-name}
Parameters Description
  • stack-create-complete - Wait until stack creation completes
  • stack-update-complete - Wait until stack update completes
  • stack-delete-complete - Wait until stack is deleted
  • --stack-name - Name or ID of the stack
Return Value

Returns when condition is met or times out after 120 polling attempts.

5 Detailed CloudFormation wait Examples
Example 1: Wait for anihpj Stack Creation
#!/bin/bash
# Wait for anihpj stack to be created
echo "Creating anihpj stack..."
aws cloudformation create-stack \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-production \
  --template-body file://anihpj-infrastructure.yaml

echo "Waiting for stack creation to complete..."
aws cloudformation wait stack-create-complete \
  --stack-name anihpj-django-production

echo "anihpj stack created successfully!"
Creating anihpj stack... { "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-production/abc123" } Waiting for stack creation to complete... anihpj stack created successfully!
Example 2: Wait for anihpj Stack Update
#!/bin/bash
# Wait for stack update to complete
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"

echo "Updating anihpj stack..."
aws cloudformation update-stack \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --template-body file://anihpj-infrastructure-v2.yaml \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM

echo "Waiting for update to complete..."
aws cloudformation wait stack-update-complete \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "anihpj stack updated successfully!"
else
  echo "Stack update failed!"
  exit 1
fi
Updating anihpj stack... { "StackId": "arn:aws:cloudformation:us-east-1:123456789012:stack/anihpj-django-production/abc123" } Waiting for update to complete... anihpj stack updated successfully!
Example 3: Wait for anihpj Stack Deletion
#!/bin/bash
# Wait for stack deletion (cleanup)
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-dev"

echo "Deleting anihpj dev stack..."
aws cloudformation delete-stack --stack-name $STACK_NAME

echo "Waiting for deletion to complete..."
aws cloudformation wait stack-delete-complete \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "anihpj dev stack deleted successfully!"
else
  echo "Warning: Stack may not have been fully deleted"
fi
Deleting anihpj dev stack... Waiting for deletion to complete... anihpj dev stack deleted successfully!
Example 4: anihpj Deployment with All Wait Conditions
#!/bin/bash
# Complete deployment workflow with wait conditions
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"
TEMPLATE_FILE="anihpj-infrastructure.yaml"

echo "anihpj Full Deployment Workflow"
echo "================================="

# Check if stack exists
STACK_EXISTS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME 2>/dev/null && echo "yes" || echo "no")

if [ "$STACK_EXISTS" == "yes" ]; then
  echo "Stack exists - performing update..."
  
  aws cloudformation update-stack \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --template-body file://$TEMPLATE_FILE \
    --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM 2>/dev/null
  
  if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "Waiting for update..."
    aws cloudformation wait stack-update-complete \
      --stack-name $STACK_NAME
    ACTION="updated"
  else
    echo "No updates to perform"
    ACTION="unchanged"
  fi
else
  echo "Stack does not exist - creating..."
  
  aws cloudformation create-stack \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --template-body file://$TEMPLATE_FILE \
    --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM
  
  echo "Waiting for creation..."
  aws cloudformation wait stack-create-complete \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME
  ACTION="created"
fi

echo ""
echo "anihpj stack $ACTION successfully!"

# Show outputs
echo ""
echo "Stack Outputs:"
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --query 'Stacks[0].Outputs[*].[OutputKey,OutputValue]' \
  --output table
anihpj Full Deployment Workflow ================================= Stack exists - performing update... Waiting for update... anihpj stack updated successfully! Stack Outputs: +------------------+-------------------------------------+ | ApplicationURL | https://anihpj.example.com | | DatabaseEndpoint | anihpj-db.abc123.us-east-1.rds.aws | | LoadBalancerDNS | anihpj-alb-123456.elb.amazonaws.com | +------------------+-------------------------------------+
Example 5: anihpj Wait with Timeout Handling
#!/bin/bash
# Wait with custom timeout and status checking
STACK_NAME="anihpj-django-production"
TIMEOUT=1800  # 30 minutes
INTERVAL=30

echo "anihpj Stack Operation with Timeout"
echo "====================================="

# Start the operation
echo "Starting stack update..."
aws cloudformation update-stack \
  --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
  --use-previous-template \
  --parameters ParameterKey=InstanceType,ParameterValue=t3.medium

ELAPSED=0
while [ $ELAPSED -lt $TIMEOUT ]; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
    --stack-name $STACK_NAME \
    --query 'Stacks[0].StackStatus' \
    --output text)
  
  echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] Status: $STATUS (${ELAPSED}s elapsed)"
  
  case $STATUS in
    *_COMPLETE)
      if [ "$STATUS" == "UPDATE_COMPLETE" ]; then
        echo ""
        echo "✓ anihpj stack update completed successfully!"
        exit 0
      else
        echo ""
        echo "Stack in terminal state: $STATUS"
        exit 0
      fi
      ;;
    *_FAILED|*ROLLBACK*)
      echo ""
      echo "✗ Stack operation failed: $STATUS"
      exit 1
      ;;
    *_IN_PROGRESS)
      sleep $INTERVAL
      ELAPSED=$((ELAPSED + INTERVAL))
      ;;
    *)
      echo "Unexpected status: $STATUS"
      sleep $INTERVAL
      ELAPSED=$((ELAPSED + INTERVAL))
      ;;
  esac
done

echo ""
echo "✗ Timeout waiting for anihpj stack operation"
exit 1
anihpj Stack Operation with Timeout ===================================== Starting stack update... [15:00:00] Status: UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS (0s elapsed) [15:00:30] Status: UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS (30s elapsed) [15:01:00] Status: UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS (60s elapsed) [15:01:30] Status: UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS (90s elapsed) [15:02:00] Status: UPDATE_COMPLETE_CLEANUP_IN_PROGRESS (120s elapsed) [15:02:30] Status: UPDATE_COMPLETE (150s elapsed) ✓ anihpj stack update completed successfully!

AWS Systems Manager

Operational Management for AWS Resources

AWS Systems Manager is a management service that helps you automatically collect software inventory, apply OS patches, create system images, and configure Windows and Linux operating systems. Use these commands to manage your anihpj Django application infrastructure at scale.

Run Command

  • send-command
  • list-commands
  • list-command-invocations
  • get-command-invocation
  • cancel-command

Session Manager

  • start-session
  • terminate-session
  • resume-session
  • describe-sessions
  • get-connection-status

Parameter Store

  • put-parameter
  • get-parameter
  • get-parameters
  • get-parameters-by-path
  • delete-parameter
  • describe-parameters

Documents

  • create-document
  • update-document
  • delete-document
  • list-documents
  • describe-document
  • get-document

send-command

Runs commands on one or more managed instances. Use this to execute scripts, install software, or perform administrative tasks on your anihpj infrastructure.

Syntax
aws ssm send-command \
    --document-name <value> \
    --targets <value> \
    [--parameters <value>] \
    [--timeout-seconds <value>] \
    [--comment <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --document-name - Name of the SSM document to run (e.g., AWS-RunShellScript)
  • --targets - Array of targets (instances) to run the command on
  • --parameters - Parameters for the document (JSON format)
  • --timeout-seconds - Command execution timeout
  • --comment - Description of the command purpose
Return Value

Returns Command object with CommandId, Status, TargetCount, and document details.

5 Detailed SSM send-command Examples
Example 1: Run Shell Script on Tagged Instances
#!/bin/bash
# Run a shell script on all anihpj web servers
aws ssm send-command \
    --document-name "AWS-RunShellScript" \
    --targets "Key=tag:Application,Values=anihpj" \
    --parameters '{"commands":["#!/bin/bash","cd /var/www/anihpj","git pull origin main","python manage.py migrate","sudo systemctl restart gunicorn"]}' \
    --comment "Deploy latest anihpj code"
{ "Command": { "CommandId": "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "DocumentName": "AWS-RunShellScript", "Comment": "Deploy latest anihpj code", "Status": "Pending", "TargetCount": 3, "CompletedCount": 0, "ErrorCount": 0 } }
# Django Models Integration - Tracking SSM Deployments from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime # Create Skills for tracking deployment capabilities python_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Python', level='Expert') django_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Django', level='Advanced') aws_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='AWS SSM', level='Intermediate') print(Skills.objects.all()) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: Python - Expert>, <Skills: Django - Advanced>, <Skills: AWS SSM - Intermediate>]> # Create deployment admin user deploy_user = User.objects.create( username='ssm_deployer', email='deployer@anihpj.com', is_active=True ) deploy_user.skills.add(python_skill, django_skill, aws_skill) print(f"Deployer skills: {list(deploy_user.skills.values_list('name', flat=True))}") # Output: Deployer skills: ['Python', 'Django', 'AWS SSM'] # Create Author for deployment documentation deploy_author = Author.objects.create( user=deploy_user, bio='DevOps Engineer responsible for anihpj SSM deployments', company='anihpj' ) print(deploy_author) # Output: <Author: ssm_deployer at anihpj> # Track deployment target locations nyc_location = Location.objects.create(city='New York', state='NY', country='USA') sf_location = Location.objects.create(city='San Francisco', state='CA', country='USA') print(Location.objects.filter(country='USA').count()) # Output: 2 # Create JobPost for the deployment task deployment_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='SSM Deployment - Django Application Update', author=deploy_author, location=nyc_location, description='Deploy latest anihpj code via AWS SSM send-command', job_type='Contract', salary_min=0, salary_max=0, is_active=True ) deployment_job.required_skills.add(python_skill, django_skill, aws_skill) print(f"JobPost: {deployment_job.title}") print(f"Required Skills: {list(deployment_job.required_skills.values_list('name', flat=True))}") # Output: JobPost: SSM Deployment - Django Application Update # Output: Required Skills: ['Python', 'Django', 'AWS SSM']
Example 2: Install Package on Multiple Instances
#!/bin/bash
# Install nginx on specific instances for anihpj load balancing
aws ssm send-command \
    --document-name "AWS-RunShellScript" \
    --instance-ids "i-0abc123def456" "i-0def456abc789" \
    --parameters '{"commands":["sudo apt-get update","sudo apt-get install -y nginx","sudo systemctl enable nginx"]}' \
    --timeout-seconds 300 \
    --comment "Install nginx for anihpj"
{ "Command": { "CommandId": "def45678-5678-90ab-cdef-123456789abc", "DocumentName": "AWS-RunShellScript", "Status": "Pending", "TargetCount": 2, "RequestedDateTime": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z" } }
# Django Models Integration - Nginx Load Balancer Setup from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create infrastructure skills nginx_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Nginx', level='Expert') loadbalancer_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Load Balancing', level='Advanced') linux_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Linux Admin', level='Expert') print(Skills.objects.filter(level='Expert')) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: Nginx - Expert>, <Skills: Linux Admin - Expert>]> # Create infrastructure engineer infra_user = User.objects.create( username='infra_engineer', email='infra@anihpj.com', is_active=True ) infra_user.skills.add(nginx_skill, loadbalancer_skill, linux_skill) # Create Author profile infra_author = Author.objects.create( user=infra_user, bio='Infrastructure engineer specializing in high-availability setups', company='anihpj' ) print(f"Author: {infra_author.user.username}, Company: {infra_author.company}") # Output: Author: infra_engineer, Company: anihpj # Define server locations east_location = Location.objects.create(city='Virginia', state='VA', country='USA') west_location = Location.objects.create(city='Oregon', state='OR', country='USA') print(Location.objects.all().values('city', 'state')) # Output: <QuerySet [{'city': 'Virginia', 'state': 'VA'}, {'city': 'Oregon', 'state': 'OR'}]> # Create load balancer setup JobPost lb_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Nginx Load Balancer Installation - 2 Instance Setup', author=infra_author, location=east_location, description='Install and configure nginx on i-0abc123def456 and i-0def456abc789 for anihpj load balancing', job_type='Full-time', salary_min=80000, salary_max=120000, is_active=True ) lb_job.required_skills.add(nginx_skill, loadbalancer_skill, linux_skill) print(f"Job: {lb_job.title}") print(f"Skills needed: {lb_job.required_skills.count()}") # Output: Job: Nginx Load Balancer Installation - 2 Instance Setup # Output: Skills needed: 3
Example 3: Run PowerShell on Windows Instances
#!/bin/bash
# Run PowerShell commands on Windows instances
aws ssm send-command \
    --document-name "AWS-RunPowerShellScript" \
    --targets "Key=tag:OS,Values=Windows" \
    --parameters '{"commands":["Get-Service | Where-Object {$_.Status -eq \"Running\"}","Write-Host \"anihpj Windows health check complete\""]}' \
    --output-s3-bucket-name "anihpj-ssm-logs" \
    --output-s3-key-prefix "powershell-output"
{ "Command": { "CommandId": "win12345-abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop12345678", "DocumentName": "AWS-RunPowerShellScript", "Status": "Pending", "OutputS3BucketName": "anihpj-ssm-logs", "OutputS3KeyPrefix": "powershell-output" } }
# Django Models Integration - Windows Health Check Monitoring from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create Windows-specific skills powershell_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='PowerShell', level='Expert') windows_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Windows Server', level='Advanced') monitoring_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='System Monitoring', level='Intermediate') print(Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains='Windows')) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: Windows Server - Advanced>]> # Create Windows admin user windows_admin = User.objects.create( username='windows_admin', email='winadmin@anihpj.com', is_active=True ) windows_admin.skills.add(powershell_skill, windows_skill, monitoring_skill) print(f"Windows Admin skills: {windows_admin.skills.count()}") # Output: Windows Admin skills: 3 # Create Author for Windows operations win_author = Author.objects.create( user=windows_admin, bio='Windows Systems Administrator for anihpj hybrid infrastructure', company='anihpj' ) # Location for Windows servers azure_location = Location.objects.create(city='Ashburn', state='VA', country='USA') print(f"Server Location: {azure_location.city}, {azure_location.state}") # Output: Server Location: Ashburn, VA # Create Windows monitoring JobPost win_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Windows Server Health Check - PowerShell Automation', author=win_author, location=azure_location, description='Run PowerShell health checks on Windows instances tagged with OS=Windows', job_type='Contract', salary_min=50000, salary_max=75000, is_active=True ) win_job.required_skills.add(powershell_skill, windows_skill, monitoring_skill) print(JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains='Windows').values('title', 'job_type')) # Output: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Windows Server Health Check - PowerShell Automation', 'job_type': 'Contract'}]>
Example 4: Execute Ansible Playbook
#!/bin/bash
# Run Ansible playbook via SSM for anihpj configuration
aws ssm send-command \
    --document-name "AWS-ApplyAnsiblePlaybooks" \
    --targets "Key=tag:Environment,Values=production" \
    --parameters '{
        "SourceType":["S3"],
        "SourceInfo":["{\"path\":\"https://s3.amazonaws.com/anihpj-ansible/playbooks.zip\"}"],
        "PlaybookFile":["deploy-anihpj.yml"],
        "ExtraVariables":["django_version=4.2"]
    }' \
    --max-concurrency "50%" \
    --max-errors "10%"
{ "Command": { "CommandId": "ansible-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "DocumentName": "AWS-ApplyAnsiblePlaybooks", "Status": "Pending", "MaxConcurrency": "50%", "MaxErrors": "10%" } }
# Django Models Integration - Ansible Deployment Tracking from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create Ansible/DevOps skills ansible_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Ansible', level='Expert') devops_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='DevOps', level='Advanced') iac_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Infrastructure as Code', level='Advanced') print(Skills.objects.filter(name__in=['Ansible', 'DevOps'])) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: Ansible - Expert>, <Skills: DevOps - Advanced>]> # Create DevOps engineer user devops_user = User.objects.create( username='devops_engineer', email='devops@anihpj.com', is_active=True ) devops_user.skills.add(ansible_skill, devops_skill, iac_skill) print(f"DevOps Engineer: {devops_user.username}") # Output: DevOps Engineer: devops_engineer # Create Author profile devops_author = Author.objects.create( user=devops_user, bio='Senior DevOps Engineer managing anihpj production deployments', company='anihpj' ) # Production deployment locations prod_location = Location.objects.create(city='Portland', state='OR', country='USA') print(Location.objects.filter(state='OR').values('city', 'country')) # Output: <QuerySet [{'city': 'Portland', 'country': 'USA'}]> # Create Ansible deployment JobPost ansible_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Ansible Playbook Deployment - Django 4.2 Update', author=devops_author, location=prod_location, description='Execute Ansible playbooks via SSM for anihpj production configuration', job_type='Full-time', salary_min=90000, salary_max=130000, is_active=True ) ansible_job.required_skills.add(ansible_skill, devops_skill, iac_skill) print(JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains='Ansible').count()) # Output: 1
Example 5: Collect Diagnostics with S3 Output
#!/bin/bash
# Collect diagnostic information from anihpj servers
aws ssm send-command \
    --document-name "AWS-RunShellScript" \
    --targets "Key=tag:Application,Values=anihpj" \
    --parameters '{"commands":["df -h","free -m","ps aux | head -20","tail -100 /var/log/anihpj/django.log"]}' \
    --output-s3-bucket-name "anihpj-diagnostics" \
    --output-s3-key-prefix "health-check/$(date +%Y%m%d)" \
    --cloud-watch-output-config '{"CloudWatchLogGroupName":"/aws/ssm/anihpj","CloudWatchOutputEnabled":true}'
{ "Command": { "CommandId": "diag-5678-90ab-cdef-123456789012", "DocumentName": "AWS-RunShellScript", "Status": "Pending", "OutputS3BucketName": "anihpj-diagnostics", "CloudWatchOutputConfig": { "CloudWatchLogGroupName": "/aws/ssm/anihpj", "CloudWatchOutputEnabled": true } } }
# Django Models Integration - Diagnostics Collection from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create diagnostic/monitoring skills diag_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='System Diagnostics', level='Advanced') log_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Log Analysis', level='Expert') cloud_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='CloudWatch', level='Intermediate') print(Skills.objects.filter(level='Expert')) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: Log Analysis - Expert>]> # Create SRE user for diagnostics sre_user = User.objects.create( username='sre_analyst', email='sre@anihpj.com', is_active=True ) sre_user.skills.add(diag_skill, log_skill, cloud_skill) print(f"SRE Skills Count: {sre_user.skills.count()}") # Output: SRE Skills Count: 3 # Create Author profile for SRE sre_author = Author.objects.create( user=sre_user, bio='Site Reliability Engineer for anihpj health monitoring', company='anihpj' ) # Diagnostics collection locations diag_location = Location.objects.create(city='Seattle', state='WA', country='USA') print(f"Diagnostics from: {diag_location}") # Output: Diagnostics from: Seattle, WA, USA # Create diagnostics JobPost diag_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Server Diagnostics Collection - Health Check Automation', author=sre_author, location=diag_location, description='Collect diagnostic info from anihpj servers with S3 and CloudWatch output', job_type='Contract', salary_min=70000, salary_max=95000, is_active=True ) diag_job.required_skills.add(diag_skill, log_skill, cloud_skill) print(JobPost.objects.filter(author__company='anihpj').values_list('title', flat=True)) # Output: <QuerySet ['Server Diagnostics Collection - Health Check Automation']>

list-commands

Lists the commands requested by users of the AWS account. Filter by command ID, instance ID, or status to monitor anihpj deployment progress.

Syntax
aws ssm list-commands \
    [--command-id <value>] \
    [--instance-id <value>] \
    [--filters <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --command-id - Filter by specific command ID
  • --instance-id - Filter commands for a specific instance
  • --filters - Filter by status, document name, or time
  • --max-results - Maximum number of items to return
Return Value

Returns list of Command objects with CommandId, DocumentName, Status, and execution details.

5 Detailed SSM list-commands Examples
Example 1: List All Recent Commands
#!/bin/bash
# List all recent SSM commands for anihpj monitoring
aws ssm list-commands \
    --max-results 20 \
    --query 'Commands[*].{ID:CommandId,Doc:DocumentName,Status:Status,Targets:TargetCount}' \
    --output table
------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListCommands | +----------------------------------+--------------------+----------+ | ID | Doc | Status | +----------------------------------+--------------------+----------+ | abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef123 | AWS-RunShellScript| Success | | def45678-5678-90ab-cdef-456789 | AWS-RunShellScript| InProgress| +----------------------------------+--------------------+----------+
# Django Models Integration - List Recent Commands from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Query skills related to command monitoring ops_skills = Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains='monitoring') print(ops_skills) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: System Monitoring - Intermediate>]> # Get all active users who can run commands active_users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True) print(f"Active command users: {active_users.count()}") # Output: Active command users: 5 # List authors by company authors = Author.objects.filter(company='anihpj') print(authors.values('user__username', 'bio')[:2]) # Output: <QuerySet [{'user__username': 'ssm_deployer', 'bio': 'DevOps engineer...'}, {'user__username': 'infra_engineer', 'bio': 'Infrastructure...'}]> # Get locations for command targets locations = Location.objects.all().order_by('city') print(locations.values_list('city', flat=True)) # Output: <QuerySet ['Ashburn', 'New York', 'Portland', 'San Francisco', 'Seattle']> # List all active job posts jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True).order_by('-created_at') print(jobs.values('title', 'job_type')[:2]) # Output: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Server Diagnostics Collection...', 'job_type': 'Contract'}, {'title': 'Ansible Playbook Deployment...', 'job_type': 'Full-time'}]>
Example 2: Filter by Status
#!/bin/bash
# List failed commands for anihpj troubleshooting
aws ssm list-commands \
    --filters "Key=Status,Value=Failed" \
    --query 'Commands[*].{ID:CommandId,Doc:DocumentName,Error:StatusDetails,Time:RequestedDateTime}'
[ { "ID": "fail-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "Doc": "AWS-RunShellScript", "Error": "Failed", "Time": "2024-01-15T09:00:00.000Z" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Filter Failed Commands from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Find skills needing troubleshooting expertise troubleshoot_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Troubleshooting', level='Expert') print(Skills.objects.filter(level='Expert')) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: Log Analysis - Expert>, <Skills: Troubleshooting - Expert>]> # Get users with troubleshooting skills Users_with_skill = User.objects.filter(skills__name='Troubleshooting') print(f"Troubleshooters: {Users_with_skill.count()}") # Output: Troubleshooters: 1 # Authors who handle failures failure_authors = Author.objects.filter(bio__icontains='monitoring') print(failure_authors.values_list('user__username', flat=True)) # Output: <QuerySet ['sre_analyst']> # Locations with failed deployments failed_locations = Location.objects.filter(city__in=['Seattle', 'Portland']) print(failed_locations) # Output: <QuerySet [<Location: Seattle, WA, USA>, <Location: Portland, OR, USA>]> # Jobs related to failure investigation failure_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(description__icontains='troubleshooting') print(f"Failure investigation jobs: {failure_jobs.count()}") # Output: Failure investigation jobs: 0
Example 3: List Commands for Specific Instance
#!/bin/bash
# List commands executed on anihpj web server
aws ssm list-commands \
    --instance-id "i-0abc123def456789" \
    --max-results 10
{ "Commands": [ { "CommandId": "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "DocumentName": "AWS-RunShellScript", "Status": "Success", "TargetCount": 1, "CompletedCount": 1 } ] }
# Django Models Integration - Commands for Specific Instance from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get skill for single instance management instance_skill = Skills.objects.get_or_create(name='EC2 Management', defaults={'level': 'Advanced'})[0] print(instance_skill) # Output: EC2 Management - Advanced # User managing specific instance instance_admin = User.objects.filter(username='ssm_deployer').first() print(f"Instance admin: {instance_admin.email}") # Output: Instance admin: deployer@anihpj.com # Author for instance operations instance_author = Author.objects.get(user=instance_admin) print(f"Company: {instance_author.company}") # Output: Company: anihpj # Location of the specific instance instance_location = Location.objects.filter(city='New York').first() print(instance_location) # Output: New York, NY, USA # Jobs for instance-specific work instance_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(location=instance_location) print(instance_jobs.values('title')[:1]) # Output: <QuerySet [{'title': 'SSM Deployment Automation - anihpj Django Application'}]>
Example 4: Filter by Document Name
#!/bin/bash
# List all patch commands for anihpj instances
aws ssm list-commands \
    --filters "Key=DocumentName,Value=AWS-RunPatchBaseline" \
    --query 'Commands[?Status==`Success`].{ID:CommandId,Completed:CompletedCount,Errors:ErrorCount}'
[ { "ID": "patch-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "Completed": 5, "Errors": 0 }, { "ID": "patch-5678-90ab-cdef-123456789abc", "Completed": 3, "Errors": 1 } ]
# Django Models Integration - Filter by Document Name from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for patch management patch_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Patch Management', level='Advanced') security_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Security Updates', level='Expert') print(Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains='patch')) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: Patch Management - Advanced>]> # Users with patching expertise patch_users = User.objects.filter(skills__level='Expert') print(f"Expert patchers: {patch_users.distinct().count()}") # Output: Expert patchers: 3 # Authors handling patch operations patch_authors = Author.objects.all()[:3] print(patch_authors.values_list('user__username', flat=True)) # Output: <QuerySet ['ssm_deployer', 'infra_engineer', 'windows_admin']> # Locations needing patches patch_locations = Location.objects.filter(country='USA') print(f"US locations to patch: {patch_locations.count()}") # Output: US locations to patch: 5 # Jobs related to patching patch_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains='automation') print(patch_jobs.values('title', 'salary_max')) # Output: <QuerySet [{'title': 'SSM Deployment Automation...', 'salary_max': 85000}, {'title': 'Server Diagnostics Collection...', 'salary_max': 95000}]>
Example 5: List In-Progress Commands
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor currently running commands on anihpj infrastructure
aws ssm list-commands \
    --filters "Key=Status,Value=InProgress" "Key=Status,Value=Pending" \
    --query 'Commands[*].{ID:CommandId,Doc:DocumentName,Progress:CompletedCount,Total:TargetCount}' \
    --output table
------------------------------------------------------------- | ListCommands | +----------------------------------+-------------------+-----+ | ID | Doc |Total| +----------------------------------+-------------------+-----+ | run-1234-5678-90ab-cdef1234567 | AWS-RunShellScript| 5 | +----------------------------------+-------------------+-----+
# Django Models Integration - In-Progress Commands Monitoring from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for real-time monitoring realtime_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Real-time Monitoring', level='Advanced') print(Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains='monitoring').count()) # Output: 2 # Active users currently running commands active_commanders = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).exclude(username='admin') print(active_commanders.values_list('username', flat=True)) # Output: <QuerySet ['ssm_deployer', 'infra_engineer', 'windows_admin', 'devops_engineer', 'sre_analyst']> # Authors with in-progress work busy_authors = Author.objects.filter(user__is_active=True) print(f"Authors with active work: {busy_authors.count()}") # Output: Authors with active work: 5 # Locations with in-progress commands active_locations = Location.objects.all() for loc in active_locations[:2]: print(f" {loc.city}: Active") # Output: # Ashburn: Active # New York: Active # In-progress job posts active_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True) print(f"Active jobs tracking: {active_jobs.count()}") # Output: Active jobs tracking: 5

list-command-invocations

Returns invocation details for a command sent to target instances. Track individual instance execution status for anihpj deployments.

Syntax
aws ssm list-command-invocations \
    [--command-id <value>] \
    [--instance-id <value>] \
    [--details]
Parameters Description
  • --command-id - ID of the command to get invocations for
  • --instance-id - Filter by specific instance
  • --details - Include command output details
  • --filters - Filter by status or document name
Return Value

Returns list of CommandInvocation objects with per-instance status and output.

5 Detailed SSM list-command-invocations Examples
Example 1: Get Invocations for Specific Command
#!/bin/bash
# Get all invocations for anihpj deployment command
aws ssm list-command-invocations \
    --command-id "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" \
    --query 'CommandInvocations[*].{Instance:InstanceId,Status:Status,Start:RequestedDateTime}'
[ { "Instance": "i-0abc123def456789", "Status": "Success", "Start": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z" }, { "Instance": "i-0def456abc789012", "Status": "Success", "Start": "2024-01-15T10:30:05.000Z" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Get Invocations for Command from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Track invocation-related skills invocation_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Command Invocation', level='Intermediate') print(Skills.objects.all().count()) # Output: 12 # Users who triggered invocations invokers = User.objects.filter(is_active=True)[:2] for user in invokers: print(f"Invoker: {user.username}") # Output: # Invoker: ssm_deployer # Invoker: infra_engineer # Authors for invocation tracking authors = Author.objects.all()[:2] print(authors.values('user__email', 'company')) # Output: <QuerySet [{'user__email': 'deployer@anihpj.com', 'company': 'anihpj'}, {'user__email': 'infra@anihpj.com', 'company': 'anihpj'}]> # Locations of invoked instances invoked_locs = Location.objects.filter(city__in=['New York', 'San Francisco']) print(invoked_locs) # Output: <QuerySet [<Location: New York, NY, USA>, <Location: San Francisco, CA, USA>]> # Jobs tracking invocations invocation_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True)[:2] print(invocation_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)) # Output: <QuerySet ['SSM Deployment Automation - anihpj Django Application', 'Load Balancer Configuration - Nginx Setup']>
Example 2: Get Detailed Output
#!/bin/bash
# Get command output with details for anihpj troubleshooting
aws ssm list-command-invocations \
    --command-id "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" \
    --details \
    --query 'CommandInvocations[*].{Instance:InstanceId,Output:CommandPlugins[0].Output}'
[ { "Instance": "i-0abc123def456789", "Output": "Deployment successful\nMigrations applied: 3\nGunicorn restarted" } ]
