# Django Models Integration - Get Detailed Command Output from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for output analysis analysis_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Output Analysis', level='Advanced') print(f"Skill created: {analysis_skill.name}") # Output: Skill created: Output Analysis # User analyzing detailed output analyst = User.objects.get(username='sre_analyst') print(f"Analyst email: {analyst.email}") # Output: Analyst email: sre@anihpj.com # Author profile for analysis work analyst_author = Author.objects.get(user=analyst) print(f"Bio: {analyst_author.bio[:50]}...") # Output: Bio: Site Reliability Engineer for anihpj health mo... # Location of analyzed instance analysis_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Analysis from: {analysis_loc.city}, {analysis_loc.state}") # Output: Analysis from: New York, NY # Job for output review output_job = JobPost.objects.filter(author=analyst_author).first() print(f"Related job: {output_job.title if output_job else 'None'}") # Output: Related job: Server Diagnostics Collection - Health Check Automation
Example 3: Filter Failed Invocations
#!/bin/bash
# Find failed invocations for anihpj debugging
aws ssm list-command-invocations \
    --command-id "def45678-5678-90ab-cdef-123456789abc" \
    --filters "Key=Status,Value=Failed" \
    --details
{ "CommandInvocations": [ { "CommandId": "def45678-5678-90ab-cdef-123456789abc", "InstanceId": "i-0fail123abc456", "Status": "Failed", "StatusDetails": "Failed", "CommandPlugins": [ { "Name": "aws:runShellScript", "Status": "Failed", "Output": "Error: Permission denied" } ] } ] }
# Django Models Integration - Filter Failed Invocations from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for failure investigation failure_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Failure Analysis', level='Expert') debug_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Debugging', level='Advanced') print(Skills.objects.filter(level='Expert').count()) # Output: 4 # Users handling failures failure_handlers = User.objects.filter(skills__name__icontains='troubleshoot') print(f"Failure handlers: {failure_handlers.count()}") # Output: Failure handlers: 1 # Author for failure documentation failure_author = Author.objects.filter(bio__icontains='reliability').first() print(f"Failure handler: {failure_author.user.username}") # Output: Failure handler: sre_analyst # Locations with failed invocations failed_locs = Location.objects.all() print(failed_locs.values_list('city', flat=True)[:3]) # Output: <QuerySet ['Ashburn', 'New York', 'Portland']> # Jobs for failure remediation remediation_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(description__icontains='troubleshooting') if not remediation_jobs.exists(): print("No remediation jobs found - creating one") # Output: No remediation jobs found - creating one
Example 4: List Invocations for Instance
#!/bin/bash
# List all command invocations on anihpj primary server
aws ssm list-command-invocations \
    --instance-id "i-0abc123def456789" \
    --max-results 10 \
    --query 'CommandInvocations[*].{Cmd:CommandId,Doc:DocumentName,Status:Status}'
[ { "Cmd": "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "Doc": "AWS-RunShellScript", "Status": "Success" }, { "Cmd": "patch-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "Doc": "AWS-RunPatchBaseline", "Status": "Success" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Invocations for Instance from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for instance management instance_mgmt = Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains='EC2') print(instance_mgmt) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: EC2 Management - Advanced>]> # User managing this instance instance_user = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') print(f"Instance manager: {instance_user.email}") # Output: Instance manager: deployer@anihpj.com # Author profile instance_author = Author.objects.get(user=instance_user) print(f"Author company: {instance_author.company}") # Output: Author company: anihpj # Instance location primary_loc = Location.objects.filter(city='New York').first() print(f"Primary server location: {primary_loc}") # Output: Primary server location: New York, NY, USA # Jobs for this instance instance_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(location=primary_loc) print(instance_jobs.values('title', 'job_type')) # Output: <QuerySet [{'title': 'SSM Deployment Automation - anihpj Django Application', 'job_type': 'Full-time'}]>
Example 5: Monitor In-Progress Invocations
#!/bin/bash
# Track ongoing command execution on anihpj fleet
aws ssm list-command-invocations \
    --filters "Key=Status,Value=InProgress" \
    --query 'CommandInvocations[*].{Instance:InstanceId,Cmd:CommandId,Status:StatusDetails}' \
    --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListCommandInvocations | +--------------------+----------------------------------+-----------+ | Instance | Cmd | Status | +--------------------+----------------------------------+-----------+ | i-0abc123def45678 | run-1234-5678-90ab-cdef1234567 | InProgress| +--------------------+----------------------------------+-----------+
# Django Models Integration - Monitor In-Progress Invocations from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for monitoring progress progress_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Progress Monitoring', level='Intermediate') print(f"New skill: {progress_skill}") # Output: New skill: Progress Monitoring - Intermediate # Active users monitoring invocations monitors = User.objects.filter(is_active=True) print(f"Active monitors: {monitors.count()}") # Output: Active monitors: 5 # Authors tracking progress tracking_authors = Author.objects.all() for author in tracking_authors[:2]: print(f" Tracking: {author.user.username}") # Output: # Tracking: ssm_deployer # Tracking: infra_engineer # Locations with in-progress invocations progress_locs = Location.objects.filter(state__in=['NY', 'CA']) print(progress_locs.values_list('city', flat=True)) # Output: <QuerySet ['New York', 'San Francisco']> # Active jobs being monitored monitored_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True).order_by('title') print(f"Jobs in progress: {monitored_jobs.count()}") # Output: Jobs in progress: 6

get-command-invocation

Returns detailed information about command execution on a specific instance. Get full output and status for anihpj command verification.

Syntax
aws ssm get-command-invocation \
    --command-id <value> \
    --instance-id <value> \
    [--plugin-name <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --command-id - (Required) The command ID
  • --instance-id - (Required) The instance ID
  • --plugin-name - Name of the plugin (step) to get details for
Return Value

Returns CommandInvocation with full output, status, execution times, and error details.

5 Detailed SSM get-command-invocation Examples
Example 1: Get Full Command Output
#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed output from anihpj deployment command
aws ssm get-command-invocation \
    --command-id "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" \
    --instance-id "i-0abc123def456789"
{ "CommandId": "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "InstanceId": "i-0abc123def456789", "Status": "Success", "StatusDetails": "Success", "StandardOutputContent": "Pulling from origin main...\nAlready up to date.\nMigrations applied successfully.\nGunicorn restarted.", "StandardErrorContent": "", "ExecutionStartDateTime": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z", "ExecutionEndDateTime": "2024-01-15T10:30:45.000Z" }
# Django Models Integration - Get Full Command Output from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills used in successful deployment deployment_skills = Skills.objects.filter(name__in=['Python', 'Django', 'AWS SSM']) print(deployment_skills) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: Python - Expert>, <Skills: Django - Expert>, <Skills: AWS SSM - Advanced>]> # User who ran successful command deployer = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') print(f"Deployer: {deployer.username} ({deployer.email})") # Output: Deployer: ssm_deployer (deployer@anihpj.com) # Author profile for deployment deployer_author = Author.objects.get(user=deployer) print(f"Company: {deployer_author.company}") # Output: Company: anihpj # Deployment target location target_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Deployed to: {target_loc}") # Output: Deployed to: New York, NY, USA # Related deployment job deploy_job = JobPost.objects.filter(author=deployer_author).first() print(f"Job: {deploy_job.title}") # Output: Job: SSM Deployment Automation - anihpj Django Application
Example 2: Check Failed Command Details
#!/bin/bash
# Get error details for failed anihpj command
aws ssm get-command-invocation \
    --command-id "fail-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" \
    --instance-id "i-0fail123abc456" \
    --query '{Status:Status,Error:StandardErrorContent,Output:StandardOutputContent}'
{ "Status": "Failed", "Error": "fatal: Could not read from remote repository.\nPermission denied (publickey).", "Output": "Attempting git pull..." }
# Django Models Integration - Check Failed Command from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills needed for failure analysis error_skills = Skills.objects.filter(level='Expert') print(f"Expert skills for debugging: {error_skills.count()}") # Output: Expert skills for debugging: 4 # User investigating failure investigator = User.objects.get(username='sre_analyst') print(f"Investigator: {investigator.email}") # Output: Investigator: sre@anihpj.com # Author handling failure report investigator_author = Author.objects.get(user=investigator) print(f"Bio: {investigator_author.bio[:40]}...") # Output: Bio: Site Reliability Engineer for anihpj... # Location of failed instance fail_loc = Location.objects.filter(city='Seattle').first() print(f"Failed instance location: {fail_loc}") # Output: Failed instance location: Seattle, WA, USA # Create incident job post incident_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='SSH Key Failure Investigation', author=investigator_author, location=fail_loc, description='Investigate permission denied error on git pull', job_type='Contract', is_active=True ) print(f"Incident job created: {incident_job.title}") # Output: Incident job created: SSH Key Failure Investigation
Example 3: Get Specific Plugin Output
#!/bin/bash
# Get output from specific plugin step
aws ssm get-command-invocation \
    --command-id "multi-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" \
    --instance-id "i-0abc123def456789" \
    --plugin-name "aws:runShellScript"
{ "CommandId": "multi-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "InstanceId": "i-0abc123def456789", "PluginName": "aws:runShellScript", "Status": "Success", "StandardOutputContent": "anihpj Django server health check passed", "ResponseCode": 0 }
# Django Models Integration - Get Specific Plugin Output from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Plugin-related skills plugin_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='SSM Plugins', level='Advanced') print(f"Created: {plugin_skill}") # Output: Created: SSM Plugins - Advanced # User with plugin expertise plugin_expert = User.objects.filter(skills__name__icontains='SSM').first() print(f"Plugin expert: {plugin_expert.username}") # Output: Plugin expert: ssm_deployer # Author for plugin documentation plugin_author = Author.objects.get(user=plugin_expert) print(f"Author: {plugin_author.user.username} @ {plugin_author.company}") # Output: Author: ssm_deployer @ anihpj # Plugin execution location plugin_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Plugin ran at: {plugin_loc.city}") # Output: Plugin ran at: New York # Health check job health_job = JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains='health').first() print(f"Health job: {health_job.title if health_job else 'None'}") # Output: Health job: Server Diagnostics Collection - Health Check Automation
Example 4: Wait for Command Completion
#!/bin/bash
# Poll for command completion on anihpj instance
COMMAND_ID="abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678"
INSTANCE_ID="i-0abc123def456789"

while true; do
    STATUS=$(aws ssm get-command-invocation \
        --command-id "$COMMAND_ID" \
        --instance-id "$INSTANCE_ID" \
        --query 'Status' --output text)
    
    echo "Status: $STATUS"
    if [[ "$STATUS" == "Success" || "$STATUS" == "Failed" ]]; then
        break
    fi
    sleep 5
done

aws ssm get-command-invocation \
    --command-id "$COMMAND_ID" \
    --instance-id "$INSTANCE_ID"
Status: InProgress Status: InProgress Status: Success { "CommandId": "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "Status": "Success", "StandardOutputContent": "Deployment complete" }
# Django Models Integration - Wait for Command Completion from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import time # Skills for async monitoring async_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Async Operations', level='Advanced') print(f"Skill: {async_skill}") # Output: Skill: Async Operations - Advanced # User waiting for completion waiting_user = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') print(f"Waiting user: {waiting_user.email}") # Output: Waiting user: devops@anihpj.com # Author tracking completion waiting_author = Author.objects.get(user=waiting_user) print(f"Tracker: {waiting_author.company}") # Output: Tracker: anihpj # Polling simulation for status in ['InProgress', 'InProgress', 'Success']: print(f"Polling status: {status}") # Output: # Polling status: InProgress # Polling status: InProgress # Polling status: Success # Completion job update complete_job = JobPost.objects.filter(author=waiting_author).first() if complete_job: print(f"Job completed: {complete_job.title}") # Output: Job completed: Ansible Playbook Deployment - Django 4.2 Update
Example 5: Extract Output URL for Large Results
#!/bin/bash
# Get S3 URL for large command output
aws ssm get-command-invocation \
    --command-id "large-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" \
    --instance-id "i-0abc123def456789" \
    --query '{Status:Status,OutputUrl:StandardOutputUrl,ErrorUrl:StandardErrorUrl}'
{ "Status": "Success", "OutputUrl": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/anihpj-ssm-logs/abc123/i-0abc123def456789/awsrunShellScript/stdout", "ErrorUrl": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/anihpj-ssm-logs/abc123/i-0abc123def456789/awsrunShellScript/stderr" }
# Django Models Integration - Extract Large Output URLs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for S3/large output handling s3_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='S3 Integration', level='Advanced') large_data = Skills.objects.create(name='Large Data Processing', level='Expert') print(Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains='S3')) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: S3 Integration - Advanced>]> # User handling large outputs data_user = User.objects.get(username='sre_analyst') data_user.skills.add(s3_skill, large_data) print(f"Data handler skills: {data_user.skills.count()}") # Output: Data handler skills: 5 # Author for data processing data_author = Author.objects.get(user=data_user) print(f"Data processor: {data_author.user.email}") # Output: Data processor: sre@anihpj.com # S3 output location reference s3_loc = Location.objects.filter(state='VA').first() print(f"S3 region location: {s3_loc}") # Output: S3 region location: Ashburn, VA, USA # Large output processing job large_job = JobPost.objects.filter(description__icontains='S3').first() if large_job: print(f"S3 job: {large_job.title}") # Output: S3 job: Server Diagnostics Collection - Health Check Automation

cancel-command

Attempts to cancel a command that was sent to one or more instances. Use to stop runaway or incorrect commands on anihpj infrastructure.

Syntax
aws ssm cancel-command \
    --command-id <value> \
    [--instance-ids <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --command-id - (Required) ID of the command to cancel
  • --instance-ids - (Optional) Specific instances to cancel on
Return Value

Returns empty response on success. Command status changes to Cancelled.

5 Detailed SSM cancel-command Examples
Example 1: Cancel All Invocations
#!/bin/bash
# Cancel runaway command on all anihpj instances
aws ssm cancel-command \
    --command-id "runaway-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678"

echo "Command cancelled"
Command cancelled
# Django Models Integration - Cancel All Invocations from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for emergency operations emergency_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Emergency Response', level='Expert') print(f"Created: {emergency_skill}") # Output: Created: Emergency Response - Expert # User cancelling command ops_user = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') print(f"Cancelled by: {ops_user.email}") # Output: Cancelled by: devops@anihpj.com # Author for cancellation record ops_author = Author.objects.get(user=ops_user) print(f"Operator: {ops_author.user.username}") # Output: Operator: devops_engineer # Affected locations affected_locs = Location.objects.all() print(f"Cancellation affects {affected_locs.count()} locations") # Output: Cancellation affects 5 locations # Jobs affected by cancellation active_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True) print(f"Active jobs at time of cancel: {active_jobs.count()}") # Output: Active jobs at time of cancel: 7
Example 2: Cancel on Specific Instances
#!/bin/bash
# Cancel command on specific anihpj servers
aws ssm cancel-command \
    --command-id "deploy-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" \
    --instance-ids "i-0abc123def456789" "i-0def456abc789012"

echo "Cancelled on specified instances"
Cancelled on specified instances
# Django Models Integration - Cancel on Specific Instances from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for targeted operations targeted_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Targeted Ops', level='Advanced') print(Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains='Ops')) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: Async Operations - Advanced>, <Skills: Targeted Ops - Advanced>]> # User performing selective cancel selective_user = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') print(f"Selective cancel by: {selective_user.username}") # Output: Selective cancel by: ssm_deployer # Author record selective_author = Author.objects.get(user=selective_user) print(f"Company: {selective_author.company}") # Output: Company: anihpj # Specific locations affected specific_locs = Location.objects.filter(city__in=['New York', 'San Francisco']) print(specific_locs.values_list('city', flat=True)) # Output: <QuerySet ['New York', 'San Francisco']> # Jobs on affected instances affected_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(location__in=specific_locs) print(f"Jobs affected: {affected_jobs.count()}") # Output: Jobs affected: 2
Example 3: Cancel and Verify Status
#!/bin/bash
# Cancel command and verify cancellation
COMMAND_ID="wrong-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678"

aws ssm cancel-command --command-id "$COMMAND_ID"

# Verify cancellation
aws ssm list-commands \
    --command-id "$COMMAND_ID" \
    --query 'Commands[0].{Status:Status,StatusDetails:StatusDetails}'
{ "Status": "Cancelled", "StatusDetails": "Cancelled" }
# Django Models Integration - Cancel and Verify Status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for verification tasks verify_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Status Verification', level='Intermediate') print(f"Skill: {verify_skill}") # Output: Skill: Status Verification - Intermediate # User verifying cancellation verifier = User.objects.get(username='sre_analyst') verifier.skills.add(verify_skill) print(f"Verifier: {verifier.email}") # Output: Verifier: sre@anihpj.com # Author for verification verify_author = Author.objects.get(user=verifier) print(f"Verified by: {verify_author.user.username}") # Output: Verified by: sre_analyst # Verification at location verify_loc = Location.objects.first() print(f"Verification location: {verify_loc}") # Output: Verification location: Ashburn, VA, USA # Update job status after cancellation job_to_update = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True).first() print(f"Job status check: {job_to_update.title[:30]}... is_active={job_to_update.is_active}") # Output: Job status check: SSM Deployment Automation - ani... is_active=True
Example 4: Emergency Cancel All Pending
#!/bin/bash
# Emergency: Cancel all pending commands on anihpj infrastructure
PENDING_COMMANDS=$(aws ssm list-commands \
    --filters "Key=Status,Value=Pending" "Key=Status,Value=InProgress" \
    --query 'Commands[*].CommandId' --output text)

for cmd in $PENDING_COMMANDS; do
    echo "Cancelling: $cmd"
    aws ssm cancel-command --command-id "$cmd"
done
Cancelling: cmd-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678 Cancelling: cmd-5678-90ab-cdef-123456789012
# Django Models Integration - Emergency Cancel All Pending from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for emergency response incident_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Incident Response', level='Expert') print(f"Emergency skill: {incident_skill}") # Output: Emergency skill: Incident Response - Expert # Emergency responder responder = User.objects.filter(skills__level='Expert').first() print(f"Responder: {responder.email}") # Output: Responder: sre@anihpj.com # Responder profile responder_author = Author.objects.get(user=responder) print(f"Emergency handler: {responder_author.company}") # Output: Emergency handler: anihpj # All affected locations all_locs = Location.objects.all() for loc in all_locs[:3]: print(f" Emergency at: {loc.city}") # Output: # Emergency at: Ashburn # Emergency at: New York # Emergency at: Portland # Emergency job tracking emergency_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True) print(f"Jobs during emergency: {emergency_jobs.count()}") # Output: Jobs during emergency: 8
Example 5: Cancel with Error Handling
#!/bin/bash
# Cancel command with error handling for anihpj operations
COMMAND_ID="test-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678"

if aws ssm cancel-command --command-id "$COMMAND_ID" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Successfully cancelled command: $COMMAND_ID"
else
    echo "Failed to cancel or command already completed"
    aws ssm list-commands --command-id "$COMMAND_ID" \
        --query 'Commands[0].Status' --output text
fi
Successfully cancelled command: test-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678
# Django Models Integration - Cancel with Error Handling from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for error handling error_handling = Skills.objects.create(name='Error Handling', level='Advanced') print(f"Skill added: {error_handling}") # Output: Skill added: Error Handling - Advanced # User with error handling expertise handler = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') handler.skills.add(error_handling) print(f"Handler skills: {handler.skills.count()}") # Output: Handler skills: 4 # Handler author profile handler_author = Author.objects.get(user=handler) print(f"Error handler: {handler_author.user.email}") # Output: Error handler: devops@anihpj.com # Error handling location handler_loc = Location.objects.get(city='Portland') print(f"Handler location: {handler_loc}") # Output: Handler location: Portland, OR, USA # Successful cancellation job record success_job = JobPost.objects.filter(author=handler_author).first() print(f"Related job: {success_job.title if success_job else 'None'}") # Output: Related job: Ansible Playbook Deployment - Django 4.2 Update

start-session

Initiates a connection to a target managed instance for interactive shell access. Provides secure, auditable access to anihpj servers without SSH keys.

Syntax
aws ssm start-session \
    --target <value> \
    [--document-name <value>] \
    [--parameters <value>] \
    [--reason <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --target - (Required) Instance ID to connect to
  • --document-name - SSM document for the session type
  • --parameters - Session document parameters
  • --reason - Reason for starting the session (audit purposes)
Return Value

Opens interactive session. Returns SessionId and StreamUrl for connection.

5 Detailed SSM start-session Examples
Example 1: Start Basic Shell Session
#!/bin/bash
# Connect to anihpj web server via Session Manager
aws ssm start-session \
    --target "i-0abc123def456789"
Starting session with SessionId: user-0abc123def456789 $ whoami ssm-user $ cd /var/www/anihpj $ ls manage.py anihpj/ requirements.txt static/
# Django Models Integration - Start Basic Shell Session from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for session management session_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Session Manager', level='Advanced') shell_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Shell Scripting', level='Expert') print(Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains='session')) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: Session Manager - Advanced>]> # User starting session session_user = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') print(f"Session started by: {session_user.email}") # Output: Session started by: deployer@anihpj.com # Author profile session_author = Author.objects.get(user=session_user) print(f"Company: {session_author.company}") # Output: Company: anihpj # Session target location target_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Connected to instance in: {target_loc}") # Output: Connected to instance in: New York, NY, USA # Related deployment job session_job = JobPost.objects.filter(location=target_loc).first() print(f"Session for job: {session_job.title[:40]}...") # Output: Session for job: SSM Deployment Automation - anihpj Dj...
Example 2: Port Forwarding Session
#!/bin/bash
# Forward local port to anihpj database server
aws ssm start-session \
    --target "i-0db123abc456789" \
    --document-name "AWS-StartPortForwardingSession" \
    --parameters '{"portNumber":["5432"],"localPortNumber":["15432"]}'
Starting session with SessionId: user-portfwd-abc123 Port 15432 opened for sessionId user-portfwd-abc123 Waiting for connections...
# Django Models Integration - Port Forwarding Session from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for port forwarding port_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Port Forwarding', level='Advanced') db_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='PostgreSQL', level='Expert') print(Skills.objects.filter(level='Expert').count()) # Output: 7 # User setting up port forward db_user = User.objects.get(username='sre_analyst') db_user.skills.add(port_skill, db_skill) print(f"Port forwarder: {db_user.username}") # Output: Port forwarder: sre_analyst # Author for database operations db_author = Author.objects.get(user=db_user) print(f"DB expert at: {db_author.company}") # Output: DB expert at: anihpj # Database server location db_loc = Location.objects.filter(state='VA').first() print(f"DB location: {db_loc}") # Output: DB location: Ashburn, VA, USA # Database maintenance job db_job = JobPost.objects.filter(description__icontains='database').first() print(f"DB job: {db_job.title if db_job else 'Creating DB maintenance job'}") # Output: DB job: Creating DB maintenance job
Example 3: Session with Audit Reason
#!/bin/bash
# Start session with documented reason for compliance
aws ssm start-session \
    --target "i-0abc123def456789" \
    --reason "Investigating anihpj error logs - Ticket INC-12345"
Starting session with SessionId: user-audit-def456789 Session reason logged: Investigating anihpj error logs - Ticket INC-12345 $
# Django Models Integration - Session with Audit Reason from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for audit compliance audit_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Audit Compliance', level='Advanced') print(f"Audit skill: {audit_skill}") # Output: Audit skill: Audit Compliance - Advanced # User with audit access audit_user = User.objects.get(username='sre_analyst') audit_user.skills.add(audit_skill) print(f"Auditor: {audit_user.email}") # Output: Auditor: sre@anihpj.com # Audit author profile audit_author = Author.objects.get(user=audit_user) print(f"Audit handler: {audit_author.user.username}") # Output: Audit handler: sre_analyst # Audit location audit_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Audited instance at: {audit_loc}") # Output: Audited instance at: New York, NY, USA # Create audit investigation job audit_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Log Investigation - Ticket INC-12345', author=audit_author, location=audit_loc, description='Investigating anihpj error logs for compliance audit', job_type='Contract', is_active=True ) print(f"Audit job created: {audit_job.title}") # Output: Audit job created: Log Investigation - Ticket INC-12345
Example 4: SSH Over Session Manager
#!/bin/bash
# Use Session Manager as SSH transport (requires ~/.ssh/config setup)
# First, ensure ProxyCommand is configured in ~/.ssh/config:
# Host i-* mi-*
#     ProxyCommand sh -c "aws ssm start-session --target %h --document-name AWS-StartSSHSession --parameters 'portNumber=%p'"

ssh -i ~/.ssh/anihpj-key.pem ec2-user@i-0abc123def456789
Last login: Mon Jan 15 10:30:00 2024 from localhost [ec2-user@ip-10-0-1-50 ~]$ cd /var/www/anihpj [ec2-user@ip-10-0-1-50 anihpj]$
# Django Models Integration - SSH Over Session Manager from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for SSH operations ssh_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='SSH Management', level='Expert') proxy_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Proxy Configuration', level='Advanced') print(Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains='SSH')) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: SSH Management - Expert>]> # User with SSH access ssh_user = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') ssh_user.skills.add(ssh_skill, proxy_skill) print(f"SSH user: {ssh_user.email}") # Output: SSH user: devops@anihpj.com # SSH user profile ssh_author = Author.objects.get(user=ssh_user) print(f"SSH configured by: {ssh_author.company}") # Output: SSH configured by: anihpj # SSH target location ssh_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"SSH target: {ssh_loc}") # Output: SSH target: New York, NY, USA # SSH access job ssh_job = JobPost.objects.filter(author=ssh_author).first() print(f"SSH job context: {ssh_job.title[:35]}...") # Output: SSH job context: Ansible Playbook Deployment - Djang...
Example 5: Interactive Commands Session
#!/bin/bash
# Start session with specific shell preferences
aws ssm start-session \
    --target "i-0abc123def456789" \
    --document-name "AWS-StartInteractiveCommand" \
    --parameters '{"command":["bash -l"]}'
Starting session with SessionId: user-interactive-ghi789 [ssm-user@ip-10-0-1-50 ~]$ source /var/www/anihpj/venv/bin/activate (venv) [ssm-user@ip-10-0-1-50 ~]$ python manage.py shell Python 3.11.0 >>> from anihpj.models import User >>> User.objects.count() 1542
# Django Models Integration - Interactive Commands Session from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for interactive Django work django_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Django') interactive_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Interactive Debugging', level='Expert') print(f"Django skill: {django_skill}") # Output: Django skill: Django - Expert # User running interactive session interactive_user = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') interactive_user.skills.add(interactive_skill) print(f"Interactive session by: {interactive_user.email}") # Output: Interactive session by: deployer@anihpj.com # Author for interactive work interactive_author = Author.objects.get(user=interactive_user) print(f"Interactive operator: {interactive_author.company}") # Output: Interactive operator: anihpj # Interactive session location interactive_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Interactive session at: {interactive_loc.city}") # Output: Interactive session at: New York # Count all job posts from shell job_count = JobPost.objects.count() print(f"Total JobPosts in database: {job_count}") # Output: Total JobPosts in database: 9

terminate-session

Permanently ends a Session Manager session. Use to forcibly disconnect sessions or clean up idle connections to anihpj instances.

Syntax
aws ssm terminate-session \
    --session-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --session-id - (Required) The ID of the session to terminate
Return Value

Returns SessionId of the terminated session.

5 Detailed SSM terminate-session Examples
Example 1: Terminate Specific Session
#!/bin/bash
# Terminate an active session on anihpj server
aws ssm terminate-session \
    --session-id "user-0abc123def456789"
{ "SessionId": "user-0abc123def456789" }
# Django Models Integration - Terminate Specific Session from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for session termination terminate_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Session Termination', level='Intermediate') print(f"Skill: {terminate_skill}") # Output: Skill: Session Termination - Intermediate # User terminating session terminator = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') print(f"Terminated by: {terminator.email}") # Output: Terminated by: devops@anihpj.com # Terminator author profile term_author = Author.objects.get(user=terminator) print(f"Operator: {term_author.user.username}") # Output: Operator: devops_engineer # Session termination location term_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Session terminated at: {term_loc}") # Output: Session terminated at: New York, NY, USA # Update job after session termination term_job = JobPost.objects.filter(author=term_author).first() print(f"Related job: {term_job.title[:35]}...") # Output: Related job: Ansible Playbook Deployment - Djang...
Example 2: Terminate All User Sessions
#!/bin/bash
# Terminate all active sessions for a specific user
SESSIONS=$(aws ssm describe-sessions \
    --state "Active" \
    --filters "key=Owner,value=arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/developer" \
    --query 'Sessions[*].SessionId' --output text)

for session in $SESSIONS; do
    echo "Terminating: $session"
    aws ssm terminate-session --session-id "$session"
done
Terminating: user-session-abc123 { "SessionId": "user-session-abc123" } Terminating: user-session-def456 { "SessionId": "user-session-def456" }
# Django Models Integration - Terminate All User Sessions from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for bulk termination bulk_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Bulk Operations', level='Advanced') print(f"Bulk skill: {bulk_skill}") # Output: Bulk skill: Bulk Operations - Advanced # User whose sessions are terminated target_user = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') print(f"Sessions terminated for: {target_user.email}") # Output: Sessions terminated for: deployer@anihpj.com # Admin performing termination admin_user = User.objects.get(username='sre_analyst') admin_author = Author.objects.get(user=admin_user) print(f"Terminated by: {admin_author.user.username}") # Output: Terminated by: sre_analyst # Affected locations affected_locs = Location.objects.all()[:2] for loc in affected_locs: print(f" Session at {loc.city} terminated") # Output: # Session at Ashburn terminated # Session at New York terminated # Jobs affected user_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() print(f"Active jobs during termination: {user_jobs}") # Output: Active jobs during termination: 10
Example 3: Emergency Terminate All Sessions
#!/bin/bash
# Emergency: Terminate all sessions to anihpj production instances
PROD_INSTANCES=$(aws ec2 describe-instances \
    --filters "Name=tag:Environment,Values=production" "Name=tag:Application,Values=anihpj" \
    --query 'Reservations[*].Instances[*].InstanceId' --output text)

for instance in $PROD_INSTANCES; do
    SESSIONS=$(aws ssm describe-sessions \
        --state "Active" \
        --filters "key=Target,value=$instance" \
        --query 'Sessions[*].SessionId' --output text)
    
    for session in $SESSIONS; do
        aws ssm terminate-session --session-id "$session"
        echo "Terminated $session on $instance"
    done
done
Terminated user-prod-abc123 on i-0abc123def456789 Terminated user-prod-def456 on i-0def456abc789012
# Django Models Integration - Emergency Terminate All Sessions from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Emergency response skills emerg_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Emergency Response') print(f"Using skill: {emerg_skill}") # Output: Using skill: Emergency Response - Expert # Emergency responder responder = User.objects.filter(skills=emerg_skill).first() print(f"Emergency responder: {responder.email}") # Output: Emergency responder: devops@anihpj.com # Responder profile responder_author = Author.objects.get(user=responder) print(f"Company: {responder_author.company}") # Output: Company: anihpj # Production locations affected prod_locs = Location.objects.filter(state__in=['NY', 'CA']) print(f"Production locations: {prod_locs.count()}") # Output: Production locations: 2 # Create emergency incident job emerg_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Emergency Session Termination - Production', author=responder_author, location=prod_locs.first(), description='Emergency termination of all production sessions', job_type='Contract', is_active=True ) print(f"Emergency job: {emerg_job.title}") # Output: Emergency job: Emergency Session Termination - Production
Example 4: Terminate with Verification
#!/bin/bash
# Terminate session and verify it's ended
SESSION_ID="user-0abc123def456789"

aws ssm terminate-session --session-id "$SESSION_ID"

# Verify termination
STATUS=$(aws ssm describe-sessions \
    --state "History" \
    --filters "key=SessionId,value=$SESSION_ID" \
    --query 'Sessions[0].Status' --output text)

echo "Session $SESSION_ID status: $STATUS"
{ "SessionId": "user-0abc123def456789" } Session user-0abc123def456789 status: Terminated
# Django Models Integration - Terminate with Verification from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for verification verify_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Status Verification') print(f"Verification skill: {verify_skill}") # Output: Verification skill: Status Verification - Intermediate # User verifying termination verifier = User.objects.get(username='sre_analyst') print(f"Verified by: {verifier.email}") # Output: Verified by: sre@anihpj.com # Verifier profile verify_author = Author.objects.get(user=verifier) print(f"Verifier company: {verify_author.company}") # Output: Verifier company: anihpj # Verification location verify_loc = Location.objects.get(city='Seattle') print(f"Verified at: {verify_loc}") # Output: Verified at: Seattle, WA, USA # Related verification job verify_job = JobPost.objects.filter(author=verify_author).first() print(f"Verification job: {verify_job.title[:40]}...") # Output: Verification job: Server Diagnostics Collection - Health Ch...
Example 5: Terminate Idle Sessions Script
#!/bin/bash
# Terminate sessions idle for more than 30 minutes
IDLE_THRESHOLD=1800  # 30 minutes in seconds
NOW=$(date +%s)

aws ssm describe-sessions --state "Active" --query 'Sessions[*]' | \
jq -r ".[] | select(($NOW - (.StartDate | split(\".\")[0] | strptime(\"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S\") | mktime)) > $IDLE_THRESHOLD) | .SessionId" | \
while read session; do
    echo "Terminating idle session: $session"
    aws ssm terminate-session --session-id "$session"
done
Terminating idle session: user-idle-abc123 { "SessionId": "user-idle-abc123" }
# Django Models Integration - Terminate Idle Sessions from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import datetime # Skills for idle session management idle_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Session Timeout Management', level='Advanced') print(f"Idle management: {idle_skill}") # Output: Idle management: Session Timeout Management - Advanced # User managing idle sessions idle_manager = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') idle_manager.skills.add(idle_skill) print(f"Idle manager: {idle_manager.username}") # Output: Idle manager: devops_engineer # Manager profile idle_author = Author.objects.get(user=idle_manager) print(f"Manager: {idle_author.company}") # Output: Manager: anihpj # Locations with idle sessions idle_locs = Location.objects.all() print(f"Locations checked for idle sessions: {idle_locs.count()}") # Output: Locations checked for idle sessions: 5 # Cleanup job for idle sessions idle_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Idle Session Cleanup Automation', author=idle_author, location=idle_locs.first(), description='Automated cleanup of sessions idle for 30+ minutes', job_type='Full-time', is_active=True ) print(f"Cleanup job: {idle_job.title}") # Output: Cleanup job: Idle Session Cleanup Automation

resume-session

Reconnects to a Session Manager session that was disconnected. Resume work on anihpj servers after network interruption.

Syntax
aws ssm resume-session \
    --session-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --session-id - (Required) The ID of the disconnected session to resume
Return Value

Returns SessionId, StreamUrl, and TokenValue for reconnection.

5 Detailed SSM resume-session Examples
Example 1: Resume Disconnected Session
#!/bin/bash
# Resume a disconnected session to anihpj server
aws ssm resume-session \
    --session-id "user-0abc123def456789"
{ "SessionId": "user-0abc123def456789", "StreamUrl": "wss://ssmmessages.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/data-channel/user-0abc123def456789", "TokenValue": "AAEAAVbH..." }
# Django Models Integration - Resume Disconnected Session from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for session resumption resume_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Session Resumption', level='Intermediate') print(f"Resume skill: {resume_skill}") # Output: Resume skill: Session Resumption - Intermediate # User resuming session resumer = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') print(f"Resuming user: {resumer.email}") # Output: Resuming user: deployer@anihpj.com # Resumer profile resume_author = Author.objects.get(user=resumer) print(f"Company: {resume_author.company}") # Output: Company: anihpj # Resume location resume_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Resumed session at: {resume_loc}") # Output: Resumed session at: New York, NY, USA # Job context for resume resume_job = JobPost.objects.filter(author=resume_author).first() print(f"Resumed job work: {resume_job.title[:30]}...") # Output: Resumed job work: SSM Deployment Automation - a...
Example 2: Resume with Session Plugin
#!/bin/bash
# Resume session (typically handled by session-manager-plugin)
SESSION_ID="user-0abc123def456789"

# Get resume info
RESUME_INFO=$(aws ssm resume-session --session-id "$SESSION_ID")
echo "$RESUME_INFO" | jq '.'

# Plugin automatically reconnects using StreamUrl and Token
{ "SessionId": "user-0abc123def456789", "StreamUrl": "wss://ssmmessages.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/v1/data-channel/user-0abc123def456789", "TokenValue": "AAEAAVbH..." }
# Django Models Integration - Resume with Session Plugin from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Plugin management skills plugin_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='SSM Plugins') print(f"Plugin skill: {plugin_skill}") # Output: Plugin skill: SSM Plugins - Advanced # User with plugin expertise plugin_user = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') print(f"Plugin user: {plugin_user.email}") # Output: Plugin user: devops@anihpj.com # Plugin author plugin_author = Author.objects.get(user=plugin_user) print(f"Author: {plugin_author.user.username}") # Output: Author: devops_engineer # Plugin usage location plugin_loc = Location.objects.get(city='Portland') print(f"Plugin location: {plugin_loc}") # Output: Plugin location: Portland, OR, USA # Plugin-related job plugin_job = JobPost.objects.filter(author=plugin_author).first() print(f"Plugin job: {plugin_job.title}") # Output: Plugin job: Ansible Playbook Deployment - Django 4.2 Update
Example 3: Check and Resume Session
#!/bin/bash
# Check if session is resumable before attempting
SESSION_ID="user-0abc123def456789"

STATUS=$(aws ssm describe-sessions \
    --state "Active" \
    --filters "key=SessionId,value=$SESSION_ID" \
    --query 'Sessions[0].Status' --output text)

if [ "$STATUS" == "Disconnected" ]; then
    echo "Resuming disconnected session..."
    aws ssm resume-session --session-id "$SESSION_ID"
else
    echo "Session status: $STATUS - Cannot resume"
fi
Resuming disconnected session... { "SessionId": "user-0abc123def456789", "StreamUrl": "wss://ssmmessages.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/..." }
# Django Models Integration - Check and Resume Session from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for session checking check_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Session Status Check', level='Intermediate') print(f"Check skill: {check_skill}") # Output: Check skill: Session Status Check - Intermediate # User checking session status checker = User.objects.get(username='sre_analyst') checker.skills.add(check_skill) print(f"Checker: {checker.email}") # Output: Checker: sre@anihpj.com # Checker profile check_author = Author.objects.get(user=checker) print(f"Status checker: {check_author.user.username}") # Output: Status checker: sre_analyst # Check location check_loc = Location.objects.get(city='Seattle') print(f"Check performed at: {check_loc}") # Output: Check performed at: Seattle, WA, USA # Session check job check_job = JobPost.objects.filter(author=check_author).first() print(f"Check job: {check_job.title[:35]}...") # Output: Check job: Server Diagnostics Collection - Hea...
Example 4: Resume Latest Disconnected Session
#!/bin/bash
# Find and resume the most recent disconnected session to anihpj
INSTANCE_ID="i-0abc123def456789"

LATEST_SESSION=$(aws ssm describe-sessions \
    --state "Active" \
    --filters "key=Target,value=$INSTANCE_ID" "key=Status,value=Disconnected" \
    --query 'Sessions | sort_by(@, &StartDate) | [-1].SessionId' \
    --output text)

if [ "$LATEST_SESSION" != "None" ]; then
    echo "Resuming session: $LATEST_SESSION"
    aws ssm resume-session --session-id "$LATEST_SESSION"
else
    echo "No disconnected sessions found"
fi
Resuming session: user-disc-abc123 { "SessionId": "user-disc-abc123", "StreamUrl": "wss://ssmmessages.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/..." }
# Django Models Integration - Resume Latest Disconnected Session from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for latest session retrieval latest_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Session Recovery', level='Advanced') print(f"Recovery skill: {latest_skill}") # Output: Recovery skill: Session Recovery - Advanced # User recovering latest session recoverer = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') recoverer.skills.add(latest_skill) print(f"Recoverer: {recoverer.email}") # Output: Recoverer: deployer@anihpj.com # Recovery author recovery_author = Author.objects.get(user=recoverer) print(f"Recovery by: {recovery_author.company}") # Output: Recovery by: anihpj # Recovery location recovery_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Recovered session at: {recovery_loc}") # Output: Recovered session at: New York, NY, USA # Recovery job recovery_job = JobPost.objects.filter(location=recovery_loc).first() print(f"Recovery context: {recovery_job.title[:30]}...") # Output: Recovery context: SSM Deployment Automation - a...
Example 5: Resume with Error Handling
#!/bin/bash
# Attempt resume with fallback to new session
SESSION_ID="user-0abc123def456789"
INSTANCE_ID="i-0abc123def456789"

if aws ssm resume-session --session-id "$SESSION_ID" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Session resumed successfully"
else
    echo "Cannot resume, starting new session..."
    aws ssm start-session --target "$INSTANCE_ID" \
        --reason "Replacement session - previous session not resumable"
fi
Cannot resume, starting new session... Starting session with SessionId: user-new-ghi789 $
# Django Models Integration - Resume with Error Handling from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Error handling skills error_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Error Handling') fallback_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Fallback Strategies', level='Advanced') print(Skills.objects.filter(level='Advanced').count()) # Output: 15 # User with fallback handling fallback_user = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') fallback_user.skills.add(fallback_skill) print(f"Fallback handler: {fallback_user.email}") # Output: Fallback handler: devops@anihpj.com # Fallback author fallback_author = Author.objects.get(user=fallback_user) print(f"Fallback by: {fallback_author.user.username}") # Output: Fallback by: devops_engineer # Fallback location fallback_loc = Location.objects.get(city='Portland') print(f"Fallback location: {fallback_loc}") # Output: Fallback location: Portland, OR, USA # Create new session job new_session_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='New Session After Resume Failure', author=fallback_author, location=fallback_loc, description='Started new session as fallback', job_type='Contract', is_active=True ) print(f"New job: {new_session_job.title}") # Output: New job: New Session After Resume Failure

describe-sessions

Retrieves information about Session Manager sessions. Monitor active and historical sessions to anihpj infrastructure.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-sessions \
    --state <value> \
    [--filters <value>] \
    [--max-results <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --state - (Required) Session state: Active or History
  • --filters - Filter by Owner, Target, Status, or SessionId
  • --max-results - Maximum sessions to return (1-200)
Return Value

Returns list of Session objects with Owner, Target, Status, and timestamps.

5 Detailed SSM describe-sessions Examples
Example 1: List Active Sessions
#!/bin/bash
# List all active sessions to anihpj infrastructure
aws ssm describe-sessions \
    --state "Active" \
    --query 'Sessions[*].{ID:SessionId,Target:Target,Owner:Owner,Status:Status}' \
    --output table
---------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeSessions | +------------------+---------------------+------------------+---------+ | ID | Target | Owner | Status | +------------------+---------------------+------------------+---------+ | user-abc123 | i-0abc123def456789 | arn:aws:iam:.. | Connected| | user-def456 | i-0def456abc789012 | arn:aws:iam:.. | Connected| +------------------+---------------------+------------------+---------+
# Django Models Integration - List Active Sessions from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for session listing list_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Session Monitoring', level='Intermediate') print(f"Monitoring skill: {list_skill}") # Output: Monitoring skill: Session Monitoring - Intermediate # All active users with sessions active_users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True) print(f"Active users: {active_users.count()}") # Output: Active users: 5 # Authors with active sessions active_authors = Author.objects.all()[:2] for author in active_authors: print(f" {author.user.username}: {author.company}") # Output: # ssm_deployer: anihpj # infra_engineer: anihpj # Locations with active sessions active_locs = Location.objects.filter(state__in=['NY', 'CA']) print(active_locs.values_list('city', flat=True)) # Output: <QuerySet ['New York', 'San Francisco']> # Active jobs during sessions active_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True) print(f"Active jobs: {active_jobs.count()}") # Output: Active jobs: 13
Example 2: Get Session History
#!/bin/bash
# Get session history for audit purposes
aws ssm describe-sessions \
    --state "History" \
    --max-results 20 \
    --query 'Sessions[*].{ID:SessionId,Target:Target,Start:StartDate,End:EndDate,Reason:Reason}'
[ { "ID": "user-hist-abc123", "Target": "i-0abc123def456789", "Start": "2024-01-15T09:00:00.000Z", "End": "2024-01-15T09:45:00.000Z", "Reason": "anihpj deployment verification" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Get Session History from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone from datetime import timedelta # Skills for audit audit_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Audit Compliance') print(f"Audit: {audit_skill}") # Output: Audit: Audit Compliance - Advanced # Users with historical sessions history_users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True) print(f"Users with history: {history_users.count()}") # Output: Users with history: 5 # Audit authors audit_authors = Author.objects.filter(company='anihpj') print(audit_authors.values_list('user__username', flat=True)[:3]) # Output: <QuerySet ['ssm_deployer', 'infra_engineer', 'windows_admin']> # Historical session locations hist_locs = Location.objects.all() print(f"Historical locations: {hist_locs.count()}") # Output: Historical locations: 5 # Get recent jobs (simulating session history) recent_jobs = JobPost.objects.all().order_by('-created_at')[:3] for job in recent_jobs: print(f" Job: {job.title[:30]}...") # Output: # Job: New Session After Resume Failur... # Job: Idle Session Cleanup Automation...
Example 3: Filter Sessions by Instance
#!/bin/bash
# Get all sessions to specific anihpj server
aws ssm describe-sessions \
    --state "Active" \
    --filters "key=Target,value=i-0abc123def456789" \
    --query 'Sessions[*].{Owner:Owner,Status:Status,Duration:DocumentName}'
[ { "Owner": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/developer", "Status": "Connected", "Duration": null } ]
# Django Models Integration - Filter Sessions by Instance from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for instance filtering filter_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Session Filtering', level='Intermediate') print(f"Filter skill: {filter_skill}") # Output: Filter skill: Session Filtering - Intermediate # User filtering sessions filter_user = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') print(f"Filtered by: {filter_user.email}") # Output: Filtered by: deployer@anihpj.com # Filter author filter_author = Author.objects.get(user=filter_user) print(f"Author: {filter_author.company}") # Output: Author: anihpj # Target instance location target_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Target instance at: {target_loc}") # Output: Target instance at: New York, NY, USA # Jobs on this instance instance_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(location=target_loc) print(f"Jobs on instance: {instance_jobs.count()}") # Output: Jobs on instance: 1
Example 4: Filter by Owner
#!/bin/bash
# List sessions owned by specific IAM user
aws ssm describe-sessions \
    --state "Active" \
    --filters "key=Owner,value=arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/admin" \
    --output json
{ "Sessions": [ { "SessionId": "admin-session-abc123", "Target": "i-0abc123def456789", "Status": "Connected", "StartDate": "2024-01-15T10:00:00.000Z", "Owner": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/admin" } ] }
# Django Models Integration - Filter by Owner from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Admin skills admin_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Admin Access', level='Expert') print(f"Admin skill: {admin_skill}") # Output: Admin skill: Admin Access - Expert # Admin user admin_user = User.objects.get(username='sre_analyst') admin_user.skills.add(admin_skill) print(f"Admin: {admin_user.email}") # Output: Admin: sre@anihpj.com # Admin author admin_author = Author.objects.get(user=admin_user) print(f"Admin from: {admin_author.company}") # Output: Admin from: anihpj # Admin location admin_loc = Location.objects.get(city='Seattle') print(f"Admin at: {admin_loc}") # Output: Admin at: Seattle, WA, USA # Filter jobs by admin author admin_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(author=admin_author) print(f"Admin's jobs: {admin_jobs.count()}") # Output: Admin's jobs: 2
Example 5: Session Monitoring Dashboard
#!/bin/bash
# Generate session monitoring report for anihpj
echo "=== Active Sessions ==="
aws ssm describe-sessions --state "Active" \
    --query 'length(Sessions)'

echo -e "\n=== Sessions by Status ==="
aws ssm describe-sessions --state "Active" \
    --query 'Sessions[*].Status' | sort | uniq -c

echo -e "\n=== Recent Session History ==="
aws ssm describe-sessions --state "History" --max-results 5 \
    --query 'Sessions[*].{Target:Target,Duration:(EndDate)}' \
    --output table
=== Active Sessions === 3 === Sessions by Status === 2 Connected 1 Disconnected === Recent Session History === --------------------------------------------- | DescribeSessions | +---------------------+---------------------+ | Target | Duration | +---------------------+---------------------+ | i-0abc123def456789 | 2024-01-15T09:45:00| +---------------------+---------------------+
# Django Models Integration - Session Monitoring Dashboard from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count # Monitoring dashboard skills dashboard_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Dashboard Monitoring', level='Advanced') print(f"Dashboard skill: {dashboard_skill}") # Output: Dashboard skill: Dashboard Monitoring - Advanced # Count users by activity active_count = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() inactive_count = User.objects.filter(is_active=False).count() print(f"Active: {active_count}, Inactive: {inactive_count}") # Output: Active: 5, Inactive: 0 # Authors dashboard author_count = Author.objects.count() print(f"Total authors: {author_count}") # Output: Total authors: 5 # Locations dashboard loc_stats = Location.objects.values('state').annotate(count=Count('id')) for stat in loc_stats[:3]: print(f" {stat['state']}: {stat['count']} location(s)") # Output: # VA: 1 location(s) # NY: 1 location(s) # CA: 1 location(s) # Jobs dashboard job_types = JobPost.objects.values('job_type').annotate(count=Count('id')) for jt in job_types: print(f" {jt['job_type']}: {jt['count']}") # Output: # Full-time: 3 # Contract: 10

get-connection-status

Retrieves the Session Manager connection status for an instance. Verify if anihpj servers are available for Session Manager connections.

Syntax
aws ssm get-connection-status \
    --target <value>
Parameters Description
  • --target - (Required) Instance ID to check connection status
Return Value

Returns Target (instance ID) and Status (connected/notconnected).

5 Detailed SSM get-connection-status Examples
Example 1: Check Single Instance Status
#!/bin/bash
# Check if anihpj web server is ready for Session Manager
aws ssm get-connection-status \
    --target "i-0abc123def456789"
{ "Target": "i-0abc123def456789", "Status": "connected" }
# Django Models Integration - Check Single Instance Status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for connection checking connection_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Connection Monitoring', level='Intermediate') print(f"Connection skill: {connection_skill}") # Output: Connection skill: Connection Monitoring - Intermediate # User checking connection checker = User.objects.get(username='sre_analyst') print(f"Checker: {checker.email}") # Output: Checker: sre@anihpj.com # Checker profile check_author = Author.objects.get(user=checker) print(f"Checking from: {check_author.company}") # Output: Checking from: anihpj # Instance location inst_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Instance at: {inst_loc}") # Output: Instance at: New York, NY, USA # Connection check job check_job = JobPost.objects.filter(location=inst_loc).first() print(f"Connection check for: {check_job.title[:30]}...") # Output: Connection check for: SSM Deployment Automation - a...
Example 2: Check Multiple Instances
#!/bin/bash
# Check connection status for all anihpj instances
INSTANCES="i-0abc123def456789 i-0def456abc789012 i-0ghi789def012345"

for instance in $INSTANCES; do
    STATUS=$(aws ssm get-connection-status \
        --target "$instance" \
        --query 'Status' --output text)
    echo "$instance: $STATUS"
done
i-0abc123def456789: connected i-0def456abc789012: connected i-0ghi789def012345: notconnected
# Django Models Integration - Check Multiple Instances from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Multi-check skills multi_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Bulk Operations') print(f"Multi-check: {multi_skill}") # Output: Multi-check: Bulk Operations - Advanced # User checking multiple multi_user = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') print(f"Multi-checker: {multi_user.email}") # Output: Multi-checker: devops@anihpj.com # Multi-check author multi_author = Author.objects.get(user=multi_user) print(f"Company: {multi_author.company}") # Output: Company: anihpj # Multiple instance locations multi_locs = Location.objects.all()[:3] for loc in multi_locs: print(f" Checking: {loc.city}") # Output: # Checking: Ashburn # Checking: New York # Checking: Portland # Count checks check_count = JobPost.objects.count() print(f"Total checks tracked: {check_count}") # Output: Total checks tracked: 13
Example 3: Pre-Connection Validation
#!/bin/bash
# Validate connection before starting session
INSTANCE_ID="i-0abc123def456789"

STATUS=$(aws ssm get-connection-status \
    --target "$INSTANCE_ID" \
    --query 'Status' --output text)

if [ "$STATUS" == "connected" ]; then
    echo "Instance ready, starting session..."
    aws ssm start-session --target "$INSTANCE_ID"
else
    echo "ERROR: Instance not connected to Session Manager"
    echo "Check SSM Agent status and IAM permissions"
fi
Instance ready, starting session... Starting session with SessionId: user-0abc123def456789 $
# Django Models Integration - Pre-Connection Validation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Validation skills validation_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Status Verification') print(f"Validation: {validation_skill}") # Output: Validation: Status Verification - Intermediate # Validator user validator = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') print(f"Validator: {validator.email}") # Output: Validator: deployer@anihpj.com # Validator profile val_author = Author.objects.get(user=validator) print(f"Validated by: {val_author.company}") # Output: Validated by: anihpj # Validation location val_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Validation at: {val_loc}") # Output: Validation at: New York, NY, USA # Validation job val_job = JobPost.objects.filter(author=val_author).first() print(f"Validation for: {val_job.title[:35]}...") # Output: Validation for: SSM Deployment Automation - aniph...
Example 4: Fleet Connection Health Check
#!/bin/bash
# Check Session Manager connectivity for entire anihpj fleet
INSTANCES=$(aws ec2 describe-instances \
    --filters "Name=tag:Application,Values=anihpj" "Name=instance-state-name,Values=running" \
    --query 'Reservations[*].Instances[*].InstanceId' --output text)

CONNECTED=0
NOT_CONNECTED=0

for instance in $INSTANCES; do
    STATUS=$(aws ssm get-connection-status --target "$instance" --query 'Status' --output text)
    if [ "$STATUS" == "connected" ]; then
        ((CONNECTED++))
    else
        ((NOT_CONNECTED++))
        echo "NOT CONNECTED: $instance"
    fi
done

echo "Summary: $CONNECTED connected, $NOT_CONNECTED not connected"
NOT CONNECTED: i-0problem123abc456 Summary: 4 connected, 1 not connected
# Django Models Integration - Fleet Connection Health Check from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Fleet management skills fleet_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Fleet Management', level='Expert') print(f"Fleet skill: {fleet_skill}") # Output: Fleet skill: Fleet Management - Expert # Fleet manager fleet_mgr = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') fleet_mgr.skills.add(fleet_skill) print(f"Fleet manager: {fleet_mgr.email}") # Output: Fleet manager: devops@anihpj.com # Fleet author fleet_author = Author.objects.get(user=fleet_mgr) print(f"Managing fleet for: {fleet_author.company}") # Output: Managing fleet for: anihpj # Fleet locations fleet_locs = Location.objects.all() print(f"Fleet across {fleet_locs.count()} locations") # Output: Fleet across 5 locations # Fleet jobs fleet_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True) print(f"Active fleet operations: {fleet_jobs.count()}") # Output: Active fleet operations: 13
Example 5: Connection Status Monitoring
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor instance connection status with retry
INSTANCE_ID="i-0abc123def456789"
MAX_RETRIES=10
RETRY_INTERVAL=30

for ((i=1; i<=MAX_RETRIES; i++)); do
    STATUS=$(aws ssm get-connection-status \
        --target "$INSTANCE_ID" \
        --query 'Status' --output text)
    
    echo "Attempt $i: $STATUS"
    
    if [ "$STATUS" == "connected" ]; then
        echo "Instance is now connected!"
        exit 0
    fi
    
    sleep $RETRY_INTERVAL
done

echo "Instance did not connect within timeout"
exit 1
Attempt 1: notconnected Attempt 2: notconnected Attempt 3: connected Instance is now connected!
# Django Models Integration - Connection Status Monitoring from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import time # Retry monitoring skills retry_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Retry Logic', level='Advanced') print(f"Retry skill: {retry_skill}") # Output: Retry skill: Retry Logic - Advanced # Monitor user monitor_user = User.objects.get(username='sre_analyst') monitor_user.skills.add(retry_skill) print(f"Monitor: {monitor_user.email}") # Output: Monitor: sre@anihpj.com # Monitor author monitor_author = Author.objects.get(user=monitor_user) print(f"Monitoring: {monitor_author.company}") # Output: Monitoring: anihpj # Monitoring attempts for i in range(1, 4): print(f"Attempt {i} logged") # Output: # Attempt 1 logged # Attempt 2 logged # Attempt 3 logged # Monitor location monitor_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Monitoring at: {monitor_loc}") # Output: Monitoring at: New York, NY, USA

put-parameter

Add a parameter to Parameter Store. Store configuration data, secrets, and credentials for anihpj Django application.

Syntax
aws ssm put-parameter \
    --name <value> \
    --value <value> \
    --type <value> \
    [--description <value>] \
    [--overwrite]
Parameters Description
  • --name - (Required) Parameter name (supports hierarchy with /)
  • --value - (Required) Parameter value
  • --type - (Required) String, StringList, or SecureString
  • --description - Description of the parameter
  • --overwrite - Overwrite existing parameter
Return Value

Returns Version number and Tier of the parameter.

5 Detailed SSM put-parameter Examples
Example 1: Store Database Connection String
#!/bin/bash
# Store anihpj database connection as SecureString
aws ssm put-parameter \
    --name "/anihpj/production/database/url" \
    --value "postgresql://user:password@rds-endpoint:5432/anihpj_db" \
    --type "SecureString" \
    --description "anihpj production database connection string"
{ "Version": 1, "Tier": "Standard" }
# Django Models Integration - Store Database Connection String from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Skills for parameter management param_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Parameter Store', level='Advanced') db_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='PostgreSQL') print(Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains='Post')) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: PostgreSQL - Expert>]> # User storing parameters param_user = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') param_user.skills.add(param_skill) print(f"Parameter manager: {param_user.email}") # Output: Parameter manager: devops@anihpj.com # Parameter author param_author = Author.objects.get(user=param_user) print(f"Storing for: {param_author.company}") # Output: Storing for: anihpj # Database location db_loc = Location.objects.get(city='Ashburn') print(f"Database location: {db_loc}") # Output: Database location: Ashburn, VA, USA # Create parameter storage job param_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Parameter Store Setup - Database Credentials', author=param_author, location=db_loc, description='Secure storage of database connection strings', job_type='Full-time', is_active=True ) print(f"Parameter job: {param_job.title}") # Output: Parameter job: Parameter Store Setup - Database Credentials
Example 2: Store Django Secret Key
#!/bin/bash
# Store Django SECRET_KEY securely
aws ssm put-parameter \
    --name "/anihpj/production/django/secret_key" \
    --value "django-insecure-abc123xyz789supersecretkey" \
    --type "SecureString" \
    --key-id "alias/anihpj-kms-key" \
    --description "Django SECRET_KEY for anihpj"
{ "Version": 1, "Tier": "Standard" }
# Django Models Integration - Store Django Secret Key from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Django security skills security_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Security Updates') django_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Django') print(f"Django skill: {django_skill.level}") # Output: Django skill: Expert # Security admin sec_admin = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') print(f"Security admin: {sec_admin.email}") # Output: Security admin: deployer@anihpj.com # Security author sec_author = Author.objects.get(user=sec_admin) print(f"Security by: {sec_author.company}") # Output: Security by: anihpj # Production location prod_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Production at: {prod_loc}") # Output: Production at: New York, NY, USA # Security job sec_job = JobPost.objects.filter(description__icontains='secret').first() if not sec_job: print("Creating security job...") # Output: Creating security job...
Example 3: Store Configuration List
#!/bin/bash
# Store allowed hosts as StringList
aws ssm put-parameter \
    --name "/anihpj/production/django/allowed_hosts" \
    --value "anihpj.com,www.anihpj.com,api.anihpj.com" \
    --type "StringList" \
    --description "Django ALLOWED_HOSTS for anihpj"
{ "Version": 1, "Tier": "Standard" }
# Django Models Integration - Store Configuration List from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Configuration skills config_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Configuration Management', level='Advanced') print(f"Config skill: {config_skill}") # Output: Config skill: Configuration Management - Advanced # Config manager config_mgr = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') config_mgr.skills.add(config_skill) print(f"Config manager: {config_mgr.email}") # Output: Config manager: devops@anihpj.com # Config author config_author = Author.objects.get(user=config_mgr) print(f"Config by: {config_author.company}") # Output: Config by: anihpj # Get all locations (like ALLOWED_HOSTS) all_locs = Location.objects.values_list('city', flat=True) print(f"Locations: {list(all_locs)}") # Output: Locations: ['Ashburn', 'New York', 'Portland', 'San Francisco', 'Seattle'] # Config job config_job = JobPost.objects.filter(author=config_author).first() print(f"Config job: {config_job.title[:35]}...") # Output: Config job: Ansible Playbook Deployment - Djang...
Example 4: Update Existing Parameter
#!/bin/bash
# Update anihpj configuration with new value
aws ssm put-parameter \
    --name "/anihpj/production/feature_flags/new_ui" \
    --value "true" \
    --type "String" \
    --overwrite \
    --description "Enable new UI for anihpj"
{ "Version": 2, "Tier": "Standard" }
# Django Models Integration - Update Existing Parameter from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Update/versioning skills update_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Version Control', level='Intermediate') print(f"Update skill: {update_skill}") # Output: Update skill: Version Control - Intermediate # User updating parameters update_user = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') update_user.skills.add(update_skill) print(f"Updater: {update_user.email}") # Output: Updater: deployer@anihpj.com # Update author update_author = Author.objects.get(user=update_user) print(f"Updated by: {update_author.company}") # Output: Updated by: anihpj # Update location update_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Updated at: {update_loc}") # Output: Updated at: New York, NY, USA # Update job with new version update_job = JobPost.objects.filter(author=update_author).first() print(f"Job version: Updated {update_job.title[:25]}...") # Output: Job version: Updated SSM Deployment Automat...
Example 5: Store with Tags and Policies
#!/bin/bash
# Store parameter with tags for anihpj organization
aws ssm put-parameter \
    --name "/anihpj/staging/api/key" \
    --value "staging-api-key-12345" \
    --type "SecureString" \
    --tier "Advanced" \
    --policies '[{"Type":"Expiration","Version":"1.0","Attributes":{"Timestamp":"2024-12-31T23:59:59.000Z"}}]' \
    --tags '[{"Key":"Environment","Value":"staging"},{"Key":"Application","Value":"anihpj"}]'
{ "Version": 1, "Tier": "Advanced" }
# Django Models Integration - Store with Tags and Policies from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Advanced parameter skills advanced_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Advanced Parameter Store', level='Expert') print(f"Advanced: {advanced_skill}") # Output: Advanced: Advanced Parameter Store - Expert # Policy manager policy_mgr = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') policy_mgr.skills.add(advanced_skill) print(f"Policy manager: {policy_mgr.email}") # Output: Policy manager: devops@anihpj.com # Policy author policy_author = Author.objects.get(user=policy_mgr) print(f"Policies by: {policy_author.company}") # Output: Policies by: anihpj # Staging location staging_loc = Location.objects.filter(state='OR').first() print(f"Staging at: {staging_loc}") # Output: Staging at: Portland, OR, USA # Create advanced tier job adv_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Advanced Parameter Store - Policy Management', author=policy_author, location=staging_loc, description='Manage advanced tier parameters with expiration policies', job_type='Contract', is_active=True ) print(f"Advanced job: {adv_job.title}") # Output: Advanced job: Advanced Parameter Store - Policy Management

get-parameter

Get information about a single parameter. Retrieve anihpj configuration values at runtime.

Syntax
aws ssm get-parameter \
    --name <value> \
    [--with-decryption]
Parameters Description
  • --name - (Required) Name of the parameter
  • --with-decryption - Decrypt SecureString values
Return Value

Returns Parameter object with Name, Type, Value, Version, and metadata.

5 Detailed SSM get-parameter Examples
Example 1: Get Simple Parameter
#!/bin/bash
# Get anihpj configuration parameter
aws ssm get-parameter \
    --name "/anihpj/production/feature_flags/new_ui"
{ "Parameter": { "Name": "/anihpj/production/feature_flags/new_ui", "Type": "String", "Value": "true", "Version": 2, "LastModifiedDate": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z", "ARN": "arn:aws:ssm:us-east-1:123456789012:parameter/anihpj/production/feature_flags/new_ui" } }
# Django Models Integration - Get Simple Parameter from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get parameter skills get_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Parameter Store') print(f"Get skill: {get_skill}") # Output: Get skill: Parameter Store - Advanced # User retrieving parameter get_user = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') print(f"Getter: {get_user.email}") # Output: Getter: deployer@anihpj.com # Getter author get_author = Author.objects.get(user=get_user) print(f"Company: {get_author.company}") # Output: Company: anihpj # Parameter location get_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Retrieved at: {get_loc}") # Output: Retrieved at: New York, NY, USA # Feature flag job feature_job = JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains='feature').first() print(f"Feature job: {feature_job.title if feature_job else 'None'}") # Output: Feature job: None
Example 2: Get Decrypted Secret
#!/bin/bash
# Get anihpj database password decrypted
aws ssm get-parameter \
    --name "/anihpj/production/database/url" \
    --with-decryption \
    --query 'Parameter.Value' \
    --output text
postgresql://user:password@rds-endpoint:5432/anihpj_db
# Django Models Integration - Get Decrypted Secret from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Decryption skills decrypt_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Security Updates') print(f"Decrypt: {decrypt_skill}") # Output: Decrypt: Security Updates - Expert # User with decryption access decrypt_user = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') print(f"Decryptor: {decrypt_user.email}") # Output: Decryptor: devops@anihpj.com # Decrypt author decrypt_author = Author.objects.get(user=decrypt_user) print(f"Decryption by: {decrypt_author.company}") # Output: Decryption by: anihpj # Database location db_loc = Location.objects.get(city='Ashburn') print(f"DB at: {db_loc}") # Output: DB at: Ashburn, VA, USA # Database credential job db_job = JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains='Database').first() print(f"DB job: {db_job.title if db_job else 'Creating...'}") # Output: DB job: Parameter Store Setup - Database Credentials
Example 3: Get Parameter for Django Settings
#!/bin/bash
# Export parameter as environment variable
export DATABASE_URL=$(aws ssm get-parameter \
    --name "/anihpj/production/database/url" \
    --with-decryption \
    --query 'Parameter.Value' \
    --output text)

echo "Database URL configured for anihpj"
Database URL configured for anihpj
# Django Models Integration - Get Parameter for Django Settings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import os # Django configuration skills django_config = Skills.objects.get(name='Django') print(f"Django config: {django_config}") # Output: Django config: Django - Expert # Django config user django_user = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') print(f"Django user: {django_user.email}") # Output: Django user: deployer@anihpj.com # Config author django_author = Author.objects.get(user=django_user) print(f"Config by: {django_author.company}") # Output: Config by: anihpj # Production location prod_loc = Location.objects.get(city='New York') print(f"Production at: {prod_loc}") # Output: Production at: New York, NY, USA # Environment export simulation print("DATABASE_URL configured for anihpj") # Output: DATABASE_URL configured for anihpj
Example 4: Get Parameter with Full Metadata
#!/bin/bash
# Get full parameter details for audit
aws ssm get-parameter \
    --name "/anihpj/production/django/secret_key" \
    --query 'Parameter.{Name:Name,Version:Version,Modified:LastModifiedDate,Type:Type}'
{ "Name": "/anihpj/production/django/secret_key", "Version": 1, "Modified": "2024-01-10T08:00:00.000Z", "Type": "SecureString" }
# Django Models Integration - Get Parameter with Full Metadata from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Audit skills audit_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Audit Compliance') print(f"Audit: {audit_skill}") # Output: Audit: Audit Compliance - Advanced # Auditor auditor = User.objects.get(username='sre_analyst') print(f"Auditor: {auditor.email}") # Output: Auditor: sre@anihpj.com # Audit author audit_author = Author.objects.get(user=auditor) print(f"Audit by: {audit_author.company}") # Output: Audit by: anihpj # Audit location audit_loc = Location.objects.get(city='Seattle') print(f"Audit at: {audit_loc}") # Output: Audit at: Seattle, WA, USA # Metadata audit job meta_job = JobPost.objects.filter(author=audit_author).first() print(f"Audit job: {meta_job.title[:35]}...") # Output: Audit job: Server Diagnostics Collection - Hea...
Example 5: Conditional Parameter Fetch
#!/bin/bash
# Get parameter with error handling for anihpj startup
PARAM_NAME="/anihpj/production/api/external_key"

if VALUE=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "$PARAM_NAME" --with-decryption --query 'Parameter.Value' --output text 2>/dev/null); then
    echo "Parameter loaded successfully"
    export EXTERNAL_API_KEY="$VALUE"
else
    echo "ERROR: Failed to load parameter $PARAM_NAME"
    exit 1
fi
Parameter loaded successfully
