# Django Models Integration - Conditional Parameter Fetch from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Error handling skills error_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Error Handling') print(f"Error handling: {error_skill}") # Output: Error handling: Error Handling - Advanced # Error handler user error_user = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') print(f"Handler: {error_user.email}") # Output: Handler: devops@anihpj.com # Handler author error_author = Author.objects.get(user=error_user) print(f"Handled by: {error_author.company}") # Output: Handled by: anihpj # Conditional check try: param_job = JobPost.objects.get(title__icontains='API') print(f"Parameter loaded: {param_job.title}") except JobPost.DoesNotExist: print("Creating new API parameter job...") # Output: Creating new API parameter job...

get-parameters

Get information about multiple parameters. Batch retrieve anihpj configuration values efficiently.

Syntax
aws ssm get-parameters \
    --names <value> [<value>...] \
    [--with-decryption]
Parameters Description
  • --names - (Required) List of parameter names (up to 10)
  • --with-decryption - Decrypt SecureString values
Return Value

Returns Parameters array and InvalidParameters list.

5 Detailed SSM get-parameters Examples
Example 1: Get Multiple Configuration Values
#!/bin/bash
# Get all anihpj database parameters
aws ssm get-parameters \
    --names "/anihpj/production/database/url" \
        "/anihpj/production/database/pool_size" \
        "/anihpj/production/database/timeout" \
    --with-decryption
{ "Parameters": [ { "Name": "/anihpj/production/database/url", "Type": "SecureString", "Value": "postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/db", "Version": 1 }, { "Name": "/anihpj/production/database/pool_size", "Type": "String", "Value": "20", "Version": 1 } ], "InvalidParameters": [ "/anihpj/production/database/timeout" ] }
# Django Models Integration - Get Multiple Configuration Values from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Batch retrieval skills batch_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Bulk Operations') print(f"Batch skill: {batch_skill}") # Output: Batch skill: Bulk Operations - Advanced # User doing batch retrieval batch_user = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') print(f"Batch user: {batch_user.email}") # Output: Batch user: devops@anihpj.com # Batch author batch_author = Author.objects.get(user=batch_user) print(f"Batch by: {batch_author.company}") # Output: Batch by: anihpj # Multiple locations (like multiple params) multi_locs = Location.objects.all()[:3] print(multi_locs.values_list('city', flat=True)) # Output: <QuerySet ['Ashburn', 'New York', 'Portland']> # Batch job batch_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(is_active=True)[:2] print(f"Batch jobs: {batch_jobs.count()}") # Output: Batch jobs: 2
Example 2: Load Django Settings
#!/bin/bash
# Load multiple Django settings for anihpj
PARAMS=$(aws ssm get-parameters \
    --names "/anihpj/production/django/secret_key" \
        "/anihpj/production/django/debug" \
        "/anihpj/production/django/allowed_hosts" \
    --with-decryption \
    --query 'Parameters[*].[Name,Value]' \
    --output text)

echo "$PARAMS" | while read name value; do
    export "${name##*/}"="$value"
    echo "Loaded: ${name##*/}"
done
Loaded: secret_key Loaded: debug Loaded: allowed_hosts
# Django Models Integration - Load Django Settings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Django settings skills django_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Django') print(f"Django: {django_skill}") # Output: Django: Django - Expert # Settings loader loader = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') print(f"Loader: {loader.email}") # Output: Loader: deployer@anihpj.com # Loader profile loader_author = Author.objects.get(user=loader) print(f"Loading for: {loader_author.company}") # Output: Loading for: anihpj # Settings simulation settings = ['SECRET_KEY', 'DEBUG', 'ALLOWED_HOSTS'] for s in settings: print(f"Loaded: {s}") # Output: # Loaded: SECRET_KEY # Loaded: DEBUG # Loaded: ALLOWED_HOSTS
Example 3: Check for Invalid Parameters
#!/bin/bash
# Validate all required anihpj parameters exist
RESULT=$(aws ssm get-parameters \
    --names "/anihpj/production/database/url" \
        "/anihpj/production/redis/url" \
        "/anihpj/production/s3/bucket")

INVALID=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.InvalidParameters[]')
if [ -n "$INVALID" ]; then
    echo "Missing parameters: $INVALID"
    exit 1
fi
echo "All parameters valid"
All parameters valid
# Django Models Integration - Check for Invalid Parameters from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Validation skills valid_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Status Verification') print(f"Validation: {valid_skill}") # Output: Validation: Status Verification - Intermediate # Validator validator = User.objects.get(username='sre_analyst') print(f"Validator: {validator.email}") # Output: Validator: sre@anihpj.com # Validator author val_author = Author.objects.get(user=validator) print(f"Validated by: {val_author.company}") # Output: Validated by: anihpj # Check for invalid (non-existent) records invalid_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(title='NonExistent') if not invalid_jobs.exists(): print("All parameters valid") # Output: All parameters valid
Example 4: Export as Environment Variables
#!/bin/bash
# Export anihpj parameters as environment variables
aws ssm get-parameters \
    --names "/anihpj/production/database/url" \
        "/anihpj/production/cache/url" \
    --with-decryption \
    --query 'Parameters' \
    --output json | jq -r '.[] | "export \(.Name | split("/") | last | ascii_upcase)=\"\(.Value)\""' > /tmp/env.sh

source /tmp/env.sh
echo "Environment configured"
Environment configured
# Django Models Integration - Export as Environment Variables from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import os # Environment skills env_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Configuration Management') print(f"Env skill: {env_skill}") # Output: Env skill: Configuration Management - Advanced # Env manager env_mgr = User.objects.get(username='devops_engineer') print(f"Env manager: {env_mgr.email}") # Output: Env manager: devops@anihpj.com # Manager author env_author = Author.objects.get(user=env_mgr) print(f"Env config by: {env_author.company}") # Output: Env config by: anihpj # Simulate environment export env_vars = {'DATABASE_URL': 'configured', 'CACHE_URL': 'configured'} for k, v in env_vars.items(): print(f" {k}={v}") print("Environment configured") # Output: # DATABASE_URL=configured # CACHE_URL=configured # Environment configured
Example 5: Batch Parameter Validation
#!/bin/bash
# Validate anihpj deployment parameters
REQUIRED_PARAMS=(
    "/anihpj/production/database/url"
    "/anihpj/production/django/secret_key"
    "/anihpj/production/aws/region"
)

RESULT=$(aws ssm get-parameters --names "${REQUIRED_PARAMS[@]}")
FOUND=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.Parameters | length')
TOTAL=${#REQUIRED_PARAMS[@]}

echo "Found $FOUND of $TOTAL required parameters"
if [ "$FOUND" -ne "$TOTAL" ]; then
    echo "Missing: $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.InvalidParameters[]')"
fi
Found 3 of 3 required parameters
# Django Models Integration - Batch Parameter Validation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Batch validation skills batch_val = Skills.objects.create(name='Batch Validation', level='Advanced') print(f"Batch validation: {batch_val}") # Output: Batch validation: Batch Validation - Advanced # Validator batch_validator = User.objects.get(username='ssm_deployer') print(f"Validator: {batch_validator.email}") # Output: Validator: deployer@anihpj.com # Validator author bv_author = Author.objects.get(user=batch_validator) print(f"Validated by: {bv_author.company}") # Output: Validated by: anihpj # Count required items required_skills = ['Django', 'Python', 'AWS SSM'] found = Skills.objects.filter(name__in=required_skills).count() total = len(required_skills) print(f"Found {found} of {total} required parameters") # Output: Found 3 of 3 required parameters

get-parameters-by-path

Retrieve all parameters within a hierarchy path. Get all anihpj configuration at once.

Syntax
aws ssm get-parameters-by-path \
    --path <value> \
    [--recursive] \
    [--with-decryption]
Parameters Description
  • --path - (Required) Hierarchy path to retrieve
  • --recursive - Retrieve all parameters in hierarchy
  • --with-decryption - Decrypt SecureString values
Return Value

Returns Parameters array with all matching parameters.

5 Detailed SSM get-parameters-by-path Examples
Example 1: Get All Production Parameters
#!/bin/bash
# Get all anihpj production configuration
aws ssm get-parameters-by-path \
    --path "/anihpj/production" \
    --recursive \
    --with-decryption
{ "Parameters": [ { "Name": "/anihpj/production/database/url", "Type": "SecureString", "Value": "postgresql://...", "Version": 1 }, { "Name": "/anihpj/production/django/secret_key", "Type": "SecureString", "Value": "django-secret-...", "Version": 1 } ] }
# Django Models Integration - Hierarchical Config Retrieval from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Hierarchical skills path path_skills = Skills.objects.filter(name__startswith='Production') print(f"Production skills: {list(path_skills.values_list('name', flat=True))}") # Output: Production skills: ['Production Database', 'Production Django', 'Production Redis'] # All config users config_users = User.objects.filter(is_staff=True) for user in config_users: print(f"Config user: {user.username}") # Output: Config user: db_admin # Output: Config user: django_admin # Production authors prod_authors = Author.objects.filter(company='anihpj') print(f"<QuerySet {list(prod_authors.values_list('user__username', flat=True))}>") # Output: <QuerySet ['admin', 'deployer']> # Get all production job posts prod_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(status='published') print(f"Production jobs: {prod_jobs.count()}") # Output: Production jobs: 15
Example 2: Export Path as Environment
#!/bin/bash
# Export all anihpj parameters as environment variables
aws ssm get-parameters-by-path \
    --path "/anihpj/production" \
    --recursive \
    --with-decryption \
    --query 'Parameters[*]' \
    --output json | jq -r '.[] | "\(.Name | split("/") | last | ascii_upcase)=\(.Value)"' > .env

echo "Created .env file with $(wc -l < .env) variables"
Created .env file with 12 variables
# Django Models Integration - Export Config as Environment from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Export skills to env-like format all_skills = Skills.objects.all() env_count = 0 for skill in all_skills: env_var = f"{skill.name.upper().replace(' ', '_')}={skill.level}" print(env_var) env_count += 1 print(f"Created .env file with {env_count} variables") # Output: DJANGO=Expert # Output: PYTHON=Advanced # Output: Created .env file with 12 variables # Export user config users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True) print(f"Active users exported: {users.count()}") # Output: Active users exported: 12 # Export author details authors = Author.objects.all() for author in authors[:3]: print(f"AUTHOR_{author.id}={author.user.email}") # Output: AUTHOR_1=admin@anihpj.com # Output: AUTHOR_2=deployer@anihpj.com
Example 3: List Parameters Without Values
#!/bin/bash
# List all parameter names in anihpj hierarchy
aws ssm get-parameters-by-path \
    --path "/anihpj" \
    --recursive \
    --query 'Parameters[*].Name' \
    --output table
--------------------------------------------- | GetParametersByPath | +-------------------------------------------+ | /anihpj/production/database/url | | /anihpj/production/django/secret_key | | /anihpj/staging/database/url | +-------------------------------------------+
# Django Models Integration - List Config Names Only from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List skill names only (no values) print("--------------------------------------------") print("| Skill Parameters |") print("+------------------------------------------+") for skill in Skills.objects.all()[:5]: print(f"| /anihpj/skills/{skill.name.lower().replace(' ', '_')} |") print("+------------------------------------------+") # Output: | /anihpj/skills/django | # Output: | /anihpj/skills/python | # List usernames only print("\nUser parameter names:") for user in User.objects.all()[:3]: print(f"/anihpj/users/{user.username}") # Output: /anihpj/users/admin # Output: /anihpj/users/deployer # List location paths locations = Location.objects.values_list('name', flat=True) print(f"<QuerySet {list(locations)}>") # Output: <QuerySet ['New York', 'San Francisco', 'Remote']>
Example 4: Filter by Environment
#!/bin/bash
# Get staging-specific anihpj configuration
ENV="staging"
aws ssm get-parameters-by-path \
    --path "/anihpj/$ENV" \
    --recursive \
    --with-decryption \
    --query 'Parameters[*].{Key:Name,Value:Value}'
[ { "Key": "/anihpj/staging/database/url", "Value": "postgresql://staging-db..." }, { "Key": "/anihpj/staging/debug", "Value": "true" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Filter by Environment from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Filter skills by environment/level env = 'Intermediate' env_skills = Skills.objects.filter(level=env) for skill in env_skills: print(f"{{Key: {skill.name}, Value: {skill.level}}}") # Output: {Key: HTML, Value: Intermediate} # Output: {Key: CSS, Value: Intermediate} # Staging users (not staff) staging_users = User.objects.filter(is_staff=False) print(f"Staging users: {staging_users.count()}") # Output: Staging users: 8 # Authors for staging environment staging_authors = Author.objects.filter(user__is_staff=False) print(f"<QuerySet {list(staging_authors.values_list('company', flat=True)[:3])}>") # Output: <QuerySet ['startup1', 'startup2', 'freelance']> # Draft job posts (staging) draft_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(status='draft') for job in draft_jobs[:2]: print(f"{{Key: {job.title}, Value: draft}}") # Output: {Key: Django Developer, Value: draft}
Example 5: Paginated Parameter Retrieval
#!/bin/bash
# Get all parameters with pagination for large configs
PATH_PREFIX="/anihpj/production"
NEXT_TOKEN=""

while true; do
    if [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ]; then
        RESULT=$(aws ssm get-parameters-by-path --path "$PATH_PREFIX" --recursive --max-results 10)
    else
        RESULT=$(aws ssm get-parameters-by-path --path "$PATH_PREFIX" --recursive --max-results 10 --next-token "$NEXT_TOKEN")
    fi
    
    echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.Parameters[].Name'
    NEXT_TOKEN=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.NextToken // empty')
    [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ] && break
done
/anihpj/production/database/url /anihpj/production/django/secret_key /anihpj/production/redis/url
# Django Models Integration - Paginated Parameter Retrieval from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.core.paginator import Paginator # Paginated skills retrieval all_skills = Skills.objects.all().order_by('id') paginator = Paginator(all_skills, 10) # 10 per page for page_num in range(1, paginator.num_pages + 1): page = paginator.page(page_num) for skill in page.object_list: print(f"/anihpj/skills/{skill.name.lower()}") # Output: /anihpj/skills/django # Output: /anihpj/skills/python # Output: /anihpj/skills/aws # Paginated users user_paginator = Paginator(User.objects.all(), 5) page1 = user_paginator.page(1) print(f"Page 1 users: {list(page1.object_list.values_list('username', flat=True))}") # Output: Page 1 users: ['admin', 'deployer', 'developer', 'tester', 'analyst'] # Check for more pages has_next = page1.has_next() print(f"Has next page: {has_next}") # Output: Has next page: True

delete-parameter

Delete a parameter from Parameter Store. Remove outdated anihpj configuration.

Syntax
aws ssm delete-parameter \
    --name <value>
Parameters Description
  • --name - (Required) Name of the parameter to delete
Return Value

Returns empty response on success.

5 Detailed SSM delete-parameter Examples
Example 1: Delete Single Parameter
#!/bin/bash
# Delete deprecated anihpj configuration
aws ssm delete-parameter \
    --name "/anihpj/production/deprecated/old_api_key"

echo "Parameter deleted"
Parameter deleted
# Django Models Integration - Delete Single Record from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete deprecated skill deprecated_skill = Skills.objects.filter(name='Old API Skill').first() if deprecated_skill: deprecated_skill.delete() print("Parameter deleted") # Output: Parameter deleted # Delete old user old_user = User.objects.filter(username='deprecated_user').first() if old_user: old_user.delete() print("User deleted") # Output: User deleted # Verify deletion remaining = Skills.objects.filter(name='Old API Skill').exists() print(f"Still exists: {remaining}") # Output: Still exists: False
Example 2: Delete with Confirmation
#!/bin/bash
# Delete parameter with confirmation
PARAM_NAME="/anihpj/staging/test_config"

read -p "Delete $PARAM_NAME? (y/n) " -n 1 -r
echo
if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    aws ssm delete-parameter --name "$PARAM_NAME"
    echo "Deleted: $PARAM_NAME"
fi
Delete /anihpj/staging/test_config? (y/n) y Deleted: /anihpj/staging/test_config
# Django Models Integration - Delete with Confirmation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete skill with confirmation skill_name = 'Test Config Skill' skill_to_delete = Skills.objects.filter(name=skill_name).first() confirm = input(f"Delete {skill_name}? (y/n) ") if confirm.lower() == 'y' and skill_to_delete: skill_to_delete.delete() print(f"Deleted: {skill_name}") # Output: Delete Test Config Skill? (y/n) y # Output: Deleted: Test Config Skill # Delete user with confirmation user_del = User.objects.get(username='staging_tester') print(f"Confirming deletion of: {user_del.email}") user_del.delete() print("User deleted after confirmation") # Output: Confirming deletion of: tester@staging.anihpj.com # Output: User deleted after confirmation
Example 3: Delete with Backup
#!/bin/bash
# Backup parameter before deleting
PARAM_NAME="/anihpj/production/old_secret"

# Backup current value
aws ssm get-parameter --name "$PARAM_NAME" --with-decryption > "/tmp/backup_$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

# Delete parameter
aws ssm delete-parameter --name "$PARAM_NAME"
echo "Backed up and deleted: $PARAM_NAME"
Backed up and deleted: /anihpj/production/old_secret
# Django Models Integration - Delete with Backup from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json from datetime import datetime # Backup skill before deleting skill_to_backup = Skills.objects.get(name='Old Secret Skill') backup_data = { 'name': skill_to_backup.name, 'level': skill_to_backup.level, 'backed_up': datetime.now().isoformat() } # Save backup with open(f'/tmp/backup_{datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d")}.json', 'w') as f: json.dump(backup_data, f) # Delete after backup skill_to_backup.delete() print(f"Backed up and deleted: {backup_data['name']}") # Output: Backed up and deleted: Old Secret Skill # Verify backup exists print("Backup saved to /tmp/backup_20241215.json") # Output: Backup saved to /tmp/backup_20241215.json
Example 4: Delete with Error Handling
#!/bin/bash
# Safe delete with error handling
PARAM_NAME="/anihpj/staging/temp_config"

if aws ssm delete-parameter --name "$PARAM_NAME" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Successfully deleted: $PARAM_NAME"
else
    echo "Parameter not found or already deleted: $PARAM_NAME"
fi
Successfully deleted: /anihpj/staging/temp_config
# Django Models Integration - Delete with Error Handling from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.core.exceptions import ObjectDoesNotExist # Safe delete with error handling skill_name = 'Temp Config Skill' try: skill = Skills.objects.get(name=skill_name) skill.delete() print(f"Successfully deleted: {skill_name}") except ObjectDoesNotExist: print(f"Parameter not found or already deleted: {skill_name}") # Output: Successfully deleted: Temp Config Skill # Safe user deletion try: user = User.objects.get(username='temp_user') user.delete() print("User deleted successfully") except User.DoesNotExist: print("User not found") # Output: User deleted successfully # Bulk safe delete deleted, _ = Skills.objects.filter(name__contains='temp').delete() print(f"Deleted {deleted} temporary skills") # Output: Deleted 3 temporary skills
Example 5: Cleanup Old Parameters
#!/bin/bash
# Delete all parameters in deprecated path
DEPRECATED_PATH="/anihpj/deprecated"

PARAMS=$(aws ssm get-parameters-by-path --path "$DEPRECATED_PATH" --recursive --query 'Parameters[*].Name' --output text)

for param in $PARAMS; do
    echo "Deleting: $param"
    aws ssm delete-parameter --name "$param"
done

echo "Cleanup complete"
Deleting: /anihpj/deprecated/old_db_url Deleting: /anihpj/deprecated/legacy_key Cleanup complete
# Django Models Integration - Cleanup Old Records from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete all deprecated skills deprecated_skills = Skills.objects.filter(name__startswith='deprecated_') for skill in deprecated_skills: print(f"Deleting: /anihpj/skills/{skill.name}") skill.delete() # Output: Deleting: /anihpj/skills/deprecated_old_db # Output: Deleting: /anihpj/skills/deprecated_legacy print("Cleanup complete") # Output: Cleanup complete # Bulk cleanup old job posts from datetime import timedelta from django.utils import timezone old_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__lt=timezone.now() - timedelta(days=365) ) count = old_jobs.count() old_jobs.delete() print(f"Cleaned up {count} old job posts") # Output: Cleaned up 15 old job posts # Verify cleanup remaining = Skills.objects.filter(name__startswith='deprecated_').count() print(f"Remaining deprecated: {remaining}") # Output: Remaining deprecated: 0

describe-parameters

Get metadata about parameters. List and search anihpj configuration parameters.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-parameters \
    [--filters <value>] \
    [--parameter-filters <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --filters - Filter by Name, Type, or KeyId
  • --parameter-filters - Advanced filters (Path, Name, etc.)
  • --max-results - Maximum number to return
Return Value

Returns Parameters array with metadata (no values).

5 Detailed SSM describe-parameters Examples
Example 1: List All Parameters
#!/bin/bash
# List all anihpj parameters
aws ssm describe-parameters \
    --parameter-filters "Key=Path,Option=Recursive,Values=/anihpj" \
    --query 'Parameters[*].{Name:Name,Type:Type,Modified:LastModifiedDate}' \
    --output table
------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeParameters | +--------------------------------+--------------+------------------+ | Name | Type | Modified | +--------------------------------+--------------+------------------+ | /anihpj/production/db/url | SecureString| 2024-01-15T10:00| | /anihpj/production/debug | String | 2024-01-10T08:00| +--------------------------------+--------------+------------------+
# Django Models Integration - List All Records with Metadata from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all skills with metadata print("-------------------------------------------------------------------") print("| Describe Skills |") print("+--------------------------------+--------------+------------------+") print("| Name | Level | Modified |") print("+--------------------------------+--------------+------------------+") for skill in Skills.objects.all()[:5]: print(f"| {skill.name:30}| {skill.level:12}| 2024-01-15T10:00|") print("+--------------------------------+--------------+------------------+") # Output: | Django | Expert | 2024-01-15T10:00| # List users with types users = User.objects.values('username', 'is_staff', 'date_joined') print(f"<QuerySet {list(users[:3])}>") # Output: <QuerySet [{'username': 'admin', 'is_staff': True, ...}]>
Example 2: Filter by Type
#!/bin/bash
# Find all SecureString parameters for anihpj
aws ssm describe-parameters \
    --parameter-filters "Key=Type,Values=SecureString" \
    --query 'Parameters[?contains(Name, `anihpj`)].Name'
[ "/anihpj/production/database/url", "/anihpj/production/django/secret_key", "/anihpj/staging/database/url" ]
# Django Models Integration - Filter by Type from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Find all Expert level skills (like SecureString type) expert_skills = Skills.objects.filter(level='Expert') skill_names = list(expert_skills.values_list('name', flat=True)) print(skill_names) # Output: ['Django', 'Python', 'AWS'] # Find staff users (like admin type) staff_users = User.objects.filter(is_staff=True).values_list('username', flat=True) print(f"<QuerySet {list(staff_users)}>") # Output: <QuerySet ['admin', 'deployer', 'developer']> # Filter authors by company type tech_authors = Author.objects.filter(company='anihpj') for author in tech_authors: print(f"/anihpj/authors/{author.user.username}") # Output: /anihpj/authors/admin # Output: /anihpj/authors/developer
Example 3: Search by Name Pattern
#!/bin/bash
# Find parameters matching pattern
aws ssm describe-parameters \
    --parameter-filters "Key=Name,Option=Contains,Values=database" \
    --query 'Parameters[*].{Name:Name,Version:Version}'
[ { "Name": "/anihpj/production/database/url", "Version": 3 }, { "Name": "/anihpj/staging/database/url", "Version": 1 } ]
# Django Models Integration - Search by Name Pattern from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Find skills containing 'database' db_skills = Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains='database') results = [] for skill in db_skills: results.append({'Name': f'/anihpj/skills/{skill.name}', 'Version': 1}) print(results) # Output: [{'Name': '/anihpj/skills/Database Admin', 'Version': 1}] # Search users by pattern dev_users = User.objects.filter(username__icontains='dev') for user in dev_users: print(f"{{Name: {user.username}, Version: {user.id}}}") # Output: {Name: developer, Version: 3} # Output: {Name: devops, Version: 5} # Search job posts by title pattern django_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains='django') print(f"<QuerySet {list(django_jobs.values('title', 'id')[:2])}>") # Output: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Django Developer', 'id': 1}]>
Example 4: Get Parameter Metadata with Tags
#!/bin/bash
# List parameters with their tags
aws ssm describe-parameters \
    --parameter-filters "Key=Path,Option=OneLevel,Values=/anihpj/production" \
    --query 'Parameters[*].{Name:Name,Description:Description,Tier:Tier}'
[ { "Name": "/anihpj/production/database", "Description": "Database configuration", "Tier": "Standard" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Get Metadata with Tags from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get skill metadata with description skills_meta = Skills.objects.values('name', 'level') results = [] for skill in skills_meta[:3]: results.append({ 'Name': f"/anihpj/skills/{skill['name']}", 'Description': f"{skill['name']} skill configuration", 'Tier': 'Standard' }) print(results) # Output: [{'Name': '/anihpj/skills/Django', 'Description': 'Django skill configuration', 'Tier': 'Standard'}] # User metadata with role info user_meta = User.objects.filter(is_staff=True).values('username', 'email', 'is_superuser') for u in user_meta[:2]: tier = 'Premium' if u['is_superuser'] else 'Standard' print(f"{{Name: {u['username']}, Tier: {tier}}}") # Output: {Name: admin, Tier: Premium} # Output: {Name: developer, Tier: Standard}
Example 5: Audit Parameter Inventory
#!/bin/bash
# Generate parameter inventory report for anihpj
echo "=== anihpj Parameter Inventory ==="
echo ""

for env in production staging; do
    COUNT=$(aws ssm describe-parameters \
        --parameter-filters "Key=Path,Option=Recursive,Values=/anihpj/$env" \
        --query 'length(Parameters)')
    echo "$env: $COUNT parameters"
done

echo ""
echo "SecureString parameters:"
aws ssm describe-parameters \
    --parameter-filters "Key=Type,Values=SecureString" "Key=Path,Option=Recursive,Values=/anihpj" \
    --query 'Parameters[*].Name' --output text
=== anihpj Parameter Inventory === production: 15 parameters staging: 8 parameters SecureString parameters: /anihpj/production/database/url /anihpj/production/django/secret_key
# Django Models Integration - Audit Record Inventory from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Generate inventory report print("=== anihpj Record Inventory ===") print() # Count by category for level in ['Expert', 'Advanced', 'Intermediate']: count = Skills.objects.filter(level=level).count() print(f"{level}: {count} skills") # Output: Expert: 15 skills # Output: Advanced: 8 skills print() print("Staff users:") staff = User.objects.filter(is_staff=True).values_list('username', flat=True) print(' '.join(staff)) # Output: admin deployer developer # Full inventory summary print(f"\nTotal Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") print(f"Total Users: {User.objects.count()}") print(f"Total Authors: {Author.objects.count()}") print(f"Total Locations: {Location.objects.count()}") print(f"Total JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") # Output: Total Skills: 25 # Output: Total Users: 12

create-document

Creates an SSM document. Define custom automation runbooks for anihpj operations.

Syntax
aws ssm create-document \
    --name <value> \
    --content <value> \
    --document-type <value> \
    [--document-format <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --name - (Required) Name for the document
  • --content - (Required) Document content (JSON or YAML)
  • --document-type - Type: Command, Automation, Policy, etc.
  • --document-format - JSON or YAML format
Return Value

Returns DocumentDescription with Name, Hash, Status, and DocumentType.

5 Detailed SSM create-document Examples
Example 1: Create Command Document
#!/bin/bash
# Create custom deployment document for anihpj
aws ssm create-document \
    --name "anihpj-deploy" \
    --document-type "Command" \
    --document-format "YAML" \
    --content 'schemaVersion: "2.2"
description: "Deploy anihpj Django application"
parameters:
  branch:
    type: String
    default: main
mainSteps:
  - action: aws:runShellScript
    name: deploy
    inputs:
      runCommand:
        - cd /var/www/anihpj
        - git pull origin {{ branch }}
        - pip install -r requirements.txt
        - python manage.py migrate
        - sudo systemctl restart gunicorn'
{ "DocumentDescription": { "Name": "anihpj-deploy", "DocumentType": "Command", "Status": "Creating", "DocumentVersion": "1", "Hash": "abc123..." } }
# Django Models Integration - Create Command Document from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create deployment skill record deploy_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='Deployment Automation', level='Expert' ) print(f"Created: {deploy_skill.name}") # Output: Created: Deployment Automation # Document description equivalent doc_info = { 'Name': 'anihpj-deploy', 'DocumentType': 'Command', 'Status': 'Creating', 'Version': '1' } print(doc_info) # Output: {'Name': 'anihpj-deploy', 'DocumentType': 'Command', 'Status': 'Creating', 'Version': '1'} # Track document creator creator = User.objects.get(username='admin') author = Author.objects.get(user=creator) print(f"Document created by: {author.company}") # Output: Document created by: anihpj
Example 2: Create Automation Document
#!/bin/bash
# Create automation runbook for anihpj scaling
aws ssm create-document \
    --name "anihpj-scale-asg" \
    --document-type "Automation" \
    --document-format "JSON" \
    --content '{
        "schemaVersion": "0.3",
        "description": "Scale anihpj Auto Scaling Group",
        "parameters": {
            "DesiredCapacity": {"type": "Integer", "default": 2}
        },
        "mainSteps": [{
            "name": "scaleASG",
            "action": "aws:executeAwsApi",
            "inputs": {
                "Service": "autoscaling",
                "Api": "SetDesiredCapacity",
                "AutoScalingGroupName": "anihpj-asg",
                "DesiredCapacity": "{{ DesiredCapacity }}"
            }
        }]
    }'
{ "DocumentDescription": { "Name": "anihpj-scale-asg", "DocumentType": "Automation", "Status": "Creating" } }
# Django Models Integration - Create Automation Document from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create scaling automation skill auto_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='Auto Scaling', level='Advanced' ) # Automation document info print({ 'DocumentDescription': { 'Name': 'anihpj-scale-asg', 'DocumentType': 'Automation', 'Status': 'Creating' } }) # Output: {'DocumentDescription': {'Name': 'anihpj-scale-asg', ...}} # Create automation job post scale_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Auto Scaling Engineer', author=Author.objects.first(), location=Location.objects.first(), status='published' ) print(f"Created job: {scale_job.title}") # Output: Created job: Auto Scaling Engineer
Example 3: Create Session Document
#!/bin/bash
# Create custom session document for anihpj admin access
aws ssm create-document \
    --name "anihpj-admin-session" \
    --document-type "Session" \
    --content '{
        "schemaVersion": "1.0",
        "description": "anihpj admin session with logging",
        "sessionType": "Standard_Stream",
        "inputs": {
            "s3BucketName": "anihpj-session-logs",
            "s3KeyPrefix": "admin-sessions",
            "cloudWatchLogGroupName": "/aws/ssm/anihpj-sessions",
            "runAsEnabled": true,
            "runAsDefaultUser": "anihpj-admin"
        }
    }'
{ "DocumentDescription": { "Name": "anihpj-admin-session", "DocumentType": "Session", "Status": "Creating" } }
# Django Models Integration - Create Session Document from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create session management skill session_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='Session Management', level='Expert' ) print(f"Created skill: {session_skill.name}") # Output: Created skill: Session Management # Admin session user admin_user = User.objects.create_user( username='anihpj-admin', email='admin@anihpj.com', is_staff=True ) print(f"Session user created: {admin_user.username}") # Output: Session user created: anihpj-admin # Document description doc = {'Name': 'anihpj-admin-session', 'DocumentType': 'Session', 'Status': 'Creating'} print(f"<Document {doc['Name']} - {doc['Status']}>") # Output: <Document anihpj-admin-session - Creating>
Example 4: Create Document from File
#!/bin/bash
# Create document from external file
cat > /tmp/anihpj-healthcheck.yaml <<'EOF'
schemaVersion: "2.2"
description: "anihpj health check document"
mainSteps:
  - action: aws:runShellScript
    name: healthcheck
    inputs:
      runCommand:
        - curl -f http://localhost:8000/health/ || exit 1
        - echo "anihpj health check passed"
EOF

aws ssm create-document \
    --name "anihpj-healthcheck" \
    --document-type "Command" \
    --document-format "YAML" \
    --content file:///tmp/anihpj-healthcheck.yaml
{ "DocumentDescription": { "Name": "anihpj-healthcheck", "Status": "Creating" } }
# Django Models Integration - Create Document from File from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json # Load skill from file with open('/tmp/skill_config.json', 'w') as f: json.dump({'name': 'Health Check', 'level': 'Advanced'}, f) with open('/tmp/skill_config.json', 'r') as f: config = json.load(f) healthcheck_skill = Skills.objects.create( name=config['name'], level=config['level'] ) print(f"Created from file: {healthcheck_skill.name}") # Output: Created from file: Health Check # Document info print({'DocumentDescription': {'Name': 'anihpj-healthcheck', 'Status': 'Creating'}}) # Output: {'DocumentDescription': {'Name': 'anihpj-healthcheck', 'Status': 'Creating'}}
Example 5: Create Document with Tags
#!/bin/bash
# Create document with tags for organization
aws ssm create-document \
    --name "anihpj-backup-db" \
    --document-type "Automation" \
    --tags '[{"Key":"Application","Value":"anihpj"},{"Key":"Purpose","Value":"backup"}]' \
    --content '{
        "schemaVersion": "0.3",
        "description": "Backup anihpj database",
        "mainSteps": [{
            "name": "createSnapshot",
            "action": "aws:executeAwsApi",
            "inputs": {
                "Service": "rds",
                "Api": "CreateDBSnapshot",
                "DBInstanceIdentifier": "anihpj-db",
                "DBSnapshotIdentifier": "anihpj-backup-{{global:DATE}}"
            }
        }]
    }'
{ "DocumentDescription": { "Name": "anihpj-backup-db", "Tags": [ {"Key": "Application", "Value": "anihpj"}, {"Key": "Purpose", "Value": "backup"} ] } }
# Django Models Integration - Create Document with Tags from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create skill with "tags" (using level as category) backup_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='Database Backup', level='Expert' ) # Simulate tags with related data tags = [ {'Key': 'Application', 'Value': 'anihpj'}, {'Key': 'Purpose', 'Value': 'backup'} ] doc_with_tags = { 'DocumentDescription': { 'Name': 'anihpj-backup-db', 'Tags': tags } } print(doc_with_tags) # Output: {'DocumentDescription': {'Name': 'anihpj-backup-db', 'Tags': [...]}} # Create tagged job post backup_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Backup Engineer', author=Author.objects.first(), location=Location.objects.first() ) print(f"Job with tags: {backup_job.title}") # Output: Job with tags: Backup Engineer

update-document

Updates an SSM document with a new version. Modify anihpj automation runbooks.

Syntax
aws ssm update-document \
    --name <value> \
    --content <value> \
    --document-version <value>
Parameters Description
  • --name - (Required) Name of the document to update
  • --content - (Required) Updated document content
  • --document-version - (Required) Version to update (use $LATEST)
Return Value

Returns updated DocumentDescription with new version number.

5 Detailed SSM update-document Examples
Example 1: Update Document Content
#!/bin/bash
# Update anihpj deploy document with new steps
aws ssm update-document \
    --name "anihpj-deploy" \
    --document-version '$LATEST' \
    --document-format "YAML" \
    --content 'schemaVersion: "2.2"
description: "Deploy anihpj Django application v2"
parameters:
  branch:
    type: String
    default: main
mainSteps:
  - action: aws:runShellScript
    name: deploy
    inputs:
      runCommand:
        - cd /var/www/anihpj
        - git fetch --all
        - git checkout {{ branch }}
        - git pull
        - pip install -r requirements.txt
        - python manage.py migrate
        - python manage.py collectstatic --noinput
        - sudo systemctl restart gunicorn'
{ "DocumentDescription": { "Name": "anihpj-deploy", "DocumentVersion": "2", "Status": "Updating" } }
# Django Models Integration - Update Document Content from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Update existing skill skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Deployment Automation') skill.level = 'Expert' # Updated level skill.save() print(f"Updated: {skill.name} - Version 2") # Output: Updated: Deployment Automation - Version 2 # Document update response doc_update = { 'DocumentDescription': { 'Name': 'anihpj-deploy', 'DocumentVersion': '2', 'Status': 'Updating' } } print(doc_update) # Output: {'DocumentDescription': {'Name': 'anihpj-deploy', 'DocumentVersion': '2', 'Status': 'Updating'}} # Track update in job post job = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='Deploy').first() if job: job.status = 'updated' job.save() print(f"<JobPost {job.title} - updated>") # Output: <JobPost Django Deployer - updated>
Example 2: Update and Set Default Version
#!/bin/bash
# Update document and make it default
DOC_NAME="anihpj-healthcheck"

# Update document
RESULT=$(aws ssm update-document \
    --name "$DOC_NAME" \
    --document-version '$LATEST' \
    --content file:///tmp/healthcheck-v2.yaml)

NEW_VERSION=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.DocumentDescription.DocumentVersion')

# Set as default
aws ssm update-document-default-version \
    --name "$DOC_NAME" \
    --document-version "$NEW_VERSION"

echo "Updated to version $NEW_VERSION"
Updated to version 3
# Django Models Integration - Update and Set Default Version from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Update skill and track version skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Health Check') # Simulate version tracking current_version = 2 skill.level = 'Expert' # New update skill.save() new_version = current_version + 1 # Set as default (primary skill) skill.name = f'{skill.name} (Default)' skill.save() print(f"Updated to version {new_version}") # Output: Updated to version 3 # Verify default default_skill = Skills.objects.filter(name__contains='Default').first() print(f"Default version: {default_skill.name}") # Output: Default version: Health Check (Default)
Example 3: Update with Version Label
#!/bin/bash
# Update document with semantic versioning
aws ssm update-document \
    --name "anihpj-deploy" \
    --document-version '$LATEST' \
    --version-name "v2.1.0" \
    --content file:///tmp/deploy-v2.1.yaml

echo "Document updated with version label v2.1.0"
{ "DocumentDescription": { "Name": "anihpj-deploy", "DocumentVersion": "4", "VersionName": "v2.1.0" } } Document updated with version label v2.1.0
# Django Models Integration - Update with Version Label from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Update skill with semantic versioning in name skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Deployment Automation') version_label = 'v2.1.0' # Store version info update_info = { 'DocumentDescription': { 'Name': skill.name, 'DocumentVersion': '4', 'VersionName': version_label } } print(update_info) # Output: {'DocumentDescription': {'Name': 'Deployment Automation', 'DocumentVersion': '4', 'VersionName': 'v2.1.0'}} print(f"Document updated with version label {version_label}") # Output: Document updated with version label v2.1.0 # Track in author notes author = Author.objects.first() print(f"Updated by: {author.user.username} - {version_label}") # Output: Updated by: admin - v2.1.0
Example 4: Safe Update with Validation
#!/bin/bash
# Validate content before updating
DOC_NAME="anihpj-automation"
CONTENT_FILE="/tmp/automation-update.json"

# Validate JSON
if jq empty "$CONTENT_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    aws ssm update-document \
        --name "$DOC_NAME" \
        --document-version '$LATEST' \
        --content "file://$CONTENT_FILE"
    echo "Document updated successfully"
else
    echo "ERROR: Invalid JSON in content file"
    exit 1
fi
Document updated successfully
# Django Models Integration - Safe Update with Validation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError # Validate before updating skill_name = 'Automation Skill' new_level = 'Expert' try: skill = Skills.objects.get(name=skill_name) # Validate level is valid valid_levels = ['Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Advanced', 'Expert'] if new_level in valid_levels: skill.level = new_level skill.full_clean() # Django validation skill.save() print("Document updated successfully") else: print("ERROR: Invalid level value") except ValidationError as e: print(f"Validation error: {e}") # Output: Document updated successfully # Verify update updated = Skills.objects.get(name=skill_name) print(f"Verified: {updated.name} - {updated.level}") # Output: Verified: Automation Skill - Expert
Example 5: Update Specific Version
#!/bin/bash
# Update from specific version (not $LATEST)
aws ssm update-document \
    --name "anihpj-deploy" \
    --document-version "2" \
    --content 'schemaVersion: "2.2"
description: "Hotfix for anihpj deploy"
mainSteps:
  - action: aws:runShellScript
    name: hotfix
    inputs:
      runCommand:
        - echo "Emergency hotfix applied"'
{ "DocumentDescription": { "Name": "anihpj-deploy", "DocumentVersion": "5", "Status": "Updating" } }
# Django Models Integration - Update Specific Version from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Update based on specific version/criteria skill = Skills.objects.filter( name='Deployment Automation', level='Intermediate' # Specific version condition ).first() if skill: skill.level = 'Expert' # Hotfix update skill.save() print({ 'DocumentDescription': { 'Name': skill.name, 'DocumentVersion': '5', 'Status': 'Updating' } }) # Output: {'DocumentDescription': {'Name': 'anihpj-deploy', 'DocumentVersion': '5', 'Status': 'Updating'}} # Hotfix applied print("Emergency hotfix applied") # Output: Emergency hotfix applied # Verify specific update hotfixed = Skills.objects.get(name='Deployment Automation') print(f"<Skill {hotfixed.name} - Version 5>") # Output: <Skill Deployment Automation - Version 5>

delete-document

Deletes an SSM document. Remove obsolete anihpj automation documents.

Syntax
aws ssm delete-document \
    --name <value> \
    [--document-version <value>] \
    [--force]
Parameters Description
  • --name - (Required) Name of the document to delete
  • --document-version - Delete specific version only
  • --force - Force delete even if in use
Return Value

Returns empty response on success.

5 Detailed SSM delete-document Examples
Example 1: Delete Document
#!/bin/bash
# Delete obsolete anihpj document
aws ssm delete-document \
    --name "anihpj-old-deploy"

echo "Document deleted"
Document deleted
# Django Models Integration - Delete Document from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete obsolete skill record old_skill = Skills.objects.filter(name='Old Deploy Skill').first() if old_skill: old_skill.delete() print("Document deleted") # Output: Document deleted # Verify deletion exists = Skills.objects.filter(name='Old Deploy Skill').exists() print(f"Still exists: {exists}") # Output: Still exists: False # Track deletion deleted_count = Skills.objects.filter(name__contains='obsolete').delete()[0] print(f"Deleted {deleted_count} obsolete documents") # Output: Deleted 3 obsolete documents
Example 2: Delete Specific Version
#!/bin/bash
# Delete only version 2 of anihpj document
aws ssm delete-document \
    --name "anihpj-deploy" \
    --document-version "2"

echo "Version 2 deleted"
Version 2 deleted
# Django Models Integration - Delete Specific Version from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete specific version of skill (using id as version) skill_name = 'Deployment Automation' version_id = 2 # Specific version skill = Skills.objects.filter(name=skill_name, id=version_id).first() if skill: skill.delete() print(f"Version {version_id} deleted") # Output: Version 2 deleted # Alternative: Soft delete by marking job = JobPost.objects.get(id=2) job.status = 'deleted' job.save() print(f"Version 2 marked as deleted") # Output: Version 2 marked as deleted
Example 3: Force Delete
#!/bin/bash
# Force delete document even if associations exist
aws ssm delete-document \
    --name "anihpj-legacy-automation" \
    --force

echo "Document force deleted"
Document force deleted
# Django Models Integration - Force Delete from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import ProtectedError # Force delete skill even if referenced skill_name = 'Legacy Automation' try: skill = Skills.objects.get(name=skill_name) # Force delete ignoring related objects skill.delete() print("Document force deleted") except ProtectedError: # Override protection Skills.objects.filter(name=skill_name).delete() print("Document force deleted") # Output: Document force deleted # Force delete all legacy records forced, _ = Skills.objects.filter(name__contains='legacy').delete() print(f"Force deleted {forced} legacy documents") # Output: Force deleted 5 legacy documents
Example 4: Safe Delete with Backup
#!/bin/bash
# Backup document content before deleting
DOC_NAME="anihpj-deprecated-script"

# Export current content
aws ssm get-document --name "$DOC_NAME" \
    --document-format "YAML" > "/backup/${DOC_NAME}_$(date +%Y%m%d).yaml"

# Delete document
aws ssm delete-document --name "$DOC_NAME"
echo "Backed up and deleted: $DOC_NAME"
Backed up and deleted: anihpj-deprecated-script
# Django Models Integration - Safe Delete with Backup from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json from datetime import datetime # Backup skill before deletion skill_name = 'Deprecated Script Skill' skill = Skills.objects.get(name=skill_name) # Create backup backup = { 'name': skill.name, 'level': skill.level, 'backup_date': datetime.now().isoformat() } with open(f'/backup/{skill_name}_{datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d")}.json', 'w') as f: json.dump(backup, f) # Delete after backup skill.delete() print(f"Backed up and deleted: {skill_name}") # Output: Backed up and deleted: anihpj-deprecated-script # Verify backup exists and record deleted print("Backup saved successfully") # Output: Backup saved successfully
Example 5: Cleanup Old Versions
#!/bin/bash
# Delete all versions except current default
DOC_NAME="anihpj-deploy"

# Get default version
DEFAULT=$(aws ssm describe-document --name "$DOC_NAME" --query 'Document.DefaultVersion' --output text)

# Get all versions
VERSIONS=$(aws ssm list-document-versions --name "$DOC_NAME" --query 'DocumentVersions[*].DocumentVersion' --output text)

for ver in $VERSIONS; do
    if [ "$ver" != "$DEFAULT" ]; then
        echo "Deleting version: $ver"
        aws ssm delete-document --name "$DOC_NAME" --document-version "$ver"
    fi
done
Deleting version: 1 Deleting version: 2 Deleting version: 4
# Django Models Integration - Cleanup Old Versions from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Cleanup old versions except default/current skill_name = 'Deployment Automation' default_version = 3 # Current default # Get all versions (using id as version) all_skills = Skills.objects.filter(name=skill_name) for skill in all_skills: if skill.id != default_version: print(f"Deleting version: {skill.id}") skill.delete() # Output: Deleting version: 1 # Output: Deleting version: 2 # Output: Deleting version: 4 # Verify only default remains remaining = Skills.objects.filter(name=skill_name).count() print(f"Remaining versions: {remaining}") # Output: Remaining versions: 1 # Cleanup old job posts old_jobs = JobPost.objects.exclude(id=3).filter(title__contains='Deploy') for job in old_jobs: print(f"Deleting job version: {job.id}") job.delete() # Output: Deleting job version: 1

list-documents

Returns all SSM documents in the current account. List anihpj automation documents.

Syntax
aws ssm list-documents \
    [--document-filter-list <value>] \
    [--filters <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --document-filter-list - Filter by Owner, Name, PlatformTypes
  • --filters - Advanced filters (tag, name prefix)
  • --max-results - Maximum documents to return
Return Value

Returns DocumentIdentifiers array with Name, Owner, DocumentType.

5 Detailed SSM list-documents Examples
Example 1: List Custom Documents
#!/bin/bash
# List all anihpj custom documents
aws ssm list-documents \
    --document-filter-list "key=Owner,value=Self" \
    --query 'DocumentIdentifiers[*].{Name:Name,Type:DocumentType}' \
    --output table
------------------------------------------ | ListDocuments | +----------------------+-----------------+ | Name | Type | +----------------------+-----------------+ | anihpj-deploy | Command | | anihpj-healthcheck | Command | | anihpj-scale-asg | Automation | +----------------------+-----------------+
# Django Models Integration - List Custom Documents from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all custom skills (like self-owned documents) print("------------------------------------------") print("| ListDocuments |") print("+----------------------+-----------------+") print("| Name | Type |") print("+----------------------+-----------------+") for skill in Skills.objects.all()[:5]: print(f"| {skill.name:20}| {skill.level:15}|") print("+----------------------+-----------------+") # Output: | anihpj-deploy | Command | # Query equivalent skills = Skills.objects.values('name', 'level') print(f"<QuerySet {list(skills[:3])}>") # Output: <QuerySet [{'name': 'Django', 'level': 'Expert'}]>
Example 2: List by Document Type
#!/bin/bash
# List all automation documents
aws ssm list-documents \
    --filters "Key=DocumentType,Values=Automation" \
    --query 'DocumentIdentifiers[?contains(Name, `anihpj`)].Name'
[ "anihpj-scale-asg", "anihpj-backup-db", "anihpj-deploy-automation" ]
# Django Models Integration - List by Document Type from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List skills by type (level = Automation) automation_skills = Skills.objects.filter(level='Automation') skill_names = list(automation_skills.values_list('name', flat=True)) print(skill_names) # Output: ['anihpj-scale-asg', 'anihpj-backup-db', 'anihpj-deploy-automation'] # Filter job posts by type automation_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains='automation') for job in automation_jobs: print(f"Automation job: {job.title}") # Output: Automation job: DevOps Automation Engineer # Query with contains filter print(f"<QuerySet {list(automation_skills.values_list('name', flat=True)[:3])}>") # Output: <QuerySet ['Scale ASG', 'Backup DB', 'Deploy Automation']>
Example 3: List by Tag
#!/bin/bash
# List documents tagged for anihpj
aws ssm list-documents \
    --filters "Key=tag:Application,Values=anihpj" \
    --query 'DocumentIdentifiers[*].{Name:Name,Version:DocumentVersion}'
[ { "Name": "anihpj-deploy", "Version": "3" }, { "Name": "anihpj-healthcheck", "Version": "1" } ]
# Django Models Integration - List by Tag from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List skills tagged for anihpj (simulated with name filter) anihpj_skills = Skills.objects.filter(name__icontains='anihpj') results = [] for idx, skill in enumerate(anihpj_skills, 1): results.append({'Name': skill.name, 'Version': str(idx)}) print(results) # Output: [{'Name': 'anihpj-deploy', 'Version': '3'}, {'Name': 'anihpj-healthcheck', 'Version': '1'}] # Filter job posts by application tag tagged_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(author__company='anihpj') for job in tagged_jobs[:2]: print(f"{{Name: {job.title}, Version: {job.id}}}") # Output: {Name: Django Developer, Version: 1} # QuerySet output print(f"<QuerySet {list(anihpj_skills.values('name')[:2])}>") # Output: <QuerySet [{'name': 'anihpj-deploy'}, {'name': 'anihpj-healthcheck'}]>
Example 4: List AWS Managed Documents
#!/bin/bash
# List useful AWS-managed documents
aws ssm list-documents \
    --document-filter-list "key=Owner,value=Amazon" \
    --filters "Key=Name,Values=AWS-Run" \
    --query 'DocumentIdentifiers[*].Name' \
    --max-results 10
[ "AWS-RunShellScript", "AWS-RunPowerShellScript", "AWS-RunPatchBaseline", "AWS-RunAnsiblePlaybook" ]
# Django Models Integration - List AWS Managed Documents from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List managed/system skills (like AWS-owned documents) managed_skills = Skills.objects.filter(name__startswith='AWS-')[:10] skill_names = list(managed_skills.values_list('name', flat=True)) print(skill_names) # Output: ['AWS-RunShellScript', 'AWS-RunPowerShellScript', 'AWS-RunPatchBaseline', 'AWS-RunAnsiblePlaybook'] # System users (like Amazon-owned) system_users = User.objects.filter(is_superuser=True) for user in system_users[:4]: print(f"AWS-{user.username}") # Output: AWS-admin # Output: AWS-system # QuerySet result print(f"<QuerySet {skill_names[:4]}>") # Output: <QuerySet ['AWS-RunShellScript', 'AWS-RunPowerShellScript']>
Example 5: Document Inventory Report
#!/bin/bash
# Generate document inventory for anihpj
echo "=== anihpj Document Inventory ==="

echo -e "\nCommand Documents:"
aws ssm list-documents \
    --document-filter-list "key=Owner,value=Self" \
    --filters "Key=DocumentType,Values=Command" \
    --query 'DocumentIdentifiers[*].Name' --output text

echo -e "\nAutomation Documents:"
aws ssm list-documents \
    --document-filter-list "key=Owner,value=Self" \
    --filters "Key=DocumentType,Values=Automation" \
    --query 'DocumentIdentifiers[*].Name' --output text
=== anihpj Document Inventory === Command Documents: anihpj-deploy anihpj-healthcheck Automation Documents: anihpj-scale-asg anihpj-backup-db
# Django Models Integration - Document Inventory Report from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Generate skill inventory for anihpj print("=== anihpj Document Inventory ===") print("\nCommand Skills:") command_skills = Skills.objects.filter(level='Command') print(' '.join(command_skills.values_list('name', flat=True))) # Output: anihpj-deploy anihpj-healthcheck print("\nAutomation Skills:") automation_skills = Skills.objects.filter(level='Automation') print(' '.join(automation_skills.values_list('name', flat=True))) # Output: anihpj-scale-asg anihpj-backup-db # Full inventory print(f"\nTotal Skills: {Skills.objects.count()}") print(f"Total Users: {User.objects.count()}") print(f"Total JobPosts: {JobPost.objects.count()}") # Output: Total Skills: 25 # Output: Total Users: 12

describe-document

Describes an SSM document. Get metadata for anihpj automation documents.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-document \
    --name <value> \
    [--document-version <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --name - (Required) Name of the document
  • --document-version - Specific version to describe
Return Value

Returns Document object with full metadata including parameters and steps.

5 Detailed SSM describe-document Examples
Example 1: Get Document Details
#!/bin/bash
# Get details of anihpj deploy document
aws ssm describe-document \
    --name "anihpj-deploy"
{ "Document": { "Name": "anihpj-deploy", "DocumentVersion": "3", "Status": "Active", "DocumentType": "Command", "Description": "Deploy anihpj Django application", "Parameters": [ { "Name": "branch", "Type": "String", "DefaultValue": "main" } ] } }
# Django Models Integration - Get Document Details from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get detailed skill information skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Deployment Automation') # Build document-like response document = { 'Document': { 'Name': skill.name, 'DocumentVersion': '3', 'Status': 'Active', 'DocumentType': 'Command', 'Description': f'Deploy anihpj Django application', 'Parameters': [{'Name': 'branch', 'Type': 'String', 'DefaultValue': 'main'}] } } print(document) # Output: {'Document': {'Name': 'Deployment Automation', 'DocumentVersion': '3', ...}} # QuerySet details print(f"<Skill: {skill.name} - {skill.level}>") # Output: <Skill: Deployment Automation - Expert>
Example 2: Get Specific Version
#!/bin/bash
# Describe specific version for comparison
aws ssm describe-document \
    --name "anihpj-deploy" \
    --document-version "1" \
    --query 'Document.{Version:DocumentVersion,Status:Status,Hash:Hash}'
{ "Version": "1", "Status": "Active", "Hash": "abc123def456..." }
# Django Models Integration - Get Specific Version from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import hashlib # Get specific version of skill (using id) skill = Skills.objects.filter(name='Deployment Automation', id=1).first() if skill: # Generate hash like SSM document skill_hash = hashlib.md5(skill.name.encode()).hexdigest()[:12] version_info = { 'Version': str(skill.id), 'Status': 'Active', 'Hash': f"{skill_hash}..." } print(version_info) # Output: {'Version': '1', 'Status': 'Active', 'Hash': 'abc123def456...'} # Compare with another version skill_v2 = Skills.objects.filter(name='Deployment Automation', id=2).first() if skill_v2: print(f"Version 2 exists: {skill_v2.level}") # Output: Version 2 exists: Expert
Example 3: Get Document Parameters
#!/bin/bash
# List all parameters for anihpj document
aws ssm describe-document \
    --name "anihpj-scale-asg" \
    --query 'Document.Parameters[*].{Name:Name,Type:Type,Default:DefaultValue}'
[ { "Name": "DesiredCapacity", "Type": "Integer", "Default": "2" }, { "Name": "MaxSize", "Type": "Integer", "Default": "10" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Get Document Parameters from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List skill parameters (using model fields as parameters) skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Auto Scaling') # Simulate document parameters from model fields parameters = [ {'Name': 'DesiredCapacity', 'Type': 'Integer', 'Default': '2'}, {'Name': 'MaxSize', 'Type': 'Integer', 'Default': '10'} ] print(parameters) # Output: [{'Name': 'DesiredCapacity', 'Type': 'Integer', 'Default': '2'}, ...] # Get model field info from django.db import models for field in Skills._meta.get_fields(): if hasattr(field, 'name'): print(f"Parameter: {field.name}") # Output: Parameter: name # Output: Parameter: level
Example 4: Check Document Status
#!/bin/bash
# Verify document is active before use
DOC_NAME="anihpj-deploy"

STATUS=$(aws ssm describe-document --name "$DOC_NAME" --query 'Document.Status' --output text)

if [ "$STATUS" == "Active" ]; then
    echo "Document $DOC_NAME is ready for use"
else
    echo "Document status: $STATUS - not ready"
fi
Document anihpj-deploy is ready for use
# Django Models Integration - Check Document Status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Verify skill is active before use skill_name = 'Deployment Automation' skill = Skills.objects.filter(name=skill_name).first() if skill and skill.level: # Has level = active print(f"Document {skill_name} is ready for use") else: print(f"Document status: inactive - not ready") # Output: Document anihpj-deploy is ready for use # Check job post status job = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='Deploy').first() if job and job.status == 'published': print(f"Job {job.title} is active") # Output: Job Django Deployer is active # Check user active status user = User.objects.get(username='deployer') print(f"User active: {user.is_active}") # Output: User active: True
Example 5: Compare Document Versions
#!/bin/bash
# Compare document versions for anihpj
DOC_NAME="anihpj-deploy"

echo "Version comparison for $DOC_NAME:"
for ver in 1 2 3; do
    INFO=$(aws ssm describe-document \
        --name "$DOC_NAME" \
        --document-version "$ver" \
        --query 'Document.{V:DocumentVersion,Hash:Hash,Status:Status}' 2>/dev/null)
    [ -n "$INFO" ] && echo "v$ver: $(echo $INFO | jq -r '.Hash[:12]') - $(echo $INFO | jq -r '.Status')"
done
Version comparison for anihpj-deploy: v1: abc123def456 - Active v2: def456ghi789 - Active v3: ghi789jkl012 - Active
# Django Models Integration - Compare Document Versions from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import hashlib # Compare skill versions skill_name = 'Deployment Automation' print(f"Version comparison for {skill_name}:") # Simulate version history with related skills for ver in range(1, 4): try: skill = Skills.objects.get(id=ver) hash_val = hashlib.md5(f"{skill.name}{ver}".encode()).hexdigest()[:12] status = 'Active' print(f"v{ver}: {hash_val} - {status}") except Skills.DoesNotExist: pass # Output: v1: abc123def456 - Active # Output: v2: def456ghi789 - Active # Output: v3: ghi789jkl012 - Active # QuerySet comparison all_versions = Skills.objects.all()[:3] print(f"<QuerySet versions: {list(all_versions.values_list('id', flat=True))}>") # Output: <QuerySet versions: [1, 2, 3]>

get-document

Gets the content of an SSM document. Retrieve anihpj automation script content.

Syntax
aws ssm get-document \
    --name <value> \
    [--document-version <value>] \
    [--document-format <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --name - (Required) Name of the document
  • --document-version - Version to retrieve
  • --document-format - Output format (JSON or YAML)
Return Value

Returns document Name, DocumentVersion, Content, and DocumentType.

5 Detailed SSM get-document Examples
Example 1: Get Document Content
#!/bin/bash
# Get anihpj deploy document content
aws ssm get-document \
    --name "anihpj-deploy" \
    --document-format "YAML"
{ "Name": "anihpj-deploy", "DocumentVersion": "3", "Content": "schemaVersion: \"2.2\"\ndescription: Deploy anihpj...", "DocumentType": "Command", "DocumentFormat": "YAML" }
# Django Models Integration - Get Document Content from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json # Get skill content details skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Deployment Automation') document = { 'Name': skill.name, 'DocumentVersion': '3', 'Content': f"level: {skill.level}\ndescription: Deploy anihpj...", 'DocumentType': 'Command', 'DocumentFormat': 'YAML' } print(json.dumps(document, indent=2)) # Output: {'Name': 'anihpj-deploy', 'DocumentVersion': '3', ...} # Get full skill info print(f"<Skill {skill.name}: {skill.level}>") # Output: <Skill Deployment Automation: Expert>
Example 2: Export Document to File
#!/bin/bash
# Export anihpj document for backup
aws ssm get-document \
    --name "anihpj-deploy" \
    --document-format "YAML" \
    --query 'Content' \
    --output text > anihpj-deploy-backup.yaml

echo "Document exported to anihpj-deploy-backup.yaml"
Document exported to anihpj-deploy-backup.yaml
# Django Models Integration - Export Document to File from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import yaml # Export skill to YAML file skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Deployment Automation') skill_data = { 'name': skill.name, 'level': skill.level, 'description': 'Deploy anihpj Django application' } with open('anihpj-deploy-backup.yaml', 'w') as f: yaml.dump(skill_data, f) print("Document exported to anihpj-deploy-backup.yaml") # Output: Document exported to anihpj-deploy-backup.yaml # Verify export with open('anihpj-deploy-backup.yaml', 'r') as f: loaded = yaml.safe_load(f) print(f"Exported: {loaded['name']}") # Output: Exported: Deployment Automation
Example 3: Get Specific Version
#!/bin/bash
# Get previous version of anihpj document
aws ssm get-document \
    --name "anihpj-deploy" \
    --document-version "1" \
    --document-format "JSON" \
    --query 'Content' | jq -r '.' | jq '.'
{ "schemaVersion": "2.2", "description": "Deploy anihpj Django application", "mainSteps": [...] }
# Django Models Integration - Get Specific Version from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get specific version of skill skill = Skills.objects.filter(id=1).first() # Version 1 if skill: content = { 'schemaVersion': '2.2', 'description': f'Deploy anihpj Django application', 'mainSteps': ['cd /var/www/anihpj', 'git pull', 'pip install -r requirements.txt'] } print(content) # Output: {'schemaVersion': '2.2', 'description': 'Deploy anihpj Django application', 'mainSteps': [...]} # Get version details print(f"Version 1 skill: {skill.name}") # Output: Version 1 skill: Django # All versions versions = Skills.objects.values('id', 'name')[:3] print(f"<QuerySet {list(versions)}>") # Output: <QuerySet [{'id': 1, 'name': 'Django'}]>
Example 4: Get AWS Managed Document
#!/bin/bash
# Study AWS-RunShellScript document structure
aws ssm get-document \
    --name "AWS-RunShellScript" \
    --document-format "JSON" \
    --query '{Schema:Content}' | jq '.Schema | fromjson | .mainSteps[0]'
{ "action": "aws:runShellScript", "name": "runShellScript", "inputs": { "runCommand": "{{ commands }}" } }
# Django Models Integration - Get AWS Managed Document from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Study managed skill structure aws_skill = Skills.objects.filter(name__startswith='AWS-').first() if aws_skill: # Simulate document structure main_step = { 'action': 'aws:runShellScript', 'name': 'runShellScript', 'inputs': { 'runCommand': '{{ commands }}' } } print(main_step) # Output: {'action': 'aws:runShellScript', 'name': 'runShellScript', ...} # Get managed skill details print(f"Managed skill: {aws_skill.name}") # Output: Managed skill: AWS-RunShellScript # List AWS managed skills aws_skills = Skills.objects.filter(name__startswith='AWS-') print(f"<QuerySet {list(aws_skills.values_list('name', flat=True)[:3])}>") # Output: <QuerySet ['AWS-RunShellScript', 'AWS-RunPowerShellScript']>
Example 5: Version Control Export
#!/bin/bash
# Export all versions for git version control
DOC_NAME="anihpj-deploy"
EXPORT_DIR="./ssm-documents/$DOC_NAME"
mkdir -p "$EXPORT_DIR"

VERSIONS=$(aws ssm list-document-versions --name "$DOC_NAME" --query 'DocumentVersions[*].DocumentVersion' --output text)

for ver in $VERSIONS; do
    aws ssm get-document \
        --name "$DOC_NAME" \
        --document-version "$ver" \
        --document-format "YAML" \
        --query 'Content' \
        --output text > "$EXPORT_DIR/v$ver.yaml"
    echo "Exported version $ver"
done
Exported version 1 Exported version 2 Exported version 3
# Django Models Integration - Version Control Export from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import os import yaml # Export all skill versions for version control skill_name = 'Deployment Automation' export_dir = f'./skills-export/{skill_name}' os.makedirs(export_dir, exist_ok=True) # Get all related skills (versions) versions = Skills.objects.all()[:3] for skill in versions: export_path = f"{export_dir}/v{skill.id}.yaml" with open(export_path, 'w') as f: yaml.dump({'name': skill.name, 'level': skill.level}, f) print(f"Exported version {skill.id}") # Output: Exported version 1 # Output: Exported version 2 # Output: Exported version 3 # Verify exports print(f"Exported {versions.count()} versions to {export_dir}") # Output: Exported 3 versions to ./skills-export/Deployment Automation

start-automation-execution

Initiates execution of an Automation runbook. Run automated workflows for anihpj infrastructure.

Syntax
aws ssm start-automation-execution \
    --document-name <value> \
    [--parameters <value>] \
    [--target-parameter-name <value>] \
    [--targets <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --document-name - (Required) Name of the Automation document
  • --parameters - Input parameters for the automation
  • --targets - Target resources for the automation
  • --mode - Execution mode (Auto or Interactive)
Return Value

Returns AutomationExecutionId for tracking the execution.

5 Detailed SSM start-automation-execution Examples
Example 1: Run Automation Runbook
#!/bin/bash
# Start anihpj scaling automation
aws ssm start-automation-execution \
    --document-name "anihpj-scale-asg" \
    --parameters '{"DesiredCapacity":["5"]}'
{ "AutomationExecutionId": "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" }
# Django Models Integration - Run Automation Runbook from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Start scaling automation for skills scale_skill = Skills.objects.get(name='Auto Scaling') # Generate execution ID execution_id = str(uuid.uuid4()) # Start automation print(f"Starting automation for: {scale_skill.name}") automation_result = { 'AutomationExecutionId': execution_id } print(automation_result) # Output: {'AutomationExecutionId': 'abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678'} # Track execution print(f"<AutomationExecution {execution_id[:8]}... - InProgress>") # Output: <AutomationExecution abc12345... - InProgress>
Example 2: Run AWS Managed Automation
#!/bin/bash
# Restart anihpj EC2 instances using AWS automation
aws ssm start-automation-execution \
    --document-name "AWS-RestartEC2Instance" \
    --parameters '{"InstanceId":["i-0abc123def456789"]}'
{ "AutomationExecutionId": "restart-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" }
# Django Models Integration - Run AWS Managed Automation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Restart user (simulate EC2 restart) user = User.objects.get(username='web_server') # Deactivate and reactivate (restart) user.is_active = False user.save() user.is_active = True user.save() execution_id = f"restart-{str(uuid.uuid4())[:23]}" print({'AutomationExecutionId': execution_id}) # Output: {'AutomationExecutionId': 'restart-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678'} print(f"Restarted user: {user.username}") # Output: Restarted user: web_server
Example 3: Target Multiple Instances
#!/bin/bash
# Run automation on all anihpj tagged instances
aws ssm start-automation-execution \
    --document-name "AWS-StopEC2Instance" \
    --target-parameter-name "InstanceId" \
    --targets "Key=tag:Application,Values=anihpj" \
    --max-concurrency "50%" \
    --max-errors "10%"
{ "AutomationExecutionId": "stop-5678-90ab-cdef-123456789abc" }
# Django Models Integration - Target Multiple Instances from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Target all anihpj tagged job posts target_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(author__company='anihpj') # Stop automation on all targets for job in target_jobs[:5]: job.status = 'stopped' job.save() print(f"Stopped: {job.title}") execution_id = f"stop-{str(uuid.uuid4())[:27]}" print({'AutomationExecutionId': execution_id}) # Output: {'AutomationExecutionId': 'stop-5678-90ab-cdef-123456789abc'} # Verify targets print(f"<QuerySet stopped {target_jobs.count()} instances>") # Output: <QuerySet stopped 5 instances>
Example 4: Run with Approval
#!/bin/bash
# Start automation requiring approval
aws ssm start-automation-execution \
    --document-name "anihpj-production-deploy" \
    --parameters '{"Branch":["release-v2.0"]}' \
    --mode "Auto"
{ "AutomationExecutionId": "deploy-90ab-cdef-1234-56789abcdef0" }
# Django Models Integration - Run with Approval from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Start production deploy with approval job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Production Deploy v2.0', author=Author.objects.first(), location=Location.objects.first(), status='pending_approval' ) # Check approval approver = User.objects.filter(is_superuser=True).first() if approver: job.status = 'approved' job.save() execution_id = f"deploy-{str(uuid.uuid4())[:27]}" print({'AutomationExecutionId': execution_id}) # Output: {'AutomationExecutionId': 'deploy-90ab-cdef-1234-56789abcdef0'} print(f"Deploy approved by: {approver.username}") # Output: Deploy approved by: admin
Example 5: Run and Wait for Completion
#!/bin/bash
# Start automation and wait for result
EXEC_ID=$(aws ssm start-automation-execution \
    --document-name "anihpj-backup-db" \
    --query 'AutomationExecutionId' --output text)

echo "Started: $EXEC_ID"

while true; do
    STATUS=$(aws ssm get-automation-execution \
        --automation-execution-id "$EXEC_ID" \
        --query 'AutomationExecution.AutomationExecutionStatus' --output text)
    echo "Status: $STATUS"
    [[ "$STATUS" == "Success" || "$STATUS" == "Failed" ]] && break
    sleep 10
done
Started: backup-cdef-1234-5678-90abcdef1234 Status: InProgress Status: InProgress Status: Success
# Django Models Integration - Run and Wait for Completion from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid import time # Start backup automation exec_id = f"backup-{str(uuid.uuid4())[:27]}" print(f"Started: {exec_id}") # Simulate async execution with status updates statuses = ['InProgress', 'InProgress', 'Success'] for status in statuses: print(f"Status: {status}") time.sleep(0.1) # Simulate delay # Output: Started: backup-cdef-1234-5678-90abcdef1234 # Output: Status: InProgress # Output: Status: InProgress # Output: Status: Success # Create backup record on success backup_skill = Skills.objects.create( name=f'Backup {exec_id[:8]}', level='Expert' ) print(f"<Backup completed: {backup_skill.name}>") # Output: <Backup completed: Backup backup-cd>

stop-automation-execution

Stop an Automation execution. Cancel runaway or incorrect automation for anihpj.

Syntax
aws ssm stop-automation-execution \
    --automation-execution-id <value> \
    [--type <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --automation-execution-id - (Required) ID of the execution to stop
  • --type - Stop type: Complete or Cancel
Return Value

Returns empty response on success.

5 Detailed SSM stop-automation-execution Examples
Example 1: Cancel Automation
#!/bin/bash
# Cancel runaway anihpj automation
aws ssm stop-automation-execution \
    --automation-execution-id "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" \
    --type "Cancel"

echo "Automation cancelled"
Automation cancelled
# Django Models Integration - Cancel Automation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Cancel runaway job post process job = JobPost.objects.filter(status='in_progress').first() if job: job.status = 'cancelled' job.save() print("Automation cancelled") # Output: Automation cancelled # Verify cancellation cancelled = JobPost.objects.filter(status='cancelled').count() print(f"Total cancelled: {cancelled}") # Output: Total cancelled: 1 # Log the cancellation print(f"<JobPost {job.title} - cancelled at {job.updated_at}>") # Output: <JobPost Django Deploy - cancelled at 2024-01-15>
Example 2: Complete Current Step
#!/bin/bash
# Stop after current step completes
aws ssm stop-automation-execution \
    --automation-execution-id "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" \
    --type "Complete"

echo "Automation will stop after current step"
Automation will stop after current step
# Django Models Integration - Complete Current Step from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Complete current step then stop job = JobPost.objects.filter(status='step_2').first() if job: # Complete current step job.status = 'step_2_complete' job.save() # Mark for stop after completion print("Automation will stop after current step") # Output: Automation will stop after current step # Verify step completion print(f"Current status: {job.status}") # Output: Current status: step_2_complete # QuerySet showing completed completed = JobPost.objects.filter(status__endswith='complete') print(f"<QuerySet {list(completed.values_list('title', flat=True)[:2])}>") # Output: <QuerySet ['Deploy Step 2']>
Example 3: Emergency Stop All
#!/bin/bash
# Emergency: Stop all running anihpj automations
RUNNING=$(aws ssm describe-automation-executions \
    --filters "Key=ExecutionStatus,Values=InProgress" \
    --query 'AutomationExecutionMetadataList[?contains(DocumentName, `anihpj`)].AutomationExecutionId' \
    --output text)

for exec_id in $RUNNING; do
    echo "Stopping: $exec_id"
    aws ssm stop-automation-execution \
        --automation-execution-id "$exec_id" \
        --type "Cancel"
done
Stopping: abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678 Stopping: def45678-5678-90ab-cdef-123456789abc
# Django Models Integration - Emergency Stop All from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Emergency: Stop all running job posts running_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(status='in_progress') for job in running_jobs: print(f"Stopping: job-{job.id}") job.status = 'emergency_stopped' job.save() # Output: Stopping: abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678 # Output: Stopping: def45678-5678-90ab-cdef-123456789abc # Verify all stopped stopped_count = JobPost.objects.filter(status='emergency_stopped').count() print(f"Emergency stopped: {stopped_count} automations") # Output: Emergency stopped: 2 automations # QuerySet of stopped print(f"<QuerySet emergency stopped {stopped_count} jobs>") # Output: <QuerySet emergency stopped 2 jobs>
Example 4: Stop with Verification
#!/bin/bash
# Stop and verify cancellation
EXEC_ID="abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678"

aws ssm stop-automation-execution \
    --automation-execution-id "$EXEC_ID" \
    --type "Cancel"

sleep 2

STATUS=$(aws ssm get-automation-execution \
    --automation-execution-id "$EXEC_ID" \
    --query 'AutomationExecution.AutomationExecutionStatus' --output text)

echo "Final status: $STATUS"
Final status: Cancelled
