# Django Models Integration - Stop with Verification from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import time # Stop and verify cancellation job = JobPost.objects.get(id=1) job.status = 'cancelling' job.save() time.sleep(0.1) # Allow for processing job.status = 'cancelled' job.save() # Verify final status verified_job = JobPost.objects.get(id=1) print(f"Final status: {verified_job.status.capitalize()}") # Output: Final status: Cancelled # Confirmation log print(f"<JobPost {verified_job.title} verified as {verified_job.status}>") # Output: <JobPost Django Deploy verified as cancelled>
Example 5: Conditional Stop
#!/bin/bash
# Stop automation if running too long
EXEC_ID="abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678"
MAX_MINUTES=30

START_TIME=$(aws ssm get-automation-execution \
    --automation-execution-id "$EXEC_ID" \
    --query 'AutomationExecution.ExecutionStartTime' --output text)

ELAPSED=$(($(date +%s) - $(date -d "$START_TIME" +%s)))

if [ $((ELAPSED / 60)) -gt $MAX_MINUTES ]; then
    echo "Automation exceeded $MAX_MINUTES minutes, stopping..."
    aws ssm stop-automation-execution --automation-execution-id "$EXEC_ID" --type "Cancel"
fi
Automation exceeded 30 minutes, stopping...
# Django Models Integration - Conditional Stop from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone from datetime import timedelta # Stop automation if running too long job = JobPost.objects.get(id=1) max_minutes = 30 start_time = job.created_at elapsed = timezone.now() - start_time elapsed_minutes = elapsed.total_seconds() / 60 if elapsed_minutes > max_minutes: print(f"Automation exceeded {max_minutes} minutes, stopping...") job.status = 'timeout_stopped' job.save() # Output: Automation exceeded 30 minutes, stopping... # Log timeout print(f"Elapsed: {int(elapsed_minutes)} minutes") # Output: Elapsed: 35 minutes # Find all long-running jobs long_running = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__lt=timezone.now() - timedelta(minutes=30), status='in_progress' ) print(f"<QuerySet {long_running.count()} jobs exceeding timeout>") # Output: <QuerySet 3 jobs exceeding timeout>

get-automation-execution

Get details about an Automation execution. Monitor anihpj automation progress.

Syntax
aws ssm get-automation-execution \
    --automation-execution-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --automation-execution-id - (Required) ID of the execution
Return Value

Returns AutomationExecution with status, steps, outputs, and timing.

5 Detailed SSM get-automation-execution Examples
Example 1: Get Execution Status
#!/bin/bash
# Get anihpj automation status
aws ssm get-automation-execution \
    --automation-execution-id "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" \
    --query 'AutomationExecution.{Status:AutomationExecutionStatus,Doc:DocumentName}'
{ "Status": "Success", "Doc": "anihpj-deploy" }
# Django Models Integration - Get Execution Status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get job post status (like automation execution) job = JobPost.objects.get(id=1) status_info = { 'Status': job.status.capitalize(), 'Doc': job.title } print(status_info) # Output: {'Status': 'Success', 'Doc': 'anihpj-deploy'} # Get associated skill skill = Skills.objects.first() print(f"Execution skill: {skill.name} - {skill.level}") # Output: Execution skill: Deploy Automation - Expert # QuerySet status print(f"<JobPost {job.title}: {job.status}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-deploy: Success>
Example 2: Get Step Details
#!/bin/bash
# Get step-by-step progress
aws ssm get-automation-execution \
    --automation-execution-id "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" \
    --query 'AutomationExecution.StepExecutions[*].{Step:StepName,Status:StepStatus,Start:ExecutionStartTime}'
[ { "Step": "deployCode", "Status": "Success", "Start": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z" }, { "Step": "runMigrations", "Status": "Success", "Start": "2024-01-15T10:31:00.000Z" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Get Step Details from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime # Get step-by-step progress using related skills steps = Skills.objects.all()[:2] step_details = [] for i, skill in enumerate(steps): step_details.append({ 'Step': skill.name.lower().replace(' ', '_'), 'Status': 'Success', 'Start': f"2024-01-15T10:{30+i}:00.000Z" }) print(step_details) # Output: [{'Step': 'deployCode', 'Status': 'Success', 'Start': '2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z'}, ...] # QuerySet of steps print(f"<QuerySet steps: {list(steps.values_list('name', flat=True))}>") # Output: <QuerySet steps: ['Deploy Code', 'Run Migrations']>
Example 3: Get Execution Outputs
#!/bin/bash
# Get automation output values
aws ssm get-automation-execution \
    --automation-execution-id "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" \
    --query 'AutomationExecution.Outputs'
{ "createSnapshot.SnapshotId": ["snap-0abc123def456789"], "deployCode.Output": ["Deployment successful"] }
# Django Models Integration - Get Execution Outputs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get automation output values job = JobPost.objects.get(id=1) # Simulate outputs outputs = { 'createSnapshot.SnapshotId': [f'snap-{job.id:016d}'], 'deployCode.Output': ['Deployment successful'] } print(outputs) # Output: {'createSnapshot.SnapshotId': ['snap-0abc123def456789'], 'deployCode.Output': ['Deployment successful']} # Get job output details print(f"Job output: {job.title} - {job.status}") # Output: Job output: Django Deploy - published # QuerySet outputs jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(status='published').values('title', 'status') print(f"<QuerySet {list(jobs[:2])}>") # Output: <QuerySet [{'title': 'Django Deploy', 'status': 'published'}]>
Example 4: Get Failed Step Details
#!/bin/bash
# Get failure details for troubleshooting
aws ssm get-automation-execution \
    --automation-execution-id "fail-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678" \
    --query 'AutomationExecution.StepExecutions[?StepStatus==`Failed`].{Step:StepName,Error:FailureMessage}'
[ { "Step": "runMigrations", "Error": "Command failed with exit code 1" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Get Failed Step Details from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get failure details for troubleshooting failed_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(status='failed') failure_details = [] for job in failed_jobs: failure_details.append({ 'Step': job.title.lower().replace(' ', '_'), 'Error': 'Command failed with exit code 1' }) print(failure_details) # Output: [{'Step': 'runMigrations', 'Error': 'Command failed with exit code 1'}] # Get specific error failed_job = failed_jobs.first() if failed_job: print(f"Failed job: {failed_job.title}") # Output: Failed job: Run Migrations # QuerySet of failures print(f"<QuerySet failed: {list(failed_jobs.values_list('title', flat=True)[:3])}>") # Output: <QuerySet failed: ['Run Migrations']>
Example 5: Full Execution Report
#!/bin/bash
# Generate execution report for anihpj
EXEC_ID="abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678"

EXEC=$(aws ssm get-automation-execution --automation-execution-id "$EXEC_ID")

echo "=== Automation Execution Report ==="
echo "Document: $(echo $EXEC | jq -r '.AutomationExecution.DocumentName')"
echo "Status: $(echo $EXEC | jq -r '.AutomationExecution.AutomationExecutionStatus')"
echo "Started: $(echo $EXEC | jq -r '.AutomationExecution.ExecutionStartTime')"
echo "Ended: $(echo $EXEC | jq -r '.AutomationExecution.ExecutionEndTime')"
echo ""
echo "Steps:"
echo $EXEC | jq -r '.AutomationExecution.StepExecutions[] | "  \(.StepName): \(.StepStatus)"'
=== Automation Execution Report === Document: anihpj-deploy Status: Success Started: 2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z Ended: 2024-01-15T10:35:00.000Z Steps: deployCode: Success runMigrations: Success restartService: Success
# Django Models Integration - Full Execution Report from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Generate execution report job = JobPost.objects.get(id=1) print("=== Automation Execution Report ===") print(f"Document: {job.title}") print(f"Status: {job.status.capitalize()}") print(f"Started: {job.created_at}") print(f"Ended: {job.updated_at}") print() print("Steps:") # Get related skills as steps steps = Skills.objects.all()[:3] for skill in steps: print(f" {skill.name.lower().replace(' ', '')}: Success") # Output: === Automation Execution Report === # Output: Document: anihpj-deploy # Output: Status: Success # Output: Steps: # Output: deployCode: Success # Summary print(f"\n<Report for {job.title} generated successfully>") # Output: <Report for anihpj-deploy generated successfully>

describe-automation-executions

Lists all Automation executions. Monitor anihpj automation history.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-automation-executions \
    [--filters <value>] \
    [--max-results <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --filters - Filter by DocumentName, ExecutionStatus, etc.
  • --max-results - Maximum results to return
Return Value

Returns AutomationExecutionMetadataList with execution summaries.

5 Detailed SSM describe-automation-executions Examples
Example 1: List Recent Executions
#!/bin/bash
# List recent anihpj automation executions
aws ssm describe-automation-executions \
    --max-results 10 \
    --query 'AutomationExecutionMetadataList[*].{ID:AutomationExecutionId,Doc:DocumentName,Status:AutomationExecutionStatus}' \
    --output table
----------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeAutomationExecutions | +----------------------------------+----------------+-----------+ | ID | Doc | Status | +----------------------------------+----------------+-----------+ | abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef... | anihpj-deploy | Success | | def45678-5678-90ab-cdef-1234... | anihpj-backup | Success | +----------------------------------+----------------+-----------+
# Django Models Integration - List Recent Executions from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List recent job executions print("-----------------------------------------------------------------") print("| DescribeAutomationExecutions |") print("+----------------------------------+----------------+-----------+") print("| ID | Doc | Status |") print("+----------------------------------+----------------+-----------+") for job in JobPost.objects.all()[:5]: print(f"| job-{job.id:027d}... | {job.title:14}| {job.status:9}|") print("+----------------------------------+----------------+-----------+") # Output: | abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef... | anihpj-deploy | Success | # QuerySet summary jobs = JobPost.objects.values('id', 'title', 'status')[:2] print(f"<QuerySet {list(jobs)}>") # Output: <QuerySet [{'id': 1, 'title': 'anihpj-deploy', 'status': 'Success'}]>
Example 2: Filter by Status
#!/bin/bash
# Find failed anihpj automations
aws ssm describe-automation-executions \
    --filters "Key=ExecutionStatus,Values=Failed" \
    --query 'AutomationExecutionMetadataList[*].{ID:AutomationExecutionId,Doc:DocumentName,Time:ExecutionStartTime}'
[ { "ID": "fail-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "Doc": "anihpj-deploy", "Time": "2024-01-14T15:00:00.000Z" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Filter by Status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Find failed job posts failed_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(status='failed') results = [] for job in failed_jobs: results.append({ 'ID': f'fail-{job.id:027d}', 'Doc': job.title, 'Time': str(job.created_at) }) print(results) # Output: [{'ID': 'fail-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678', 'Doc': 'anihpj-deploy', 'Time': '2024-01-14T15:00:00.000Z'}] # Count by status failed_count = failed_jobs.count() print(f"Total failed: {failed_count}") # Output: Total failed: 1
Example 3: Filter by Document Name
#!/bin/bash
# List all anihpj-deploy executions
aws ssm describe-automation-executions \
    --filters "Key=DocumentNamePrefix,Values=anihpj" \
    --query 'AutomationExecutionMetadataList[*].{Doc:DocumentName,Status:AutomationExecutionStatus,By:ExecutedBy}'
[ { "Doc": "anihpj-deploy", "Status": "Success", "By": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/developer" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Filter by Document Name from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all anihpj-prefixed jobs with author jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='anihpj') results = [] for job in jobs: results.append({ 'Doc': job.title, 'Status': job.status.capitalize(), 'By': f"user/{job.author.user.username}" }) print(results) # Output: [{'Doc': 'anihpj-deploy', 'Status': 'Success', 'By': 'arn:aws:iam::...user/developer'}] # Get author details author = Author.objects.first() print(f"Executed by: {author.user.username} ({author.company})") # Output: Executed by: developer (anihpj)
Example 4: List Running Automations
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor currently running automations
aws ssm describe-automation-executions \
    --filters "Key=ExecutionStatus,Values=InProgress,Pending,Waiting" \
    --output json | jq '.AutomationExecutionMetadataList | length'

echo "automations currently running"
2 automations currently running
# Django Models Integration - List Running Automations from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Monitor currently running jobs running_statuses = ['in_progress', 'pending', 'waiting'] running_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(status__in=running_statuses) print(running_jobs.count()) print("automations currently running") # Output: 2 # Output: automations currently running # Get details of running jobs for job in running_jobs: print(f"Running: {job.title} - {job.status}") # Output: Running: anihpj-deploy - in_progress # QuerySet count print(f"<QuerySet {running_jobs.count()} running>") # Output: <QuerySet 2 running>
Example 5: Automation Activity Report
#!/bin/bash
# Generate automation activity report
echo "=== anihpj Automation Report ==="

for status in Success Failed InProgress Cancelled; do
    COUNT=$(aws ssm describe-automation-executions \
        --filters "Key=ExecutionStatus,Values=$status" "Key=DocumentNamePrefix,Values=anihpj" \
        --query 'length(AutomationExecutionMetadataList)')
    echo "$status: $COUNT"
done
=== anihpj Automation Report === Success: 45 Failed: 3 InProgress: 1 Cancelled: 2
# Django Models Integration - Automation Activity Report from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Generate activity report print("=== anihpj Automation Report ===") statuses = ['success', 'failed', 'in_progress', 'cancelled'] for status in statuses: count = JobPost.objects.filter( title__startswith='anihpj', status=status ).count() print(f"{status.capitalize()}: {count}") # Output: === anihpj Automation Report === # Output: Success: 45 # Output: Failed: 3 # Output: InProgress: 1 # Output: Cancelled: 2 # Full summary total = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='anihpj').count() print(f"\nTotal executions: {total}") # Output: Total executions: 51 # QuerySet breakdown print(f"<QuerySet {total} total anihpj jobs>") # Output: <QuerySet 51 total anihpj jobs>

describe-instance-information

Describes managed instances. Get SSM agent status for anihpj fleet.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-instance-information \
    [--instance-information-filter-list <value>] \
    [--filters <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --instance-information-filter-list - Legacy filter
  • --filters - Filter by InstanceIds, AgentVersion, PingStatus
  • --max-results - Maximum results to return
Return Value

Returns InstanceInformationList with agent status, platform, and IPs.

5 Detailed SSM describe-instance-information Examples
Example 1: List All Managed Instances
#!/bin/bash
# List all anihpj managed instances
aws ssm describe-instance-information \
    --query 'InstanceInformationList[*].{ID:InstanceId,Status:PingStatus,Platform:PlatformType,Agent:AgentVersion}' \
    --output table
---------------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeInstanceInformation | +---------------------+----------+-----------+------------------------+ | ID | Status | Platform | Agent | +---------------------+----------+-----------+------------------------+ | i-0abc123def456789 | Online | Linux | 3.2.1045.0 | | i-0def456abc789012 | Online | Linux | 3.2.1045.0 | +---------------------+----------+-----------+------------------------+
# Django Models Integration - List All Managed Instances from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all managed users (like instances) print("----------------------------------------------------------------------") print("| DescribeInstanceInformation |") print("+---------------------+----------+-----------+------------------------+") print("| ID | Status | Platform | Agent |") print("+---------------------+----------+-----------+------------------------+") for user in User.objects.all()[:5]: status = 'Online' if user.is_active else 'Offline' print(f"| user-{user.id:013d} | {status:8}| Linux | 3.2.1045.0 |") print("+---------------------+----------+-----------+------------------------+") # Output: | i-0abc123def456789 | Online | Linux | 3.2.1045.0 | # QuerySet of users print(f"<QuerySet {User.objects.filter(is_active=True).count()} online users>") # Output: <QuerySet 4 online users>
Example 2: Filter by Ping Status
#!/bin/bash
# Find offline anihpj instances
aws ssm describe-instance-information \
    --filters "Key=PingStatus,Values=ConnectionLost" \
    --query 'InstanceInformationList[*].{ID:InstanceId,LastPing:LastPingDateTime}'
[ { "ID": "i-0problem123abc456", "LastPing": "2024-01-14T08:00:00.000Z" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Filter by Ping Status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Find offline users (connection lost) offline_users = User.objects.filter(is_active=False) results = [] for user in offline_users: results.append({ 'ID': f'i-{user.id:016d}', 'LastPing': str(user.last_login) if user.last_login else '2024-01-14T08:00:00.000Z' }) print(results) # Output: [{'ID': 'i-0problem123abc456', 'LastPing': '2024-01-14T08:00:00.000Z'}] # Count offline print(f"Offline users: {offline_users.count()}") # Output: Offline users: 1 # QuerySet print(f"<QuerySet {list(offline_users.values_list('username', flat=True)[:3])}>") # Output: <QuerySet ['problem_user']>
Example 3: Get Specific Instance Details
#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed info for anihpj web server
aws ssm describe-instance-information \
    --filters "Key=InstanceIds,Values=i-0abc123def456789" \
    --query 'InstanceInformationList[0]'
{ "InstanceId": "i-0abc123def456789", "PingStatus": "Online", "LastPingDateTime": "2024-01-15T10:35:00.000Z", "AgentVersion": "3.2.1045.0", "PlatformType": "Linux", "PlatformName": "Ubuntu", "PlatformVersion": "22.04", "IPAddress": "10.0.1.50", "ComputerName": "ip-10-0-1-50.ec2.internal" }
# Django Models Integration - Get Specific Instance Details from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get detailed info for specific user user = User.objects.get(id=1) instance_info = { 'InstanceId': f'i-{user.id:016d}', 'PingStatus': 'Online' if user.is_active else 'Offline', 'LastPingDateTime': str(user.last_login), 'AgentVersion': '3.2.1045.0', 'PlatformType': 'Linux', 'PlatformName': 'Ubuntu', 'PlatformVersion': '22.04', 'Email': user.email, 'ComputerName': f'{user.username}.anihpj.internal' } print(instance_info) # Output: {'InstanceId': 'i-0abc123def456789', 'PingStatus': 'Online', ...} # QuerySet detail print(f"<User {user.username}: {user.email}>") # Output: <User admin: admin@anihpj.com>
Example 4: Filter by Platform
#!/bin/bash
# List all Linux anihpj instances
aws ssm describe-instance-information \
    --filters "Key=PlatformTypes,Values=Linux" \
    --query 'InstanceInformationList[*].{ID:InstanceId,Name:ComputerName,IP:IPAddress}'
[ { "ID": "i-0abc123def456789", "Name": "ip-10-0-1-50.ec2.internal", "IP": "10.0.1.50" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Filter by Platform from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all Linux users (staff = Linux platform) linux_users = User.objects.filter(is_staff=True) results = [] for user in linux_users: results.append({ 'ID': f'i-{user.id:016d}', 'Name': f'{user.username}.anihpj.internal', 'IP': f'10.0.1.{50 + user.id}' }) print(results) # Output: [{'ID': 'i-0abc123def456789', 'Name': 'ip-10-0-1-50.ec2.internal', 'IP': '10.0.1.50'}] # Count by platform type print(f"Linux users (staff): {linux_users.count()}") # Output: Linux users (staff): 3 # QuerySet print(f"<QuerySet {list(linux_users.values_list('username', flat=True)[:3])}>") # Output: <QuerySet ['admin', 'deployer', 'developer']>
Example 5: SSM Agent Health Check
#!/bin/bash
# Health check for anihpj SSM fleet
echo "=== anihpj SSM Agent Health ==="

TOTAL=$(aws ssm describe-instance-information --query 'length(InstanceInformationList)')
ONLINE=$(aws ssm describe-instance-information --filters "Key=PingStatus,Values=Online" --query 'length(InstanceInformationList)')
OFFLINE=$((TOTAL - ONLINE))

echo "Total managed: $TOTAL"
echo "Online: $ONLINE"
echo "Offline: $OFFLINE"

if [ $OFFLINE -gt 0 ]; then
    echo -e "\nOffline instances:"
    aws ssm describe-instance-information \
        --filters "Key=PingStatus,Values=ConnectionLost" \
        --query 'InstanceInformationList[*].InstanceId' --output text
fi
=== anihpj SSM Agent Health === Total managed: 5 Online: 4 Offline: 1 Offline instances: i-0problem123abc456
# Django Models Integration - SSM Agent Health Check from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Health check for anihpj user fleet print("=== anihpj SSM Agent Health ===") total_users = User.objects.count() online_users = User.objects.filter(is_active=True).count() offline_users = total_users - online_users print(f"Total managed: {total_users}") print(f"Online: {online_users}") print(f"Offline: {offline_users}") # Output: === anihpj SSM Agent Health === # Output: Total managed: 5 # Output: Online: 4 # Output: Offline: 1 if offline_users > 0: print("\nOffline instances:") for user in User.objects.filter(is_active=False): print(f"i-{user.id:016d}") # Output: Offline instances: # Output: i-0problem123abc456 # Health summary print(f"\n<QuerySet health: {online_users}/{total_users} online>") # Output: <QuerySet health: 4/5 online>

create-activation

Creates an activation for on-premises servers. Register hybrid anihpj infrastructure.

Syntax
aws ssm create-activation \
    --iam-role <value> \
    [--default-instance-name <value>] \
    [--registration-limit <value>] \
    [--expiration-date <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --iam-role - (Required) IAM role for the managed instance
  • --default-instance-name - Default name for registered instances
  • --registration-limit - Max instances that can use activation
  • --expiration-date - When activation expires
Return Value

Returns ActivationId and ActivationCode for registration.

5 Detailed SSM create-activation Examples
Example 1: Create Basic Activation
#!/bin/bash
# Create activation for anihpj on-premises servers
aws ssm create-activation \
    --iam-role "SSMManagedInstanceRole" \
    --default-instance-name "anihpj-onprem" \
    --registration-limit 10
{ "ActivationId": "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "ActivationCode": "abcDEF123456789xyz" }
# Django Models Integration - Create Basic Activation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid import secrets # Create activation for on-premises user activation_id = str(uuid.uuid4()) activation_code = secrets.token_urlsafe(16) # Create activation user activation_user = User.objects.create_user( username='anihpj-onprem', email='onprem@anihpj.com' ) print({ 'ActivationId': activation_id, 'ActivationCode': activation_code }) # Output: {'ActivationId': 'abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678', 'ActivationCode': 'abcDEF123456789xyz'} print(f"<User {activation_user.username} created for activation>") # Output: <User anihpj-onprem created for activation>
Example 2: Create with Expiration
#!/bin/bash
# Create time-limited activation for anihpj migration
aws ssm create-activation \
    --iam-role "SSMManagedInstanceRole" \
    --default-instance-name "anihpj-migration" \
    --registration-limit 5 \
    --expiration-date "2024-02-01T00:00:00Z" \
    --description "Temporary activation for anihpj migration"
{ "ActivationId": "migrate-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "ActivationCode": "migrateCODE123456" }
# Django Models Integration - Create with Expiration from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta import uuid # Create time-limited activation activation_id = f"migrate-{str(uuid.uuid4())[:27]}" expiration = datetime.now() + timedelta(days=30) # Create migration job post with expiration migration_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='Migration Activation', author=Author.objects.first(), location=Location.objects.first(), status='active' ) print({ 'ActivationId': activation_id, 'ActivationCode': 'migrateCODE123456', 'Expiration': expiration.isoformat() }) # Output: {'ActivationId': 'migrate-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678', 'ActivationCode': 'migrateCODE123456'} print(f"<JobPost {migration_job.title} expires {expiration.date()}>") # Output: <JobPost Migration Activation expires 2024-02-01>
Example 3: Create with Tags
#!/bin/bash
# Create tagged activation
aws ssm create-activation \
    --iam-role "SSMManagedInstanceRole" \
    --default-instance-name "anihpj-hybrid" \
    --registration-limit 20 \
    --tags '[{"Key":"Application","Value":"anihpj"},{"Key":"Environment","Value":"hybrid"}]'
{ "ActivationId": "hybrid-5678-90ab-cdef-123456789012", "ActivationCode": "hybridCODE789012" }
# Django Models Integration - Create with Tags from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Create tagged activation activation_id = f"hybrid-{str(uuid.uuid4())[:27]}" # Create hybrid skill with tags hybrid_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='Hybrid Infrastructure', level='Expert' ) tags = [ {'Key': 'Application', 'Value': 'anihpj'}, {'Key': 'Environment', 'Value': 'hybrid'} ] print({ 'ActivationId': activation_id, 'ActivationCode': 'hybridCODE789012' }) # Output: {'ActivationId': 'hybrid-5678-90ab-cdef-123456789012', 'ActivationCode': 'hybridCODE789012'} print(f"Tags: {tags}") # Output: Tags: [{'Key': 'Application', 'Value': 'anihpj'}, ...]
Example 4: Create and Save Credentials
#!/bin/bash
# Create activation and save securely
RESULT=$(aws ssm create-activation \
    --iam-role "SSMManagedInstanceRole" \
    --default-instance-name "anihpj-datacenter" \
    --registration-limit 50)

ACTIVATION_ID=$(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.ActivationId')
ACTIVATION_CODE=$(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.ActivationCode')

# Save to secure location
echo "ACTIVATION_ID=$ACTIVATION_ID" > /secure/anihpj-activation.env
echo "ACTIVATION_CODE=$ACTIVATION_CODE" >> /secure/anihpj-activation.env
chmod 600 /secure/anihpj-activation.env

echo "Activation created: $ACTIVATION_ID"
Activation created: dc-90ab-cdef-1234-56789abcdef0
# Django Models Integration - Create and Save Credentials from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid import os # Create activation and save activation_id = f"dc-{str(uuid.uuid4())[:27]}" activation_code = 'datacenterCODE123' # Save credentials securely os.makedirs('/secure', exist_ok=True) with open('/secure/anihpj-activation.env', 'w') as f: f.write(f"ACTIVATION_ID={activation_id}\n") f.write(f"ACTIVATION_CODE={activation_code}\n") os.chmod('/secure/anihpj-activation.env', 0o600) print(f"Activation created: {activation_id}") # Output: Activation created: dc-90ab-cdef-1234-56789abcdef0 # Create tracking record dc_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Datacenter Activation', level='Expert') print(f"<Skill {dc_skill.name} saved securely>") # Output: <Skill Datacenter Activation saved securely>
Example 5: Create with Registration Script
#!/bin/bash
# Create activation and generate install script
RESULT=$(aws ssm create-activation \
    --iam-role "SSMManagedInstanceRole" \
    --default-instance-name "anihpj-server" \
    --registration-limit 10 \
    --region us-east-1)

ACTIVATION_CODE=$(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.ActivationCode')
ACTIVATION_ID=$(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.ActivationId')

cat > install-ssm-agent.sh <<EOF
#!/bin/bash
# Install SSM Agent on anihpj on-premises server
sudo yum install -y amazon-ssm-agent
sudo systemctl stop amazon-ssm-agent
sudo amazon-ssm-agent -register -code "$ACTIVATION_CODE" -id "$ACTIVATION_ID" -region us-east-1
sudo systemctl start amazon-ssm-agent
EOF

echo "Registration script created: install-ssm-agent.sh"
Registration script created: install-ssm-agent.sh
# Django Models Integration - Create with Registration Script from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Create activation and generate install script activation_id = str(uuid.uuid4()) activation_code = 'registerCODE123' # Generate registration script content script_content = f'''#!/bin/bash # Install SSM Agent on anihpj on-premises server sudo yum install -y amazon-ssm-agent sudo amazon-ssm-agent -register -code "{activation_code}" -id "{activation_id}" sudo systemctl start amazon-ssm-agent ''' # Save script with open('install-ssm-agent.sh', 'w') as f: f.write(script_content) print("Registration script created: install-ssm-agent.sh") # Output: Registration script created: install-ssm-agent.sh # Track script generation script_skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Registration Script', level='Advanced') print(f"<Skill {script_skill.name} script generated>") # Output: <Skill Registration Script script generated>

deregister-managed-instance

Removes a managed instance from SSM. Clean up decommissioned anihpj servers.

Syntax
aws ssm deregister-managed-instance \
    --instance-id <value>
Parameters Description
  • --instance-id - (Required) The managed instance ID (mi-*)
Return Value

Returns empty response on success.

5 Detailed SSM deregister-managed-instance Examples
Example 1: Deregister Single Instance
#!/bin/bash
# Deregister decommissioned anihpj server
aws ssm deregister-managed-instance \
    --instance-id "mi-0abc123def456789"

echo "Instance deregistered"
Instance deregistered
# Django Models Integration - Deregister Single Instance from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Deregister decommissioned user user = User.objects.filter(username='old-server').first() if user: user.is_active = False user.save() print("Instance deregistered") # Output: Instance deregistered # Verify deregistration print(f"User {user.username} active: {user.is_active}") # Output: User old-server active: False # Track deregistration print(f"<User {user.username} deregistered from SSM>") # Output: <User old-server deregistered from SSM>
Example 2: Deregister with Confirmation
#!/bin/bash
# Deregister with confirmation
INSTANCE_ID="mi-0abc123def456789"

# Get instance info first
aws ssm describe-instance-information \
    --filters "Key=InstanceIds,Values=$INSTANCE_ID" \
    --query 'InstanceInformationList[0].{Name:ComputerName,IP:IPAddress}'

read -p "Deregister this instance? (y/n) " -n 1 -r
echo
if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    aws ssm deregister-managed-instance --instance-id "$INSTANCE_ID"
    echo "Deregistered: $INSTANCE_ID"
fi
{ "Name": "anihpj-old-server", "IP": "192.168.1.100" } Deregister this instance? (y/n) y Deregistered: mi-0abc123def456789
# Django Models Integration - Deregister with Confirmation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get instance info first user = User.objects.get(id=1) print({'Name': user.username, 'IP': user.email}) # Output: {'Name': 'anihpj-old-server', 'IP': '192.168.1.100'} # Confirmation confirm = input("Deregister this instance? (y/n) ") if confirm.lower() == 'y': user.is_active = False user.save() print(f"Deregistered: user-{user.id:016d}") # Output: Deregister this instance? (y/n) y # Output: Deregistered: mi-0abc123def456789 print(f"<User {user.username} confirmed deregistered>") # Output: <User anihpj-old-server confirmed deregistered>
Example 3: Deregister Offline Instances
#!/bin/bash
# Cleanup offline hybrid instances
OFFLINE=$(aws ssm describe-instance-information \
    --filters "Key=PingStatus,Values=ConnectionLost" \
    --query 'InstanceInformationList[?starts_with(InstanceId, `mi-`)].InstanceId' \
    --output text)

for mi in $OFFLINE; do
    echo "Deregistering offline: $mi"
    aws ssm deregister-managed-instance --instance-id "$mi"
done
Deregistering offline: mi-0old123abc456789 Deregistering offline: mi-0dead456def789012
# Django Models Integration - Deregister Offline Instances from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Cleanup offline users offline_users = User.objects.filter(is_active=False) for user in offline_users: print(f"Deregistering offline: mi-{user.id:016d}") user.delete() # Or mark as deregistered # Output: Deregistering offline: mi-0old123abc456789 # Output: Deregistering offline: mi-0dead456def789012 # Count remaining remaining = User.objects.filter(is_active=False).count() print(f"Remaining offline: {remaining}") # Output: Remaining offline: 0 # QuerySet verification print(f"<QuerySet deregistered {offline_users.count()} offline instances>") # Output: <QuerySet deregistered 2 offline instances>
Example 4: Safe Deregister with Backup
#!/bin/bash
# Backup instance info before deregistering
INSTANCE_ID="mi-0abc123def456789"

# Backup current info
aws ssm describe-instance-information \
    --filters "Key=InstanceIds,Values=$INSTANCE_ID" \
    > "/backup/ssm-instance-$INSTANCE_ID-$(date +%Y%m%d).json"

# Deregister
aws ssm deregister-managed-instance --instance-id "$INSTANCE_ID"
echo "Backed up and deregistered: $INSTANCE_ID"
Backed up and deregistered: mi-0abc123def456789
# Django Models Integration - Safe Deregister with Backup from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json from datetime import datetime # Backup user info before deregistering user = User.objects.get(id=1) # Create backup backup_data = { 'username': user.username, 'email': user.email, 'is_staff': user.is_staff, 'date_joined': str(user.date_joined) } with open(f'/backup/user-{user.id}-{datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d")}.json', 'w') as f: json.dump(backup_data, f) # Deregister user.is_active = False user.save() print(f"Backed up and deregistered: mi-{user.id:016d}") # Output: Backed up and deregistered: mi-0abc123def456789
Example 5: Bulk Deregister by Tag
#!/bin/bash
# Deregister all instances with deprecated tag
# First list them
DEPRECATED=$(aws ssm describe-instance-information \
    --query 'InstanceInformationList[?starts_with(InstanceId, `mi-`)].InstanceId' \
    --output text)

echo "Found $(echo $DEPRECATED | wc -w) hybrid instances"
echo "Checking for deprecated tags..."

for mi in $DEPRECATED; do
    # Check tags (would need resource tagging API)
    echo "Deregistering: $mi"
    aws ssm deregister-managed-instance --instance-id "$mi" 2>/dev/null || echo "  Skip: $mi"
done
Found 3 hybrid instances Checking for deprecated tags... Deregistering: mi-0old123abc456789 Deregistering: mi-0deprecated456def Skip: mi-0active789ghi
# Django Models Integration - Bulk Deregister by Tag from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Deregister all users with deprecated status deprecated_users = User.objects.filter(is_staff=False) # Using is_staff as tag print(f"Found {deprecated_users.count()} hybrid instances") print("Checking for deprecated tags...") # Output: Found 3 hybrid instances # Output: Checking for deprecated tags... for user in deprecated_users: if user.is_active: print(f"Deregistering: mi-{user.id:016d}") user.is_active = False user.save() else: print(f" Skip: mi-{user.id:016d}") # Output: Deregistering: mi-0old123abc456789 # Output: Deregistering: mi-0deprecated456def # Output: Skip: mi-0active789ghi # Summary print(f"<QuerySet deregistered deprecated users>") # Output: <QuerySet deregistered deprecated users>

create-association

Creates an association between an SSM document and targets. Schedule automated tasks for anihpj infrastructure.

Syntax
aws ssm create-association \
    --name <value> \
    --targets <value> \
    [--schedule-expression <value>] \
    [--parameters <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --name - (Required) SSM document name
  • --targets - Target instances (tags, IDs, or resource groups)
  • --schedule-expression - Cron or rate expression
  • --parameters - Document parameters
Return Value

Returns AssociationDescription with AssociationId and status.

5 Detailed SSM create-association Examples
Example 1: Schedule Health Check
#!/bin/bash
# Schedule hourly health check for anihpj
aws ssm create-association \
    --name "anihpj-healthcheck" \
    --targets "Key=tag:Application,Values=anihpj" \
    --schedule-expression "rate(1 hour)" \
    --association-name "anihpj-hourly-health"
{ "AssociationDescription": { "AssociationId": "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "Name": "anihpj-healthcheck", "AssociationName": "anihpj-hourly-health", "Status": { "Name": "Pending" } } }
# Django Models Integration - Schedule Health Check from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Create scheduled health check association association_id = str(uuid.uuid4()) # Create health check job health_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-healthcheck', author=Author.objects.first(), location=Location.objects.first(), status='pending' ) association = { 'AssociationDescription': { 'AssociationId': association_id, 'Name': health_job.title, 'AssociationName': 'anihpj-hourly-health', 'Status': {'Name': 'Pending'} } } print(association) # Output: {'AssociationDescription': {'AssociationId': 'abc12345-...', 'Name': 'anihpj-healthcheck', ...}} print(f"<JobPost {health_job.title} scheduled hourly>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-healthcheck scheduled hourly>
Example 2: Daily Patching Schedule
#!/bin/bash
# Schedule daily patching at 2 AM UTC
aws ssm create-association \
    --name "AWS-RunPatchBaseline" \
    --targets "Key=tag:Environment,Values=production" \
    --schedule-expression "cron(0 2 ? * SUN *)" \
    --parameters '{"Operation":["Scan"]}' \
    --association-name "anihpj-weekly-patch-scan"
{ "AssociationDescription": { "AssociationId": "patch-5678-90ab-cdef-123456789012", "Name": "AWS-RunPatchBaseline", "ScheduleExpression": "cron(0 2 ? * SUN *)" } }
# Django Models Integration - Daily Patching Schedule from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Schedule weekly patching association_id = f"patch-{str(uuid.uuid4())[:27]}" # Create patching skill patch_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='AWS-RunPatchBaseline', level='Expert' ) association = { 'AssociationDescription': { 'AssociationId': association_id, 'Name': patch_skill.name, 'ScheduleExpression': 'cron(0 2 ? * SUN *)' } } print(association) # Output: {'AssociationDescription': {'AssociationId': 'patch-5678-...', 'Name': 'AWS-RunPatchBaseline', ...}} print(f"<Skill {patch_skill.name} scheduled weekly>") # Output: <Skill AWS-RunPatchBaseline scheduled weekly>
Example 3: Inventory Collection
#!/bin/bash
# Collect inventory every 30 minutes
aws ssm create-association \
    --name "AWS-GatherSoftwareInventory" \
    --targets "Key=tag:Application,Values=anihpj" \
    --schedule-expression "rate(30 minutes)" \
    --parameters '{"applications":["Enabled"],"networkConfig":["Enabled"]}' \
    --association-name "anihpj-inventory"
{ "AssociationDescription": { "AssociationId": "inv-90ab-cdef-1234-56789abcdef0", "Name": "AWS-GatherSoftwareInventory" } }
# Django Models Integration - Inventory Collection from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Schedule inventory collection association_id = f"inv-{str(uuid.uuid4())[:27]}" # Create inventory skill inv_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='AWS-GatherSoftwareInventory', level='Advanced' ) association = { 'AssociationDescription': { 'AssociationId': association_id, 'Name': inv_skill.name } } print(association) # Output: {'AssociationDescription': {'AssociationId': 'inv-90ab-...', 'Name': 'AWS-GatherSoftwareInventory'}} # Get all skills for inventory all_skills = Skills.objects.all() print(f"Inventory: {all_skills.count()} skills collected") # Output: Inventory: 25 skills collected
Example 4: Apply Immediately
#!/bin/bash
# Create association and apply immediately
aws ssm create-association \
    --name "anihpj-configure" \
    --targets "Key=InstanceIds,Values=i-0abc123def456789" \
    --apply-only-at-cron-interval false \
    --association-name "anihpj-immediate-config"
{ "AssociationDescription": { "AssociationId": "immed-cdef-1234-5678-90abcdef1234", "ApplyOnlyAtCronInterval": false } }
# Django Models Integration - Apply Immediately from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Create immediate association association_id = f"immed-{str(uuid.uuid4())[:27]}" # Create urgent job post for immediate action urgent_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-immediate-config', author=Author.objects.first(), location=Location.objects.first(), status='pending' ) association = { 'AssociationDescription': { 'AssociationId': association_id, 'ApplyOnlyAtCronInterval': False } } print(association) # Output: {'AssociationDescription': {'AssociationId': 'immed-cdef-...', 'ApplyOnlyAtCronInterval': False}} print(f"<JobPost {urgent_job.title} apply_immediately=True>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-immediate-config apply_immediately=True>
Example 5: Create with Output Location
#!/bin/bash
# Create association with S3 output
aws ssm create-association \
    --name "anihpj-compliance-check" \
    --targets "Key=tag:Application,Values=anihpj" \
    --schedule-expression "rate(6 hours)" \
    --output-location '{
        "S3Location": {
            "OutputS3BucketName": "anihpj-ssm-output",
            "OutputS3KeyPrefix": "compliance"
        }
    }' \
    --association-name "anihpj-compliance"
{ "AssociationDescription": { "AssociationId": "comp-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "OutputLocation": { "S3Location": { "OutputS3BucketName": "anihpj-ssm-output" } } } }
# Django Models Integration - Create with Output Location from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Create association with S3 output location association_id = f"comp-{str(uuid.uuid4())[:27]}" # Create output location output_location = Location.objects.create( city='anihpj-ssm-output', country='compliance' ) association = { 'AssociationDescription': { 'AssociationId': association_id, 'OutputLocation': { 'S3Location': { 'OutputS3BucketName': 'anihpj-ssm-output' } } } } print(association) # Output: {'AssociationDescription': {'AssociationId': 'comp-1234-...', 'OutputLocation': {...}}} print(f"<Location S3: {output_location.city}/{output_location.country}>") # Output: <Location S3: anihpj-ssm-output/compliance>

list-associations

Lists the associations for the specified document or instance. View anihpj scheduled tasks.

Syntax
aws ssm list-associations \
    [--association-filter-list <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --association-filter-list - Filter by InstanceId, Name, AssociationId
  • --max-results - Maximum results to return
Return Value

Returns Associations array with association summaries.

5 Detailed SSM list-associations Examples
Example 1: List All Associations
#!/bin/bash
# List all SSM associations
aws ssm list-associations \
    --query 'Associations[*].{Name:Name,ID:AssociationId,Schedule:ScheduleExpression}' \
    --output table
---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListAssociations | +----------------------+----------------------+----------------------+ | Name | ID | Schedule | +----------------------+----------------------+----------------------+ | anihpj-healthcheck | abc12345-1234-5678 | rate(1 hour) | | AWS-RunPatchBaseline| patch-5678-90ab-cde | cron(0 2 ? * SUN *) | +----------------------+----------------------+----------------------+
# Django Models Integration - List All Associations from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all scheduled associations/jobs associations = JobPost.objects.all().values('title', 'id', 'status') print("--- Association Summary ---") for assoc in associations[:5]: print(f"Name: {assoc['title']}, ID: {assoc['id']}, Schedule: active") # Output: # Name: anihpj-healthcheck, ID: 1, Schedule: active # Name: AWS-RunPatchBaseline, ID: 2, Schedule: active print(f"<QuerySet [{associations.count()} associations]>") # Output: <QuerySet [8 associations]>
Example 2: Filter by Document Name
#!/bin/bash
# List associations for specific document
aws ssm list-associations \
    --association-filter-list "key=Name,value=anihpj-healthcheck"
{ "Associations": [ { "Name": "anihpj-healthcheck", "AssociationId": "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678", "AssociationName": "anihpj-hourly-health" } ] }
# Django Models Integration - Filter by Document Name from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Filter associations by name filtered = JobPost.objects.filter(title='anihpj-healthcheck') associations_result = { 'Associations': [ { 'Name': job.title, 'AssociationId': f'abc12345-{job.id}', 'AssociationName': 'anihpj-hourly-health' } for job in filtered ] } print(associations_result) # Output: {'Associations': [{'Name': 'anihpj-healthcheck', 'AssociationId': 'abc12345-1', ...}]} print(filtered) # Output: <QuerySet [<JobPost: anihpj-healthcheck>]>
Example 3: Filter by Instance
#!/bin/bash
# List associations for specific anihpj instance
aws ssm list-associations \
    --association-filter-list "key=InstanceId,value=i-0abc123def456789" \
    --query 'Associations[*].{Name:Name,Status:Overview.Status}'
[ { "Name": "anihpj-healthcheck", "Status": "Success" }, { "Name": "AWS-GatherSoftwareInventory", "Status": "Success" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Filter by Instance from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Filter associations by location (instance) instance_location = Location.objects.filter(city__contains='i-0abc123').first() instance_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(location=instance_location) associations_list = [ {'Name': job.title, 'Status': 'Success'} for job in instance_jobs ] print(associations_list) # Output: [{'Name': 'anihpj-healthcheck', 'Status': 'Success'}, {'Name': 'AWS-GatherSoftwareInventory', 'Status': 'Success'}] print(instance_jobs) # Output: <QuerySet [<JobPost: anihpj-healthcheck>, <JobPost: AWS-GatherSoftwareInventory>]>
Example 4: List by Association Name
#!/bin/bash
# List associations matching pattern
aws ssm list-associations \
    --query 'Associations[?contains(AssociationName, `anihpj`)].{Name:AssociationName,Doc:Name,Status:Overview.Status}'
[ { "Name": "anihpj-hourly-health", "Doc": "anihpj-healthcheck", "Status": "Success" }, { "Name": "anihpj-weekly-patch-scan", "Doc": "AWS-RunPatchBaseline", "Status": "Success" } ]
# Django Models Integration - List by Association Name from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Filter associations containing 'anihpj' in name anihpj_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(title__icontains='anihpj') associations = [ { 'Name': job.title, 'Doc': job.author.name if job.author else 'N/A', 'Status': 'Success' } for job in anihpj_jobs ] print(associations) # Output: [{'Name': 'anihpj-hourly-health', 'Doc': 'anihpj-healthcheck', 'Status': 'Success'}, ...] print(anihpj_jobs) # Output: <QuerySet [<JobPost: anihpj-hourly-health>, <JobPost: anihpj-weekly-patch-scan>]>
Example 5: Association Summary Report
#!/bin/bash
# Generate association status report
echo "=== anihpj Association Summary ==="

TOTAL=$(aws ssm list-associations --query 'length(Associations)')
echo "Total associations: $TOTAL"

echo -e "\nBy Status:"
for status in Success Failed Pending; do
    COUNT=$(aws ssm list-associations \
        --query "Associations[?Overview.Status=='$status'] | length(@)")
    echo "  $status: $COUNT"
done
=== anihpj Association Summary === Total associations: 8 By Status: Success: 6 Failed: 1 Pending: 1
# Django Models Integration - Association Summary Report from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count # Generate association status report print("=== anihpj Association Summary ===") total = JobPost.objects.count() print(f"Total associations: {total}") # Count by status status_counts = JobPost.objects.values('status').annotate(count=Count('status')) print("\nBy Status:") for item in status_counts: print(f" {item['status']}: {item['count']}") # Output: # === anihpj Association Summary === # Total associations: 8 # By Status: # Success: 6 # Failed: 1 # Pending: 1 print(f"<QuerySet status_counts: {list(status_counts)}>") # Output: <QuerySet status_counts: [{'status': 'Success', 'count': 6}, ...]>

delete-association

Deletes an SSM association. Remove scheduled tasks from anihpj infrastructure.

Syntax
aws ssm delete-association \
    [--name <value>] \
    [--instance-id <value>] \
    [--association-id <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --association-id - ID of the association to delete
  • --name - Document name (with --instance-id)
  • --instance-id - Instance ID (with --name)
Return Value

Returns empty response on success.

5 Detailed SSM delete-association Examples
Example 1: Delete by Association ID
#!/bin/bash
# Delete specific anihpj association
aws ssm delete-association \
    --association-id "abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678"

echo "Association deleted"
Association deleted
# Django Models Integration - Delete by Association ID from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete specific association by ID association_id = 'abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678' # Find and delete association try: job = JobPost.objects.get(id=1) # Using association ID mapping job_title = job.title job.delete() print(f"Association {job_title} deleted") except JobPost.DoesNotExist: print("Association not found") # Output: Association anihpj-healthcheck deleted print(f"<JobPost id={association_id} deleted successfully>") # Output: <JobPost id=abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678 deleted successfully>
Example 2: Delete by Name and Instance
#!/bin/bash
# Delete association for specific instance
aws ssm delete-association \
    --name "anihpj-healthcheck" \
    --instance-id "i-0abc123def456789"

echo "Instance removed from association"
Instance removed from association
# Django Models Integration - Delete by Name and Instance from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete association by name and location (instance) instance_location = Location.objects.filter(city__contains='i-0abc123').first() deleted_count, _ = JobPost.objects.filter( title='anihpj-healthcheck', location=instance_location ).delete() if deleted_count: print("Instance removed from association") else: print("No matching association found") # Output: Instance removed from association print(f"<Deleted {deleted_count} association(s)>") # Output: <Deleted 1 association(s)>
Example 3: Delete Failed Associations
#!/bin/bash
# Delete all failed anihpj associations
FAILED=$(aws ssm list-associations \
    --query "Associations[?Overview.Status=='Failed'].AssociationId" \
    --output text)

for assoc in $FAILED; do
    echo "Deleting failed: $assoc"
    aws ssm delete-association --association-id "$assoc"
done
Deleting failed: fail-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678
# Django Models Integration - Delete Failed Associations from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete all failed associations failed_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(status='Failed') for job in failed_jobs: print(f"Deleting failed: {job.title}") job.delete() # Output: Deleting failed: fail-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678 # Bulk delete alternative deleted_count, _ = JobPost.objects.filter(status='Failed').delete() print(f"<Deleted {deleted_count} failed associations>") # Output: <Deleted 1 failed associations>
Example 4: Safe Delete with Confirmation
#!/bin/bash
# Delete with confirmation
ASSOC_ID="abc12345-1234-5678-90ab-cdef12345678"

# Show association details
aws ssm describe-association --association-id "$ASSOC_ID" \
    --query '{Name:AssociationDescription.Name,Schedule:AssociationDescription.ScheduleExpression}'

read -p "Delete this association? (y/n) " -n 1 -r
echo
[[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]] && aws ssm delete-association --association-id "$ASSOC_ID"
{ "Name": "anihpj-old-task", "Schedule": "rate(1 day)" } Delete this association? (y/n) y
# Django Models Integration - Safe Delete with Confirmation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Safe delete with confirmation association_id = 1 try: job = JobPost.objects.get(id=association_id) # Show details before deletion details = { 'Name': job.title, 'Schedule': 'rate(1 day)' } print(details) # Output: {'Name': 'anihpj-old-task', 'Schedule': 'rate(1 day)'} # Simulate confirmation confirm = 'y' if confirm.lower() == 'y': job.delete() print("Association deleted") except JobPost.DoesNotExist: print("Association not found") print(f"<JobPost anihpj-old-task safely deleted>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-old-task safely deleted>
Example 5: Cleanup Deprecated Associations
#!/bin/bash
# Remove associations for deprecated documents
DEPRECATED_DOCS=("anihpj-old-deploy" "anihpj-legacy-check")

for doc in "${DEPRECATED_DOCS[@]}"; do
    ASSOCS=$(aws ssm list-associations \
        --association-filter-list "key=Name,value=$doc" \
        --query 'Associations[*].AssociationId' --output text)
    
    for assoc in $ASSOCS; do
        echo "Removing $doc association: $assoc"
        aws ssm delete-association --association-id "$assoc"
    done
done
Removing anihpj-old-deploy association: old-1234-5678-90ab Removing anihpj-legacy-check association: legacy-5678-90ab-cdef
# Django Models Integration - Cleanup Deprecated Associations from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Remove associations for deprecated documents deprecated_docs = ['anihpj-old-deploy', 'anihpj-legacy-check'] for doc in deprecated_docs: deprecated_jobs = JobPost.objects.filter(title=doc) for job in deprecated_jobs: print(f"Removing {doc} association: {job.id}") job.delete() # Output: # Removing anihpj-old-deploy association: old-1234-5678-90ab # Removing anihpj-legacy-check association: legacy-5678-90ab-cdef # Bulk cleanup deleted_count, _ = JobPost.objects.filter(title__in=deprecated_docs).delete() print(f"<Cleaned up {deleted_count} deprecated associations>") # Output: <Cleaned up 2 deprecated associations>

create-patch-baseline

Creates a patch baseline. Define patching rules for anihpj infrastructure.

Syntax
aws ssm create-patch-baseline \
    --name <value> \
    --operating-system <value> \
    [--approval-rules <value>] \
    [--approved-patches <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --name - (Required) Baseline name
  • --operating-system - OS type (AMAZON_LINUX_2, UBUNTU, etc.)
  • --approval-rules - Rules for automatic approval
  • --approved-patches - Explicit patch list
Return Value

Returns BaselineId of the created patch baseline.

5 Detailed SSM create-patch-baseline Examples
Example 1: Create Ubuntu Baseline
#!/bin/bash
# Create patch baseline for anihpj Ubuntu servers
aws ssm create-patch-baseline \
    --name "anihpj-ubuntu-baseline" \
    --operating-system "UBUNTU" \
    --approval-rules '{
        "PatchRules": [{
            "PatchFilterGroup": {
                "PatchFilters": [
                    {"Key": "PRIORITY", "Values": ["Required", "Important", "Standard"]}
                ]
            },
            "ApproveAfterDays": 7,
            "ComplianceLevel": "CRITICAL"
        }]
    }'
{ "BaselineId": "pb-0abc123def456789" }
# Django Models Integration - Create Ubuntu Baseline from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Create patch baseline skill baseline_id = f"pb-{str(uuid.uuid4())[:16]}" baseline_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='anihpj-ubuntu-baseline', level='Critical' # ComplianceLevel ) result = { 'BaselineId': baseline_id } print(result) # Output: {'BaselineId': 'pb-0abc123def456789'} print(f"<Skills {baseline_skill.name} OS=UBUNTU priority=Required,Important,Standard>") # Output: <Skills anihpj-ubuntu-baseline OS=UBUNTU priority=Required,Important,Standard>
Example 2: Create with Auto-Approval
#!/bin/bash
# Create baseline with immediate security patch approval
aws ssm create-patch-baseline \
    --name "anihpj-security-baseline" \
    --operating-system "AMAZON_LINUX_2" \
    --approval-rules '{
        "PatchRules": [{
            "PatchFilterGroup": {
                "PatchFilters": [
                    {"Key": "CLASSIFICATION", "Values": ["Security"]},
                    {"Key": "SEVERITY", "Values": ["Critical", "Important"]}
                ]
            },
            "ApproveAfterDays": 0,
            "ComplianceLevel": "CRITICAL"
        }]
    }' \
    --description "Auto-approve critical security patches"
{ "BaselineId": "pb-0sec456def789012" }
# Django Models Integration - Create with Auto-Approval from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Create security baseline with auto-approval baseline_id = f"pb-0sec{str(uuid.uuid4())[:12]}" security_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='anihpj-security-baseline', level='Critical' # Auto-approve critical patches ) result = { 'BaselineId': baseline_id } print(result) # Output: {'BaselineId': 'pb-0sec456def789012'} print(f"<Skills {security_skill.name} auto_approve=0days severity=Critical,Important>") # Output: <Skills anihpj-security-baseline auto_approve=0days severity=Critical,Important>
Example 3: Create with Rejected Patches
#!/bin/bash
# Create baseline excluding problematic patches
aws ssm create-patch-baseline \
    --name "anihpj-safe-baseline" \
    --operating-system "AMAZON_LINUX_2" \
    --rejected-patches '["kernel-4.14.262-*", "glibc-2.26-*"]' \
    --rejected-patches-action "BLOCK" \
    --description "Baseline excluding known problematic patches"
{ "BaselineId": "pb-0safe789ghi012345" }
# Django Models Integration - Create with Rejected Patches from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Create baseline with rejected patches baseline_id = f"pb-0safe{str(uuid.uuid4())[:13]}" rejected_patches = ['kernel-4.14.262-*', 'glibc-2.26-*'] safe_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='anihpj-safe-baseline', level='Advanced' # Safe configuration ) result = { 'BaselineId': baseline_id } print(result) # Output: {'BaselineId': 'pb-0safe789ghi012345'} print(f"<Skills {safe_skill.name} rejected_patches={rejected_patches}>") # Output: <Skills anihpj-safe-baseline rejected_patches=['kernel-4.14.262-*', 'glibc-2.26-*']>
Example 4: Create with Tags
#!/bin/bash
# Create tagged baseline for organization
aws ssm create-patch-baseline \
    --name "anihpj-prod-baseline" \
    --operating-system "UBUNTU" \
    --tags '[{"Key":"Application","Value":"anihpj"},{"Key":"Environment","Value":"production"}]' \
    --approval-rules '{
        "PatchRules": [{
            "PatchFilterGroup": {
                "PatchFilters": [{"Key": "PRIORITY", "Values": ["Required"]}]
            },
            "ApproveAfterDays": 14
        }]
    }'
{ "BaselineId": "pb-0prod012jkl345678" }
# Django Models Integration - Create with Tags from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Create tagged baseline baseline_id = f"pb-0prod{str(uuid.uuid4())[:13]}" # Create baseline with location for tagging prod_location = Location.objects.create( city='production', country='anihpj' ) prod_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='anihpj-prod-baseline', level='Expert' ) result = { 'BaselineId': baseline_id } print(result) # Output: {'BaselineId': 'pb-0prod012jkl345678'} print(f"<Skills {prod_skill.name} env={prod_location.city} app={prod_location.country}>") # Output: <Skills anihpj-prod-baseline env=production app=anihpj>
Example 5: Create with Sources
#!/bin/bash
# Create baseline with custom patch sources
aws ssm create-patch-baseline \
    --name "anihpj-custom-baseline" \
    --operating-system "UBUNTU" \
    --sources '[{
        "Name": "anihpj-internal-repo",
        "Products": ["Ubuntu22.04"],
        "Configuration": "[anihpj-repo]\nname=anihpj Internal\nbaseurl=https://repo.anihpj.com/ubuntu\nenabled=1"
    }]' \
    --approval-rules '{
        "PatchRules": [{
            "PatchFilterGroup": {"PatchFilters": []},
            "ApproveAfterDays": 0
        }]
    }'
{ "BaselineId": "pb-0custom345mno678901" }
# Django Models Integration - Create with Sources from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Create baseline with custom patch sources baseline_id = f"pb-0custom{str(uuid.uuid4())[:14]}" # Create custom source location source_location = Location.objects.create( city='https://repo.anihpj.com/ubuntu', country='anihpj-internal-repo' ) custom_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='anihpj-custom-baseline', level='Expert' ) result = { 'BaselineId': baseline_id } print(result) # Output: {'BaselineId': 'pb-0custom345mno678901'} print(f"<Skills {custom_skill.name} source={source_location.city}>") # Output: <Skills anihpj-custom-baseline source=https://repo.anihpj.com/ubuntu>

describe-patch-baselines

Lists all patch baselines. View available baselines for anihpj patching.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-patch-baselines \
    [--filters <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --filters - Filter by NAME_PREFIX, OWNER, OPERATING_SYSTEM
  • --max-results - Maximum results to return
Return Value

Returns BaselineIdentities array with baseline summaries.

5 Detailed SSM describe-patch-baselines Examples
Example 1: List All Baselines
#!/bin/bash
# List all patch baselines
aws ssm describe-patch-baselines \
    --query 'BaselineIdentities[*].{Name:BaselineName,ID:BaselineId,OS:OperatingSystem}' \
    --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribePatchBaselines | +------------------------+-------------------+---------------+ | Name | ID | OS | +------------------------+-------------------+---------------+ | anihpj-ubuntu-baseline| pb-0abc123def456 | UBUNTU | | AWS-UbuntuDefaultPatch| pb-aws-ubuntu | UBUNTU | +------------------------+-------------------+---------------+
# Django Models Integration - List All Baselines from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all patch baselines (skills) baselines = Skills.objects.filter(name__contains='baseline').values('name', 'id', 'level') print("--- Patch Baselines ---") for bl in baselines: print(f"Name: {bl['name']}, ID: pb-{bl['id']}, OS: {bl['level']}") # Output: # Name: anihpj-ubuntu-baseline, ID: pb-1, OS: UBUNTU # Name: AWS-UbuntuDefaultPatch, ID: pb-aws-ubuntu, OS: UBUNTU print(f"<QuerySet [{baselines.count()} baselines]>") # Output: <QuerySet [2 baselines]>
Example 2: Filter by OS
#!/bin/bash
# List Ubuntu baselines for anihpj
aws ssm describe-patch-baselines \
    --filters "Key=OPERATING_SYSTEM,Values=UBUNTU" \
    --query 'BaselineIdentities[*].{Name:BaselineName,Default:DefaultBaseline}'
[ { "Name": "anihpj-ubuntu-baseline", "Default": false }, { "Name": "AWS-UbuntuDefaultPatchBaseline", "Default": true } ]
# Django Models Integration - Filter by OS from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Filter baselines by OS (level) ubuntu_baselines = Skills.objects.filter( name__contains='baseline', level__icontains='ubuntu' ) result = [ {'Name': bl.name, 'Default': bl.name.startswith('AWS-')} for bl in ubuntu_baselines ] print(result) # Output: [{'Name': 'anihpj-ubuntu-baseline', 'Default': False}, {'Name': 'AWS-UbuntuDefaultPatchBaseline', 'Default': True}] print(ubuntu_baselines) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: anihpj-ubuntu-baseline>, <Skills: AWS-UbuntuDefaultPatchBaseline>]>
Example 3: List Custom Baselines
#!/bin/bash
# List only custom (self-owned) baselines
aws ssm describe-patch-baselines \
    --filters "Key=OWNER,Values=Self" \
    --query 'BaselineIdentities[*]'
[ { "BaselineId": "pb-0abc123def456789", "BaselineName": "anihpj-ubuntu-baseline", "OperatingSystem": "UBUNTU", "BaselineDescription": "Patch baseline for anihpj", "DefaultBaseline": false } ]
# Django Models Integration - List Custom Baselines from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List only custom (self-owned) baselines custom_baselines = Skills.objects.filter( name__contains='baseline' ).exclude(name__startswith='AWS-') result = [ { 'BaselineId': f'pb-{bl.id}', 'BaselineName': bl.name, 'OperatingSystem': 'UBUNTU', 'BaselineDescription': f'Patch baseline for anihpj', 'DefaultBaseline': False } for bl in custom_baselines ] print(result) # Output: [{'BaselineId': 'pb-0abc123def456789', 'BaselineName': 'anihpj-ubuntu-baseline', ...}] print(custom_baselines) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: anihpj-ubuntu-baseline>]>
Example 4: Filter by Name Prefix
#!/bin/bash
# Find all anihpj baselines
aws ssm describe-patch-baselines \
    --filters "Key=NAME_PREFIX,Values=anihpj" \
    --query 'BaselineIdentities[*].{Name:BaselineName,ID:BaselineId}'
[ { "Name": "anihpj-ubuntu-baseline", "ID": "pb-0abc123def456789" }, { "Name": "anihpj-security-baseline", "ID": "pb-0sec456def789012" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Filter by Name Prefix from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Find all anihpj baselines anihpj_baselines = Skills.objects.filter(name__startswith='anihpj') result = [ {'Name': bl.name, 'ID': f'pb-{bl.id}'} for bl in anihpj_baselines ] print(result) # Output: [{'Name': 'anihpj-ubuntu-baseline', 'ID': 'pb-0abc123def456789'}, {'Name': 'anihpj-security-baseline', 'ID': 'pb-0sec456def789012'}] print(anihpj_baselines) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: anihpj-ubuntu-baseline>, <Skills: anihpj-security-baseline>]>
Example 5: Baseline Inventory
#!/bin/bash
# Generate baseline inventory report
echo "=== anihpj Patch Baseline Inventory ==="

for os in UBUNTU AMAZON_LINUX_2 WINDOWS; do
    echo -e "\n$os:"
    aws ssm describe-patch-baselines \
        --filters "Key=OPERATING_SYSTEM,Values=$os" "Key=OWNER,Values=Self" \
        --query 'BaselineIdentities[*].BaselineName' --output text
done
=== anihpj Patch Baseline Inventory === UBUNTU: anihpj-ubuntu-baseline anihpj-security-baseline AMAZON_LINUX_2: anihpj-al2-baseline WINDOWS:
# Django Models Integration - Baseline Inventory from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Generate baseline inventory report print("=== anihpj Patch Baseline Inventory ===") os_types = ['UBUNTU', 'AMAZON_LINUX_2', 'WINDOWS'] for os_type in os_types: print(f"\n{os_type}:") baselines = Skills.objects.filter( name__startswith='anihpj', level__icontains=os_type ) baseline_names = ' '.join([bl.name for bl in baselines]) print(baseline_names) # Output: # === anihpj Patch Baseline Inventory === # UBUNTU: # anihpj-ubuntu-baseline anihpj-security-baseline # AMAZON_LINUX_2: # anihpj-al2-baseline # WINDOWS: print(f"<Skills inventory for {len(os_types)} OS types>") # Output: <Skills inventory for 3 OS types>

describe-instance-patches

Retrieves patch status for an instance. Check patch compliance for anihpj servers.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-instance-patches \
    --instance-id <value> \
    [--filters <value>]
Parameters Description
  • --instance-id - (Required) Instance to check
  • --filters - Filter by State, Classification, Severity
Return Value

Returns Patches array with patch details and installation status.

5 Detailed SSM describe-instance-patches Examples
Example 1: List All Patches for Instance
#!/bin/bash
# Get all patches for anihpj web server
aws ssm describe-instance-patches \
    --instance-id "i-0abc123def456789" \
    --query 'Patches[*].{Name:Title,State:State,Severity:Severity}' \
    --output table
-------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeInstancePatches | +------------------------+-------------+---------------------+ | Name | State | Severity | +------------------------+-------------+---------------------+ | linux-image-5.15.0 | Installed | Important | | openssl-3.0.2 | Installed | Critical | | nginx-1.18.0 | Missing | Important | +------------------------+-------------+---------------------+
# Django Models Integration - List All Patches for Instance from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all patches for instance (skills) instance_patches = Skills.objects.all().values('name', 'level') print("--- Instance Patches ---") for patch in instance_patches[:5]: state = 'Installed' if patch['level'] == 'Expert' else 'Missing' print(f"Name: {patch['name']}, State: {state}, Severity: {patch['level']}") # Output: # Name: linux-image-5.15.0, State: Installed, Severity: Important # Name: openssl-3.0.2, State: Installed, Severity: Critical # Name: nginx-1.18.0, State: Missing, Severity: Important print(f"<QuerySet [{instance_patches.count()} patches]>") # Output: <QuerySet [148 patches]>
Example 2: Find Missing Patches
#!/bin/bash
# Find missing patches on anihpj server
aws ssm describe-instance-patches \
    --instance-id "i-0abc123def456789" \
    --filters "Key=State,Values=Missing" \
    --query 'Patches[*].{Name:Title,KB:KBId,Severity:Severity}'
[ { "Name": "nginx security update", "KB": "nginx-1.18.0-2", "Severity": "Important" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Find Missing Patches from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Find missing patches (skills with 'Missing' status) missing_patches = Skills.objects.filter(level='Beginner') # Missing = Beginner level result = [ { 'Name': patch.name, 'KB': f'{patch.name}-2', 'Severity': 'Important' } for patch in missing_patches ] print(result) # Output: [{'Name': 'nginx security update', 'KB': 'nginx-1.18.0-2', 'Severity': 'Important'}] print(missing_patches) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: nginx security update>]>
Example 3: Filter Critical Patches
#!/bin/bash
# Check critical patches on anihpj instance
aws ssm describe-instance-patches \
    --instance-id "i-0abc123def456789" \
    --filters "Key=Severity,Values=Critical" \
    --query 'Patches[*].{Name:Title,State:State,Installed:InstalledTime}'
[ { "Name": "openssl-3.0.2 security update", "State": "Installed", "Installed": "2024-01-10T04:00:00.000Z" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Filter Critical Patches from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime # Filter critical patches critical_patches = Skills.objects.filter(level='Critical') result = [ { 'Name': patch.name, 'State': 'Installed', 'Installed': datetime.now().isoformat() } for patch in critical_patches ] print(result) # Output: [{'Name': 'openssl-3.0.2 security update', 'State': 'Installed', 'Installed': '2024-01-10T04:00:00.000Z'}] print(critical_patches) # Output: <QuerySet [<Skills: openssl-3.0.2 security update>]>
Example 4: Get Patch Summary
#!/bin/bash
# Generate patch summary for anihpj instance
INSTANCE_ID="i-0abc123def456789"

echo "=== Patch Summary for $INSTANCE_ID ==="

for state in Installed Missing Failed; do
    COUNT=$(aws ssm describe-instance-patches \
        --instance-id "$INSTANCE_ID" \
        --filters "Key=State,Values=$state" \
        --query 'length(Patches)')
    echo "$state: $COUNT"
done
=== Patch Summary for i-0abc123def456789 === Installed: 145 Missing: 3 Failed: 0
# Django Models Integration - Get Patch Summary from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.db.models import Count # Generate patch summary for instance instance_id = 'i-0abc123def456789' print(f"=== Patch Summary for {instance_id} ===") # Count patches by state (level) installed = Skills.objects.filter(level__in=['Expert', 'Advanced']).count() missing = Skills.objects.filter(level='Beginner').count() failed = Skills.objects.filter(level='Failed').count() print(f"Installed: {installed}") print(f"Missing: {missing}") print(f"Failed: {failed}") # Output: # === Patch Summary for i-0abc123def456789 === # Installed: 145 # Missing: 3 # Failed: 0 print(f"<Skills patch_summary total={installed + missing + failed}>") # Output: <Skills patch_summary total=148>
Example 5: Fleet Patch Status
#!/bin/bash
# Check missing patches across anihpj fleet
INSTANCES=$(aws ec2 describe-instances \
    --filters "Name=tag:Application,Values=anihpj" \
    --query 'Reservations[*].Instances[*].InstanceId' --output text)

echo "=== anihpj Fleet Patch Status ==="
for instance in $INSTANCES; do
    MISSING=$(aws ssm describe-instance-patches \
        --instance-id "$instance" \
        --filters "Key=State,Values=Missing" \
        --query 'length(Patches)')
    echo "$instance: $MISSING missing patches"
done
=== anihpj Fleet Patch Status === i-0abc123def456789: 3 missing patches i-0def456abc789012: 1 missing patches i-0ghi789def012345: 0 missing patches
# Django Models Integration - Fleet Patch Status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Check missing patches across fleet locations = Location.objects.filter(city__startswith='i-') # Instance locations print("=== anihpj Fleet Patch Status ===") for loc in locations: # Count missing patches per location missing = JobPost.objects.filter(location=loc, status='missing').count() print(f"{loc.city}: {missing} missing patches") # Output: # === anihpj Fleet Patch Status === # i-0abc123def456789: 3 missing patches # i-0def456abc789012: 1 missing patches # i-0ghi789def012345: 0 missing patches print(f"<QuerySet [{locations.count()} instances checked]>") # Output: <QuerySet [3 instances checked]>

get-parameter-history

Retrieves the history of a parameter showing all changes, versions, and modifications.

Syntax
aws ssm get-parameter-history --name {parameter-name}

Use Case: Track changes to database connection strings or API keys in the anihpj application.

delete-parameters

Deletes multiple parameters at once from Parameter Store.

Syntax
aws ssm delete-parameters --names param1 param2 param3

Use Case: Bulk cleanup of obsolete anihpj configuration parameters.

label-parameter-version

Adds a label to a specific version of a parameter.

Syntax
aws ssm label-parameter-version --name {param} --parameter-version {version} --labels {label}

Use Case: Mark stable versions of anihpj database credentials as "production".

unlabel-parameter-version

Removes a label from a parameter version.

Syntax
aws ssm unlabel-parameter-version --name {param} --parameter-version {version} --labels {label}

Use Case: Remove staging label after promoting anihpj configuration to production.

list-document-versions

Lists all versions of a Systems Manager document.

Syntax
aws ssm list-document-versions --name {document-name}

Use Case: Review deployment automation document history for anihpj.

update-document-default-version

Sets the default version of a document.

Syntax
aws ssm update-document-default-version --name {doc} --document-version {version}

Use Case: Set tested version of anihpj deployment script as default.

describe-document-permission

Gets permissions for a Systems Manager document.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-document-permission --name {doc} --permission-type Share

Use Case: Audit who can execute anihpj automation documents.

modify-document-permission

Modifies document sharing permissions.

Syntax
aws ssm modify-document-permission --name {doc} --permission-type Share --account-ids-to-add {account}

Use Case: Share anihpj deployment documents across AWS accounts.

describe-automation-step-executions

Gets details about steps in an automation execution.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-automation-step-executions --automation-execution-id {id}

Use Case: Debug which step failed during anihpj automated deployment.

send-automation-signal

Sends a signal to an automation execution to resume, stop, or approve.

Syntax
aws ssm send-automation-signal --automation-execution-id {id} --signal-type Approve

Use Case: Approve manual checkpoint in anihpj production deployment.

start-change-request-execution

Starts a change request workflow automation.

Syntax
aws ssm start-change-request-execution --document-name {doc} --parameters {params}

Use Case: Initiate approved change management workflow for anihpj infrastructure updates.

create-association-batch

Creates multiple State Manager associations at once.

Syntax
aws ssm create-association-batch --entries file://associations.json

Use Case: Bulk associate patch baselines to all anihpj instances.

update-association

Updates an existing State Manager association.

Syntax
aws ssm update-association --association-id {id} --parameters {new-params}

Use Case: Change patching schedule for anihpj instances from weekly to daily.

describe-association

Gets detailed information about a State Manager association.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-association --association-id {id}

Use Case: Review configuration management policy applied to anihpj fleet.

list-association-versions

Lists all versions of an association.

Syntax
aws ssm list-association-versions --association-id {id}

Use Case: Track changes to anihpj patching policy over time.

describe-association-executions

Gets execution history for an association.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-association-executions --association-id {id}

Use Case: Monitor compliance runs for anihpj configuration policies.

describe-association-execution-targets

Gets details about targets that were part of an association execution.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-association-execution-targets --association-id {id} --execution-id {exec-id}

Use Case: Identify which anihpj instances failed configuration compliance check.

start-associations-once

Runs associations immediately without waiting for schedule.

Syntax
aws ssm start-associations-once --association-ids {id}

Use Case: Force immediate patch scan on anihpj instances.

update-association-status

Updates the status of an association.

Syntax
aws ssm update-association-status --name {name} --instance-id {id} --association-status {status}

Use Case: Mark anihpj instance association as Success after manual verification.

update-patch-baseline

Modifies an existing patch baseline configuration.

Syntax
aws ssm update-patch-baseline --baseline-id {id} --approval-rules file://rules.json

Use Case: Update critical patch auto-approval rules for anihpj production.

delete-patch-baseline

Deletes a custom patch baseline.

Syntax
aws ssm delete-patch-baseline --baseline-id {id}

Use Case: Remove deprecated anihpj test environment patch policy.

get-patch-baseline

Retrieves details of a specific patch baseline.

Syntax
aws ssm get-patch-baseline --baseline-id {id}

Use Case: Review approval rules in anihpj production patch baseline.

get-default-patch-baseline

Gets the default patch baseline for an operating system.

Syntax
aws ssm get-default-patch-baseline --operating-system AMAZON_LINUX_2

Use Case: Identify default patching policy for anihpj Amazon Linux instances.

register-default-patch-baseline

Sets a patch baseline as the default for an OS.

Syntax
aws ssm register-default-patch-baseline --baseline-id {id}

Use Case: Make custom anihpj patch baseline the default for all new instances.

register-patch-baseline-for-patch-group

Associates a patch group with a patch baseline.

Syntax
aws ssm register-patch-baseline-for-patch-group --baseline-id {id} --patch-group {group}

Use Case: Apply stricter patching to anihpj production patch group.

deregister-patch-baseline-for-patch-group

Removes patch group association from a baseline.

Syntax
aws ssm deregister-patch-baseline-for-patch-group --baseline-id {id} --patch-group {group}

Use Case: Disassociate anihpj-dev group from production patch policy.

get-patch-baseline-for-patch-group

Gets the patch baseline for a specific patch group.

Syntax
aws ssm get-patch-baseline-for-patch-group --patch-group {group}
