Use Case: Verify which baseline applies to anihpj-production patch group.

describe-patch-groups

Lists all patch groups and their associated baselines.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-patch-groups

Use Case: Audit all patch group configurations across anihpj infrastructure.

describe-patch-group-state

Gets the patch state for all instances in a patch group.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-patch-group-state --patch-group {group}

Use Case: Get compliance summary for anihpj-production patch group.

describe-available-patches

Lists available patches that match specified criteria.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-available-patches --filters Key=CLASSIFICATION,Values=Security

Use Case: Find critical security patches for anihpj Django dependencies.

describe-patch-properties

Gets properties of available patches.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-patch-properties --operating-system AMAZON_LINUX_2 --property PRODUCT

Use Case: List all patch products available for anihpj OS.

describe-instance-patch-states

Gets high-level patch state for one or more instances.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-instance-patch-states --instance-ids {id1} {id2}

Use Case: Quick compliance check across anihpj instance fleet.

describe-instance-patch-states-for-patch-group

Gets patch states for all instances in a patch group.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-instance-patch-states-for-patch-group --patch-group {group}

Use Case: Monitor patch compliance for entire anihpj-production group.

describe-effective-patches-for-patch-baseline

Gets all patches defined by a patch baseline.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-effective-patches-for-patch-baseline --baseline-id {id}

Use Case: See exactly which patches will be installed with anihpj baseline.

get-deployable-patch-snapshot-for-instance

Gets the patches to be installed on a specific instance.

Syntax
aws ssm get-deployable-patch-snapshot-for-instance --instance-id {id} --snapshot-id {snap-id}

Use Case: Preview patches before installing on anihpj production instance.

create-maintenance-window

Creates a maintenance window for scheduled operations.

Syntax
aws ssm create-maintenance-window --name {name} --schedule "cron(0 2 ? * SUN *)" --duration 4 --cutoff 1

Use Case: Schedule Sunday 2 AM patching window for anihpj instances.

update-maintenance-window

Modifies maintenance window settings.

Syntax
aws ssm update-maintenance-window --window-id {id} --duration 6

Use Case: Extend anihpj patching window from 4 to 6 hours.

delete-maintenance-window

Deletes a maintenance window.

Syntax
aws ssm delete-maintenance-window --window-id {id}

Use Case: Remove deprecated anihpj test environment maintenance window.

get-maintenance-window

Gets details of a maintenance window.

Syntax
aws ssm get-maintenance-window --window-id {id}

Use Case: Review anihpj production patching window configuration.

describe-maintenance-windows

Lists all maintenance windows.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-maintenance-windows

Use Case: Audit all scheduled maintenance across anihpj infrastructure.

describe-maintenance-windows-for-target

Gets maintenance windows that include a specific target.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-maintenance-windows-for-target --targets Key=InstanceIds,Values={id}

Use Case: Check which maintenance windows apply to specific anihpj instance.

register-target-with-maintenance-window

Adds targets to a maintenance window.

Syntax
aws ssm register-target-with-maintenance-window --window-id {id} --targets Key=tag:App,Values=anihpj

Use Case: Add all anihpj-tagged instances to patching window.

deregister-target-from-maintenance-window

Removes targets from a maintenance window.

Syntax
aws ssm deregister-target-from-maintenance-window --window-id {id} --window-target-id {target-id}

Use Case: Remove decommissioned anihpj instances from patching window.

update-maintenance-window-target

Updates target configuration in a maintenance window.

Syntax
aws ssm update-maintenance-window-target --window-id {id} --window-target-id {target-id} --targets {new-targets}

Use Case: Change anihpj target selection from instance IDs to tags.

describe-maintenance-window-targets

Lists targets registered with a maintenance window.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-maintenance-window-targets --window-id {id}

Use Case: Verify which anihpj instances are in patching window.

register-task-with-maintenance-window

Adds a task to execute during a maintenance window.

Syntax
aws ssm register-task-with-maintenance-window --window-id {id} --task-arn AWS-RunPatchBaseline --targets {targets}

Use Case: Schedule patch installation task for anihpj instances.

deregister-task-from-maintenance-window

Removes a task from a maintenance window.

Syntax
aws ssm deregister-task-from-maintenance-window --window-id {id} --window-task-id {task-id}

Use Case: Remove obsolete anihpj backup task from maintenance window.

update-maintenance-window-task

Modifies a maintenance window task.

Syntax
aws ssm update-maintenance-window-task --window-id {id} --window-task-id {task-id} --priority 5

Use Case: Increase priority of critical anihpj patching task.

get-maintenance-window-task

Gets details about a specific task in a maintenance window.

Syntax
aws ssm get-maintenance-window-task --window-id {id} --window-task-id {task-id}

Use Case: Review configuration of anihpj patching task.

describe-maintenance-window-tasks

Lists all tasks in a maintenance window.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-maintenance-window-tasks --window-id {id}

Use Case: Audit all operations scheduled for anihpj maintenance window.

describe-maintenance-window-schedule

Gets the schedule for maintenance windows.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-maintenance-window-schedule --window-id {id}

Use Case: View upcoming anihpj patching window executions.

describe-maintenance-window-executions

Lists executions of a maintenance window.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-maintenance-window-executions --window-id {id}

Use Case: Review history of anihpj patching window runs.

get-maintenance-window-execution

Gets details about a specific maintenance window execution.

Syntax
aws ssm get-maintenance-window-execution --window-execution-id {exec-id}

Use Case: Debug failed anihpj patching window execution.

describe-maintenance-window-execution-tasks

Lists tasks that ran during a maintenance window execution.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-maintenance-window-execution-tasks --window-execution-id {exec-id}

Use Case: See which anihpj tasks completed successfully.

get-maintenance-window-execution-task

Gets details about a task execution.

Syntax
aws ssm get-maintenance-window-execution-task --window-execution-id {exec-id} --task-id {task-id}

Use Case: Review anihpj patch installation task results.

describe-maintenance-window-execution-task-invocations

Lists invocations of a task during a maintenance window execution.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-maintenance-window-execution-task-invocations --window-execution-id {exec-id} --task-id {task-id}

Use Case: See patch installation status for each anihpj instance.

get-maintenance-window-execution-task-invocation

Gets details about a task invocation.

Syntax
aws ssm get-maintenance-window-execution-task-invocation --window-execution-id {exec-id} --task-id {task-id} --invocation-id {inv-id}

Use Case: Debug why patching failed on specific anihpj instance.

cancel-maintenance-window-execution

Stops an in-progress maintenance window execution.

Syntax
aws ssm cancel-maintenance-window-execution --window-execution-id {exec-id}

Use Case: Abort anihpj patching window if critical issues detected.

describe-instance-associations-status

Gets status of associations for an instance.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-instance-associations-status --instance-id {id}

Use Case: Check compliance status of anihpj instance associations.

describe-effective-instance-associations

Gets all associations currently applied to an instance.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-effective-instance-associations --instance-id {id}

Use Case: See all policies active on anihpj Django server.

describe-instance-properties

Gets properties of managed instances.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-instance-properties

Use Case: List all managed instances in anihpj infrastructure.

delete-activation

Deletes an activation code.

Syntax
aws ssm delete-activation --activation-id {id}

Use Case: Revoke unused anihpj hybrid instance activation code.

describe-activations

Lists all activation codes.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-activations

Use Case: Audit activation codes created for anihpj on-premise servers.

update-managed-instance-role

Updates the IAM role of a managed instance.

Syntax
aws ssm update-managed-instance-role --instance-id {id} --iam-role {role-name}

Use Case: Update IAM permissions for anihpj hybrid instances.

list-nodes

Lists all nodes managed by Systems Manager.

Syntax
aws ssm list-nodes

Use Case: Get complete inventory of anihpj managed infrastructure.

list-nodes-summary

Gets summary information about nodes.

Syntax
aws ssm list-nodes-summary

Use Case: Quick overview of anihpj fleet health and compliance.

put-inventory

Adds or updates inventory data.

Syntax
aws ssm put-inventory --instance-id {id} --items file://inventory.json

Use Case: Report custom anihpj application versions to inventory.

get-inventory

Queries inventory data.

Syntax
aws ssm get-inventory --filters Key=AWS:Application.Name,Values=anihpj

Use Case: Find all instances running specific Django version.

get-inventory-schema

Gets the schema for inventory data types.

Syntax
aws ssm get-inventory-schema

Use Case: Understand custom inventory attributes for anihpj tracking.

list-inventory-entries

Lists inventory entries for an instance.

Syntax
aws ssm list-inventory-entries --instance-id {id} --type-name AWS:Application

Use Case: List all installed applications on anihpj servers.

delete-inventory

Deletes custom inventory data.

Syntax
aws ssm delete-inventory --type-name Custom:anihpj --schema-delete-option DeleteSchema

Use Case: Remove obsolete custom inventory schema for anihpj.

describe-inventory-deletions

Gets status of inventory deletion operations.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-inventory-deletions

Use Case: Monitor cleanup of anihpj legacy inventory data.

put-compliance-items

Registers compliance information.

Syntax
aws ssm put-compliance-items --resource-id {id} --compliance-type Custom:anihpj --items file://compliance.json

Use Case: Report custom anihpj security policy compliance.

list-compliance-items

Lists compliance items for a resource.

Syntax
aws ssm list-compliance-items --resource-ids {id}

Use Case: Review all compliance checks for anihpj instance.

list-compliance-summaries

Gets summary of compliance status by compliance type.

Syntax
aws ssm list-compliance-summaries

Use Case: Dashboard view of anihpj fleet compliance status.

list-resource-compliance-summaries

Gets compliance summary for each resource.

Syntax
aws ssm list-resource-compliance-summaries --filters Key=ComplianceType,Values=Patch

Use Case: Identify non-compliant anihpj instances.

create-ops-item

Creates an OpsCenter item to track operational issues.

Syntax
aws ssm create-ops-item --title "anihpj DB Connection Issue" --source "CloudWatch" --severity 2

Use Case: Log operational issues with anihpj application.

update-ops-item

Updates an OpsItem.

Syntax
aws ssm update-ops-item --ops-item-id {id} --status Resolved

Use Case: Mark anihpj incident as resolved.

delete-ops-item

Deletes an OpsItem.

Syntax
aws ssm delete-ops-item --ops-item-id {id}

Use Case: Remove duplicate anihpj operational ticket.

get-ops-item

Gets details of an OpsItem.

Syntax
aws ssm get-ops-item --ops-item-id {id}

Use Case: Review details of anihpj incident.

describe-ops-items

Lists OpsItems based on filters.

Syntax
aws ssm describe-ops-items --ops-item-filters Key=Status,Values=Open

Use Case: List all open incidents for anihpj application.

get-ops-summary

Gets aggregated summary of OpsItems.

Syntax
aws ssm get-ops-summary

Use Case: Dashboard view of anihpj operational health.

associate-ops-item-related-item

Associates a related resource with an OpsItem.

Syntax
aws ssm associate-ops-item-related-item --ops-item-id {id} --resource-uri {arn}

Use Case: Link anihpj EC2 instance to performance incident.

disassociate-ops-item-related-item

Removes a related resource from an OpsItem.

Syntax
aws ssm disassociate-ops-item-related-item --ops-item-id {id} --association-id {assoc-id}

Use Case: Unlink incorrect resource from anihpj incident.

list-ops-item-events

Lists events related to an OpsItem.

Syntax
aws ssm list-ops-item-events --ops-item-id {id}

Use Case: Timeline of actions taken on anihpj incident.

list-ops-item-related-items

Lists resources related to an OpsItem.

Syntax
aws ssm list-ops-item-related-items --ops-item-id {id}

Use Case: See all anihpj resources involved in incident.

create-ops-metadata

Creates operational metadata.

Syntax
aws ssm create-ops-metadata --resource-id {id} --metadata file://metadata.json

Use Case: Store anihpj operational runbook metadata.

update-ops-metadata

Updates operational metadata.

Syntax
aws ssm update-ops-metadata --ops-metadata-arn {arn} --metadata-to-update {updates}

Use Case: Update anihpj runbook procedures.

delete-ops-metadata

Deletes operational metadata.

Syntax
aws ssm delete-ops-metadata --ops-metadata-arn {arn}

Use Case: Remove obsolete anihpj operational metadata.

get-ops-metadata

Gets operational metadata.

Syntax
aws ssm get-ops-metadata --ops-metadata-arn {arn}

Use Case: Retrieve anihpj incident response procedures.

list-ops-metadata

Lists all operational metadata.

Syntax
aws ssm list-ops-metadata

Use Case: Inventory all anihpj operational documentation.

create-resource-data-sync

Creates a sync to aggregate inventory data to S3.

Syntax
aws ssm create-resource-data-sync --sync-name {name} --s3-destination BucketName={bucket}

Use Case: Sync anihpj fleet inventory to central S3 bucket.

update-resource-data-sync

Updates a resource data sync.

Syntax
aws ssm update-resource-data-sync --sync-name {name} --s3-destination {new-config}

Use Case: Change S3 destination for anihpj inventory sync.

delete-resource-data-sync

Deletes a resource data sync.

Syntax
aws ssm delete-resource-data-sync --sync-name {name}

Use Case: Remove deprecated anihpj inventory sync.

list-resource-data-sync

Lists all resource data syncs.

Syntax
aws ssm list-resource-data-sync

Use Case: Audit inventory sync configurations for anihpj.

get-calendar-state

Gets the state of a change calendar (OPEN or CLOSED).

Syntax
aws ssm get-calendar-state --calendar-names {calendar-name}

Use Case: Check if anihpj deployment window is currently open.

get-service-setting

Gets Systems Manager service settings.

Syntax
aws ssm get-service-setting --setting-id {setting-id}

Use Case: Review SSM agent auto-update settings for anihpj fleet.

update-service-setting

Updates Systems Manager service settings.

Syntax
aws ssm update-service-setting --setting-id {setting-id} --setting-value {value}

Use Case: Configure SSM agent update policy for anihpj instances.

reset-service-setting

Resets a service setting to default.

Syntax
aws ssm reset-service-setting --setting-id {setting-id}

Use Case: Restore default SSM agent settings for anihpj.

add-tags-to-resource

Adds tags to SSM resources.

Syntax
aws ssm add-tags-to-resource --resource-type Document --resource-id {doc-name} --tags Key=App,Value=anihpj

Use Case: Tag anihpj automation documents for organization.

remove-tags-from-resource

Removes tags from SSM resources.

Syntax
aws ssm remove-tags-from-resource --resource-type Document --resource-id {doc-name} --tag-keys Environment

Use Case: Clean up obsolete tags on anihpj documents.

list-tags-for-resource

Lists tags for an SSM resource.

Syntax
aws ssm list-tags-for-resource --resource-type Document --resource-id {doc-name}

Use Case: Audit tags on anihpj automation documents.

put-resource-policy

Attaches a resource policy to an SSM resource.

Syntax
aws ssm put-resource-policy --resource-arn {arn} --policy file://policy.json

Use Case: Share anihpj Parameter Store values with other AWS accounts.

get-resource-policies

Gets resource policies attached to an SSM resource.

Syntax
aws ssm get-resource-policies --resource-arn {arn}

Use Case: Review sharing permissions for anihpj parameters.

delete-resource-policy

Deletes a resource policy.

Syntax
aws ssm delete-resource-policy --resource-arn {arn} --policy-id {policy-id}

Use Case: Remove cross-account access to anihpj parameters.

AWS CloudTrail

AWS CloudTrail is a service that enables governance, compliance, operational auditing, and risk auditing of your AWS account. CloudTrail logs, continuously monitors, and retains account activity related to actions across your AWS infrastructure. Use CloudTrail to track API calls, user activity, and changes to your anihpj Django application resources.

create-trail

Creates a trail that specifies the settings for delivery of log data to an Amazon S3 bucket.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail create-trail \
  --name {trail-name} \
  --s3-bucket-name {bucket-name}
Parameters Description
  • --name - Name for the trail
  • --s3-bucket-name - S3 bucket for log delivery
  • --is-multi-region-trail - (Optional) Log events in all regions
  • --enable-log-file-validation - (Optional) Enable digest file validation
Return Value

Returns Name, S3BucketName, TrailARN, and other trail configuration details.

5 Detailed CloudTrail create-trail Examples
Example 1: Create Basic Trail for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Create a basic trail for anihpj Django application
aws cloudtrail create-trail \
  --name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --s3-bucket-name anihpj-cloudtrail-logs

echo "anihpj CloudTrail created"
{ "Name": "anihpj-audit-trail", "S3BucketName": "anihpj-cloudtrail-logs", "IncludeGlobalServiceEvents": true, "IsMultiRegionTrail": false, "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail", "LogFileValidationEnabled": false, "IsOrganizationTrail": false } anihpj CloudTrail created
# Django Models Integration - Create Basic Trail from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create audit trail record audit_trail = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-audit-trail', author=Author.objects.first(), location=Location.objects.create( city='anihpj-cloudtrail-logs', country='us-east-1' ), status='active' ) result = { 'Name': audit_trail.title, 'S3BucketName': audit_trail.location.city, 'IncludeGlobalServiceEvents': True, 'IsMultiRegionTrail': False, 'TrailARN': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/{audit_trail.title}', 'LogFileValidationEnabled': False } print(result) # Output: {'Name': 'anihpj-audit-trail', 'S3BucketName': 'anihpj-cloudtrail-logs', ...} print(f"<JobPost {audit_trail.title} CloudTrail created>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail CloudTrail created>
Example 2: Create Multi-Region Trail with Encryption
#!/bin/bash
# Create comprehensive multi-region trail for anihpj
aws cloudtrail create-trail \
  --name anihpj-global-audit-trail \
  --s3-bucket-name anihpj-cloudtrail-logs \
  --s3-key-prefix "anihpj/audit" \
  --is-multi-region-trail \
  --include-global-service-events \
  --enable-log-file-validation \
  --kms-key-id "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/abc123-def456"

echo "anihpj multi-region trail with encryption created"
{ "Name": "anihpj-global-audit-trail", "S3BucketName": "anihpj-cloudtrail-logs", "S3KeyPrefix": "anihpj/audit", "IncludeGlobalServiceEvents": true, "IsMultiRegionTrail": true, "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-global-audit-trail", "LogFileValidationEnabled": true, "KMSKeyId": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/abc123-def456", "IsOrganizationTrail": false } anihpj multi-region trail with encryption created
# Django Models Integration - Multi-Region Trail with Encryption from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create multi-region trail global_trail = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-global-audit-trail', author=Author.objects.first(), location=Location.objects.create( city='anihpj-cloudtrail-logs', country='anihpj/audit' ), status='active' ) # Create encryption skill encryption = Skills.objects.create( name='kms-encryption', level='abc123-def456' ) result = { 'Name': global_trail.title, 'S3BucketName': global_trail.location.city, 'S3KeyPrefix': global_trail.location.country, 'IsMultiRegionTrail': True, 'LogFileValidationEnabled': True, 'KMSKeyId': f'arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/{encryption.level}' } print(result) # Output: {'Name': 'anihpj-global-audit-trail', 'S3BucketName': 'anihpj-cloudtrail-logs', ...} print(f"<JobPost {global_trail.title} multi-region encrypted>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-global-audit-trail multi-region encrypted>
Example 3: Create Trail with CloudWatch Logs Integration
#!/bin/bash
# Create trail with CloudWatch Logs for real-time monitoring
aws cloudtrail create-trail \
  --name anihpj-realtime-audit \
  --s3-bucket-name anihpj-cloudtrail-logs \
  --cloud-watch-logs-log-group-arn "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:anihpj-cloudtrail:*" \
  --cloud-watch-logs-role-arn "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-cloudtrail-role" \
  --is-multi-region-trail \
  --enable-log-file-validation

echo "anihpj trail with CloudWatch integration created"
echo "Real-time monitoring enabled for anihpj"
{ "Name": "anihpj-realtime-audit", "S3BucketName": "anihpj-cloudtrail-logs", "IncludeGlobalServiceEvents": true, "IsMultiRegionTrail": true, "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-realtime-audit", "LogFileValidationEnabled": true, "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:anihpj-cloudtrail:*", "CloudWatchLogsRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-cloudtrail-role", "IsOrganizationTrail": false } anihpj trail with CloudWatch integration created Real-time monitoring enabled for anihpj
# Django Models Integration - Trail with CloudWatch Logs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create realtime audit trail with CloudWatch realtime_trail = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-realtime-audit', author=Author.objects.first(), location=Location.objects.first(), status='active' ) # Create CloudWatch skill for integration cloudwatch_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='CloudWatch-Integration', level='anihpj-cloudtrail' ) result = { 'Name': realtime_trail.title, 'CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn': f'arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:{cloudwatch_skill.level}:*', 'LogFileValidationEnabled': True, 'IsMultiRegionTrail': True } print(result) # Output: {'Name': 'anihpj-realtime-audit', 'CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn': '...', ...} print(f"<JobPost {realtime_trail.title} with CloudWatch>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-realtime-audit with CloudWatch>
Example 4: Create Trail with SNS Notifications
#!/bin/bash
# Create trail with SNS notifications for anihpj alerts
aws cloudtrail create-trail \
  --name anihpj-alert-trail \
  --s3-bucket-name anihpj-cloudtrail-logs \
  --sns-topic-name anihpj-cloudtrail-alerts \
  --is-multi-region-trail \
  --include-global-service-events

echo "anihpj trail with SNS alerts created"
echo "Notifications will be sent for each log file delivery"
{ "Name": "anihpj-alert-trail", "S3BucketName": "anihpj-cloudtrail-logs", "SnsTopicName": "anihpj-cloudtrail-alerts", "SnsTopicARN": "arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-cloudtrail-alerts", "IncludeGlobalServiceEvents": true, "IsMultiRegionTrail": true, "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-alert-trail", "IsOrganizationTrail": false } anihpj trail with SNS alerts created Notifications will be sent for each log file delivery
# Django Models Integration - Trail with SNS Notifications from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create trail with SNS alerts alert_trail = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-alert-trail', author=Author.objects.first(), location=Location.objects.first(), status='active' ) # Create SNS skill for notifications sns_skill = Skills.objects.create( name='SNS-Notifications', level='anihpj-cloudtrail-alerts' ) result = { 'Name': alert_trail.title, 'SnsTopicName': sns_skill.level, 'SnsTopicARN': f'arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:{sns_skill.level}', 'IsMultiRegionTrail': True } print(result) # Output: {'Name': 'anihpj-alert-trail', 'SnsTopicName': 'anihpj-cloudtrail-alerts', ...} print(f"<JobPost {alert_trail.title} with SNS alerts>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-alert-trail with SNS alerts>
Example 5: Create Organization Trail for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Create organization-wide trail (requires organization management account)
aws cloudtrail create-trail \
  --name anihpj-organization-trail \
  --s3-bucket-name anihpj-org-cloudtrail-logs \
  --is-organization-trail \
  --is-multi-region-trail \
  --include-global-service-events \
  --enable-log-file-validation \
  --kms-key-id "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/org-key-123"

echo "anihpj organization-wide trail created"
echo "All member accounts will be audited"
{ "Name": "anihpj-organization-trail", "S3BucketName": "anihpj-org-cloudtrail-logs", "IncludeGlobalServiceEvents": true, "IsMultiRegionTrail": true, "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-organization-trail", "LogFileValidationEnabled": true, "KMSKeyId": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/org-key-123", "IsOrganizationTrail": true } anihpj organization-wide trail created All member accounts will be audited
# Django Models Integration - Organization Trail from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create organization-wide trail org_trail = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-organization-trail', author=Author.objects.first(), location=Location.objects.create( city='anihpj-org-cloudtrail-logs', country='organization' ), status='active' ) result = { 'Name': org_trail.title, 'S3BucketName': org_trail.location.city, 'IsOrganizationTrail': True, 'IsMultiRegionTrail': True, 'LogFileValidationEnabled': True } print(result) # Output: {'Name': 'anihpj-organization-trail', 'S3BucketName': 'anihpj-org-cloudtrail-logs', ...} print(f"<JobPost {org_trail.title} organization_wide=True>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-organization-trail organization_wide=True>

delete-trail

Deletes a trail. This operation does not delete the Amazon S3 bucket or the log files in the bucket.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail delete-trail \
  --name {trail-name}
Parameters Description
  • --name - Name or ARN of the trail to delete
Return Value

Returns no content on success. S3 logs are retained.

5 Detailed CloudTrail delete-trail Examples
Example 1: Delete anihpj Trail
#!/bin/bash
# Delete an existing anihpj trail
aws cloudtrail delete-trail \
  --name anihpj-old-audit-trail

echo "anihpj trail deleted"
echo "Note: S3 logs are retained"
anihpj trail deleted Note: S3 logs are retained
# Django Models Integration - Delete anihpj Trail from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete trail record try: trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-old-audit-trail') trail_name = trail.title trail.delete() print(f"anihpj trail deleted") print("Note: S3 logs are retained") except JobPost.DoesNotExist: print("Trail not found") # Output: anihpj trail deleted # Output: Note: S3 logs are retained print(f"<JobPost {trail_name} deleted, logs retained>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-old-audit-trail deleted, logs retained>
Example 2: Delete Trail with Confirmation
#!/bin/bash
# Delete trail with confirmation check
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-deprecated-trail"

# Check if trail exists
EXISTS=$(aws cloudtrail describe-trails \
  --trail-name-list $TRAIL_NAME \
  --query 'trailList[0].Name' \
  --output text 2>/dev/null)

if [ "$EXISTS" != "None" ] && [ -n "$EXISTS" ]; then
  echo "Found trail: $TRAIL_NAME"
  read -p "Are you sure you want to delete? (y/n): " CONFIRM
  
  if [ "$CONFIRM" == "y" ]; then
    aws cloudtrail delete-trail --name $TRAIL_NAME
    echo "anihpj trail deleted successfully"
  else
    echo "Deletion cancelled"
  fi
else
  echo "Trail not found: $TRAIL_NAME"
fi
Found trail: anihpj-deprecated-trail Are you sure you want to delete? (y/n): y anihpj trail deleted successfully
# Django Models Integration - Delete with Confirmation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete trail with confirmation check trail_name = 'anihpj-deprecated-trail' try: trail = JobPost.objects.get(title=trail_name) print(f"Found trail: {trail_name}") confirm = 'y' # Simulated user input if confirm == 'y': trail.delete() print("anihpj trail deleted successfully") else: print("Deletion cancelled") except JobPost.DoesNotExist: print(f"Trail not found: {trail_name}") # Output: Found trail: anihpj-deprecated-trail # Output: anihpj trail deleted successfully print(f"<JobPost {trail_name} deleted after confirmation>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-deprecated-trail deleted after confirmation>
Example 3: Delete Trail and Archive Logs
#!/bin/bash
# Delete trail but first archive logs to Glacier
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-old-production-trail"

# Get S3 bucket info
BUCKET=$(aws cloudtrail describe-trails \
  --trail-name-list $TRAIL_NAME \
  --query 'trailList[0].S3BucketName' \
  --output text)

PREFIX=$(aws cloudtrail describe-trails \
  --trail-name-list $TRAIL_NAME \
  --query 'trailList[0].S3KeyPrefix' \
  --output text)

echo "Archiving anihpj CloudTrail logs to Glacier..."
echo "Bucket: $BUCKET"
echo "Prefix: $PREFIX"

# Set lifecycle rule to transition to Glacier
aws s3api put-bucket-lifecycle-configuration \
  --bucket $BUCKET \
  --lifecycle-configuration '{
    "Rules": [{
      "ID": "archive-cloudtrail-logs",
      "Filter": {"Prefix": "'$PREFIX'"},
      "Status": "Enabled",
      "Transitions": [{
        "Days": 0,
        "StorageClass": "GLACIER"
      }]
    }]
  }'

echo "Logs scheduled for Glacier archival"
echo ""
echo "Deleting trail..."
aws cloudtrail delete-trail --name $TRAIL_NAME
echo "anihpj trail deleted"
Archiving anihpj CloudTrail logs to Glacier... Bucket: anihpj-cloudtrail-logs Prefix: production/audit Logs scheduled for Glacier archival Deleting trail... anihpj trail deleted
# Django Models Integration - Delete Trail and Archive Logs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete trail but first archive logs trail_name = 'anihpj-old-production-trail' try: trail = JobPost.objects.get(title=trail_name) # Get bucket info from location bucket = trail.location.city if trail.location else 'unknown' prefix = trail.location.country if trail.location else '' print("Archiving anihpj CloudTrail logs to Glacier...") print(f"Bucket: {bucket}") print(f"Prefix: {prefix}") print("Logs scheduled for Glacier archival") print("") print("Deleting trail...") trail.delete() print("anihpj trail deleted") except JobPost.DoesNotExist: print("Trail not found") # Output: Archiving anihpj CloudTrail logs to Glacier... print(f"<JobPost {trail_name} archived and deleted>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-old-production-trail archived and deleted>
Example 4: Delete Multiple anihpj Trails
#!/bin/bash
# Delete multiple old trails for cleanup
TRAILS=("anihpj-test-trail" "anihpj-dev-trail" "anihpj-legacy-trail")

echo "Deleting anihpj deprecated trails..."
echo "===================================="

for TRAIL in "${TRAILS[@]}"; do
  echo "Deleting: $TRAIL"
  
  # Stop logging first
  aws cloudtrail stop-logging --name $TRAIL 2>/dev/null
  
  # Delete trail
  if aws cloudtrail delete-trail --name $TRAIL 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "  ✓ Deleted"
  else
    echo "  ✗ Not found or error"
  fi
done

echo ""
echo "Cleanup complete"
Deleting anihpj deprecated trails... ==================================== Deleting: anihpj-test-trail ✓ Deleted Deleting: anihpj-dev-trail ✓ Deleted Deleting: anihpj-legacy-trail ✗ Not found or error Cleanup complete
# Django Models Integration - Delete Multiple Trails from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete multiple old trails for cleanup trails = ['anihpj-test-trail', 'anihpj-dev-trail', 'anihpj-legacy-trail'] print("Deleting anihpj deprecated trails...") print("====================================") for trail_name in trails: print(f"Deleting: {trail_name}") try: trail = JobPost.objects.get(title=trail_name) trail.delete() print(" ✓ Deleted") except JobPost.DoesNotExist: print(" ✗ Not found or error") print("") print("Cleanup complete") # Output: Deleting anihpj deprecated trails... # Output: ✓ Deleted / ✗ Not found or error print(f"<JobPost bulk_delete trails={len(trails)}>") # Output: <JobPost bulk_delete trails=3>
Example 5: Safe Trail Deletion Script
#!/bin/bash
# Safe deletion with backup verification
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-audit-trail"
BACKUP_BUCKET="anihpj-cloudtrail-backup"

echo "anihpj Safe Trail Deletion"
echo "==========================="

# Get trail details
TRAIL_INFO=$(aws cloudtrail describe-trails \
  --trail-name-list $TRAIL_NAME \
  --query 'trailList[0]' \
  --output json)

if [ -z "$TRAIL_INFO" ]; then
  echo "Trail not found"
  exit 1
fi

SOURCE_BUCKET=$(echo "$TRAIL_INFO" | jq -r '.S3BucketName')
echo "Source bucket: $SOURCE_BUCKET"

# Verify backup exists
echo "Verifying backup in $BACKUP_BUCKET..."
BACKUP_COUNT=$(aws s3 ls s3://$BACKUP_BUCKET/cloudtrail-backup/ --recursive | wc -l)

if [ "$BACKUP_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "✓ Backup verified ($BACKUP_COUNT files)"
  
  # Stop logging
  echo "Stopping logging..."
  aws cloudtrail stop-logging --name $TRAIL_NAME
  
  # Delete trail
  echo "Deleting trail..."
  aws cloudtrail delete-trail --name $TRAIL_NAME
  
  echo ""
  echo "anihpj trail safely deleted"
  echo "Backup available at: s3://$BACKUP_BUCKET/cloudtrail-backup/"
else
  echo "✗ No backup found!"
  echo "Run backup before deletion"
  exit 1
fi
anihpj Safe Trail Deletion =========================== Source bucket: anihpj-cloudtrail-logs Verifying backup in anihpj-cloudtrail-backup... ✓ Backup verified (1523 files) Stopping logging... Deleting trail... anihpj trail safely deleted Backup available at: s3://anihpj-cloudtrail-backup/cloudtrail-backup/
# Django Models Integration - Safe Trail Deletion from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Safe deletion with backup verification trail_name = 'anihpj-audit-trail' backup_bucket = 'anihpj-cloudtrail-backup' print("anihpj Safe Trail Deletion") print("===========================") try: trail = JobPost.objects.get(title=trail_name) source_bucket = trail.location.city if trail.location else 'unknown' print(f"Source bucket: {source_bucket}") # Verify backup exists (simulated) print(f"Verifying backup in {backup_bucket}...") backup_count = 1523 # Simulated backup count if backup_count > 0: print(f"✓ Backup verified ({backup_count} files)") print("Stopping logging...") print("Deleting trail...") trail.delete() print("") print("anihpj trail safely deleted") print(f"Backup available at: s3://{backup_bucket}/cloudtrail-backup/") else: print("✗ No backup found!") except JobPost.DoesNotExist: print("Trail not found") print(f"<JobPost {trail_name} safely deleted with backup>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail safely deleted with backup>

describe-trails

Retrieves settings for one or more trails associated with the current region.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail describe-trails \
  --trail-name-list {trail-names}
Parameters Description
  • --trail-name-list - (Optional) Trail names or ARNs to describe
  • --include-shadow-trails - (Optional) Include replicated trails
Return Value

Returns trailList array with Name, S3BucketName, IsMultiRegionTrail, and trail configuration.

5 Detailed CloudTrail describe-trails Examples
Example 1: Describe All anihpj Trails
#!/bin/bash
# List all trails in the account
aws cloudtrail describe-trails \
  --query 'trailList[*].[Name,S3BucketName,IsMultiRegionTrail]' \
  --output table
------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeTrails | +------------------------+----------------------+-----------+ | anihpj-audit-trail | anihpj-cloudtrail | False | | anihpj-global-trail | anihpj-global-logs | True | | anihpj-security-trail | anihpj-security-logs | True | +------------------------+----------------------+-----------+
# Django Models Integration - Describe All Trails from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all trails in the account trails = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='trail') print("--- DescribeTrails ---") for trail in trails: bucket = trail.location.city if trail.location else 'N/A' multi_region = trail.status == 'global' print(f"{trail.title} | {bucket} | {multi_region}") # Output: anihpj-audit-trail | anihpj-cloudtrail | False # Output: anihpj-global-trail | anihpj-global-logs | True print(f"<QuerySet [{trails.count()} trails]>") # Output: <QuerySet [3 trails]>
Example 2: Describe Specific anihpj Trail
#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed info for specific trail
aws cloudtrail describe-trails \
  --trail-name-list anihpj-audit-trail \
  --output json | jq '.'
{ "trailList": [ { "Name": "anihpj-audit-trail", "S3BucketName": "anihpj-cloudtrail-logs", "S3KeyPrefix": "audit", "IncludeGlobalServiceEvents": true, "IsMultiRegionTrail": true, "HomeRegion": "us-east-1", "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail", "LogFileValidationEnabled": true, "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:anihpj-cloudtrail", "CloudWatchLogsRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-cloudtrail-role", "KMSKeyId": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/abc123", "HasCustomEventSelectors": true, "HasInsightSelectors": true, "IsOrganizationTrail": false } ] }
# Django Models Integration - Describe Specific Trail from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get detailed info for specific trail trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') trail_info = { 'trailList': [{ 'Name': trail.title, 'S3BucketName': trail.location.city if trail.location else 'N/A', 'S3KeyPrefix': 'audit', 'IncludeGlobalServiceEvents': True, 'IsMultiRegionTrail': True, 'HomeRegion': 'us-east-1', 'LogFileValidationEnabled': True, 'HasCustomEventSelectors': True }] } print(trail_info) # Output: {'trailList': [{'Name': 'anihpj-audit-trail', ...}]} print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} details retrieved>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail details retrieved>
Example 3: Check anihpj Trail Security Settings
#!/bin/bash
# Audit security settings for all anihpj trails
echo "anihpj CloudTrail Security Audit"
echo "=================================="

aws cloudtrail describe-trails \
  --include-shadow-trails \
  --output json | jq -r '.trailList[] | select(.Name | startswith("anihpj")) | "
Trail: \(.Name)
  Encryption: \(if .KMSKeyId then "✓ KMS Enabled" else "✗ Not Encrypted" end)
  Log Validation: \(if .LogFileValidationEnabled then "✓ Enabled" else "✗ Disabled" end)
  Multi-Region: \(if .IsMultiRegionTrail then "✓ Yes" else "✗ No" end)
  CloudWatch: \(if .CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn then "✓ Integrated" else "✗ Not Configured" end)
"'
anihpj CloudTrail Security Audit ================================== Trail: anihpj-audit-trail Encryption: ✓ KMS Enabled Log Validation: ✓ Enabled Multi-Region: ✓ Yes CloudWatch: ✓ Integrated Trail: anihpj-dev-trail Encryption: ✗ Not Encrypted Log Validation: ✗ Disabled Multi-Region: ✗ No CloudWatch: ✗ Not Configured
# Django Models Integration - Check Trail Security Settings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Audit security settings for all anihpj trails print("anihpj CloudTrail Security Audit") print("==================================") trails = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='anihpj') for trail in trails: skills = Skills.objects.filter(name=trail.title) has_encryption = skills.filter(level='KMS').exists() has_validation = trail.status == 'validated' is_multi_region = trail.status == 'global' has_cloudwatch = skills.filter(level='CloudWatch').exists() print(f"\nTrail: {trail.title}") print(f" Encryption: {'✓ KMS Enabled' if has_encryption else '✗ Not Encrypted'}") print(f" Log Validation: {'✓ Enabled' if has_validation else '✗ Disabled'}") print(f" Multi-Region: {'✓ Yes' if is_multi_region else '✗ No'}") print(f" CloudWatch: {'✓ Integrated' if has_cloudwatch else '✗ Not Configured'}") print(f"<QuerySet security audit for {trails.count()} trails>") # Output: <QuerySet security audit for 2 trails>
Example 4: Export anihpj Trail Configuration
#!/bin/bash
# Export trail configuration for documentation
OUTPUT_FILE="anihpj-trails-config.json"

echo "Exporting anihpj CloudTrail configuration..."

aws cloudtrail describe-trails \
  --include-shadow-trails \
  --output json | jq '{
    ExportDate: now | strftime("%Y-%m-%d"),
    Trails: [.trailList[] | select(.Name | startswith("anihpj"))]
  }' > $OUTPUT_FILE

echo "Configuration exported to $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo ""
echo "Summary:"
jq -r '.Trails[] | "- \(.Name) (\(.HomeRegion))"' $OUTPUT_FILE
Exporting anihpj CloudTrail configuration... Configuration exported to anihpj-trails-config.json Summary: - anihpj-audit-trail (us-east-1) - anihpj-global-trail (us-east-1) - anihpj-security-trail (us-east-1)
# Django Models Integration - Export Trail Configuration from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json from datetime import datetime # Export trail configuration print("Exporting anihpj CloudTrail configuration...") trails = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='anihpj') export_data = { 'ExportDate': datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d'), 'Trails': [ {'Name': t.title, 'HomeRegion': 'us-east-1'} for t in trails ] } print("Configuration exported to anihpj-trails-config.json") print("") print("Summary:") for trail in export_data['Trails']: print(f"- {trail['Name']} ({trail['HomeRegion']})") # Output: - anihpj-audit-trail (us-east-1) print(f"<QuerySet exported {trails.count()} trail configs>") # Output: <QuerySet exported 3 trail configs>
Example 5: Compare anihpj Trail Settings
#!/bin/bash
# Compare settings between trails
echo "anihpj Trail Comparison"
echo "========================"

TRAILS=$(aws cloudtrail describe-trails \
  --output json | jq -r '.trailList[] | select(.Name | startswith("anihpj")) | .Name')

printf "%-25s %-12s %-12s %-10s %-10s\n" "Trail" "MultiRegion" "Encryption" "Validation" "CloudWatch"
printf "%-25s %-12s %-12s %-10s %-10s\n" "-------------------------" "------------" "------------" "----------" "----------"

aws cloudtrail describe-trails \
  --output json | jq -r '.trailList[] | select(.Name | startswith("anihpj")) | [
    .Name,
    (if .IsMultiRegionTrail then "Yes" else "No" end),
    (if .KMSKeyId then "Yes" else "No" end),
    (if .LogFileValidationEnabled then "Yes" else "No" end),
    (if .CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn then "Yes" else "No" end)
  ] | @tsv' | while IFS=$'\t' read -r name mr enc val cw; do
  printf "%-25s %-12s %-12s %-10s %-10s\n" "$name" "$mr" "$enc" "$val" "$cw"
done
anihpj Trail Comparison ======================== Trail MultiRegion Encryption Validation CloudWatch ------------------------- ------------ ------------ ---------- ---------- anihpj-audit-trail Yes Yes Yes Yes anihpj-global-trail Yes Yes Yes Yes anihpj-dev-trail No No No No
# Django Models Integration - Compare Trail Settings from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Compare settings between trails print("anihpj Trail Comparison") print("========================") print(f"{'Trail':<25} {'MultiRegion':<12} {'Encryption':<12} {'Validation':<10} {'CloudWatch':<10}") print("-" * 70) trails = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='anihpj') for trail in trails: skills = Skills.objects.filter(name=trail.title) mr = 'Yes' if trail.status == 'global' else 'No' enc = 'Yes' if skills.filter(level='KMS').exists() else 'No' val = 'Yes' if trail.status == 'validated' else 'No' cw = 'Yes' if skills.filter(level='CloudWatch').exists() else 'No' print(f"{trail.title:<25} {mr:<12} {enc:<12} {val:<10} {cw:<10}") # Output: anihpj-audit-trail Yes Yes Yes Yes print(f"<QuerySet compared {trails.count()} trails>") # Output: <QuerySet compared 3 trails>

get-trail

Returns settings information for a specified trail.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail get-trail \
  --name {trail-name}
Parameters Description
  • --name - Name or ARN of the trail
Return Value

Returns Trail object with full configuration including S3BucketName, KMSKeyId, and logging settings.

5 Detailed CloudTrail get-trail Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Trail Details
#!/bin/bash
# Get detailed information about anihpj trail
aws cloudtrail get-trail \
  --name anihpj-audit-trail
{ "Trail": { "Name": "anihpj-audit-trail", "S3BucketName": "anihpj-cloudtrail-logs", "S3KeyPrefix": "audit", "IncludeGlobalServiceEvents": true, "IsMultiRegionTrail": true, "HomeRegion": "us-east-1", "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail", "LogFileValidationEnabled": true, "KMSKeyId": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/abc123-def456", "HasCustomEventSelectors": true, "HasInsightSelectors": true, "IsOrganizationTrail": false } }
# Django Models Integration - Get Trail Details from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get detailed information about anihpj trail trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') trail_info = { 'Trail': { 'Name': trail.title, 'S3BucketName': trail.location.city if trail.location else 'N/A', 'S3KeyPrefix': 'audit', 'IncludeGlobalServiceEvents': True, 'IsMultiRegionTrail': True, 'HomeRegion': 'us-east-1', 'TrailARN': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/{trail.title}', 'LogFileValidationEnabled': True, 'HasCustomEventSelectors': True } } print(trail_info) # Output: {'Trail': {'Name': 'anihpj-audit-trail', ...}} print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} retrieved>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail retrieved>
Example 2: Get anihpj Trail S3 Configuration
#!/bin/bash
# Get S3 storage details for anihpj trail
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-audit-trail"

echo "anihpj Trail Storage Configuration"
echo "===================================="

aws cloudtrail get-trail \
  --name $TRAIL_NAME \
  --query 'Trail.{Bucket:S3BucketName,Prefix:S3KeyPrefix,KMSKey:KMSKeyId}' \
  --output table
anihpj Trail Storage Configuration ==================================== ------------------------------------------------------------ | GetTrail | +------------+---------------------------------------------+ | Bucket | anihpj-cloudtrail-logs | | KMSKey | arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/abc | | Prefix | audit | +------------+---------------------------------------------+
# Django Models Integration - Get Trail S3 Configuration from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get S3 storage details for anihpj trail trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') print("anihpj Trail Storage Configuration") print("====================================") config = { 'Bucket': trail.location.city if trail.location else 'N/A', 'Prefix': trail.location.country if trail.location else 'N/A', 'KMSKey': 'arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/abc' } print(f"Bucket: {config['Bucket']}") print(f"Prefix: {config['Prefix']}") print(f"KMSKey: {config['KMSKey']}") # Output: Bucket: anihpj-cloudtrail-logs print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} S3 config retrieved>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail S3 config retrieved>
Example 3: Verify anihpj Trail Compliance
#!/bin/bash
# Check if trail meets anihpj compliance requirements
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-audit-trail"

echo "anihpj Trail Compliance Check"
echo "==============================="

TRAIL=$(aws cloudtrail get-trail --name $TRAIL_NAME --output json)

# Check requirements
MULTI_REGION=$(echo "$TRAIL" | jq -r '.Trail.IsMultiRegionTrail')
ENCRYPTION=$(echo "$TRAIL" | jq -r '.Trail.KMSKeyId // empty')
VALIDATION=$(echo "$TRAIL" | jq -r '.Trail.LogFileValidationEnabled')
GLOBAL_EVENTS=$(echo "$TRAIL" | jq -r '.Trail.IncludeGlobalServiceEvents')

echo "Trail: $TRAIL_NAME"
echo ""
echo "Compliance Requirements:"
[ "$MULTI_REGION" == "true" ] && echo "✓ Multi-region trail enabled" || echo "✗ Multi-region trail NOT enabled"
[ -n "$ENCRYPTION" ] && echo "✓ KMS encryption enabled" || echo "✗ KMS encryption NOT enabled"
[ "$VALIDATION" == "true" ] && echo "✓ Log file validation enabled" || echo "✗ Log file validation NOT enabled"
[ "$GLOBAL_EVENTS" == "true" ] && echo "✓ Global service events included" || echo "✗ Global service events NOT included"

# Overall compliance
if [ "$MULTI_REGION" == "true" ] && [ -n "$ENCRYPTION" ] && [ "$VALIDATION" == "true" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "Overall: ✓ COMPLIANT"
else
  echo ""
  echo "Overall: ✗ NOT COMPLIANT"
fi
anihpj Trail Compliance Check =============================== Trail: anihpj-audit-trail Compliance Requirements: ✓ Multi-region trail enabled ✓ KMS encryption enabled ✓ Log file validation enabled ✓ Global service events included Overall: ✓ COMPLIANT
# Django Models Integration - Verify Trail Compliance from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Check if trail meets compliance requirements trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') skills = Skills.objects.filter(name=trail.title) print("anihpj Trail Compliance Check") print("===============================") print(f"Trail: {trail.title}") print("") print("Compliance Requirements:") multi_region = trail.status == 'global' has_encryption = skills.filter(level='KMS').exists() has_validation = trail.status == 'validated' global_events = True # Always true for anihpj print(f"{'✓' if multi_region else '✗'} Multi-region trail enabled") print(f"{'✓' if has_encryption else '✗'} KMS encryption enabled") print(f"{'✓' if has_validation else '✗'} Log file validation enabled") print(f"{'✓' if global_events else '✗'} Global service events included") is_compliant = multi_region and has_encryption and has_validation print(f"\nOverall: {'✓ COMPLIANT' if is_compliant else '✗ NOT COMPLIANT'}") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} compliance_check complete>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail compliance_check complete>
Example 4: Get Trail ARN for anihpj Policies
#!/bin/bash
# Get trail ARN for IAM policy creation
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-audit-trail"

TRAIL_ARN=$(aws cloudtrail get-trail \
  --name $TRAIL_NAME \
  --query 'Trail.TrailARN' \
  --output text)

echo "anihpj Trail ARN: $TRAIL_ARN"
echo ""
echo "Use this ARN in IAM policies:"
cat << EOF
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [{
    "Effect": "Allow",
    "Action": [
      "cloudtrail:GetTrailStatus",
      "cloudtrail:LookupEvents"
    ],
    "Resource": "$TRAIL_ARN"
  }]
}
EOF
anihpj Trail ARN: arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail Use this ARN in IAM policies: { "Version": "2012-10-17", "Statement": [{ "Effect": "Allow", "Action": [ "cloudtrail:GetTrailStatus", "cloudtrail:LookupEvents" ], "Resource": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail" }] }
# Django Models Integration - Get Trail ARN for Policies from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get trail ARN for IAM policy creation trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') trail_arn = f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/{trail.title}' print(f"anihpj Trail ARN: {trail_arn}") print("") print("Use this ARN in IAM policies:") policy = { 'Version': '2012-10-17', 'Statement': [{ 'Effect': 'Allow', 'Action': ['cloudtrail:GetTrailStatus', 'cloudtrail:LookupEvents'], 'Resource': trail_arn }] } print(policy) # Output: {'Version': '2012-10-17', 'Statement': [...]} print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} ARN for IAM policy>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail ARN for IAM policy>
Example 5: Get Trail with CloudWatch Details
#!/bin/bash
# Get trail with CloudWatch integration details
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-audit-trail"

echo "anihpj Trail CloudWatch Configuration"
echo "========================================"

TRAIL=$(aws cloudtrail get-trail --name $TRAIL_NAME --output json)

LOG_GROUP=$(echo "$TRAIL" | jq -r '.Trail.CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn // "Not configured"')
LOG_ROLE=$(echo "$TRAIL" | jq -r '.Trail.CloudWatchLogsRoleArn // "Not configured"')

echo "Trail: $TRAIL_NAME"
echo ""
echo "CloudWatch Logs:"
echo "  Log Group: $LOG_GROUP"
echo "  IAM Role: $LOG_ROLE"

if [ "$LOG_GROUP" != "Not configured" ]; then
  # Get log group info
  LOG_GROUP_NAME=$(echo "$LOG_GROUP" | grep -oP 'log-group:\K[^:]+')
  echo ""
  echo "Recent log streams:"
  aws logs describe-log-streams \
    --log-group-name "$LOG_GROUP_NAME" \
    --order-by LastEventTime \
    --descending \
    --limit 3 \
    --query 'logStreams[*].logStreamName' \
    --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sed 's/^/  - /'
fi
anihpj Trail CloudWatch Configuration ======================================== Trail: anihpj-audit-trail CloudWatch Logs: Log Group: arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:anihpj-cloudtrail:* IAM Role: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-cloudtrail-role Recent log streams: - 123456789012_CloudTrail_us-east-1 - 123456789012_CloudTrail_eu-west-1 - 123456789012_CloudTrail_ap-southeast-1
# Django Models Integration - Get Trail with CloudWatch Details from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get trail with CloudWatch integration details trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') print("anihpj Trail CloudWatch Configuration") print("========================================") print(f"Trail: {trail.title}") print("") print("CloudWatch Logs:") log_group = 'arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:anihpj-cloudtrail:*' iam_role = 'arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-cloudtrail-role' print(f" Log Group: {log_group}") print(f" IAM Role: {iam_role}") print("") print("Recent log streams:") streams = ['us-east-1', 'eu-west-1', 'ap-southeast-1'] for stream in streams: print(f" - 123456789012_CloudTrail_{stream}") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} CloudWatch config>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail CloudWatch config>

get-trail-status

Returns a JSON-formatted list of information about the specified trail.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail get-trail-status \
  --name {trail-name}
Parameters Description
  • --name - Name or ARN of the trail
Return Value

Returns IsLogging status, LatestDeliveryTime, LatestNotificationTime, and error information.

5 Detailed CloudTrail get-trail-status Examples
Example 1: Check anihpj Trail Status
#!/bin/bash
# Get current status of anihpj trail
aws cloudtrail get-trail-status \
  --name anihpj-audit-trail
{ "IsLogging": true, "LatestDeliveryTime": "2026-01-17T14:30:00.000Z", "LatestDeliveryAttemptTime": "2026-01-17 14:30:00", "LatestNotificationTime": "2026-01-17T14:30:00.000Z", "LatestDeliveryAttemptSucceeded": "2026-01-17 14:30:00", "LatestNotificationAttemptTime": "2026-01-17 14:30:00", "LatestNotificationAttemptSucceeded": "2026-01-17 14:30:00", "LatestDigestDeliveryTime": "2026-01-17T14:15:00.000Z", "StartLoggingTime": "2026-01-01T00:00:00.000Z", "TimeLoggingStarted": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
# Django Models Integration - Check Trail Status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime # Get current status of anihpj trail trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') status = { 'IsLogging': trail.status == 'active', 'LatestDeliveryTime': datetime.now().isoformat(), 'LatestNotificationTime': datetime.now().isoformat(), 'LatestDigestDeliveryTime': datetime.now().isoformat(), 'StartLoggingTime': trail.created_at.isoformat() if hasattr(trail, 'created_at') else 'N/A' } print(status) # Output: {'IsLogging': True, 'LatestDeliveryTime': '2026-01-17T14:30:00...', ...} print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} status: {'logging' if status['IsLogging'] else 'stopped'}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail status: logging>
Example 2: Check if anihpj Trail is Logging
#!/bin/bash
# Quick check if trail is actively logging
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-audit-trail"

IS_LOGGING=$(aws cloudtrail get-trail-status \
  --name $TRAIL_NAME \
  --query 'IsLogging' \
  --output text)

if [ "$IS_LOGGING" == "True" ]; then
  echo "✓ anihpj trail is actively logging"
  
  LAST_DELIVERY=$(aws cloudtrail get-trail-status \
    --name $TRAIL_NAME \
    --query 'LatestDeliveryTime' \
    --output text)
  
  echo "Last log delivery: $LAST_DELIVERY"
else
  echo "✗ anihpj trail is NOT logging!"
  echo "Run: aws cloudtrail start-logging --name $TRAIL_NAME"
fi
✓ anihpj trail is actively logging Last log delivery: 2026-01-17T14:30:00.000Z
# Django Models Integration - Check if Trail is Logging from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime # Quick check if trail is actively logging trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') is_logging = trail.status == 'active' if is_logging: print("✓ anihpj trail is actively logging") last_delivery = datetime.now().isoformat() print(f"Last log delivery: {last_delivery}") else: print("✗ anihpj trail is NOT logging!") print(f"Activate trail: {trail.title}") # Output: ✓ anihpj trail is actively logging print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} is_logging={is_logging}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail is_logging=True>
Example 3: anihpj Trail Health Check
#!/bin/bash
# Comprehensive trail health check
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-audit-trail"

echo "anihpj Trail Health Check"
echo "=========================="

STATUS=$(aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name $TRAIL_NAME --output json)

IS_LOGGING=$(echo "$STATUS" | jq -r '.IsLogging')
LAST_DELIVERY=$(echo "$STATUS" | jq -r '.LatestDeliveryTime // "Never"')
LAST_ERROR=$(echo "$STATUS" | jq -r '.LatestDeliveryError // "None"')
LAST_NOTIFICATION=$(echo "$STATUS" | jq -r '.LatestNotificationTime // "Never"')
NOTIFICATION_ERROR=$(echo "$STATUS" | jq -r '.LatestNotificationError // "None"')

echo "Trail: $TRAIL_NAME"
echo ""
echo "Logging Status:"
[ "$IS_LOGGING" == "true" ] && echo "  ✓ Logging active" || echo "  ✗ Logging stopped"
echo "  Last delivery: $LAST_DELIVERY"
echo "  Delivery error: $LAST_ERROR"
echo ""
echo "Notification Status:"
echo "  Last notification: $LAST_NOTIFICATION"
echo "  Notification error: $NOTIFICATION_ERROR"

# Check for issues
if [ "$LAST_ERROR" != "None" ] || [ "$NOTIFICATION_ERROR" != "None" ]; then
  echo ""
  echo "⚠️  Issues detected - review trail configuration"
else
  echo ""
  echo "✓ Trail is healthy"
fi
anihpj Trail Health Check ========================== Trail: anihpj-audit-trail Logging Status: ✓ Logging active Last delivery: 2026-01-17T14:30:00.000Z Delivery error: None Notification Status: Last notification: 2026-01-17T14:30:00.000Z Notification error: None ✓ Trail is healthy
# Django Models Integration - Trail Health Check from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime # Comprehensive trail health check trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') print("anihpj Trail Health Check") print("==========================") print(f"Trail: {trail.title}") print("") is_logging = trail.status == 'active' last_delivery = datetime.now().isoformat() has_error = False # Check for errors print("Logging Status:") print(f" {'✓' if is_logging else '✗'} Logging {'active' if is_logging else 'stopped'}") print(f" Last delivery: {last_delivery}") print(f" Delivery error: {'None' if not has_error else 'Error detected'}") print("") print("Notification Status:") print(f" Last notification: {last_delivery}") print(f" Notification error: None") print("") print("✓ Trail is healthy" if not has_error else "✗ Issues detected") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} health_status=healthy>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail health_status=healthy>
Example 4: Monitor All anihpj Trails
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor status of all anihpj trails
echo "anihpj Trail Status Dashboard"
echo "==============================="
echo ""

TRAILS=$(aws cloudtrail describe-trails \
  --query 'trailList[*].Name' \
  --output text)

printf "%-25s %-10s %-20s %-15s\n" "Trail" "Logging" "Last Delivery" "Errors"
printf "%-25s %-10s %-20s %-15s\n" "-------------------------" "----------" "--------------------" "---------------"

for TRAIL in $TRAILS; do
  if [[ "$TRAIL" == anihpj* ]]; then
    STATUS=$(aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name $TRAIL --output json 2>/dev/null)
    
    IS_LOGGING=$(echo "$STATUS" | jq -r '.IsLogging')
    LAST=$(echo "$STATUS" | jq -r '.LatestDeliveryTime // "Never"' | cut -c1-19)
    ERRORS=$(echo "$STATUS" | jq -r 'if .LatestDeliveryError then "Yes" else "No" end')
    
    LOGGING_ICON=$([ "$IS_LOGGING" == "true" ] && echo "✓" || echo "✗")
    
    printf "%-25s %-10s %-20s %-15s\n" "$TRAIL" "$LOGGING_ICON" "$LAST" "$ERRORS"
  fi
done
anihpj Trail Status Dashboard =============================== Trail Logging Last Delivery Errors ------------------------- ---------- -------------------- --------------- anihpj-audit-trail ✓ 2026-01-17T14:30:00 No anihpj-global-trail ✓ 2026-01-17T14:28:00 No anihpj-dev-trail ✗ 2026-01-15T09:00:00 Yes
# Django Models Integration - Monitor All Trails from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime # Get all audit trails for dashboard view trails = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='trail') print("anihpj Trail Status Dashboard") print("===============================") print("") print(f"{'Trail':<25} {'Logging':<10} {'Last Delivery':<20} {'Errors'}") print(f"{'-'*25} {'-'*10} {'-'*20} {'-'*15}") healthy_count = 0 for trail in trails: is_logging = trail.status == 'active' last_delivery = datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S') has_errors = trail.status == 'error' status_icon = '✓' if is_logging else '✗' error_text = 'Yes' if has_errors else 'No' print(f"{trail.title:<25} {status_icon:<10} {last_delivery:<20} {error_text}") if is_logging: healthy_count += 1 print(f"<QuerySet count={trails.count()} healthy={healthy_count}>") # Output: <QuerySet count=3 healthy=2>
Example 5: anihpj Trail Latency Check
#!/bin/bash
# Check delivery latency for anihpj trail
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-audit-trail"

echo "anihpj Trail Delivery Latency Check"
echo "====================================="

STATUS=$(aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name $TRAIL_NAME --output json)

LAST_DELIVERY=$(echo "$STATUS" | jq -r '.LatestDeliveryTime')
LAST_DIGEST=$(echo "$STATUS" | jq -r '.LatestDigestDeliveryTime')
LAST_CW=$(echo "$STATUS" | jq -r '.LatestCloudWatchLogsDeliveryTime // "N/A"')

echo "Trail: $TRAIL_NAME"
echo ""
echo "Delivery Times:"
echo "  S3 Log Delivery: $LAST_DELIVERY"
echo "  Digest Delivery: $LAST_DIGEST"
echo "  CloudWatch Logs: $LAST_CW"

# Calculate time since last delivery
if [ "$LAST_DELIVERY" != "null" ]; then
  DELIVERY_EPOCH=$(date -d "$LAST_DELIVERY" +%s)
  NOW_EPOCH=$(date +%s)
  DIFF_MINUTES=$(( (NOW_EPOCH - DELIVERY_EPOCH) / 60 ))
  
  echo ""
  if [ $DIFF_MINUTES -lt 15 ]; then
    echo "✓ Last delivery: $DIFF_MINUTES minutes ago (healthy)"
  elif [ $DIFF_MINUTES -lt 60 ]; then
    echo "⚠️  Last delivery: $DIFF_MINUTES minutes ago (monitor)"
  else
    echo "✗ Last delivery: $DIFF_MINUTES minutes ago (check trail)"
  fi
fi
anihpj Trail Delivery Latency Check ===================================== Trail: anihpj-audit-trail Delivery Times: S3 Log Delivery: 2026-01-17T14:30:00.000Z Digest Delivery: 2026-01-17T14:15:00.000Z CloudWatch Logs: 2026-01-17T14:30:05.000Z ✓ Last delivery: 5 minutes ago (healthy)
# Django Models Integration - Trail Latency Check from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta # Analyze trail delivery latency trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') print("anihpj Trail Delivery Latency Check") print("=====================================") print(f"Trail: {trail.title}") print("") last_s3 = datetime.now() - timedelta(minutes=5) last_digest = datetime.now() - timedelta(minutes=20) last_cw = datetime.now() - timedelta(minutes=5) print("Delivery Times:") print(f" S3 Log Delivery: {last_s3.isoformat()}") print(f" Digest Delivery: {last_digest.isoformat()}") print(f" CloudWatch Logs: {last_cw.isoformat()}") print("") diff_minutes = 5 if diff_minutes < 15: print(f"✓ Last delivery: {diff_minutes} minutes ago (healthy)") elif diff_minutes < 60: print(f"⚠️ Last delivery: {diff_minutes} minutes ago (monitor)") else: print(f"✗ Last delivery: {diff_minutes} minutes ago (check trail)") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} latency_minutes={diff_minutes}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail latency_minutes=5>

list-trails

Lists trails that are in the current account.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail list-trails
Parameters Description
  • --next-token - (Optional) Token for pagination
Return Value

Returns Trails array with Name, TrailARN, and HomeRegion for each trail.

5 Detailed CloudTrail list-trails Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Trails
#!/bin/bash
# List all trails in the account
aws cloudtrail list-trails \
  --query 'Trails[*].[Name,HomeRegion]' \
  --output table
------------------------------------------ | ListTrails | +------------------------+---------------+ | anihpj-audit-trail | us-east-1 | | anihpj-global-trail | us-east-1 | | anihpj-security-trail | us-east-1 | | anihpj-dev-trail | us-west-2 | +------------------------+---------------+
# Django Models Integration - List All Trails from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all audit trails trails = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='trail') print("-" * 42) print("| ListTrails |") print("+" + "-"*24 + "+" + "-"*15 + "+") for trail in trails: region = Location.objects.first().city if Location.objects.exists() else 'us-east-1' print(f"| {trail.title:<22} | {region:<13} |") print("+" + "-"*24 + "+" + "-"*15 + "+") print(f"<QuerySet [{', '.join([t.title for t in trails])}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [anihpj-audit-trail, anihpj-global-trail, ...]>
Example 2: List anihpj Trail ARNs
#!/bin/bash
# Get ARNs for all anihpj trails
echo "anihpj CloudTrail ARNs"
echo "======================="

aws cloudtrail list-trails \
  --query 'Trails[*].TrailARN' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | grep anihpj
anihpj CloudTrail ARNs ======================= arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-global-trail arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-security-trail
# Django Models Integration - List Trail ARNs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get ARNs for all anihpj trails trails = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='trail') print("anihpj CloudTrail ARNs") print("=======================") account_id = '123456789012' for trail in trails: arn = f"arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:{account_id}:trail/{trail.title}" print(arn) # Using values_list for efficient query arns = [f"arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:{account_id}:trail/{t}" for t in trails.values_list('title', flat=True)] print(f"<QuerySet ARN count={len(arns)}>") # Output: <QuerySet ARN count=3>
Example 3: Export anihpj Trail List
#!/bin/bash
# Export trail list for documentation
OUTPUT_FILE="anihpj-trails-inventory.csv"

echo "Name,ARN,HomeRegion" > $OUTPUT_FILE

aws cloudtrail list-trails \
  --output json | jq -r '.Trails[] | select(.Name | startswith("anihpj")) | [.Name, .TrailARN, .HomeRegion] | @csv' >> $OUTPUT_FILE

echo "anihpj trail inventory exported to $OUTPUT_FILE"
cat $OUTPUT_FILE
anihpj trail inventory exported to anihpj-trails-inventory.csv Name,ARN,HomeRegion "anihpj-audit-trail","arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail","us-east-1" "anihpj-global-trail","arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-global-trail","us-east-1"
# Django Models Integration - Export Trail List from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import csv # Export trail list for documentation trails = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='trail') print("anihpj trail inventory exported to anihpj-trails-inventory.csv") print("") print("Name,ARN,HomeRegion") account_id = '123456789012' for trail in trails: arn = f"arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:{account_id}:trail/{trail.title}" region = 'us-east-1' print(f'"{trail.title}","{arn}","{region}"') # Export to CSV file using Django with open('anihpj-trails-inventory.csv', 'w', newline='') as f: writer = csv.writer(f) writer.writerow(['Name', 'ARN', 'HomeRegion']) for trail in trails: writer.writerow([trail.title, f"arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:{account_id}:trail/{trail.title}", 'us-east-1']) print(f"<QuerySet exported={trails.count()} trails>") # Output: <QuerySet exported=2 trails>

start-logging

Starts the recording of AWS API calls and log file delivery for a trail.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail start-logging \
  --name {trail-name}
Parameters Description
  • --name - Name or ARN of the trail
Return Value

Returns no content on success. Trail begins recording API calls.

5 Detailed CloudTrail start-logging Examples
Example 1: Start anihpj Trail Logging
#!/bin/bash
# Start logging for anihpj trail
aws cloudtrail start-logging \
  --name anihpj-audit-trail

echo "Logging started for anihpj-audit-trail"
Logging started for anihpj-audit-trail
# Django Models Integration - Start Trail Logging from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Start logging for anihpj trail trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') trail.status = 'active' trail.save() print(f"Logging started for {trail.title}") # Output: Logging started for anihpj-audit-trail print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} status={trail.status}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail status=active>
Example 2: Start Logging with Verification
#!/bin/bash
# Start logging and verify
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-audit-trail"

echo "Starting logging for $TRAIL_NAME..."
aws cloudtrail start-logging --name $TRAIL_NAME

# Verify
sleep 2
IS_LOGGING=$(aws cloudtrail get-trail-status \
  --name $TRAIL_NAME \
  --query 'IsLogging' \
  --output text)

if [ "$IS_LOGGING" == "True" ]; then
  echo "✓ anihpj trail is now logging"
else
  echo "✗ Failed to start logging"
fi
Starting logging for anihpj-audit-trail... ✓ anihpj trail is now logging
# Django Models Integration - Start Logging with Verification from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import time # Start logging and verify trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') print(f"Starting logging for {trail.title}...") trail.status = 'active' trail.save() # Refresh and verify time.sleep(2) trail.refresh_from_db() is_logging = trail.status == 'active' if is_logging: print("✓ anihpj trail is now logging") else: print("✗ Failed to start logging") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} is_logging={is_logging}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail is_logging=True>
Example 3: Start All Stopped anihpj Trails
#!/bin/bash
# Start all stopped trails
echo "Starting All Stopped anihpj Trails"
echo "===================================="

TRAILS=$(aws cloudtrail list-trails --query 'Trails[*].Name' --output text)

for TRAIL in $TRAILS; do
  if [[ "$TRAIL" == anihpj* ]]; then
    IS_LOGGING=$(aws cloudtrail get-trail-status \
      --name $TRAIL \
      --query 'IsLogging' \
      --output text 2>/dev/null)
    
    if [ "$IS_LOGGING" == "False" ]; then
      echo "Starting: $TRAIL"
      aws cloudtrail start-logging --name $TRAIL
    fi
  fi
done
Starting All Stopped anihpj Trails ==================================== Starting: anihpj-dev-trail Already running: anihpj-audit-trail
# Django Models Integration - Start All Stopped Trails from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Start all stopped trails print("Starting All Stopped anihpj Trails") print("====================================") trails = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='trail') for trail in trails: if trail.status != 'active': print(f"Starting: {trail.title}") trail.status = 'active' trail.save() else: print(f"Already running: {trail.title}") # Bulk update stopped trails stopped = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='trail').exclude(status='active') started_count = stopped.update(status='active') print(f"Started {started_count} trails") print(f"<QuerySet started={started_count}>") # Output: <QuerySet started=1>

stop-logging

Suspends the recording of AWS API calls and log file delivery for the specified trail.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail stop-logging \
  --name {trail-name}
Parameters Description
  • --name - Name or ARN of the trail
Return Value

Returns no content on success. Trail stops recording API calls.

5 Detailed CloudTrail stop-logging Examples
Example 1: Stop anihpj Trail Logging
#!/bin/bash
# Stop logging for dev trail
aws cloudtrail stop-logging \
  --name anihpj-dev-trail

echo "Logging stopped for anihpj-dev-trail"
Logging stopped for anihpj-dev-trail
# Django Models Integration - Stop Trail Logging from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Stop logging for dev trail trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-dev-trail') trail.status = 'stopped' trail.save() print(f"Logging stopped for {trail.title}") # Output: Logging stopped for anihpj-dev-trail print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} status={trail.status}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-dev-trail status=stopped>
Example 2: Stop with Safety Check
#!/bin/bash
# Stop logging with production safety check
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-dev-trail"

# Safety check - don't stop production trails
if [[ "$TRAIL_NAME" == *"production"* ]] || [[ "$TRAIL_NAME" == *"audit"* ]]; then
  echo "⚠️  WARNING: This is a production/audit trail!"
  echo "Cancelled - logging continues"
  exit 0
fi

aws cloudtrail stop-logging --name $TRAIL_NAME
echo "Logging stopped for $TRAIL_NAME"
Logging stopped for anihpj-dev-trail
# Django Models Integration - Stop with Safety Check from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Stop logging with production safety check trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-dev-trail') # Safety check - don't stop production trails if 'production' in trail.title or 'audit' in trail.title: print("⚠️ WARNING: This is a production/audit trail!") print("Cancelled - logging continues") else: trail.status = 'stopped' trail.save() print(f"Logging stopped for {trail.title}") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} status={trail.status}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-dev-trail status=stopped>
Example 3: Stop Non-Production Trails
#!/bin/bash
# Stop all dev/test trails for cost optimization
echo "Stopping Non-Production anihpj Trails"

TRAILS=$(aws cloudtrail list-trails --query 'Trails[*].Name' --output text)

for TRAIL in $TRAILS; do
  if [[ "$TRAIL" == anihpj* ]] && [[ "$TRAIL" == *"dev"* ]]; then
    echo "Stopping: $TRAIL"
    aws cloudtrail stop-logging --name $TRAIL
  fi
done
Stopping Non-Production anihpj Trails Stopping: anihpj-dev-trail Production trails remain active
# Django Models Integration - Stop Non-Production Trails from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Stop all dev/test trails for cost optimization print("Stopping Non-Production anihpj Trails") trails = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='trail') for trail in trails: if 'dev' in trail.title or 'test' in trail.title: print(f"Stopping: {trail.title}") trail.status = 'stopped' trail.save() print("Production trails remain active") # Bulk update dev trails dev_trails = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='trail', title__contains='dev') stopped_count = dev_trails.update(status='stopped') print(f"<QuerySet stopped={stopped_count}>") # Output: <QuerySet stopped=1>

create-event-data-store

Creates a new event data store for CloudTrail Lake.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail create-event-data-store \
  --name {name}
Parameters Description
  • --name - Name for the event data store
  • --retention-period - (Optional) Days to retain events (default: 2557)
  • --advanced-event-selectors - (Optional) Event filters
Return Value

Returns EventDataStoreArn, Name, Status, and RetentionPeriod.

5 Detailed CloudTrail create-event-data-store Examples
Example 1: Create Basic Event Data Store
#!/bin/bash
# Create event data store for anihpj
aws cloudtrail create-event-data-store \
  --name anihpj-event-store

echo "anihpj CloudTrail Lake event data store created"
{ "EventDataStoreArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/abc123-def456", "Name": "anihpj-event-store", "Status": "CREATED", "RetentionPeriod": 2557, "MultiRegionEnabled": true } anihpj CloudTrail Lake event data store created
# Django Models Integration - Create Basic Event Data Store from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create event data store for anihpj store = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-event-store', status='created', description='CloudTrail Lake event data store' ) response = { 'EventDataStoreArn': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/{store.id}', 'Name': store.title, 'Status': 'CREATED', 'RetentionPeriod': 2557, 'MultiRegionEnabled': True } print(response) print(f"anihpj CloudTrail Lake event data store created") print(f"<JobPost {store.title} status=created>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-event-store status=created>
Example 2: Create Event Data Store with Custom Retention
#!/bin/bash
# Create with 1 year retention for compliance
aws cloudtrail create-event-data-store \
  --name anihpj-compliance-store \
  --retention-period 365 \
  --termination-protection-enabled

echo "anihpj compliance event store created (1 year retention)"
{ "EventDataStoreArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/xyz789", "Name": "anihpj-compliance-store", "Status": "CREATED", "RetentionPeriod": 365, "TerminationProtectionEnabled": true } anihpj compliance event store created (1 year retention)
# Django Models Integration - Create Event Data Store with Custom Retention from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create with 1 year retention for compliance store = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-compliance-store', status='created', description='Compliance event store with 1 year retention' ) # Store retention metadata store_config = { 'retention_period': 365, 'termination_protection': True } response = { 'EventDataStoreArn': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/{store.id}', 'Name': store.title, 'Status': 'CREATED', 'RetentionPeriod': 365, 'TerminationProtectionEnabled': True } print(response) print("anihpj compliance event store created (1 year retention)") print(f"<JobPost {store.title} retention=365>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-compliance-store retention=365>
Example 3: Create Event Data Store with Advanced Selectors
#!/bin/bash
# Create with specific event selectors for anihpj resources
aws cloudtrail create-event-data-store \
  --name anihpj-security-events \
  --retention-period 730 \
  --advanced-event-selectors '[
    {
      "Name": "anihpj-iam-events",
      "FieldSelectors": [
        {"Field": "eventSource", "Equals": ["iam.amazonaws.com"]}
      ]
    }
  ]'

echo "anihpj security event data store created"
{ "EventDataStoreArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/sec123", "Name": "anihpj-security-events", "Status": "CREATED", "RetentionPeriod": 730 } anihpj security event data store created
