# Django Models Integration - Create Event Data Store with Advanced Selectors from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create with specific event selectors for anihpj resources store = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-security-events', status='created', description='Security event store with IAM event selectors' ) # Store advanced selectors as config selectors = [ { 'Name': 'anihpj-iam-events', 'FieldSelectors': [ {'Field': 'eventSource', 'Equals': ['iam.amazonaws.com']} ] } ] response = { 'EventDataStoreArn': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/{store.id}', 'Name': store.title, 'Status': 'CREATED', 'RetentionPeriod': 730 } print(response) print("anihpj security event data store created") print(f"<JobPost {store.title} selectors={len(selectors)}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-security-events selectors=1>

delete-event-data-store

Disables the event data store specified by EventDataStore, which accepts an event data store ARN.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail delete-event-data-store \
  --event-data-store {event-data-store-arn}
Parameters Description
  • --event-data-store - ARN of the event data store
Return Value

Returns no content on success. Event data store is marked for deletion.

5 Detailed CloudTrail delete-event-data-store Examples
Example 1: Delete anihpj Event Data Store
#!/bin/bash
# Delete event data store
aws cloudtrail delete-event-data-store \
  --event-data-store "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-test-store"

echo "anihpj event data store deleted"
anihpj event data store deleted
# Django Models Integration - Delete Event Data Store from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete event data store store = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-test-store') store_name = store.title store.delete() print(f"{store_name} event data store deleted") # Output: anihpj-test-store event data store deleted print(f"<JobPost deleted={store_name}>") # Output: <JobPost deleted=anihpj-test-store>
Example 2: Delete with Confirmation
#!/bin/bash
# Delete with confirmation
EVENT_DATA_STORE="arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-dev-store"

# Get store details first
STORE_NAME=$(aws cloudtrail get-event-data-store \
  --event-data-store $EVENT_DATA_STORE \
  --query 'Name' \
  --output text)

echo "Deleting: $STORE_NAME"
read -p "Are you sure? (y/n): " CONFIRM

if [ "$CONFIRM" == "y" ]; then
  aws cloudtrail delete-event-data-store \
    --event-data-store $EVENT_DATA_STORE
  echo "Deleted anihpj event data store"
fi
Deleting: anihpj-dev-store Are you sure? (y/n): y Deleted anihpj event data store
# Django Models Integration - Delete with Confirmation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete with confirmation store = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-dev-store') print(f"Deleting: {store.title}") confirm = input("Are you sure? (y/n): ") if confirm.lower() == 'y': store_name = store.title store.delete() print(f"Deleted {store_name} event data store") else: print("Deletion cancelled") print(f"<JobPost deleted=anihpj-dev-store confirmed=True>") # Output: <JobPost deleted=anihpj-dev-store confirmed=True>
Example 3: Clean Up Old anihpj Stores
#!/bin/bash
# Delete old event data stores
echo "Cleaning up old anihpj event data stores"

aws cloudtrail list-event-data-stores \
  --query 'EventDataStores[?contains(Name, `anihpj-test`)].EventDataStoreArn' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | while read ARN; do
    if [ -n "$ARN" ]; then
      echo "Deleting: $ARN"
      aws cloudtrail delete-event-data-store --event-data-store $ARN
    fi
done
Cleaning up old anihpj event data stores Deleting: arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/test1 Deleting: arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/test2
# Django Models Integration - Clean Up Old Stores from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete old event data stores print("Cleaning up old anihpj event data stores") test_stores = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='anihpj-test') for store in test_stores: arn = f"arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/{store.id}" print(f"Deleting: {arn}") # Bulk delete test stores deleted_count, _ = test_stores.delete() print(f"Deleted {deleted_count} test event data stores") print(f"<QuerySet deleted={deleted_count}>") # Output: <QuerySet deleted=2>

get-event-data-store

Returns information about an event data store specified as either an ARN or the ID portion of the ARN.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail get-event-data-store \
  --event-data-store {event-data-store-arn}
Parameters Description
  • --event-data-store - ARN or ID of the event data store
Return Value

Returns EventDataStoreArn, Name, Status, RetentionPeriod, and configuration details.

5 Detailed CloudTrail get-event-data-store Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Event Data Store Info
#!/bin/bash
# Get event data store details
aws cloudtrail get-event-data-store \
  --event-data-store "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store"
{ "EventDataStoreArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store", "Name": "anihpj-event-store", "Status": "ENABLED", "RetentionPeriod": 2557, "OrganizationEnabled": false, "MultiRegionEnabled": true, "TerminationProtectionEnabled": true, "CreatedTimestamp": "2026-01-15T10:00:00.000Z" }
# Django Models Integration - Get Event Data Store Info from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get event data store details store = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-event-store') response = { 'EventDataStoreArn': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/{store.id}', 'Name': store.title, 'Status': 'ENABLED', 'RetentionPeriod': 2557, 'OrganizationEnabled': False, 'MultiRegionEnabled': True, 'TerminationProtectionEnabled': True, 'CreatedTimestamp': store.created_at.isoformat() if hasattr(store, 'created_at') else 'N/A' } print(response) print(f"<JobPost {store.title} status=ENABLED>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-event-store status=ENABLED>
Example 2: Check anihpj Store Status
#!/bin/bash
# Check event data store status
STORE_ARN="arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store"

STATUS=$(aws cloudtrail get-event-data-store \
  --event-data-store $STORE_ARN \
  --query 'Status' \
  --output text)

echo "anihpj Event Store Status: $STATUS"
anihpj Event Store Status: ENABLED
# Django Models Integration - Check Store Status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Check event data store status store = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-event-store') status = store.status.upper() if store.status else 'ENABLED' print(f"anihpj Event Store Status: {status}") # Output: anihpj Event Store Status: ENABLED print(f"<JobPost {store.title} status={status}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-event-store status=ENABLED>
Example 3: Get Retention Info
#!/bin/bash
# Get retention and protection details
STORE_ARN="arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store"

echo "anihpj Event Store Configuration"
echo "=================================="

aws cloudtrail get-event-data-store \
  --event-data-store $STORE_ARN \
  --query '{
    Name: Name,
    RetentionDays: RetentionPeriod,
    TerminationProtection: TerminationProtectionEnabled,
    MultiRegion: MultiRegionEnabled
  }' \
  --output table
anihpj Event Store Configuration ================================== ---------------------------------------------- | GetEventDataStore | +-----------------------+--------------------+ | MultiRegion | True | | Name | anihpj-event-store | | RetentionDays | 2557 | | TerminationProtection | True | +-----------------------+--------------------+
# Django Models Integration - Get Retention Info from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get retention and protection details store = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-event-store') print("anihpj Event Store Configuration") print("==================================") print("-" * 46) print("| GetEventDataStore |") print(f"+{'-'*23}+{'-'*20}+") print(f"| {'MultiRegion':<21} | {'True':<18} |") print(f"| {'Name':<21} | {store.title:<18} |") print(f"| {'RetentionDays':<21} | {'2557':<18} |") print(f"| {'TerminationProtection':<21} | {'True':<18} |") print(f"+{'-'*23}+{'-'*20}+") print(f"<JobPost {store.title} retention=2557>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-event-store retention=2557>

list-event-data-stores

Returns information about all event data stores in the account, in the current region.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail list-event-data-stores
Parameters Description
  • --next-token - (Optional) Token for pagination
Return Value

Returns EventDataStores array with ARN, Name, and Status for each store.

5 Detailed CloudTrail list-event-data-stores Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Event Data Stores
#!/bin/bash
# List all event data stores
aws cloudtrail list-event-data-stores \
  --query 'EventDataStores[*].[Name,Status]' \
  --output table
---------------------------------------- | ListEventDataStores | +------------------------+-------------+ | anihpj-event-store | ENABLED | | anihpj-security-store | ENABLED | | anihpj-compliance | ENABLED | +------------------------+-------------+
# Django Models Integration - List All Event Data Stores from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all event data stores stores = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='store') print("-" * 40) print("| ListEventDataStores |") print(f"+{'-'*24}+{'-'*13}+") for store in stores: status = store.status.upper() if store.status else 'ENABLED' print(f"| {store.title:<22} | {status:<11} |") print(f"+{'-'*24}+{'-'*13}+") print(f"<QuerySet count={stores.count()}>") # Output: <QuerySet count=3>
Example 2: List anihpj Stores with Details
#!/bin/bash
# List with full details
echo "anihpj CloudTrail Lake Stores"
echo "==============================="

aws cloudtrail list-event-data-stores \
  --output json | jq -r '.EventDataStores[] | select(.Name | startswith("anihpj")) | "Name: \(.Name)\nStatus: \(.Status)\nRetention: \(.RetentionPeriod) days\n---"'
anihpj CloudTrail Lake Stores =============================== Name: anihpj-event-store Status: ENABLED Retention: 2557 days --- Name: anihpj-security-store Status: ENABLED Retention: 365 days ---
# Django Models Integration - List Stores with Details from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List with full details print("anihpj CloudTrail Lake Stores") print("===============================") stores = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='anihpj').filter(title__contains='store') for store in stores: retention = 2557 if 'event' in store.title else 365 print(f"Name: {store.title}") print(f"Status: ENABLED") print(f"Retention: {retention} days") print("---") print(f"<QuerySet [{', '.join([s.title for s in stores])}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [anihpj-event-store, anihpj-security-store]>
Example 3: Export anihpj Store Inventory
#!/bin/bash
# Export to CSV
OUTPUT_FILE="anihpj-event-stores.csv"

echo "Name,ARN,Status,Retention" > $OUTPUT_FILE

aws cloudtrail list-event-data-stores \
  --output json | jq -r '.EventDataStores[] | select(.Name | startswith("anihpj")) | [.Name, .EventDataStoreArn, .Status, .RetentionPeriod] | @csv' >> $OUTPUT_FILE

echo "anihpj event stores exported to $OUTPUT_FILE"
cat $OUTPUT_FILE
anihpj event stores exported to anihpj-event-stores.csv Name,ARN,Status,Retention "anihpj-event-store","arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:eventdatastore/abc123","ENABLED",2557 "anihpj-security-store","arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:eventdatastore/def456","ENABLED",365
# Django Models Integration - Export Store Inventory from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import csv # Export to CSV stores = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='anihpj').filter(title__contains='store') print("anihpj event stores exported to anihpj-event-stores.csv") print("") print("Name,ARN,Status,Retention") for store in stores: arn = f"arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:eventdatastore/{store.id}" retention = 2557 if 'event' in store.title else 365 print(f'"{store.title}","{arn}","ENABLED",{retention}') # Export to file with open('anihpj-event-stores.csv', 'w', newline='') as f: writer = csv.writer(f) writer.writerow(['Name', 'ARN', 'Status', 'Retention']) for store in stores: writer.writerow([store.title, f"arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:eventdatastore/{store.id}", 'ENABLED', retention]) print(f"<QuerySet exported={stores.count()}>") # Output: <QuerySet exported=2>

update-event-data-store

Updates an event data store. The required EventDataStore value is an ARN or the ID portion of the ARN.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail update-event-data-store \
  --event-data-store {event-data-store-arn} \
  [--retention-period {days}]
Parameters Description
  • --event-data-store - ARN of the event data store
  • --retention-period - (Optional) Days to retain events
  • --kms-key-id - (Optional) KMS key for encryption
Return Value

Returns updated EventDataStoreArn, Name, and RetentionPeriod.

5 Detailed CloudTrail update-event-data-store Examples
Example 1: Update Retention Period
#!/bin/bash
# Update retention to 1 year
aws cloudtrail update-event-data-store \
  --event-data-store "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store" \
  --retention-period 365

echo "anihpj event store retention updated to 365 days"
{ "EventDataStoreArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store", "Name": "anihpj-event-store", "RetentionPeriod": 365 } anihpj event store retention updated to 365 days
# Django Models Integration - Update Retention Period from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Update retention to 1 year store = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-event-store') # Store retention in description or a config field store.description = f'Retention: 365 days - {store.description}' store.save() response = { 'EventDataStoreArn': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/{store.id}', 'Name': store.title, 'RetentionPeriod': 365 } print(response) print("anihpj event store retention updated to 365 days") print(f"<JobPost {store.title} retention=365>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-event-store retention=365>
Example 2: Enable Termination Protection
#!/bin/bash
# Enable termination protection for anihpj store
aws cloudtrail update-event-data-store \
  --event-data-store "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store" \
  --termination-protection-enabled

echo "anihpj event store termination protection enabled"
{ "EventDataStoreArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...", "TerminationProtectionEnabled": true } anihpj event store termination protection enabled
# Django Models Integration - Enable Termination Protection from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Enable termination protection for anihpj store store = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-event-store') store.status = 'protected' store.save() response = { 'EventDataStoreArn': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:...', 'TerminationProtectionEnabled': True } print(response) print("anihpj event store termination protection enabled") print(f"<JobPost {store.title} protected=True>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-event-store protected=True>
Example 3: Update KMS Encryption
#!/bin/bash
# Add KMS encryption to anihpj store
STORE_ARN="arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store"
KMS_KEY="arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/abc123"

aws cloudtrail update-event-data-store \
  --event-data-store $STORE_ARN \
  --kms-key-id $KMS_KEY

echo "anihpj event store KMS encryption enabled"
{ "EventDataStoreArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...", "KmsKeyId": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/abc123" } anihpj event store KMS encryption enabled
# Django Models Integration - Update KMS Encryption from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Add KMS encryption to anihpj store store = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-event-store') kms_key = 'arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/abc123' store.description = f'KMS: {kms_key}' store.save() response = { 'EventDataStoreArn': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:...', 'KmsKeyId': kms_key } print(response) print("anihpj event store KMS encryption enabled") print(f"<JobPost {store.title} kms=enabled>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-event-store kms=enabled>

restore-event-data-store

Restores a previously deleted event data store specified by EventDataStore.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail restore-event-data-store \
  --event-data-store {event-data-store-arn}
Parameters Description
  • --event-data-store - ARN of the deleted event data store
Return Value

Returns restored EventDataStoreArn, Name, and Status.

5 Detailed CloudTrail restore-event-data-store Examples
Example 1: Restore anihpj Event Data Store
#!/bin/bash
# Restore deleted event data store
aws cloudtrail restore-event-data-store \
  --event-data-store "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store"

echo "anihpj event data store restored"
{ "EventDataStoreArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store", "Name": "anihpj-event-store", "Status": "ENABLED" } anihpj event data store restored
# Django Models Integration - Restore Event Data Store from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Restore deleted event data store (from soft delete) store, created = JobPost.objects.get_or_create( title='anihpj-event-store', defaults={'status': 'enabled', 'description': 'Restored event store'} ) if not created: store.status = 'enabled' store.save() response = { 'EventDataStoreArn': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/{store.id}', 'Name': store.title, 'Status': 'ENABLED' } print(response) print("anihpj event data store restored") print(f"<JobPost {store.title} status=ENABLED>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-event-store status=ENABLED>
Example 2: Restore with Verification
#!/bin/bash
# Restore and verify status
STORE_ARN="arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store"

echo "Restoring anihpj event data store..."
aws cloudtrail restore-event-data-store --event-data-store $STORE_ARN

# Verify
sleep 5
STATUS=$(aws cloudtrail get-event-data-store \
  --event-data-store $STORE_ARN \
  --query 'Status' \
  --output text)

echo "anihpj store status: $STATUS"
Restoring anihpj event data store... { "EventDataStoreArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...", "Status": "ENABLED" } anihpj store status: ENABLED
# Django Models Integration - Restore with Verification from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import time # Restore and verify status print("Restoring anihpj event data store...") store, created = JobPost.objects.get_or_create( title='anihpj-event-store', defaults={'status': 'enabled'} ) if not created: store.status = 'enabled' store.save() response = { 'EventDataStoreArn': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:...', 'Status': 'ENABLED' } print(response) # Verify time.sleep(5) store.refresh_from_db() print(f"anihpj store status: {store.status.upper()}") print(f"<JobPost {store.title} verified=True>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-event-store verified=True>

start-event-data-store-ingestion

Starts the ingestion of live events on an event data store specified as either an ARN or the ID portion of the ARN.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail start-event-data-store-ingestion \
  --event-data-store {event-data-store-arn}
Parameters Description
  • --event-data-store - ARN or ID of the event data store
Return Value

Returns no content on success. Event ingestion begins.

5 Detailed CloudTrail start-event-data-store-ingestion Examples
Example 1: Start anihpj Ingestion
#!/bin/bash
# Start ingestion for anihpj store
aws cloudtrail start-event-data-store-ingestion \
  --event-data-store "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store"

echo "anihpj event store ingestion started"
anihpj event store ingestion started
# Django Models Integration - Start Ingestion from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Start ingestion for anihpj store store = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-event-store') store.status = 'ingesting' store.save() print(f"anihpj event store ingestion started") # Output: anihpj event store ingestion started print(f"<JobPost {store.title} status=ingesting>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-event-store status=ingesting>
Example 2: Start All anihpj Store Ingestion
#!/bin/bash
# Start ingestion for all anihpj stores
echo "Starting ingestion for all anihpj stores"

aws cloudtrail list-event-data-stores \
  --query 'EventDataStores[?contains(Name, `anihpj`)].EventDataStoreArn' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | while read ARN; do
    if [ -n "$ARN" ]; then
      echo "Starting: $ARN"
      aws cloudtrail start-event-data-store-ingestion --event-data-store $ARN
    fi
done
Starting ingestion for all anihpj stores Starting: arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store Starting: arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-security-store
# Django Models Integration - Start All Store Ingestion from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Start ingestion for all anihpj stores print("Starting ingestion for all anihpj stores") stores = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='anihpj').filter(title__contains='store') for store in stores: arn = f"arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/{store.id}" print(f"Starting: {arn}") store.status = 'ingesting' store.save() # Bulk update started_count = stores.update(status='ingesting') print(f"<QuerySet started={started_count}>") # Output: <QuerySet started=2>

stop-event-data-store-ingestion

Stops the ingestion of live events on an event data store specified as either an ARN or the ID portion of the ARN.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail stop-event-data-store-ingestion \
  --event-data-store {event-data-store-arn}
Parameters Description
  • --event-data-store - ARN or ID of the event data store
Return Value

Returns no content on success. Event ingestion stops.

5 Detailed CloudTrail stop-event-data-store-ingestion Examples
Example 1: Stop anihpj Ingestion
#!/bin/bash
# Stop ingestion for dev store
aws cloudtrail stop-event-data-store-ingestion \
  --event-data-store "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-dev-store"

echo "anihpj dev store ingestion stopped"
anihpj dev store ingestion stopped
# Django Models Integration - Stop Ingestion from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Stop ingestion for dev store store = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-dev-store') store.status = 'stopped' store.save() print(f"anihpj dev store ingestion stopped") # Output: anihpj dev store ingestion stopped print(f"<JobPost {store.title} status=stopped>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-dev-store status=stopped>
Example 2: Stop with Cost Logging
#!/bin/bash
# Stop ingestion and log for billing
STORE_ARN="arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-dev-store"

echo "[$(date)] Stopping ingestion for cost optimization"
aws cloudtrail stop-event-data-store-ingestion --event-data-store $STORE_ARN

echo "anihpj store ingestion stopped for cost savings"
[2026-01-17 15:30:00] Stopping ingestion for cost optimization anihpj store ingestion stopped for cost savings
# Django Models Integration - Stop with Cost Logging from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime # Stop ingestion and log for billing store = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-dev-store') print(f"[{datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}] Stopping ingestion for cost optimization") store.status = 'stopped' store.save() print("anihpj store ingestion stopped for cost savings") print(f"<JobPost {store.title} stopped_at={datetime.now().isoformat()}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-dev-store stopped_at=2026-01-17T15:30:00>

get-event-selectors

Describes the settings for the event selectors that you configured for your trail.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors \
  --trail-name {trail-name}
Parameters Description
  • --trail-name - Name or ARN of the trail
Return Value

Returns TrailARN and EventSelectors with ReadWriteType, DataResources, and filters.

5 Detailed CloudTrail get-event-selectors Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Trail Event Selectors
#!/bin/bash
# Get event selectors for anihpj trail
aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors \
  --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail
{ "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail", "EventSelectors": [ { "ReadWriteType": "All", "IncludeManagementEvents": true, "DataResources": [ { "Type": "AWS::S3::Object", "Values": ["arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-media/"] } ] } ] }
# Django Models Integration - Get Event Selectors from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get event selectors for anihpj trail trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') selectors = { 'TrailARN': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/{trail.title}', 'EventSelectors': [ { 'ReadWriteType': 'All', 'IncludeManagementEvents': True, 'DataResources': [ {'Type': 'AWS::S3::Object', 'Values': ['arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-media/']} ] } ] } print(selectors) print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} selectors={len(selectors['EventSelectors'])}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail selectors=1>
Example 2: Check Data Event Configuration
#!/bin/bash
# Check if data events are configured
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-audit-trail"

echo "anihpj Trail Data Event Configuration"
echo "========================================"

aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors \
  --trail-name $TRAIL_NAME \
  --query 'EventSelectors[].DataResources' \
  --output json | jq -r '.[][] | "Type: \(.Type)\nResources: \(.Values | join(", "))\n---"'
anihpj Trail Data Event Configuration ======================================== Type: AWS::S3::Object Resources: arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-media/, arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-static/ --- Type: AWS::Lambda::Function Resources: arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:anihpj-* ---
# Django Models Integration - Check Data Event Configuration from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Check if data events are configured trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') print("anihpj Trail Data Event Configuration") print("========================================") data_resources = [ {'Type': 'AWS::S3::Object', 'Values': ['arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-media/', 'arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-static/']}, {'Type': 'AWS::Lambda::Function', 'Values': ['arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:anihpj-*']} ] for resource in data_resources: print(f"Type: {resource['Type']}") print(f"Resources: {', '.join(resource['Values'])}") print("---") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} data_resources={len(data_resources)}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail data_resources=2>
Example 3: Audit Event Selector Summary
#!/bin/bash
# Summarize event selectors for anihpj trail
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-audit-trail"

echo "anihpj Trail Event Selector Summary"
echo "======================================"

SELECTORS=$(aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name $TRAIL_NAME)

echo "Management Events: $(echo $SELECTORS | jq -r '.EventSelectors[0].IncludeManagementEvents')"
echo "Read/Write Type: $(echo $SELECTORS | jq -r '.EventSelectors[0].ReadWriteType')"
echo "Data Resource Types: $(echo $SELECTORS | jq -r '[.EventSelectors[].DataResources[].Type] | unique | join(", ")')"
anihpj Trail Event Selector Summary ====================================== Management Events: true Read/Write Type: All Data Resource Types: AWS::S3::Object, AWS::Lambda::Function
# Django Models Integration - Audit Event Selector Summary from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Summarize event selectors for anihpj trail trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') print("anihpj Trail Event Selector Summary") print("======================================") config = { 'IncludeManagementEvents': True, 'ReadWriteType': 'All', 'DataResourceTypes': ['AWS::S3::Object', 'AWS::Lambda::Function'] } print(f"Management Events: {str(config['IncludeManagementEvents']).lower()}") print(f"Read/Write Type: {config['ReadWriteType']}") print(f"Data Resource Types: {', '.join(config['DataResourceTypes'])}") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} config_keys={len(config)}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail config_keys=3>

put-event-selectors

Configures an event selector or advanced event selectors for your trail.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail put-event-selectors \
  --trail-name {trail-name} \
  --event-selectors {event-selectors}
Parameters Description
  • --trail-name - Name or ARN of the trail
  • --event-selectors - JSON array of event selector configurations
  • --advanced-event-selectors - (Optional) More precise event filtering
Return Value

Returns TrailARN and configured EventSelectors array.

5 Detailed CloudTrail put-event-selectors Examples
Example 1: Configure S3 Data Events for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Add S3 data events for anihpj buckets
aws cloudtrail put-event-selectors \
  --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --event-selectors '[
    {
      "ReadWriteType": "All",
      "IncludeManagementEvents": true,
      "DataResources": [
        {
          "Type": "AWS::S3::Object",
          "Values": ["arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-media/", "arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-static/"]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]'

echo "S3 data events configured for anihpj trail"
{ "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail", "EventSelectors": [...] } S3 data events configured for anihpj trail
# Django Models Integration - Configure S3 Data Events from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Add S3 data events for anihpj buckets trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') event_selectors = [ { 'ReadWriteType': 'All', 'IncludeManagementEvents': True, 'DataResources': [ { 'Type': 'AWS::S3::Object', 'Values': ['arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-media/', 'arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-static/'] } ] } ] # Store config in trail metadata trail.description = f"Event selectors: {len(event_selectors)}" trail.save() print({'TrailARN': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/{trail.title}', 'EventSelectors': event_selectors}) print("S3 data events configured for anihpj trail") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} selectors={len(event_selectors)}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail selectors=1>
Example 2: Configure Lambda Data Events
#!/bin/bash
# Add Lambda function invocation logging
aws cloudtrail put-event-selectors \
  --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --event-selectors '[
    {
      "ReadWriteType": "All",
      "IncludeManagementEvents": true,
      "DataResources": [
        {
          "Type": "AWS::Lambda::Function",
          "Values": ["arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:anihpj-*"]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]'

echo "Lambda data events configured for anihpj"
{ "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...", "EventSelectors": [...] } Lambda data events configured for anihpj
# Django Models Integration - Configure Lambda Data Events from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Add Lambda function invocation logging trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') event_selectors = [ { 'ReadWriteType': 'All', 'IncludeManagementEvents': True, 'DataResources': [ { 'Type': 'AWS::Lambda::Function', 'Values': ['arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:anihpj-*'] } ] } ] trail.description = f"Lambda events configured" trail.save() print({'TrailARN': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:...', 'EventSelectors': event_selectors}) print("Lambda data events configured for anihpj") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} lambda_events=True>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail lambda_events=True>
Example 3: Configure Advanced Event Selectors
#!/bin/bash
# Use advanced event selectors for granular control
aws cloudtrail put-event-selectors \
  --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --advanced-event-selectors '[
    {
      "Name": "anihpj-management-events",
      "FieldSelectors": [
        {"Field": "eventCategory", "Equals": ["Management"]}
      ]
    },
    {
      "Name": "anihpj-s3-writes",
      "FieldSelectors": [
        {"Field": "eventCategory", "Equals": ["Data"]},
        {"Field": "eventSource", "Equals": ["s3.amazonaws.com"]},
        {"Field": "eventName", "StartsWith": ["Put", "Delete"]},
        {"Field": "resources.ARN", "StartsWith": ["arn:aws:s3:::anihpj-"]}
      ]
    }
  ]'

echo "Advanced event selectors configured for anihpj"
{ "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...", "AdvancedEventSelectors": [...] } Advanced event selectors configured for anihpj
# Django Models Integration - Configure Advanced Event Selectors from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Use advanced event selectors for granular control trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') advanced_selectors = [ { 'Name': 'anihpj-management-events', 'FieldSelectors': [ {'Field': 'eventCategory', 'Equals': ['Management']} ] }, { 'Name': 'anihpj-s3-writes', 'FieldSelectors': [ {'Field': 'eventCategory', 'Equals': ['Data']}, {'Field': 'eventSource', 'Equals': ['s3.amazonaws.com']} ] } ] trail.description = f"Advanced selectors: {len(advanced_selectors)}" trail.save() print({'TrailARN': 'arn:aws:cloudtrail:...', 'AdvancedEventSelectors': advanced_selectors}) print("Advanced event selectors configured for anihpj") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} advanced_selectors={len(advanced_selectors)}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail advanced_selectors=2>

get-insight-selectors

Describes the settings for the Insights event selectors that you configured for your trail.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail get-insight-selectors \
  --trail-name {trail-name}
Parameters Description
  • --trail-name - Name or ARN of the trail
Return Value

Returns InsightSelectors array with enabled InsightType values.

5 Detailed CloudTrail get-insight-selectors Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Insight Selectors
#!/bin/bash
# Get insight selectors for anihpj trail
aws cloudtrail get-insight-selectors \
  --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail
{ "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail", "InsightSelectors": [ { "InsightType": "ApiCallRateInsight" }, { "InsightType": "ApiErrorRateInsight" } ] }
# Django Models Integration - Get Insight Selectors from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get insight selectors for anihpj trail trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') response = { 'TrailARN': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/{trail.title}', 'InsightSelectors': [ {'InsightType': 'ApiCallRateInsight'}, {'InsightType': 'ApiErrorRateInsight'} ] } print(response) print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} insights={len(response['InsightSelectors'])}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail insights=2>
Example 2: Check Insight Types Enabled
#!/bin/bash
# Check which insights are enabled
TRAIL_NAME="anihpj-audit-trail"

echo "anihpj Insights Configuration"
echo "==============================="

aws cloudtrail get-insight-selectors \
  --trail-name $TRAIL_NAME \
  --query 'InsightSelectors[].InsightType' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | while read TYPE; do
    echo "✓ $TYPE"
done
anihpj Insights Configuration =============================== ✓ ApiCallRateInsight ✓ ApiErrorRateInsight
# Django Models Integration - Check Insight Types Enabled from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Check which insights are enabled trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') print("anihpj Insights Configuration") print("===============================") insight_types = ['ApiCallRateInsight', 'ApiErrorRateInsight'] for insight_type in insight_types: print(f"✓ {insight_type}") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} insight_count={len(insight_types)}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail insight_count=2>

put-insight-selectors

Lets you enable Insights event logging by specifying the Insights selectors that you want to enable on an existing trail.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail put-insight-selectors \
  --trail-name {trail-name} \
  --insight-selectors {insight-selectors}
Parameters Description
  • --trail-name - Name or ARN of the trail
  • --insight-selectors - JSON array of insight types to enable
Return Value

Returns TrailARN and configured InsightSelectors.

5 Detailed CloudTrail put-insight-selectors Examples
Example 1: Enable All Insights for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Enable both insight types
aws cloudtrail put-insight-selectors \
  --trail-name anihpj-audit-trail \
  --insight-selectors '[
    {"InsightType": "ApiCallRateInsight"},
    {"InsightType": "ApiErrorRateInsight"}
  ]'

echo "All insights enabled for anihpj trail"
{ "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail", "InsightSelectors": [ {"InsightType": "ApiCallRateInsight"}, {"InsightType": "ApiErrorRateInsight"} ] } All insights enabled for anihpj trail
# Django Models Integration - Enable All Insights from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Enable both insight types trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') insight_selectors = [ {'InsightType': 'ApiCallRateInsight'}, {'InsightType': 'ApiErrorRateInsight'} ] trail.description = f"Insights enabled: {len(insight_selectors)}" trail.save() response = { 'TrailARN': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/{trail.title}', 'InsightSelectors': insight_selectors } print(response) print("All insights enabled for anihpj trail") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} insights={len(insight_selectors)}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-audit-trail insights=2>
Example 2: Enable API Error Rate Insight
#!/bin/bash
# Enable error rate insights for anihpj security monitoring
aws cloudtrail put-insight-selectors \
  --trail-name anihpj-security-trail \
  --insight-selectors '[{"InsightType": "ApiErrorRateInsight"}]'

echo "API Error Rate Insight enabled for anihpj security monitoring"
{ "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...", "InsightSelectors": [{"InsightType": "ApiErrorRateInsight"}] } API Error Rate Insight enabled for anihpj security monitoring
# Django Models Integration - Enable API Error Rate Insight from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Enable error rate insights for anihpj security monitoring trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-security-trail') insight_selectors = [{'InsightType': 'ApiErrorRateInsight'}] trail.description = "Error rate insight enabled" trail.save() response = { 'TrailARN': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:...', 'InsightSelectors': insight_selectors } print(response) print("API Error Rate Insight enabled for anihpj security monitoring") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} error_insight=True>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-security-trail error_insight=True>
Example 3: Disable Insights
#!/bin/bash
# Disable insights to reduce costs
aws cloudtrail put-insight-selectors \
  --trail-name anihpj-dev-trail \
  --insight-selectors '[]'

echo "Insights disabled for anihpj dev trail (cost savings)"
{ "TrailARN": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...", "InsightSelectors": [] } Insights disabled for anihpj dev trail (cost savings)
# Django Models Integration - Disable Insights from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Disable insights to reduce costs trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-dev-trail') insight_selectors = [] # Empty list to disable trail.description = "Insights disabled for cost savings" trail.save() response = { 'TrailARN': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:...', 'InsightSelectors': insight_selectors } print(response) print("Insights disabled for anihpj dev trail (cost savings)") print(f"<JobPost {trail.title} insights_enabled=False>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-dev-trail insights_enabled=False>

start-query

Starts a CloudTrail Lake query.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail start-query \
  --query-statement {query-statement}
Parameters Description
  • --query-statement - SQL query for CloudTrail Lake
  • --delivery-s3-uri - (Optional) S3 location for results
Return Value

Returns QueryId to track query progress and get results.

5 Detailed CloudTrail start-query Examples
Example 1: Query anihpj S3 Events
#!/bin/bash
# Query S3 events for anihpj buckets
aws cloudtrail start-query \
  --query-statement "SELECT eventTime, eventName, userIdentity.userName, requestParameters.bucketName 
    FROM abc123-def456-event-store 
    WHERE eventSource = 's3.amazonaws.com' 
    AND requestParameters.bucketName LIKE 'anihpj%' 
    ORDER BY eventTime DESC 
    LIMIT 100"

echo "anihpj S3 query started"
{ "QueryId": "query-abc123-def456" } anihpj S3 query started
# Django Models Integration - Query S3 Events from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Simulate CloudTrail Lake query for S3 events query_statement = """ SELECT eventTime, eventName, userIdentity.userName, requestParameters.bucketName FROM abc123-event-store WHERE eventSource = 's3.amazonaws.com' AND requestParameters.bucketName LIKE 'anihpj%' """ # Create query tracking record query_id = f"query-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}" query_record = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'query-{query_id}', status='running', description=query_statement ) print({'QueryId': query_id}) print("anihpj S3 query started") print(f"<JobPost {query_record.title} status=running>") # Output: <JobPost query-abc123-def456 status=running>
Example 2: Query IAM Events
#!/bin/bash
# Query IAM activity for anihpj users
QUERY="SELECT eventTime, eventName, userIdentity.userName, sourceIPAddress 
       FROM abc123-event-store 
       WHERE eventSource = 'iam.amazonaws.com' 
       AND userIdentity.userName LIKE 'anihpj%' 
       ORDER BY eventTime DESC 
       LIMIT 50"

QUERY_ID=$(aws cloudtrail start-query \
  --query-statement "$QUERY" \
  --query 'QueryId' \
  --output text)

echo "anihpj IAM query started: $QUERY_ID"
anihpj IAM query started: query-xyz789-abc123
# Django Models Integration - Query IAM Events from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Query IAM activity for anihpj users query_id = f"query-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:16]}" query_record = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'iam-query-{query_id[:8]}', status='running', description='IAM events query for anihpj users' ) print(f"anihpj IAM query started: {query_id}") print(f"<JobPost {query_record.title} query_id={query_id}>") # Output: <JobPost iam-query-xyz789-abc123 query_id=query-xyz789-abc123>
Example 3: Query Error Events
#!/bin/bash
# Query errors related to anihpj resources
aws cloudtrail start-query \
  --query-statement "SELECT eventTime, eventName, errorCode, errorMessage, userIdentity.userName 
    FROM abc123-event-store 
    WHERE errorCode IS NOT NULL 
    AND (requestParameters LIKE '%anihpj%' OR resources LIKE '%anihpj%')
    ORDER BY eventTime DESC 
    LIMIT 100"

echo "anihpj error query started"
{ "QueryId": "query-err123-456" } anihpj error query started
# Django Models Integration - Query Error Events from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import uuid # Query errors related to anihpj resources query_id = f"query-err{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}" query_record = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'error-query-{query_id[:8]}', status='running', description='Error events query for anihpj resources' ) print({'QueryId': query_id}) print("anihpj error query started") print(f"<JobPost {query_record.title} query_type=error>") # Output: <JobPost error-query-err123-456 query_type=error>

get-query-results

Gets event data results of a query.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail get-query-results \
  --query-id {query-id}
Parameters Description
  • --query-id - ID of the query
  • --next-token - (Optional) Token for pagination
Return Value

Returns QueryStatus, QueryStatistics, and QueryResultRows with event data.

5 Detailed CloudTrail get-query-results Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Query Results
#!/bin/bash
# Get results from query
aws cloudtrail get-query-results \
  --query-id "query-abc123-def456"
{ "QueryStatus": "FINISHED", "QueryStatistics": { "ResultsCount": 25, "TotalResultsCount": 25 }, "QueryResultRows": [ [ {"eventTime": "2026-01-17T15:00:00Z"}, {"eventName": "PutObject"}, {"userName": "anihpj-admin"}, {"bucketName": "anihpj-media"} ], ... ] }
# Django Models Integration - Get Query Results from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get results from query query_record = JobPost.objects.get(title__contains='query-abc123') query_record.status = 'finished' query_record.save() results = { 'QueryStatus': 'FINISHED', 'QueryStatistics': {'ResultsCount': 25, 'TotalResultsCount': 25}, 'QueryResultRows': [ [{'eventTime': '2026-01-17T15:00:00Z'}, {'eventName': 'PutObject'}, {'userName': 'anihpj-admin'}, {'bucketName': 'anihpj-media'}] ] } print(results) print(f"<JobPost {query_record.title} results={results['QueryStatistics']['TotalResultsCount']}>") # Output: <JobPost query-abc123-def456 results=25>
Example 2: Wait for Query and Get Results
#!/bin/bash
# Start query, wait for completion, get results
QUERY_ID=$(aws cloudtrail start-query \
  --query-statement "SELECT eventTime, eventName FROM abc123-store WHERE eventSource = 's3.amazonaws.com' LIMIT 10" \
  --query 'QueryId' \
  --output text)

echo "Query ID: $QUERY_ID"
echo "Waiting for anihpj query to complete..."

while true; do
  STATUS=$(aws cloudtrail describe-query \
    --query-id $QUERY_ID \
    --query 'QueryStatus' \
    --output text)
  
  if [ "$STATUS" == "FINISHED" ]; then
    echo "Query complete!"
    aws cloudtrail get-query-results --query-id $QUERY_ID
    break
  elif [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ]; then
    echo "Query failed"
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
Query ID: query-abc123-def456 Waiting for anihpj query to complete... Query complete! { "QueryStatus": "FINISHED", "QueryResultRows": [...] }
# Django Models Integration - Wait for Query and Get Results from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import time # Start query, wait for completion, get results query_record = JobPost.objects.create( title='query-abc123-def456', status='running' ) print(f"Query ID: {query_record.title}") print("Waiting for anihpj query to complete...") # Simulate polling while True: query_record.refresh_from_db() if query_record.status == 'finished': print("Query complete!") print({'QueryStatus': 'FINISHED', 'QueryResultRows': [...]}) break elif query_record.status == 'failed': print("Query failed") break time.sleep(5) # Simulate completion query_record.status = 'finished' query_record.save() print(f"<JobPost {query_record.title} status=finished>") # Output: <JobPost query-abc123-def456 status=finished>
Example 3: Export Results to File
#!/bin/bash
# Export query results to JSON file
QUERY_ID="query-abc123-def456"
OUTPUT_FILE="anihpj-query-results.json"

aws cloudtrail get-query-results \
  --query-id $QUERY_ID > $OUTPUT_FILE

echo "anihpj query results saved to $OUTPUT_FILE"
echo "Total rows: $(jq '.QueryStatistics.TotalResultsCount' $OUTPUT_FILE)"
anihpj query results saved to anihpj-query-results.json Total rows: 150
# Django Models Integration - Export Results to File from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json # Export query results to JSON file query_record = JobPost.objects.get(title='query-abc123-def456') results = { 'QueryStatus': 'FINISHED', 'QueryStatistics': {'TotalResultsCount': 150}, 'QueryResultRows': [...] # Actual result rows } with open('anihpj-query-results.json', 'w') as f: json.dump(results, f, indent=2) print("anihpj query results saved to anihpj-query-results.json") print(f"Total rows: {results['QueryStatistics']['TotalResultsCount']}") print(f"<JobPost {query_record.title} exported_rows=150>") # Output: <JobPost query-abc123-def456 exported_rows=150>

describe-query

Returns metadata about a query, including query run time in milliseconds, number of events scanned and matched, and query status.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail describe-query \
  --query-id {query-id}
Parameters Description
  • --query-id - ID of the query to describe
Return Value

Returns QueryId, QueryStatus, QueryString, and QueryStatistics with execution metrics.

5 Detailed CloudTrail describe-query Examples
Example 1: Describe anihpj Query
#!/bin/bash
# Describe query status
aws cloudtrail describe-query \
  --query-id "query-abc123-def456"
{ "QueryId": "query-abc123-def456", "QueryString": "SELECT eventTime, eventName FROM...", "QueryStatus": "FINISHED", "QueryStatistics": { "EventsMatched": 150, "EventsScanned": 10000, "ExecutionTimeInMillis": 2500, "BytesScanned": 1048576 } }
# Django Models Integration - Describe Query from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Describe query status query_record = JobPost.objects.get(title='query-abc123-def456') response = { 'QueryId': query_record.title, 'QueryString': query_record.description, 'QueryStatus': query_record.status.upper(), 'QueryStatistics': { 'EventsMatched': 150, 'EventsScanned': 10000, 'ExecutionTimeInMillis': 2500, 'BytesScanned': 1048576 } } print(response) print(f"<JobPost {query_record.title} status={response['QueryStatus']}>") # Output: <JobPost query-abc123-def456 status=FINISHED>
Example 2: Check Query Progress
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor query progress
QUERY_ID="query-abc123-def456"

echo "anihpj Query Progress"
echo "======================"

aws cloudtrail describe-query \
  --query-id $QUERY_ID \
  --query '{
    Status: QueryStatus,
    EventsMatched: QueryStatistics.EventsMatched,
    EventsScanned: QueryStatistics.EventsScanned,
    RuntimeMs: QueryStatistics.ExecutionTimeInMillis
  }' \
  --output table
anihpj Query Progress ====================== ------------------------------------------- | DescribeQuery | +-----------------+-----------------------+ | EventsMatched | 150 | | EventsScanned | 10000 | | RuntimeMs | 2500 | | Status | FINISHED | +-----------------+-----------------------+
# Django Models Integration - Check Query Progress from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Monitor query progress query_record = JobPost.objects.get(title='query-abc123-def456') print("anihpj Query Progress") print("======================") print("-" * 43) print("| DescribeQuery |") print(f"+{'-'*17}+{'-'*23}+") stats = { 'EventsMatched': 150, 'EventsScanned': 10000, 'RuntimeMs': 2500, 'Status': query_record.status.upper() } for key, value in stats.items(): print(f"| {key:<15} | {value:<21} |") print(f"+{'-'*17}+{'-'*23}+") print(f"<JobPost {query_record.title} events_matched=150>") # Output: <JobPost query-abc123-def456 events_matched=150>

list-queries

Returns a list of queries and query statuses for the past seven days.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail list-queries \
  --event-data-store {event-data-store-arn}
Parameters Description
  • --event-data-store - ARN of the event data store
  • --query-status - (Optional) Filter by QUEUED, RUNNING, FINISHED, FAILED, CANCELLED
  • --start-time - (Optional) Filter by start time
Return Value

Returns Queries array with QueryId, QueryStatus, and CreationTime.

5 Detailed CloudTrail list-queries Examples
Example 1: List anihpj Queries
#!/bin/bash
# List recent queries
aws cloudtrail list-queries \
  --event-data-store "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store" \
  --query 'Queries[*].[QueryId,QueryStatus,CreationTime]' \
  --output table
-------------------------------------------------------- | ListQueries | +------------------------+----------+------------------+ | query-abc123-def456 | FINISHED | 2026-01-17T15:00 | | query-xyz789-abc123 | RUNNING | 2026-01-17T15:05 | | query-err123-456 | FINISHED | 2026-01-17T14:30 | +------------------------+----------+------------------+
# Django Models Integration - List Queries from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List recent queries queries = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='query-').order_by('-id')[:10] print("-" * 56) print("| ListQueries |") print(f"+{'-'*24}+{'-'*10}+{'-'*18}+") for query in queries: print(f"| {query.title:<22} | {query.status.upper():<8} | {query.created_at if hasattr(query, 'created_at') else 'N/A':<16} |") print(f"+{'-'*24}+{'-'*10}+{'-'*18}+") print(f"<QuerySet count={queries.count()}>") # Output: <QuerySet count=3>
Example 2: List Running Queries
#!/bin/bash
# List only running queries
STORE_ARN="arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store"

echo "anihpj Running Queries"
echo "========================"

aws cloudtrail list-queries \
  --event-data-store $STORE_ARN \
  --query-status RUNNING \
  --output json | jq -r '.Queries[] | "ID: \(.QueryId)\nStarted: \(.CreationTime)\n---"'
anihpj Running Queries ======================== ID: query-xyz789-abc123 Started: 2026-01-17T15:05:00Z --- ID: query-long-001 Started: 2026-01-17T15:00:00Z ---
# Django Models Integration - List Running Queries from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List only running queries print("anihpj Running Queries") print("========================") running_queries = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='query-', status='running') for query in running_queries: print(f"ID: {query.title}") started = query.created_at.isoformat() if hasattr(query, 'created_at') else 'N/A' print(f"Started: {started}") print("---") print(f"<QuerySet running={running_queries.count()}>") # Output: <QuerySet running=2>

cancel-query

Cancels a query if the query is not in a terminated state, such as CANCELLED, FAILED, TIMED_OUT, or FINISHED.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail cancel-query \
  --query-id {query-id}
Parameters Description
  • --query-id - ID of the query to cancel
Return Value

Returns QueryId and QueryStatus as CANCELLED.

5 Detailed CloudTrail cancel-query Examples
Example 1: Cancel anihpj Query
#!/bin/bash
# Cancel a running query
aws cloudtrail cancel-query \
  --query-id "query-abc123-def456"

echo "anihpj query cancelled"
{ "QueryId": "query-abc123-def456", "QueryStatus": "CANCELLED" } anihpj query cancelled
# Django Models Integration - Cancel Query from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Cancel a running query query = JobPost.objects.get(title='query-abc123-def456') query.status = 'cancelled' query.save() response = { 'QueryId': query.title, 'QueryStatus': 'CANCELLED' } print(response) print("anihpj query cancelled") print(f"<JobPost {query.title} status=CANCELLED>") # Output: <JobPost query-abc123-def456 status=CANCELLED>
Example 2: Cancel All Running Queries
#!/bin/bash
# Cancel all running queries for anihpj store
STORE_ARN="arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store"

echo "Cancelling all running anihpj queries..."

aws cloudtrail list-queries \
  --event-data-store $STORE_ARN \
  --query-status RUNNING \
  --query 'Queries[].QueryId' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | while read QUERY_ID; do
    if [ -n "$QUERY_ID" ]; then
      echo "Cancelling: $QUERY_ID"
      aws cloudtrail cancel-query --query-id $QUERY_ID
    fi
done

echo "All anihpj queries cancelled"
Cancelling all running anihpj queries... Cancelling: query-xyz789-abc123 Cancelling: query-long-001 All anihpj queries cancelled
# Django Models Integration - Cancel All Running Queries from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Cancel all running queries for anihpj store print("Cancelling all running anihpj queries...") running_queries = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='query-', status='running') for query in running_queries: print(f"Cancelling: {query.title}") # Bulk cancel cancelled_count = running_queries.update(status='cancelled') print(f"All anihpj queries cancelled") print(f"<QuerySet cancelled={cancelled_count}>") # Output: <QuerySet cancelled=2>

lookup-events

Looks up management events or CloudTrail Insights events captured by CloudTrail.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  [--lookup-attributes {attributes}] \
  [--start-time {timestamp}] \
  [--end-time {timestamp}]
Parameters Description
  • --lookup-attributes - (Optional) AttributeKey/AttributeValue pairs
  • --start-time - (Optional) Start of time range
  • --end-time - (Optional) End of time range
Return Value

Returns Events array with EventTime, EventName, Username, and CloudTrailEvent.

5 Detailed CloudTrail lookup-events Examples
Example 1: Lookup anihpj User Events
#!/bin/bash
# Lookup events by username
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=Username,AttributeValue=anihpj-admin \
  --query 'Events[*].[EventTime,EventName,Username]' \
  --output table
------------------------------------------------------ | LookupEvents | +-----------------------+----------------+-----------+ | 2026-01-17T15:00:00Z | CreateBucket | anihpj-admin | | 2026-01-17T14:55:00Z | PutBucketPolicy| anihpj-admin | | 2026-01-17T14:50:00Z | CreateFunction | anihpj-admin | +-----------------------+----------------+-----------+
# Django Models Integration - Lookup Events by Username from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Lookup events by username user = Author.objects.filter(name='anihpj-admin').first() events = JobPost.objects.filter(author=user).order_by('-id')[:10] if user else [] print("-" * 54) print("| LookupEvents |") print(f"+{'-'*23}+{'-'*16}+{'-'*11}+") for event in events: print(f"| {event.created_at if hasattr(event, 'created_at') else 'N/A'} | {event.title:<14} | {user.name if user else 'N/A'} |") print(f"+{'-'*23}+{'-'*16}+{'-'*11}+") print(f"<QuerySet count={len(events)}>") # Output: <QuerySet count=3>
Example 2: Lookup Events by Resource
#!/bin/bash
# Lookup events for anihpj S3 bucket
aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=ResourceName,AttributeValue=anihpj-media \
  --max-results 20 \
  --query 'Events[*].{Time:EventTime,Event:EventName,User:Username}'
[ { "Time": "2026-01-17T15:00:00Z", "Event": "PutObject", "User": "anihpj-app" }, { "Time": "2026-01-17T14:55:00Z", "Event": "DeleteObject", "User": "anihpj-admin" } ]
# Django Models Integration - Lookup Events by Resource from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Lookup events for anihpj S3 bucket (filter by resource name) events = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='anihpj-media')[:20] result = [] for event in events: author = event.author.name if event.author else 'unknown' result.append({ 'Time': event.created_at.isoformat() if hasattr(event, 'created_at') else 'N/A', 'Event': event.status, 'User': author }) print(result) print(f"<QuerySet [{', '.join([e['Event'] for e in result])}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [PutObject, DeleteObject]>
Example 3: Lookup Events by Time Range
#!/bin/bash
# Lookup anihpj events from last hour
START_TIME=$(date -u -d '1 hour ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
END_TIME=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

echo "anihpj Events (Last Hour)"
echo "=========================="

aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --start-time $START_TIME \
  --end-time $END_TIME \
  --lookup-attributes AttributeKey=Username,AttributeValue=anihpj-admin \
  --output json | jq -r '.Events[] | "\(.EventTime) - \(.EventName)"'
anihpj Events (Last Hour) ========================== 2026-01-17T15:00:00Z - CreateBucket 2026-01-17T14:55:00Z - PutBucketPolicy 2026-01-17T14:45:00Z - CreateFunction 2026-01-17T14:30:00Z - UpdateFunctionCode
# Django Models Integration - Lookup Events by Time Range from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta # Lookup anihpj events from last hour start_time = datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=1) end_time = datetime.now() print("anihpj Events (Last Hour)") print("==========================") # Filter by time if created_at field exists if hasattr(JobPost.objects.first(), 'created_at'): events = JobPost.objects.filter( created_at__gte=start_time, created_at__lte=end_time ).filter(author__name='anihpj-admin') else: events = JobPost.objects.filter(author__name='anihpj-admin')[:10] for event in events: print(f"{event.created_at.isoformat() if hasattr(event, 'created_at') else 'N/A'} - {event.title}") print(f"<QuerySet count={events.count()}>") # Output: <QuerySet count=4>
Example 4: Lookup Error Events
#!/bin/bash
# Find access denied events
echo "anihpj Access Denied Events"
echo "=============================="

aws cloudtrail lookup-events \
  --max-results 50 \
  --output json | jq -r '.Events[] | select(.CloudTrailEvent | fromjson | .errorCode == "AccessDenied") | select(.Username | startswith("anihpj")) | "\(.EventTime) - \(.EventName) by \(.Username)"'
anihpj Access Denied Events ============================== 2026-01-17T14:30:00Z - GetSecretValue by anihpj-dev 2026-01-17T14:25:00Z - AssumeRole by anihpj-lambda 2026-01-17T14:20:00Z - ListBuckets by anihpj-readonly
# Django Models Integration - Lookup Error Events from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Find access denied events print("anihpj Access Denied Events") print("==============================") # Filter for error status or failed events error_events = JobPost.objects.filter( status='error', author__name__startswith='anihpj' ) for event in error_events: author_name = event.author.name if event.author else 'unknown' timestamp = event.created_at.isoformat() if hasattr(event, 'created_at') else 'N/A' print(f"{timestamp} - {event.title} by {author_name}") print(f"<QuerySet errors={error_events.count()}>") # Output: <QuerySet errors=3>

create-channel

Creates a channel for CloudTrail to ingest events from a partner or external source.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail create-channel \
  --name {channel-name} \
  --source {source} \
  --destinations {destinations}
Parameters Description
  • --name - Name for the channel
  • --source - Source type (Custom for external events)
  • --destinations - JSON array specifying EVENT_DATA_STORE destinations
Return Value

Returns ChannelArn, Name, Source, and Destinations configuration.

5 Detailed CloudTrail create-channel Examples
Example 1: Create anihpj Event Channel
#!/bin/bash
# Create channel for external events
aws cloudtrail create-channel \
  --name anihpj-external-events \
  --source Custom \
  --destinations '[
    {
      "Type": "EVENT_DATA_STORE",
      "Location": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-store"
    }
  ]'

echo "anihpj event channel created"
{ "ChannelArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-external-events", "Name": "anihpj-external-events", "Source": "Custom", "Destinations": [...] } anihpj event channel created
# Django Models Integration - Create Event Channel from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create channel for external events channel = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-external-events', status='active', description='External events channel - Source: Custom' ) response = { 'ChannelArn': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/{channel.title}', 'Name': channel.title, 'Source': 'Custom', 'Destinations': [{'Type': 'EVENT_DATA_STORE'}] } print(response) print("anihpj event channel created") print(f"<JobPost {channel.title} source=Custom>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-external-events source=Custom>
Example 2: Create Channel with Tags
#!/bin/bash
# Create channel with tags
aws cloudtrail create-channel \
  --name anihpj-app-events \
  --source Custom \
  --destinations '[
    {
      "Type": "EVENT_DATA_STORE",
      "Location": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-store"
    }
  ]' \
  --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=Environment,Value=production

echo "anihpj app channel created with tags"
{ "ChannelArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-app-events", "Name": "anihpj-app-events" } anihpj app channel created with tags
# Django Models Integration - Create Channel with Tags from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create channel with tags channel = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-app-events', status='active', description='App events channel - Tags: Project=anihpj, Environment=production' ) # Simulate tags using Skills tags = Skills.objects.get_or_create(name='Project:anihpj')[0] channel.skills.add(tags) response = { 'ChannelArn': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/{channel.title}', 'Name': channel.title } print(response) print("anihpj app channel created with tags") print(f"<JobPost {channel.title} tags={channel.skills.count()}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-app-events tags=1>

get-channel

Returns information about a specific channel.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail get-channel \
  --channel {channel-arn}
Parameters Description
  • --channel - ARN of the channel
Return Value

Returns ChannelArn, Name, Source, SourceConfig, and Destinations.

5 Detailed CloudTrail get-channel Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Channel Info
#!/bin/bash
# Get channel details
aws cloudtrail get-channel \
  --channel "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-external-events"
{ "ChannelArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-external-events", "Name": "anihpj-external-events", "Source": "Custom", "SourceConfig": { "ApplyToAllRegions": true }, "Destinations": [ { "Type": "EVENT_DATA_STORE", "Location": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-store" } ] }
# Django Models Integration - Get Channel Info from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get channel details channel = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-external-events') response = { 'ChannelArn': f'arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/{channel.title}', 'Name': channel.title, 'Source': 'Custom', 'SourceConfig': {'ApplyToAllRegions': True}, 'Destinations': [{'Type': 'EVENT_DATA_STORE', 'Location': 'arn:aws:cloudtrail:...'}] } print(response) print(f"<JobPost {channel.title} source=Custom>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-external-events source=Custom>
Example 2: Get Channel Ingestion Token
#!/bin/bash
# Get ingestion token for anihpj channel
CHANNEL_ARN="arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-external-events"

TOKEN=$(aws cloudtrail get-channel \
  --channel $CHANNEL_ARN \
  --query 'IngestionStatus.LatestIngestionSuccessTime' \
  --output text)

echo "anihpj Channel Last Ingestion: $TOKEN"
anihpj Channel Last Ingestion: 2026-01-17T15:00:00Z
# Django Models Integration - Get Channel Ingestion Info from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime # Get ingestion token for anihpj channel channel = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-external-events') last_ingestion = channel.updated_at.isoformat() if hasattr(channel, 'updated_at') else datetime.now().isoformat() print(f"anihpj Channel Last Ingestion: {last_ingestion}") print(f"<JobPost {channel.title} last_ingestion={last_ingestion}>") # Output: <JobPost anihpj-external-events last_ingestion=2026-01-17T15:00:00Z>

list-channels

Lists the channels in the current account, and their source names.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail list-channels
Parameters Description
  • --next-token - (Optional) Token for pagination
Return Value

Returns Channels array with Name and ChannelArn for each channel.

5 Detailed CloudTrail list-channels Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Channels
#!/bin/bash
# List all channels
aws cloudtrail list-channels \
  --query 'Channels[*].[Name,ChannelArn]' \
  --output table
--------------------------------------------------------------- | ListChannels | +------------------------+------------------------------------+ | anihpj-external-events | arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:channel/... | | anihpj-app-events | arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:channel/... | +------------------------+------------------------------------+
# Django Models Integration - List All Channels from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all channels channels = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='events') print("-" * 63) print("| ListChannels |") print(f"+{'-'*24}+{'-'*36}+") for channel in channels: arn = f"arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:channel/{channel.id}" print(f"| {channel.title:<22} | {arn:<34} |") print(f"+{'-'*24}+{'-'*36}+") print(f"<QuerySet count={channels.count()}>") # Output: <QuerySet count=2>
Example 2: Filter anihpj Channels
#!/bin/bash
# List only anihpj channels
echo "anihpj CloudTrail Channels"
echo "==========================="

aws cloudtrail list-channels \
  --output json | jq -r '.Channels[] | select(.Name | startswith("anihpj")) | "Name: \(.Name)\nARN: \(.ChannelArn)\n---"'
anihpj CloudTrail Channels =========================== Name: anihpj-external-events ARN: arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-external-events --- Name: anihpj-app-events ARN: arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-app-events ---
# Django Models Integration - Filter Channels from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List only anihpj channels print("anihpj CloudTrail Channels") print("===========================") channels = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='anihpj').filter(title__contains='events') for channel in channels: print(f"Name: {channel.title}") print(f"ARN: arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/{channel.title}") print("---") print(f"<QuerySet [{', '.join([c.title for c in channels])}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [anihpj-external-events, anihpj-app-events]>

delete-channel

Deletes a channel.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail delete-channel \
  --channel {channel-arn}
Parameters Description
  • --channel - ARN of the channel to delete
Return Value

Returns no content on success. Channel is deleted.

5 Detailed CloudTrail delete-channel Examples
Example 1: Delete anihpj Channel
#!/bin/bash
# Delete unused channel
aws cloudtrail delete-channel \
  --channel "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-test-channel"

echo "anihpj test channel deleted"
anihpj test channel deleted
# Django Models Integration - Delete Channel from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete unused channel channel = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-test-channel') channel_name = channel.title channel.delete() print(f"anihpj test channel deleted") print(f"<JobPost deleted={channel_name}>") # Output: <JobPost deleted=anihpj-test-channel>
Example 2: Delete with Confirmation
#!/bin/bash
# Delete channel with confirmation
CHANNEL_ARN="arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-old-channel"

# Get channel name first
CHANNEL_NAME=$(aws cloudtrail get-channel \
  --channel $CHANNEL_ARN \
  --query 'Name' \
  --output text)

echo "Deleting channel: $CHANNEL_NAME"
read -p "Confirm deletion (y/n): " CONFIRM

if [ "$CONFIRM" == "y" ]; then
  aws cloudtrail delete-channel --channel $CHANNEL_ARN
  echo "anihpj channel deleted"
fi
Deleting channel: anihpj-old-channel Confirm deletion (y/n): y anihpj channel deleted
# Django Models Integration - Delete with Confirmation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete channel with confirmation channel = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-old-channel') print(f"Deleting channel: {channel.title}") confirm = input("Confirm deletion (y/n): ") if confirm.lower() == 'y': channel_name = channel.title channel.delete() print("anihpj channel deleted") print(f"<JobPost deleted={channel_name} confirmed=True>") else: print("Deletion cancelled") # Output: <JobPost deleted=anihpj-old-channel confirmed=True>

update-channel

Updates a channel specified by a required channel ARN or UUID.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail update-channel \
  --channel {channel-arn} \
  [--destinations {destinations}]
Parameters Description
  • --channel - ARN of the channel to update
  • --destinations - (Optional) Updated destinations
Return Value

Returns updated ChannelArn and Destinations configuration.

5 Detailed CloudTrail update-channel Examples
Example 1: Update anihpj Channel Destination
#!/bin/bash
# Update channel to use new event data store
aws cloudtrail update-channel \
  --channel "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-external-events" \
  --destinations '[
    {
      "Type": "EVENT_DATA_STORE",
      "Location": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-new-store"
    }
  ]'

echo "anihpj channel destination updated"
{ "ChannelArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-external-events", "Destinations": [ { "Type": "EVENT_DATA_STORE", "Location": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-new-store" } ] } anihpj channel destination updated
# Django Models Integration - Update Channel Destination from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Update channel to use new event data store channel = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-external-events') new_store = Location.objects.get(city='anihpj-new-store') channel.location = new_store channel.save() print(f"ChannelArn: arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:channel/{channel.title}") print(f"Destination: {channel.location.city}") print("anihpj channel destination updated") print(f"<JobPost title={channel.title} location={channel.location.city}>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-external-events location=anihpj-new-store>

start-import

Starts an import of logged trail events from a source S3 bucket to a destination event data store.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail start-import \
  --destinations {destinations} \
  --import-source {import-source}
Parameters Description
  • --destinations - ARN of event data store destinations
  • --import-source - S3 source configuration for import
Return Value

Returns ImportId, ImportSource, Destinations, ImportStatus, and CreatedTimestamp.

5 Detailed CloudTrail start-import Examples
Example 1: Import anihpj Historical Events
#!/bin/bash
# Import historical events to CloudTrail Lake
aws cloudtrail start-import \
  --destinations '["arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-store"]' \
  --import-source '{
    "S3": {
      "S3LocationUri": "s3://anihpj-cloudtrail-logs/AWSLogs/123456789012/CloudTrail/us-east-1/",
      "S3BucketRegion": "us-east-1",
      "S3BucketAccessRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-cloudtrail-import-role"
    }
  }'

echo "anihpj historical event import started"
{ "ImportId": "import-abc123-def456", "ImportSource": {...}, "Destinations": [...], "ImportStatus": "INITIALIZING", "CreatedTimestamp": "2026-01-17T15:00:00Z" } anihpj historical event import started
# Django Models Integration - Start Import from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone import uuid # Create import record for historical events import_job = JobPost.objects.create( title=f'import-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}', description='Historical event import from S3', is_active=True, created=timezone.now() ) print(f"ImportId: {import_job.title}") print(f"ImportStatus: INITIALIZING") print(f"CreatedTimestamp: {import_job.created}") print("anihpj historical event import started") print(f"<JobPost id={import_job.id} title={import_job.title} status=INITIALIZING>") # Output: <JobPost id=1 title=import-abc123def456 status=INITIALIZING>
Example 2: Import with Date Range
#!/bin/bash
# Import events from specific date range
aws cloudtrail start-import \
  --destinations '["arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-store"]' \
  --import-source '{
    "S3": {
      "S3LocationUri": "s3://anihpj-cloudtrail-logs/AWSLogs/",
      "S3BucketRegion": "us-east-1",
      "S3BucketAccessRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-import-role"
    }
  }' \
  --start-event-time "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z" \
  --end-event-time "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z"

echo "anihpj 2025 events import started"
{ "ImportId": "import-2025-xyz789", "ImportStatus": "INITIALIZING", "StartEventTime": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", "EndEventTime": "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z" } anihpj 2025 events import started
# Django Models Integration - Import with Date Range from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone from datetime import datetime # Create import record with date range import_job = JobPost.objects.create( title='import-2025-xyz789', description='2025 events import', is_active=True, created=timezone.now() ) start_date = datetime(2025, 1, 1) end_date = datetime(2025, 12, 31, 23, 59, 59) print(f"ImportId: {import_job.title}") print(f"ImportStatus: INITIALIZING") print(f"StartEventTime: {start_date.isoformat()}Z") print(f"EndEventTime: {end_date.isoformat()}Z") print("anihpj 2025 events import started") print(f"<JobPost title={import_job.title} range={start_date.year}>") # Output: <JobPost title=import-2025-xyz789 range=2025>

get-import

Returns information about a specific import.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail get-import \
  --import-id {import-id}
Parameters Description
  • --import-id - ID of the import to retrieve
Return Value

Returns ImportId, ImportSource, ImportStatus, and ImportStatistics.

5 Detailed CloudTrail get-import Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Import Status
#!/bin/bash
# Get import details
aws cloudtrail get-import \
  --import-id "import-abc123-def456"
{ "ImportId": "import-abc123-def456", "ImportSource": { "S3": { "S3LocationUri": "s3://anihpj-cloudtrail-logs/AWSLogs/", "S3BucketRegion": "us-east-1" } }, "ImportStatus": "IN_PROGRESS", "ImportStatistics": { "PrefixesFound": 365, "PrefixesCompleted": 180, "FilesCompleted": 5400, "EventsCompleted": 150000 } }
# Django Models Integration - Get Import Status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get import details import_job = JobPost.objects.get(title='import-abc123-def456') print(f"ImportId: {import_job.title}") print(f"ImportSource: s3://anihpj-cloudtrail-logs/AWSLogs/") print(f"ImportStatus: {'IN_PROGRESS' if import_job.is_active else 'COMPLETED'}") # Simulate import statistics stats = { 'PrefixesFound': 365, 'PrefixesCompleted': 180, 'FilesCompleted': 5400, 'EventsCompleted': 150000 } print(f"ImportStatistics: {stats}") print(f"<JobPost id={import_job.id} title={import_job.title} active={import_job.is_active}>") # Output: <JobPost id=1 title=import-abc123-def456 active=True>
Example 2: Monitor Import Progress
#!/bin/bash
# Monitor import progress
IMPORT_ID="import-abc123-def456"

echo "anihpj Import Progress"
echo "========================"

aws cloudtrail get-import \
  --import-id $IMPORT_ID \
  --query '{
    Status: ImportStatus,
    PrefixesFound: ImportStatistics.PrefixesFound,
    PrefixesCompleted: ImportStatistics.PrefixesCompleted,
    EventsImported: ImportStatistics.EventsCompleted
  }' \
  --output table
anihpj Import Progress ======================== --------------------------------------------- | GetImport | +-------------------+-----------------------+ | EventsImported | 150000 | | PrefixesCompleted | 180 | | PrefixesFound | 365 | | Status | IN_PROGRESS | +-------------------+-----------------------+
# Django Models Integration - Monitor Import Progress from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Monitor import progress import_job = JobPost.objects.get(title='import-abc123-def456') print("anihpj Import Progress") print("========================") # Table format print("---------------------------------------------") print("| GetImport |") print("+-------------------+-----------------------+") print(f"| EventsImported | 150000 |") print(f"| PrefixesCompleted | 180 |") print(f"| PrefixesFound | 365 |") print(f"| Status | {'IN_PROGRESS' if import_job.is_active else 'COMPLETED':<22}|") print("+-------------------+-----------------------+") print(f"<JobPost title={import_job.title} progress=49%>") # Output: <JobPost title=import-abc123-def456 progress=49%>

list-imports

Returns information on all imports, or a select set of imports by ImportStatus or Destination.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail list-imports \
  [--destination {destination-arn}]
Parameters Description
