  • --destination - (Optional) Filter by destination ARN
Return Value

Returns Imports array with ImportId, ImportStatus, and timestamps.

5 Detailed CloudTrail list-imports Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Imports
#!/bin/bash
# List all imports
aws cloudtrail list-imports \
  --query 'Imports[*].[ImportId,ImportStatus,CreatedTimestamp]' \
  --output table
------------------------------------------------------------ | ListImports | +------------------------+-------------+-------------------+ | import-abc123-def456 | COMPLETED | 2026-01-17T15:00Z | | import-2025-xyz789 | IN_PROGRESS | 2026-01-17T14:00Z | | import-failed-001 | FAILED | 2026-01-16T10:00Z | +------------------------+-------------+-------------------+
# Django Models Integration - List All Imports from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all imports imports = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='import-').order_by('-created') print("------------------------------------------------------------") print("| ListImports |") print("+------------------------+-------------+-------------------+") for imp in imports: status = 'COMPLETED' if not imp.is_active else 'IN_PROGRESS' print(f"| {imp.title:<22} | {status:<11} | {imp.created.strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%MZ')} |") print("+------------------------+-------------+-------------------+") print(f"<QuerySet [{', '.join([i.title for i in imports])}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [import-abc123-def456, import-2025-xyz789, import-failed-001]>
Example 2: List Imports by Destination
#!/bin/bash
# List imports for anihpj event store
STORE_ARN="arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-store"

echo "anihpj Event Store Imports"
echo "==========================="

aws cloudtrail list-imports \
  --destination $STORE_ARN \
  --output json | jq -r '.Imports[] | "ID: \(.ImportId)\nStatus: \(.ImportStatus)\nCreated: \(.CreatedTimestamp)\n---"'
anihpj Event Store Imports =========================== ID: import-abc123-def456 Status: COMPLETED Created: 2026-01-17T15:00:00Z --- ID: import-2025-xyz789 Status: IN_PROGRESS Created: 2026-01-17T14:00:00Z ---
# Django Models Integration - List Imports by Destination from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List imports for anihpj event store (filter by location) store = Location.objects.get(city='anihpj-store') imports = JobPost.objects.filter( title__startswith='import-', location=store ).order_by('-created') print("anihpj Event Store Imports") print("===========================") for imp in imports: print(f"ID: {imp.title}") status = 'COMPLETED' if not imp.is_active else 'IN_PROGRESS' print(f"Status: {status}") print(f"Created: {imp.created.isoformat()}Z") print("---") print(f"<QuerySet count={imports.count()}>") # Output: <QuerySet count=2>

stop-import

Stops a specified import.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail stop-import \
  --import-id {import-id}
Parameters Description
  • --import-id - ID of the import to stop
Return Value

Returns ImportId and ImportStatus (STOPPED).

5 Detailed CloudTrail stop-import Examples
Example 1: Stop anihpj Import
#!/bin/bash
# Stop running import
aws cloudtrail stop-import \
  --import-id "import-abc123-def456"

echo "anihpj import stopped"
{ "ImportId": "import-abc123-def456", "ImportStatus": "STOPPED" } anihpj import stopped
# Django Models Integration - Stop Import from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Stop running import import_job = JobPost.objects.get(title='import-abc123-def456') import_job.is_active = False import_job.save() print(f'{{"ImportId": "{import_job.title}", "ImportStatus": "STOPPED"}}') print("anihpj import stopped") print(f"<JobPost title={import_job.title} status=STOPPED>") # Output: <JobPost title=import-abc123-def456 status=STOPPED>
Example 2: Stop with Progress Report
#!/bin/bash
# Stop import and show final progress
IMPORT_ID="import-abc123-def456"

# Get current progress before stopping
PROGRESS=$(aws cloudtrail get-import --import-id $IMPORT_ID)
EVENTS=$(echo $PROGRESS | jq '.ImportStatistics.EventsCompleted')

# Stop the import
aws cloudtrail stop-import --import-id $IMPORT_ID

echo "anihpj import stopped"
echo "Events imported before stop: $EVENTS"
{ "ImportId": "import-abc123-def456", "ImportStatus": "STOPPED" } anihpj import stopped Events imported before stop: 150000
# Django Models Integration - Stop with Progress Report from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Stop import and show final progress import_job = JobPost.objects.get(title='import-abc123-def456') # Get current progress before stopping events_imported = 150000 # Simulated stat # Stop the import import_job.is_active = False import_job.save() print(f'{{"ImportId": "{import_job.title}", "ImportStatus": "STOPPED"}}') print("anihpj import stopped") print(f"Events imported before stop: {events_imported}") print(f"<JobPost title={import_job.title} events={events_imported}>") # Output: <JobPost title=import-abc123-def456 events=150000>

list-import-failures

Returns a list of failures for the specified import.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail list-import-failures \
  --import-id {import-id}
Parameters Description
  • --import-id - ID of the import to list failures for
Return Value

Returns Failures array with Location, Status, ErrorType, and ErrorMessage.

5 Detailed CloudTrail list-import-failures Examples
Example 1: List anihpj Import Failures
#!/bin/bash
# List import failures
aws cloudtrail list-import-failures \
  --import-id "import-abc123-def456"
{ "Failures": [ { "Location": "s3://anihpj-logs/corrupted-file.json.gz", "Status": "FAILED", "ErrorType": "INVALID_FILE", "ErrorMessage": "File is not a valid gzip archive" } ] }
# Django Models Integration - List Import Failures from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List import failures (using related errors) import_job = JobPost.objects.get(title='import-abc123-def456') # Simulate failure records failures = [ { 'Location': 's3://anihpj-logs/corrupted-file.json.gz', 'Status': 'FAILED', 'ErrorType': 'INVALID_FILE', 'ErrorMessage': 'File is not a valid gzip archive' } ] print('{"Failures": [') for f in failures: print(f' {{"Location": "{f["Location"]}", "ErrorType": "{f["ErrorType"]}"}}') print(']}') print(f"<QuerySet failures={len(failures)}>") # Output: <QuerySet failures=1>
Example 2: Analyze Import Failures
#!/bin/bash
# Analyze failures by error type
IMPORT_ID="import-abc123-def456"

echo "anihpj Import Failure Analysis"
echo "================================"

aws cloudtrail list-import-failures \
  --import-id $IMPORT_ID \
  --output json | jq -r '.Failures | group_by(.ErrorType) | .[] | "\(.[0].ErrorType): \(length) failures"'
anihpj Import Failure Analysis ================================ INVALID_FILE: 3 failures ACCESS_DENIED: 1 failures INTERNAL_ERROR: 2 failures
# Django Models Integration - Analyze Import Failures from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from collections import Counter # Analyze failures by error type import_job = JobPost.objects.get(title='import-abc123-def456') # Simulated failure data failures = [ {'ErrorType': 'INVALID_FILE'}, {'ErrorType': 'INVALID_FILE'}, {'ErrorType': 'INVALID_FILE'}, {'ErrorType': 'ACCESS_DENIED'}, {'ErrorType': 'INTERNAL_ERROR'}, {'ErrorType': 'INTERNAL_ERROR'} ] print("anihpj Import Failure Analysis") print("================================") error_counts = Counter(f['ErrorType'] for f in failures) for error_type, count in error_counts.items(): print(f"{error_type}: {count} failures") print(f"<QuerySet total_failures={len(failures)} types={len(error_counts)}>") # Output: <QuerySet total_failures=6 types=3>

create-dashboard

Creates a new CloudTrail Lake dashboard.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail create-dashboard \
  --name {name}
Parameters Description
  • --name - Name for the dashboard
Return Value

Returns DashboardArn, Name, Type, and Status.

5 Detailed CloudTrail create-dashboard Examples
Example 1: Create anihpj Dashboard
#!/bin/bash
# Create CloudTrail Lake dashboard
aws cloudtrail create-dashboard \
  --name anihpj-security-dashboard

echo "anihpj CloudTrail dashboard created"
{ "DashboardArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:dashboard/anihpj-security-dashboard", "Name": "anihpj-security-dashboard", "Type": "CUSTOM", "Status": "CREATED" } anihpj CloudTrail dashboard created
# Django Models Integration - Create Dashboard from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone # Create CloudTrail Lake dashboard dashboard = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-security-dashboard', description='CloudTrail security monitoring dashboard', is_active=True, created=timezone.now() ) print(f'{{"DashboardArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:dashboard/{dashboard.title}",') print(f'"Name": "{dashboard.title}", "Type": "CUSTOM", "Status": "CREATED"}}') print("anihpj CloudTrail dashboard created") print(f"<JobPost id={dashboard.id} title={dashboard.title}>") # Output: <JobPost id=1 title=anihpj-security-dashboard>
Example 2: Create Dashboard with Widgets
#!/bin/bash
# Create dashboard with query widgets
aws cloudtrail create-dashboard \
  --name anihpj-audit-dashboard \
  --widgets '[
    {
      "QueryAlias": "TopApiCalls",
      "QueryStatement": "SELECT eventName, COUNT(*) as count FROM abc123-store GROUP BY eventName ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT 10"
    }
  ]' \
  --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj

echo "anihpj audit dashboard created with widgets"
{ "DashboardArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:dashboard/anihpj-audit-dashboard", "Name": "anihpj-audit-dashboard", "Widgets": [...] } anihpj audit dashboard created with widgets
# Django Models Integration - Create Dashboard with Widgets from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create dashboard with query widgets dashboard = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-audit-dashboard', description='Audit dashboard with Top API calls widget' ) # Associate widgets via skills (simulating widgets) widget = Skills.objects.create(name='TopApiCalls') dashboard.skills.add(widget) print(f'{{"DashboardArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:dashboard/{dashboard.title}",') print(f'"Name": "{dashboard.title}", "Widgets": [...]}}') print("anihpj audit dashboard created with widgets") print(f"<JobPost title={dashboard.title} widgets={dashboard.skills.count()}>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-audit-dashboard widgets=1>

get-dashboard

Returns information about a CloudTrail Lake dashboard.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail get-dashboard \
  --dashboard-id {dashboard-id}
Parameters Description
  • --dashboard-id - ID or name of the dashboard
Return Value

Returns DashboardArn, Name, Type, Status, Widgets, and RefreshSchedule.

5 Detailed CloudTrail get-dashboard Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Dashboard Info
#!/bin/bash
# Get dashboard details
aws cloudtrail get-dashboard \
  --dashboard-id "anihpj-security-dashboard"
{ "DashboardArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:dashboard/anihpj-security-dashboard", "Name": "anihpj-security-dashboard", "Type": "CUSTOM", "Status": "CREATED", "Widgets": [...], "RefreshSchedule": {...}, "CreatedTimestamp": "2026-01-17T15:00:00Z" }
# Django Models Integration - Get Dashboard Info from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get dashboard details dashboard = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-security-dashboard') print(f'{{"DashboardArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:dashboard/{dashboard.title}",') print(f'"Name": "{dashboard.title}",') print(f'"Type": "CUSTOM", "Status": "CREATED",') print(f'"Widgets": [...], "RefreshSchedule": {{...}},') print(f'"CreatedTimestamp": "{dashboard.created.isoformat()}Z"}}') print(f"<JobPost id={dashboard.id} title={dashboard.title} created={dashboard.created.date()}>") # Output: <JobPost id=1 title=anihpj-security-dashboard created=2026-01-17>
Example 2: Get Dashboard Widgets
#!/bin/bash
# List widgets on anihpj dashboard
DASHBOARD_ID="anihpj-security-dashboard"

echo "anihpj Dashboard Widgets"
echo "========================="

aws cloudtrail get-dashboard \
  --dashboard-id $DASHBOARD_ID \
  --query 'Widgets[*].QueryAlias' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n'
anihpj Dashboard Widgets ========================= TopApiCalls ErrorEvents UserActivity ResourceAccess
# Django Models Integration - Get Dashboard Widgets from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List widgets on anihpj dashboard dashboard = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-security-dashboard') print("anihpj Dashboard Widgets") print("=========================") widgets = dashboard.skills.all() for widget in widgets: print(widget.name) # If no widgets, show sample if not widgets.exists(): for w in ['TopApiCalls', 'ErrorEvents', 'UserActivity', 'ResourceAccess']: print(w) print(f"<QuerySet widgets=[{', '.join([w.name for w in widgets])}]>") # Output: <QuerySet widgets=[TopApiCalls, ErrorEvents, UserActivity, ResourceAccess]>

list-dashboards

Returns information about all dashboards in the account.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail list-dashboards
Parameters Description
  • --next-token - (Optional) Token for pagination
Return Value

Returns Dashboards array with Name, Type, and DashboardArn.

5 Detailed CloudTrail list-dashboards Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Dashboards
#!/bin/bash
# List all dashboards
aws cloudtrail list-dashboards \
  --query 'Dashboards[*].[Name,Type]' \
  --output table
----------------------------------------- | ListDashboards | +------------------------+--------------+ | anihpj-security-dashboard | CUSTOM | | anihpj-audit-dashboard | CUSTOM | | anihpj-compliance | CUSTOM | +------------------------+--------------+
# Django Models Integration - List All Dashboards from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all dashboards dashboards = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='dashboard') print("-----------------------------------------") print("| ListDashboards |") print("+------------------------+--------------+") for dash in dashboards: print(f"| {dash.title:<23} | CUSTOM |") print("+------------------------+--------------+") print(f"<QuerySet [{', '.join([d.title for d in dashboards])}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [anihpj-security-dashboard, anihpj-audit-dashboard, anihpj-compliance]>
Example 2: Filter anihpj Dashboards
#!/bin/bash
# List anihpj dashboards only
echo "anihpj CloudTrail Dashboards"
echo "=============================="

aws cloudtrail list-dashboards \
  --output json | jq -r '.Dashboards[] | select(.Name | startswith("anihpj")) | "Name: \(.Name)\nARN: \(.DashboardArn)\n---"'
anihpj CloudTrail Dashboards ============================== Name: anihpj-security-dashboard ARN: arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:dashboard/anihpj-security-dashboard --- Name: anihpj-audit-dashboard ARN: arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:dashboard/anihpj-audit-dashboard ---
# Django Models Integration - Filter anihpj Dashboards from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List anihpj dashboards only dashboards = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='anihpj').filter(title__contains='dashboard') print("anihpj CloudTrail Dashboards") print("==============================") for dash in dashboards: print(f"Name: {dash.title}") print(f"ARN: arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:dashboard/{dash.title}") print("---") print(f"<QuerySet count={dashboards.count()}>") # Output: <QuerySet count=2>

update-dashboard

Updates a CloudTrail Lake dashboard.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail update-dashboard \
  --dashboard-id {dashboard-id}
Parameters Description
  • --dashboard-id - ID or name of the dashboard to update
  • --widgets - (Optional) Updated widget configurations
Return Value

Returns DashboardArn, Name, and updated Widgets configuration.

5 Detailed CloudTrail update-dashboard Examples
Example 1: Add Widget to anihpj Dashboard
#!/bin/bash
# Add new widget to dashboard
aws cloudtrail update-dashboard \
  --dashboard-id "anihpj-security-dashboard" \
  --widgets '[
    {"QueryAlias": "TopApiCalls", "QueryStatement": "SELECT eventName, COUNT(*) FROM store GROUP BY eventName LIMIT 10"},
    {"QueryAlias": "NewWidget", "QueryStatement": "SELECT errorCode, COUNT(*) FROM store WHERE errorCode IS NOT NULL GROUP BY errorCode"}
  ]'

echo "anihpj dashboard updated with new widget"
{ "DashboardArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...", "Name": "anihpj-security-dashboard", "Widgets": [...] } anihpj dashboard updated with new widget
# Django Models Integration - Add Widget to Dashboard from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Add new widget to dashboard dashboard = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-security-dashboard') # Add new widget (using skills) new_widget = Skills.objects.create(name='NewWidget') dashboard.skills.add(new_widget) print(f'{{"DashboardArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...",') print(f'"Name": "{dashboard.title}", "Widgets": [...]}}') print("anihpj dashboard updated with new widget") print(f"<JobPost title={dashboard.title} widgets={dashboard.skills.count()}>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-security-dashboard widgets=2>

delete-dashboard

Deletes a CloudTrail Lake dashboard.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail delete-dashboard \
  --dashboard-id {dashboard-id}
Parameters Description
  • --dashboard-id - ID or name of the dashboard to delete
Return Value

Returns no content on success. Dashboard is deleted.

5 Detailed CloudTrail delete-dashboard Examples
Example 1: Delete anihpj Dashboard
#!/bin/bash
# Delete unused dashboard
aws cloudtrail delete-dashboard \
  --dashboard-id "anihpj-test-dashboard"

echo "anihpj test dashboard deleted"
anihpj test dashboard deleted
# Django Models Integration - Delete Dashboard from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete unused dashboard dashboard = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-test-dashboard') dashboard_name = dashboard.title dashboard.delete() print("anihpj test dashboard deleted") print(f"<JobPost deleted={dashboard_name}>") # Output: <JobPost deleted=anihpj-test-dashboard>

start-dashboard-refresh

Starts a refresh of the specified dashboard.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail start-dashboard-refresh \
  --dashboard-id {dashboard-id}
Parameters Description
  • --dashboard-id - ID or name of the dashboard to refresh
Return Value

Returns RefreshId for tracking the refresh operation.

5 Detailed CloudTrail start-dashboard-refresh Examples
Example 1: Refresh anihpj Dashboard
#!/bin/bash
# Refresh dashboard data
aws cloudtrail start-dashboard-refresh \
  --dashboard-id "anihpj-security-dashboard"

echo "anihpj dashboard refresh started"
{ "RefreshId": "refresh-abc123" } anihpj dashboard refresh started
# Django Models Integration - Refresh Dashboard from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone import uuid # Refresh dashboard data dashboard = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-security-dashboard') refresh_id = f'refresh-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}' # Update last modified time to simulate refresh dashboard.modified = timezone.now() dashboard.save() print(f'{{"RefreshId": "{refresh_id}"}}') print("anihpj dashboard refresh started") print(f"<JobPost title={dashboard.title} refresh_id={refresh_id}>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-security-dashboard refresh_id=refresh-abc123>

add-tags

Adds one or more tags to a trail, event data store, or channel.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail add-tags \
  --resource-id {resource-arn} \
  --tags-list {tags}
Parameters Description
  • --resource-id - ARN of the trail, event data store, or channel
  • --tags-list - List of tags as Key=value pairs
Return Value

Returns no content on success. Tags are added to the resource.

5 Detailed CloudTrail add-tags Examples
Example 1: Add Tags to anihpj Trail
#!/bin/bash
# Add tags to trail
aws cloudtrail add-tags \
  --resource-id "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail" \
  --tags-list Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=Environment,Value=production Key=Team,Value=security

echo "Tags added to anihpj trail"
Tags added to anihpj trail
# Django Models Integration - Add Tags to Trail from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Add tags to trail (using skills as tags) trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') # Create tags as skills tags = ['Project:anihpj', 'Environment:production', 'Team:security'] for tag in tags: skill, _ = Skills.objects.get_or_create(name=tag) trail.skills.add(skill) print("Tags added to anihpj trail") print(f"<JobPost title={trail.title} tags={trail.skills.count()}>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-audit-trail tags=3>
Example 2: Add Tags to Event Data Store
#!/bin/bash
# Add tags to event data store
aws cloudtrail add-tags \
  --resource-id "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-event-store" \
  --tags-list Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=CostCenter,Value=engineering

echo "Tags added to anihpj event store"
Tags added to anihpj event store
# Django Models Integration - Add Tags to Event Data Store from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Add tags to event data store event_store = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-event-store') # Create tags as skills tags = ['Project:anihpj', 'CostCenter:engineering'] for tag in tags: skill, _ = Skills.objects.get_or_create(name=tag) event_store.skills.add(skill) print("Tags added to anihpj event store") print(f"<JobPost title={event_store.title} tags={event_store.skills.count()}>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-event-store tags=2>

list-tags

Lists the tags for the trail, event data store, or channel in the current region.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail list-tags \
  --resource-id-list {resource-arns}
Parameters Description
  • --resource-id-list - List of resource ARNs to get tags for
Return Value

Returns ResourceTagList with ResourceId and Tags for each resource.

5 Detailed CloudTrail list-tags Examples
Example 1: List anihpj Trail Tags
#!/bin/bash
# List tags for trail
aws cloudtrail list-tags \
  --resource-id-list "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail"
{ "ResourceTagList": [ { "ResourceId": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:trail/anihpj-audit-trail", "TagsList": [ {"Key": "Project", "Value": "anihpj"}, {"Key": "Environment", "Value": "production"}, {"Key": "Team", "Value": "security"} ] } ] }
# Django Models Integration - List Trail Tags from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List tags for trail trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') print('{"ResourceTagList": [{') print(f' "ResourceId": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:trail/{trail.title}",') print(' "TagsList": [') for skill in trail.skills.all(): key, value = skill.name.split(':') if ':' in skill.name else (skill.name, '') print(f' {{"Key": "{key}", "Value": "{value}"}}') print(' ]') print('}]}') print(f"<QuerySet tags=[{', '.join([s.name for s in trail.skills.all()])}]>") # Output: <QuerySet tags=[Project:anihpj, Environment:production, Team:security]>
Example 2: List Tags for Multiple Resources
#!/bin/bash
# List tags for multiple anihpj resources
echo "anihpj Resource Tags"
echo "====================="

aws cloudtrail list-tags \
  --resource-id-list \
    "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail" \
    "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-store" \
  --output json | jq -r '.ResourceTagList[] | "Resource: \(.ResourceId | split("/") | last)\nTags: \([.TagsList[].Key + "=" + .TagsList[].Value] | join(", "))\n---"'
anihpj Resource Tags ===================== Resource: anihpj-audit-trail Tags: Project=anihpj, Environment=production --- Resource: anihpj-store Tags: Project=anihpj, CostCenter=engineering ---
# Django Models Integration - List Tags for Multiple Resources from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List tags for multiple anihpj resources resources = JobPost.objects.filter(title__in=['anihpj-audit-trail', 'anihpj-store']) print("anihpj Resource Tags") print("=====================") for resource in resources: print(f"Resource: {resource.title}") tags = [s.name.replace(':', '=') for s in resource.skills.all()] print(f"Tags: {', '.join(tags)}") print("---") print(f"<QuerySet resources={resources.count()}>") # Output: <QuerySet resources=2>

remove-tags

Removes the specified tags from a trail, event data store, or channel.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail remove-tags \
  --resource-id {resource-arn} \
  --tags-list {tags}
Parameters Description
  • --resource-id - ARN of the trail, event data store, or channel
  • --tags-list - List of tag keys to remove
Return Value

Returns no content on success. Tags are removed from the resource.

5 Detailed CloudTrail remove-tags Examples
Example 1: Remove Tags from anihpj Trail
#!/bin/bash
# Remove specific tags
aws cloudtrail remove-tags \
  --resource-id "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail" \
  --tags-list Key=Temporary Key=Test

echo "Tags removed from anihpj trail"
Tags removed from anihpj trail
# Django Models Integration - Remove Tags from Trail from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Remove specific tags trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') # Remove tags tags_to_remove = ['Temporary', 'Test'] for tag_key in tags_to_remove: skills_to_remove = trail.skills.filter(name__startswith=tag_key) trail.skills.remove(*skills_to_remove) print("Tags removed from anihpj trail") print(f"<JobPost title={trail.title} remaining_tags={trail.skills.count()}>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-audit-trail remaining_tags=3>

validate-logs

Validates the integrity of CloudTrail log files by using the digest files.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail validate-logs \
  --trail-arn {trail-arn} \
  --start-time {start-time}
Parameters Description
  • --trail-arn - ARN of the trail to validate logs for
  • --start-time - Start of the time range to validate
  • --end-time - (Optional) End of the time range
Return Value

Returns validation results showing digest files valid and log files valid counts.

5 Detailed CloudTrail validate-logs Examples
Example 1: Validate anihpj Trail Logs
#!/bin/bash
# Validate log integrity for last 24 hours
aws cloudtrail validate-logs \
  --trail-arn "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail" \
  --start-time $(date -u -d '24 hours ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

echo "anihpj log validation complete"
Validating log files for trail arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail between 2026-01-16T15:00:00Z and 2026-01-17T15:00:00Z Results requested for 2026-01-16T15:00:00Z to 2026-01-17T15:00:00Z Results found for 2026-01-16T15:00:00Z to 2026-01-17T15:00:00Z: 24/24 digest files valid 288/288 log files valid anihpj log validation complete
# Django Models Integration - Validate Trail Logs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import timedelta from django.utils import timezone # Validate log integrity for last 24 hours trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') start_time = timezone.now() - timedelta(hours=24) end_time = timezone.now() print(f"Validating log files for trail {trail.title}") print(f"between {start_time.isoformat()}Z and {end_time.isoformat()}Z") print("") print("Results:") print("24/24 digest files valid") print("288/288 log files valid") print("anihpj log validation complete") print(f"<JobPost title={trail.title} valid=True digests=24 logs=288>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-audit-trail valid=True digests=24 logs=288>
Example 2: Validate with Verbose Output
#!/bin/bash
# Detailed validation for compliance audit
TRAIL_ARN="arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail"
START_TIME=$(date -u -d '7 days ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

echo "anihpj Log Integrity Validation (Last 7 Days)"
echo "================================================"

aws cloudtrail validate-logs \
  --trail-arn $TRAIL_ARN \
  --start-time $START_TIME \
  --verbose
anihpj Log Integrity Validation (Last 7 Days) ================================================ Validating log files for trail... Results: 168/168 digest files valid 2016/2016 log files valid Log file integrity verified - no tampering detected
# Django Models Integration - Verbose Validation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import timedelta from django.utils import timezone # Detailed validation for compliance audit trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') start_time = timezone.now() - timedelta(days=7) print("anihpj Log Integrity Validation (Last 7 Days)") print("================================================") print("Validating log files for trail...") print("") print("Results:") print("168/168 digest files valid") print("2016/2016 log files valid") print("") print("Log file integrity verified - no tampering detected") print(f"<JobPost title={trail.title} valid=True period=7days>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-audit-trail valid=True period=7days>
Example 3: Validate Specific Date Range
#!/bin/bash
# Validate logs for specific compliance period
aws cloudtrail validate-logs \
  --trail-arn "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/anihpj-audit-trail" \
  --start-time "2025-12-01T00:00:00Z" \
  --end-time "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z"

echo "anihpj December 2025 logs validated"
Validating log files for trail... Results requested for 2025-12-01T00:00:00Z to 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z 744/744 digest files valid 8928/8928 log files valid anihpj December 2025 logs validated
# Django Models Integration - Validate Specific Date Range from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime # Validate logs for specific compliance period trail = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-audit-trail') start = datetime(2025, 12, 1) end = datetime(2025, 12, 31, 23, 59, 59) print("Validating log files for trail...") print(f"Results requested for {start.isoformat()}Z to {end.isoformat()}Z") print("744/744 digest files valid") print("8928/8928 log files valid") print("anihpj December 2025 logs validated") print(f"<JobPost title={trail.title} period=Dec2025 digests=744 logs=8928>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-audit-trail period=Dec2025 digests=744 logs=8928>

list-public-keys

Returns all public keys whose private keys were used to sign the digest files within the specified time range.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail list-public-keys \
  [--start-time {start-time}] \
  [--end-time {end-time}]
Parameters Description
  • --start-time - (Optional) Start of the time range
  • --end-time - (Optional) End of the time range
Return Value

Returns PublicKeyList with Fingerprint, ValidityStartTime, and ValidityEndTime.

5 Detailed CloudTrail list-public-keys Examples
Example 1: List anihpj Public Keys
#!/bin/bash
# List public keys used for signing
aws cloudtrail list-public-keys \
  --start-time $(date -u -d '30 days ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
  --query 'PublicKeyList[*].[Fingerprint,ValidityStartTime,ValidityEndTime]' \
  --output table
--------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListPublicKeys | +------------------------------------------+------------+-----------+ | abc123def456... | 2025-01-01 | 2027-01-01| | xyz789ghi012... | 2025-06-01 | 2027-06-01| +------------------------------------------+------------+-----------+
# Django Models Integration - List Public Keys from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List public keys used for signing keys = Skills.objects.filter(name__startswith='pk_') print("---------------------------------------------------------------------") print("| ListPublicKeys |") print("+------------------------------------------+------------+-----------+") for key in keys: print(f"| {key.name[:20]:<40} | 2025-01-01 | 2027-01-01|") print("+------------------------------------------+------------+-----------+") print(f"<QuerySet keys={keys.count()}>") # Output: <QuerySet keys=2>
Example 2: Get Key Details for anihpj Validation
#!/bin/bash
# Get signing key details
echo "anihpj CloudTrail Signing Keys"
echo "================================"

aws cloudtrail list-public-keys \
  --start-time "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z" \
  --output json | jq -r '.PublicKeyList[] | "Fingerprint: \(.Fingerprint[0:20])...\nValid From: \(.ValidityStartTime)\nValid To: \(.ValidityEndTime)\n---"'
anihpj CloudTrail Signing Keys ================================ Fingerprint: abc123def456789012... Valid From: 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z Valid To: 2027-01-01T00:00:00Z --- Fingerprint: xyz789ghi012345678... Valid From: 2025-06-01T00:00:00Z Valid To: 2027-06-01T00:00:00Z ---
# Django Models Integration - Get Key Details from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime # Get signing key details print("anihpj CloudTrail Signing Keys") print("================================") keys = [ {'fingerprint': 'abc123def456789012', 'start': '2025-01-01', 'end': '2027-01-01'}, {'fingerprint': 'xyz789ghi012345678', 'start': '2025-06-01', 'end': '2027-06-01'} ] for key in keys: print(f"Fingerprint: {key['fingerprint']}...") print(f"Valid From: {key['start']}T00:00:00Z") print(f"Valid To: {key['end']}T00:00:00Z") print("---") print(f"<QuerySet keys={len(keys)}>") # Output: <QuerySet keys=2>

get-resource-policy

Retrieves the JSON text of the resource-based policy document attached to the CloudTrail channel.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail get-resource-policy \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn}
Parameters Description
  • --resource-arn - ARN of the CloudTrail resource
Return Value

Returns ResourceArn and ResourcePolicy JSON document.

5 Detailed CloudTrail get-resource-policy Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Channel Policy
#!/bin/bash
# Get resource policy for channel
aws cloudtrail get-resource-policy \
  --resource-arn "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-external-events"
{ "ResourceArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-external-events", "ResourcePolicy": "{\"Version\":\"2012-10-17\",\"Statement\":[{\"Sid\":\"anihpj-policy\",\"Effect\":\"Allow\",\"Principal\":{\"AWS\":\"123456789012\"},\"Action\":\"cloudtrail:PutAuditEvents\",\"Resource\":\"arn:aws:cloudtrail:...\"}]}" }
# Django Models Integration - Get Channel Policy from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json # Get resource policy for channel channel = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-external-events') # Simulate policy (stored in description) policy = { 'Version': '2012-10-17', 'Statement': [{ 'Sid': 'anihpj-policy', 'Effect': 'Allow', 'Action': 'cloudtrail:PutAuditEvents' }] } print(f'{{"ResourceArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:channel/{channel.title}",') print(f'"ResourcePolicy": {json.dumps(json.dumps(policy))}}}') print(f"<JobPost title={channel.title} has_policy=True>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-external-events has_policy=True>

put-resource-policy

Attaches a resource-based permission policy to a CloudTrail channel used for an integration with an event source.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail put-resource-policy \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn} \
  --resource-policy {policy-document}
Parameters Description
  • --resource-arn - ARN of the CloudTrail resource
  • --resource-policy - JSON policy document
Return Value

Returns ResourceArn and the attached ResourcePolicy.

5 Detailed CloudTrail put-resource-policy Examples
Example 1: Set anihpj Channel Policy
#!/bin/bash
# Set resource policy for channel
aws cloudtrail put-resource-policy \
  --resource-arn "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-external-events" \
  --resource-policy '{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
      {
        "Sid": "anihpj-external-access",
        "Effect": "Allow",
        "Principal": {"AWS": "987654321098"},
        "Action": "cloudtrail:PutAuditEvents",
        "Resource": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-external-events"
      }
    ]
  }'

echo "anihpj channel policy configured"
{ "ResourceArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-external-events", "ResourcePolicy": "..." } anihpj channel policy configured
# Django Models Integration - Set Channel Policy from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json # Set resource policy for channel channel = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-external-events') # Store policy in description policy = { 'Version': '2012-10-17', 'Statement': [{ 'Sid': 'anihpj-external-access', 'Effect': 'Allow', 'Principal': {'AWS': '987654321098'}, 'Action': 'cloudtrail:PutAuditEvents' }] } channel.description = json.dumps(policy) channel.save() print(f'{{"ResourceArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:channel/{channel.title}"}}') print("anihpj channel policy configured") print(f"<JobPost title={channel.title} policy_set=True>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-external-events policy_set=True>

delete-resource-policy

Deletes the resource-based policy attached to the CloudTrail channel.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail delete-resource-policy \
  --resource-arn {resource-arn}
Parameters Description
  • --resource-arn - ARN of the CloudTrail resource
Return Value

Returns no content on success. Resource policy is deleted.

5 Detailed CloudTrail delete-resource-policy Examples
Example 1: Delete anihpj Channel Policy
#!/bin/bash
# Delete resource policy
aws cloudtrail delete-resource-policy \
  --resource-arn "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:channel/anihpj-external-events"

echo "anihpj channel policy deleted"
anihpj channel policy deleted
# Django Models Integration - Delete Channel Policy from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete resource policy channel = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-external-events') channel.description = '' # Clear policy channel.save() print("anihpj channel policy deleted") print(f"<JobPost title={channel.title} policy_deleted=True>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-external-events policy_deleted=True>

register-organization-delegated-admin

Registers an organization's member account as the CloudTrail delegated administrator.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail register-organization-delegated-admin \
  --member-account-id {account-id}
Parameters Description
  • --member-account-id - AWS account ID to register as delegated admin
Return Value

Returns no content on success. Account is registered as delegated admin.

5 Detailed CloudTrail register-organization-delegated-admin Examples
Example 1: Register anihpj Delegated Admin
#!/bin/bash
# Register member account as delegated admin
aws cloudtrail register-organization-delegated-admin \
  --member-account-id "123456789012"

echo "anihpj account registered as CloudTrail delegated admin"
anihpj account registered as CloudTrail delegated admin
# Django Models Integration - Register Delegated Admin from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Register member account as delegated admin user = User.objects.get(id=1) # Account 123456789012 admin_role, _ = Skills.objects.get_or_create(name='CloudTrailDelegatedAdmin') user.skills.add(admin_role) print("anihpj account registered as CloudTrail delegated admin") print(f"<User id={user.id} role=CloudTrailDelegatedAdmin>") # Output: <User id=1 role=CloudTrailDelegatedAdmin>

deregister-organization-delegated-admin

Removes CloudTrail delegated administrator permissions from a member account in an organization.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail deregister-organization-delegated-admin \
  --delegated-admin-account-id {account-id}
Parameters Description
  • --delegated-admin-account-id - Account ID to deregister
Return Value

Returns no content on success. Delegated admin permissions removed.

5 Detailed CloudTrail deregister-organization-delegated-admin Examples
Example 1: Deregister anihpj Delegated Admin
#!/bin/bash
# Remove delegated admin permissions
aws cloudtrail deregister-organization-delegated-admin \
  --delegated-admin-account-id "123456789012"

echo "anihpj account removed as CloudTrail delegated admin"
anihpj account removed as CloudTrail delegated admin
# Django Models Integration - Deregister Delegated Admin from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Remove delegated admin permissions user = User.objects.get(id=1) # Account 123456789012 admin_role = Skills.objects.get(name='CloudTrailDelegatedAdmin') user.skills.remove(admin_role) print("anihpj account removed as CloudTrail delegated admin") print(f"<User id={user.id} delegated_admin_removed=True>") # Output: <User id=1 delegated_admin_removed=True>

enable-federation

Enables Lake query federation on the specified event data store.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail enable-federation \
  --event-data-store {event-data-store-arn} \
  --federation-role-arn {role-arn}
Parameters Description
  • --event-data-store - ARN of the event data store
  • --federation-role-arn - IAM role ARN for federation
Return Value

Returns EventDataStoreArn, FederationStatus, and FederationRoleArn.

5 Detailed CloudTrail enable-federation Examples
Example 1: Enable Federation for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Enable Lake query federation
aws cloudtrail enable-federation \
  --event-data-store "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-store" \
  --federation-role-arn "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-federation-role"

echo "Federation enabled for anihpj event data store"
{ "EventDataStoreArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-store", "FederationStatus": "ENABLED", "FederationRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-federation-role" } Federation enabled for anihpj event data store
# Django Models Integration - Enable Federation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Enable Lake query federation event_store = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-store') federation_role = Skills.objects.create(name='anihpj-federation-role') event_store.skills.add(federation_role) event_store.is_active = True # Federation enabled event_store.save() print(f'{{"EventDataStoreArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:eventdatastore/{event_store.title}",') print(f'"FederationStatus": "ENABLED",') print(f'"FederationRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam:...:role/{federation_role.name}"}}') print("Federation enabled for anihpj event data store") print(f"<JobPost title={event_store.title} federation=ENABLED>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-store federation=ENABLED>

disable-federation

Disables Lake query federation on the specified event data store.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail disable-federation \
  --event-data-store {event-data-store-arn}
Parameters Description
  • --event-data-store - ARN of the event data store
Return Value

Returns EventDataStoreArn and FederationStatus (DISABLED).

5 Detailed CloudTrail disable-federation Examples
Example 1: Disable Federation for anihpj
#!/bin/bash
# Disable Lake query federation
aws cloudtrail disable-federation \
  --event-data-store "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-store"

echo "Federation disabled for anihpj event data store"
{ "EventDataStoreArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-store", "FederationStatus": "DISABLED" } Federation disabled for anihpj event data store
# Django Models Integration - Disable Federation from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Disable Lake query federation event_store = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-store') federation_roles = event_store.skills.filter(name__contains='federation') event_store.skills.remove(*federation_roles) print(f'{{"EventDataStoreArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:eventdatastore/{event_store.title}",') print(f'"FederationStatus": "DISABLED"}}') print("Federation disabled for anihpj event data store") print(f"<JobPost title={event_store.title} federation=DISABLED>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-store federation=DISABLED>

get-event-configuration

Returns the settings for the event configuration.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail get-event-configuration
Parameters Description
  • No required parameters - Retrieves current event configuration
Return Value

Returns EventConfigurationArn and EventConfigurationName.

5 Detailed CloudTrail get-event-configuration Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Event Configuration
#!/bin/bash
# Get event configuration settings
aws cloudtrail get-event-configuration

echo "anihpj event configuration retrieved"
{ "EventConfigurationArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventconfiguration/default", "EventConfigurationName": "default" } anihpj event configuration retrieved
# Django Models Integration - Get Event Configuration from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get event configuration settings config = JobPost.objects.filter(title__contains='eventconfiguration').first() if not config: config_name = 'default' print(f'{{"EventConfigurationArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:eventconfiguration/{config_name}",') print(f'"EventConfigurationName": "{config_name}"}}') else: print(f'{{"EventConfigurationArn": "arn:aws:cloudtrail:...:eventconfiguration/{config.title}",') print(f'"EventConfigurationName": "{config.title}"}}') print("anihpj event configuration retrieved") print(f"<JobPost config=default>") # Output: <JobPost config=default>

put-event-configuration

Updates the settings for the event configuration.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail put-event-configuration \
  --event-selectors {event-selectors}
Parameters Description
  • --event-selectors - Event selector configuration
  • --advanced-event-selectors - (Optional) Advanced event selectors
Return Value

Returns no content on success. Event configuration is updated.

5 Detailed CloudTrail put-event-configuration Examples
Example 1: Configure anihpj Events
#!/bin/bash
# Set event configuration
aws cloudtrail put-event-configuration \
  --advanced-event-selectors '[
    {
      "Name": "anihpj-events",
      "FieldSelectors": [
        {"Field": "eventCategory", "Equals": ["Management"]}
      ]
    }
  ]'

echo "anihpj event configuration updated"
anihpj event configuration updated
# Django Models Integration - Configure Events from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json # Set event configuration config, _ = JobPost.objects.get_or_create(title='anihpj-events-config') # Store advanced event selectors selectors = [{ 'Name': 'anihpj-events', 'FieldSelectors': [ {'Field': 'eventCategory', 'Equals': ['Management']} ] }] config.description = json.dumps(selectors) config.save() print("anihpj event configuration updated") print(f"<JobPost title={config.title} selectors={len(selectors)}>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-events-config selectors=1>

generate-query

Generates a query from a natural language prompt.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail generate-query \
  --event-data-stores {event-data-store-arns} \
  --prompt {prompt}
Parameters Description
  • --event-data-stores - List of event data store ARNs
  • --prompt - Natural language query prompt
Return Value

Returns QueryString generated from the natural language prompt.

5 Detailed CloudTrail generate-query Examples
Example 1: Generate Query from Natural Language
#!/bin/bash
# Generate query from prompt
aws cloudtrail generate-query \
  --event-data-stores '["arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-store"]' \
  --prompt "Show me all S3 PutObject events in the last 24 hours"
{ "QueryString": "SELECT eventTime, eventName, userIdentity.userName, requestParameters.bucketName FROM abc123-store WHERE eventSource = 's3.amazonaws.com' AND eventName = 'PutObject' AND eventTime > date_add('hour', -24, current_timestamp)" } Query generated for anihpj
# Django Models Integration - Generate Query from Natural Language from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Generate query from prompt prompt = "Show me all S3 PutObject events in the last 24 hours" # Simulated query generation generated_query = """ SELECT eventTime, eventName, userIdentity.userName, requestParameters.bucketName FROM anihpj-store WHERE eventSource = 's3.amazonaws.com' AND eventName = 'PutObject' AND eventTime > date_add('hour', -24, current_timestamp) """ print(f'{{"QueryString": "{generated_query.strip()}"}}') print("Query generated for anihpj") print(f"<QueryString prompt='{prompt[:30]}...'>") # Output: <QueryString prompt='Show me all S3 PutObject event...'>
Example 2: Generate anihpj Security Query
#!/bin/bash
# Generate security-focused query
STORE_ARN="arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:eventdatastore/anihpj-store"

aws cloudtrail generate-query \
  --event-data-stores "[\"$STORE_ARN\"]" \
  --prompt "Find all failed authentication attempts for anihpj users"
{ "QueryString": "SELECT eventTime, eventName, userIdentity.userName, errorCode, errorMessage FROM abc123-store WHERE errorCode = 'AccessDenied' AND userIdentity.userName LIKE 'anihpj%' ORDER BY eventTime DESC LIMIT 100" } Query generated for anihpj security analysis
# Django Models Integration - Generate Security Query from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Generate security-focused query prompt = "Find all failed authentication attempts for anihpj users" # Simulated query generation generated_query = """ SELECT eventTime, eventName, userIdentity.userName, errorCode, errorMessage FROM anihpj-store WHERE errorCode = 'AccessDenied' AND userIdentity.userName LIKE 'anihpj%' ORDER BY eventTime DESC LIMIT 100 """ print(f'{{"QueryString": "{generated_query.strip()}"}}') print("Query generated for anihpj security analysis") print(f"<QueryString type=security prompt='{prompt[:25]}...'>") # Output: <QueryString type=security prompt='Find all failed authenti...'>

list-insights-metric-data

Returns Insights metric data for trails that have Insights enabled.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail list-insights-metric-data \
  --event-source {event-source} \
  --event-name {event-name} \
  --insight-type {insight-type}
Parameters Description
  • --event-source - AWS service event source
  • --event-name - Name of the event
  • --insight-type - Type of insight (ApiCallRateInsight, etc.)
Return Value

Returns InsightMetricData array with Timestamp, BaselineAverage, and InsightValue.

5 Detailed CloudTrail list-insights-metric-data Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj API Call Metrics
#!/bin/bash
# Get insights metric data for S3 API calls
aws cloudtrail list-insights-metric-data \
  --event-source s3.amazonaws.com \
  --event-name PutObject \
  --insight-type ApiCallRateInsight \
  --start-time $(date -u -d '7 days ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
{ "InsightMetricData": [ { "Timestamp": "2026-01-17T12:00:00Z", "BaselineAverage": 50.0, "InsightValue": 250.0 }, ... ] }
# Django Models Integration - Get Insights Metric Data from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta # Get insights metric data for S3 API calls event_source = 's3.amazonaws.com' event_name = 'PutObject' # Simulated metric data metrics = [ {'Timestamp': '2026-01-17T12:00:00Z', 'BaselineAverage': 50.0, 'InsightValue': 250.0}, {'Timestamp': '2026-01-17T13:00:00Z', 'BaselineAverage': 55.0, 'InsightValue': 180.0}, ] print('{"InsightMetricData": [') for m in metrics: print(f' {{"Timestamp": "{m["Timestamp"]}", "BaselineAverage": {m["BaselineAverage"]}, "InsightValue": {m["InsightValue"]}}}') print(']}') print(f"<QuerySet metrics={len(metrics)} event={event_name}>") # Output: <QuerySet metrics=2 event=PutObject>

list-insights-data

Returns information about Insights events data for trails that have Insights enabled.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail list-insights-data \
  --event-source {event-source} \
  --event-name {event-name}
Parameters Description
  • --event-source - AWS service event source
  • --event-name - Name of the event
  • --start-time - (Optional) Start of the time range
Return Value

Returns InsightsData array with EventTime, EventSource, EventName, and InsightType.

5 Detailed CloudTrail list-insights-data Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Insights Data
#!/bin/bash
# Get insights data for IAM events
aws cloudtrail list-insights-data \
  --event-source iam.amazonaws.com \
  --event-name CreateUser \
  --start-time $(date -u -d '30 days ago' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

echo "anihpj IAM insights data retrieved"
{ "InsightsData": [ { "EventTime": "2026-01-15T10:00:00Z", "EventSource": "iam.amazonaws.com", "EventName": "CreateUser", "InsightType": "ApiCallRateInsight" } ] } anihpj IAM insights data retrieved
# Django Models Integration - Get Insights Data from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from datetime import datetime, timedelta # Get insights data for IAM events insights = [{ 'EventTime': '2026-01-15T10:00:00Z', 'EventSource': 'iam.amazonaws.com', 'EventName': 'CreateUser', 'InsightType': 'ApiCallRateInsight' }] print('{"InsightsData": [') for insight in insights: print(f' {{"EventTime": "{insight["EventTime"]}", "EventName": "{insight["EventName"]}", "InsightType": "{insight["InsightType"]}"}}') print(']}') print("anihpj IAM insights data retrieved") print(f"<QuerySet insights={len(insights)}>") # Output: <QuerySet insights=1>

search-sample-queries

Searches for sample CloudTrail Lake queries.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail search-sample-queries \
  --search-phrase {phrase}
Parameters Description
  • --search-phrase - Search term for sample queries
Return Value

Returns SampleQueries array with Name, Description, and QueryString.

5 Detailed CloudTrail search-sample-queries Examples
Example 1: Search for Security Queries
#!/bin/bash
# Search sample queries for security
aws cloudtrail search-sample-queries \
  --search-phrase "security access denied"
{ "SampleQueries": [ { "Name": "Access Denied Events", "Description": "Find all access denied errors", "QueryString": "SELECT eventTime, eventName, errorCode FROM ... WHERE errorCode = 'AccessDenied'" }, { "Name": "Unauthorized API Calls", "Description": "Track unauthorized API attempts", "QueryString": "SELECT * FROM ... WHERE errorCode IN ('UnauthorizedAccess', 'AccessDenied')" } ] }
# Django Models Integration - Search Sample Queries from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Search sample queries for security search_phrase = "security access denied" # Simulated sample queries sample_queries = [ {'Name': 'Access Denied Events', 'Description': 'Find all access denied errors'}, {'Name': 'Unauthorized API Calls', 'Description': 'Track unauthorized API attempts'} ] print('{"SampleQueries": [') for q in sample_queries: print(f' {{"Name": "{q["Name"]}", "Description": "{q["Description"]}"}}') print(']}') print(f"<QuerySet sample_queries={len(sample_queries)} search='{search_phrase}'>") # Output: <QuerySet sample_queries=2 search='security access denied'>

verify-query-results

Verifies that the query results are signed by CloudTrail.

Syntax
aws cloudtrail verify-query-results \
  --event-data-store-owner-account-id {account-id} \
  --results-location-s3-uri {s3-uri}
Parameters Description
  • --event-data-store-owner-account-id - Account ID of the event data store owner
  • --results-location-s3-uri - S3 URI of the query results
Return Value

Returns verification status indicating if results are signed by CloudTrail.

5 Detailed CloudTrail verify-query-results Examples
Example 1: Verify anihpj Query Results
#!/bin/bash
# Verify query results integrity
aws cloudtrail verify-query-results \
  --event-data-store-owner-account-id "123456789012" \
  --results-location-s3-uri "s3://anihpj-query-results/query-abc123/"

echo "anihpj query results verification complete"
{ "VerificationStatus": "SUCCESS", "VerificationMessage": "Query results are valid and signed by CloudTrail" } anihpj query results verification complete
# Django Models Integration - Verify Query Results from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Verify query results integrity account_id = "123456789012" results_uri = "s3://anihpj-query-results/query-abc123/" # Simulated verification verification = { 'status': 'SUCCESS', 'message': 'Query results are valid and signed by CloudTrail' } print(f'{{"VerificationStatus": "{verification["status"]}",') print(f'"VerificationMessage": "{verification["message"]}"}}') print("anihpj query results verification complete") print(f"<Verification status={verification['status']} uri={results_uri}>") # Output: <Verification status=SUCCESS uri=s3://anihpj-query-results/query-abc123/>

🐳 Containers

AWS Container services provide fully managed container orchestration and registry. This section covers ECS for container orchestration, EKS for Kubernetes, and ECR for container registry. Learn how to deploy your anihpj Django application in containers.

Amazon ECS

ECS section will be added in STEP 17 - Coming soon...

Amazon EKS

EKS section will be added in STEP 18 - Coming soon...

Amazon ECR

Amazon Elastic Container Registry (ECR) is a fully managed container image registry that makes it easy to store, manage, share, and deploy container images. Deploy your anihpj Django application as Docker containers.

create-repository

Creates a repository. For more information, see Amazon ECR Repositories in the Amazon Elastic Container Registry User Guide.

Syntax
aws ecr create-repository \
  --repository-name {repository-name}
Parameters Description
  • --repository-name - Name for the ECR repository
  • --image-scanning-configuration - (Optional) Scanning configuration
  • --encryption-configuration - (Optional) Encryption settings
Return Value

Returns repository details including repositoryArn, repositoryName, and repositoryUri.

5 Detailed ECR create-repository Examples
Example 1: Create anihpj Repository
#!/bin/bash
# Create ECR repository for anihpj Django app
aws ecr create-repository \
  --repository-name anihpj-django

echo "anihpj ECR repository created"
{ "repository": { "repositoryArn": "arn:aws:ecr:us-east-1:123456789012:repository/anihpj-django", "registryId": "123456789012", "repositoryName": "anihpj-django", "repositoryUri": "123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-django", "createdAt": "2026-01-17T15:00:00+00:00", "imageTagMutability": "MUTABLE", "imageScanningConfiguration": { "scanOnPush": false }, "encryptionConfiguration": { "encryptionType": "AES256" } } } anihpj ECR repository created
# Django Models Integration - Create ECR Repository from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost from django.utils import timezone # Create ECR repository for anihpj Django app repository = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-django', description='ECR repository for Django application', is_active=True, created=timezone.now() ) repo_uri = f"123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/{repository.title}" print(f"repositoryName: {repository.title}") print(f"repositoryUri: {repo_uri}") print(f"createdAt: {repository.created}") print("anihpj ECR repository created") print(f"<JobPost id={repository.id} title={repository.title}>") # Output: <JobPost id=1 title=anihpj-django>
Example 2: Create Repository with Scan on Push
#!/bin/bash
# Create repository with automatic scanning
aws ecr create-repository \
  --repository-name anihpj-django \
  --image-scanning-configuration scanOnPush=true \
  --image-tag-mutability IMMUTABLE

echo "anihpj repository created with security scanning"
{ "repository": { "repositoryName": "anihpj-django", "repositoryUri": "123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-django", "imageTagMutability": "IMMUTABLE", "imageScanningConfiguration": { "scanOnPush": true } } } anihpj repository created with security scanning
# Django Models Integration - Create Repository with Scan on Push from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create repository with automatic scanning repository = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-django', description='ECR repo with scan on push enabled' ) # Add scanning config as skill scan_config = Skills.objects.create(name='scanOnPush:true') repository.skills.add(scan_config) print(f"repositoryName: {repository.title}") print(f"imageTagMutability: IMMUTABLE") print(f"scanOnPush: true") print("anihpj repository created with security scanning") print(f"<JobPost title={repository.title} scanOnPush=True>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-django scanOnPush=True>
Example 3: Create Repository with KMS Encryption
#!/bin/bash
# Create repository with KMS encryption
aws ecr create-repository \
  --repository-name anihpj-django \
  --encryption-configuration encryptionType=KMS,kmsKey=arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/abc123 \
  --tags Key=Project,Value=anihpj Key=Environment,Value=production

echo "anihpj repository created with KMS encryption"
{ "repository": { "repositoryName": "anihpj-django", "encryptionConfiguration": { "encryptionType": "KMS", "kmsKey": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/abc123" } } } anihpj repository created with KMS encryption
# Django Models Integration - Create Repository with KMS Encryption from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Create repository with KMS encryption repository = JobPost.objects.create( title='anihpj-django', description='ECR repo with KMS encryption' ) # Add encryption config and tags encryption = Skills.objects.create(name='encryption:KMS') project_tag = Skills.objects.get_or_create(name='Project:anihpj')[0] env_tag = Skills.objects.get_or_create(name='Environment:production')[0] repository.skills.add(encryption, project_tag, env_tag) print(f"repositoryName: {repository.title}") print(f"encryptionType: KMS") print("anihpj repository created with KMS encryption") print(f"<JobPost title={repository.title} encryption=KMS tags={repository.skills.count()}>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-django encryption=KMS tags=3>

delete-repository

Deletes a repository. If the repository contains images, you must either delete all images in the repository or use the force option.

Syntax
aws ecr delete-repository \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  [--force]
Parameters Description
  • --repository-name - Name of the repository to delete
  • --force - (Optional) Delete repository with images
Return Value

Returns deleted repository details including repositoryArn and repositoryName.

5 Detailed ECR delete-repository Examples
Example 1: Delete Empty anihpj Repository
#!/bin/bash
# Delete empty repository
aws ecr delete-repository \
  --repository-name anihpj-django-test

echo "anihpj test repository deleted"
{ "repository": { "repositoryArn": "arn:aws:ecr:us-east-1:123456789012:repository/anihpj-django-test", "repositoryName": "anihpj-django-test" } } anihpj test repository deleted
# Django Models Integration - Delete Empty Repository from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Delete empty repository repository = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-django-test') repo_name = repository.title repository.delete() print(f"repositoryArn: arn:aws:ecr:...:repository/{repo_name}") print(f"repositoryName: {repo_name}") print("anihpj test repository deleted") print(f"<JobPost deleted={repo_name}>") # Output: <JobPost deleted=anihpj-django-test>
Example 2: Force Delete Repository with Images
#!/bin/bash
# Force delete repository with all images
aws ecr delete-repository \
  --repository-name anihpj-django-old \
  --force

echo "anihpj old repository and all images deleted"
{ "repository": { "repositoryName": "anihpj-django-old", "repositoryUri": "123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-django-old" } } anihpj old repository and all images deleted
# Django Models Integration - Force Delete Repository with Images from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Force delete repository with all images repository = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-django-old') repo_name = repository.title # Remove associated images (skills) repository.skills.clear() repository.delete() print(f"repositoryName: {repo_name}") print(f"repositoryUri: 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/{repo_name}") print("anihpj old repository and all images deleted") print(f"<JobPost deleted={repo_name} force=True>") # Output: <JobPost deleted=anihpj-django-old force=True>

describe-repositories

Describes image repositories in a registry.

Syntax
aws ecr describe-repositories \
  [--repository-names {repository-names}]
Parameters Description
  • --repository-names - (Optional) List of repository names
Return Value

Returns repositories array with details for each repository.

5 Detailed ECR describe-repositories Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Repositories
#!/bin/bash
# List all repositories
aws ecr describe-repositories \
  --query 'repositories[*].[repositoryName,repositoryUri]' \
  --output table
---------------------------------------------------------------- | DescribeRepositories | +-----------------+--------------------------------------------+ | anihpj-django | 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.../anihpj-django | | anihpj-nginx | 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.../anihpj-nginx | | anihpj-celery | 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.../anihpj-celery | +-----------------+--------------------------------------------+
# Django Models Integration - List All Repositories from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all repositories repositories = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='anihpj-') print("----------------------------------------------------------------") print("| DescribeRepositories |") print("+-----------------+--------------------------------------------+") for repo in repositories: uri = f"123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.../{repo.title}" print(f"| {repo.title:<15} | {uri:<42} |") print("+-----------------+--------------------------------------------+") print(f"<QuerySet [{', '.join([r.title for r in repositories])}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [anihpj-django, anihpj-nginx, anihpj-celery]>
Example 2: Describe Specific anihpj Repository
#!/bin/bash
# Get details for specific repository
aws ecr describe-repositories \
  --repository-names anihpj-django
{ "repositories": [ { "repositoryArn": "arn:aws:ecr:us-east-1:123456789012:repository/anihpj-django", "repositoryName": "anihpj-django", "repositoryUri": "123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-django", "createdAt": "2026-01-17T15:00:00+00:00", "imageTagMutability": "IMMUTABLE", "imageScanningConfiguration": {"scanOnPush": true} } ] }
# Django Models Integration - Describe Specific Repository from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get details for specific repository repository = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-django') print('{"repositories": [{') print(f' "repositoryArn": "arn:aws:ecr:...:repository/{repository.title}",') print(f' "repositoryName": "{repository.title}",') print(f' "repositoryUri": "123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/{repository.title}",') print(f' "createdAt": "{repository.created}"') print('}]}') print(f"<JobPost id={repository.id} title={repository.title}>") # Output: <JobPost id=1 title=anihpj-django>
Example 3: Get anihpj Repository URIs
#!/bin/bash
# Get repository URIs for Docker push
echo "anihpj ECR Repository URIs"
echo "==========================="

aws ecr describe-repositories \
  --output json | jq -r '.repositories[] | select(.repositoryName | startswith("anihpj")) | "\(.repositoryName): \(.repositoryUri)"'
anihpj ECR Repository URIs =========================== anihpj-django: 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-django anihpj-nginx: 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-nginx anihpj-celery: 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/anihpj-celery
# Django Models Integration - Get Repository URIs from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Get repository URIs for Docker push print("anihpj ECR Repository URIs") print("===========================") repositories = JobPost.objects.filter(title__startswith='anihpj-') for repo in repositories: uri = f"123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/{repo.title}" print(f"{repo.title}: {uri}") print(f"<QuerySet count={repositories.count()}>") # Output: <QuerySet count=3>

get-repository-policy

Retrieves the repository policy for the specified repository.

Syntax
aws ecr get-repository-policy \
  --repository-name {repository-name}
Parameters Description
  • --repository-name - Name of the repository
Return Value

Returns registryId, repositoryName, and policyText JSON document.

5 Detailed ECR get-repository-policy Examples
Example 1: Get anihpj Repository Policy
#!/bin/bash
# Get repository policy
aws ecr get-repository-policy \
  --repository-name anihpj-django
{ "registryId": "123456789012", "repositoryName": "anihpj-django", "policyText": "{\"Version\":\"2012-10-17\",\"Statement\":[{\"Sid\":\"AllowPull\",\"Effect\":\"Allow\",\"Principal\":{\"AWS\":\"arn:aws:iam::123456789012:root\"},\"Action\":[\"ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer\",\"ecr:BatchGetImage\"]}]}" }
# Django Models Integration - Get Repository Policy from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json # Get repository policy repository = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-django') policy = { 'Version': '2012-10-17', 'Statement': [{ 'Sid': 'AllowPull', 'Effect': 'Allow', 'Action': ['ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer', 'ecr:BatchGetImage'] }] } print(f'registryId: 123456789012') print(f'repositoryName: {repository.title}') print(f'policyText: {json.dumps(policy)}') print(f"<JobPost title={repository.title} has_policy=True>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-django has_policy=True>

set-repository-policy

Applies a repository policy to the specified repository.

Syntax
aws ecr set-repository-policy \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --policy-text {policy-text}
Parameters Description
  • --repository-name - Name of the repository
  • --policy-text - JSON policy document
Return Value

Returns registryId, repositoryName, and applied policyText.

5 Detailed ECR set-repository-policy Examples
Example 1: Allow Cross-Account Pull
#!/bin/bash
# Allow another account to pull anihpj images
aws ecr set-repository-policy \
  --repository-name anihpj-django \
  --policy-text '{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
      {
        "Sid": "AllowCrossAccountPull",
        "Effect": "Allow",
        "Principal": {"AWS": "arn:aws:iam::987654321098:root"},
        "Action": [
          "ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer",
          "ecr:BatchGetImage",
          "ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability"
        ]
      }
    ]
  }'

echo "Cross-account pull access granted for anihpj repository"
{ "registryId": "123456789012", "repositoryName": "anihpj-django", "policyText": "..." } Cross-account pull access granted for anihpj repository
# Django Models Integration - Allow Cross-Account Pull from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json # Allow another account to pull anihpj images repository = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-django') policy = { 'Version': '2012-10-17', 'Statement': [{ 'Sid': 'AllowCrossAccountPull', 'Effect': 'Allow', 'Principal': {'AWS': 'arn:aws:iam::987654321098:root'}, 'Action': ['ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer', 'ecr:BatchGetImage'] }] } repository.description = json.dumps(policy) repository.save() print(f"registryId: 123456789012") print(f"repositoryName: {repository.title}") print("Cross-account pull access granted for anihpj repository") print(f"<JobPost title={repository.title} policy_set=True>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-django policy_set=True>
Example 2: Allow Lambda to Pull Images
#!/bin/bash
# Allow Lambda to pull anihpj images
aws ecr set-repository-policy \
  --repository-name anihpj-django \
  --policy-text '{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
      {
        "Sid": "LambdaECRAccess",
        "Effect": "Allow",
        "Principal": {"Service": "lambda.amazonaws.com"},
        "Action": [
          "ecr:BatchGetImage",
          "ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer"
        ],
        "Condition": {
          "StringEquals": {
            "aws:SourceAccount": "123456789012"
          }
        }
      }
    ]
  }'

echo "Lambda pull access configured for anihpj repository"
Lambda pull access configured for anihpj repository
# Django Models Integration - Allow Lambda to Pull Images from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import json # Allow Lambda to pull anihpj images repository = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-django') policy = { 'Version': '2012-10-17', 'Statement': [{ 'Sid': 'LambdaECRAccess', 'Effect': 'Allow', 'Principal': {'Service': 'lambda.amazonaws.com'}, 'Action': ['ecr:BatchGetImage', 'ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer'] }] } repository.description = json.dumps(policy) repository.save() print("Lambda pull access configured for anihpj repository") print(f"<JobPost title={repository.title} lambda_access=True>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-django lambda_access=True>

delete-repository-policy

Deletes the repository policy associated with the specified repository.

Syntax
aws ecr delete-repository-policy \
  --repository-name {repository-name}
Parameters Description
  • --repository-name - Name of the repository
Return Value

Returns registryId and repositoryName. Policy is deleted.

5 Detailed ECR delete-repository-policy Examples
Example 1: Delete anihpj Repository Policy
#!/bin/bash
# Remove repository policy
aws ecr delete-repository-policy \
  --repository-name anihpj-django

echo "anihpj repository policy deleted"
{ "registryId": "123456789012", "repositoryName": "anihpj-django" } anihpj repository policy deleted
# Django Models Integration - Delete Repository Policy from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # Remove repository policy repository = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-django') repository.description = '' # Clear policy repository.save() print(f"registryId: 123456789012") print(f"repositoryName: {repository.title}") print("anihpj repository policy deleted") print(f"<JobPost title={repository.title} policy_deleted=True>") # Output: <JobPost title=anihpj-django policy_deleted=True>

describe-images

Returns metadata about the images in a repository.

Syntax
aws ecr describe-images \
  --repository-name {repository-name}
Parameters Description
  • --repository-name - Name of the repository
  • --image-ids - (Optional) Filter by specific image IDs
Return Value

Returns imageDetails array with imageTags, imageSizeInBytes, and imagePushedAt.

5 Detailed ECR describe-images Examples
Example 1: List anihpj Images
#!/bin/bash
# List all images in repository
aws ecr describe-images \
  --repository-name anihpj-django \
  --query 'imageDetails[*].[imageTags[0],imageSizeInBytes,imagePushedAt]' \
  --output table
----------------------------------------------------- | DescribeImages | +------------+---------------+----------------------+ | latest | 256000000 | 2026-01-17T15:00:00Z | | v1.0.0 | 254000000 | 2026-01-16T10:00:00Z | | v0.9.0 | 252000000 | 2026-01-15T08:00:00Z | +------------+---------------+----------------------+
# Django Models Integration - List Images from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all images in repository (using skills as images) repository = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-django') images = repository.skills.all() print("-----------------------------------------------------") print("| DescribeImages |") print("+------------+---------------+----------------------+") for img in images: print(f"| {img.name:<10} | 256000000 | 2026-01-17T15:00:00Z |") print("+------------+---------------+----------------------+") print(f"<QuerySet images=[{', '.join([i.name for i in images])}]>") # Output: <QuerySet images=[latest, v1.0.0, v0.9.0]>
Example 2: Get Image Digest
#!/bin/bash
# Get image digest for specific tag
aws ecr describe-images \
  --repository-name anihpj-django \
  --image-ids imageTag=latest \
  --query 'imageDetails[0].imageDigest' \
  --output text
sha256:abc123def456789012345678901234567890123456789012345678901234
# Django Models Integration - Get Image Digest from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import hashlib # Get image digest for specific tag repository = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-django') latest_image = repository.skills.filter(name='latest').first() # Simulate digest digest = hashlib.sha256(f"{repository.title}:latest".encode()).hexdigest() print(f"sha256:{digest}") print(f"<Skills name={latest_image.name if latest_image else 'latest'} digest=sha256:{digest[:12]}...>") # Output: <Skills name=latest digest=sha256:abc123def456...>
Example 3: List Images with Scan Status
#!/bin/bash
# List images with vulnerability scan status
echo "anihpj Image Scan Status"
echo "========================="

aws ecr describe-images \
  --repository-name anihpj-django \
  --output json | jq -r '.imageDetails[] | "\(.imageTags[0] // "untagged"): \(.imageScanStatus.status // "NOT_SCANNED")"'
anihpj Image Scan Status ========================= latest: COMPLETE v1.0.0: COMPLETE v0.9.0: NOT_SCANNED
# Django Models Integration - List Images with Scan Status from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List images with vulnerability scan status repository = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-django') print("anihpj Image Scan Status") print("=========================") images = repository.skills.all() for img in images: # Simulate scan status based on name status = 'COMPLETE' if img.name in ['latest', 'v1.0.0'] else 'NOT_SCANNED' print(f"{img.name}: {status}") print(f"<QuerySet images={images.count()} scanned={images.filter(name__in=['latest', 'v1.0.0']).count()}>") # Output: <QuerySet images=3 scanned=2>

list-images

Lists all the image IDs for the specified repository.

Syntax
aws ecr list-images \
  --repository-name {repository-name}
Parameters Description
  • --repository-name - Name of the repository
  • --filter - (Optional) Filter by tagStatus (TAGGED, UNTAGGED)
Return Value

Returns imageIds array with imageTag and imageDigest for each image.

5 Detailed ECR list-images Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Image Tags
#!/bin/bash
# List all image tags
aws ecr list-images \
  --repository-name anihpj-django \
  --query 'imageIds[*].imageTag' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n' | sort
latest v0.9.0 v1.0.0 v1.1.0
# Django Models Integration - List All Image Tags from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost # List all image tags repository = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-django') images = repository.skills.all().order_by('name') for img in images: print(img.name) print(f"<QuerySet [{', '.join(sorted([i.name for i in images]))}]>") # Output: <QuerySet [latest, v0.9.0, v1.0.0, v1.1.0]>
Example 2: List Untagged Images
#!/bin/bash
# Find untagged images to clean up
echo "anihpj Untagged Images"
echo "========================"

aws ecr list-images \
  --repository-name anihpj-django \
  --filter tagStatus=UNTAGGED \
  --query 'imageIds[*].imageDigest' \
  --output text | tr '\t' '\n'
anihpj Untagged Images ======================== sha256:abc123... sha256:def456... sha256:ghi789...
# Django Models Integration - List Untagged Images from jobpost.models import Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost import hashlib # Find untagged images to clean up repository = JobPost.objects.get(title='anihpj-django') print("anihpj Untagged Images") print("========================") # Simulate untagged images untagged = ['orphan1', 'orphan2', 'orphan3'] for img in untagged: digest = hashlib.sha256(img.encode()).hexdigest()[:10] print(f"sha256:{digest}...") print(f"<QuerySet untagged={len(untagged)}>") # Output: <QuerySet untagged=3>

batch-get-image

Gets detailed information for an image. Images are specified with either an imageTag or imageDigest.

Syntax
aws ecr batch-get-image \
  --repository-name {repository-name} \
  --image-ids {image-ids}
Parameters Description
  • --repository-name - Name of the repository
  • --image-ids - Image identifiers (imageTag or imageDigest)
Return Value

Returns images array with imageManifest and imageId details.
